PN CN108979815-A; CN108979815-B
TI Silencing-type tail gas purifier for automobile, has nano-film separator whose inlet is connected with outlet of carbon dioxide recovery device, where separating port of nano-film separator is connected with hydrocarbon gas recovery device.
AU CHENG Q
   QIN X
AE GUANGXI KANGNA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUAN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN KANGNA ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A2538F
AB    NOVELTY - The purifier has a dust collector fixed to a rear side of a dust collecting and purifying chamber. A driving device drives a hollow rotating shaft. An inlet of a nitrogen dioxide recovery device is connected with an air outlet of a main shell. An inlet of a carbon dioxide recovery device is connected with an outlet of the nitrogen dioxide recovery device. A nano-film separator is fixed with a hydrocarbon gas recovery device, where an inlet of the nano-film separator is connected with an outlet of the carbon dioxide recovery device. A separating port of the nano-film separator is connected with the hydrocarbon gas recovery device. A baffle plate, a silencing pore plate and a sound exit hole plate are made of copper material.
   USE - Silencing-type tail gas purifier for an automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen dioxide recovery device, the carbon dioxide recovery device, the nano-film separator and the hydrocarbon gas recovery device are orderly connected together to completely purify harmful tail gas of the automobile.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of a silencing-type tail gas purifier.

TI Asymmetric membrane used for e.g. gas separation membrane for e.g. fuel cell and recovery of hydrogen from ammonia purge gas and purge gas of hydrodesulfurization plant, comprises polyimide resin containing repeating unit.
AU KAWAI R
   ONO D
   ABIKO Y
   SENDA S
   OHIGASHI M
AE MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
GA 201862056P
AB    NOVELTY - An asymmetric membrane comprises a polyimide resin having a repeating unit (I).
   USE - Asymmetric membrane is used in gas separation membrane (claimed) for catalytic reaction, fuel cell, recovery of hydrogen from ammonia purge gas and purge gas of hydrodesulfurization plant, molar ratio adjustment of carbon monoxide and hydrogen in synthesis gas, concentration of helium in natural gas, separation and removal of carbon dioxide gas in natural gas, separation and recovery of carbon dioxide gas injected at crude oil recovery from hydrocarbon, separation and recovery of methane from biogas produced by anaerobic microbial degradation, exhaust gas separation processing, desalting of water by reverse osmosis, ethanol/water separation, non-aqueous liquid separation such as depth desulfurization of gasoline and diesel fuel, recovery and purification of helium from spent helium mixed with welding gas, oxygen enrichment of air, nitrogen enrichment, etc. Can also be used for foodstuff concentration, pharmaceutical industry, brewing factory and refinery process.
   ADVANTAGE - The asymmetric membrane has excellent carbon dioxide permeability, heat resistance, mechanical strength, carbon dioxide selectivity and workability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The polyimide resin has a repeating unit of formula (I).
   Ar' = residue derived from aromatic diamine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polyimide resin comprises 20 mol% or more repeating unit (I). The asymmetric membrane is of hollow fibrous form or film form.
   Preferred Definitions:
   Ar' = group of formula (i) or (ii).

PT P
PN CN113929955-A
TI Preparing rigid polyimide foam comprises e.g.            adding aromatic dianhydride and aliphatic alcohol into            polar solvent to obtain mixed solution, heating, adding            water to obtain transparent polyimide foam precursor            solution and placing into a foaming machine.
AU ZHU M
   WANG Z
   WANG F
   YANG R
   TENG C
   ZHAO Y
   JIU Y
   MA J
AE AEROSPACE RES INST MATERIALS & PROCESSIN (CAER-C)
GA 202217410X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rigid polyimide foam comprises e.g.                (i) sequentially adding aromatic dianhydride and                aliphatic alcohol into a polar solvent to obtain a                mixed solution, (ii) heating the mixed solution                obtained in the resultant step for 0.5-3 hours and                adding water into the mixed solution to obtain a                transparent polyimide foam precursor solution,                (iii) adding the resultant polyimide foam precursor                solution into a storage tank A in a foaming machine                and adding a polyisocyanate compound into a storage                tank B in the foaming machine, (iv) injecting the                materials in the material storage tank A and the                material storage tank B into a high-pressure                container and introducing cobalt into the                high-pressure container to carrying out wet gel                reaction to obtain wet gel, (v), transforming                carbon dioxide liquid into supercritical state                carbon dioxide and maintain the supercritical state                of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rigid                polyimide foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the material cost and                improves the production efficiency; and the                obtained product has small pore diameter, uniform                pores, controllable density, high temperature                resistance, high compression strength, low thermal                conductivity and excellent performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing rigid polyimide foam comprises (i)                sequentially adding aromatic dianhydride and                aliphatic alcohol into a polar solvent to obtain a                mixed solution, (ii) heating the mixed solution                obtained in the resultant step for 0.5-3 hours and                adding water into the mixed solution to obtain a                transparent polyimide foam precursor solution,                (iii) adding the resultant polyimide foam precursor                solution into a storage tank A in a foaming machine                and adding a polyisocyanate compound into a storage                tank B in the foaming machine, (iv) injecting the                materials in the material storage tank A and the                material storage tank B into a high-pressure                container and introducing cobalt into the                high-pressure container to carrying out wet gel                reaction to obtain wet gel, where the pressure in                the high-pressure container is 4-11 microPa and                carbon dioxide in a supercritical state or a liquid                state, (v), when the carbon dioxide liquid in step                (iv) raising the temperature and pressure of the                high-pressure vessel, transforming carbon dioxide                liquid into supercritical state carbon dioxide,                when carbon dioxide is in supercritical state in                the step (iv), keeping the temperature and pressure                of the high-pressure vessel unchanged or increase                the temperature and pressure of the high-pressure                vessel to maintain the supercritical state of                carbon dioxide, extracting the polar solvent in the                wet gel by supercritical carbon dioxide in a high                pressure vessel to obtain porous polyimide gel, and                (vi) placing the porous polyimide gel into a vacuum                oven and heating to imidize the porous polyimide                gel to obtain the product.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                rigid polyimide foam obtained by the above                preparation method, where the density of the rigid                polyimide foam is 100-500 kg/m3, the compressive                strength is 2-15 microPa and room temperature heat                conductivity is 0.03-0.039 W/mK.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  aromatic dianhydride comprises pyromellitic                  dianhydride, 2,3,3',4'-diphenyl ether                  tetracarboxylic acid dianhydride,                  3,3',4,4'-benzophenonetetracarboxylic                  dianhydride, bisphenol A diether dianhydride                  and/or 2,3,3,4-biphenyltetracarboxylic                  dianhydride. The recording solvent is dimethyl                  sulfoxide, N, N-dimethylformamide, N,                  N-dimethylacetamide and/or N-methylpyrrolidone.                  The aliphatic alcohol comprises methanol,                  ethanol, propanol and/or isopropanol. The ratio                  of the amount of the aliphatic alcohol to the                  aromatic dianhydride is 0.5:1-2: 1. In the step                  (ii), the mass of the added water accounts for                  5-15 wt.% of the total mass of the mixed solution                  and water. The mass concentration of the obtained                  polyimide foam precursor solution is 10-65%. In                  the step (ii), the polyisocyanate compound                  comprises polymethylene polyphenyl                  polyisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate                  and/or toluene diisocyanate.Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the                  heating temperature range is 70-100degrees                  Celsius and the reaction time is 0.5-3 hours. In                  the step (iv), the materials in the material                  storage tank A and material storage tank B are                  injected into the high-pressure container                  according to the mass ratio of 1:1-1.3:1. The                  reaction temperature of the wet gel reaction is                  4-45degrees Celsius and the reaction time is 1-3                  hours. In the step (v), when carbon dioxide                  liquid in step (iv), the temperature of the                  high-pressure vessel is raised to 31.1-100degrees                  Celsius, the pressure rises to 7.52-40 microPa                  and the liquid carbon dioxide is transformed into                  supercritical state carbon dioxide, when in step                  (iv) carbon dioxide is in a supercritical state,                  keep the temperature and pressure of the                  high-pressure container unchanged or increase the                  temperature of the high-pressure container to                  31.1-100degrees Celsius, the pressure is raised                  to 7.52-40 microPa to maintain the supercritical                  state of carbon dioxide. In the step (vi), the                  polyimide gel is placed in a vacuum oven and the                  vacuum degree is -0.03to -0.1 microPa, the                  temperature is 200-300degrees Celsius under the                  condition of heating for 1-4 hours to make                  imidize to obtain polyimide foam.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.)
MC A08-S02; A05-J01A; A10-D03; A12-S04A3; A08-D04A; A11-B06

TI Carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation system used            for preparing synthesis gas, comprises fixed-bed            tubular reaction unit provided with catalyst bed,            heating component, air inlet pipe and air outlet pipe,            and air supply unit including argon tank, hydrogen            tank, carbon dioxide tank, and alkane tank.
AU HAN S
   CUI G
   LIANG Y
   WANG Y
   LI X
AE BEIJING MACHINERY EQUIP RES INST (CAES-C)
GA 2021D0208U
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation                system comprises a fixed-bed tubular reaction unit                provided with a catalyst bed, a heating component,                an air inlet pipe and an air outlet pipe, and an                air supply unit. The air outlet pipe is provided                with a gas composition detection component. The air                supply unit includes an argon tank, a hydrogen                tank, a carbon dioxide tank, and an alkane tank,                which are respectively connected with the air inlet                pipe through branches (A)-(D).
   USE - Carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation system                is useful for preparing synthesis gas including                ethylene and propylene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation                system: solves the problems that the composition of                the carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst product                is complicated, the separation is difficult, the                hydrogen source is mostly hydrogen, and the cost is                high; and adopts hydrogen dioxide catalytic                hydrogenation system, which realizes the direct                conversion of carbon dioxide with alkanes as                hydrogen source, and designs catalyst with high                activation performance for the conversion                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   preparation of a nickel-iron compound of                formula: NiFe (I)/ZSM-5 bimetallic catalyst, which                involves (1) weighing ZSM-5 molecular sieve                carrier, adding deionized water, constantly                stirring, recording the volume of deionized water                used when the ZSM-5 molecular sieve carrier is                completely saturated with water, and calculating                unit saturated water absorption amount of ZSM-5                molecular sieve carrier, (2) weighing the ZSM-5                molecular sieve carrier with the same mass as in                step (1), and pretreating at 400-800&#8451;                under a pretreatment atmosphere including argon,                nitrogen or air for 1-8 hours, (3) weighing nickel                nitrate and ferric nitrate powder in a container,                adding deionized water according to the unit                saturated water absorption of the ZSM-5 molecular                sieve carrier, and fully dissolving to obtain an                immersion liquid, (4) adding the immersion liquid                to the ZSM-5 molecular sieve carrier, evenly                stirring to obtain a mixture, and ultrasonically                treating for 0.5-2 hours to obtain an equal volume                immersion method sample, and (5) drying the sample,                and calcining in a muffle furnace at                400-800&#8451; for 1-8 hours, where x, y is                1-8;xy
   preparation of synthesis gas by using the                carbon dioxide catalytic hydrogenation system,                which involves (A) fixing 0.5-2 g bimetallic                catalyst nickel-iron (NiFe)/ZSM-5 in the catalyst                bed in a fixed-bed tubular reactor, pretreating the                bimetallic catalyst with hydrogen having a flow                rate of 100-280 mL/minute before the reaction gas                is introduced at 400-600&#8451; for 2-5 hours,                (B) feeding hydrogen with a flow rate of 120-400                mL/hour and carbon dioxide with a flow rate of                100-150 mL/hour according to the volume flow ratio                of 4-1 into the fixed-bed tubular reactor, (C)                controlling the reaction temperature of the                fixed-bed tubular reactor at 500-700&#8451;,                the reaction pressure at 0.1-2.5 MPa and the                reaction time at 3-8 hours, and (D) quantitatively                analyzing by gas chromatography, and detecting that                the yield of synthesis gas is greater than                95%.3
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The air inlet pipe is                  provided with a three-way valve whose end (A) is                  communicated with the fixed-bed tubular reaction                  unit through the air inlet pipe, end (B) is in                  communication with the air supply unit and end                  (C) is in communication with the gas component                  detection component through the branch (D).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal loading of                  the bimetallic catalyst is 1-20%.Preferred Components: The active components                  of the bimetallic catalyst are iron and nickel in                  the form of metal nanoparticles with an average                  particle size of 2-10 nm. The nickel is reduced                  state or oxidized state. The iron is ferric                  oxide, iron(II,III) oxide and/or ferrous oxide.                  The catalyst bed is provided with the                  nickel-iron/ZSM-5 bimetallic catalyst, preferably                  nickel-iron (NiFe8)/ZSM-5 bimetallic catalyst,                  and nickel-iron (Ni2Fe)/ZSM-5 bimetallic                  catalyst.Preferred Method: The step (C) involves                  controlling the reaction temperature of the                  fixed-bed tubular reactor at 560-650&#8451;,                  and the reaction pressure at 0.5-1.5 MPa.

PN WO2020187583-A1; AU2020244047-A1; AR118325-A1; CN113727964-A; US2022048853-A1; VN82852-A
TI Producing urea formaldehyde concentrates from a hydrocarbon feedstock comprises e.g. producing synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and dioxide and nitrogen by steam reforming hydrocarbon feedstock in primary reforming stage.
AU SYMRENG M
   HAN P A
AE HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
   HALDOR TOPSOE AS (TOPS-C)
GA 202093836J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing urea formaldehyde concentrates from a hydrocarbon feedstock comprises: (a) producing a synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and dioxide and nitrogen by steam reforming the hydrocarbon feedstock in a primary reforming stage and sub sequently in a secondary reforming stage; (b) splitting synthesis gas from step (a) into a methanol synthesis gas and an ammonia synthesis gas; (c) subjecting the ammonia synthesis gas from step (b) in series to a high temperature water gas shift and a low tem perature water gas shift conversion; (d) removing at least part of the carbon dioxide from the ammonia synthesis gas from step (c) to obtain a carbon dioxide depleted ammonia synthesis gas; (e) subjecting a carbon dioxide depleted ammonia synthesis gas from step and (d) to catalytic methanation to remove the unconverted carbon monoxide to obtain a purified ammonia synthesis gas.
   USE - The process is useful for producing urea formaldehyde concentrates from hydrocarbon feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: cost-effective, simple and robust process; and requires minimum of equipment and unit operations with improved energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing urea formaldehyde concentrates from a hydrocarbon feedstock comprises: (a) producing a synthesis gas containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and dioxide and nitrogen by steam reforming the hydrocarbon feedstock in a primary reforming stage and sub sequently in a secondary reforming stage; (b) splitting the synthesis gas from step (a) into a methanol synthesis gas and an ammonia synthesis gas; (c) subjecting the ammonia synthesis gas from step (b) in series to a high temperature water gas shift and a low tem perature water gas shift conversion; (d) removing at least part of the carbon dioxide from the ammonia synthesis gas from step (c) to obtain a carbon dioxide depleted ammonia synthesis gas; (e) subjecting a carbon dioxide depleted ammonia synthesis gas from step (d) to catalytic methanation to remove the unconverted carbon monoxide to obtain a purified ammonia synthesis gas; (f) catalytically converting the nitrogen and hydrogen in the purified ammonia synthesis gas from step (e) in an ammonia synthesis stage and withdrawing an effluent containing ammonia; and (g) passing at least part of the ammonia containing effluent to an urea synthesis stage and converting the ammonia in the effluent to urea product by reaction with at least part of the carbon dioxide being removed from the synthesis gas in step (d) , (h) catalytically converting the carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of the methanol synthesis gas from step (b) in a once-through methanol synthesis stage and withdrawing an effluent containing methanol and an effluent containing nitrogen, hydrogen and unconverted carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide; (i) recycling the effluent containing nitrogen, hydrogen and un-converted carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to a fuel header as fuel to the primary reforming stage in step (a) and/or to the low temperature shift conversion in step (c); (j) subjecting the effluent containing methanol to distillation and withdrawing purified methanol from the distillation; (k) oxidizing at least a part of the purified methanol from step (j) to formaldehyde; and (l) absorbing the formaldehyde from step (k) in an absorber with water and an aqueous urea solution prepared from the urea product in step (g) to obtain the urea formaldehyde concentrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The urea formaldehyde concentrate is admixed to urea pellets or granulate. The effluent containing nitrogen, hydrogen and unconverted carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide is recycled to the low temperature shift conversion in step (c) by means of an ejector booster compressor/blower. The methanol synthesis stage in step (h) is performed in at least one methanol reactor containing at least two fixed bed methanol catalyst.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111610312-A; CN111610312-B
TI Monitoring soil respiration value involves using a            soil respiration monitor to monitor data of soil carbon            dioxide concentration, temperature, air pressure and            humidity in each open multi-air chamber and combining            all data in stable manner.
AU XIE B
   ZHANG X
   GU Q
   ZHU Y
   ZHENG K
AE ZHEJIANG AGRIC SCI ACAD (ZJAG-C)
GA 202090430T
AB    NOVELTY - Monitoring soil respiration value involves                using a soil respiration monitor to monitor the                data of soil carbon dioxide concentration,                temperature, air pressure and humidity in each open                multi-air chamber, and combining all above data in                a stable and continuous soil carbon dioxide                concentration in time series values through Fick's                second law, then substituting the soil carbon                dioxide concentration time series value into Fick's                first law to calculate the theoretical soil carbon                flux value Sn(x,t) that changes with time, where x                represents the height from the soil surface, and t                represents time; according to the theoretical soil                carbon flux value Sn(x,t), using wavelet packet                transform to perform multi-layer decomposition to                eliminate high-frequency noise to obtain a soil                carbon flux time series S with lower noise content                'n(x,t).
   USE - Method for monitoring soil respiration                value.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to monitor soil respiration                value, which allows to accurately obtain the soil                respiration value of each moment, and realize                low-cost soil respiration monitoring.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Monitoring soil respiration value involves                using a soil respiration monitor to monitor the                data of soil carbon dioxide concentration,                temperature, air pressure and humidity in each open                multi-air chamber, and combining all above data in                a stable and continuous soil carbon dioxide                concentration in time series values through Fick's                second law, then substituting the soil carbon                dioxide concentration time series value into Fick's                first law to calculate the theoretical soil carbon                flux value Sn(x,t) that changes with time, where x                represents the height from the soil surface, and t                represents time; according to the theoretical soil                carbon flux value Sn(x,t), using wavelet packet                transform to perform multi-layer decomposition to                eliminate high-frequency noise to obtain a soil                carbon flux time series S with lower noise content                'n(x,t). The signal-to-noise ratio of carbon                dioxide concentration is calcuulated based on the                soil carbon dioxide concentration of each air                chamber monitored in the first step, and according                to the signal-to-noise ratio of the carbon dioxide                concentration, using the large minimum closeness                method to assign a basic probability distribution                value Wn(t) to each sensor. The Dempster-Shafer                fusion is performed on the basic probability                distribution value Wn(t) of each sensor obtained to                obtain the distribution weight W'n(t) of each gas                cell. The low-noise soil carbon flux time series                S'n(x,t) calculated in Step 2 by the distribution                weight W'n(t) of each air cell obtained is                multiplied for weighting Fusion, the data fusion                result Y(t) is finally obtained, where W'n(t) is                the weight assigned to each gas chamber, and                S'n(x,t) is obtained by analyzing Sn(x,t) by                wavelet packet transform Time series of soil carbon                fluxes with lower noise content; and making a                decision based on the weighted fusion result.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The diffusion                  coefficient D and time series of soil carbon                  dioxide (CO2) concentration at each height of the                  air chamber are obtained and finally the stable                  concentration time period that needs to be                  analyzed as the soil CO2 concentration time                  period is obtained. The theoretical soil carbon                  flux time series is calculated using entropy as                  information cost function, then using the method                  from the bottom of the tree to the top of the                  tree to search for the wavelet packet basis                  functit packet basis function suitable for the                  soil carbon flux timeon that minimizes the cost                  function value, and obtain the best wavele                  series; according to the soil carbon flux time                  series and the selected best wavelet packet basis                  function, through comparative analysis, determine                  the decomposition layer number N of the soil                  carbon flux time series; according to the soil                  carbon flux time series, use the determined                  optimal wavelet packet basis function and                  decomposition layer to perform wavelet packet                  decomposition transformation, and obtain the                  wavelet packet decomposition coefficient of the                  tree structure, and for the wavelet packet                  decomposition coefficients, using the threshold                  function threshold and set the threshold level to                  2 to obtain the wavelet packet decomposition                  coefficients after threshold quantization;                  performing wavelet packet reconstruction on the                  wavelet packet decomposition coefficients after                  threshold quantization, and finally obtaining a                  time series of soil carbon flux with lower noise;                  using the maximum and minimum closeness method                  according to the signal-to-noise ratio of the                  carbon dioxide concentration and calculating the                  four closeness degrees of carbon dioxide                  concentration, temperature, pressure, and                  humidity of each air chamber obtained by                  monitoring; furrther calculating the reliability                  according to the fusion of the four closeness                  degrees; and assigning a basic probability                  distribution value Wn(t) to each sensor according                  to the reliability.
DC S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC S03-E14E7

PN CN111484394-A; CN111484394-B
TI Performing in-situ catalytic gas production of combustible ice to synthesize methanol involves combusting ice methane to produce high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor, simultaneously providing heat dry and wet dual integration.
AU XIE J
   ZHANG Z
   BI G
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA AGRIC (USCG-C)
GA 202075967Q
AB    NOVELTY - Performing in-situ catalytic gas production of combustible ice to synthesize methanol involves combusting combustible ice methane to produce high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor, simultaneously providing heat for dry and wet dual integration, and mixing unburned combustible ice methane with the high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor. The dry and wet double integration is carried out to react to obtain synthesis gas and prepare methanol under the condition of a catalyst.
   USE - Method for performing in-situ catalytic gas production of combustible ice to synthesize methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform in-situ catalytic gas production of combustible ice to synthesize methanol, which is convenient for transportation, has high energy utilization efficiency, prevents combustible ice methane from being discharged into the air and reduce energy utilization, inhibits the formation of carbon deposits in the reaction, obtains high energy efficiency methanol, and avoids environmental pollution by reaction waste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for in-situ catalytic gas production of combustible ice to synthesize methanol, which comprises combustible ice methane supply module, combustible ice methane combustion to produce high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor and a functional module, a gas purification unit, dry and wet double reaction tower, gas pressurization system, synthesis gas cooling device, synthesis gas to methanol synthesis tower, cooling recovery device, the combustible ice The methane supply module is respectively connected to the combustible ice methane to produce high temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor, and the functional module and the wet and dry dual reaction tower to transport portion of the combustible ice methane to the combustible ice methane to produce high temperature carbon dioxide and steam, steam and functional module prepares high-temperature carbon dioxide and steam raw materials and simultaneously supplies heat to the wet and dry dual reaction tower, and a portion is directly transported to the dry and wet dual reaction tower as reaction raw materials, the gas purification unit dry and wet double reaction tower, gas pressurization system, synthesis gas cooling device, synthesis gas to methanol synthesis tower, cooling recovery device are connected in sequence, high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor are prepared and the high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor generated by the functional module enter the gas purification unit, and enter the dry and wet double reaction tower after purification, and perform dry and wet double integration with combustible ice Syngas is obtained, and the syngas enters the gas pressurization system to be pressurized to 1.5-2Mpa, then enters the syngas cooling device to cool to 200-300 degrees C, and then enters the syngas to methanol synthesis tower, methanol gas is produced, and then cooled by the cooling recovery device to output methanol liquid, and the exhaust gas produced in the cooling recovery device is returned to combustible ice for combustion to prepare high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor and the functional module for combustion and supply can.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor are purified and mixed with combustible ice methane. The volume ratio of combustible ice methane to carbon dioxide and water vapor is 3:1:2. The pressure of high-temperature carbon dioxide and water vapor is controlled at 0.7-0.9 Megapsacals (MPa) during both dry and wet conditioning, reaction temperature is 750-850 degrees C when preparing the synthesis gas, synthesis gas is under the conditions of 1.5-2Mpa and 100-300 degrees C to produce methanol. The catalyst is selected from one of nickel-based catalysts, copper-based catalysts, and copper-zinc catalysts.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN JP2020121944-A
TI Hydrocarbon synthesis system in which carbon monoxide is supplied from reverse water-based shift device, and hydrogen is supplied from material supply unit, and hydrocarbon synthesis device is configured to generate hydrocarbon.
AU HIRAMITSU Y
AE MITSUBISHI MOTOR CORP (MITM-C)
GA 202076624G
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a material supply unit which is configured to supply carbon dioxide concentrated from air, and hydrogen. A reverse water-based shift device (60) is configured to generate carbon monoxide and water by reverse water-based shift reaction. The carbon monoxide is supplied from reverse water-based shift device. The hydrogen is supplied from material supply unit, and a hydrocarbon synthesis device (70) is configured to generate a hydrocarbon by fischer-tropsch method. A methane gas supply unit (10) supplies methane gas. A first concentrator (21) concentrates carbon dioxide, and a second concentrator (22). An air supply unit (30) supplies air, and a molten carbonate fuel cell (40) is provided with an anode and a cathode. The methane gas supply unit supplies methane gas to anode, and the first concentrator concentrates inair carbon dioxide, and supplies it to cathode. The second concentrator collects portion of gas discharged from anode.
   USE - Hydrocarbon synthesis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The hydrocarbon synthesis system collects and effectively uses the carbon dioxide in atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The hydrogen gas contained in one portion is made to recirculate to the gas discharged from anode, while concentrating the carbon dioxide contained in one portion and supplying the cathode. The air supply unit supplies air to cathode. The gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen are discharged from anode, and the hydrogen which recirculated from second concentrator is supplied. The reverse water-based shift device generates carbon monoxide and water by reverse water-based shift reaction. The heat which arose in molten carbonate fuel cell is supplied to reverse water-based shift device. The water supply unit supplies water, and an electrolyzer is provided with anode and cathode. A thermal-power-generation apparatus is burned and electric-power-generated using a non-fossil fuel. The molten carbonate form electrolyzer electrolyzes carbon dioxide and water, and a first gas tank stores the carbon dioxide and oxygen discharged from anode. A second gas tank stores hydrogen discharged from cathode, and the carbon dioxide and oxygen are supplied from anode or first gas tank to thermal-power-generation apparatus. The hydrogen is supplied from the gas containing carbon dioxide emitted from thermal-power-generation apparatus, and second gas tank. The molten carbonate form electrolyzer is made to supply and electrolyze electric power from electric power system. The methane gas is the hydrocarbon generated by hydrocarbon synthesis device is stored. A second hydrocarbon synthesis system main portion is operated, and the methane gas supply unit of first hydrocarbon synthesis system main portion, when the electric power of electric power system is insufficient. The methane gas stored in second hydrocarbon synthesis system main portion is supplied to molten carbonate fuel cell. The hydrogen stored in second gas tank in second hydrocarbon synthesis system main portion is supplied to hydrocarbon synthesis device, and then first hydrocarbon synthesis system main potion is operated.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the hydrocarbon synthesis system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Hydrocarbon synthesis system (1)
   Methane gas supply unit (10)
   Concentrators (21,22)
   Air supply unit (30)
   Molten carbonate fuel cell (40)
   Reverse water-based shift device (60)
   Hydrocarbon synthesis device (70)

PT P
PN US10450212-B1
TI Treatment of source water involves separating endotoxins and carbon dioxide from source water by mixing aluminum source or iron source with source water, separating foulants and sulfate source water, and removing precipitates.
AU BADER M S
AE BADER M S (BADE-Individual)
GA 201987836P
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment of source water involves separating endotoxins and carbon dioxide from source water by (a) mixing aluminum source or iron source with source water to separate endotoxins from acidic proteins and convert naturally present bicarbonate in source water to carbon dioxide and (b) removing endotoxins and carbon dioxide from source water by hydrophobic membranes to produce detoxified and decarbonated source water, separating foulants and sulfate from detoxified and decarbonated source water by (a) mixing calcium hydroxide with detoxified and decarbonated source water to form precipitates comprising calcium sulfoaluminate or calcium sulfoferrate in precipitator unit, and removing precipitates by filter.
   USE - The method is used for treating source water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses recoverable and reusable amine solvent to induce efficient precipitation, minimizes excessive uses of potable water including hydro-fracturing of shale and sand formations and heavy oil recovery by steam injection, provides zero-liquid-discharge (ZLD) methods to directly reclaim effluent streams from wastewater treatment plants (WWTP) and/or properly amend WWTRP, completely eliminates the use of pressure-driven hydrophilic membranes (microfiltration (MF), ultrafiltration (UF), nanofiltration (NF) and/or reverse osmosis (RO)), and direct and independently treats effluent streams and/or reject streams (sludge thickener, sludge de-watering, and sludge incineration) from WWTP.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) treatment of source water comprising separating endotoxins and carbon dioxide from source water by (a) mixing calcium nitrate with source water to separate endotoxins from acidic proteins and convert naturally present bicarbonate in source water to carbon dioxide and (b) removing endotoxins and carbon dioxide from source water by hydrophobic membranes to produce detoxified and decarbonated source water, and separating foulants and sulfate from detoxified and decarbonated source water by (a) mixing aluminum hydroxide or iron hydroxide with detoxified and decarbonated source water to form precipitates comprising either calcium sulfoaluminate or calcium sulfoferrate in precipitator unit and (b) removing precipitates by filter;
   (2) treatment of source water comprising separating endotoxins and carbon dioxide from source water by (a) mixing amine solvent in anionated form with source water to separate endotoxins from acidic proteins and convert naturally present bicarbonate in source water to carbon dioxide and (b) removing endotoxins and carbon dioxide from source water by hydrophobic membranes to produce detoxified and decarbonated source water, and separating foulants and sulfate from detoxified and decarbonated source water by (a) mixing calcium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide or iron hydroxide, regenerating amine solvent, forming precipitates comprising calcium sulfoaluminate or calcium sulfoferrate in precipitator unit and removing precipitates by filter; and
   (3) treatment of source water comprising separating endotoxins and foulants from source water by (a) mixing amine solvent with source water to form first precipitates comprising endotoxins and foulants in first precipitator unit and (b) removing first precipitates by first filter to produce detoxified and defouled source water, and separating sulfate from detoxified and defouled source water by (a) mixing detoxified and defouled source water with calcium hydroxide and aluminum hydroxide or iron hydroxide, forming second precipitates comprising calcium sulfoaluminate or calcium sulfoferrate in second precipitator unit and (b) removing second precipitates by second filter.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The aluminum source is aluminum chloride, aluminum chlorohydrate, aluminum nitrate, aluminum sulfate, aluminum acetate and/or aluminum formate. The iron source is iron chloride, iron chlorohydrate, iron nitrate, iron sulfate, iron acetate and/or iron formate. The foulants comprise magnesium, phosphates, extracellular polymeric substances (EPS), silica, boron and/or transition metals. The amine solvent is isopropylamine, propylamine, dipropylamine, diisopropylamine, ethylamine, diethylamine, methylamine and/or dimethylamine. The gas is nitrogen, air and/or water vapor. The source water is domestic wastewater, effluent stream from wastewater treatment plant, effluent stream from wastewater treatment and reclamation plant, reverse osmosis reject stream from wastewater treatment and reclamation plant, nanofiltration reject stream from wastewater treatment and reclamation plant, ultrafiltration reject stream from wastewater treatment and reclamation plant, microfiltration reject stream from wastewater treatment and reclamation plant, sludge thickening/dewatering reject streams from wastewater treatment plant, sludge thickening/dewatering reject streams from wastewater treatment and reclamation plant, produced water, agricultural drainage water, and/or mine drainage water. Preferred Method: The method further involves mixing amine solvent. The amine solvent is recovered by gas.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN KR2022110691-A
TI Catalyst for methane synthesis using carbon            dioxide and hydrogen in stoichiometric ratio comprises            hygroscopic material, which is chabazite-type zeolite,            and active ingredient, where active ingredient            comprises nickel, magnesium and iron.
AU JUN-WOO K
   JEONG C W
   JUN K
AE RES INST IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (POSC-C)
GA 2022A1576C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst, comprises 0.1-20 wt.% hygroscopic                material, which is a chabazite-type zeolite, and                active ingredient (remaining amount). The active                ingredient comprises nickel, magnesium and iron in                a weight ratio of 40:10:5, and having methane                selectivity at a temperature of &#8805;                450&#176;C and diameter of 1-3 mm.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for methane                synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst prevents the decrease in the                activity of the nickel-based active ingredient, and                increases the carbon dioxide conversion and methane                selectivity even under methanation conditions with                low hydrogen/carbon dioxide ratio.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing catalyst for methane synthesis,                comprising forming active ingredient containing                nickel, magnesium and iron ions in a weight ratio                of 40:10:5 by adding basic compound to metal                precursor solution containing nickel, magnesium and                iron ions, and mixing active ingredient and a                hygroscopic material, which is chabazite-type                zeolite to contain 0.1-20 wt.% hygroscopic material                and remainder of the active ingredient, and                synthesizing methane using carbon dioxide and                hydrogen in stoichiometric ratio of less than 4                molecules of hydrogen per 1 molecule of carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The catalyst                  comprises 5-10 wt.% hygroscopic material which is                  a chabazite-type zeolite and remainder of the                  active ingredient. Preferred Method: The basic                  compound is added to adjust the pH of the metal                  precursor solution to 7-9.
   EXAMPLE - Metal precursor solution (1.2 M) was prepared                to contain nickel ions, magnesium ions, and iron                ions in a weight ratio of 40:10:5, respectively.                The metal precursor solution was stirred at                50&#176;C to sufficiently dissolve the metal                ions. Ammonia water having a concentration of 15.7                M (30 wt.%) was added to the metal precursor                solution to precipitate metal ions. When the pH of                the metal precursor solution was reached 7-8, the                introduction of ammonia water was stopped, and the                temperature was raised to 90&#176;C to ripen                the initial precipitate, followed by stirring for 2                hours. After aging, the precipitate-containing                solution was cooled, distilled water was                additionally supplied, and unnecessary ions were                removed by filtration and washing. The filtered                precipitate was dried in oven at 120&#176;C,                and calcined in kiln at 600&#176;C for 5 hours                to convert to metal oxide form. 90 wt.% calcined                metal oxide and 10 wt.% chabazite-type zeolite was                mixed to form catalyst for methane synthesis in                pellet form. A catalyst for methane synthesis                including 40Ni10Mg5Fe and chabazite-type zeolite                was prepared by injecting 20 ml catalyst having a                diameter of 1-3 mm into reactor and reducing at                600&#176;C for 5 hours.

PN CN114410697-A
TI Converting carbon dioxide into alcohol and protein by performing gas treatment of first gas, performing biological fermentation of second gas, performing alcohol separation of product and performing protein concentration and drying process.
AU CHEN C
   TONG S
   SONG Q
   CHAO W
   WANG X
   CHEN Q
   DONG Y
   MO Z
AE HEBEI SHOULANG NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (HEBE-Non-standard)
   BEIJING SHOUGANG LANZATECH TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202264898E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for converting carbon dioxide into alcohol and protein involves performing gas treatment of the first gas to obtain a second gas, where the first gas comprises carbon dioxide, performing biological fermentation of the second gas to obtain mixed fermentation product, performing alcohol separation of the mixed fermentation product to obtain alcohol and thallus mixture, and performing protein concentration and drying process of thallus mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbon dioxide into alcohol and protein.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high reaction selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system, which comprises: a gas processing device, where the gas processing device is connected with the air inlet pipeline, and the first gas containing carbon dioxide is used for controlling and purifying the components of the first gas, a biological fermentation device, where the biological fermentation device is connected with the gas processing device, for converting the first gas into alcohol and protein, alcohol separating device which is connected with the biological fermentation device, for separating the alcohol generated in the biological fermentation device, and protein concentrating and dry device which is connected with the alcohol separating device, for concentrating the dryness in the alcohol separating device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The gas treatment is gas component regulation or gas purification. The first gas further comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The thalli of the biological fermentation comprise Clostridium ljungdahlii, C.autoethanogenum, C.carboxidivorans, Butyribacterium methylotrophicum and C.ragsdalei. The system further comprises tail gas processing device, where the tail gas processing device is connected with the outlet end of the biological fermentation device, for processing the tail gas generated by the biological fermentation device and/or tail gas connecting pipeline, where one end of the tail gas connecting pipeline is connected with the outlet end of the biological fermentation device, the other end of the tail gas connecting pipeline is connected with the inlet end of the gas processing device and the tail gas generated by the biological fermentation device is recycled.Preferred Composition: The volume concentration ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide is not less than 2:1. The pH of the biological fermentation is 4-6, and the fermentation temperature is 30-42degreesC.Preferred Method: The method involves subjecting second gas to biological fermentation to obtain a mixed fermentation product, performing biological fermentation of the second gas to obtain tail gas, processing the tail gas and/or mixing the tail gas with the first gas to realize tail gas recycling. The method involves mixing the tail gas with the first gas, mixing the tail gas with the first gas to obtain mixed gas, performing gas treatment to the mixed gas to obtain a second gas, performing biological fermentation of the second gas to obtain mixed fermentation product, performing alcohol separation of the mixed fermentation product to obtain alcohol and thallus mixture, and dry-processing and concentrating the thalli mixture.

PN WO2021236221-A1
TI Membrane used for separating gas such as carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and hydrogen chloride from feed gas stream, comprises base, support layer comprising gas permeable polymer and having carbon dioxide permeance and selective polymer layer comprising selective polymer matrix.
AU HO W S W
   PANG R
   CHEN T
AE OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
GA 2021D5239A
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane comprises a base, a support layer comprising a gas permeable polymer arranged on the base, and a selective polymer layer arranged on the support layer. The selective polymer layer comprises a selective polymer matrix. The support layer has carbon dioxide permeance of at least 25000 GPU at 57 degrees C and ambient pressure.
   USE - Membrane used for separating gas such as carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and hydrogen chloride from feed gas stream (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane is selectively permeable to acidic gas and has excellent carbon dioxide and nitrogen selectivity and high performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) manufacture of the membrane, which involves depositing the support layer on a base and depositing a selective polymer layer on the support layer. The selective polymer layer comprises a selective polymer matrix. The support layer, comprises a gas permeable polymer, hydrophilic additive, solvent, and pore forming agent; and
   (2) separating of gas (G1) from a feed gas stream, which involves contacting the membrane with the feed gas stream comprising the gas (G1) under conditions effective to afford transmembrane permeation of the gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - P referred Components: The support layer has a bicontinuous structure. The graphene oxide has carbon to oxygen ratio of 3-20, preferably 1-3. The graphene oxide is nanoporous. The feed gas comprises hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen chloride, carbon monoxide, nitrogen, methane, steam, sulfur oxides, and/or nitrogen oxides. The feed gas comprises syngas. The gas (G1) is chosen from carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and hydrogen chloride, preferably carbon dioxide. The feed gas comprises a gas (G2) chosen from nitrogen, hydrogen and carbon monoxide and nitrogen, sulfur oxides, or nitrogen oxides, Preferred Property: The support layer has carbon dioxide permeance of from 25000-200000 GPU at 57 degrees C and ambient pressure. The membrane exhibits gas (G1) and (G2) selectivity of 20-300 at 57 degrees C and 1 bar feed pressure and 80-800 at 57 degrees C and 1 atm feed pressure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent comprises 2-pyrrolidone and/or 2-methoxyethanol. The pore forming agent comprises an alcohol, such as 2-methoxyethanol, a polyol, water, an inorganic salt, such as lithium chloride and/or zinc chloride. The low molecular weight amino compound comprises a salt of a primary amine or a salt of a secondary amine, preferably 2-(1-piperazinyl)ethylamine sarcosinate. The cross-linking agent comprises a compound selected from the group consisting of aminosilane, formaldehyde, glutaraldehyde, maleic anhydride, glyoxal, divinylsulfone, toluenediisocyanate, trimethylol melamine, terephthalate aldehyde, epichlorohydrin, and/or vinyl acrylate, preferably 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl) ethylenediamine, (N,N-dimethylaminopropyl)timethoxysilane, (N,N-dimenthylaminopropyl) dimethoxymethylsilane, (N,N-dimethylaminopropyl)dimethylmethoxysilane, (N,N-diethylaminopropyl)dimethoxymethylsilane, (N,N-diisopropylaminopropyl)dimethoxysilane, (N,N-diisopropylaminopropyl)trimethoxysilane and/or its blends. Preferred Property: The low molecular weight amino compound has molecular weight of less than 1000 Da.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The support layer further comprises a hydrophilic additive and is prepared from a casting solution comprising a gas permeable polymer, a hydrophilic additive, a solvent, and/or a pore forming agent. The gas permeable polymer comprises a polymer chosen from polyamides, polyimides, polypyrrolones, polyesters, sulfone-based polymers, nitrile-based polymers, polymeric organosilicones, fluorinated polymers, polyolefins, and/or its copolymer, preferably polyethersulfone or polysulfone. The hydrophilic additive comprises polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyvinylalcohol, a polyalkylene oxides, such as polyethylene oxide, polypropylene oxide and/or copolymer surfactants, inorganic additives, hydrophilically-modified carbon nanotubes, graphene oxide, and zeolites. The selective polymer matrix comprises a hydrophilic polymer, a cross-linking agent, an amino compound, carbon dioxide-philic ether, preferably amino compound. The amino compound comprises an amine-containing polymer. The amine-containing polymer is chosen from polyvinylamine, polyallylamine, polyethyleneimine, poly-N-isopropylallylamine, poly-N-tert-butylallylamine, poly-N-1,2-dimethylpropylallylamine, poly-N-1-methylpropylallylamine, poly-N-2-methylpropylallylamine, poly-N-1-ethylpropylallylamine, poly-N-2-ethylpropylallylamine, poly-N-methylallylamine, poly-N,N-dimethylallylamine, poly-N-ethylallylamine, poly-N,N-diethylallylamine, poly(N-methyl-N-vinylamine), poly(N-ethyl-N-vinylamine), poly(N-isopropyl-N-vinylamine), poly(N-tert-butyl-N-vinylamine), poly(N-propyl-N-vinylamine), poly(N,N-dimethyl-N-vinylamine), poly(N,N-diethyl-N-vinylamine), poly-2-vinylpiperidine, poly-4-vinylpiperidine, polyaminostyrene, chitosan, its copolymer, and its blends. The amine-containing polymer comprises a sterically hindered derivative of polyvinylamine of formula (I). The sterically hindered derivative of polyvinylamine comprises a random copolymer or block copolymer having the structure of formula (II), preferably of formula (III). The amine-containing polymer comprises a sterically hindered amine-containing polymer comprising 5-50 mol%, preferably 45-90 mol% secondary amine-containing monomers, based on the total of all amine-containing monomers in the amine-containing polymer, and less than 5 mol% or 1 mol% or less tertiary amine-containing monomers, based on the total of all amine-containing monomers in the amine-containing polymer. The hydrophilic polymer comprises a polymer selected from the group consisting of polyvinylalcohol, polyvinylacetate, polyethylene oxide, polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyacrylamine, polyamines such as polyallylamine, polyvinylamine, and polyethylenimine, and its copolymer. The hydrophilic polymer comprises a crosslinked hydrophilic polymer. The crosslinked hydrophilic polymer is an aminosilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol. The crosslinked hydrophilic polymer is chosen from 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)propyl) ethylenediamine crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, (N,N-dimethylaminopropyl)timethoxysilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, (N,N-dimenthylaminopropyl)dimethoxymethylsilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, (N,N-dimethylaminopropyl)dimethylmethoxysilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, (N,N-diethylaminopropyl)dimethoxymethylsilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, (N,N-diisopropylaminopropyl)dimethoxysilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, (N,N-diisopropylaminopropyl)trimethoxysilane crosslinked polyvinyl alcohol, its copolymer and its blend. The carbon dioxide-philic ether is chosen from an alcohol ether, a polyalkylene alcohol ether, a polyalkylene glycol, a poly(oxyalkylene)glycol, a poly(oxyalkylene)glycol ether, and an ethoxylated phenol, preferably from ethylene glycol butyl ether (EGBE), diethylene glycol monobutyl ether (DGBE), triethylene glycol monobutyl ether (TEGBE), ethylene glycol dibutyl ether (EGDE), polyethylene glycol monomethyl ether (mPEG), and poly(ethylene glycol) dimethyl ether (PEGDME). The selective polymer layer further comprises 0.01-5 wt.% graphene oxide dispersed within the selective polymer matrix, and carbon nanotubes dispersed within the selective polymer matrix. The membrane is configured in a flat sheet, a spiral-wound, a hollow fiber, or a plate-and-frame configuration. The membrane is selectively permeable to an acidic gas chosen from carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide, nitrogen oxide, hydrogen chloride and water. The amine-containing polymer comprises a sterically hindered derivative of polyamine. The concentration of polyamine used to make the sterically-hindered polyamine is 2 wt.%. The selective polymer layer comprises 5-95 wt.% amino acid salt.R1,R2 = H, alkyl, alkenyl, alkynyl, aryl, or cycloalkyl;or R1+R2+N = hetercyclyl;n = 10-25000;andm,p = 10-10000.Preferred Properties: The sterically hindered amine-containing polymer exhibits degree of N-methylation degree of 20-50%, preferably 30-50%. The amine-containing polymer has weight average molecular weight of at least 50000 Da, preferably at least 1000000 Da. The selective polymer matrix has carbon dioxide and nitrogen selectivity of at least 50, preferably 80 or more at 57 degrees C and 1 bar feed pressure. Preferred Process: The poly(N-methyl-N-vinylamine) is formed using paraformaldehyde as a methylation agent and hexafluoroisopropanol solvent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The base comprises a non-woven fabric. The non-woven fabric comprises a fibers formed from a polyester, polypropylene and/or polyethylene.

PN CN113117747-A
TI New imidazolium salt containing interface ionic liquid supported catalyst useful for preparation of cyclic carbonate from carbon dioxide.
AU MIAO S
   PENG J
   LI J
   ZHANG Y
   WANG J
   WEI C
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202184031A
AB    NOVELTY - Imidazolium salt containing interface ionic liquid supported catalyst (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for preparation of cyclic carbonate from carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): utilizes carrier as mineral material, which is low cost, has large specific surface area and porous structure, increases the catalytic performance of the ionic liquid, but also greatly reduces the amount of the ionic liquid and saves the cost, can be used as a heterogeneous catalyst and is suitable for recovery and recycling of the catalyst; is low cost, easy to prepare and suitable for large-scale production; and has excellent catalytic activity and selectivity for catalyzing epoxide and carbon dioxide to generate cyclic carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Imidazolium salt containing interface ionic liquid supported catalyst of formula (I) is new.
   n = integer.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use of (I) in preparing cyclic carbonate from carbon dioxide, comprising (1) adding certain amount of (I) and epoxy compound into a reactor, (2) placing the reactor on a oil bath, introducing carbon dioxide with pressure of 1-4 MPa while maintaining the reactor tightly closed, maintaining the oil bath temperature at 80-140 degrees C, and performing constant-temperature and constant-pressure cycloaddition reaction for 1-16 hours, and (3) extracting and centrifuging after the reaction to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) taking certain amount of zinc halide, ionic liquid and mineral materials in a single-necked round bottom flask, (ii) placing the round bottom flask under a nitrogen atmosphere, heating it at 80-120 degrees C in an oil bath, and mechanically stirring for a certain period of time, (iii) washing the above obtained reaction product by centrifugation with absolute ethanol, and (iv) placing the washed sample in a vacuum drying oven at 60 degrees C for overnight to dry, grinding the sample and sealing for storage. Preferred Components: (I) contains carrier as mineral material, preferably zeolite, montmorillonite, attapulgite, palygorskite, kaolinite, halloysite, diatomite, saponite, laponite or pore structure containing other minerals. The ionic liquid in (I) is 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, 1-octyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide or 1-decyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide. In step (i), the mass ratio between zinc halide, ionic liquid and mineral material is 0.5-2:1-3:0.2-1. In the use method, the epoxy compound is linear alkylene oxides, aromatic hydrocarbon-substituted alkylene oxides or branched chains substituted epoxy compounds; the mass ratio between (II) and epoxy compound is 12.5:60-20; the reaction temperature is 80-140 degrees C; and the reaction time is 1-16 hours. Preferred Method: In step (i), the reaction time is 10-14 hours.

PT P
PN WO2020260064-A1; FR3097778-A1; FR3097778-B1; EP3990173-A1; CN114555225-A; US2022226802-A1; JP2022538607-W
TI Photocatalytic reducing carbon dioxide carried out            in liquid phase and/or in gas phase, involves bringing            charge having carbon dioxide and sacrificial compound            into contact with photocatalyst of semiconductor and            irradiating photocatalyst.
AU FECANT A
   MARTINEZ FRANCO R
   PIRNGRUBER G
   LEFLAIVE P
   MARTINEZ F R
   ANTOINE F
   RAQUEL M F
   GERHARD P
   PHILIBERT L
   MART N F R
AE IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
   IFP ENERGIES NOUVELLES (INSF-C)
GA 2021010427
AB    NOVELTY - Photocatalytic reducing carbon dioxide carried                out in liquid phase and/or in gas phase, comprises:                (a) bringing a charge containing carbon dioxide and                at least one sacrificial compound into contact with                a photocatalyst comprising at least one                semiconductor based on microporous crystallized                metal sulfide; and (b) irradiating the                photocatalyst by at least one irradiation source                producing at least one wavelength less than the                forbidden bandwidth of the photocatalyst, where the                step (b) is carried out at a temperature of - 10 to                200 degrees C, and at a pressure of 0.01-70                MPa.
   USE - The process is useful for photocatalytic                reducing carbon dioxide carried out in liquid phase                and/or in gas phase.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is new, durable and more                efficient; utilizes microporous crystallized                semiconductors that have high band gap widths                (greater than 3 eV) which do not allow the                materials to absorb a large quantity of photons                from the visible part of the solar spectrum;                utilizes photocatalysts based on metal sulfide                which do not exhibit microporosity; and utilizes                microporosity in the photocatalyst that allows a                low travel time of the reactive charges, electrons                and holes, and thus lower recombination                rates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: When the process is                  carried out in the gas phase, the sacrificial                  compound is a gaseous compound comprising water,                  ammonia, hydrogen, methane and an alcohol. When                  the process is carried out in the liquid phase,                  the sacrificial compound is a soluble liquid or                  solid compound comprising water, ammonia, an                  alcohol, an aldehyde or amine. The source of                  irradiation is a source of natural irradiation.                  The irradiation source emits at at least a                  wavelength of 315-800 (preferably greater than                  280) nm. Preferred Components: The photocatalyst                  comprises at least one semiconductor is in the                  form of a solid comprising anhydrous basis of                  formula (X1aY1bS8:cR) (I).X1 = Sn, Ge, Ti or Zr, preferably Sn;Y1 = at least one divalent metal comprising                  Zn, Cd or Ni, preferably Zn;R = at least one nitrogenous organic                  species, preferably 1,3-bis (4-piperidinyl)                  propane;a = the molar amount of X1 is 0.1-5;b = the molar amount of Y1 is 0.2-8;                  andc = the molar quantity of the nitrogenous                  organic species R is 0-4, preferably 0.2-4.The solid exhibits an X-ray diffraction                  pattern including at least the lines listed in as                  given specification table. The semiconductor has                  a microporous volume of 0.01-0.5 cm3/g. The width                  of the forbidden band of photocatalyst is 1.24-3                  eV.
   EXAMPLE - 0.228 g Tin dioxide was mixed with 0.146 g                zinc nitrate. Subsequently, 0.277 g of sulfur and                2.001 g 1,3-bis(4-piperidyl)propane were added to                the previous mixture. Finally, 11.2 ml of ethylene                glycol and 3.7 ml of deionized water were                incorporated and the synthetic gel was kept under                stirring (250 rpm) for 30 minutes. The precursor                gel was then transferred, after homogenization in                an autoclave. The autoclave was closed and then                heated for 12 days at 190 degrees C under static                conditions. The crystalline product obtained was                filtered, washed with ethanol and then dried                overnight at 100 degrees C to give solid                photocatalyst IZM-5 (Sn3.4Zn0.6S8:1,1R).

TI Carbon dioxide purifying device, has filter tank divided into first filter cavity and second filter cavity, and electric plug board equipped with copper oxide rods and connected with cable plug that is fixed on outer wall surface of filter tank.
AU SHEN M
   QI G
   QING J
   GU J
   YANG D
AE CHENGDU SHENGLI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C2479L
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide purifying device, comprising a filter tank connected by a conduit, a subcooler and a spherical tank, the filter tank is divided into a first filter cavity and a second filter cavity by a clapboard; the second filter cavity is provided with a cyclone separator; the clapboard is provided with an air hole matched with the air outlet of the cyclone separator; the air hole is paved with a hydrophobic breathable film, the inner wall surface of the first filter cavity is paved with an electric plug board; the electric plug board is equipped with several uniformly arranged copper oxide rods; the electric plug board is connected with the cable plug set on the outer wall surface of the filter tank; the device uses the cyclone separator to perform the first section of physical separation of impurities in the carbon dioxide, and the carbon monoxide and copper oxide react to generate carbon dioxide to realize purification, which greatly reduces the purification process of the carbon dioxide, and reduces the cost of the purification process.

TI Making crystalline carbon from carbon dioxide involves polarizing monocrystalline magnesium oxide by contacting monocrystalline magnesium oxide with electron beam and contacting carbon dioxide with polarized monocrystalline magnesium oxide.
AU KANDIANIS M
AE SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL USA INC (SHEL-C)
GA 2020A70956
AB    NOVELTY - Producing crystalline carbon from carbon dioxide, comprises: polarizing monocrystalline magnesium oxide by contacting monocrystalline magnesium oxide with an electron beam, thus producing a polarized monocrystalline magnesium oxide having a purity of at least 98% and an emergent electron velocity of at least 10-8 m/second; and contacting carbon dioxide with the polarized monocrystalline magnesium oxide, thus producing at least in part crystalline carbon with graphene structure.
   USE - The process is useful for producing crystalline carbon from carbon dioxide, where the crystalline carbon is used in crystalline magnesium oxide carbon composite for an energy storage device, a wide band gap semiconductor and a gate dielectric (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides polarized monocrystalline magnesium oxide, which has: a purity of at least 98%, preferably at least 99%; emergent electron velocity of at least 10-8 m/second, preferably at least 10-3 m/second; and enhanced conductivity and low dielectric loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a crystalline magnesium oxide carbon composite comprising polarized crystalline magnesium oxide and the crystalline carbon having graphene structure which are interwoven; and
   (2) an energy storage device comprising at least one of electrodes, electrolytes and/or binders comprising the crystalline magnesium oxide carbon composite.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The contacting is carried out at ambient temperature and ambient pressure. Preferred Components: The molar ratio of crystalline magnesium oxide and crystalline carbon is 999:1 to 1:55.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. GA 2020787711
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide absorbing tank comprises a transparent main body (10) provided with an inner cavity (13) for containing carbon dioxide absorbing material. One top side of the transparent main body is provided with an air inlet pipe (11) connected to an inner cavity and the other side is provided with an air outlet pipe (12) connected to inner cavity. The side end part of the air inlet pipe close to the air outlet pipe is connected to the inner cavity. The end part is provided with a water absorbing sheet. One side of the water absorbing sheet close to the transparent main body is provided with a first buffer cavity. The edge of the water absorbing sheet is fixedly connected with the transparent main body.
   USE - The tank is useful for effectively removing water vapor.
   ADVANTAGE - The tank increases the airflow resistance of the circulation pipeline, improves safety and water absorption, avoids the appearance of fog, and affects the accurate judgment of medical staff.

PN CN111501127-A; CN111501127-B
TI Carbon dioxide-based polyurethane fiber comprises poly(carbonate-ether)diol, aromatic diol, aromatic diisocyanate, organic solvent, amine chain extender, chain terminator, antioxidant and ultraviolet absorber.
AU ZHANG H
   ZHAO Q
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202077186W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-based polyurethane fiber comprises 180-350 pts. wt. poly(carbonate-ether)diol, 3.5-7.8 pts. wt. aromatic diol, 45-92 pts. wt. aromatic diisocyanate, 650-1450 pts. wt. organic solvent, 11.5-18.7 pts. wt. amine chain extender, 0.8-2.1 pts. wt. chain terminator, 0.03-0.11 pts. wt. antioxidant and 0.05-0.09 pts. wt. ultraviolet absorber.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide-based polyurethane fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The fiber has 75% tensile strength of the fiber filament.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbon dioxide-based polyurethane fibers comprising (1) stirring the poly(carbonate ether) diol, aromatic diol and aromatic diisocyanate and reacting at 70-90 degrees C to obtain polyurethane prepolymer, (2) stirring and mixing the polyurethane prepolymer and organic solvent at 5-30 degrees C to obtain a prepolymer solution and adjusting the amine chain extender and chain terminator at 8-15 ml/min adding to the prepolymer solution, (3) stirring and mixing the polyurethane elastic fiber, antioxidant and ultraviolet absorber to obtain a polyurethane elastic fiber spinning dope and (4) spinning the polyurethane elastic fiber spinning dope to obtain carbon dioxide-based polyurethane fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The molecular weight of the poly(carbonate-ether) diol is 2200-3800 g/mol, and the content of carbonate in the poly(carbonate-ether) diol is 32.5-56.8 wt.%. The aromatic diol is hydroquinone bishydroxyethyl ether, benzenediol ( beta -hydroxyethyl) ether, dihydroxyethyl terephthalate or dihydroxyethyl anisole. The aromatic diisocyanate is diphenylmethane diisocyanate, m-xylylene diisocyanate, naphthalene diisocyanate or toluene-2,4-diisocyanate. The organic solvent is N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-dimethylformamide and N-methylpyrrolidone. The amine chain extender is 1,2-ethylenediamine, 1,3-propanediamine, 1,4-butanediamine, 1,5-pentanediamine, 1,6-hexanediamine, 2 -Methyl-1,5-pentanediamine, diethylenetriamine, isophoronediamine, 1,3-cyclohexanediamine, hexafluorodiamine or triethylenediamine, the chain terminator is dimethylamine, diethylamine, dipropylamine, di-n-butylamine, cyclohexylamine, n-pentylamine or ethanolamine. The antioxidant is tetrakis (methylene- beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)-propionate)methane (CHEMNOX 1010), Octadecyl 3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate (CHEMNOX 1076), benzenepropanamide (CHEMNOX 1098) or N,N'-Bis(3-(3.5-di-tert-butyl-4-nydroxyphenol)propionyl) (CHEMNOX 1024) and the ultraviolet absorber is UV-326, UV-327, UV328, UV-329, UV-234 or UV-531.
