PN CN111346532-A
TI Preparing carbonic acid-hypochlorous acid water            comprises injecting water and sodium hypochlorite            solution, adjusting first jetting device, injecting            carbon dioxide, adjusting second jet suction device and            injecting into storage tank.
AU GAO B
   LI Q
   GAO Y
AE TANGSHAN XINGAINIAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TANG-Non-standard)
GA 202063081J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonic acid-hypochlorous acid                water comprises (a) injecting water into the first                jetting device through the water inlet, injecting                sodium hypochlorite solution into the first jet                suction device through the liquid inlet and mixing                water and sodium hypochlorite solution in first jet                suction device, (b) adjusting the first jetting                device and injecting the mixture of water and                sodium hypochlorite solution in first jetting                device into second jetting device, (c) injecting                carbon dioxide into the second jet device through                the air inlet and mixing carbon dioxide with a                mixture of water and sodium hypochlorite solution,                (d) adjusting the second jet suction device to                inject the mixture of carbon dioxide, water and                sodium hypochlorite solution into buffer tank and                (e) stirring the mixture of carbon dioxide, water                and sodium hypochlorite solution in buffer tank to                obtain carbonic acid-hypochlorous acid water and                injecting into storage tank through liquid outlet                for storage.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbonic                acid-hypochlorous acid water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and convenient; has high                preparation efficiency and small power consumption;                does not cause pollution; saves energy; and is                environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The rate of the solution                  ejected by the first ejection device in the step                  (b) is 10-100 l/minutes. The rate of the solution                  ejected by the second ejection device in the step                  (d) is 10-100 l/minutes. The stirring in the step                  (e) is linear cutting stirring. The stirring time                  of the wire cutting is 5-10 minutes. Preferred                  Components: The concentration of the sodium                  hypochlorite solution in the step (a) is 5-15                  wt.%. The carbon dioxide in the step (c) is food                  grade carbon dioxide. The pH value of carbonic                  acid-hypochlorous acid water in the step (e) is                  5-6.9. The first jet suction device comprises a                  water inlet, a liquid inlet, a first jet suction                  tube and a liquid outlet. The second jet suction                  device comprises an air inlet, a liquid inlet, a                  second jet suction tube and a liquid                  outlet.

GA 202049967U

The circulation pipe (7) and the cold storage room (8) are provided a refrigerant circulation pipe which passes between the compressor. The condenser, the expansion device and the evaporator communicates with each other. The evaporator includes an evaporation shell. The evaporation shell is fixedly installed with evaporation fins inside and the evaporation fin is provided with an evaporation coil. The inlet and outlet of the evaporation coil both penetrate and extend to the top of the outer side of the evaporation shell.

PN WO2020064929-A1; CA3113544-A1; AU2019350393-A1; IN202117011431-A; BR112021005608-A2; EP3856872-A1; CN113272407-A; US2022220053-A1; ZA202102231-A
TI Preparing composition comprising oxygenates and            hydrocarbons by Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction by            contacting mixture of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and            carbon dioxide gas with supported cobalt-manganese            Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst.
AU SUNLEY J G
   PATERSON A J
   PATTERSON A J
AE BP PLC (BRPE-C)
   BP PLC (BRPE-C)
   BP PLC (BRPE-C)
   BP PLC (BRPE-C)
GA 202024938E
AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprising oxygenates and                hydrocarbons is prepared by Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis reaction, which involves contacting                mixture of hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon                dioxide gas with supported cobalt-manganese (Co-Mn)                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst, where supported                synthesis catalyst comprises greater than or equal                to 2.5 wt.% manganese, on an elemental basis, based                on the total weight of the supported synthesis                catalyst. Weight ratio of manganese to cobalt, on                an elemental basis, is greater than or equal to                0.2; and amount of carbon dioxide present in the                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is greater than                or equal to 5 vol.%.
   USE - Preparation of a composition comprising                oxygenates and hydrocarbons by Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The combination of the use of carbon dioxide                in the gaseous feed, and the use of a supported                catalyst comprising manganese and cobalt can                improve activity and selectivity of a                Fischer-Tropsch process for the production of                oxygenates, in particularly alcohols.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                carbon dioxide in the feed to a Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis reaction using a supported Co-Mn                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst, for increasing                the selectivity of the Fischer-Tropsch process for                the production of alcohols.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Amount of carbon                  dioxide present in the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis                  reaction is greater than or equal to 10 vol.%,                  preferably 5-25 vol.%. Support material of the                  supported Co-Mn Fischer-Tropsch synthesis                  catalyst comprises a material selected from                  titania, zinc oxide, zirconia, and ceria,                  preferably titania. Weight ratio of manganese to                  cobalt present in the supported Co-Mn                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst, on an                  elemental basis, is 0.2-3. Supported Co-Mn                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst contains 5-35                  wt.% cobalt and 2.5-15 wt.% manganese, on an                  elemental basis, based on the total weight of the                  supported synthesis catalyst. Combined amount of                  cobalt and manganese in the supported Co-Mn                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis catalyst is less than                  or equal to 30 wt.%, on an elemental basis, based                  on the total weight of the supported synthesis                  catalyst. Molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon                  monoxide is greater than or equal to 1. Preferred                  Condition: Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction is                  conducted at a temperature of less than or equal                  to 300 degrees C. Fischer-Tropsch synthesis                  reaction is conducted at a pressure of 1-10 MPa                  absolute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Compounds in the                  composition comprise oxygenates and hydrocarbons                  having carbon chain length of greater than or                  equal to 8 carbon atoms, greater than or equal to                  30 wt.% of which are oxygenates. At least 50 wt.%                  of the oxygenates in the composition comprising                  oxygenates and hydrocarbons are alcohols.

PN CN110156864-A
TI Extracting black ginseng Rg3 by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises e.g. taking red ginseng, adding to absolute ethanol, dissolving, placing into ultrasonic oscillator, filtering and rapidly spraying supercritical solution.
AU HE D
   LIN L
   XU S
   WU S
   CHEN Z
   WU X
   JIN C
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 2019771343
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting black ginseng Rg3 by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction comprises (i) taking red ginseng, adding to absolute ethanol, stirring to dissolve, placing into ultrasonic oscillator for 5 minutes, filtering and placing the filtrate into refrigerator, where the mass ratio of red ginseng to absolute ethanol is 1:(5-50), (ii) placing in a supercritical reaction vessel, opening a carbon dioxide cylinder, and cooling the carbon dioxide gas to a liquid by a cold machine, and pressurizing by a high pressure pump and introducing into reactor at a pressure of 15-40 MPa and a temperature of 313-343 K, and holding the pressure for 20-60 minutes to obtain a supercritical solution, (iii) rapidly spraying the supercritical solution into Phosphate buffered saline buffer solution through a nozzle, dispersing and precipitating to obtain ginsenoside which retains more than 90% of water soluble, and (iv) collecting ginsenoside suspension, which is nutrient component Rg3 of the black ginseng.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting black ginseng Rg3 by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high safety, good human absorption rate and long storage stability and no toxicity, and is environmentally-friendly and econocimal.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of phosphate buffered saline buffer solution is 0.01 mol/l and a pH of 7.2. Preferred Method: The addition amount of anhydrous ethanol is 5-30% based on the mole fraction in carbon dioxide. The nozzle diameter is 100-500 mu m. The method specifically comprises (a) taking red ginseng, adding to absolute ethanol, stirring to dissolve, placing into ultrasonic oscillator for 5 minutes, filtering and placing the filtrate into refrigerator, where the mass ratio of red ginseng to absolute ethanol, (ii) placing in a supercritical reaction vessel, opening a carbon dioxide cylinder, and cooling the carbon dioxide gas to a liquid by a cold machine, and pressurizing by a high pressure pump and introducing into reactor at a pressure of 15 MPa and a temperature of 318 K, and holding the pressure for 30 minutes to obtain a supercritical solution, (iii) rapidly spraying the supercritical solution into phosphate buffered saline buffer solution through a 500 mu m nozzle, dispersing and precipitating to obtain ginsenoside which retains more than 90% of water soluble, and (iv) collecting ginsenoside suspension, which is nutrient component Rg3 of the black ginseng.

PN WO2019141827-A1; CA3085243-A1; EP3740602-A1
TI Catalyst system used in gas-diffusion electrode, for catalyzed electrochemical reactions, preparing gas-diffusion electrode, and electrocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide, comprises a catalyst comprising bismuth and indium.
AU PARAJULI R
   ANSOVINI D
   PHILIPS M F
   SCHOUTEN K J P
AE AVANTIUM KNOWLEDGE CENT BV (AVAN-Non-standard)
   AVANTIUM KNOWLEDGE CENT BV (AVAN-Non-standard)
GA 201964701H
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst system comprises a catalyst, where the catalyst comprises 5-94 wt.% bismuth and 6-95 wt.% indium, based on the total amount of bismuth and indium.
   USE - Catalyst system used in gas-diffusion electrode, for catalyzed electrochemical reactions, preparing gas-diffusion electrode, and electrocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) gas-diffusion electrode comprises a gas-diffusion layer on a conductive substrate, where gas-diffusion layer comprises the catalyst system;
   (2) electrochemical cell comprises one gas chamber and liquid chamber, where the chambers are separated by a gas-diffusion electrode;
   (3) method of preparing gas-diffusion electrode comprising a catalyst system involves providing a conductive substrate; and applying bismuth, indium, conductive support and a binder to the conductive substrate; and
   (4) method of electrocatalytically reducing carbon dioxide, involves introducing an anolyte to a first cell compartment of an electrochemical cell, where the first cell compartment comprises an anode, involves introducing a catholyte and carbon dioxide to a second cell compartment of the electrochemical cell, the second cell compartment comprising a cathode, and applying an electrical potential between the anode and the cathode sufficient to reduce the carbon dioxide to a reduced reaction product, where the cathode comprises a catalyst system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst system comprises an electrically conductive support and catalyst, where the catalyst comprises 5-94 wt.% bismuth and 6-95 wt.% indium, based on the total amount of bismuth and indium. The amount of bismuth is in the range of 10-90 wt.% or 40-60 wt.%, based on the total amount of bismuth and indium. Preferred Components: The conductive support comprises a porous structure of carbon particles. Preferred Conditions: The catalyst system is bonded to the conductive substrate by a hydrophobic binder. The binder is a hydrophobic binder. The cathode is a gas-diffusion electrode and carbon dioxide is reduced to a reaction product selected from carboxylates and carboxylic acids. The carbon dioxide is reduced to formate or formic acid in an aqueous medium and carbon dioxide is reduced to oxalate or oxalic acid in a non-aqueous medium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

TI Carbon dioxide capture material for removing            carbon dioxide from gas or mixture of gasses comprises            zeolite monoliths having zeolite material, and            binders.
AU REZAEI F
   THAKKAR H V
AE UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
   REZAEI F (REZA-Individual)
   THAKKAR H V (THAK-Individual)
GA 2019258443
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide (CO2) capture material                comprises greater than or equal to 1 zeolite                monoliths having zeolite material selected from 13X                zeolite material and 5A zeolite material; and                greater than or equal to 1 binders.
   USE - A carbon dioxide (CO2) capture material for                removing CO2 from gas or mixture of gasses                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The 3D-printed zeolite monoliths in capture                material exhibit excellent mechanical and                adsorption properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparing a three-dimensional (3D)-printed                zeolite monolith which involves mixing zeolite                powder, bentonite clay, plasticizing organic                binder, and co-binder using high-performance                dispersing instrument at 2500 rpm to obtain a                powder mixture; adding sufficient amount of                distilled water to powder mixture and mixing using                the high-performance dispersing instrument at 2500                rpm to form aqueous paste; and depositing aqueous                paste layer-by-layer, using 3D-printing apparatus,                onto a substrate to produce 3D-printed zeolite                monolith; and
   (2) removing CO2 from gas or mixture of gasses                comprising less than or equal to 5% CO2 which                involves bringing a gas or mixture of gasses                comprising carbon dioxide in contact with CO2                capture material; and capturing greater than or                equal to 1 portion of CO2 in gas or mixture of                gasses in CO2 capture material where CO2 capture                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Zeolite monoliths are                  prepared layer by layer using a three-dimensional                  (3D) printer. The CO2 is removed from gas or                  mixture of gasses in enclosed compartment chosen                  from submarine compartment, spacecraft                  compartment, building, and dwelling. Preferred                  Components: Zeolite monoliths comprise greater                  than or equal to 80 wt.% zeolite material.                  Preferred Properties: Zeolite monoliths exhibit                  mesopore volume of greater than or equal to 0.009                  cm3/g, mesoporosity of 0.009-0.02 cm3/g, wall                  thickness of 0.4-0.8 mm, channel width of 0.2-0.6                  mm, compression strength of 0.3-0.69 MPa, Young's                  modulus of 7.5-15 MPa or 1.65-9.45 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Binders are selected                  from bentonite clay, and/or methyl cellulose.                  Binders comprise 7-15 wt.% zeolite monolith, 7-15                  wt.% bentonite clay and 2-3.5 wt.% methyl                  cellulose. Zeolite monoliths further comprise a                  polyvinyl alcohol co-binder.

AU KOGA K
   HONG D C
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 2022780985
AB    NOVELTY - Artificial photosynthetic element comprises composite nanoparticles in which metal nanoparticles and oxide semiconductor nanoparticles are heterobonded are dispersed and immobilized on a semiconductor film capable of oxidizing water molecules with light, and carbon dioxide adsorbed on the surface of composite nanoparticles can be photoconverted to methane, ethane, their alcohols, or carbon monoxide.
   USE - The artificial photosynthetic element is useful for manufacturing basic chemicals which are raw materials for fuel gas and chemical industrial products using water vapor and carbon dioxide as raw materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The artificial photosynthesis element realizes electron transfer efficiency, and reduces carbon dioxide with high efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for manufacturing the artificial photosynthesis element, involving heterojunctioning composite nanoparticles in which metal nanoparticles and oxide semiconductor nanoparticles, performing an aerosol deposition method that passes through the nozzle together with a gas flow, supporting the water molecules on a semiconductor film that can be oxidized by the light installed facing the nozzle, dispersing and immobilizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The semiconductor film is an oxide or composite oxide of one element comprising titanium, tungsten, vanadium, tantalum, manganese, cadmium, zinc, cobalt, iron, nickel, silver, antimony, indium, oxynitride, sulfide, selenium, telluride, phosphide, silicon and/or graphitic carbon nitride. The oxide semiconductor portion of the composite nanoparticles is an oxide or composite oxide comprising element comprising copper, cobalt, nickel, iron, vanadium, cadmium, silicon and/or rhenium. The metal portion of the composite nanoparticles is a metal or alloy comprising element comprising gold, platinum, palladium, rhodium, iridium, ruthenium, rhenium, silver, copper, iron, manganese, cobalt and/or nickel. The metal portion of the composite nanoparticles is an alloy containing a metal component constituting the oxide semiconductor portion. Preferred Conditions: The semiconductor film has a thickness of 0.5-10 mm, and is a dense continuous film, or a film having voids composed of semiconductor particles having a particle size of 0.5-0.1 mm. The particle size of the composite nanoparticles is 0.5-1000 nm.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing near infrared photo-thermal catalyst                involves mixing the graphene oxide dispersion                liquid and dehydrating agent to obtain graphene                oxide solution, mixing the graphene oxide solution                and branched polyethylene imine and drying to                obtain the graphene oxide-polyethylenimine carrier,                after mixing the graphene oxide-polyethylenimine                carrier with water, adjusting the pH value to                2-4.5, dropping single substituted Keggin type                polyoxometalates aqueous solution, performing ion                replacement reaction, obtaining the near infrared                photo-thermal catalyst; the solute of the                mono-substituted Keggin type POM aqueous solution                is K_6SiW_11Co (H_2O) O_39 or H_4SiW_11Ce (H_2O)                _4O_39.

PN CN112159643-A
TI Self-spraying sponge rubber comprises carbon dioxide-based polyether, graphene oxide, diisocyanate, coupling agent, catalyst, chain extender and water.
AU DING B
AE HUBEI MINGYANG SCULPTURE TECHNOLOGY CO (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021070348
AB    NOVELTY - A self-spraying sponge rubber comprises 100-140 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based polyether, 10-20 pts. wt. graphene oxide, 220-250 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 2-6 pts. wt. coupling agent, 2-9 pts. wt. catalyst, 10-20 pts. wt. chain extender and 150-200 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Self-spraying sponge rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The self-spraying sponge rubber is environmentally-friendly and has high bonding strength, fast curing speed and long opening time. The preparation method is simple, economical and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the self-spraying sponge rubber, which involves (1) heating carbon dioxide-based polyether, graphene oxide, diisocyanate, catalyst, and chain extender to 80-90 degrees C and reacting for 100-120 minutes to obtain prepolymer solution, (2) adding water to prepolymer solution, emulsifying under high-speed stirring speed of 200-300 rpm for 30-40 minutes to obtain emulsion, and (3) heating to 60-70 degrees C, adding coupling agent to the emulsion and reacting for 20-30 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The graphene oxide is epichlorohydrin modified graphene oxide. The diisocyanate is one or more of toluene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate, diphenylmethane diisocyanate and hexamethylene diisocyanate. The coupling agent is one or more of gamma -glycidoxypropyltrimethoxysilane, gamma -chloropropyltrimethoxysilane, and gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane. The catalyst is dibutyltin dilaurate or stannous octoate. The chain extender is one or more of isophorone diamine, 2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid, and dihydroxymethyl butanoic acid (DMBA). Preferred Composition: The self-spraying sponge rubber preferably comprises 120 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based polyether, 15 pts. wt. graphene oxide, 120 pts. wt. diphenylmethane diisocyanate, 120 pts. wt. hexamethylene diisocyanate, 4 pts. wt. gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, 7 pts. wt. dibutyltin dilaurate, 15 pts. wt. isophorone diamine, and 180 pts. wt. water. Preferred Condition: The step (1) is carried out at 80 degrees C for 120 minutes. The step (2) is carried out at 300 rpm for 30 minutes. The heating temperature in step (3) is 60 degrees C and reaction time in step (3) is 30 minutes.

TI Bismuth vanadate/graphitic carbon nitride            heterojunction photocatalyst used for catalytic            reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises bismuth vanadate            and graphitic carbon nitride.
AU PENG H
   GUO R
   CAI M
AE SHANGHAI YIFAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A7392X
AB    NOVELTY - Bismuth vanadate/graphitic carbon nitride                heterojunction photocatalyst comprises 5-40 wt.%                bismuth vanadate and the remainder being graphitic                carbon nitride.
   USE - The bismuth vanadate/graphitic carbon nitride                heterojunction photocatalyst is used for the                catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has excellent photocatalytic                activity, excellent catalytic reduction performance                of carbon dioxide, and long catalytic life. The                method is simple and cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                preparing the bismuth vanadate/graphitic carbon                nitride heterojunction photocatalyst comprising (a)                mixing graphitic carbon nitride powder, adding                ethylene glycol and stirring evenly, (b) adding                bismuth nitrate and ammonium vanadate to the                solution of step (a), and stirring to obtain a                uniform solution, (c) transferring the solution of                step (b) to an autoclave, heat-treating at 150-200                degrees C for 5-15 hours, washing and drying the                resulting product to obtain the product, where the                bismuth vanadate accounts for 0.01-50% of the total                weight of the catalyst, and the molar ratio of                bismuth element to vanadium element in the raw                materials bismuth nitrate and ammonium vanadate is                1.5-2.5:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The bismuth vanadate                  accounts for 10-30 wt.% of the catalyst. The mass                  ratio of the volume of the ethylene glycol to the                  graphitic carbon nitride powder in the step (a)                  is (100-200) ml:1 g. The molar ratio of bismuth                  element to vanadium element in the step (b), in                  the raw materials bismuth nitrate and ammonium                  vanadate, is 1.5-2.0:1. Preferred Process: The                  graphitic carbon nitride powder in step (a) is                  prepared by: grinding urea or melamine for 0.1-10                  hours, transferring to a crucible, and heating                  the crucible at a rate of 5-20 degrees C/minute                  at 400-450 degrees C, maintaining for 1-3 hours,                  increasing the temperature to 500-600 degrees C                  at a rate of 5-20 degrees C/minute, and                  maintaining for 1-3 hours; adding the graphitic                  carbon nitride powder to ethylene glycol and                  subjecting to ultrasonically-treating to obtain a                  graphitic carbon nitride solution. Preferred                  Conditions: The step (b) comprises controlling                  the stirring rate at 400-600 rpm to obtain a                  uniform solution. The step (c) comprises                  controlling the treatment at 170-190 degrees C                  for 7-15 hours. The drying temperature in the                  step (c) is 60-100 degrees C, and the drying time                  is 12-24 hours.

PN CN111226796-A; CN111226796-B
TI Performing sugar-free tissue culture method for Dendrobium candidum, comprises taking the stem segments of Dendrobium candidum, sterilizing them, and cutting them into stem segments containing axillary buds.
AU WANG L
   HUANG H
   TAN P
   ZHONG Z
   YU C
AE JIANGMEN XINHUI FORESTRY SCI INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
   JIANGMEN XINHUI FORESTRY SCI INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202052692H
AB    NOVELTY - Performing sugar-free tissue culture method for Dendrobium candidum comprises taking the stem segments of Dendrobium candidum, sterilizing them, and cutting them into stem segments containing axillary buds. Inoculate the stem segments containing the axillary buds into the germination medium and cultivate until germination of young shoots. Inoculate the young buds into the subculture medium to propagate the cluster buds to obtain rootless buds. Transfer the rootless shoots to the container, add the culture solution for cultivation, pass carbon dioxide for a predetermined period of time, and do not illuminate when passing carbon dioxide, and when carbon dioxide is not introduced, light is applied, humidity is controlled, and the root system is grown to obtain seedlings and transplanted.
   USE - Method for performing sugar-free tissue culture method for Dendrobium candidum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform sugar-free tissue culture method for Dendrobium candidum, which shortens the cultivation cycle of Dendrobium candidum, and iimproves the enzyme activity in plants, and the survival rate of plants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The sugar-free tissue culture method of Dendrobium candidumv further comprises taking the stem section of Dendrobium candidum and shaking disinfection with 0.08-0.12 wt.% benzalkonium bromide solution for 20-40s, rinsing with water, soaking with alcohol for 20-40s, rinsing with sterile water, sterilizing with 0.08-0.12 wt.% mercury chloride for 8-12min, rinsing with sterile water, and cutting into stem segments containing axillary buds. The time period for introducing carbon dioxide is from 18:00 to 8:00 the next day. The concentration of carbon dioxide fed in is 1200-1400 mol-1. The light intensity in the time period in which carbon dioxide is not introduced is 4500-4700 lx.

The mass percentage                manganese/(manganese and zinc) is 10-40%.

PT P
PN CN110742912-A
TI Preparing nano-phytoncide comprises e.g. crushing Juniperus chinensis wood branches and leaves, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the crushed Juniperus chinensis wood branches and leaves in the presence of a carrier.
AU WANG C
AE SHENZHEN FDJ NANO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020117801
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-phytoncide comprises (i) crushing Juniperus chinensis wood branches and leaves, (ii) performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on the crushed Juniperus chinensis wood branches and leaves obtained in the step (i) in the presence of a carrier, collecting the extract, and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain phytoncide, (iii) distilling the phytoncide obtained in the step (ii) to obtain a pretreatment solution, and (iv) slowly adding the pretreatment solution obtained in the step (iii) to the octenyl succinate starch and phosphatidylcholine solution dissolved in deionized water while sonicating for 20-40 minutes to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nano-phytoncide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes supercritical carbon dioxide to extract phytoncide extract; obtains nano-phytoncide extract, which has high purity; and ensures efficient sterilization effect. The nano-phytoncide: exists in stable for a long time; and extends its shelf life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for nano-phytoncide prepared by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the wood branches and leaves of Juniperus chinensis are crushed and passed through a 3-6 mm pore size sieve. The extraction pressure in the step (ii) is 14-18 MPa. The extraction temperature in the step (ii) is 45-55 degrees C. The distillation temperature in the step (iii) is 75-85 degrees C. The method further comprising (v) homogenizing the product obtained in the step (iv) for 2-4 times to obtain high-performance nano-phytoncide. The homogenizing pressure in the step (v) is 80-90 MPa. Preferred Components: The carrier is anhydrous ethanol, water, isopropyl alcohol, methanol, acetone, and/or ethyl acetate. The weight ratio of the octenyl succinate starch and phosphatidylcholine is (10-12):1.

PT P
PN CN110451509-A; CN110451509-B
TI Preparing nitrogen-doped porous carbon material            for carbon dioxide adsorption, by heating agar powder,            carbonizing, cooling, grinding product with zinc            nitrate hexahydrate, treating activated product with            hydrochloric acid, and drying.
AU SHI J
   CUI H
   XU J
   YAN N
   LIU Y
AE JIANGXI ACAD SCI APPLIED CHEM INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201998465L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nitrogen-doped porous                carbon material for carbon dioxide adsorption using                zinc nitrate as activator, involves (a) placing an                agar powder in a tube furnace, heating, carbonizing                under the protection of an inert atmosphere, and                naturally cooling to room temperature, (b) grinding                the carbonized product with zinc nitrate                hexahydrate, uniformly mixing to obtain a mixture,                and putting the mixture to the tube furnace, (c)                heating the tube furnace at 800-950 degrees C under                the protection of the inert atmosphere for                activation reaction, and naturally cooling to room                temperature, and (d) treating the activated product                with hydrochloric acid, repeatedly washing with                deionized water, and drying to obtain the                nitrogen-doped porous carbon material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                nitrogen-doped porous carbon material for carbon                dioxide adsorption using zinc nitrate as                activator.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts simple preparation steps,                and the prepared nitrogen-doped carbon material has                developed porous structure and excellent adsorption                properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  placing 2 g agar powder in the tube furnace,                  increasing the furnace temperature from room                  temperature to 500 degrees C at a rate of 5                  degrees C/minute under the protection 60                  ml/minute nitrogen, and heating at 500 degrees C                  for 2 hours. The step (b) involves taking 0.5 g                  carbonized product and 3 g zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate, grinding, uniformly mixing, adding                  the grinded mixture to a porcelain boat, and                  putting the porcelain boat into the tube furnace.                  The step (c) involves protecting the furnace                  under 60 ml/minute nitrogen, increasing the                  temperature at 800 degrees C at a rate of 5                  degrees C/minute and performing the activation                  treatment at a constant temperature for 2 hours.                  The step (d) involves treating the activated                  product with 10% hydrochloric acid, and drying at                  100 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain the product.                  Preferred Composition: The zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate is used as the activator of the                  carbon material, where the ratio of the zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate to the carbonized product is                  2-6 parts mass.

TI Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump system, has compressor connected with throttling device and evaporator, and gas cooler whose working medium channel is connected with throttling device and provided water inlet and water outlet.
AU YANG J
   LI X
   ZHANG Z
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201975965Q
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor connected with a throttling device and an evaporator. A gas cooling device comprises a first gas cooler and the second air cooler. A circulating working medium channel of the first gas cooler is connected with the compressor and provided with a first water inlet and a first water outlet. A circulating working medium channel of the second gas cooler is connected with the throttling device and provided a second water inlet and a second water outlet. The gas cooling device comprises a pre-heating gas cooler that is arranged between the second gas cooler and the throttling device.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides water heating segment for obtaining hot water with different temperatures, and satisfies different heat requirements, and has better circulating working substance produce cooling effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump system.

PN CN110040764-A; CN110040764-B
TI Preparing copper indium sulfur sulfide containing            sulfur defects, comprises reacting indium chloride            tetrahydrate, copper acetate monohydrate and            thioacetamide in organic solvent and calcining            meta-copper indium sulfur ultra-thin wafer.
AU SUN Y
   LI X
   XIE Y
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 201966378L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper indium sulfur sulfide                containing sulfur defects, comprises reacting                indium chloride tetrahydrate, copper acetate                monohydrate and thioacetamide in an organic solvent                to obtain ternary copper indium sulfur ultrathin                and rapidly calcining meta-CuIn5S8 ultra-thin                wafer.
   USE - The method is useful for visible light                catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide .
   ADVANTAGE - The copper indium sulfur sulfide has higher                visible light catalytic reduction carbon dioxide.                The method has 100% catalytic reduction to methane,                and rate of methane formation is 8.7 mu                mol/g/hour.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for:
   (1) photocatalytic reduction of carbgon                dioxide, comprising mixing sulfur-defective copper                indium sulfur sulfide with water to obtain a                dispersion, applying the dispersion to a surface of                the substrate, drying to obtain a reaction sample                under irradiation, reacting the carbon dioxide and                water are under the action of a reaction sample to                obtain methane, sulfur-deficient copper indium                sulfur sulfide in a sulfur-deficient copper indium                sulfur sulfide prepared by above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the                  copper acetate monohydrate and thioacetamide is                  50-100:20-60:50-100. Preferred Method: The                  reaction is carried out in the high pressure                  reaction kettle, the reaction temperature is                  120-200 degrees C and the time is 10-24 hours .                  The fast calcinations is performed in an                  argon-hydrogen atmosphere and the volume                  percentage of the hydrogen in the hydrogen-argon                  atmosphere is 5%. The temperature of the quick                  calcining is 400-450 degrees C and the time is 1-                  5 minutes .The method specifically comprises                  dissolving the indium chloride tetrahydrate and                  copper acetate monohydrate in ethylene glycol,                  adding thioacetamide to obtain a mixed solution,                  transferring the mixed solution to an autoclave,                  reacting to obtain a ternary copper indium sulfur                  ultrathin sheet.The preparaion of methane                  comprises placing the reaction sample in a closed                  reaction vessel , adding water to the bottom of                  the reaction vessel, ensuring the water is not in                  direct contact with the reaction sample, filling                  the reaction vessel with carbon dioxide, under                  visible light, reaction to obtain methane.The                  reaction time is 4-24 hour MPa and the                  temperature is 20-30 degrees C and The                  concentration of the dispersion liquid is 1                  mg/ml.

TI Purification of 10-deacetylbaccatin III, involves            dissolving crude 10-deacetylbaccatin III in mobile            phase comprising mixture of supercritical carbon            dioxide and highly-polar organic solvent, adsorbing on            stationary phase.
AU CHEN H
   LIU D
   SHEN J
   LI Z
AE JIANGSU HANBON SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201960513X
AB    NOVELTY - The method for purifying 10-deacetylbaccatin                III, involves purifying a product with a purity of                greater than or equal to 99% by supercritical fluid                chromatography using a chromatographic method. The                method involves (a) dissolving crude                10-deacetylbaccatin III in mobile phase, adsorbing                on the stationary phase, (b) sucking mobile phase                into column by an infusion pump for elution, (c)                collecting 10-deacetylbaccatin III. The mobile                phase is a mixture of supercritical carbon dioxide                and a highly-polar organic solvent. The volume of                supercritical carbon dioxide is more than 60 vol.%.                The stationary phase of the column is bonded silica                gel, and has particle diameter of 3-200 mu m, hole                diameter of 50-500 Angstrom and the shape is                amorphous or spherical.
   USE - Method for purifying 10-deacetylbaccatin                III.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables purification of                10-deacetylbaccatin III with yield of greater than                or equal to 99%, improved separation efficiency,                high product stability, low solvent consumption,                low post-processing energy consumption, improved                efficiency and low product cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The column is a series                  of two columns using two different types of                  stationary phases. The stationary phase has a                  particle diameter of 5-50 mu m, a pore diameter                  of 60-400 Angstrom and a spherical shape.                  Preferred Process: The stationary phase is a                  silica gel bonded to alkyl, phenyl, amino or diol                  group. Preferred Components: The alkyl group is                  butyl, octyl, octadecyl or tetracosyl. The                  highly-polar organic solvent is selected from                  1-4C alcohol and/or acetonitrile. The 1-4C                  alcohol is selected from methanol, ethanol or                  isopropanol. Preferred Composition: The                  supercritical carbon dioxide comprises 70-99.9                  vol.% mobile phase.

PN CN109354012-A; CN109354012-B
TI Preparing large-volume graphene useful in nanomaterial synthesis comprises melting base alloy (e.g. pure magnesium or magnesium zinc alloy), passing gas, stirring, solidifying, etching by dilute sulfuric acid, filtering and vacuum drying.
AU WANG X
   LI X
AE HARBIN INST TECHNOLOGY (HAIT-C)
GA 2019192099
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing large-volume graphene, comprises (a) adding a base alloy to a crucible to melt, adjusting the temperature of the molten alloy to 550-750 degrees C, (b) passing a gas at a flow rate of 300-10000 ml/minute into the molten alloy, continuous mechanical stirring during the process of gas introduction, and stirring at a speed of 500-5000 revolutions/minute, (c) solidifying the complex melt, etching the base alloy away by 5-20% dilute sulfuric acid, and filtering multiple times, removing impurities from the graphene solution and vacuum drying to obtain graphene powder, where the base alloy is pure magnesium, magnesium zinc alloy, magnesium calcium alloy or magnesium lithium alloy, the gas comprises carbon dioxide and nitrogen gas, carbon dioxide and a rare gas, or carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The graphene is useful in nanomaterial synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is economical; adopts magnesium thermal reduction method; produces high-quality graphene on a large scale.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The molten state alloy temperature is adjusted to 550-625 degrees C, preferably 610-750 degrees C. The method also comprises introducing a gas at a flow rate of 300-3000 ml/minute into the molten alloy, continuous mechanical stirring during the process of gas introduction at a stirring speed of 800-1500 revolutions/minute. The complex melt is solidified in a water tank. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the graphene and magnesium in base alloy is 1:4. The amount of zinc in the magnesium-zinc alloy is 6 wt.%, and the melting temperature is 720 degrees C. The high purity gas is carbon dioxide having a purity of 99.99999%.

   LIANG K
   GAO L
   LI L
   LI F
AE QINGDAO NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY IND (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201919190N
AB    NOVELTY - Low volatile organic compounds, low smell polystyrene composite material comprises 100 pts. wt. polystyrene resin, 5-50 pts. wt. modified molecular sieve, 1-3 pts. wt. lubricant, 1-3 pts. wt. toner, 1-3 pts. wt. ultraviolet absorbent, 1-3 pts. wt. compound antioxidant and 1-3 pts. wt. anti-mildew antibacterial agent, where modified molecular sieve is a 4A molecular sieve adsorbing carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as low volatile organic compounds, low smell polystyrene composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has high volatile organic compounds removal efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing low volatile organic compounds, low smell polystyrene composite material comprising mixing raw materials, adding into a twin-screw extruder, blending, granulating, extruding at 80-120 degrees C, melting at 140-200 degrees C, homogenizing at 200-220 degrees C, vacuum removing the azeotrope of volatile organic compounds and carbon dioxide, cooling to remove the azeotrope of volatile organic compounds and carbon dioxide and then granulating to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The particle size of the 4A molecular sieve is 0.5-3 mu m. The removal rate of 4A molecular sieve crystal water is 5-15%. The twin screw extruder includes a 12-segment heating zone. The feeding section is a first zone to a third zone. The melting section is a fourth zone to an eighth zone. The vacuum exhaust port is disposed in the tenth segment. Preferred Method: The preparation method of modified molecular sieve comprises adding 4A molecular sieve into the reaction kettle, heating at 50-200 degrees C to remove part of the crystal water, vacuumizing to remove volatile water, passing carbon dioxide and adsorbing carbon dioxide by 4A molecular sieve to obtain modified molecular sieve. The relative vacuum of the vacuum exhaust port is -0.06 MPa to -0.02 MPa.

TI Carbon dioxide absorbing-type air-conditioner, has evaporator for evaporating refrigerant, compressor for compressing refrigerant, and flow control unit for controlling heat exchange amount of refrigerant at intercooler.
AU PARK H
   KANG S
   JONG J H
   SAEM P H
   JIN K S
   JEONG H J
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 201903298K
AB    NOVELTY - The air-conditioner has a condenser for condensing refrigerant. An expansion device (151) passes the refrigerant when the condenser is throttled. An evaporator (120) evaporates the refrigerant passing through the expansion device. A compressor (110) compresses the refrigerant, which is discharged from the condenser to the evaporator. An intercooler (152) exchanges heat with the refrigerant. A flow control unit controls heat exchange amount of the refrigerant at the intercooler. The flow control unit is provided with a by-pass valve (153) to discharge the refrigerant.
   USE - Carbon dioxide absorbing-type air-conditioner.
   ADVANTAGE - The air-conditioner is environment-friendly. The intercooler exchanges heat with the refrigerant so as to secure cooling ability. The flow control unit controls the heat exchange amount of the refrigerant so as to maintain reliability of the compressor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide absorbing-type air-conditioner.
   Compressor (110)
   Evaporator (120)
   Expansion device (151)
   Intercooler (152)
   By-pass valve (153)

PN US2022306463-A1; WO2022213053-A1
TI Preparing hydrogen-rich product involves forming            mixture of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, water, methane,            carbon monoxide, nitrogen and argon, separating            effluent stream into hydrogen stream enriched in            hydrogen and hydrogen depleted tail gas stream,            separating formed cooled stream into product            stream.
AU KAYODE O
   CUELLAR K
   CADY W
   BIETTO S
   KRAUS K
   BENNETT E J
   RUSSELL B
   CRUZ A
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 2022C4472H
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen-enriched product and                recovering carbon dioxide involves: 1) processing                feed stream in hydrogen production process unit,                forming synthesis gas mixture containing hydrogen,                carbon dioxide, water and at least one selected                from methane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and argon;                2) separating effluent stream comprising synthesis                gas, in hydrogen pressure swing adsorption unit,                into first high-pressure hydrogen stream enriched                in hydrogen and hydrogen depleted tail gas stream                containing partly hydrogen, carbon dioxide, water,                and methane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and argon;                3) forming compressed tail gas stream; 4)                separating tail gas stream into CO2 -enriched                product stream and overhead stream; 5) separating                the overhead stream from CO2 recovery system,                forming second high-pressure hydrogen stream                enriched in hydrogen, and a low-pressure CO2                stream; 6) recovering second high-pressure hydrogen                stream.
   USE - Method is used for preparing hydrogen-enriched                product and recovering carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is an improved process which                enables hydrogen production, in a cost-effective                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of a hydrogen-enriched product and                recovering carbon dioxide involves: 1) processing a                feed stream containing hydrocarbons or a                carbonaceous feedstock, in a hydrogen production                process unit, to form a synthesis gas mixture                containing hydrogen, carbon dioxide, water and at                least one selected from methane, carbon monoxide,                nitrogen and argon; 2) separating an effluent                stream comprising the synthesis gas, from the                hydrogen production process unit, in a hydrogen                pressure swing adsorption (PSA) unit, into a first                high-pressure hydrogen stream enriched in hydrogen                and a hydrogen depleted tail gas stream containing                a portion of hydrogen, carbon dioxide, water, and                at least one of methane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen                and argon; 3) compressing the hydrogen depleted                tail gas stream in a compressor and forming a                compressed tail gas stream; 4) separating the                compressed tail gas stream from carbon dioxide                (CO2) recovery system, into a CO2 -enriched product                stream and an overhead stream containing portion of                hydrogen, portion of carbon dioxide and at least                one of methane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and                argon; 5) separating the overhead stream from CO2                recovery system in a PSA system that produces at                least two product streams into at least a second                high-pressure hydrogen stream enriched in hydrogen,                and a low-pressure CO2 stream enriched in carbon                dioxide; 6) recovering the second high-pressure                hydrogen stream, followed by optionally recycling                the low-pressure CO2 stream in compressor.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                apparatus used for preparation of hydrogen-enriched                product and recovering CO2, which comprises:
   1)) hydrogen production process unit                containing at least one inlet and at least one                outlet; 2) hydrogen PSA unit containing inlet,                hydrogen outlet and tail gas outlet, in fluid                communication with the hydrogen production process                unit outlet; 3) compressor containing inlet in                fluid communication with the hydrogen PSA tail gas                outlet, and outlet; 4) dryer which has inlet in                fluid communication with the compressor outlet, and                at least one outlet; 5) chiller which has a gas                inlet in fluid communication with the dryer outlet,                gas outlet, chilling fluid outlet and chiller fluid                inlet in fluid communication with a source of                chilling fluid; 6) CO2 recovery system having an                inlet, a first outlet, and an overhead outlet, CO2                recovery system inlet in fluid communication with                chiller gas outlet; and 7) PSA system having at                least an inlet, a high-pressure hydrogen outlet and                a low pressure CO2 outlet. The PSA system inlet is                in fluid communication with the CO2 recovery system                overhead outlet and the low pressure CO2 outlet is                in fluid communication with the compressor                inlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The PSA system producing                  at least two product streams, comprises a                  three-product PSA unit for separating the                  overhead stream from the CO2 recovery system                  by:a) passing the overhead stream into the                  three-product PSA unit having a three-product PSA                  cycle; b) removing the second high-pressure                  hydrogen stream during a high pressure,                  co-current adsorption step in the three-product                  PSA cycle, the second high pressure stream is                  substantially free of carbon dioxide, methane,                  carbon monoxide, nitrogen and argon; c) removing                  a second gas stream during a co-current                  depressurization step in the three-product PSA                  cycle, the second gas stream comprises at least                  one of methane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and                  argon; d) removing the low-pressure CO2 stream                  during counter-current depressurization step                  and/or counter-current purge step in the                  three-product PSA cycle; e) recovering the second                  high-pressure hydrogen stream; and f) optionally                  recycling the low-pressure CO2 stream to the                  compressor.The PSA system that produces at least two                  product streams comprises a three-product PSA                  unit having a three-product PSA cycle which                  comprises:i) a high pressure, co-current adsorption                  and hydrogen removal step; ii) at least one                  co-current depressurization step following the                  high pressure, co-current adsorption step and                  hydrogen removal step; iii) co-current                  depressurization and second gas removal step                  after the co-current depressurization step(s);                  iv) counter-current blowdown step and CO2 removal                  step, after the intermediate pressure co-current                  depressurization and second gas removal step; v)                  counter-current purge and CO2 removal step after                  the counter-current blowdown step; vi) at least                  one counter-current re-pressurization step after                  the counter-current purge and CO2 removal step;                  and vii) optionally co-current feed                  re-pressurization step after the counter-current                  re-pressurization step and/or a counter-current                  product re-pressurization after the                  counter-current re-pressurization step(s).Preferred Components: The PSA system that                  forms at least two product streams, comprises a                  second PSA unit.Preferred Method: 1) The step of separating                  the overhead stream from the CO2 recovery system                  involves passing the overhead stream into the                  second PSA unit, then separating the overhead                  stream into the low-pressure CO2 stream and                  second high-pressure hydrogen stream comprising                  more than 75% hydrogen and a portion of at least                  one of methane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and                  argon, and optionally recycling the low-pressure                  CO2 stream to the compressor.2) The PSA system further comprises a third                  PSA unit which separates the second high-pressure                  hydrogen stream in the third PSA unit, into a                  third high-pressure hydrogen stream and a second                  gas stream. The third high-pressure hydrogen                  stream is substantially free from carbon dioxide,                  methane, carbon monoxide, nitrogen and argon, and                  the second gas stream comprises methane, carbon                  monoxide, nitrogen the argon in the overhead                  stream. The third PSA system recovers the third                  high-pressure hydrogen stream.3) The CO2 recovery system enabling                  refrigerated CO2 fractionation process, provides                  refrigeration cooling by at least two                  refrigeration circuits. One of the refrigeration                  circuits utilizes a portion of the CO2 -enriched                  product stream recovered from a distillation                  column in the CO2 recovery system or a single                  closed loop multi-component mixed refrigerant                  circuit.4) The hydrogen-rich product preparation                  process further involves:a) oxidizing methane, carbon monoxide and                  any hydrogen present in the second gas stream,                  with oxygen contained in a catalytic oxidation                  unit, to form water, CO2 and heat, followed by                  recycling CO2 from the catalytic oxidation unit,                  to the compressor; b) selectively bypassing the                  compressor, CO2 recovery system and PSA system                  that produces at least two product streams,                  followed by sending the hydrogen depleted tail                  gas stream from the hydrogen PSA unit to a                  combustion unit in the hydrogen production                  process unit.5) The hydrogen production process involves                  using a water gas shift (WGS) unit producing a                  WGS effluent stream contained in the effluent                  stream from hydrogen production process unit. The                  process further involves heat exchanging the WGS                  effluent stream with a process stream and form a                  cooled effluent steam and a pre-heated process                  stream, followed by recovering waste heat from                  the cooled effluent stream to produce steam using                  a process involving reaction of reversible                  oligomerization of phosphoric acid.6) The process further involves: 1)                  recycling at least a portion of the second gas                  stream, to hydrogen production process unit; 2)                  recycling at least a portion of the second gas                  stream to a water gas shift process unit; and/or                  3) passing at least a portion of the second gas                  stream to a combustion unit.Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the                  second high-pressure hydrogen stream, is 1,000-                  6,000 kilopascals (kPa) and that of the                  low-pressure CO2 stream, is 100- 250 kPa. The                  pressure of the second gas stream, is 100- 450                  kPa. The dried tail gas stream is cooled to -20                  to - 50&#176;C.

TI Carbon dioxide generator for supplying liquefied            carbon dioxide to house during crop cultivation, has            mixer supply unit for supplying mixture of air and            fuel, and swirl forming unit providing circular flow            cross-section along direction.
AU JEONG P
AE JEONG P (JEON-Individual)
GA 2022B2244C
AB    NOVELTY - The generator has a mixer supply unit for                supplying a mixture of air and fuel. A combustion                unit burns the mixture supplied from the supply                unit. A swirl forming unit provides a circular flow                cross-section along a direction from the unit to                the combustion unit. The unit introduces a mixer                into the unit through a mixer inlet pipe that is                connected in a direction inscribed to a cross                section from a rear part of the unit. The unit                forms a swirl flow of the mixer. The mixer in the                pipe includes a straight pipe to introduce a linear                laminar flow mixer into a swirl forming part.
   USE - Carbon dioxide generator for supplying                liquefied carbon dioxide to a house during crop                cultivation.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixer supply unit supplies the mixture of                the air and the fuel so as to improve temperature                of the catalyst for maintaining stable combustion                state. The possibility of misfire is reduced. The                reliability of the start-up procedure is                improved.

PN CN113460963-A
TI Preparation of hydrogen by biogas comprises providing pre-treated crude biogass as a secondary energy source for economic development and ecological environment, providing first and second raw material gases, forming mixed gas.
AU BRIGLIA A
   PECHOVA P
   PAN X
   XU S
AE GEORGES CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV (GEOR-Non-standard)
GA 2021B8226T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen by biogas involves providing pre-treated crude biogas, providing the first raw material gas and the second raw material gas to at least portion of the crude biogas under the condition without pre-separating carbon dioxide, forming mixed gas, so that the volume ratio of the carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 30%. The mixed gas is subjected to steam reforming reaction in the presence of a catalyst to obtain synthesis gas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide, oxidized the carbon monoxide in the synthesis gas to carbon dioxide by the conversion reaction of carbon monoxide, correspondingly generated hydrogen, obtained substantially pure hydrogen product by pressure adjustment in an adsorption device, where the first raw material gas is steam and the second raw material gas is methane and/or hydrogen.
   USE - Method for preparing hydrogen by biogas used as a secondary energy source for economic development and ecological environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not only adjust the hydrogen-carbon ratio in the reaction process, but also improves the utilization rate of carbon dioxide in biogas, and reduces the carbon deposition of the catalyst in the carbon dioxide reforming process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pre-treated crude biogas comprises 30-60 wt.% methane and 30-60 wt.% carbon dioxide based on volume fraction. The second raw material gas is methane, until the volume ratio of the carbon dioxide to the total methane in the mixed gas is 1:2.3. The second raw material gas is methane, the volume ratio of the water vapour to the total methane in the mixed gas is between 1.4-5:1. The second raw material gas is hydrogen, the hydrogen in the mixed gas is 15-32 vol.% mixed gas. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of the carbon deposit balance constant K of the catalyst in the steam reforming reaction to the reaction entropy Q is 1. The steam reforming reaction is carried out in the reforming reactor, the reforming reactor is provided with a carbon forecasting and flow control module, the carbon forecasting and flow control module is pre-stored with each K and Q value in the reforming reactor in different working conditions, by monitoring the size of K/Q value and adjusting the steam reforming reaction. The second feed gas is derived from the substantially pure hydrogen product. The 10-30 vol.% substantially pure hydrogen product is used as the second raw material gas. The methane provided by the second raw material gas is from substantially pure natural gas or biomethane.

PN CN113369719-A
TI Laser punching for LED carrier plate by using            carbon dioxide laser to drill blind hole on hole center            position of to-be-drilled hole on printed circuit            board, where carbon dioxide laser spot diameter is            equal to to-be-drilled hole diameter.
AU LI Q
   LI Y
   HU Y
   HUANG J
AE HUIZHOU CHINA EAGLE ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOG (HUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2021A80445
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining printed circuit                board (PCB) data to be punched. A punching mode of                a PCB punching device is selected according to a                hole depth to be drilled. A to-be-drilled hole is                determined as a through hole if depth of the hole                to be drilled is equal to thickness of the PCB. A                carbon dioxide laser is utilized to drill a blind                hole on a hole center position of the to-be-drilled                hole on the PCB. Spot diameter of the carbon                dioxide laser irradiated on the PCB is equal to                hole diameter of the to-be-drilled hole.
   USE - The method is for laser punching for LED                carrier plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing drilling                operation of blind hole and through hole through                carbon dioxide laser so as to realize drilling of                multiple through holes in same process, reducing                multiple times of electroplating filling hole and                copper reduction process, simplifying drilling                process of the PCB board and reducing production                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for laser                punching device for LED support plate.

PT P
PN CN111798930-A; CN111798930-B
TI Carbon dioxide corrosion rate prediction method            considering influence of corrosion product film            comprises performing chemical balance calculation,            determining main electrochemical reaction, considering            reduction reaction and calculating.
AU JIANG H
   LIU Y
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2020A4304M
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide corrosion rate prediction                method considering influence of corrosion product                film comprises (i) performing chemical balance                calculation to the carbon dioxide solution system,                correcting the non-ideal property of the carbon                dioxide natural gas mixture by using coefficient                coefficient and using ion intensity and activity                coefficient to correct the non-ideal property of                the solution medium; (ii) determining the main                electrochemical reaction of metal surface and                deducing the Butler-Volmer equation for calculating                corrosion current density of electrochemical                reaction; (iii) considering hydrogen ion reduction                reaction controlling reduction of carbonic acid by                activation reaction and chemical reaction,                calculating the limit current density; and (iv)                calculating the total corrosion current density of                each electrochemical reaction, using the anode and                cathode reaction charge balance calculation to                obtain corrosion potential and using Faraday law to                obtain corrosion rate.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide corrosion rate                prediction method considering influence of                corrosion product film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method considers the situation that the                theoretical prediction value is high in the                presence of the corrosion product film; predicts                the result by drawing up the corrosion product film                factor correction; improves the accuracy of                corrosion prediction, and has good extrapolation                and flexibility.

PN CN111215149-A; CN111215149-B
TI Preparing MOF-POP-n composite catalyst used in            catalytic conversion of cyclopropane and carbon            dioxide, involves dissolving dibromobenzylbenzene in            toluene to obtain solution A, dissolving            1-vinylimidazole in toluene to obtain solution B.
AU HAN Z
   FANG T
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 2020520682
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing MOF-POP-n composite catalyst                involves dissolving dibromobenzylbenzene in toluene                to obtain solution A, and dissolving                1-vinylimidazole in toluene to obtain solution B,                mixing solution A and solution B uniformly, heating                the reaction under nitrogen atmosphere, filtering                after cooling, and washing to obtain the precursor.                The precursor, divinylbenzene and MOF shell                material are added to the N,N-dimethylformamide                solution containing azobisisobutyronitrile and                stirred ultrasonically for 15-45 minutes. The                reaction is heated under a nitrogen atmosphere,                cooled to room temperature and separated to obtain                a MOF-POP-n composite catalyst.
   USE - Method for preparing MOF-POP-n composite                catalyst used in the catalytic conversion of                cyclopropane and carbon dioxide to cyclic carbonate                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare MOF-POP-n                composite catalyst that has high production of                cyclic carbonate from propylene oxide and carbon                dioxide, and high conversion rate that is more than                90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for performing catalytic conversion of cyclopropane                and carbon dioxide to cyclic carbonate, which                involves adding propylene oxide and MOF-POP-n                composite catalyst to a high-pressure reaction                kettle and reacting for 12 hours at 1 mega pascal                in carbon dioxide atmosphere at 80&#176;                C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dibromobenzyl is                  o-dibromobenzyl, m-dibromobenzyl and                  p-dibromobenzyl. Preferred Conditions: The                  reaction is a reaction at 100-120&#176; C for                  12-24 hours. The ratio of divinylbenzene:                  precursor is 1:1 to 1:5. The ratio of propylene                  oxide and MOF-POP-n composite catalyst is                  1:2-2.5.

TI Method for processing green coffee bean involves            contacting carbon dioxide fluid to sample of coffee            green bean and then reducing the pressure allowing the            carbon dioxide fluid penetrated into the sample to            break down cell wall of sample.
AU HUANG Y
   CHIEN C
   JIAN Z
AE HUANG Y (HUAN-Individual)
   HUANG Y (HUAN-Individual)
   HUANG Y (HUAN-Individual)
GA 202023162M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting a carbon                dioxide fluid to a sample of coffee green bean                inside a chamber at a temperature of 28-50 degrees                C and a pressure of 950-3,500 pound per square inch                (psi) for 3-30 minutes to allow the carbon dioxide                fluid to penetrate into the sample of coffee green                bean and reducing the pressure in the chamber to                normal pressure in a time period of 2-15 minutes,                allowing the carbon dioxide fluid penetrated into                the sample of coffee green bean to break down cell                wall of the sample of coffee green bean to obtain a                sample of coffee green bean with broken cell                wall.
   USE - Method for processing green coffee bean.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process green coffee                bean for obtaining a processed green coffee bean                retaining a large amount of chlorogenic acid even                after the roasting process.    MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111020463-A
TI Surface treatment method of magnesium and its alloy based on gaseous carbon dioxide environment comprises e.g. forming high-quality magnesium carbonate corrosion resistant film on the surface of magnesium alloy.
AU CAO X
AE UNIV CHONGQING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYNQ-C)
GA 2020339118
AB    NOVELTY - Surface treatment method of magnesium and its alloy based on gaseous carbon dioxide environment comprises e.g. successively polishing magnesium and its alloys, rinsing with deionized water, washing with ethanol, drying, fixing with non-conductive polymer wire or rod, suspending in gas phase environment, closing pressure vessel, keep its space in a closed state, slowly increasing temperature, filling oxygen slowly into the pressure vessel, stop oxygenation, filling carbon dioxide gas into pressure vessel, raising temperature in the pressure vessel, slowly discharging the gas in the pressure vessel, recovering discharged gas and reused, when the pressure in the pressure vessel is the same as the outside pressure and the temperature reaches room temperature, removing the sample from the pressure vessel, washing sample with deionized water, blow drying, preparing high-quality magnesium carbonate or hydrated magnesium carbonate corrosion resistant film on the surface of magnesium alloy.
   USE - The method is useful for surface treatment method of magnesium and its alloy based on gaseous carbon dioxide environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy to control; ensures recycle of carbon dioxide waste gas and generated carbonic acid solution without involving any toxic substances; ensures uniform, dense and corrosion resistance protective film layer of magnesium carbonate and hydrated magnesium carbonate; repairs magnesium and its alloys with corrosion product films on the surface; does not involve any toxic substances; recycles carbon dioxide and generated carbonic acid; realizes environmentally friendly corrosion-resistant coatings, and if the coating is dissolved and destroyed, no toxic pollutants will remain in the air, soil and water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Surface treatment method of magnesium and its alloy based on gaseous carbon dioxide environment comprises (i) successively polishing magnesium and its alloys with #400-#2000 water-grinding abrasive paper in order, until the surface is smooth, (ii) rinsing magnesium and its alloy treated in step (i) with deionized water for 1-3 minutes, washing with ethanol for 1-5 minutes, (iii) quickly blow drying the magnesium and its alloy with cold compressed air, (iv) fixing magnesium and its alloy with non-conductive polymer wire or rod i.e. polytetrafluoroethylene, suspending in gas phase environment of a pressure vessel containing a certain volume of deionized water, i.e. gas phase space above the water line, (v) closing the pressure vessel, keep its space in a closed state, slowly increasing temperature to a state of 2-4 degrees C below the predetermined temperature, (vi) filling certain amount of oxygen slowly into the pressure vessel with the help of a booster device, (vii) stop oxygenation after the oxygen pressure reaches the predetermined partial pressure value, begin to slowly fill certain amount of carbon dioxide gas into the pressure vessel with the help of the booster device until the predetermined partial pressure of carbon dioxide, (viii) raising the temperature in the pressure vessel to the final predetermined temperature value, (ix) after the temperature and pressure reach the predetermined value, the carbon dioxide surface treatment starts timing until the predetermined time, (x) after the surface treatment reaches a predetermined time, slowly discharging the gas in the pressure vessel, recovering the discharged gas and reused by the subsequent carbon dioxide gas recovery device, (xi) when the pressure in the pressure vessel is the same as the outside pressure and the temperature reaches room temperature, removing the sample from the pressure vessel, and (xii) washing sample with deionized water for 1-3 minutes, and blow drying with cold compressed air, a high-quality magnesium carbonate or hydrated magnesium carbonate corrosion resistant film is prepared on the surface of the magnesium alloy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The deionized water has conductivity of 1-10 s/cm and a pH of 6-8. Preferred Method: The method further includes (xiii) in order to improve the corrosion resistance of the film, follow-up composite treatment is adopted. In the step (ii), the alkaline solution cleaning method is used, i.e. the washing with alkaline solution first for 1-3 minutes, then washing with deionized water, where alkaline solution consists of 10-20 g sodium hydroxide, 5-10 g anhydrous sodium carbonate, 10-20 g sodium silicate per liter of alkaline solution, and the balance is deionized water. The process factors to be controlled by the method include temperature is 20-50 degrees C, carbon dioxide partial pressure is 3-7 MPa, oxygen partial pressure is 0-1.5 MPa, the volume of deionized water is 1/100 to 1/5 of the volume of the enclosed space for processing the sample, and the time is controlled at 3-16 hours.
