GA 201942866M
AB    NOVELTY - Oxidizing hydrocarbon comprises mixing branched aromatic hydrocarbon compound and/or branched heterocyclic compound with gas containing oxygen and carbon dioxide, and carrying out oxidation under the action of catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for oxidizing hydrocarbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The branched aromatic hydrocarbon compound is a compound in which at least one hydrogens on the aromatic ring is substituted by at least one 1-12C alkyl groups, preferably toluene, paraxylene, orthoxylene, meta-xylene, trimethylbenzene, and pseudocumene, durene. The branched heterocyclic compound chain is a compound in which at least one hydrogen on the hetero ring which is substituted by at least one 1-12C alkyl group-substituted compound, and the heterocyclic compound is a compound in which at least one carbons in the cyclic carbon compound is substituted with oxygen, nitrogen or sulfur, preferably methylpyridine, lutidine or trimethylpyridine. The gas containing oxygen and carbon dioxide has a carbon dioxide/oxygen molar ratio of 10:1-0.5:1, preferably not greater than 8, or not less than 1 or 2. The supplemental gas used is nitrogen, helium or argon gas, and the molar ratio of the supplemental gas to carbon dioxide is 0:1-15:1. The catalyst comprises (I) molybdenum rare earth metal oxide complex (MoaRbMcOd), (II) silver vanadium molybdenum metal oxide complex (Aga1Vb1Moc1M1d1Oe1), (III) silver vanadium nickel metal oxide complex (Aga2Vb2Nic2M2d2Oe2), (IV) silver vanadium silicon metal oxide complex (Aga3Vb3Sic3M3d3Oe3), (V) tungsten oxide complex (Wa4Xb4Yc4Od4), and/or (VI) tungsten oxide complex (Wa5X'b5Y'c5Od5). Preferred Method: The branched aromatic hydrocarbon compound and/or branched heterocyclic compound are mixed with a gas containing oxygen and carbon dioxide, and the concentration of the branched aromatic hydrocarbon compound and/or branched heterocyclic compound in the gas is 10-110 gm-3. The gas outlet of the marsh gas cabinet and the air inlet of cooling dust remover are connected with inlet of the shunt room. The gas outlet of the cooling dust remover is connected with the methane producing device. The carbon dioxide conveys the split chamber and rises into the methane chamber. The gas discharge port of the Roots blower is connected to the intake port of the gas generator set. The gas generator set generates electricity. The electric energy is supplied to the oxygen generator. The oxygen generator realizes zero-consumption conventional energy.    USE - Method for desulfurizing and denitrating coal combustion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A coal combustion desulfurizing and denitrating method involves introducing coal, placing a burning coal on the coal feeder, adjusting the rotation speed of the coal feeder to adjust the coal feeding amount of the coal feeder, introducing the supplied coal into heating tank to heat-treat the burning coal, raising the temperature in the heating tank to 820-935 degrees C, introducing air into the heating tank, and the excess air coefficient is 0.8-1.2, introducing carbon dioxide mixed gas in the raw material pipe into dust removal tower, and initially removing the dust through the gray shield plate, and blocking the dust by the gray shield plate and entering to the dust collecting tank, passing through to first-stage dust removal chamber, introducing the carbon dioxide mixed gas into the dust-removing pipe, and discharging into the secondary dust-removing chamber after cleaning by the dust-removing filter cloth, passing the carbon dioxide mixed gas to an activated carbon layer, and filtering the activated carbon layer for three times and discharging, preparing ammonia mixture gas, introducing the ammonia solution into the ammonia removal pump and the ammonia solution ammonia storage tank through the ammonia removal pump for storage, utilizing the ammonia storage tank to slowly introduce the ammonia into the gas ammonia generator, heating the ammonia liquid and vaporizing, and introducing air into the intake pipe, and mixing the heated ammonia gas with the air, and discharging the mixed gas of the mixed ammonia gas and the air at a temperature of 300-400 degrees C, and the gas and air volume percentage concentration is not higher than 5%, and introducing the mixed gas of ammonia gas and air into the reaction furnace, and uniformly spraying from top to bottom through the ammonia spraying device, mixing the ammonia air mixture with the flue gas, mixing flue gas mixed with injected ammonia gas and rectifying by the rectifying mechanism, and mixing gas and reacting with the catalyst in the reaction tube to react the nitrogen oxide and ammonia in the flue gas under the catalytic action of the catalyst, introducing the carbon dioxide mixed gas into drying tower, and dehydrating the mixed gas and drying, and cooling, and discharging through the raw material tube to the next process, closing the on-off valve, and passing the dechlorinated carbon dioxide mixed gas into the washing tower, passing the carbon dioxide mixed gas into the alkali liquid pool through the lye tube, the height of the alkali liquid pool is higher than the depth of the alkali liquid pipe, heating the heating device in the lye bath to fully react, preliminary washing flue gas, again circulating the preliminary washed flue gas in the lye pool through the circulation reactor, opening the on-off valve, and discharging the mixed flue gas after multiple washings through the discharge pipe, passing the washed mixed flue gas into the flue gas heat exchanger for temperature rise and discharging.

TI Automatically detecting pH value during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis comprises sampling algae liquid during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis, detecting pH value and controlling access of carbon dioxide traffic and time.
AU LI L
   ZHONG L
   BAI L
AE YUNNAN BOXIN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 201917720G
AB    NOVELTY - Automatically detecting pH value during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis comprises automatically sampling algae liquid during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis in real time, automatically detecting pH value and automatically controlling access of carbon dioxide traffic and time including e.g. periodically collecting automatic detection and control system of PH value of culture liquid into pH detection pool through non-contact sampling device and discharging culture liquid back into photobioreactor pipeline in time after detection, using pure water cleaning sampling pipeline and pH detection pool, determining system pH value of the test, automatically calculates volume of carbon dioxide to be added and controlling flow and time of carbon dioxide automatic valve to ensure accurate insertion into place, adjusting first addition of carbon dioxide and extracting adjusted culture solution for testing to determine whether pH is adjusted of the system will again.
   USE - The method is useful for automatically detecting pH value during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Automatically detecting pH value during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis comprises automatically sampling algae liquid during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis in real time, automatically detecting pH value and automatically controlling the access of carbon dioxide traffic and time including (i) periodically collecting automatic detection and control system of PH value of culture liquid into pH detection pool through non-contact sampling device and discharging the culture liquid back into the photobioreactor pipeline in time after the detection; (ii) using pure water cleaning sampling pipeline and pH detection pool; (iii) determining system pH value of the test, automatically calculates the volume of carbon dioxide to be added and controlling the flow and time of carbon dioxide automatic valve to ensure accurate insertion into place; (iv) adjusting the first addition of carbon dioxide, extracting the adjusted culture solution for testing to determine whether the pH is adjusted of the system will again, if it is not precisely in place, it will be fine-tuned again until the pH of the culture solution is accurately controlled within the scope of the setting; (v) displaying the system data and status on office computer through server, the operator can remotely view and control the pH online detection instrument through data acquisition and processing system and can remotely send the instruction to check measurement accuracy of instrument and working state of each component; (vi) starting PH value automatic detection and control system, judging the water sample of Haematococcus pluvialis photobioreactor in real time and allowing to stand the system if the water sample is abnormal; (vii) starting the sampling pump and water sample is normal and judging the water level of PH detection pool and if the water level is insufficient to soak it to the pH probe it will be re-sampled; (viii) making the water level of PH detection pool to normal, stopping sampling pump, synchronously detecting the pH automatic detector and calculating the amount of carbon dioxide; (ix) ensuring the precise addition of the position by controlling the flow and time of the carbon dioxide automatic valve; (x) extracting the adjusted culture solution using system for testing to determine whether the pH is adjusted after the first addition of carbon dioxide is adjusted, if it is not precisely in place, then it will be fine-tuned again until the pH of culture solution is accurately controlled within the scope of the setting; (xi) storing the detected data in real time through data collection device and transmitting to the central control room computer through server; and (xii) maintaining the standby state after the system detects one cycle after waiting for about 10 minutes and the system starts the next detection control cycle again. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for automatic pH detecting device for detecting pH value during cultivation of Haematococcus pluvialis comprising automatic pH detection control system and automatic pH detecting device, the pH automatic detection control system comprises DOS system, operating station and online analytical instrument comprise software control system, a server and data acquisition device in the DOS system. Each component being connected by data line signal. The operator station includes engineering station and operator station.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The DCS system further comprises field control unit, data acquisition device, an analysis instrument, and a server, remote control unit and DCS software control system are connected by relevant data signal lines. PN JP2022027365-A
TI Producing acetic acid and formic acid by creating mixed sol that contains dispersant with organic solid solution binder using electron acceptor slurry made of ruthenium seed dye, ethyl viologen dichloride and artificial acetic acid bacterium enzyme, and reacting carbon dioxide emulsion micelles.
AU YAMAGUCHI Y
AE YAMAGUCHI Y (YAMA-Individual)
   YAMAGUCHI M (YAMA-Individual)
GA 202251627T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing acetic acid (ethane acid) and formic acid (methane acid), involves creating a mixed sol that collectively contains each dispersant with an organic solid solution binder using an electron acceptor slurry made of a ruthenium seed dye, ethyl viologen dichloride (C12H14CL2N2), artificial acetic acid bacterium enzyme, and tungsten oxide fine particles, coating the mixed sol on the substrate, embedding the carbon dioxide in the reaction active plant, using a visible light source from a solar daylight transmission device, and photocatalytically reacting the carbon dioxide emulsion micelles.
   USE - The method is used for producing acetic acid (ethane acid) and formic acid (methane acid). The produced acetic acid and formic acid is used for converting into ethanol, methanol, or hydrogen by re-decomposition, where the converted hydrogen and oxygen is used for generating electricity supplied to lighting and power energy. The acetic acid is used for supply of industrial materials such as foods, daily miscellaneous goods, cosmetics, chemicals, dyes, industrial detergents, sterilizing and disinfecting agents, used for basic materials for industrial products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces acetic acid and formic acid from carbon dioxide by utilizing a photocatalytic reaction of tungsten oxide semiconductor fine particles. By the action of the artificial acetic acid bacterium (genus Acetobacter), the method efficiently converts the dehydration of alcohol groups and carboxylic acid groups, and possesses a rule function for formic acid production and acetic acid production, and a control function for production. Carbon dioxide is used as a recycled material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The average particle size of the tungsten oxide fine particles exhibiting the secondary order reaction is 20-50 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process of converting carbon dioxide to produce acetic acid and formic acid, the step of controlling the production increase/decrease of both acetic acid and formic acid. Preferred Conditions: The average particle size of the tungsten oxide fine particles of the secondary reaction order species with light is 40 nm. In the method, regarding a solid solution in which the mass ratio of the tungsten oxide fine particles (the former) to the artificial acetic acid bacterium enzyme, ruthenium seed dye, and ethyl viologen dichloride (the latter) is 1 in mass with respect to 10 in mass in the former. TI Method for extracting liquid such as volatile oil            of agarwood, involves separating agilawood essential            oil with large solubility and ethanol in separator with            carbon dioxide fluid through pressure reducing            valve.

AE HAINAN CANGXINGE AGILAWOOD CO LTD (HAIN-Non-standard)
GA 2022077482
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves placing the agarwood                medicinal material into a traditional Chinese                medicine pulverizer. The agarwood medicinal                material is pulverized into agarwood raw material                powder with an average particle size of 50 meshes.                The 5 wt.% ethanol of the raw material powder is                used as an entrainer to mix with the raw material                powder to obtain a raw material solution. The raw                material solution is introduced into the extraction                kettle, and the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                is input into the extraction kettle. The oleoresin                and fatty oil with low solubility are precipitated                at the bottom of the rectification column. The                agilawood essential oil with large solubility and                the ethanol are separated in the separator with the                carbon dioxide fluid through the pressure reducing                valve.
   USE - Method for extracting liquid such as volatile                oil of agarwood such as resin-containing wood of                Aquilaria sinensis (Lour) Gilg of Rhizoma family                used as common clinical medicine for reducing air                temperature, warming kidney and mainly treating                chest, abdominal distensible pain, stomach cold                vomiting hiccup, kidney deficiency and asthma                acute, used in technical field of agarwood                processing.
   ADVANTAGE - The ethanol is selected as the entrainer for                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of agarwood                essential oil, and the supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction technology and rectification technology                are combined to effectively improve the solubility                and dissolution range of the extraction solvent.                The extraction rate of agarwood essential oil is                increased, and the obtained agarwood essential oil                has a uniform and clear color and a strong                smell.

PN WO2021256506-A1
TI Measuring sucrase activity of sucrase-isomaltase            complex used for diagnosing sucrase-related disease, by            using exhaled breath of subject to which 13C-labeled            sucrose is orally administered as test sample, and            calculating ratio of 13-carbon dioxide amount to            unlabeled carbon dioxide amount.
AU INADA M
   KAWATA K
   INOUE N
   KOGA D
AE OTSUKA PHARM CO LTD (SAKA-C)
GA 2021E80767
AB    NOVELTY - Method for measuring sucrase activity of                sucrase-isomaltase complex involves using exhaled                breath discharged from the subject to which                13C-labeled sucrose is orally administered as test                sample, calculating ratio of the amount of                13-carbon dioxide to the amount of unlabeled carbon                dioxide or the total amount of carbon dioxide in                the test sample as the measured value of sucrase                activity, where 13C-labeled sucrose, at least one                carbon atom of the fructose moiety is 13C-labeled,                and the carbon atom of the glucose moiety is not                13C-labeled. The test sample is taken at a time                determined based on a period of time when the                proportions are comparable in a healthy population                and a population with reduced glucose                metabolism.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring sucrase                activity of sucrase-isomaltase complex in subject                having a metabolic disease and diagnosing                sucrase-related diseases. The metabolic diseases                are sucrase or isomaltase deficiency, irritable                bowel syndrome, mucosal disorders caused by                infection or drug-induced mucosal disorders. The                sucrase-related disease is sucrase-isomaltase                deficiency, irritable bowel syndrome, mucosal                disorder caused by infection or drug-induced                mucosal disorder. The sucrase-related disease is                sucrase or isomaltase deficiency (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides information for diagnosis                of sucrase-related diseases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a composition for measuring the sucrase                activity of a sucrase-isomaltase complex comprising                at least one carbon atom of the fructose moiety 13C                labeled and a carbon atom of the glucose moiety 13C                unlabeled and 13C labeled sucrose;
   a method for diagnosing sucrase-related                diseases; and
   a composition for diagnosing sucrase-related                diseases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The subject has or is                  suspected of having a metabolic disease. The test                  sample is taken at a time determined based on the                  time point at which the proportion is maximal in                  a healthy population. The test sample is                  collected at any time from the administration of                  13C-labeled sucrose to 10 minutes after the time                  when the ratio became maximum in the healthy                  population. The test sample is collected in the                  vicinity of the time when the ratio is maximum in                  a healthy population. The exhaled breath                  discharged from the subject at multiple time                  points is used as the test sample, the ratio is                  calculated for each test sample, and the peak                  value of the ratio is used as the measured value                  of sucrase activity. The test sample is collected                  at any time from the administration of                  13C-labeled sucrose to 20 minutes after                  administration. The 13C-labeled sucrose is one in                  which all carbon atoms in the fructose moiety are                  13C-labeled.

PN CN113604833-A
TI Preparation method of carbon quantum dot catalyst applied to electro-reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare methane, involves uniformly coating catalyst ink on carrier material, and drying and combining carbon quantum dot powder particles on surface of carrier to obtain catalyst material.
AU GUO H
   WANG L
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 2021C9314K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using 0.01-1.0 g of uric acid compound as a carbon source, add 10-50 mL of solvent, and ultrasonically disperse and dissolve for 0.5-2 hours to obtain a dispersion. The obtained initial product mixture is cooled to room temperature and filtered with a 0.22 mum filter membrane to obtain a solution of carbon quantum dots. 1-5 mg of the carbon quantum dot powder is weighed, mixed with 5-250muL of Nafion solution with a concentration of not less than 5% by mass, 200-500muL of isopropanol and 500-700muL of ultrapure water, and ultrasonic mixing treatment is performed at room temperature for 0.5-3h. The catalyst ink is obtained, and the catalyst ink is uniformly coated on the carrier material, and dried, so that the carbon quantum dot powder particles are combined on the surface of the carrier to obtain the catalyst material.
   USE - Preparation method of carbon quantum dot catalyst (claimed) applied to electro-reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The synthesized carbon quantum dot has high efficiency selectivity and high Faraday efficiency for preparing methane. The raw material of the invention is cheap and easy to obtain; the preparation technique is simple; the cost is low; it can efficiently catalyze electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to methane; can effectively realize resource utilization of carbon-dioxide; and has good application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon quantum dot catalyst applied to electro-reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare methane.

The upper end of the connecting rod is connected with bottle cap. The lower end of the connecting rod is connected with plastic film between acid tank and alkali tank, and the connecting rod is also connected with plastic film at the lower end of filling tank. The filler in the bin is a desiccant, preferably foam or wire mesh.

PN EP3744700-A1; WO2020239683-A1; AU2020281669-A1; CA3139016-A1; CN113840813-A; US2022212992-A1; EP3744700-B1; ES2926719-T3
TI Manufacturing supplementary cementitious material            and sequestering carbon dioxide by grinding concrete            fines obtained from crushed concrete demolition waste            and circulating in fluidized bed reactor in contact            with carbonating atmosphere.
AU SKOCEK J
   ZAJAC M
   BEN HAHA M
   FEDERHEN S
   MAJCHROWICZ M
   BEN H M
AE HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
   HEIDELBERGCEMENT AG (HEID-Non-standard)
   HCONNECT 2 GMBH (HCON-Non-standard)
GA 2020B9572D
AB    NOVELTY - A supplementary cementitious material is                manufactured, and carbon dioxide (CO2) is                sequestered by grinding concrete fines obtained                from crushed concrete demolition waste in a mill at                1-10 degrees C above the water dew point in a                carbonating atmosphere provided by a gas containing                10-99 vol.% CO2, circulating ground and partially                carbonated concrete fines in a fluidized bed                reactor in contact with carbonating atmosphere, and                withdrawing decarbonated gas and carbonated                concrete fines.
   USE - Manufacture of supplementary cementitious                material and sequestering carbon dioxide (CO2) by                carbonating concrete fines.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and device can carbonate the                recycled concrete fine (RCF) rapidly and adjust                conditions so that very reactive supplementary                cementitious material (SCM) is obtained. The                indirect carbonation using gas concentrated in CO2,                enriched with it or even liquefied CO2, allows even                faster carbonation and manufacturing of SCM.                Although concentration and liquefaction of carbon                dioxide consume energy and need additional                equipment, the device allows more flexibility in                plant design. Only the carbon dioxide capture needs                to be installed at the point of exhaust gas                generation, the device can be installed centrally                and have a bigger scale. Its operation is                independent of that of the exhaust gas generating                plant. In the event of a heat deficiency in the gas                cycle after heating up, the burner off-gas can at                least partially be guided to the mill. However,                usually the grinding heat, the crushing heat, and                the heat developed during gas conveying is                sufficient to drive the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) device for sequestering CO2 and                carbonation of concrete fines; and
   (2) use of carbonated concrete fines as                supplementary cementitious material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: Carbonating atmosphere                  contains 10-30 vol.% CO2 or 50-90 vol.% CO2.                  Carbonating atmosphere is an exhaust gas,                  preferably selected from coal fired power plant,                  cement plant, or cement plant operating in the                  oxyfuel mode, or gas concentrated in CO2.                  Preferred Composition: At least 80 wt.%,                  preferably greater than or equal to 95 wt.%, of                  the concrete fines are carbonated.

PN CN111916789-A
TI Coupled power generation system for molten carbonate fuel cell and biomass gasification, has fuel cell stack whose anode is connected to carbon dioxide capture device and cathode is connected to combustion chamber and heat exchanger.
AU HUANG Y
   ZHU L
   WANG J
   XU Y
   WANG T
   HAN S
AE HARBIN BOILER FACTORY CO LTD (HRBE-C)
GA 2020B3985B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an electrostatic cyclone separator (5) that is connected to a naphthalene washing tower (6). The naphthalene washing tower is connected with a temperature swing adsorption device (7). The temperature swing adsorption device is connected with a pressure swing adsorption device (8). The pressure swing adsorption device is connected to a desulfurization tower (9). The desulfurization tower is connected to a hydrogen dryer (10). The hydrogen dryer is connected to an anode of a fuel cell stack (13). The anode of the fuel cell stack is connected to a carbon dioxide capture device (15). The cathode is connected to combustion chamber (17), a heat exchanger (12) and a carbon dioxide storage tank (14) respectively. The direct current generated by fuel cell stack is connected to direct current/alternate current (DC/AC) converter (18), and the alternating current of the DC/AC converter is connected to power grid (19).
   USE - Coupled power generation system for molten carbonate fuel cell and biomass gasification in thermal power generation industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses biomass gasification to produce hydrogen, which reduces the consumption rate of non-renewable energy and realizes carbon-free power generation.

TI Carbon dioxide cascade heating system has low-temperature compressor and high-temperature compressor which are provided as inverter compressors, and water pump which is provided as inverter water pump.
AU PAN H
   TANG X
   XIONG D
   YOU J
   KANG Q
   SONG X
AE JIANGSU SUJING PURIFICATION GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   SUZHOU SUJING AIMFAR AIR CONDITIONING CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
   JIANGSU SUJING GROUP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202032254B
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a low-temperature grade circuit, high-temperature grade circuit and heating circuit. The high-temperature stage circuit is provided with an intermediate heat exchanger (4) evaporation tube, a high-temperature stage compressor (7), a condenser tube (8), and a high-temperature stage expansion valve (9), which are sequentially arranged in series in the loop. The heating circuit is provided with a condenser evaporating tube, a user-side heat exchange component and water pump (10) which are sequentially arranged in series in the loop. The intermediate heat exchanger condensation tube and the intermediate heat exchanger evaporation tube are arranged to exchange heat with each other. The condenser tube and the condenser evaporator tube are arranged to exchange heat with each other. Both low-temperature compressor (3) and the high-temperature compressor is provided as an inverter compressors, and the water pump is provided as an inverter water pump.
   USE - Carbon dioxide cascade heating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is provided with the characteristics of high efficiency, energy saving and environmental protection. The control method adopted is provided to reasonably adjust operating parameters and operation in the most efficient state is ensured according to the size of the user heat load demand. The functions of saving energy and improving heating comfort are achieved, and the defects of the existing carbon dioxide cascade heating system are improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control method of carbon dioxide cascade heating system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide cascade heating system.

PT P
PN KR1961313-B1
TI Producing carbon dioxide-generating nonwoven fabric sheet used for mask pack set comprises e.g. coating acidic hydrogel composition containing acidic solvent and hydrogel aqueous solution on nonwoven fabric, drying, aging and perforating.
AU HO G I
   IL P J
   JU J H
AE SKINTREE CO LTD (SKIN-Non-standard)
GA 201929153K
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon dioxide-generating nonwoven fabric sheet comprises (a) producing an acidic hydrogel composition containing acidic solvent and hydrogel aqueous solution, (b) coating the acidic hydrogel composition on nonwoven fabric, (c) drying the nonwoven fabric coated with the acidic hydrogel composition, (d) aging the nonwoven fabric coated with the acidic hydrogel composition at 80-90 degrees C for 24-48 hours in an aging chamber, and (e) perforating the aged nonwoven fabric coated with the acidic hydrogel composition into form capable of attaching to the face. The hydrogel aqueous solution is prepared by adding 0.5-5.9 pts. wt. hydrogel raw material and 0.1-11.8 pts. wt. rice bran powder in 100 pts. wt. warm water and stirring at 50-80 degrees C.
   USE - The carbon dioxide-generating nonwoven fabric sheet is useful for mask pack set (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide-generating nonwoven fabric sheet simultaneously removes dead skin cells and waste materials of skin and supplies nutrients. The mask pack set acts directly on skin to remove dead skin cells and other wastes, improves skin care effects e.g. exfoliation and pore cleaning, enables nutrition and whitening of skin by acting on active components e.g. vitamin C, B, and tocopherol, can supply skin care and nutrition at the same time with one mask pack, and prevents falling out of carbonate powder during storage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for mask pack set comprising carbon dioxide-generating nonwoven fabric sheet, and carbonate composition containing 8-33 pts. wt. hydrogel aqueous solution and 100 pts. wt. carbonate powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate powder includes potassium carbonate, calcium carbonate, sodium carbonate, ammonium carbonate, sodium hydrogen carbonate, potassium hydrogen carbonate, potassium sesquicarbonate, calcium sesquicarbonate, and/or sodium sesquicarbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acidic solvent includes citric acid, formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid, butyric acid, valeric acid, oxalic acid, malonic acid, succinic acid, adipic acid, pimelic acid, fumaric acid, lactic acid, phthalic acid, isophthalic acid, terephthalic acid, glutamic acid, aspartic acid, malic acid, tartaric acid, phosphoric acid, potassium dihydrogen phosphate, sodium dihydrogen phosphate, sodium hexametaphosphate, acidic potassium hexametaphosphate, sodium pyrophosphate, sodium pyrophosphate, gallic acid, and/or salicylic acid. The hydrogel raw material includes cellulose gum, carrageenan extract, seaweed extract, mushroom extract, and/or kelp extract. The acidic hydrogel composition comprises 100 pts. wt. acidic solvent and 5-10 pts. wt. hydrogel aqueous solution. The mask pack set is prepared by reacting acid of carbon dioxide-generating nonwoven fabric sheet with carbonate powder of the carbonate composition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: The step (b) comprises (b1) filling the prepared acidic hydrogel composition in a deposition chamber, (b2) immersing the acidic hydrogel composition on the surface of the cylindrical copper plate in a sedimentation tank while rotating a roller with the cylindrical copper plate having the engraved pattern formed in predetermined shape, (b3) removing the acidic hydrogel composition deposited on the surface of the copper plate by rotating the blade adjacent to the copper plate rotated using the roller, not the engraved pattern, and (b4) immersing an aqueous solution of acidic hydrogel contained in the engraved pattern of the cylindrical copper plate on the nonwoven fabric. The step (c) comprises (c1) drying the non-woven fabric coated with the acidic hydrogel composition in a far infrared ray drying chamber at 150-180 degrees C and rate of 15-20 m/min to dry 85-95% water, and (c2) allowing the dried nonwoven fabric coated with the acidic hydrogel composition to pass through a heated press roller to absorb the solution remaining on the surface of the nonwoven fabric sheet deeply between the nonwoven fabric fabrics. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-generating nonwoven fabric sheet has thickness of 0.3-0.5 mm.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Refrigerator has clean water discharge line that is set to connect water tank and dispensation space, and carbonated water regulator that is adapted to maintain discharge pressure of carbonated water at predetermined pressure or below.
AU JEON H R
   LEE S T
   KYONG Y S
   HWANG K H
AE SAMSUNG ELECTRONICS CO LTD (SMSU-C)
GA 201903487S
AB    NOVELTY - The refrigerator comprises a storage chamber having an opened front side that is defined in a main portion. A door (21) is provided to open or close opened front side of storage chamber. A water tank is provided to store clean water. A carbonated water production module is mounted to a back surface of the door and comprising a carbon dioxide gas cylinder (120) is stored with carbon dioxide gas. A carbonated water tank (110) is adapted to produce carbonated water through mixing of the clean water with the carbon dioxide gas. A dispenser (90) comprising a dispensation space is formed at a front surface of the door. A clean water discharge line is provided to connect the water tank and the dispensation space without passing through the carbonated water tank so as to retrieve the clean water in the dispensation space. A carbonated water regulator is adapted to maintain a discharge pressure of the carbonated water at a predetermined pressure or below.
   USE - Refrigerator.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the first cover is maintained in a closed state, the cold air in the upper accommodation space from being outwardly discharged during replacement of the carbon dioxide gas cylinder is prevented. The ability to retrieve water or ice stored in the refrigerator from the outside of the refrigerator without opening the refrigerating compartment door is achieved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbonated discharge line is provided to connect the carbonated water tank and the dispensation space so as to retrieve the carbonated water in the dispensation space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective view illustrating an assembled structure of a carbonated water production module in the refrigerator.
   Door (21)
   Dispenser (90)
   Carbonated water tank (110)
   Carbon dioxide gas cylinder (120)
   Valve assembly (130)

AE XUZHOU CHILDRENS HOSPITAL (XUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A54277
AB    NOVELTY - The catheter has a carbon dioxide collecting pipe (1) is mounted at a side away from the partitioning mechanism (3) and is provided with a standard interface (6) of the carbon dioxide collecting pipe. An oxygen inlet joint (7) is mounted at one end of the oxygen absorbing tube (2) away from the partitioning mechanism. The partition mechanism includes a connection block. The connecting block is symmetrically mounted with two connecting channels. The two connecting channels are horizontally arranged. The openings of the two connecting passages are respectively connected to the carbon dioxide collecting pipe and the oxygen collecting pipe. One end of the connecting channel runs through the access end. A threaded hole is defined in a top surface of the other end of the connecting passage. The top of the connecting block is symmetrically mounted with two nasal tubes. The bottom of the nasal tube extends through the connecting block and is threadedly coupled to the threaded bore.
   USE - Oxygen inhalation and expiratory sampling duplex-cavity nasal catheter used in clinic.
   ADVANTAGE - The catheter provides simple structure and convenient use, and the separating mechanism can ensure that the device collects the carbon dioxide gas exhaled from the nasal cavity of the patient while performing oxygen absorption, thereby saving the workflow. The clamping mechanism enables the two conduits to clamp differently sized heads so that they are not easily detached.

AU ZHANG H

AE NINGBO SUNS CELL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018918889
AB    NOVELTY - The system has shaking control unit (6) which controls the shaking device to start or stop according to the parameter information. The shaking device drives an airtight box carrying a gas permeable cell culture bag for eccentric rotation. The gas control unit (5) controls the concentration of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas entering the closed box according to the parameter information and adjusts the concentration in real time according to the feedback data of the carbon dioxide gas concentration sensor. The closed box is provided with a gas input interface for mixed gas input and a gas output interface for excess mixed gas output. The gas input interface is connected to the HEPA filter. The gas output interface is connected with a gas shutoff valve, when the gas pressure of the mixed gas in the closed box reaches a limit value. The main control unit (1) controls the gas shut-off valve to open until the gas shut-off valve is closed when the gas pressure falls within the limit value.
   USE - Cell culture system for culturing cells in carbon dioxide incubator.
   ADVANTAGE - The stable carbon dioxide concentration environment is provided for the gas permeable cell culture bag, and also effectively avoid heat loss and prevent large fluctuations in the temperature inside the gas permeable cell culture bag.

GA 2018714075
AB    NOVELTY - The portable unit (10) has a device that closes the test subject's nose and allows the test subject to only breathe through the mouth. A first bi-directional air flow path (16L) within the first pipe comprises inhalation air flow towards the mouth of the test subject and exhalation air flow from the mouth of the test subject. A second and third bi-directional air flow path (16R) is within the first and second manifold portions. An oxygen sensor subsystem (70) is arranged in the first manifold portion. An airflow rate sensor (22) is arranged in the second manifold portion. A computer (30) is connected to the carbon dioxide sensor subsystem (50), the oxygen sensor subsystem, and the airflow rate sensor for receiving data signals from the carbon dioxide sensor subsystem, the oxygen sensor subsystem, and the airflow rate flow sensor and for digitizing the data signals and wirelessly conveying the digitized data to another computer which stores the digitized data for display and analysis.
   USE - Portable unit for metabolic analysis of test subject.
   ADVANTAGE - The close-to-the-mouth feature minimizes or eliminates the timing issues present in other portable and fixed commercial units. The pressure within the manifold flow channels varies minimally during inhalation and exhalation, therefore, the pressure transducer needs only to provide a measure of the ambient air pressure. The LEDs are pulsed at about 5 to about 15 Hz and preferably at about 10 Hz and at a low duty cycle so as to minimize the amount of heat that is conveyed to the detector.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front isometric view of a portable unit.

PN JP2020105150-A
TI Manufacture of 1-methoxy-2-propanol involves using high pressure fluid comprising supercritical fluid containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and mixing composite catalyst comprising solid catalysts containing different metal species.
AU KAWANAMI H
   MAYA C
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 202062308P
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of 1-methoxy-2-propanol involves using a high pressure fluid comprising supercritical fluid containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen as a raw material, and physically mixing composite catalyst comprising two or more types of solid catalysts containing different metal species of the metal microparticles to be supported.
   USE - Manufacture of 1-methoxy-2-propanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces 1-methoxy-2-propanol from stable carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The total pressure of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 1 MPa or more, preferably 10 MPa or more or a critical pressure. Preferred Components: The metal species of the metal fine particles supported by the solid catalyst comprise iron, ruthenium and osmium of group 8 in the periodic table, cobalt, rhodium and iridium of group 9 in the periodic table, nickel, palladium and platinum of group 10 in the periodic table, copper, silver and gold of group 11 in the periodic table, and zinc, cadmium and mercury of group 12 in the periodic table. The composite catalyst comprises a solid catalyst supported ruthenium fine particles, solid catalyst supported iridium fine particles, ruthenium nanoparticles-supporting mesoporous silica and iridium nanoparticles-supporting mesoporous silica. The support of the solid catalyst is at least one chosen from carbon, manganese oxide, alumina, silica, titania, magnesia, zirconia, and ceria.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The method is carried out at a reaction temperature of 25-250 degrees C, preferably 100 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN108753845-A
TI Producing methane from carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from conventional energy combustion and carbon dioxide extracted from air comprises e.g. mixing methane strain, nitrogen fertilizer, phosphate and water to form sludge water.
AU GAO J
AE GAO J (GAOJ-Individual)
GA 201888533F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methane from carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from conventional energy combustion and carbon dioxide extracted from air comprises e.g. mixing methane strain, nitrogen fertilizer, phosphate and water, placing in methanogen poisoning treatment center, mixing to form sludge water, sending sludge water to diversion chamber of the methane-producing device to rise to the methane chamber, entering in separation chamber, overflowing sludge water from separation chamber to the methane bacteria poisoning treatment center, adopting zero emissions, forming electrolyzed water through the water electrolyzer, sending electrolyzed water to water tank, flowing into cathode chamber, the anode chamber, water tank and anode chamber, forming water pressure between the cathode chambers, smoothly flowing electrolyzed water in water tank to cathode chamber and the anode chamber, flowing through the holes in anode plate, holes in the cathode plate, and electrolytic diaphragm into the methane chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methane from carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from conventional energy combustion and carbon dioxide extracted from air.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes zero-emission of conventional energy combustion equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing methane from carbon dioxide in exhaust gas from conventional energy combustion and carbon dioxide extracted from air comprises (i) mixing methane strain, nitrogen fertilizer, phosphate and water, placing in methanogen poisoning treatment center, mixing to form sludge water, sending sludge water to diversion chamber of the methane-producing device to rise to the methane chamber, entering in separation chamber, overflowing sludge water from separation chamber to the methane bacteria poisoning treatment center, and adopting zero emissions, (ii) forming electrolyzed water through the water electrolyzer, sending electrolyzed water to water tank, flowing into cathode chamber, the anode chamber, water tank and anode chamber, forming water pressure between the cathode chambers, smoothly flowing electrolyzed water in the water tank to cathode chamber and the anode chamber, flowing through the holes in the anode plate, holes in the cathode plate, and electrolytic diaphragm into the methane chamber, (iii) turning on power of anode plate and the cathode plate, generating hydrogen ions by anode plate, generating hydrogen ions form hydrogen ion water in the anode chamber, entering hydrogen ionized water in methanation chamber through the electrolytic diaphragm under the action of water pressure, transferring hydrogen ions to the cathode plate under the attraction of the cathode plate, passing hydrogen ions through the methanogenesis chamber during the transfer from the anode chamber to the cathode chamber, (iv) injecting carbon dioxide into the splitter chamber by pressure and rises into the methanogenesis chamber, where carbon dioxide and hydrogen ions meet methanogens in the methanogenesis chamber, synthesizing methane under the action of methanogens, releasing oxygen, and methane, entering in separation chamber, and discharging methane, and oxygen from gas discharge port of the separation chamber, An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for methane producing system comprising a methanogenic device (1), a gas generator set, and a methane bacteria poisoning treatment center (2). The methanogenesis chamber is set between anode plate and cathode plate. The sludge water discharge port of the methanogen poisoning treatment rehabilitation center is connected to the sludge inlet of the distribution chamber. The sludge water discharge port of the methanogen poisoning treatment rehabilitation center is connected to the sludge inlet of the distribution chamber. The sludge water rises into the methane chamber in the diversion chamber. The sludge water continues to rise into the separation chamber. The separation chamber is provided with a sludge water discharge port. The sludge water discharge port is connected to the sludge inlet of the methane bacteria poisoning treatment center. The sludge water recycling uses zero discharge. The methane bacteria poisoning treatment center is set with a biogas outlet. The biogas outlet is connected to the inlet of the conventional energy combustion device. The carbon dioxide in the high-temperature exhaust gas discharged from the conventional energy combustion device is piped to the cooling dust remover. The cooling dust collector is provided with a carbon dioxide discharge port. The carbon dioxide discharge port is connected with the intake port of the flow dividing chamber. The carbon dioxide extracted from the air can also be sent directly to the split chamber. The carbon dioxide rises in the diversion chamber and enters the methane chamber. Part of the carbon dioxide continues to rise into the separation chamber and discharged from the gas discharge port of the separation chamber. The gas barrier is provided inside the gas discharge port. The water pipe is connected with a water electrolyzer inlet. The electrolysis water discharge port of the water electrolyzer is connected to the water inlet of the water tank. The electrolysis water discharge port of the water tank, anode chamber, and inlet of the cathode chamber is connected to the electrolysis port. The terminal of the gas generator set is connected to the terminal of the AC/DC transformer, positive and negative terminals of AC/DC transformer and the terminal of the anode plate. The gas discharge port is connected with the intake port of the conventional energy combustion device. The gas discharge port is connected with the intake port of the gas generator. The gas generator set generates power for anode plate, and cathode plate. The conventional energy combustion equipment, exhaust gas discharge port, and gas generator set are connected with the air inlet of the cooling dust remover. The carbon dioxide discharge port of the cooling dust remover is connected with the intake port of the splitter chamber and the exhaust gas rises in the split flow chamber.

PN CN108179999-A
TI Comparing carbon dioxide-foam drive displacement stage comprises e. g. simulating actual situation of the mine, determining degree of recovery in the water-flooding stage and determining water drive deadline node for the indoor experiment.
AU LIU L
   PI Y
   HE Y
   WANG L
   FAN J
   LIU J
   LI J
AE UNIV NORTHEAST PETROLEUM (UYPT-C)
GA 2018503303
AB    NOVELTY - Comparing carbon dioxide-foam drive displacement stage comprises simulating actual situation of the mine, determining the degree of recovery in the water-flooding stage according to the water-flood recovery rate before the carbon dioxide-foam flooding in the mine, determining the water drive deadline node for the indoor experiment, conducting comparative experiment with carbon dioxide-foam flooding, controlling the degree of error in the stage of production during the water flooding phase within plus minus 0.01%, making the water flooding in the laboratory, saturating the remaining oil before the start of the carbon dioxide-foam drive phase remains the same, carrying out comparative experiment of carbon dioxide-foam flooding, increasing the degree of recovery according to the carbon dioxide-foam flooding stage in each comparative experiment and comparing using carbon dioxide-foam drive parameter or optimal carbon dioxide-foam drive solution.
   USE - The method is useful for comparing carbon dioxide-foam drive displacement stage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide-foam drive displacement stage comparing device, comprising artificial cores, high pressure displacement pump, steel pipeline, six-way, water injection piston container, carbon dioxide piston container, injection foaming piston container, upper valve, lower valve, pressure gauge, control valve, core holder, ordinary cable and insulated cable. The device also includes a foaming unit, automatic metering device, thermostat, agitator and computer, high pressure observation window, back pressure valve, gas mass flow controller and gas meter. The foaming device has an inlet end and an outlet end and an observation window. A nozzle and a suction speed setter are built in the foaming unit. The high pressure displacement pump is connected through a steel pipe line and a six-way connection. The six-way is connected to the lower valve of the water injection piston container and the carbon dioxide piston container through the steel pipe line. The upper part of the water injection piston container is connected by a steel pipe line and a six-way connection. The upper part of the carbon dioxide piston container is connected to the gas mass flow controller. The gas mass flow controller is connected by a steel line and a foaming unit. The high pressure displacement pump is connected through the steel pipe line to the lower valve of the injection foam piston container. The upper part of the injection foam piston container is connected by a steel pipe line and a foaming unit. The lower part of the foaming unit is connected by a steel pipe line and a six-way pipe, and the pressure gauge is connected to the six-way. The six-way connection is made through the steel pipe line and the control valve on the left side of the core holder. The control valve on the right side of the core holder is connected by a steel pipe line and a high-pressure observation window. The high pressure observation window is connected by a steel line and a back pressure valve. The back pressure valve is connected by a steel pipe line and an automatic metering device. One end of the automatic metering device is connected by a steel pipe line and a gas meter. The other end of the automatic metering device is connected to the computer via an insulated cable. The computer is connected to the high pressure displacement pump by a common cable. The high-pressure displacement pump supplies power to the entire displacement device and the rated working pressure is less than or equal to 50 MPa. The six-way provides multiple paths for the device. The water injection piston container, the carbon dioxide injection piston container and the injection foaming liquid piston container are containers for injecting water, injecting carbon dioxide, and injecting a foaming liquid and at maximum pressure of 50 MPa. The gas mass flow controller is used for controlling gas flow rate, with a pressure resistance of 40 MPa. The flow range is 0-50 ml/minute, and the flow rate is consistent with the high pressure displacement pump displacement speed. A nozzle and an agitator disposed in the foaming unit for sufficiently bringing the blowing agent into contact with carbon dioxide to generate foam. A suction pump is built in the foaming unit for discharging the foam into the subsequent steel pipeline. The pumping speed is consistent with the displacement of the displacement pump. The pressure gauge is used to record the injection pressure of the liquid. The artificial core is a model close to the physical properties of the mine block. The rated working pressure of the high-pressure observation window is less than or equal to 50 MPa, and is used for observing the phase state of the fluid at the production end. The back pressure valve is used for controlling the pressure at the production end to ensure the pressure in the entire injection device is stable. The automatic metering device undertakes the production liquid, can display the current oil quantity and the liquid quantity, and transmits the above value to the computer. The gas meter is used to measure the volume of the produced gas. The computer can calculate the amount of oil produced when the degree of recovery in the water flooding stage reaches a certain value by the saturated oil quantity of the artificial core under the control of the built-in computer program when the calculated oil production amount is the same as the current oil quantity collected in the automatic metering device. The computer will send a command to stop the injection of liquid to the high-pressure displacement pump through the ordinary cable. The computer calculates the amount of oil produced, the degree of production in the actual water flooding stage is obtained. The allowable error range is between this value and the stage production degree when the water content of the mine reaches 98% between plus minus 0.01%. The incubator keeps the entire experimental process at the formation temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises (i) determining the dynamic mining characteristics of this mine block based on the mine block simulated, determining the degree of recovery in the water flooding stage according to the dynamic mining feature, representing the value of the degree of recovery in the water flooding stage as A, determining by the degree of recovery in the water flooding stage when the water content of the mine water flooding reaches 98% and determining the geological characteristics of the mine block, where the geological characteristics of the mine block include porosity, permeability, block size distribution, adhesive content and formation fracture pressure; (ii) preparing artificial core according to the geological characteristics of the mine block; (iii) determining the carbon dioxide parameters and the type of blowing agent according to the mine situation and the development of carbon dioxide flooding in similar blocks and determining the carbon dioxide parameters and the type of blowing agent; (iv) evacuating multiple cores from the cores in the second step, and the number of the cores is B sequentially and evacuating saturated water and saturated oil; (v) determining the purity of the carbon dioxide used in the experiment and the injection pressure according to the carbon dioxide parameters determined in the third step, adding the carbon dioxide into the carbon dioxide piston container, formulating the foaming agent into foaming liquid and filling into the foaming liquid piston container, injecting water, and adding into the water injection piston container; (vi) connecting the displacement experimental device and starting the experiment; (vii) sending the water of the B-core separately into the water-discharging stage C when the water content of the mine water-flooding stage reaches 98%, stopping water flooding phase when the C value is the same as the production A value in the water flooding stage or the error range is between plus minus 0.01%; (viii) replacing the carbon dioxide-foam flooding stage according to the carbon dioxide-foam flooding design scheme, and designing the displacement to the design; and (ix) comparing the increase in the degree of recovery of the carbon dioxide-foam drive stage in the comparison experiment and comparing using carbon dioxide-foam drive parameter or an optimal carbon dioxide-foam drive solution.

PN CN113856766-A
TI Preparation of copper Schiff base chelate intercalated zinc-chromium hydrotalcite for degradation of methylene blue, involves dissolving copper Schiff base chelate in distilled water, dripping solution containing zinc nitrate and chromium nitrate, and reacting with hexamethylenetetramine.
AU JIN Z
   MENG Y
AE ZHEJIANG YUTU TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022146903
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of copper Schiff base chelate intercalated zinc-chromium hydrotalcite involves dissolving p-aminobenzoic acid in absolute ethanol, dripping salicylaldehyde under stirring condition, reacting for 3-4 hours, cooling, suction filtering, washing, drying to obtain Schiff base, dissolving the Schiff base in absolute ethanol, adjusting pH to obtain reaction solution, dissolving copper acetate hydrate in distilled water, dripping in reaction solution, adding to hydrothermal reactor, hydrothermally reacting, suction filtering, washing, drying to obtain copper Schiff base chelate, dissolving the copper Schiff base chelate in carbon dioxide-free distilled water, stirring, dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and chromium nitrate nonahydrate in carbon dioxide-free distilled water to obtain solution (A), dissolving hexamethylenetetramine in carbon dioxide-free distilled water, dripping the solution (A), stirring, hydrothermally reacting, suction filtering, washing, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of copper Schiff base chelate intercalated zinc-chromium hydrotalcite as photocatalyst for degradation of methylene blue (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method adopts one-step hydrothermal method to form hydrotalcite structure and introduce the copper Schiff base chelate into the interlayer of the hydrotalcite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of copper Schiff base chelate intercalated zinc-chromium hydrotalcite involves dissolving p-aminobenzoic acid in absolute ethanol, slowly dripping salicylaldehyde under stirring condition, reacting for 3-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, suction filtering, washing with absolute ethanol, drying at 60-80degrees Celsius for 10-16 hours to obtain Schiff base, dissolving the Schiff base in absolute ethanol, adjusting pH to 7.0-8.0 with 0.1-0.2 mol/L sodium hydroxide-ethanol solution to obtain reaction solution, dissolving copper acetate hydrate in distilled water, dripping in reaction solution, adding to hydrothermal reactor, hydrothermally reacting at 100-110degrees Celsius for 2-3 hours, cooling to room temperature, suction filtering, washing with absolute ethanol, drying at 60-80degrees Celsius for 12-18 hours to obtain copper Schiff base chelate, dissolving the copper Schiff base chelate in carbon dioxide-free distilled water, stirring, dissolving zinc nitrate hexahydrate and chromium nitrate nonahydrate in carbon dioxide-free distilled water to obtain solution (A), dissolving hexamethylenetetramine in carbon dioxide-free distilled water, dripping the solution (A), stirring for 0.5-1 hour, adding to hydrothermal reactor, hydrothermally reacting at 100-120degrees Celsius for 14-16 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, suction filtering, washing with distilled water and dehydrated alcohol to remove carbon dioxide, and drying at 60-80degrees Celsius for 12-20 hours.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the copper Schiff base chelate intercalated zinc-chromium hydrotalcite as photocatalyst in solution containing methylene blue for degradation under irradiation of 300 W xenon light source at room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of p-aminobenzoic acid dissolved in ethanol is 1-2 mL/mmol based on the amount of p-aminobenzoic acid. The concentration of Schiff base dissolved in absolute ethanol is 10-15 mL/mmol based on amount of the Schiff base. The ratio of copper acetate and schiff base is 1:2. The concentration of copper acetate hydrate dissolved in distilled water is 2-5 mL/mmol, copper Schiff base chelate compound dissolved in carbon dioxide-free distilled water is 4-8 mL/mmol, zinc nitrate hexahydrate and chromium nitrate nonahydrate dissolved in carbon dioxide-free distilled water is 1.5-3 mL/mmol, and hexamethylenetetramine dissolved in carbon dioxide-free distilled water is 4-6 mL/mmol. The ratio of zinc nitrate hexahydrate and chromium nitrate nonahydrate is 2-4:1. The ratio of hexamethylenetetramine and chromium nitrate nonahydrate is 0.5-1:1. The ratio of copper Schiff base chelate and chromium nitrate nonahydrate is 0.5-1:1. The concentration of solution containing methylene blue is 15 mg/L. The amount of copper Schiff base chelate intercalated zinc-chromium hydrotalcite photocatalyst used is 0.8-1.4 g/L.

PN CN113526537-A
TI Preparing light calcium carbonate from limestone for use in plastics, involves pouring block limestone into crusher for crushing, taking out limestone powder from crusher, and pouring limestone powder into forging furnace and calcining.
AU ZHAO X
AE GONGXIAN XUYANG CALCIUM IND CO LTD (GONG-Non-standard)
GA 2021C2610P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing light calcium carbonate from limestone involves taking multiple block limestone, pouring block limestone into crusher for crushing, and taking out limestone powder from crusher. The limestone powder is poured into forging furnace and calcinated for a period of time at high temperature, and taken out to obtain quicklime. The quicklime is poured into container, a certain amount of water is poured, stirred for a certain period of time with stirrer, until turbid liquid is obtained, and glass container is used to take out portion of turbid liquid and reserved.
   USE - Method for preparing light calcium carbonate from limestone for use in the fields of plastic, rubber, adhesive, coating, cosmetic, textile and sewage purification.
   ADVANTAGE - The process enables to avoid large amount of manpower to process the carbon dioxide, and increase yield of the calcium carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing light calcium carbonate from limestone involves taking multiple block limestone, pouring block limestone into crusher for crushing, and taking out limestone powder from crusher. The limestone powder is poured into forging furnace and calcinated for a period of time at high temperature, and taken out to obtain quicklime. The quicklime is poured into container, a certain amount of water is poured, stirred for a certain period of time with stirrer, until turbid liquid is obtained, and glass container is used to take out portion of turbid liquid and reserved. The prepared sodium carbonate solution of certain concentration is poured into turbid liquid, reaction is stirred by stirrer, and allowed to stand for a period of time for precipitation of particles produced by reaction. A sealed container is used to collect carbon dioxide gas produced by reaction of sodium carbonate solution and turbid liquid, and carbon dioxide gas is repeatedly flushed with clean water to remove dust in carbon dioxide gas to obtain pure carbon dioxide gas. The white precipitate produced by reaction between sodium carbonate solution and turbid liquid is taken out through filter, and dried with dryer. The water in white precipitate is removed, and white particles are taken out and ground into powder to obtain calcium carbonate. The collected pure carbon dioxide gas is collected into container, clean water is added, booster pump is connected to container through pipe, air pump is started to pressurize container, and carbon dioxide is dissolved in water under high pressure for carbonation. The carbonated water is poured into reserved turbid liquid container, stirred for a period of time with stirrer, sealing and pressurization treatment is performed to make container react under high pressure, and allowed to stand for a period of time for the sediment inside the container to settle sufficiently, container is opened, and filtered and dried.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The conduit used is made of silica gel. Preferred Conditions: The particles size of limestone after crushing is less than 0.05-0.1 mm. The ratio of quicklime to clear water is 1:8. The number of times of cleaning collected carbon dioxide with clean water is 3-5 times.The gas pressure during carbonation is 0.23- 0.25 megapascal (MPa).

TI Machine room communication data monitoring system,            has heat dissipation module configured to control and            adjust air quantity of radiating device of machine            room, and carbon dioxide monitoring module configured            to monitor change of carbon dioxide concentration in            machine room.
AU XU M
   XU H
   WAN J
AE SHENZHEN MEKELA NETWORK TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021C01421
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a machine room center                monitoring server provided with a power supply                device to continuously supply power. An emergency                fire-fighting module is connected with a safety                lamp and a fire extinguisher to spray fire when                fire is detected in a machine room. A storage                module is configured to store real-time monitoring                data in the machine room. An alarm module is                configured for triggering an alarm bell to alarm                when the machine room is out of fire. A                heat dissipation module is configured to control                and adjust air quantity of a radiating device of                the machine room. A carbon dioxide monitoring                module is configured to monitor change of carbon                dioxide concentration in the machine room. A                concentration feedback module is configured to                detect the change of the carbon dioxide                concentration in different directions.

PN US2019105594-A1; US11229871-B2
TI Gas capture and conversion system comprises first            gas feed, second gas feed and adsorption column that is            fluidly coupled to first gas feed and to second gas            feed, where adsorption column comprises adsorbent.
AU HASAN M M F
   IYER S S
   BALASUBRAMANIAN P
   BAJAJ I
   ARORA A
AE UNIV TEXAS A & M SYSTEM (UTAM-C)
   UNIV TEXAS A & M SYSTEM (UTAM-C)
GA 201932189R
AB    NOVELTY - Gas capture and conversion system comprises a                first gas feed, a second gas feed and an adsorption                column that is fluidly coupled to the first gas                feed and to the second gas feed, where the                adsorption column comprises an adsorbent having an                affinity for first component of the first gas. A                vent is provided for fluidly coupling the                adsorption column to the atmosphere, where the vent                exhausts second component of the first gas. The                second gas is supplied to the adsorption column,                which increases a partial pressure of the second                gas and results in a reduction of a partial                pressure of the first component of the first gas                causing the first component of the first gas being                desorbed from the adsorbent.
   USE - Gas capture and conversion system.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas capture and conversion system enables                to efficiently capture and convert a gas in a                simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a method for capturing and converting a gas,                which involves supplying a first gas to an                adsorption column, adsorbing a first component of                the first gas into an adsorbent contained in the                adsorption column responsive to the adsorbent                having an increased affinity for the first                component of the gas, venting a second component of                the first gas out of the adsorption column,                supplying a second gas to the adsorption column so                as to increase a partial pressure of the second gas                and decrease the partial pressure of the first                component of the first gas within the adsorption                column, desorbing the first component of the first                gas from the adsorbent responsive to the increased                partial pressure of the second gas and decreased                partial pressure of the first component of the                first gas, and transmitting a mixture of the first                component of the first gas and the second gas to a                reactor via a reactor line; and
   a method for capturing and converting carbon,                which involves supplying flue gas to an adsorption                column, adsorbing carbon dioxide from the flue gas                into an adsorbent contained in the adsorption                column responsive to the adsorbent having an                increased affinity for the carbon dioxide, venting                nitrogen out of the adsorption column, supplying                methane to the adsorption column so as to increase                a partial pressure of the methane inside the                adsorption column and decrease a partial pressure                of the carbon dioxide, desorbing the carbon dioxide                from the adsorbent responsive to the increased                partial pressure of the methane and a reduced                partial pressure of carbon dioxide, and                transmitting a mixture of the carbon dioxide and                the methane to a reactor via a reactor line.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                concentration driving force for adsorption and                desorption of carbon dioxide from multi-component                mixture using feed switching.

AB    NOVELTY - Processing high-hardness water hardening comprises e.g. providing tangential mixer, self-circulation lifting groove, calcium salt crystal settling tank, calcium salt crystal slurry collecting tank, filter device and using carbon dioxide, sodium hydroxide solution, flocculating and so one process and hard to soft recovery comprising adding high-hard water, sodium hydroxide solution, calcium salt crystal slurry collection tank reflux nucleation liquid and filtering and pressing mother liquid to enter the tangential direction of the tangential mixer wall, when the material enters the tangential mixer, then automatically mixing by the self-rotating method, and entering the uniformly mixed material to the self-circulating lifting tank from the bottom by using the difference, increasing the liquid from the outlet line of the tangential mixer by the lifting mixing device, and mixing with the carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide line and flocculating agent.
   USE - The method is useful for processing high-hardness water hardening.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing high-hardness water hardening comprises providing tangential mixer, self-circulation lifting groove, calcium salt crystal settling tank, calcium salt crystal slurry collecting tank, filter device and using carbon dioxide, sodium hydroxide solution, flocculating and so one process and hard to soft recovery comprising (i) adding high-hard water, sodium hydroxide solution, calcium salt crystal slurry collection tank reflux nucleation liquid and filtering and pressing mother liquid to enter the tangential direction of the tangential mixer wall, when the material enters the tangential mixer, then automatically mixing by the self-rotating method, and entering the uniformly mixed material to the self-circulating lifting tank from the bottom by using the difference; (ii) increasing the liquid from the outlet line of the tangential mixer by the lifting mixing device, and mixing with the carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide line and flocculating agent from flocculating agent line to form a calcium-containing salt liquid; (iii) adding calcium-containing salt liquid from the self-circulating lifting tank overflows from the overflow line from the top of the circulating lifting tank to the calcium salt crystal precipitation tank, and the crystal nucleus of the liquid tangent mixer feed line from the calcium salt crystal sedimentation tank under the action of flocculant, gradually growing into settleable particles and entering the calcium salt slurry collection tank; and (iv) sending the calcium salt crystal slurry from calcium salt crystal slurry collection tank to the filter press by the screw pump, taking the dry material after the pressure filtration is calcium salt, and feeding the mother liquid through the mother liquor tangential mixer of the filter press, returning pipeline to the tangential mixer and recycling to the system, transferring the soft water to the post-process via the soft water line.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The calcium salt slurry collection tank reflux liquid tangential mixer feed line flow rate is 0.1-0.15 times of the flow rate of the high-hard water tangential mixer feed line, the concentration of sodium hydroxide solution is 10-30 wt.%, where the self-circulating lifting tank increasing the stirring speed to 40-80 revolutions/minute, preferably 55-65 revolutions/minute, and the pH of the overflow line at the top of the self-circulating lifting tank is controlled at 11.5-12, and the flocculant concentration is 10-50 mg/l, preferably 20-40 mg/l, the flocculant flow rate is 0.5-3 m3/hour, preferably 1.5-2.5 m3/hour, the calcium salt crystal sedimentation tank equipment rotation speed is 5-20 rpm, preferably 10-15 rpm. the high hard water tangential mixer feed pipeline mixer with tangential wall upper welding points distance cutting mixer on the most end 100- 500 mm, preferably 300-400 mm, with tangential mixer wall tangent angle is 20-40 degrees , preferably 25-35 degrees , the sodium hydroxide solution tangential mixer feed pipeline mixer with tangential wall welding point of the top tangent distance mixer uppermost end 400-800 mm, preferably 600-700 mm, with tangential mixer wall tangent included angle is 20-40 degrees , preferably 25-35 degrees , the calcium salt crystal slurry collecting tank reflux liquid tangential mixer feed pipeline mixer with tangential wall welding point of the top tangent distance mixer top end 700-1100 mm, preferably 900-1000 mm, and the tangential mixer wall tangent included angle is 20-40 degrees , preferably 25-35 degrees , filter mother liquid tangential mixer feed pipeline mixer with tangential wall upper welding points distance cutting mixer on the most end 1000- 1400 mm, preferably 1200-1300 mm, with tangential mixer wall tangent included angle is 20-40 degrees , preferably 25-35 degrees .
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The line mixer equipped with a high hard water tangential mixer tangential mixer feed pipeline, the sodium hydroxide solution feed pipeline, the calcium salt crystal slurry collecting tank reflux liquid tangential mixer feed lines, filter mother liquor to tangential mixer feeding pipeline, the tangential mixer outlet pipeline with self-circulation lifting groove is connected with the lower part, a self-circulation lifting groove top of the overflow pipeline and the calcium salt crystal settling tank upper part; the calcium salt crystal settling tank lower part is calcium salt crystal slurry collecting groove; calcium salt crystal slurry collecting groove is provided with a calcium salt crystal slurry outlet pipeline, the calcium salt crystal slurry outlet pipeline respectively collecting reflux liquid tangent groove mixer feeding pipeline connected with the filter and the calcium salt crystal slurry, the filter is set with the filter mother liquor recycling pipeline and the soft water pipeline, filter mother liquor recycle pipeline and filter mother liquor to tangential mixer feeding pipeline, and water pipeline is connected to post-process.
