AE NAT CENT NANOSCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CHINA (NANI-C)
GA 2021C0323Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous polymer film involves taking linear polymer containing carbazolyl as raw material, performing in-situ chemical reaction. The in-situ chemical reaction is oxidative coupling reaction or Friedel-Crafts reaction.
   USE - Method for preparing porous polymer film used in absorbing and separating carbon dioxide, absorbing and separating organic pollutant, water-proof coating and organic solvent nanofiltration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare porous polymer film in a simple, efficient and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method involves dissolving the linear polymer containing carbazolyl in an organic solvent to obtain a polymer solution, after the organic solvent is volatilized, obtaining polymer film, carrying out oxidative coupling reaction or Friedel-Crafts reaction to the polymer film. The Friedel-Crafts reaction is carried out under the action of catalyst and cross-linking agent; said catalyst is Lewis acid, preferably the catalyst is one of ferric chloride, aluminium chloride, tin chloride; more preferably, the mol ratio of the carbazole structure unit in the linear polymer and the catalyst is 1.5 to 3.0; the cross-linking agent is an alkylating agent; preferably the crosslinking agent is one of dimethyl acetal, 1, 4-dichloro-methyl benzene, 1, 3, 5-bromine methyl benzene; more preferably, the molar ratio of the carbazole structure unit in the linear polymer and the crosslinking agent is 1.0 to 3.0. Preferred Components: The linear polymer containing carbazolyl is selected from one or more of poly (N-vinyl carbazole), poly (N-vinyl carbazole) - polystyrene block copolymer, poly (3-vinyl carbazole). and/or, the organic solvent is selected from anhydrous chloroform, tetrahydrofuran, methylene dichloride, toluene in the one kind of or multiple.

GA 202142466G
AB    NOVELTY - Fluid comprises underivatized guar or guar gum derivative selected from carboxyalkyl guars, hydroxyalkylated guars, modified hydroxyalkylated guars and mixtures, crosslinking agent, carbon dioxide as foaming gas and urea.
   USE - Fluid for treating wellbore.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluid improves thermal stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of fracturing subterranean formation penetrated by well, which involves pumping fluid into well under pressure and creating or enlarging fracture in the formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The foaming gas further comprises nitrogen. The fluid further comprises non-gaseous foaming agent. The non-gaseous foaming agent is selected from sulfate ethers, alkyl ether sulfates, ethoxylated ether sulfates, ammonium ether sulfates, phosphate esters, alkyl ether phosphates, ethoxylated alcohol phosphate esters, alkyl sulfates, alpha olefin sulfonates, alkyl quaternary ammonium salts, alkyl benzyl quaternary ammonium salts, alkyl amido amine quaternary ammonium salts and mixtures. The bifunctional organic compound is choline chloride. The hydroxyalkyl ammonium salt is bis(hydroxyethyl)dimethyl ammonium chloride. The bifunctional organic compound is hydroxyalkyl ammonium salt of formula (I). R=ethyl, butyl, hexyl, dodecyl, octadecyl; and#n=1-3. The formation temperature in the well is greater than or equal to 250?oF.

PN CN111187244-A; CN111187244-B
TI Extracting apigenin from celery comprises using celery/celery leaves as raw materials, firstly supercritical carbon dioxide fluid main solvent, using ethanol as co-solvent and prepared liquid phase chromatographic separation.
AU JI Z
   CHEN F
   WANG G
   WANG X
   SONG X
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 2020486824
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting apigenin from celery comprises using celery/celery leaves as raw materials, firstly supercritical carbon dioxide fluid the main solvent, using ethanol as a co-solvent for co-extraction and then prepared liquid phase chromatographic separation of apigenin.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting apigenin from celery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is safe; produces non-toxic products; is suitable for the production of food and medicine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ethanol is ethanol with a volume fraction of 95%. Preferred Method: The extraction apigenin from celery comprises (a) drying and crushing the raw materials and passing 40 mesh sieve to obtain celery powder, (b) taking the above celery powder X1 g and placing supercritical extraction kettle at 25-40 degrees C under extraction pressure 25-40 MPa for 2.5 hours, (c) collecting the extraction liquid from the separation tank of the supercritical extraction kettle and evaporating to no alcohol taste to obtain the concentrated extraction liquid, (d) using preparative liquid chromatography to separate, purifying and collecting the concentrated extract and (e) freeze-drying the collected material.

PT P
PN US2019322803-A1
TI Preparing poly(limonene)dicarbonate comprises reacting poly(limonene)carbonate with epoxidation agent to form poly(limonene-8,9-oxide)carbonate (PLOC) and reacting the PLOC with carbon dioxide to obtain poly(limonene)dicarbonate.
AU KLEIJ A
   CRISTOFOL M A
   KINDERMANN N
AE FUNDACIO INST CATALA DINVESTIGACIO QUIMI (CATA-Non-standard)
   ICREA CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AVANCAT (ICRE-Non-standard)
GA 201988228U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing poly(limonene)dicarbonate (PLDC) comprises: (a) reacting poly(limonene)carbonate (PLC) with an epoxidation agent to form poly(limonene-8,9-oxide)carbonate (PLOC) and (b) reacting the PLOC obtained in step (a) with carbon dioxide to obtain PLDC.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing poly(limonene)dicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides PLDC with high glass transition temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the PLDC obtainable by the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The step (b) is carried out in the presence of a halide salt as a catalyst. The epoxidation agent of step (a) is meta-chloroperoxybenzoic acid. The step (b) is carried out in the presence of bis-triphenylphosphine iminium halide as a catalyst. The halide is chloride. The halide salt is sodium bromide. The step (b) is further carried out in the presence of the amino-triphenolate catalyst of formula (I).M1 = Al (preferred) or Fe;R1-R6 = H or 1-6C alkyl (preferably CH3);L1 = tetrahydrofuran (preferred), H2O or R-O-R'; andR, R' = 1-6C alkyl.The step (b) is carried out in methyl ethyl ketone as a solvent. The PLC used in step (a) is obtained by reacting limonene oxide with carbon dioxide in the presence of a halide and (I). The halide is bis-triphenylphosphine iminium halide, i.e. bis-triphenylphosphine iminium chloride. The PLDC has a glass transition temperature of at least 150 degrees C, or at most 250 degrees C, or at least 160 degrees C or at least 170 degrees C or at most 220 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - 200 mg Poly(limonene-8,9-oxide)carbonate, 10 mol.% bis-triphenylphosphine iminium halide and solvent were mixed in a Teflon (RTM: PTFE) vessel equipped with a magnetic stirring bar, placed in stainless steel reactor, purged three times with 5 bar of carbon dioxide and pressurized with 20 bar of carbon dioxide at room temperature. The mixture was heated to the desired temperature specified in Table 3 of the specification, measured inside the reactor, and stirred for the time period specified in Table 3. After cooling down in an ice bath, the reactor was slowly depressurized. The liquid phase was transferred into a flask and the Teflon (RTM: PTFE) insert thoroughly rinsed with dichloromethane (DCM). Removal of all volatiles vacuum was followed by addition of DCM until the solid dissolved completely. Dropwise addition of methanol to give white to poly(limonene)dicarbonate.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.)
MC A05-E06A; A10-E06; A10-E07
IP B01J-031/18; B01J-031/22; C08G-064/34; C08G-064/42; C08K-005/14

GA 201935816P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,2-dimethylbutyric acid comprises performing hydrolysis reaction of t-amyl magnesium halide as Grignard reagent with carbon dioxide. The t-amyl magnesium halide Grignard reagent includes t-amyl magnesium chloride Grignard reagent and t-amyl magnesium bromide Grignard reagent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,2-dimethylbutyric acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; uses raw materials, which are easy to obtain; and has high yield. The 2,2-dimethylbutyric acid is easy to purify, and has purification of more than 99%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The addition reaction is carried out at 35-45 degrees C, preferably incubating for 0.5-2 hours after addition reaction. The method comprises performing purification by alkalization of oily crude product obtained after hydrolysis to water to obtain an aqueous solution of 2,2-dimethylbutyrate, carrying out acid displacement reaction of aqueous solution of 2,2-dimethyl butyrate, and distilling obtained oily sample, collecting product fractions at a rate of 94-95 degrees C/5 mm Hg. The contact reaction includes heating magnesium at 50 degrees C, stop heating, allowing magnesium to exothermic reaction at 60-70 degrees C, adding partial amount of accelerant comprising tetrahydrofuran and 2-methyltetrahydrofuran, t-amyl chloride to initiate the reaction, raising the temperature slowly, stopping, adding remaining amount of accelerant, t-amyl chloride and adding mixed solution of solvent A including toluene, dimethylbenzene and cyclohexane, where the mass ratio of amyl chloride, magnesium, accelerant and mixed solution of solvent A of 102-112: 25-34: 95-101: 55-71. The method comprises adding mixed solution dropwise at 80-90 degrees C and incubating for 1.5-2.5 hours. Preferred Components: The tertiary amyl halide magnesium format reagent is tert-amyl chloride magnesium format reagent, where the mass ratio of the tertiary amyl chloride magnesium format reagent to carbon dioxide is (52-54): (43-45). The mass ratio of partial amount of accelerator to remaining amount of accelerant is 1: (52-53), and the mass ratio of partial amount of accelerant to partial amount of t-amyl chloride is (0.8 -1.2):(0.8-1.2).

PT P
PN CN109553524-A; CN109553524-B
TI Synthesizing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride useful for industrial continuous production comprises e.g. preparing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride by using 2,4-dichlorofluorobenzene, carbon dioxide and carbon tetrachloride.
AU GU H
   WU Z
   YU Y
   XU H
AE ZHEJIANG BENLI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019324933
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride comprises preparing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride by using 2,4-dichlorofluorobenzene, carbon dioxide and carbon tetrachloride under the action of cationic resin catalyst.
   USE - The synthesizing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride is useful for industrial continuous production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has cheap raw material, easy to obtain, technique route, good production safety, little waste discharge of catalyst is easy to separate, simple treatment, high yield, and good quality.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the cationic resin catalyst is used in an amount of from 1-10%, preferably from 5-10%, most preferably 5%, based on mass of 2,4-dichlorofluorobenzene. The ratio of 2,4-dichlorofluorobenzene to carbon tetrachloride material is (1-20):1, preferably (10-20):1, and most preferably 10:1. The synthesis method incorporates carbon dioxide and maintaining pressure of 1-3 MPa, preferably 2 MPa. Synthesizing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride specifically comprises preparing cationic resin catalyst comprises first treating the cationic resin with 1 mol/l sodium hydroxide, washing with hydrochloric acid, washing with water until neutral, and then immersing in a 0.5 mol/l iron(III) chloride solution for 5-10 hours, preferably 8 hours and then adding hydrogen fluoride to pH 2-3, the reaction system is aging at -3 to -6 degrees C, preferably -5 degrees C for 20-30 hours, preferably 24 hours suction filtration and washing, and then the filter cake is drying at 70-90 degrees C, preferably 80 degrees C to obtain cationic resin catalyst. Synthesizing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride comprises using 2,4-dichlorofluorobenzene as solvent and reactant, and further adding cationic resin catalyst, and the target product 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride is obtaining by introducing carbon dioxide to a pressure of 1-3 MPa, and then continuously dropwise adding carbon tetrachloride in the presence of cationic resin catalyst. Synthesizing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride comprises using 2,4-dichlorofluorobenzene as solvent and reactant, further adding cationic resin catalyst, and carbon dioxide is introducing to a pressure of 1-3 MPa, and then carbon tetrachloride is continuously adding dropwise to obtain target product in presence of cationic resin catalyst 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride. Synthesizing 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride specifically comprises (i) adding 2,4-dichlorofluorobenzene and cationic resin catalyst to the reaction vessel, heating to 30-50 degrees C, preferably 40 degrees C introducing carbon dioxide gas to 1-3 Mpa, preferably 2 Mpa and stirring the reaction for 2-4 hours, preferably 3 hours to obtain a reaction liquid, and (ii) carbon tetrachloride is further adding dropwise to the above reaction solution, and heating to 50-70 degrees C, preferably 60 degrees C and the reaction is maintaining for 1-3 hours, 2 hours and completing reaction to obtain 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride. The method further comprises filtering material obtained by reaction to recover the cationic resin catalyst, and the filtrate is further distilling under reduced pressure to obtain purified 2,4-dichloro-5-fluorobenzoyl chloride.

PN CN108557766-A; CN108557766-B
TI Method of refining hydrogen chloride involves subjecting industrial grade hydrogen chloride to precursor of doped nickel-zirconia material, extracting carbon dioxide, purifying outflowing hydrogen chloride and reacting.
AU SHEN X
   CHEN G
   ZHANG G
   LI J
   ZHOU J
   YE X
AE ZHEJIANG BORUI ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201878009Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method of refining hydrogen chloride involves subjecting industrial grade hydrogen chloride to precursor of doped nickel-zirconia material in adsorption column to extract carbon dioxide, subjecting outflowing hydrogen chloride to membrane reactor containing hydrogen chloride purification adsorbent, reacting at 27 degrees C in the flow rate of 4 BV/h, and obtaining high-purity hydrogen chloride.
   USE - Method of refining hydrogen chloride.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently provides refined hydrogen chloride having high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The hydrogen chloride purification adsorbent is obtained by mixing 100 pts. wt. carbon molecular sieve and 1-10 pts. wt. doped nickel-zirconia material, under nitrogen protection, adding 1000-2000 pts. wt. tetraallyl silicate, 0.001-0.01 pt. wt. tetramethylphosphonium acrylate, 0.01-0.001 pt. wt. bis(1,5-cyclooctadiene)nickel, 0.01-0.001 pt. wt. bis(dicarbonylcyclopentadienyl iron) and 0.05-0.5 pt. wt. mixed solution of allyl-1,1,2,2-tetrafluoroethyl ether, reacting under nitrogen protection, irradiating gamma -ray of 10-70 kGy for 10-40 minutes, filtering and drying. The doped nickel-zirconia material is obtained by adding nickel-zirconia precursor to 2-10 pts. wt. nitrate or acetylacetone compound of divalent cobalt ion, trivalent ruthenium ion, divalent palladium ion and divalent platinum ion. The nickel-zirconia precursor is obtained by dissolving 100 pts. wt. nickel nitrate hexahydrate and 100-450 pts. wt. zirconium nitrate pentahydrate in 5000-20000 pts. wt. ethanol, adding 5-20 pts. wt. acetylacetone, injecting carbon monoxide into the reactor at 20-40 degrees C, heating the reactor to 100-140 degrees C, maintaining for 2-6 hours, cooling the reactor to room temperature, filtering, drying, placing the precipitate in tube furnace at 500-800 degrees C, and calcining in air for 2-6 hours.

PN KR2020046241-A; KR2137320-B1
TI New Methanothermobacter strain useful for generating methane and water from carbon dioxide and hydrogen substrate.
AU BYOUNG-IN S
   KIM M
   JEON B
   CHOI O K
AE UNIV HANYANG IUCF-HYU (UHYG-C)
GA 202040635X
AB    NOVELTY - A Methanothermobacter strain (KCTC 13651BP) having ability to generate methane, is new.
   USE - The strain is useful for generating methane and water from carbon dioxide and hydrogen substrate (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Organism: The strain produces methane and water in a ratio of 1:1.5-1:2.5 from carbon dioxide and hydrogen substrates, where reaction ratio of the carbon dioxide and hydrogen substrate is 1:3.5-1:4.5. The strain produces methane (methanation) at 50-70 degrees C, and grows in a basic anaerobic medium. The strain comprises a 16S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) having a 1097 base pair sequence (SEQ ID NO: 1) fully defined in the specification.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  medium is supercritical carbon dioxide medium and                  dense gas carbon dioxide medium. Preferred                  Method: The method involves (a) adjusting the                  temperature of the reactor to at least 120                  degrees C, and (b) adjusting the pressure to                  values above the critical pressure (73.8 bar) of                  carbon dioxide, or (c) adjusting the pressure to                  values less than the critical pressure of carbon                  dioxide at 10-73 bar, where the steps (a) and (b)                  are applied at the desired sequence, and the                  steps (a) and (b) are applied at the desired                  sequence. The method involves (i) carrying out                  the heating and pressurization gradually, (ii)                  filling the reactor with carbon dioxide at the                  temperature and pressure ensuring that the loaded                  carbon dioxide remains in gas phase, (iii)                  increasing the temperature of the reactor to the                  reaction temperature and (iv) filling the reactor                  with carbon dioxide to the reaction pressure.                  Preferred Condition: The reaction time is at                  least 10 minutes. The reactor pressure is 74-300                  bar, preferably 103 bar, and the maximum pressure                  does not exceed the pressure that can be endured                  by the reactor. PN CN114804146-A
TI Preparing full-silicon chabazite molecular sieve            membrane useful for natural gas purification, comprises            pouring mixed silicon source, lithium hydroxide,            organic structure guide agent into reaction kettle,            synthesizing hydrothermally by carrier coated seed            crystals, washing, drying and calcining.
AU XING W
   WANG B
   LIU B
   DU J
   WANG N
   ZHOU R
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 2022A2219R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing full-silicon chabazite (CHA)                molecular sieve membrane comprises e.g. (i) mixing                the silicon source, lithium hydroxide, organic                structure guide agent and water, stirring and                aging, pouring the sol into the reaction kettle,                adding 0-1 wt.% full silicon CHA molecular sieve                crystal, synthesizing hydrothermally, centrifuging,                washing to neutral, drying and calcining to obtain                all-silicon CHA molecular sieve seed crystal; (ii)                dispersing all-silicon CHA molecular sieve seed                crystal in ethanol solution, ultra-sonicating to                obtain crystal seed suspension, sealing the two                ends of carrier with tetrafluoro plug, coating a                layer of seed crystal on carrier and drying in oven                to obtain continuous and compact crystal seed                layer; and (iii) mixing the silicon source, lithium                hydroxide, organic structure guide agent, pouring                the sol into reaction kettle, adding the carrier                coated with seed crystals, synthesizing                hydrothermally, taking out the membrane tube,                washing, drying and calcining.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                full-silicon chabazite molecular sieve membrane is                useful for natural gas purification and flue gas                carbon capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepares uniform and dense full-silicon                chabazite molecular sieve membrane and good                preferential carbon dioxide permeability for carbon                dioxide/machine and carbon dioxide/nitrogen mixed                gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing full-silicon chabazite (CHA)                molecular sieve membrane comprises (i) mixing the                silicon source, lithium hydroxide, organic                structure guide agent and water at molar                composition of 1:(0.1-1):(0.01-0.8):(15-50),                stirring and aging for 1-100 hours to form sol,                pouring the sol into the reaction kettle, adding                0-1 wt.% full silicon CHA molecular sieve crystal                into the reaction kettle, synthesizing                hydrothermally at 100-250&#8451; for 1-100                hours, centrifuging the molecular sieve crystals                obtained from the reaction, washing to neutral,                drying and calcining to obtain all-silicon CHA                molecular sieve seed crystal; (ii) dispersing the                all-silicon CHA molecular sieve seed crystal in                ethanol solution, ultra-sonicating to obtain                crystal seed suspension, sealing the two ends of                the carrier with tetrafluoro plug, coating a layer                of seed crystal on the carrier and drying in oven                to obtain continuous and compact crystal seed                layer; and (iii) mixing the silicon source, lithium                hydroxide, organic structure guide agent at molar                composition of 1:(0.1 -1): (0.01-0.8):(15-500),                pouring the sol into the reaction kettle, adding                the carrier coated with the seed crystals into the                sol, synthesizing hydrothermally at                100-250&#8451; for 1-100 hours, taking out the                membrane tube, washing with water to neutral,                drying and calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silicon source in                  the steps (i) and (iii) is tetraethyl                  orthosilicate, tetramethyl orthosilicate, sodium                  silicate, silica sol, sodium silicate or silicon                  powder. The size of the all-silicon chabazite                  (CHA) molecular sieve seed crystal in the step                  (ii) is 50-1000 nm, and the thickness of the seed                  crystal layer coated on the porous carrier is                  50-1000 nm. The carrier material comprising at                  least one of zirconium dioxide, titanium oxide,                  mullite, silicon carbide and/or silicon oxide.                  The carrier is in the form of a sheet, a tube or                  a hollow fiber, and the average pore size is                  50-2000 nm.Preferred Method: The method of coating the                  seed layer on the porous carrier is dip coating,                  vacuum suction, wipe coating or spin coating. The                  method for coating the seed layer on the porous                  carrier in the step (ii) is a dip coating method,                  and the concentration of the seed suspension is                  0.01-0.5 wt.%. The calcining adopts air, oxygen                  or ozone and the calcination is carried out at                  150-600&#8451; for 5-50 hours, and heating                  and cooling at 0.2-2&#8451;/min. The heating                  method is oven heating, oil bath heating or                  microwave heating.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic structure                  guide agent comprises at least one of                  N-N-N-trimethyl adamantyl ammonium hydroxide,                  N-N-N-trimethyladamantylammonium bromide,                  N-N-N-trimethyladamantylammonium iodide and/or                  tetraethylammonium hydroxide.

PN CN112642434-A
TI Flower cluster-shaped cuprous oxide supported zinc oxide catalyst useful for the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide nanosheets and nanoparticles.
AU ZENG S
   LIU S
   WU J
   ZHAO Y
   XUE L
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA (UNMO-C)
GA 202143690E
AB    NOVELTY - Flower cluster-shaped cuprous oxide (Cu2O) supported zinc oxide (ZnO) catalyst, is claimed. The Cu2O-supported ZnO catalyst for electrochemical reduction of CO2 is in the shape of flower clusters, ZnO is a copolymer of nanoparticles and nanosheets, and Cu2O is highly dispersed on the surface of ZnO.
   USE - The flower cluster-shaped cuprous oxide supported zinc oxide catalyst is useful for the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide nanosheets and nanoparticles (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy to implement and economical; has good electrochemical performance, and high selectivity of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction to carbon monoxide, and Faraday efficiency can reaches to 86.9%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) adding 5.96 g zinc nitrate hexahydrate in 45 ml de-ionized water, ultrasonically dissolving and stirring at normal temperature for 15 minutes to form uniform colorless transparent solution; (ii) adding 4 g sodium hydroxide, ultrasonically dissolving in 15 ml deionized water; (ii) adding the obtained solution, continuously stirring and heating to 85 degrees C and keeping for 3 hours, naturally cooling to room temperature, collecting the product, using de-ionized water and ethanol to wash for many times, drying for 12 hours at 70 degrees C in the drying box to obtain the flower cluster-shaped zinc oxide carrier; (iv) adding 0.0277 g copper sulfate pentahydrate, ultrasonically dissolving in 50 ml de-ionized water, magnetically stirring for 10 minutes at room temperature to form uniform transparent blue solution; (v) taking 4.85 ml of 50% hydrazine hydrate (N2H4.H2O), fixing the volume to 100 ml in the volumetric flask, preparing into 0.5 mol/l of N2H4.H2O; (vi) adding the obtained 0.3 g zinc oxide obtained in step (i-iii), dissolving in the solution obtained in step (iv), stirring under magnetic stirrer for 10 minutes and then ultrasonically processing for 15 minutes, and the Cu2O is loaded on the ZnO surface; (vii) stirring the solution of step (v) while increasing the temperature to 70 degrees C and keeping for 20 minutes; (viii) keeping stirring; and (v) slowly dropping 50 ml solution obtained in step (vi), keeping for 60 minutes, naturally cooling to room temperature, collecting the product, using de-ionized water and ethanol to wash for many times, keeping drying at 60 degrees C for 8 hours in the vacuum drying box, preparing 5 wt.% Cu2O loaded ZnO cluster-shaped catalyst; and (viii) according to step (v) respectively adding 0.461 g and 0.922 g copper sulfate pentahydrate into 100 ml beaker and adding 30 ml deionized water, stirring by magnetic stirrer for 20 minutes, repeating step (v), (vi),(viii) and (viii) to obtain 5 wt.% Cu2O load ZnO and 10 wt.% Cu2O load ZnO cluster catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst ZnO carrier is 5 mu m flower cluster shape. The added amount of catalyst Cu2O is 3 wt.%, 5 wt.%, 10 wt.%. Preferred Method: the method comprises taking sodium hydroxide as precipitator, sodium citrate as surfactant, performing solvent thermal method combined with thermal chemical reduction method for synthesizing catalyst structure and composition. In the step (i), zinc nitrate hexahydrate is dissolved in water to form a solution. In the step (ii), slowly adding sodium hydroxide solution, which is good for polymerizing ZnO particles to form flower cluster. In the step (iii), under the heating condition of 85 degrees C, which is good for ZnO to form stable flower cluster. In the step (iv), controlling the copper sulfate pentahydrate and zinc nitrate hexahydrate feeding ratio, which is good for controlling the Cu2O load amount. In the step (viii), controlling the adding amount of the reducing agent hydrazine hydrate, which is good for controlling the formation of the Cu2O particles and loaded on the ZnO surface, the flower cluster-shaped Cu2O load ZnO new catalyst, where synthesizing flower cluster-shaped ZnO carrier, and then loading small particle Cu2O to prepare Cu2O loaded ZnO catalyst for electrically catalyzing and reducing carbon dioxide to prepare carbon monoxide.

PN CN112608892-A
TI Method for sub-culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells compirses inoculating primary mesenchymal stem cells, washing cells with D-hanks solution, adding recombinant trypsin solution and seeding cells into T225 flasks for culturing.
AU XIAO H
   LI X
   WANG Z
   LIU B
   TANG L
AE SHENZHEN BOYA PERCEPTION PHARM CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 202138311U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for sub-culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells compirses (b1) inoculating primary mesenchymal stem cells at density of 5000/cm2 in T225 flask, supplementing passage of complete medium to 45ml, and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator, when degree of cell fusion reaches 70-80% usually up to the 3rd day, discarding old medium and washing cells with D-hanks solution, adding 5 ml recombinant trypsin solution to each bottle to digest cells for 2 minutes to detach cells, adding 15 ml of D-hanks solution to each bottle to dilute, and combining all cell suspensions into a 50 ml centrifuge tube, centrifuging, resuspending cell pellet with passage complete medium to obtain P1 generation mesenchymal stem cells, and (b2) seeding P1 generation mesenchymal stem cells into T225 flasks, supplementing complete subculture medium to 45 ml, and placing in carbon dioxide incubator, passing cells continuously to P10 generation and generating mesenchymal stem cells of each generation.
   USE - The method is useful for sub-culturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for method for isolating and subculturing umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, comprising (1) isolating and culturing of primary umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells, (a1) processing the umbilical cord samples transported to the laboratory through the cold chain at 2-8 degrees C in a biological safety cabinet, (a2) D-Hanks thoroughly cleans to remove blood stains on the surface, cuts the umbilical cord into small sections with surgical scissors, and places them in a petri dish, repeatedly squeeze with surgical forceps to remove residual blood clots in the tissue, and clean with D-Hanks, (a3) cutting the tissue, peeling off the epidermis, removing the arteries and veins to obtain the Wharton's glue tissue, cutting the Wahton's glue into small pieces, and weighing, (a4) inoculating the culture bottle according to the prescribed amount of tissue, adding the original complete medium, mixing well, making the tissue block evenly spread at the bottom of the bottle, and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for culture, and (a5) cultivating to the 3rd day supplement complete medium to continue the culture, to the 5th day supplement the original complete medium to continue the culture, to the 7th day completely change the medium, after the cell confluence reaches more than 80%, removing the old medium and using D -hanks solution wash the cells, add recombinant trypsin solution to digest the cells to make the cells fall off, adding D-hanks solution to dilute, centrifuging, and resuspending the cell pellet in primary complete medium to obtain primary umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells (ie P0 generation); and (2) subculture of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells by the method as mentioned above.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The D-hanks solution comprises 8 g sodium chloride, 0.4 g potassium chloride, 0.06 g monopotassium phosphate, 0.08 g Sodium phosphate, 0.35 g sodium bicarbonate, 1000 ml water. The preparation method is as follows dissolving by 1000ml, 0.22 m micro-porous filter film filtering and sterilizing to obtain the product. The subculture complete culture medium is prepared by DMEM-F12 culture medium as substrate and comprising: 2% platelet lysate, 2% human serum albumin, 2 g/ml recombinant insulin, 10ng/ml of EGF, 15ng/ml of bFGF, 0.035 % thioglycerol, and 1% of fructose. The DMEMF12 culture medium formulation comprises 116.6 mg anhydrous calcium chloride, 59.05 mg L-leucine, 0.042 mg linoleic acid, 0.0013 mg copper sulfate pentahydrate, 91.25 mg lipoic acid, 0.105 mg ferric nitrate nonahydrate, 0.05 mg L-methionine, 17.24 mg phenol red, 8.1 mg ferrous sulfate, heptahydrate, 0.417 mg L-phenylalanine, 35.48 mg 1,4-butanediamine dihydrochloride, 0.081 mg potassium chloride, 311.8 mg L-serine, 26.25 mg sodium pyruvate, 55 mg magnesium chloride, 28.64 mg L-threonine, 53.45 mg vitamin H, 0.0035 mg anhydrous magnesium sulfate, 48.84 mg L-alanine, 4.45 mg D-calcium pantothenate, 2.24 mg sodium chloride, 7000 mg L-asparagine, 7.5 mg choline chloride, 8.98 mg anhydrous sodium dihydrogen phosphate, 54.35 mg L-aspartic acid, 6.65 mg folic acid, 2.65 mg disodium hydrogen phosphate, 71.02 mg L-cysteine hydrochloride, 17.56 mg i-inositol 12.6 mg zinc sulfate heptahydrate, 0.432 mg L-glutamic acid, 7.35 mg nicotinamide, 2.02 mg L-arginine hydrochloride, 147.5 mg L-proline, 17.25 mg pyridoxal hydrochloride, 2 mg L-cystine hydrochloride, 31.29 mg L-tryptophan, 9.02 mg pyridoxine hydrochloride, 0.031 mg L-glutamine, 365 mg L-tyrosine, 38.4 mg riboflavin, 0.219 mg glycine, 18.75 mg L-valine, 52.85 mg thiamine hydrochloride, 2.17 mg L-histidine hydrochloride, 31.48 mg D-glucose, 3151 mg thymidine, 0.365 mg L-isoleucine, 54.47 mg hypoxanthine, 0.68 mg vitamin B12, and the amount of water is added to 1000 ml, and dissolving each material in 1000ml, filtering and sterilizing with 0.22 mu m microporous membrane. Preferred Method: In step (a3), cutting the Wharton's glue into small pieces of 0.25 cm2. In step (a4), inoculating into the culture flask according to the amount of tissue refers to weighing 1.5 g of the Wharton's gum tissue block obtained and inoculating into the T225 culture flask. In step (a4), adding 15 ml of primary complete medium to each bottle, mixing well, making the tissue block evenly spread at the bottom of the bottle, and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for culture. In step (a4), the conditions for culture in a carbon dioxide incubator are: 5% carbon dioxide, 37 degrees C, and saturated humidity. In step (a5), culturing until the 3rd day and supplementing with 10ml primary complete medium to continue the culture. In step (a5), culturing until the 5th day and supplementing with 10ml of the primary complete culture medium to continue the culture. In step (a5), adding 5ml of recombinant trypsin solution to each bottle to digest the cells for 2 minutes. In step (a5), adding 25ml of D-hanks solution to each bottle for dilution. In step (a5), centrifuging at 100xg for 10 minutes. The detection of primary umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells obtained by isolation and culture, for example, detection of cell morphology and/or immunophenotype identification, for example, the immunophenotype identification refers to the detection of CD73, CD90, CD105 and CD19, CD11b, CD31, CD45, HLADR, and CD34, for example, the obtained primary umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cells were positive for CD73, CD90, and CD105 (all greater than 98%), and CD19, CD11b, CD31, CD45, HLADR, and CD34 were all negative (all less than 2%).

PT P
PN CN112079675-A
TI Treating eutrophic algae (blue algae) and preparing marsh gas involves removing solid or macromolecular organic carbon compounds of cells by oxidizing and decomposing them into carbon dioxide or soluble small molecular organic compounds.
AU YUAN Z
   CHEN D
   XU B
AE SHANGHAI YAORONG ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOG (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7840S
AB    NOVELTY - Treating eutrophic algae, such as blue algae deeply and preparing marsh gas involves removing the solid or macromolecular organic carbon compounds of the cells by oxidizing and decomposing them into carbon dioxide or soluble small molecular organic compounds through oxidation reaction that oxygen molecules, cyanobacteria, and other algae undergo at a specific temperature and pressure in a liquid phase, polymerizing the inorganic substance in the algae cell into fine inorganic particles, completely decomposing the algae toxin into a harmless substance. The oxidation reaction product is subjected to solid-liquid separation to form low moisture content inorganic mud cake rich in phosphate fertilizer and a clear oxidation liquid. The inorganic mud cake is used as fertilizer raw material, nutrient soil raw material, garden greening soil, or building material. The clear liquid obtained is rich in small molecular organic carbon sources, has good biodegradability.
   USE - Method for treating eutrophic algae, such as blue algae deeply and preparing marsh gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method forms an inorganic mud cake that is rich in phosphate fertilizer, its weight is reduced compared with the algae residue formed by the original algae, can be used as nutrient soil and phosphate fertilizer raw material regeneration, the clear liquid is rich in the small molecular organic carbon source material, has good biodegradability, suitable for anaerobic marsh gas treatment, refines the marsh gas and sale or generate electricity, discharges oxidized liquid after marsh gas production according to the standard after normal biochemical treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treating eutrophic algae, such as blue algae deeply and preparing marsh gas involves removing the solid or macromolecular organic carbon compounds of the cells by oxidizing and decomposing them into carbon dioxide or soluble small molecular organic compounds through oxidation reaction that oxygen molecules, cyanobacteria, and other algae undergo at a specific temperature and pressure in a liquid phase, polymerizing the inorganic substance in the algae cell into fine inorganic particles, completely decomposing the algae toxin into a harmless substance. The oxidation reaction product is subjected to solid-liquid separation to form low moisture content inorganic mud cake rich in phosphate fertilizer and a clear oxidation liquid. The inorganic mud cake is used as fertilizer raw material, nutrient soil raw material, garden greening soil, or building material. The clear liquid obtained is rich in small molecular organic carbon sources, has good biodegradability. The conventional anaerobic biogas production technique is used for processing to produce biogas. The obtained biogas can be refined for sale or power generation. The oxidized liquid after marsh gas production is discharged according to the normal biochemical treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: The organic sulfur in the algae is oxidized to sulfate radical, the organic phosphorus is oxidized to phosphate radical, and the organic nitrogen is oxidized to ammonia nitrogen. The clear liquid can be purified by ammonia nitrogen stripping and membrane separation to obtain the organic carbon source with higher purity. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature ranges between 120-360 degrees C, and the reaction pressure is 0.3-20 Megapascal (Mpa). The ratio of the oxygen supply amount to the blue algae chemical oxygen demand (COD) is 0.01-4.0.
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PN CN112063467-A
TI Processing and preparing red wine by stirring            grapes, concentrated apple juice, coconut juice,            blueberry juice, rose, peony flower, sugar crystal and            pectinase, fermenting, adding activated yeast and            honey, fermenting and bottling red wine.
AU WU X
AE ZIYUN AUTONOMOUS COUNTY GETUHONG ECOLOGI (ZIYU-Non-standard)
GA 2020C6309E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for processing and preparing red wine                involves drying glutinous rice straw, selecting                grapes with full grains to remove the pedicles,                cleaning the processed grapes on the dried                glutinous rice straw, drying, adding grapes,                concentrated apple juice, coconut juice, blueberry                juice, roses, peony flowers, sugar crystals and                pectinase into an ingredient tank for breaking and                stirring, performing static fermentation,                clarifying, adding activated yeast and honey,                performing static fermentation, adding potassium                sorbate, potassium metabisulfite, sulfur dioxide,                and carbon dioxide, mixing, clarifying, irradiating                and sterilizing the clear juice and bottling the                red wine.
   USE - The method is useful for processing and                preparing red wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares red wine which is rich in                nutrients, and ch meets people's needs for the                nutritional value of red wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for processing and preparing red wine                involves (a) selecting disease-free and                high-quality glutinous rice straw, removing the                leaves of the glutinous rice straw, leaving the                stems of the glutinous rice straw, and drying the                glutinous rice straw to a moisture content of                16-18%, (b) selecting grapes with full grains to                remove the pedicles, cleaning the processed grapes                on the dried glutinous rice straw and drying until                the moisture content of the grapes is 75-80%, (c)                selecting 85-100 kg processed grapes, 10-15 kg                concentrated apple juice, 8-12 kg coconut juice,                4-8 kg blueberry juice, 2-4 kg roses, 1-3 kg peony                flowers, 1-3 kg sugar crystals, 0.8-2 kg pectinase,                0.8-1.5 kg activated yeast, 0.8-1.2 kg honey,                0.6-1.2 kg potassium sorbate, 0.6-1.2 kg potassium                metabisulfite, 0.4-0.8 kg sulfur dioxide and                0.4-0.8 kg carbon dioxide, (d) adding grapes,                concentrated apple juice, coconut juice, blueberry                juice, roses, peony flowers, sugar crystals and                pectinase into an ingredient tank for breaking and                stirring and performing static primary fermentation                at 28-32 degrees C for 1-3 days, (e) performing                low-temperature clarification of the primary                fermented liquid for 1-3 days at 6-10 degrees C and                separating the clear juice to filter out                impurities, (f) adding activated yeast and honey to                the clarified liquid, performing static secondary                fermentation for 2-4 days at 23-26 degrees C and                continuing fermenting at 26-28 degrees C for 1-2                days, (g) adding potassium sorbate, potassium                metabisulfite, sulfur dioxide, and carbon dioxide                to the secondary fermented liquid, uniformly                mixing, again performing low-temperature                clarification for 1-2 days at 2-6 degrees C and                separating the clear juice to filter out impurities                and (h) irradiating and sterilizing the clear juice                using UV rays, inspecting and bottling the red                wine.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  drying the glutinous rice straw to a moisture                  content of 18%. The step (c) involves selecting                  90 kg processed grapes, 12 kg concentrated apple                  juice, 10 kg coconut juice, 6 kg blueberry juice,                  3 kg roses, 2 kg peony flowers, 2 kg sugar                  crystals, 1.5 kg pectinase, 1.2 kg activated                  yeast, 1 kg honey, 1 kg potassium sorbate, 0.8 kg                  potassium metabisulfite, 0.6 kg sulfur dioxide                  and 0.6 kg carbon dioxide. The step (d) involves                  performing static primary fermentation at 25                  degrees C for 3 days and continuing fermenting at                  27 degrees C for 2 days. The step (g) involves                  performing clarification at 5 degrees C for 1                  day.

PN CN211956248-U; CN113760009-A
TI Plant growth environment control device, has digital point output module for transmitting operation result of central processor to external executing device, and power supply module for supplying power to plant growth environment control device.
AU SHI Y
AE SHANGHAI YINGZHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B6650Q
AB    (CN211956248-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a plant growth environment control device, comprising: a digital point input module for inputting the plant growth environment information; the plant growth environment information is input by sensor collection or button trigger; a communication module for receiving, transmitting and storing the plant growth environment information; a central processor for calculating the plant growth environment information; a digital point output module for transmitting the operation result of the central processor to the external executing device; a time synchronization module for synchronizing the time of the plant growth environment control device with the setting PC or enabling the time of the plant growth environment control device at a specified time; a power supply module for supplying power to the plant growth environment control device. The utility model can automatically control the humidity in the plant growth, temperature, illumination, CO2 concentration and nutrient solution environment variables; the control process is simple, and it is convenient to use.

PT P
PN CN111690113-A; CN111690113-B
TI Preparing microporous diamond elastic module resin involves weighing polytetrahydrofuran ether glycol, polyether ester polyol, dibromoneopentyl glycol, dimethylolpropionic acid, dimethylformamide and supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
AU ZHU L
AE ANHUI DASONG RESIN CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202095363X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microporous diamond elastic module resin involves weighing 100-200 pts. wt. of polytetrahydrofuran ether glycol, 50-60 pts. wt. of polyether ester polyol, 20-30 pts. wt. of dibromoneopentyl glycol, 10-20 pts. wt. of dimethylolpropionic acid, 30-40 pts. wt. of dimethylformamide, 6-10 pts. wt. of nano diamond powder and 20-30 pts. wt. of supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid. The polytetrahydrofuran ether glycol and polyether ester polyol are added into the reaction kettle, and reacted at 80-100 degrees C for 2-3 hours, and reduced the reaction temperature to 50 degrees C. The dibromoneopentyl glycol and dimethylolpropionic acid are added and stirred uniformly, added dimethylformamide, and reacted at 80 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain a prepolymer. The prepolymer and nano-diamond powder are mixed into the autoclave, passed in the supercritical CO2 fluid, controlled the fluid flow rate to 1 m3/hour, and keep the pressure in the autoclave for 20-40 hours.
   USE - Method for preparing microporous diamond elastic module resin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepared microporous diamond elastic module resin, that ensures a small shrinkage rate under high temperature and high pressure, and is suitable for high temperature and high pressure environments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing microporous diamond elastic module resin involves weighing 100-200 pts. wt. of polytetrahydrofuran ether glycol, 50-60 pts. wt. of polyether ester polyol, 20-30 pts. wt. of dibromoneopentyl glycol, 10-20 pts. wt. of dimethylolpropionic acid, 30-40 pts. wt. of dimethylformamide, 6-10 pts. wt. of nano diamond powder and 20-30 pts. wt. of supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) fluid. The polytetrahydrofuran ether glycol and polyether ester polyol are added into the reaction kettle, and reacted at 80-100 degrees C for 2-3 hours, and reduced the reaction temperature to 50 degrees C. The dibromoneopentyl glycol and dimethylolpropionic acid are added and stirred uniformly, added dimethylformamide, and reacted at 80 degrees C for 4-6 hours to obtain a prepolymer. The prepolymer and nano-diamond powder are mixed into the autoclave, passed in the supercritical CO2 fluid, controlled the fluid flow rate to 1 m3/hour, and keep the pressure in the autoclave for 20-40 hours. The pressure is quickly released to obtain the mixture A, and placed the mixture A in a constant temperature silicone oil at 100 degrees C for 1 minute. The foamed mixture A is poured into a mold at 80 degrees C, and demolded after 1 hour to obtain a module resin. The demolded module resin is put into an oven at 110 degrees C and mature for 15 hours to obtain the microporous diamond elastic module resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The average molecular weight of the polytetrahydrofuran ether glycol is 2000-2500. The amount ratio of the polyoxypropylene glycol, phthalic anhydride and palladium chloride is 2:1:0.1-0.3. The mass ratio of crude polyether ester polyol, deionized water, phosphoric acid, and magnesium silicate is 16-20:1:0.2:1. Preferred Process: The polyether ester polyol is prepared by adding a polyoxypropylene glycol with an average molecular weight of 700-1000 to the reaction kettle. The phthalic anhydride and palladium chloride are added successively, stirred uniformly, set the reaction temperature to 150 degrees C, and reacted for 4 hours. The temperature is lowered to 70 degrees C, added sodium hydroxide to adjust the pH to 8-10, heated up to 100 degrees C, evacuated the system, added ethylene oxide, and reacted for 1 hour, then cooled to room temperature to obtain crude polyether ester polyol. The crude polyether ester polyol is dissolved with deionized water, set the temperature to 50 degrees C and stirred for 1 hour, added phosphoric acid and stirred for 2 hours, and raised the temperature to 80 degrees C. The magnesium silicate is added and stirred for 2 hours. The temperature is raised to 100 degrees C, and the system is evacuated to remove excess deionized water to obtain the polyether ester polyol.

PN CN111268846-A; CN111268846-B
TI Purifying salty waste water from the waste            incineration fly ash process comprises adding salty            water into aeration tank with carbon dioxide, adjusting            pH value of salty water, and adding liquid alkali,            preheating the brine containing water.
AU HUANG X
   TANG K
   HUANG M
   LI C
   GUO X
   CHEN H
AE ZHEJIANG HONGSHI ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020549924
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying salty waste water from the waste                incineration fly ash process comprises adding salty                water into the aeration tank with carbon dioxide,                adjusting pH value of the salty water to 8-10, and                adding 0.1-0.5% liquid alkali, preheating the brine                containing water to 60-80 degrees C, adding                0.05-0.08% scale inhibitor, pumping the preheated                brine containing water into the forced circulation                evaporation device, using the compressed secondary                steam to after heating, evaporating and                concentrating, evaporating to 50-65%, transferring                to the second falling film evaporator, and                compressed secondary steam used to heat the                solution again, and the solution continues to                evaporate and concentrate to 22-29%, the                evaporation temperature of the first-stage forced                circulation evaporation concentration part is 86-94                degrees C, the effective temperature difference is                6-12 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 0.08-0.1 MPa,                the evaporation temperature of the second-stage                falling film evaporation crystallization part is                90-95 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying salty waste                water from the waste incineration fly ash                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has enough elastic rest design, has                good adjustability, ensures the feeding                concentration and amount fluctuation of the lower                device can continuously.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying salty waste water from the waste                incineration fly ash process comprises adding salty                water into the aeration tank with carbon dioxide,                adjusting pH value of the salty water to 8-10, and                adding 0.1-0.5% liquid alkali, preheating the brine                containing water to 60-80 degrees C, adding                0.05-0.08% scale inhibitor, pumping the preheated                brine containing water into the forced circulation                evaporation device, using the compressed secondary                steam to after heating, evaporating and                concentrating, evaporating to 50-65%, transferring                to the second falling film evaporator, and                compressed secondary steam is used to heat the                solution again, and the solution continues to                evaporate and concentrate to 22-29%, the                evaporation temperature of the first-stage forced                circulation evaporation concentration part is 86-94                degrees C, the effective temperature difference is                6-12 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 0.08-0.1 MPa,                the evaporation temperature of the second-stage                falling film evaporation crystallization part is                90-95 degrees C, effective temperature difference                4-10 degrees C, vacuum degree 0.08-0.1 MPa, the                second-level falling film evaporator uses                ultrasonic scale inhibition technology to further                inhibit scale, completing the second-level falling                film evaporation and crystallization, entering the                crystallizer the concentrated materials, cooling                and crystallizing to 30-36 degrees C under 50                rotations/minutes stirring condition, entering the                separation system to separate after the                crystallization is completed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid alkali is a                  sodium hydroxide solution with a concentration of                  2.5-6%. The ultrasonic wave power in the                  ultrasonic scale inhibition technology is 40-80                  KHz. The circulating evaporation device and                  two-stage falling film evaporator whose volume                  ratio is 2-4:1. The crystallizer and two-stage                  falling film evaporator whose volume ratio is                  1.2-1.8:1. The separating system adopts double                  piston centrifuge and centrifuge combination                  technology. The scale inhibitor uses                  (2,7-octadien-1-yl)succinic anhydride and hexyl                  dimethyl-silane undergoes a hydrosilylation                  reaction to produce a succinic anhydride compound                  with silane. The succinic anhydride compound with                  silane and 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropanesulfonic                  acid, 1-allyl-3-ethylimidazole tetrafluoroborate                  is polymerized to form succinic anhydride silane                  polymerization scale inhibitor. The antisludging                  agent is a succinic anhydride silane                  polymerization inhibitor, The preparation method                  of antisludging agent comprises adding 16-24 pts.                  wt. (2, 7,-3-octadiene-1-yl) succinic anhydride,                  200-300 pts. wt. white oil and 0.08-0.2 pts. wt.                  methacrylic acid zinc into the reaction kettle,                  adding 0.05-0.3 pts. wt. chloroplatinic acid,                  30-45 pts. wt. hexyl dimethyl-stirring uniformly,                  raising the temperature to 80-95 degrees C,                  carrying out heat preservation reaction for 2-5                  hours, finishing the reaction, reducing the                  temperature to the room temperature, adding 3-8                  pts. wt. sodium lignosulfonate, 0.5-2 pts. wt.                  ammonium persulfate, 0.1-0.8 pts. wt.                  2-propylene-2-methyl propanesulfonic acid and                  0.02-0.2 pts. wt. 1-allyl-3-imidazole                  tetrafluoroborate, stirring and mixing uniformly,                  a succinic anhydride silane polymerization                  inhibitor controlling the temperature at 80-95                  degrees C and keeping the temperature for 1-5                  hours and removing the white oil by distillation,                  after finishing can be obtained.

PT P
PN CN110916029-A
TI Preparing sea buckthorn beverage comprises quickly freezing freshly picked sea-buckthorn branches and fruits, spraying, rinsing, beating, supercritical carbon-dioxide extracting, crushing, enzymatic hydrolyzing, canning and sterilizing.
AU YUAN F
   YUAN M
   LI W
   WANG X
   YUAN H
AE QINGDAO YUNA WEIKANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLO (QING-Non-standard)
GA 2020260513
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing sea buckthorn beverage comprises (i) quickly freezing the freshly picked sea-buckthorn branches and fruits, removing the fruits from the quick-frozen branches and fruits by the de-fruiting machine sea buckthorn fruit, and packing in a freezer; (ii) hydrolyzing fresh sea buckthorn frozen fruit and freezing, spraying, rinsing, and performing double-channel beating to separate sea buckthorn puree and sea buckthorn seeds, drying sea buckthorn seeds, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain sea buckthorn seed oil; (iii) grinding colloidally the seabuckthorn puree for double-pass rubber milling, and grinding finely materials; (iv) crushing homogenously the ground slurry through a homogenizer; (v) enzymatic hydrolyzing; (vi) adding diatomite filter aid to enzymatically hydrolyzed feed solution, stirring, filtering and collecting; (vii) stirring and gradually adding raw materials in vacuum tank; (viii) canning; and (ix) sterilizing and checking.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing sea buckthorn beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures integrity of seabuckthorn fruit, reduces fruit damage, has more complete nutrition (multiple kinds of vitamin, fat, organic acid, flavone and other active ingredient); has ultra-high pressure normal temperature sterilizing technique; avoids high temperature; and ensures product sterilizing effect. The beverage easily absorbs nutrition, and taste is more saturated and more exquisite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sea buckthorn beverage comprises (i) quickly freezing the freshly picked sea-buckthorn branches and fruits at 26-28 degrees C for 6-8 hours, and removing the fruits from the quick-frozen branches and fruits by the de-fruiting machine sea buckthorn fruit, and packing in a freezer at -15-18 degrees C; (ii) hydrolyzing fresh sea buckthorn frozen fruit and freezing at 30-40 degrees C, spraying and rinsing, and performing double-channel beating to separate sea buckthorn puree and sea buckthorn seeds, drying sea buckthorn seeds, subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to obtain sea buckthorn seed oil; (iii) grinding colloidally the seabuckthorn puree for double-pass rubber milling through colloid mill, and grinding finely the materials; (iv) crushing homogenously the ground slurry through a homogenizer; (v) adding the homogenized seabuckthorn slurry to the enzymolysis tank for enzymatic hydrolysis; (vi) adding 0.1-0.5% diatomite filter aid to the enzymatically hydrolyzed feed solution, stirring, filtering and collecting sea buckthorn juice; (vii) taking the qualified raw materials and auxiliary materials in proportion, stirring and gradually adding in the vacuum tank, where the raw materials are 52-58% sea buckthorn juice, 28-32% pure water, 13-17% auxiliary materials of fructooligosaccharide liquid and 0.003% stevioside; (viii) canning; and (ix) utilizing water at room temperature as the medium and sterilizing at sterilization pressure of not less than 500 MPa for not less than 15 minutes and checking.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the homogenization pressure is 40-60 Mpa, the homogenized material is yellow or yellowish brown, and non-visible particles. In the step (v), adding cellulase and pectinase at pH 3-4, and enzymatic hydrolyzing at 50 degrees C for 3-6 hours under stirring conditions of 60 revolutions/minute. In the step (vi), adding diatomite filter aid, stirring for 30 minutes, and utilizing a plate and frame with a pore size of 2-4 mu m, filtering and collecting sea buckthorn juice. In the step (vii), the vacuum batching process comprises (a) taking weighed qualified raw materials and auxiliary materials, adding them to the vacuum tank one by one while stirring, so that all auxiliary materials are dissolved uniformly and vacuum degassing to prevent oxidation; (b) without mixing, and directly degassing the seabuckthorn raw juice in vacuum; and (c) adopting membrane concentration technology to concentrate the sea buckthorn juice filtered by the plate and frame with a nanofiltration membrane, and vacuum degassing to 6 times the concentration. Preferred Components: In the step (vii), the raw materials are 55% sea buckthorn juice, 30% pure water, 15% auxiliary materials is fructooligosaccharide solution and 0.003% stevioside.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN CN110649239-A; CN110649239-B
TI Carbon dioxide-based preparation of silicon-boron-carbon anode material used in lithium ion battery involves heating reactor containing mixture of silicon-calcium alloy, boron-containing oxide and molten salt, and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU XIE H
   DONG H
   WANG J
   YIN H
   SONG Q
   NING Z
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UNEN-C)
GA 202004576P
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2)-based preparation of silicon-boron-carbon (Si-B-C) anode material comprises respectively drying boron-containing oxide and molten salt raw materials, separately grinding silicon-calcium alloy, boron-containing oxide and molten salt raw material under inert gas protection and uniformly mixing, putting mixed material in crucible embedded in reactor and sealing, and introducing inert gas into the reactor and maintaining inert atmosphere in the reactor, while heating; heating the reactor, introducing CO2 to molten salt in the reactor when the temperature is constant, ventilating, standing, and reacting; and cooling obtained reaction product, grinding, acid washing to remove molten salt, filtering, washing with water, and drying.
   USE - The method is for CO2-based preparation of Si-B-C anode material used in lithium ion battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The Si-B-C anode material improves specific capacity and cycle performance or lithium ion battery. Preparation method has low cost and simple operation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - CO2-based preparation of Si-B-C anode material comprises respectively drying boron-containing oxide and molten salt raw material to removing water, separately grinding silicon-calcium alloy, boron-containing oxide and molten salt raw material under inert gas protection until uniform and uniformly mixing, putting mixed material in crucible embedded in reactor and sealing, and introducing inert gas into the reactor and maintaining inert atmosphere in the reactor to ensure positive pressure in the reactor, while heating; heating the reactor to 530-900 degrees C, introducing CO2 to molten salt in the reactor at greater than or equal to 400 mL/minute when the temperature is constant, ventilating for 1-2 hours, standing, and reacting for 1-5 hours; and cooling obtained reaction product, grinding, acid washing to remove molten salt, filtering, washing with water, and drying. The molten salt is calcium chloride (CaCl2)-based molten salt or calcium chloride-magnesium chloride (CaCl2-MgCl2)-based molten salt. The boron-containing oxide is boric acid, borax, calcium borate, magnesium borate, and/or potassium borate. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) an electrode plate comprising anode material which further comprises binder, conductive agent and solvent; and
   (2) a lithium ion battery comprising the electrode plate; statically synthesized Si-B-C anode material having first charge-discharge coulombic efficiency of more than 75%, first discharge specific capacity of 3800 mAhour/g, cycling of 400 times at 0.1 A/g current density, and reversible cycle specific capacity of more than 1200 mAhour/g; and Si-B-C anode material synthesized by stirring and having first charge-discharge coulombic efficiency of more than 70%, cycling of 500 times at 0.1 A/g current density and reversible cycle specific capacity of more than 1300 mAhour/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The CaCl2-based molten salt is CaCl2, CaCl2-sodium chloride, CaCl2-potassium chloride, or CaCl2-sodium chloride-potassium chloride. The CaCl2-MgCl2-based molten salt is CaCl2-MgCl2, CaCl2-MgCl2-sodium chloride, CaCl2-MgCl2-potassium chloride, or CaCl2-MgCl2-potassium chloride-sodium chloride. The molar ratio of CaCl2 and magnesium chloride is greater than or equal to 1. When the molten salt is CaCl2-based salt and the boron-containing oxide is boron oxide, the molar ratio of calcium silicide (CaSi2) in silicon-calcium alloy to boron oxide is greater than or equal to 3 and the molar ratio of CaCl2 in molten salt and CaSi2 in silicon-calcium alloy is greater than or equal to 10. When the molten salt is CaCl2-based salt and the boron-containing oxide is calcium borate, the molar ratio of CaSi2 in silicon-calcium alloy and calcium borate is greater than or equal to 3 and the molar ratio of CaCl2 in molten salt and calcium borate is greater than or equal to 40:3. When the molten salt is CaCl2-based salt and boron-containing oxide is borax, the molar ratio of CaSi2 in silicon-calcium alloy and borax is greater than or equal to 6 and the molar ratio of CaCl2 and borax is greater than or equal to 71:3. When the molten salt is CaCl2-based salt and the boron-containing oxide is magnesium borate, the molar ratio of CaSi2 in silicon-calcium alloy and magnesium borate is 3 and the molar ratio of CaCl2 and magnesium borate is greater than or equal to 10. When molten salt is CaCl2-based molten salt and the boron-containing oxide is potassium borate, the molar ratio of CaSi2 in silicon-calcium alloy and potassium borate is greater than or equal to 6 and the molar ratio of CaCl2 and potassium borate is greater than or equal to 71:3. The anode material has particle diameter of 50 nm to 50 mu m. When Si-B-C is statically prepared, the product particle diameter is 1-50 mu m. When Si-B-C is prepared by stirring, the product particle size is 50-500 nm. Preferred Method: Drying includes placing raw materials in high-temperature vacuum drying furnace and drying at 300-400 degrees C and less than - 0.1 MPa for 10-15 hours to remove adsorbed water and crystal water.
DC L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN CN110499281-A
TI Quickly establishing H9c2 myocardial cell fat-model, comprises e.g. culturing frozen H9c2 cells stored as P0 generation cardiomyocytes in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium high-sugar complete medium, and digesting.
AU LI G
   ZHANG F
AE UNIV CAPITAL MEDICAL BEIJING SHIJITAN (UYMD-C)
GA 2019A41300
AB    NOVELTY - Quickly establishing H9c2 myocardial cell fat-model, comprises e.g. (i) culturing frozen H9c2 cells stored as P0 generation cardiomyocytes in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) high-sugar complete medium at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide to 3 passages to obtain P3 generation cardiomyocytes, (ii) digesting the above-mentioned P3 generation cardiomyocytes with 0.5-1 ml trypsin for 1-2 minutes, adding 5-8 ml DMEM high-sugar complete medium to terminate the digestion, collecting the cell suspension to 15 ml centrifuge tube, placing the centrifuge tube in a centrifuge, centrifuging at 1000-1200 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5-8 minutes, discarding the supernatant, and adding 1-2 ml DMEM high-sugar complete medium to obtain a cell suspension, (iii) thoroughly pipetting and mixing the cell suspension, mixing 10 mu l cell suspension with 10 mu l trypan blue staining solution, adding to a cell counting plate, and performing cell counting, and (iv) seeding the cell suspension into six-well plate.
   USE - The method is useful for quickly establishing H9c2 myocardial cell fat-model.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: easy operation, excellent fat-forming effect, and fast molding speed; is economical; and provides a theoretical reference for further research on cardiovascular disease caused by atherosclerosis in the future.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Quickly establishing H9c2 myocardial cell fat-model, comprises (i) culturing frozen H9c2 cells stored as P0 generation cardiomyocytes in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) high-sugar complete medium at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide to 3 passages to obtain P3 generation cardiomyocytes, (ii) digesting the above-mentioned P3 generation cardiomyocytes with 0.5-1 ml trypsin for 1-2 minutes, adding 5-8 ml DMEM high-sugar complete medium to terminate the digestion, collecting the cell suspension to 15 ml centrifuge tube, placing the centrifuge tube in a centrifuge, centrifuging at 1000-1200 revolutions/minute (rpm) for 5-8 minutes, discarding the supernatant, and adding 1-2 ml DMEM high-sugar complete medium to obtain a cell suspension, (iii) thoroughly pipetting and mixing the cell suspension, mixing 10 mu l cell suspension with 10 mu l trypan blue staining solution, adding to a cell counting plate, and performing cell counting, (iv) seeding the cell suspension into a six-well plate according to the cell counting result, where the number of cells in each well was 1-5x 105 cells/ml and 2 ml DMEM high-sugar complete medium, uniformly mixing, culturing at 37 degrees C with 5% carbon dioxide for 24 hours, and discarding the upper culture solution to obtain seed plates, and (v) preparing a fat medium, adding to the seed plate, and culturing for 24-48 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), each 100 ml DMEM high-sugar complete medium comprises 10 ml fetal bovine serum, 4 mmol L-glutamine, 10000 U penicillin and 10 mg streptomycin. In the step (v), the fat medium is a mixture of OA fat medium and PA fat medium. The OA concentration in the OA lipid medium is 600 mu mol/l. The PA concentration in the PA lipidic medium is 400 mu mol/l. Preferred Method: In the step (v), the fat medium is prepared by (va) preparing OA stock solution by (va1) preparing 20 mmol/l OA solution, comprising dissolving 0.014 g sodium hydroxide in 10 ml deionized water to obtain 10 ml 0.1 mol/l solution, adding 19.04 mu l OA to 3 ml sodium hydroxide solution, placing in 75 degrees C water bath for 30 minutes until it is colorless and clear to obtain 20 mmol/l 3 ml sodium oleate saponification solution i.e., OA solution, and heat-preserving the OA solution, (va2) preparing bovine serum albumin (BSA) solution by preheating 3 ml phosphate-buffered saline solution to 55 degrees C, adding 0.6 g BSA in 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding preheated 3 ml phosphate buffered saline solution to the centrifuge tube, placing the centrifuge tube in a high-speed centrifuge, and centrifuging at 4500 rpm for 20 minutes to obtain 3 ml BSA solution, and (va3) quickly adding 3 ml OA solution to the BSA solution to obtain 6 ml 20 mmol/l OA and BSA solution, uniformly shaking, then filtering through a bacterial filter in a clean bench to obtain OA storage solution, and storing at 4 degrees C, (vb) preparing OA lipid medium by adding the OA storage solution to DMEM high-sugar complete medium, and diluting to a concentration of 400-650 mu mol/l, (vc) preparing PA storage solution by (vc1) dissolving 0.04 g sodium hydroxide in 10 ml deionized water to obtain 10 ml 0.1 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, adding 0.030 g PA to 3 ml sodium hydroxide solution, placing in 75 degrees C water bath for 30 minutes until it is colorless and clear to obtain 3 ml 40 mmol/l sodium palmitate saponification solution i.e., PA solution, and heat-preserving the PA solution, (vc2) preparing BSA solution by preheating 3 ml phosphate-buffered saline solution to 55 degrees C, adding 1.2 g BSA into 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding the preheated 3 ml phosphate buffered saline solution to the centrifuge tube, placing the centrifuge tube in a high-speed centrifuge, centrifuging at 4500 rpm for 20 minutes to obtain 3 ml of BSA solution, and (vc3) quickly adding 3 ml PA solution to the BSA solution to obtain 6 ml 40 mmol/l PA and BSA solution, uniformly shaking, then filtering by using clean bench bacteria filter to obtain PA storage solution, and storing at 4 degrees C, and (vd) preparing PA lipid medium by adding the PA storage solution to DMEM high-sugar complete medium, and diluting to a concentration of 100-550 mu mol/l.
