PN CN110116443-A
TI Preparation of mothproof slow-release wood preservative involves mixing deworming essential oil, spare leachate, wood preservative, wood adhesive, and wood swelling agent and compounding.
AU TU B
AE TU B (TUBB-Individual)
GA 201971963F
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of mothproof slow-release wood preservative involves mixing 10-12 pts. wt. deworming essential oil, 8-10 pts. wt. spare leachate, 20-22 pts. wt. wood preservative, 50-55 pts. wt. wood adhesive, and 6-7 pts. wt. wood swelling agent and compounding. The leachate is prepared by placing 200-230 g rice bran and 400-450 mL 45 %mass sodium hydroxide solution in a water bath reactor with stirrer, heating in a water bath, starting stirrer, stirring mixture for 3-5 hours, adjusting the pH to neutral with glacial acetic acid having mass fraction of 95%, transferring to a distillation flask, heating at 85-90 degrees C, and heat preserving under reflux for 2-3 hours and removing rice bran residue.
   USE - Preparation of mothproof slow-release wood preservative.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of mothproof slow-release wood preservative with large spatial structure, high strength, excellent adhesion effect to surface of wood fibers with hydroxyl groups, and sustained release function by providing antiseptic and pesticidal active ingredient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The preparation of mothproof slow-release wood preservative involves mixing 10-12 pts. wt. deworming essential oil, 8-10 pts. wt. spare leachate, 20-22 pts. wt. wood preservative, 50-55 pts. wt. wood adhesive, 6-7 pts. wt. wood swelling agent and compounding. The deworming essential oil is prepared by (1) placing Cinnamomum camphora leaves in an oven and heating at 80-85 degrees C, calcining and drying to water content of 3-5%, mixing dried C.camphora leaves and distilled water and soaking for 2-3 hours, subjecting to distillation at 60-65 degrees C for 1-2 hours, removing the distilled impurities, and collecting and purifying C.camphora leaves, (2) mixing purified C.camphora leaves and safflower Pyrethrum in mass ratio of 2:1 to obtain mixed raw material, adding mixed raw materials and anhydrous ethanol to the extraction kettle in mass ratio of 20:1, and adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into the extraction kettle, extracting for 45-50 minutes to obtain extracted supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, (3) separating the extracted supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into the rectification column, and separating obtained rectified liquid into separator. The leachate is prepared by placing 200-230 g rice bran and 400-450 mL 45 %mass sodium hydroxide solution in a water bath reactor with stirrer, heating in a water bath, starting stirrer, stirring mixture for 3-5 hours, adjusting the pH to neutral with glacial acetic acid having mass fraction of 95%, transferring to a distillation flask, heating at 85-90 degrees C, and heat preserving under reflux for 2-3 hours and removing rice bran residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The specific ratio of dried C.camphora leaves and distilled water in preparation of deworming essential oil is 1:7. The mass ratio of mixture of dried C.camphora leaves and safflower Pyrethrum in preparation of deworming essential oil is 2:1. Preferred Conditions: The extraction pressure in preparation deworming essential oil is 6-8 MPa and the extraction temperature is 25-28 degrees C. In preparation deworming essential oil, the pressure of rectification column is controlled to be 7-8 MPa, the control temperature is 20-22 degrees C, the rectification time is 0.5-1.0 hour, separation pressure is 8-10 MPa, separation temperature is controlled to 25-30 degrees C and separation time is 30-35 minutes. In the preparation of leachate, the temperature of water bath reactor is heated to 90-95 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The wood swelling agent is obtained by mixing triethylamine trihydrofluoride and ammonium hexafluorophosphate in mass ratio of 3:1. The wood preservative is prepared by mixing nano-copper oxide, chitosan and triammonium citrate in mass ratio of 4:3:1, adding distilled water for diluting, and controlling the mass fraction of nano-copper oxide in the wood preservative after dilution to 3-5%.

TI Microalgae air purifier, has two guide disks matched with flow guide passage for guiding algae to reservoir, pump matched with liquid storage groove, and light source arranged with shell for providing light to algae.
AU YOU Z
AE YOU Z (YOUZ-Individual)
   YOU Z (YOUZ-Individual)
   YOU Z (YOUZ-Individual)
GA 201913860H
AB    (CN208398349-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims an air purifier, comprising a shell, a liquid circulation unit in the shell and a light source in the shell. liquid circulation unit has a reservoir, a flow guide disc set and a pump, diaphragm group arranged above the reservoir and having two discs, the two flow guide disc can be detachably assembled together and each has a port, such that the two ports are staggered with each other to form a guide passage guiding the algae to reservoir, pump in the reservoir, flowing the algae liquid to cycle, the light source providing light to the algae, the algae in the process of circulation flow for photosynthesis. Therefore, the utility model uses the microalgae characteristic absorption of carbon dioxide in the air, and the water molecules in the shell completely intercepting according to PM2.5 of contaminants, thereby reaching the effect of purifying the air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of an air purifier.
EA    NOVELTY - The air purifier has a shell whose bottom and top parts are respectively formed with an air inlet and an air outlet. A fan is arranged near the air outlet of the shell. A liquid circulation unit is provided with a liquid storage tank, a guide disk set and a pump. The liquid storage tank is detachably arranged in the shell. The guide disk set is arranged on an upper part of the liquid storage tank and provided with two guide plates. The guide plates are arranged in up and down manner, respectively provided with two ports and detachably assembled together. The ports of the guide plates are staggered together to form a flow guide passage for guiding algae to the liquid storage tank. The pump is arranged in the liquid storage tank for guiding the algae to an upper part of each guide plate. A light source is arranged in the shell for providing light to the algae. The shell is provided with a base, two opposite guide rails, a hollow column body, an upper cover and a carbon dioxide sensor.
   USE - Air purifier for purifying air using microalgae as purifying medium and intercepting contaminants such as particulate matter-2.5 (PM 2.5) using water molecules.
   ADVANTAGE - The air purifier effectively uses characteristics of microalgae to absorb carbon dioxide in air and completely intercepts contaminants such as PM2.5, using the water molecules in the shell so as to achieve air purifying effect.

TI Catalyst comprises discrete particles comprising a lanthanum-containing support material and nanoparticles of a transition metal excluding lanthanides and actinides or an oxide, where support material comprises lanthanum oxycarbonate.
AU ROSEN B A
   GOZIN M
   DAHAN M
   KOMARALA E V P
   FADEEV L
   CHINNAM A K
   SHLOMOVICH A
AE TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION MOMENTUM FUND ISRAEL (TECH-Non-standard)
   TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION MOMENTUM FUND ISRA (TECH-Non-standard)
   TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION MOMENTUM FUND ISRA (TECH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A21047
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises discrete particles comprising a lanthanum-containing support material and nanoparticles of a transition metal excluding lanthanides and actinides or an oxide, where support material comprises lanthanum oxycarbonate in the form of La202C03 and La20(C03)2, and optionally lanthanum oxide (La203) and/or lanthanum oxychloride (LaOCl), and nanoparticles are impregnated within or attached to support material.
   USE - Catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing reforming of a hydrocarbon, which involves reacting hydrocarbon with carbon dioxide, in the presence of a catalyst to obtain hydrogen and carbon monoxide, reacting hydrocarbon with steam, in the presence of a catalyst to obtain hydrogen and carbon dioxide and reacting hydrocarbon with both carbon dioxide and steam, in the presence of a catalyst to obtain hydrogen and carbon monoxide;
   (2) a method for coating an inert ceramic monolith with a catalyst, which involves activating the surface of inert ceramic monolith, mixing catalyst with a metal oxide, a polysaccharide, polyethylene glycol, a polyvinyl compound, and water to form a slurry, coating the activated ceramic monolith with slurry and drying and then calcinating the coated ceramic monolith; and
   (3) a method for preparing catalyst comprising discrete particles comprising a lanthanide-based support material and nanoparticles of a transition metal excluding lanthanides and actinides or an oxide impregnated within or attached to support material, which involves mixing a complex of lanthanide and an energetic nitrogen-rich ligand with a complex of transition metal and the same or a different energetic nitrogen-rich ligand(s); and optionally an organic or inorganic oxidant, each in the form of a solid or semi-solid material, to obtain a homogeneous solid or semi- solid material, grinding and mixing homogeneous solid or semi-solid material, pressing homogeneous solid or semi-solid material into a form (pellet), heating optionally pressed homogeneous solid or semi-solid material at a temperature sufficient to combust energetic nitrogen-rich ligand(s), but lower than the melting temperature of each one of lanthanide and transition metal to obtain a catalyst materia, subjecting catalyst material to a temperature sufficient to oxidize residual organic matter, but lower than the melting temperature of each one of lanthanide and transition metal, in the flow of a gas mixture comprising 02 and an inert gas selected from Ar, He or N2 and subjecting the product obtained to a temperature sufficient to reduce the oxide of transition metal obtained, but lower than the melting temperature of each one of lanthanide and transition metal, in the flow of a gas mixture comprising 02 and an inert gas selected from Ar, He or N2, to obtain catalyst.
   (4) A lanthanum N,N-bis(1H-tetrazole-5-y1)-amine(La-B TA) complex.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nanoparticles are relatively uniformly distributed on support material. The catalyst does not comprise molecular carbon including fullerenes, single-walled carbon nanotubes, multi-walled carbon nanotubes, graphene, vitreous carbon, graphite, and amorphous carbon. The nanoparticles comprise both transition metal and oxide. The transition metal is titanium, vanadium, manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, molybdenum, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, tungsten, rhenium, osmium, iridium, or platinum. The transition metal is nickel or iron. The transition metal is nickel; and (a) the overall amount of La202C03, La20(C03)2, La203 and LaOCl is about 45-60%, preferably about 50-55%, by weight, of catalyst; (b) the amount of Ni is about 35-50%, preferably about 40-45%, by weight, of catalyst; or (c) the amount of NiO, when present, is about 5-15%, preferably about 8-12%, by weight, of catalyst. The overall amount of La202C03, La20(C03)2, La203 and LaOCl is about 50-52% by weight of catalyst; the amount of Ni is about 40-42% by weight of catalyst; and the amount of NiO is about 9-10% by weight of catalyst. The support material comprises La202C03, La20(C03)2, and optionally La203 and/or LaOCl, and catalyst exhibits an increase of about 0.8 eV or more in the binding energy of the Ni3p XPS spectrum compared to that of unbound Ni. The hydrocarbon is methane. The transition metal is nickel. The inert ceramic monolith is activated by cleaning with an acid such as nitric acid, sulfuric acid, hydrochloric acid, or acetic acid, and then optionally calcinating; and/or the metal oxide mixed with catalyst in step (ii) is alumina, colloidal alumina, pseudoboehmite, silica, colloidal silica, or sodium silicate; and/or the metal oxide mixed with catalyst in step (ii) is alumina, colloidal alumina, pseudoboehmite, silica, colloidal silica, or sodium silicate; and/or, the polysaccharide mixed with catalyst in step (ii) is hydroxyalkyl cellulose such as hydroxypropyl cellulose and hydroxyethyl cellulose; and/or, the polyethylene glycol mixed with catalyst in step (ii) has a molecular weight in a range of about 600 to about 6000 Da; and/or the polyvinyl compound mixed with catalyst in step (ii) is polyvinyl alcohol, polyvinyl pyrrolidone, polyvinyl butyral, or polyvinyl chloride; and/or the activated ceramic monolith is coated with slurry in step (iii) by dipping in slurry. The lanthanide is lanthanum, cerium, praseodymium, neodymium, promethium, samarium, or gadolinium. The transition metal is titanium, vanadium, manganese, iron, cobalt, nickel, copper, zinc, molybdenum, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, tungsten, rhenium, osmium, iridium, or platinum.

PT P
PN CN108641136-A
TI Preparation of rubber material, involves refluxing raw materials, ultrasonically dispersing, twin-screw extruding, high-temperature shaping, flat vulcanizing, steaming, solidifying, filling carbon dioxide gas, naturally cooling and drying.
AU ZHANG X
AE SUZHOU NICETY PLASTIC CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2018813029
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of rubber material, involves mixing 2,2-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)butane, sodium chloride and acetyltriethyl citrate to obtain modified treatment liquid, adding isoprene rubber, ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, chlorohydrin rubber, bamboo fibrils, white carbon black and 2,2,4-trimethyl-1,2-dihydroquinoline polymer, calcining, mixing, pressurizing, sintering, calcining, mixing with modified treatment liquid, adding gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, sodium o-hydroxybenzoate, carbamide, diammonium phosphate and tristyrene, ultrasonically processing to obtain suspension, adding antifoaming agent and stabilizer to suspension, adding sulfur powder and vulcanizing agent, stirring and leaving still, injecting into twin-screw extruder, extruding, pressing and shaping, vulcanizing to obtain vulcanized composite material, placing vulcanized composite material in high-temperature steam, solidifying, filling with circulating carbon dioxide gas, naturally cooling and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing rubber material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of lightweight wear-resistant rubber material with small loss, and can meet various user requirements.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing rubber material, involves (1) mixing 2,2-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)butane, sodium chloride and acetyl triethyl citrate in a mass ratio of 1:3:10, stirring, placing in a conical flask, placing conical flask in dry reflux stirred reaction device and filling argon for 60 minutes to obtain modified treatment liquid, using anhydrous calcium chloride and concentrated sulfuric acid as a desiccant, (2) adding 10-20 pts. wt. isoprene rubber, 9-16 pts. wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer, 7-13 pts. wt. chlorohydrin rubber, 10-15 pts. wt. bamboo fibrils, 3-8 pts. wt. white carbon black and 1-3 pts. wt. 2,2,4-trimethyl-1,2-dihydroquinoline polymer, injecting into high-temperature calciner, mixing, pressurizing, filling with argon, sintering at high temperature for 120-150 minutes, calcining at 820-825 degrees C, (3) mixing the obtained product with modified treatment liquid, adding 3-8 pts. wt. gamma -methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane, 2-5 pts. wt. sodium o-hydroxybenzoate, 1-3 pts. wt. carbamide, 2-4 pts. wt. diammonium phosphate and 2-7 pts. wt. tristyrene and ultrasonically processing for 2-4 hours to obtain suspension, (4) adding antifoaming agent and stabilizer to suspension, adding 2-4 pts. wt. sulfur powder and 1-2 pts. wt. vulcanizing agent, stirring and leaving still for 10-18 hours, (5) injecting the obtained mixture into twin-screw extruder, extruding, pressing and shaping, (6) placing the press-molded component on a flat vulcanizing machine and vulcanizing to obtain vulcanized composite material, (7) placing vulcanized composite material in high-temperature steam, solidifying, filling with circulating carbon dioxide gas, taking out after 30-60 minutes of reaction, naturally cooling and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The antifoaming agent and stabilizer mixture are selected from emulsified silicone oil, polydimethylsiloxane, high alcohol fatty acid ester, (2-bromoethyl)trimethylsilane and glycerol in a mass ratio of 2:3:1:2:5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The step (1) comprises refluxing at 98-102 degrees C. The step (2) comprises pressurizing the furnace at 4-8 MPa. The step (3) comprises ultrasonically processing at frequency of 50-60 kHz and power of 800-1000 W. The step (5) comprises controlling screw temperature at 220-260 degrees C and screw speed at 1200-1500 rpm. The step (6) comprises vulcanizing at 175 degrees C and 25 MPa for 30 seconds. The step (7) comprises controlling high-temperature steam at 1200-1500 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The vulcanizing agent is selected from p-tert-butyl phenolic resin, p-tert-octyl phenolic resin, hexamethylenediamine carbamate and/or ethylenediaminecarboxylate.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).)

PN CN108358485-A
TI Preparation anti-corrosive steel fiber used for building concrete involves pickling steel fiber, immersing in oxidizing solution, and subjecting to secondary treatment in calcium hydroxide solution.
AU CHEN Q
   ZENG J
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201862913R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation anti-corrosive steel fiber involves pickling steel fiber; rinsing and drying to prepare surface for oxidation; preparing oxidizing solution from 10-15 pts. wt. phosphoric acid, 8-12 pts. wt. calcium nitrate, 20-25 pts. wt. manganese dioxide and 48-2 pts. wt. water; adding oxidizing solution into reaction vessel; immersing steel fiber into oxidizing solution for oxidation treatment under high temperature and high pressure to form dense anti-corrosive iron oxide film; washing steel fiber to obtain steel fiber coated with iron oxide film; mixing coated steel fiber with clarified calcium hydroxide solution; reacting at high temperature and pressure; reducing pressure and temperature to be consistent with indoor environment; introducing carbon dioxide into reactor to convert calcium hydroxide into calcium carbonate; taking out steel fiber; and baking and drying.
   USE - Preparation anti-corrosive steel fiber used for building concrete (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces anti-corrosive steel fiber with good film coverage uniformity and large thickness, has micro-loose hole filled and passivated by calcium carbonate, and strong corrosion resistance. It is carried out at high temperature and high pressure, has high reaction efficiency, has simple preparation process, uses small amount of oxidizing solution, and has low process cost and low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation anti-corrosive steel fiber comprises pickling steel fiber; immersing in oxidizing solution comprising mixed solution of phosphoric acid, calcium nitrate and manganese dioxide for surface oxidation treatment; further subjecting to secondary treatment in calcium hydroxide solution; and adding carbon dioxide to convert calcium hydroxide into calcium carbonate to produce anti-corrosive steel fiber. It preferably involves pickling steel fiber; rinsing and drying to remove surface rust while roughening surface of steel fiber to prepare surface for oxidation to form protective film; preparing oxidizing solution from 10-15 pts. wt. phosphoric acid, 8-12 pts. wt. calcium nitrate, 20-25 pts. wt. manganese dioxide and 48-2 pts. wt. water; adding oxidizing solution into reaction vessel; immersing steel fiber into oxidizing solution; performing oxidation treatment under high temperature and high pressure conditions to oxidize surface and to form dense anti-corrosive iron oxide film; taking out steel fiber and washing to obtain steel fiber coated with iron oxide film; mixing coated steel fiber with clarified calcium hydroxide solution; adding to reactor and treating twice at high temperature and pressure to make calcium hydroxide enter into tiny porous pores of iron oxide film; stop pressing and heating; reducing pressure and temperature to be consistent with indoor environment; introducing carbon dioxide into reactor to convert calcium hydroxide into calcium carbonate to fill and passivate tiny holes; taking out steel fiber; and baking and drying steel fiber to obtain anti-corrosive steel fiber.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The calcium hydroxide solution has concentration of 0.12-0.15 mass%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Component: The steel fiber has diameter of 0.2-0.6 mm and aspect ratio of 40-70. The iron oxide film is formed at thickness of 200-400 nm. Preferred Method: The pickling step is performed using diluted sulfuric acid, diluted nitric acid, diluted hydrochloric acid, phosphoric acid, or chromic acid. The surface oxidation treatment is performed at 300-400 degrees C under 30-50 MPa pressure for 15-20 minutes. The secondary treatment is performed at 150-180 degrees C under 60-70 MPa pressure for 30-40 minutes. The pressure and temperature reduction step is performed at 3-5 MPa/minute and at 2-3 degrees C/minute. The carbon dioxide is added at 10-20 l/minute.
DC L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).); M14 (Other chemical surface treatments - including etching, brightening, forming non-metallic layers, passivation, cathodic protection and corrosion inhibitors, but not processes specifically for semiconductor production (C23F, C25). This Section also covers multistage processes.); M13 (Coating material with metals, diffusion processes, enamelling and vitreous coatings - including coating from liquid metal or solution, spraying, cementation, cathodic sputtering, enamelling and oilfree lubricant coatings, but not coatings for the production of semiconductors (C23C, D).)
MC L02-A03; L02-A04; L02-B01; L02-D14N; L02-J02C; M13-H; M14-K
IP C04B-014/48; C04B-020/10; C23C-022/22; C23C-022/83

PT P
PN KR1877020-B1
TI Producing sustained-release composite, comprises e.g. preparing Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit powder, and obtaining Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit extract mixture by extracting Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit powder.
AU YU S O
   YOO S
   HWANG G
AE OH Y S (OHYS-Individual)
   TECOPLUS CO LTD (TECO-Non-standard)
GA 201855730E
AB    NOVELTY - Producing sustained-release composite nanoparticles containing natural antimicrobial substances, comprises preparing Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit powder, obtaining Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit extract mixture by extracting Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit powder through supercritical fluid extraction method using co-solvent and supercritical fluid, preparing sustained-release antimicrobial particle mixture by mixing Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit extract mixture, chitosan powder, functional mineral, inorganic mixture and methylene chloride, preparing organic binder by supplying melanin, injecting carbon dioxide and ethanol, supplying sustained-release antibacterial particle mixture and melanin, forming sustained-release composite nanoparticles by combining sustained-release antimicrobial particle mixture and melanin, recovering waste solvent, and precipitating sustained-release composite nanoparticles.
   USE - The nanoparticles are useful in sustained-release composite pellet for producing decomposition sheet (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoparticles are safe to human body, releases anion which is beneficial to human body, has high biodegradability, reduces blight of Chinese cabbage and usage of pesticide, and improves acidity and strength of soil by supplementing inorganic salts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing sustained-release composite nanoparticles containing natural antimicrobial substances, comprises (a) preparing Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit powder, (b) obtaining Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit extract mixture by extracting Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit powder through supercritical fluid extraction method using co-solvent and supercritical fluid (carbon dioxide), (c) preparing sustained-release antimicrobial particle mixture by mixing Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit extract mixture, chitosan powder, functional minerals, inorganic mixture and methylene chloride in a the first tank (100) by airtight transfer of Zanthoxylum schinifolium fruit extract mixture without co-solvent separation, (d) preparing organic binder by supplying melanin into a fourth tank (400), (e) injecting carbon dioxide as supercritical fluid and ethanol as co-solvent into a supercritical reactor (500) under pressure of 110-150 bar at 30-40 degrees C, (f) supplying the reactant by supplying the sustained-release antibacterial particle mixture and melanin into the supercritical reactor at a flow rate of 0.5-0.9 ml/minute using a syringe pump connected to the first tank and the fourth tank, (g) forming sustained-release composite nanoparticles by combining the sustained-release antimicrobial particle mixture and melanin in the supercritical reactor, and (h) recovering the waste solvent in the supercritical reactor, and precipitating the sustained-release composite nanoparticles from a mesh filter cage set. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) sustained-release composite nanoparticles prepared by the above method; and
   (2) sustained-release composite pellet containing natural antimicrobial functional material comprising sustained-release composite nanoparticles, coconut powder, silane, starch plasticizer, decomposing agent, polymer resin and calcium carbonate. The decomposing agent is a natural polyunsaturated fatty acid having active methylene group including alpha linolenic acid and/or beta linolenic acid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the method for producing sustained-release composite nanoparticles.
   First tank (100)
   Cold trap cooling system (220)
   Third tank (300)
   Fourth tank (400)
   Supercritical reactor (500)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flow rate of the reactant in the first tank supplied into the supercritical reactor is 0.5-0.6 ml/minute. The reactant flow rate in the fourth tank is 0.8-0.9 ml/minute. The sustained-release composite nanoparticles comprise 2.5-10 wt.% Zanthoxylum schinifolium extract, 10-28 wt.% chitosan powder, 10-28 wt.% functional minerals, 5-10 wt.% inorganic mixture and 25-70 wt.% melanin. The functional mineral is a mixed powder of millerite, sassolite, pentlandite, brucite and/or gonnardite. The inorganic mixture is a mixture of sodium metasilicate pentahydrate, potassium carbonate, potassium citrate, sodium tetraborate decahydrate, potassium iodide and sulfur. The sustained-release composite nanoparticles have particle diameter of 200-400 nm. The pellet comprises 4-18 wt.% sustained-release composite nanoparticles, 5-15 wt.% coconut powder, 0.1-2 wt.% silane, 0.1-2% wt. starch plasticizer, 0.1-2 wt.% decomposing agent, 50-85 wt.% polymer resin and 5-30 wt.% calcium carbonate. Preferred Method: In the step (g), the inner side of the supercritical reactor is continuously or discontinuously decompressed and pressurized under pressure of plus minus 10 bar.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); C05 (Biological control - excluding veterinary medicine, but including use of microorganisms, predators and natural products.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Preparing hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid comprises e.g. adding water and calcium hydroxide into calcium hydroxide pulping unit to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry and adding calcium hydroxide slurry.
AU WU J
AE WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
GA 2021078290
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid comprises e.g. adding Water and calcium hydroxide into calcium hydroxide pulping unit to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, adding calcium hydroxide slurry from the calcium hydroxide pulping unit into lime milk separating unit to separating clarified lime water or lime water and lime milk, adding water, chlorine and calcium carbonate into hypochlorous acid reaction unit, reacting water and chlorine to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride, and then reacting calcium carbonate and hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide to obtain hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid, and entering carbon dioxide from the hypochlorous acid reaction unit and the clarified lime water or lime water from the lime milk separation unit into carbon dioxide recovery unit, and reacting clarified lime water or lime water with carbon dioxide to obtain calcium carbonate and water.
   USE - The chlorine-free hypochlorous acid is useful for producing epoxide by monoacid chlorohydrin method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The chlorine-free hypochlorous acid: generates no by-product chloropropane or ethyl chloride.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid comprises (i) adding Water and calcium hydroxide into calcium hydroxide pulping unit to obtain calcium hydroxide slurry, (ii) adding calcium hydroxide slurry from the calcium hydroxide pulping unit into lime milk separating unit to separating clarified lime water or lime water and lime milk, (iii) adding water, chlorine and calcium carbonate into hypochlorous acid reaction unit, reacting water and chlorine to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride, and then reacting calcium carbonate and hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide to obtain hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid, (iv) entering carbon dioxide from the hypochlorous acid reaction unit and the clarified lime water or lime water from the lime milk separation unit into carbon dioxide recovery unit, and reacting clarified lime water or lime water with carbon dioxide to obtain calcium carbonate and water, (v) separating calcium carbonate and water from the carbon dioxide recovery unit into the calcium carbonate sedimentation unit to obtain calcium carbonate precipitation and water or low-concentration clarified lime water, (vi) entering calcium carbonate or calcium carbonate aqueous slurry from the calcium carbonate sedimentation unit into hypochlorous acid reaction unit, (vii) recycling water or low-concentration clarified lime water from the calcium carbonate sedimentation unit into the calcium hydroxide pulping unit, (viii) recycling part of the lime milk from the lime milk separation unit to the calcium hydroxide pulping unit, and saponification reaction process of chlorohydrin epoxide production used as a saponification agent, the lime milk from the lime milk separation unit or all of it is used as the saponification agent in the saponification reaction process of epoxide production by the dechlorohydrin method, completely recycling the lime milk from the lime milk separation unit or the calcium hydroxide pulping unit, and (ix) chlorohydrination reaction process to obtain hydrogen chloride-free hypochlorous acid from the hypochlorous acid reaction unit (5).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lime milk separation unit is not provided, and the clarified lime water or lime water and lime milk are prepared by the calcium hydroxide pulping unit.

PN CN115059437-A
TI Improving yield and effective sealing of carbon            dioxide with multi-element impurity by using carbon            dioxide, involves selecting impurity gas, using phase            envelope line of carbon dioxide gas containing            multi-element impurity, and defining phase state change            characteristic.
AU CHEN X
   WANG Z
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG L
   YANG B
   CAO C
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
GA 2022C24457
AB    NOVELTY - Improving the yield and the effective sealing                of the carbon dioxide (CO2) with multi-element                impurity by using CO2involves selecting the                impurity gas containing a certain concentration of                CO2, impurity gas concentration is divided into                high, low, three concentration; using the phase                envelope line of the CO2 gas containing                multi-element impurity calculated by the SRK state                equation, and defining the phase state change                characteristic in the formula, establishing                withered gas reservoir model, obtaining the                production history data of the gas reservoir, using                numerical simulation technology to carry out                simulation calculation to each factor under                different parameter conditions to the dry gas                reservoir CO2 sequest/storage performance each                factor of the dry and exhaust gas reservoir                CO2sequest/storage performance.
   USE - Method for improving the yield and the                effective sealing of the carbon dioxide (CO2) with                multi-element impurity by using CO2.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the separation and                purification cost of carbon dioxide (CO2), and                improves the gas storage recovery rate and the                comprehensive benefit of carbon dioxide utilization                and sequest/storage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improving the yield and the effective sealing                of the carbon dioxide (CO2) with multi-element                impurity by using CO2 involves selecting the                impurity gas containing a certain concentration of                CO2, impurity gas concentration is divided into                high, low, three concentration; using the phase                envelope line of the CO2 gas containing                multi-element impurity calculated by the SRK state                equation, and defining the phase state change                characteristic in the formula i.e.                P=(RT/V-b)-(a/V(V+b)) where P is the system                pressure, Pa, R is the ideal gas constant,                8.314J/(mol.K); T is the temperature of the system,                K, V is the system molar volume, m3/mol, a and b                are state equation coefficients, establishing                withered gas reservoir model, obtaining the                production history data of the gas reservoir, using                numerical simulation technology to carry out                simulation calculation to each factor under                different parameter conditions to the dry gas                reservoir CO2sequest/storage performance each                factor of the dry and exhaust gas reservoir                CO2sequest/storage performance, comprises                CO2sequestment, the gas molar fraction in the gas                reservoir CO2 sequest/storage period, by comparing                the simulation calculation result, respectively                defining the influence of different parameters on                the dry air reservoir CO2sequest/storage                performance, analyzing each factor to improve the                influence degree of the withered and exhausted gas                reservoir carbon sequest, according to the analysis                result definitely containing multi- impurity                CO2improves the measure and direction of the                exhaustion gas reservoir recovery rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The CO2gas with high                  impurity concentration is low, and the                  composition is composed of components according                  to the volume ratio 96% CO2+ 1% nitrogen (N2) +                  1% argon (Ar) + 1% hydrogen (H2) + 0.5 % hydrogen                  sulfide (H2S) +0.5% sulfur dioxide (SO2), 85%                  carbon dioxide (CO2) + 10% nitrogen (N2) + 2%                  oxygen (O2), 1% Ar + 1% Ar + 1% H2+ 0.5% H2S+0.5%                  SO2, 75% CO2+ 15% N2+ 3% O2, 4% water (H2O) +1%                  Ar + 1% H2+ 0.5% H2S +0.5% SO2. The parameter                  comprises injection rate, initial recovery rate,                  and critical concentration of impurity gas in the                  gas. The influence of different injection rates                  is considered, and the other parameters are kept                  unchanged and a series of injection rates is set,                  analyzing the influence of the change of the                  CO2injection rate of the withered gas reservoir                  to the CO2sequest/storage performance of the                  withered and exhausted gas, and determining the                  influence degree of the injection rate to the                  sequest/storage performance.

TI Product comprising isotactic polypropylene homopolymers or copolymers is produced by zero carbon dioxide emission process comprising converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene and butene using renewable electric power and polymerizing.

   LAWRENCE D
   FOWLER J N
AE REXTAC LLC (REXT-Non-standard)
GA 202234807V
AB    NOVELTY - Product comprising isotactic polypropylene homopolymers or copolymers is produced by a zero carbon dioxide emission process comprising (a) converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and butene using renewable electric power in an oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant 10comprising an ethylene plant, 2-butene plant, and a propylene plant, and (b) polymerizing the propylene and optionally the ethylene in a polypropylene plant while applying a propylene polymerization technology, using renewable electric power, and scrubbing carbon dioxide from any fired heater or boiler stack gasses to emit substantially zero carbon dioxide atmospheric emissions.
   USE - Method for producing product comprising isotactic polypropylene homopolymers or copolymers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the product with zero emission of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, thus environmentally-friendly process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Product comprising isotactic polypropylene homopolymers or copolymers is produced by a zero carbon dioxide emission process comprising (a) converting alkanes to ethylene, propylene, and butene using renewable electric power in an oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant comprising an ethylene plant, 2-butene plant, and a propylene plant, comprising (i) providing an ethylene plant comprising an oxidation reactor 15and a first fractionation train and converting natural gas and oxygen in the oxidation reactor to produce an oxidation reactor effluent comprising ethylene and passing the oxidation reactor effluent to the first fractionation train to recover a first ethylene stream, a second ethylene stream, and a third ethylene stream, (ii) providing 2-butene plant comprising a dimerization unit 17 and a second fractionation train and converting a portion of the first ethylene stream in the dimerization unit to produce a dimerization unit effluent comprising 2-butene and passing the dimerization unit effluent to the second fractionation train to produce a 2-butene stream, (iii) providing a propylene plant comprising a metathesis reactor and a third fractionation train and converting the 2-butene stream and a portion of the second ethylene stream in the metathesis reactor to produce a metathesis reactor effluent comprising propylene and passing the metathesis reactor effluent to the third fractionation train to recover the propylene, and (b) polymerizing the propylene and optionally the ethylene in a polypropylene plant while applying a propylene polymerization technology, using renewable electric power, and scrubbing carbon dioxide from any fired heater or boiler stack gasses to emit substantially zero carbon dioxide atmospheric emissions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an oxidative-coupling of methane plant.
   Oxidative-coupling of methane (OCM) plant (10)
   Alkanes (14)
   Oxidation reactor (15)
   Dimerization unit (17)
   Unconverted methane (51)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural gas comprises methane, and the oxidation reactor effluent comprises unconverted methane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The dimerization unit effluent comprises unconverted ethylene, and the method further comprises recycling a stream of unconverted ethylene from the second fractionation train to the dimerization unit. The metathesis reactor effluent comprises unconverted ethylene and unconverted 2-butene, and the method further comprises recycling a stream of unconverted ethylene and unconverted 2-butene from the third fractionation train to the metathesis reactor. The dimerization unit effluent comprises 2-butene and by-products. The metathesis reactor effluent comprises propylene, unconverted ethylene, and unconverted 2-butene. The polypropylene plant further comprises hydrocarbon-fueled heaters and/or boilers, where the oxygen enhanced combustion is employed to limit nitrogen-based gas volumes.

The carbon dioxide absorption unit (140) for absorbing and removing carbon dioxide by chemically reacting by injecting aqueous ammonia from the aqueous ammonia production unit with the exhaust gas that has passed through the washing unit to convert carbon dioxide into ammonium bicarbonate(aqueous). The combustion air receiver (160) for temporarily storing the exhaust gas that has passed through the carbon dioxide absorber to remove the pulsation, and mixing with combustion air and supplying air to each cylinder of the marine engine.

PT P
PN CN111803696-A; CN111803696-B
TI Preparing patchouli oil nano composite film useful for repairing skin wound comprises e.g. placing mesoporous silicon nanoparticles into autoclave, sealing and injecting the patchouli oil through nozzle A and introducing the carbon dioxide.
AU JIA J
   GE F
   LI H
   QIN C
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   GUANGZHOU ZHONGDA NANSHA TECHNOLOGICAL (UYSY-C)
GA 2020A5069M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing patchouli oil nano composite film comprises e.g. (i) preparing patchouli oil-mesoporous silica nanoparticles by supercritical carbon dioxide cyclic permeation technology (a) placing mesoporous silicon nanoparticles into the autoclave and sealing it and injecting patchouli oil through nozzle A, (ii) introducing the carbon dioxide through nozzle B to increase the pressure to the preset pressure and at the same time raising the temperature to the preset temperature and starting the infiltration loading process of patchouli oil in the supercritical state, (iii) releasing the system from the supercritical state through cooling and pressure relief operations after the system reaches equilibrium, loading the patchouli oil in mesoporous silica nanoparticles and recovering the carbon dioxide and unloading patchouli oil are at the same time.
   USE - The patchouli oil nano composite film is useful for repairing skin wound (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes the recovery and recycling of carbon dioxide and improves the utilization rate; and the obtained product has excellent tensile strength, softness and moisturizing properties, high-loading efficiency and long-lasting; achieves long-term antibacterial and antiinflammatory effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing patchouli oil nano composite film comprises (i) preparing patchouli oil-mesoporous silica nanoparticles by supercritical carbon dioxide cyclic permeation technology (a) placing mesoporous silicon nanoparticles into the autoclave and sealing it and injecting patchouli oil through nozzle A, (ii) introducing the carbon dioxide through nozzle B to increase the pressure to the preset pressure and at the same time raising the temperature to the preset temperature and starting the infiltration loading process of patchouli oil in the supercritical state, (iii) releasing the system from the supercritical state through cooling and pressure relief operations after the system reaches equilibrium, loading the patchouli oil in mesoporous silica nanoparticles and recovering the carbon dioxide and unloading patchouli oil are at the same time, (iv) repeating the above steps for 3-4 times to obtain patchouli oil-mesoporous silica nanoparticles with high drug loading patchouli oil nanocomposite film, (ii.a) preparing 1-4% chitosan solution and 8-12% polyvinyl alcohol solution respectively and then mixing the two in a specific solution mass ratio to obtain a mixed solution, (ii.b) adding a specific proportion of patchouli oil-mesoporous silica nanoparticles prepared in step (i) to the mixed solution and mixing them uniformly with high-speed magnetic stirring to obtain a colloidal suspension, and (iii) spreading the colloidal suspension obtained in resultant step in a polytetrafluoroethylene mold into a film shape and freeze-drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i.b), the preset pressure is 18-35 MPa and the preset temperature is 35-50 degrees C. In step (i.a) and step (i.b) the nozzle A and nozzle B are coaxial nozzles or two parallel and independent nozzles. In step (i.c), the cooling operation is to reduce the temperature of the kettle body to below 20 degrees C by condensing water and the condensing water temperature is 2-6 degrees C, the pressure relief operation means that the pressure drops to normal pressure. In the step (11.b) the stirring speed of the high-speed magnetic stirring process is 600 rpm, and the stirring time is 20 min. In step (ii.c), the freeze-drying temperature is at -35 degrees C and vacuuming for 12 hours, and freeze-drying for 48 hours. The mesoporous silicon nanoparticles is prepared by adding 3 g cetyltrimethylammonium bromide to 60 ml deionized water in a 95 degrees C water bath and stirring at 350 rotations/minute (rpm) to completely dissolving, adding 120 mu l diethanolamine and mixing, adding 4.5 ml tetraethyl orthosilicate within 45 minutes, continue the reaction for 1 hour and allowing to stand to cooling to obtain a light blue emulsion and diluting with methanol and centrifuging, then soaking in ethanol/concentrated sulfuric acid system at 60 degrees C for 24 hours then adding deionized water and centrifuging for many times until the solution is neutral to obtain mesoporous silicon material, finally rinsing the mesoporous silicon material with ethanol and drying in an oven at 55 degrees C for 6 hours to obtain a dried mesoporous silicon nanoparticle product. Preferred Components: The ratio of chitosan solution to polyvinyl alcohol solution is 6:4-8:2. The proportion of patchouli oil-mesoporous silica nanoparticles added is 0.5-3.0%. The mesoporous silicon nanoparticles have a MCM-41 type nano-spherical mesoporous structure and the particle size is below 100 nm.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PT P
PN CN110521588-A
TI Promoting pollination of radish by planting radish            in plant potting medium, carrying out vernalization,            growing radish, transplanting to field, introducing            bees, simultaneously introducing carbon dioxide and            pulling out seedlings.
AU JIN W
   LI S
AE NINGXIA TIANRONG MODERN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2019A3848U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for promoting pollination of radish                involves (a) planting radish in a plant potting                medium and carrying out vernalization to the radish                plant for 3 months, (b) growing the vernalized                radish in plant medium for 2-3 months into a fleshy                radish, (c) transplanting the radish into field,                watering, placing vents on both sides of the shed,                installing insect-proof nets, sterilizing,                observing the color of the leaf and seedlings of                the radish and petal size to compare and identify                different plants and pulling out, (d) introducing                bees to an empty shed, adapting for 4-6 days,                releasing the bees in the early stage of flowering,                simultaneously introducing carbon dioxide into the                sealed shed after the sunset, opening vents to                release carbon dioxide, stopping carbon dioxide                passing and evacuating bees and (e) pulling out the                seedlings, placing in their respective place and                collecting the seeds when they become white.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting pollination                of radish.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces growth period, saves labor                and land, adopts dense planting, has high yield of                seed, increases pollination rate, is                cost-effective, and has high purity of hybrid                seeds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for promoting pollination of radish by                using carbon dioxide and bees involves (a) planting                the radish in a plant potting medium, carrying out                vernalization to the radish plant for 3 months in                night at 1-5 degrees C, during the radish seeding                stage and completing the vernalization, (b) growing                the vernalized radish in plant medium for 2-3                months into a fleshy radish, (c) transplanting the                radish into field, watering in time after                transplantation, placing vents on both sides of the                shed before flowering and after transplanting,                installing insect-proof nets at the entrance and                exit of the vents, using sterilization and                insecticide fumigants to sterilize and kill                insects, observing the color of the leaf and                seedlings of the radish and petal size to compare                and identify different plants, pulling out, marking                flowering plants with no flowers and checking after                flowering, (d) introducing bees to an empty shed                freely after transplantation for 30-50 days,                adapting for 4-6 days, releasing the bees in the                early stage of flowering, simultaneously                introducing carbon dioxide into the sealed shed                within half an hour after the sunset, maintaining                for 2 hours, opening the vents to release carbon                dioxide for 20-30 days, stopping carbon dioxide                passing and evacuating bees and (e) pulling out the                seedlings after completing the pollination for 1                month, placing in their respective place and                collecting the seeds when they become white.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The method involves                  subjecting the bees to a density of 4000 per acre                  and introducing carbon dioxide having a                  concentration of 4-5%. The carbon dioxide is                  released through a micro-spraying belt provided                  on the top of the shed. The ratio of the female                  parent to the male parent of the fleshy radish is                  4-3. Preferred Components: The plant medium                  comprises a seedling tray.

PN US2018264432-A1; US10252242-B2
TI Adsorbent used for process to remove carbon            dioxide from gas mixture containing carbon dioxide,            comprises alumina, and carbonate compound, and one or            more alkali metals, and where adsorbent is one or more            alkali metals,.
AU LAU G C
   SORENSEN E M
   TAYLOR F W
   GOLDEN T C
   QUINN R
   CASTEEL W J
AE AIR PROD & CHEM INC (AIRP-C)
GA 2018783415
AB    NOVELTY - Adsorbent comprises alumina, carbonate                compound, one or more alkali metals, where                adsorbent is 0.5-10 wt.% one or more alkali metals,                where surface area of the adsorbent from 220-400                m2/g, adsorbent has a carbonate to alumina                intensity ratio, R, the carbonate to alumina                intensity ratio, R, having a value to 0.0150, where                carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is as                determined by fourier transform infrared (FTIR)                spectroscopy of a crushed sample of the adsorbent,                where carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is a                ratio of a peak absorbance intensity for carbonate,                AIcarbonate, to a peak absorbance intensity for                alumina, AIalumina, each peak absorbance intensity                obtained after subtracting a baseline signal                intensity,-where the peak absorbance intensity for                alumina, AIalumina, is observed at a wavenumber in                a range from 420-520 per centiometer, and the peak                absorbance intensity for carbonate, AIcarbonate is                observed at a wavenumber of 1300-1400 per                centimeter.
   USE - Adsorbent used for process to remove carbon                dioxide from a gas mixture containing carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent which has high air                recoveries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for making adsorbent particles                for adsorbing carbon dioxide, which involves                washing alkali-promoted activated alumina materials                comprising a carbonate compound with water, drying                the washed alumina materials to form the adsorbent                particles, where washed and dried adsorbent                particles has carbonate to alumina intensity ratio,                R2, which is smaller than the carbonate to alumina                intensity ratio, R1, of the alkali-promoted                activated alumina materials before washing,                carbonate to alumina intensity ratio is as                determined by Fourier Transform infrared                spectroscopy of a crushed sample of the respective                washed and dried adsorbent particles and a crushed                sample of the alkali-promoted activated alumina                materials before washing, carbonate to alumina                intensity ratio is a ratio of a peak absorbance                intensity for carbonate, AIcarbonate, to a peak                absorbance intensity for alumina, AIalumina, (i.e.                R=ALcarbonate/AIalumina) each absorbance intensity                obtained after subtracting a baseline signal                intensity, where the peak absorbance intensity for                alumina, AIalumina, is observed at an FTIR                wavenumber is 420-520 per centimeter, and the peak                absorbance intensity for carbonate, AIcarbonate, is                observed at an FTIR wavenumber from 1300-1400 per                centimeter; and
   (2) a method for removing carbon dioxide from                a gas mixture containing carbon dioxide, which                involves passing the gas mixture containing carbon                dioxide into a bed containing the adsorbent, and                withdrawing a CO2-depleted gas from the bed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent is                  90-99 wt. % alumina. Preferred Components: The                  carbonate compound is an alkali carbonate. The                  alkali carbonate is potassium carbonate. The                  water is deionized water. The alkali-promoted                  activated alumina materials are washed                  sufficiently to produce adsorbent particles                  having a carbonate to alumina intensity ratio,                  R1, having a value to 0.0150. The alkali-promoted                  activated alumina materials are washed with water                  until the alkali-promoted activated alumina                  materials have a pH in solution of 9.5, forming                  washed alumina materials, the pH in solution as                  determined by measuring the pH of an equilibrated                  2 liter solution of deionized water containing                  100 g of the washed alumina materials. Preferred                  Conditions: The value of the carbonate to alumina                  intensity ratio is 0.003-0.0150. The gas mixture                  containing CO2 has a concentration of carbon                  dioxide from 5 ppmv CO2 to 1 mole % CO2. The gas                  mixture contains oxygen, nitrogen, and water. The                  gas mixture is a feed to a cryogenic air                  separation unit.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The system (2) has a cracking unit (5) in which slurry organic waste is oxidatively decomposed into a gas component containing carbon dioxide and an aqueous solution at high temperature and high pressure. A catalyst furnace (6) in which the aqueous solution is mineralized by a predetermined mineralization catalyst. A carbon dioxide gas concentrating and separating unit (9) having a concentrating vessel in which the gas component and the carbonic acid gas adsorbent are accommodated and from which the carbonic acid gas adsorbed by the zeolite is separated from the carbonic acid gas adsorbing agent. The reaction vessel contains the extracted carbon dioxide gas, hydrogen gas and a predetermined methanol producing catalyst. The methanol generating unit (10) is configured for generating methanol and water by heating the inside of reaction vessel containing materials to a predetermined temperature range and that is pressurized to a predetermined pressure range.
   USE - System for manufacturing methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - A gas component containing carbon dioxide gas and an aqueous solution are obtained from organic waste by oxidative decomposition in a cracking furnace, and the obtained aqueous solution is mineralized by treatment in a catalyst furnace. A carbon dioxide gas of high concentration with stable quality is obtained using the carbon dioxide gas concentration separating unit from the obtained gas component. The carbon dioxide gas, hydrogen gas and a predetermined methanol producing catalyst are reacted in a predetermined temperature range and a predetermined pressure range, thus methanol having stable quality is obtained. The stable quality aviation fuel is obtained by aviation fuel generating unit using methanol.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system has a methanol producing catalyst (10b) which is copper/ zinc oxide based. The predetermined temperature range is 200 degrees C or higher and 300 degrees C or lower, and the pressure range defined in the preceding paragraph is 3MPa or higher and 7MPa or lower. One of combustible refuse and an organic refuse is used as said organic waste. The disaster relief wood, construction materials, and other construction materials are used for the production of the product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for manufacturing aviation fuel, has aviation fuel production unit (11) which is configured for producing aviation fuel using methanol produced by the methanol production system and a predetermined fuel conversion catalyst.

PN CN113527021-A
TI Preparation of formamide compound comprises using carbon dioxide, amine compounds and phenylsilane used as raw materials and under the action of nanoporous palladium catalyst.
AU BAO M
   WANG J
   FENG X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021C47567
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of formamide compound (I) or (II) comprises using carbon dioxide, amine compounds (III) or (IV), and phenylsilane used as raw materials and under the action of a nanoporous palladium catalyst. Compound (I) or (II) is prepared by adding nanoporous palladium catalyst, amine compound, phenylsilane, organic solvent and water to reaction kettle in sequence, filling with carbon dioxide at 0.5-3.0 MPa, oil bath reacting at 50-120degreesC, cooling to room temperature, releasing remaining carbon dioxide, rotary evaporating, separating target compound by silica gel column chromatography, using acetone, cleaning nanoporous palladium catalyst, and vacuum drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing formamide compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has mild reaction condition, is simple and cheap, and has good functional group compatibility and reproducible carbon source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of formamide compound of formula (I) or (II) comprises using carbon dioxide, amine compound of formula (III) or (IV), and phenylsilane used as raw materials and under the action of a nanoporous palladium catalyst. Compound (I) or (II) is prepared by adding nanoporous palladium catalyst, amine compound, phenylsilane, organic solvent and water to reaction kettle in sequence, filling with carbon dioxide at 0.5-3.0 MPa, oil bath reacting at 50-120degreesC, cooling to room temperature, releasing remaining carbon dioxide, rotary evaporating, separating target compound by silica gel column chromatography, using acetone, cleaning nanoporous palladium catalyst, and vacuum drying. The eluent in silica gel column chromatography is petroleum ether and ethyl acetate at volume ratio of 2:1. The molar ratio of the amine compound to the phenylsilane is 1:1.0-1:5.0. The molar ratio of the amine compound to nanoporous palladium catalyst is 1:0.01-1:0.5. The molar concentration of the amine compound in the organic solvent is 0.01-1 mmol/mL. The volume ratio of water to organic solvent is 1:100-500.
   R1=H, Me, OMe, OEt, halo, tert-butyl, n-butyl, hydroxy, methylthio, phenyl, cyano, trifluoromethyl, or phenoxy;
   R2=H, Me, Et, Bz, allyl or isopropyl;
   R3=H, Bz, cyclohexyl or allyl; and
   R4=Bz, cyclohexyl, allyl, pyridyl, hexyl or quinoline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is acetonitrile, 1,4-dioxane, tetrahydrofuran, methanol, ethyl acetate, dichloromethane or n-hexane.

TI Cooling, heating and power system for Brayton cycle and absorption refrigeration cycle, has regulating valve and hot side inlet of heat exchanger that are connected through shunt branch of hot side outlet of high temperature regenerator in Brayton cycle power generation and heating module.
AU CHEN W
   TANG X
   QIAN Y
   ZHANG S
   YAN J
   WANG J
   LIU M
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021986896
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle power generation and heating module and lithium bromide absorption refrigeration module. The supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle power generation and heating module uses carbon dioxide as a working fluid. The supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle power generation and heating module is connected to the generator (13) of the lithium bromide absorption refrigeration module through a third regulating valve (12), so as to transfer the heat of the supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle to the lithium bromide absorption refrigeration cycle for utilization. The fourth regulating valve (21) and the hot side inlet of the heat exchanger (22) are connected through the shunt branch of the hot side outlet of the high temperature regenerator (6) in the supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle power generation and heating module. The heat is transferred to the circulating water to supply heat to the outside.
   USE - Cooling, heating and power system for Brayton cycle and absorption refrigeration cycle.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes the adjustment and flexible supply of electric energy, heat energy and cold energy under different demands, reduces the loss of working fluid and avoids frequent replenishment of working fluid. The working fluids used in the combined cooling, heating and power system have non-toxicity, good chemical stability and easy access. The Brayton cycle has high efficiency. The supercritical carbon dioxide Brayton cycle has a variety of other arrangements, which is flexibly selected according to the actual situation to improve the thermal economy of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a working method for combined cooling, heating and power system coupled with Brayton cycle and absorption refrigeration cycle.

PT P
PN WO2018116774-A1; JP2018104809-A; JP6711334-B2
TI Carbon-dioxide reducing device has photovoltaic elements whose light-receiving surfaces are not in direct contact with electrolytic solution and receive light from light source without being mediated by electrolytic solution.
AU YAMAGUCHI HITOSHI
   YAMAGUCHI H
AE DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
   DENSO CORP (NPDE-C)
GA 201850974L
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide reduction device (1) has a photovoltaic device (12) which generates an internal photoelectric effect by irradiating light. A reduction electrode (14) is electrically connected with the photovoltaic device and generates a carbon compound by the reductive reaction of a carbon dioxide. The oxidation electrode (13) generates oxygen and a hydrogen ion by the oxidation reaction of water is provided. One of the oxidation electrodes or the reduction electrodes are directly provided on the surface of the photovoltaic elements that are on the side opposite from the light-receiving surface and the other of the oxidation electrodes and reduction electrodes are provided at a distance from the photovoltaic elements. The light-receiving surfaces of the photovoltaic elements are not in direct contact with the electrolytic solution and receive light from the light source without being mediated by the electrolytic solution.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide reducing device.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the photovoltaic device which light-receives sunlight is positioned to the exterior of electrolyte solution, the light is directly irradiated to photovoltaic device, without being absorbed into electrolyte solution. The photovoltaic device can be highly efficient, can absorb light, and can enlarge the electrical energy which the photovoltaic device generates. Hence, the production efficiency of carbon compound can be improved. The temperature rise of the electrolyte solution of oxidation electrode vicinity can be carried out by integrating photovoltaic device and oxidation electrode using the heat which is generated in the photovoltaic device. The heat g of the semiconductor by sunlight can be utilized effectively, by using concentrating type photovoltaic device. Hence, the oxidation reaction of the water in an oxidation electrode can be stimulated, and the generation efficiency of carbon compound can be improved. Since the photovoltaic device is not in contact with electrolyte solution, a photovoltaic device does not get corroded with electrolyte solution. Therefore, the durability of carbon-dioxide reducing device can be improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of the carbon-dioxide reducing device.
   Carbon-dioxide reducing device (1)
   Piping (10,11)
   Photovoltaic device (12)
   Oxidation electrode (13)
   Reduction electrode (14)

PN CN112221362-A
TI Quaternized polysulfone homogeneous membrane with ion cluster structure useful for carbon dioxide separation in carbon dioxide/methane system comprises nano-scale ion cluster structure, and cavity etched by template.
AU WU H
   JIANG Z
   QU Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202109161H
AB    NOVELTY - Quaternized polysulfone homogeneous membrane with ion cluster structure comprises nano-scale ion cluster structure initiated by a template, and cavity etched by template used to greatly reduce the film thickness, where the quaternized polysulfone is polysulfone through chloromethylation reaction and quaternization reaction, the triethylamine is quaternization reagent, the ratio is 6 ml/1 g, the template is TMA-POSS, and the particle size is 1-3 nm.
   USE - The quaternized polysulfone homogeneous membrane is useful for carbon dioxide separation in carbon dioxide/methane system in wet state (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The homogeneous membrane: has strong controllability, has high flux and high selectivity to carbon dioxide in a wet state, and has good mechanical properties and operational stability. The method: uses easy availability of raw materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the quaternized polysulfone homogeneous membrane with ion cluster structure (a) dissolving polysulfone in chloroform solvent to form 0.02 g/ml solution, then adding paraformaldehyde to the solution in a mass ratio of 3:4, stirring vigorously at 55 degrees C until the solution is clear, then adding trimethylchlorosilane to the solution in a mass-volume ratio of 1 g:3 ml to the polysulfone, then adding tin tetrachloride as a catalyst, reacting at 55 degrees C for 96 hours, adding the reacted solution quickly into ethanol to obtain white solid, filtering, vacuum drying at 45 degrees C to finally obtain chloromethylated polysulfone, dissolving chloromethylated polysulfone in N,N-dimethylformamide, then adding triethylamine as a quaternization reagent in a mass-volume ratio of 1 g:6 ml, stirring at 35 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain solution A, then dissolving tetramethylammonium-polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxanes in N,N-dimethylformamide, and ultrasonically dispersing for 20 minutes to obtain dispersion, then mixing the dispersion with solution A, where the mass ratio of tetramethylammonium-polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxanes to quaternized polysulfone is 0.01-0.05, and the mass fraction of quaternized polysulfone is 5%, stirring the mixed solution at a rate of 1000 revolutions/minute at room temperature for 12 hours, allowing to stand, degassing to obtain homogeneous casting solution, and adding homogeneous film casting solution into glass plate and drying at 60 degrees C to obtain a homogeneous film, (b) immersing homogeneous film into concentrated hydrofluoric acid solution for template etching, taking out after 2-2.5 hours, and rinsing the residual hydrofluoric acid on the membrane surface with a large amount of deionized water, and (c) immersing membrane material in 1 M potassium borate for ion exchange to obtain quaternized polysulfone membrane with borate ions as counter ions; and
   (2) use of quaternized polysulfone homogeneous membrane for carbon dioxide separation in carbon dioxide/methane system in wet state at 25 degrees C under a relative humidity of 83%, raw gas pressure of 1 bar, carbon dioxide flux of 776-1207 Barrer, and carbon dioxide/methane selectivity of 39-43.

TI Preparing carbon dioxide adsorption filter cloth,            comprises e.g. reacting raw material filter cloth in            ethanol aqueous solution, adding into pore-forming            agent aqueous solution, heating, ultrasonically            oscillating and post processing.
AU SUN S
   CHEN Z
   LIU D
   LIU Y
   HUANG G
   FAN H
   WANG L
   BAI Y
AE 718TH RES INST CHINA SHIPBUILDING IND CO (CSHI-C)
GA 202085839C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorption filter                cloth, comprises e.g. (i) adding raw material                filter cloth into ethanol aqueous solution and                reacting to obtain completely soaked filter cloth,                (ii) adding completely soaked filter cloth into                pore-forming agent aqueous solution, heating to                boiling state, ultrasonically oscillating and                completely reacting to obtain filter cloth having                pores, (iii) adding step (ii) product into                crosslinking agent aqueous solution, heating to                boil and completely reacting to obtain crosslinked                and fixed filter cloth, and (iv) adding step (iii)                product into aqueous solution of amine-based                compound, reacting, intermittently introducing                carbon dioxide gas in reaction process, stopping                introducing carbon dioxide gas and continuously                reacting to obtain final product, where the amount                of amine-based compound in aqueous solution of                amine-based compound is 5-50 wt.%, the amine-based                compound is polyethylene polyamine, hydrophilic                amino acid and/or polyethylene polyamide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide adsorption filter cloth.
   ADVANTAGE - The filter cloth has large adsorption                capacity, fast adsorption rate, and sufficient                adsorption performance and mechanical properties,                which meets basic requirement of different working                conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon dioxide adsorption filter                cloth, comprises (i) adding raw material filter                cloth into ethanol aqueous solution and reacting at                80-95 degrees C for 0.5-1 hour to obtain completely                soaked filter cloth, where the amount of water in                ethanol aqueous solution is 5-40 wt.%, the raw                material filter cloth is woven cloth, non-woven                cloth and/or gauze, and the material of the raw                material filter cloth is polyester, spandex, aramid                fibers and/or orlon, (ii) adding completely soaked                filter cloth into pore-forming agent aqueous                solution, heating to boiling state, maintaining for                1-5 hours, ultrasonically oscillating and                completely reacting to obtain filter cloth having                pores, where the amount of pore-forming agent in                pore-forming agent aqueous solution is 1-5 wt.%,                and the pore-forming agent is sulfuric acid,                hydrochloric acid and/or organic acid, (iii) adding                step (ii) product into crosslinking agent aqueous                solution, heating to boil, maintaining for 2-4                hours and completely reacting to obtain crosslinked                and fixed filter cloth, where the amount of                crosslinking agent in crosslinking agent aqueous                solution is 1-15 wt.% and the crosslinking agent is                nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound or                hydrazine hydrate, and (iv) adding step (iii)                product into aqueous solution of amine-based                compound, reacting at 80-100 degrees C for 1-4                hours, intermittently introducing carbon dioxide                gas in reaction process, stopping introducing                carbon dioxide gas and continuously reacting for                more than 0.5 hours to obtain final product, where                the amount of amine-based compound in aqueous                solution of amine-based compound is 5-50 wt.%, the
