TI Production of hydrogen involves reducing iron            oxide with hydrogen, carbonizing generated iron and            methane to generate iron carbide and hydrogen and            oxidizing water vapor to generate hydrogen and carbon            dioxide and feeding into storage tank.
AU HAO H
   CAO C
   GONG J
   CUI L
   SUN R
   SHI K
   LU K
   ZHANG M
   YAN Z
   HU M
   GAO R
AE UNIV INNER MONGOLIA (UNMO-C)
GA 2019A66804
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrogen involves feeding iron                oxide into main reaction tower, passing hydrogen                into bottom of main reaction tower, reacting with                iron oxide in tower at 100-700 degrees C to produce                pure iron and water vapor, feeding unreacted                hydrogen and generated water vapor into gas                separation device, cooling water vapor, converting                hydrogen into methane gas and feeding to main                reaction tower, carrying out carbonization reaction                of pure iron at 300-800 degrees C to generates iron                carbide and hydrogen, feeding unreacted methane and                generated hydrogen into gas separation device,                recycling separated methane, purifying hydrogen and                feeding into hydrogen storage tank, performing                oxidation reaction of water vapor and iron carbide                to generate iron oxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen                at 100-500 degrees C, feeding unreacted water                vapor, generated hydrogen and carbon dioxide into                gas separation device, recycling separated water                vapor and feeding separated hydrogen and carbon                dioxide to corresponding storage tanks.
   USE - Production of hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses iron oxide as cyclic reaction                material in which iron phases are mutually                converted to produce hydrogen, although a portion                of hydrogen is consumed in the process of                converting iron oxide to pure iron, more hydrogen                is generated in the process of carbonization of                pure iron by methane and oxidation of iron carbide                by water. The method uses less equipment and raw                materials and reduces energy consumption compared                to coal hydrogen production and natural gas                reforming hydrogen production method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of hydrogen involves using methane                and water as raw material, feeding iron oxide into                main reaction tower, passing hydrogen into bottom                of the main reaction tower, reacting with iron                oxide in the main reaction tower at 100-700 degrees                C to produce pure iron and water vapor where                unreacted hydrogen and generated water vapor leave                the top of the main reaction tower and feeding into                gas separation device, cooling water vapor and                hydrogen is circulated for use, converting hydrogen                into methane gas and feeding to the bottom of the                main reaction tower, carrying out carbonization                reaction of pure iron and methane at 300-800                degrees C to generates iron carbide and hydrogen                where unreacted methane and generated hydrogen                leave from the top of the main reaction tower and                feeding into gas separation device, recycling                separated methane, purifying hydrogen and feeding                into hydrogen storage tank, oxidizing water vapor                and iron carbide to generate iron oxide, carbon                dioxide and hydrogen where after all the pure iron                in the main reaction tower is converted into iron                carbide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen are switched                to methane gas and introducing from bottom of the                main reaction tower, reacting with iron carbide at                100-500 degrees C to generate iron oxide, carbon                dioxide and hydrogen where unreacted water vapor,                generated hydrogen and carbon dioxide leave from                the top of the main reaction tower, feeding into                gas separation device, recycling separated water                vapor and feeding separated hydrogen and carbon                dioxide to corresponding storage tanks.

TI Coking process involves combusting oxygen and coke            oven gas, carbonizing, pretreating obtained flue gas            containing carbon dioxide and sulfur dioxide,            desulfurizing, converting carbon dioxide into carbon            monoxide, and removing impurities.
AU WANG M
   WU S
AE MCC ACRE COKING & REFRACTORY ENG CONSULT (CMEG-C)
   ACRE COKING & REFRACTORY ENG CONSULTING (CMEG-C)
GA 201998460Y
AB    NOVELTY - Coking process involves fully combusting                oxygen and a coke oven gas and releasing heat in a                combustion chamber of a coke oven heating system,                heating, carbonizing, passing the obtained coke                oven flue gas comprising main components comprising                carbon dioxide and water, and sulfur dioxide into a                coke oven flue gas pretreatment system,                desulfurizing, passing the pre-treated gas into a                coke oven flue gas cooling system, passing into a                carbonization chamber, contacting carbon dioxide                and high-temperature coal coke, converting carbon                dioxide into carbon monoxide, discharging coke oven                gas generated in the carbonization chamber into a                gas collection and purification system, removing                impurities to obtain tar, ammonia, benzene products                and by-products, passing the purified coke oven gas                into a hydrogen extraction system, separating                hydrogen, and using the coke oven gas after                hydrogen extraction as product gas, and returning a                portion of coke oven gas to coke oven heating                system for use as gas.
   USE - Coking process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables clean and green coking. The                method can reduce or avoid the generation of                atmospheric pollutants during coking, produce clean                energy products, improve the resource utilization                level of the coking, and reduce greenhouse gas                emissions during coking.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Coking process involves fully combusting (i)                oxygen and a coke oven gas and releasing heat in a                combustion chamber of a coke oven heating system,                heating coal materials in a carbonization chamber                of a coal high-temperature carbonization system,                and carbonizing, passing (ii) the coke oven flue                gas comprising main components comprising carbon                dioxide and water, and small amount of sulfur                dioxide generated by the combustion of the coke                oven heating system into a coke oven flue gas                pretreatment system, desulfurizing the coke oven                flue gas, and recovering the waste heat, passing                (iii) the pre-treated coke oven flue gas into a                coke oven flue gas cooling system, and condensing                water vapor in the flue gas into liquid condensed                water and recycling through a condensed water                recovery system, passing (iv) the coke oven flue                gas comprising carbon dioxide after the removal of                water vapor into a carbonization chamber of the                coal high-temperature carbonization system,                contacting carbon dioxide and high-temperature coal                coke, converting carbon dioxide into carbon                monoxide, and discharging the coke oven gas                generated in the carbonization chamber into a gas                collection and purification system, cooling (v) and                purifying the coke oven gas, removing the                impurities to obtain corresponding tar, ammonia,                benzene products and by-products, and passing the                purified coke oven gas into a hydrogen extraction                system, and separating (vii) hydrogen from the                hydrogen extraction system as the product hydrogen,                and using the coke oven gas after the hydrogen                extraction as the product gas, and returning a                portion of the coke oven gas to the coke oven                heating system for use as gas. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for system for coking process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: In the coke oven                  heating system, the combustion-supporting gas                  uses pure oxygen, and the coke oven gas after                  purification and hydrogen extraction comprises                  methane, carbon monoxide and other                  hydrocarbons.

AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of carbon dioxide into chemical energy carriers and products involves (a1) providing a synthesis gas (1) comprising hydrogen (2), carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, (a2) performing Fischer-Tropsch reaction of the synthesis gas to convert the synthesis gas into a Fischer-Tropsch reaction product comprising a fuel fraction (i), a wax fraction (ii), a gaseous by-product phase (iii) and an aqueous phase (iv), (a31) optionally hydrogenating the Fischer-Tropsch reaction product with metered addition of hydrogen, (a32) performing multistage separation of the Fischer-Tropsch reaction product or the hydrogenated product to separate into fractions (i, iii and iv), (a4) performing methanation of the gaseous by-products in the fraction (f3) with addition of hydrogen, and (a5) optionally processing the fractions (i, iii and iv).
   USE - Conversion of carbon dioxide into chemical energy carriers and products.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and efficient conversion of carbon dioxide into chemical energy carriers and products having desired calorific value and Wobbe index.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a plant, comprises a device (d1) configured to provide the synthesis gas, a Fischer-Tropsch reactor (D), optionally a hydrogenation reactor, a multistage separation device (A,B,C) preferably composed of several individual separation devices arranged one after the other, a methanation reactor (E), and optionally a device for introducing the methanation product into a natural gas network. The components of the plant are in operative connection with one another.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the plant.
   Synthesis gas (1)
   Hydrogen (2)
   Separation devices (A,B,C)
   Fischer-Tropsch reactor (D)
   Methanation reactor (E)

   Wax faction (ii)
   Gaseous by-product phase (iii)
   Aqueous phase (iv)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The device (d1) is a microstructured reactor for performing exothermic reaction between two or more reactants passed in the form of fluids over one or more catalyst(s). The microstructured reactor comprises at least one stack sequence of at least one layer having one or more catalyst(s) for performing at least one exothermic reaction, at least one layer divided into two or more cooling fields, and at least one layer having distributor structures with lines for distributing the coolant, with connections for supplying coolant to the manifold structure ducts and for connection to the cooling fields, connections for removing the heated coolant from the cooling fields, and ducts and connections for removing the heated coolant from the stack sequence. The separation device is distillation device. The methanation device comprises a water separation device or a downstream water separation device. The water separation device is device for condensation and phase separation or distillation device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The product from the process (a4) is fed directly into natural gas network as a synthetic gas which can be fed into a natural gas network. The synthesis gas is formed by high-temperature co-electrolysis of water and carbon dioxide. The synthesis gas is processed by carbon dioxide activation by hydrogen from water electrolysis via reverse water gas shift reaction. The process (a4) involves performing methanation of the obtained product, condensing water and separating.

TI Preparing carbon nano-tube by carbon dioxide-methane reforming technology, comprises e.g. preheating raw material gas and desulfurization treatment, and introducing carbon dioxide and methane respectively into pre-heater for mixing.
AU HE Y
   ZHANG Y
   WU W
   ZHAO Y
   WANG Y
   LI G
   LIANG Z
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202219280A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon nanotubes comprises introducing carbon dioxide and methane into a pre-heater for mixing and preheating, and then introducing into hydrodesulfurization device to remove sulfur. The desulfurized raw material gas is preheated through the heat exchanger, introduced the reforming reactor after reaching the reaction temperature, and prepared the carbon nanotube and the synthesis gas in the catalyst bed layer to obtain the crude carbon nanofiber product. The gas is passed after reaction in the recovering reactor flows into the heat exchange unit for heating the raw material, where the waste heat boiler is used to recycle the heat. The generated steam is used to preheat the raw materials in the preheater, collected synthesis gas, and passed synthesis gas through heat exchange to recover the heat of the waste gas boiler.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon nanotube by carbon dioxide-methane reforming technology used in advanced carbon material preparation technology and industrial catalytic technology field, preferably mechanical, electronic, material, energy, and catalysis fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The process combines the carbon dioxide-methane reforming technology and carbon nano-tube preparation, using carbon dioxide in greenhouse gas, methane as raw material, with high added value and synthesis gas, realizing the high utilization of the raw material. The process has simple preparation technique, rich raw material such as natural gas, coke oven gas, coal bed gas, oil field gas, refinery gas, methanol and Fischer-Tropsch synthetic purge gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of the preparation process of the carbon nanotube. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Preheater (1)
   Hydrogenation desulfurization device (2)
   Heat exchanger (3)
   Reforming reactor (4)
   Separating device (5)

TI Method for determining carbon dioxide concentration in measurement gas, involves adaptive setting of fluid delivery unit taking into account airway pressure in main line to generate volume flow and/or gas pressure of measurement gas in branch line to sensor unit.
AU HANSMANN H
AE DRAGERWERK & CO AG KGAA (DRAG-C)
   DRAEGERWERK & CO AG KGAA (DRAG-C)
GA 2022017055
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves branching the measurement gas from a main line (15) of a medical device (12) during an inspiratory phase of a person (13) connected to the medical device as inspiration gas and during an expiratory phase of the person as expiration gas to a sensor unit (11) through a branch line (14). The measurement gas is conveyed from the main line through the branch line to the sensor unit by fluid conveying unit (24). The adaptive setting of the fluid delivery unit is taken into account an airway pressure in the main line to generate a volume flow and/or gas pressure of the measurement gas in the branch line to the sensor unit that is uniform during the inspiratory phase and the expiratory phase. The carbon dioxide concentration in the measuring gas is determined by sensor unit.
   USE - Method for determining carbon dioxide concentration in measurement gas from medical device during ventilation of person by medical device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fluid delivery unit is adjusted using the actual measured airway pressure, so that the carbon dioxide concentration in measurement gas is determined by generic medical device in simple, inexpensive and precise manner.

PN CN113754846-A; CN113754846-B
TI Preparing foaming agent useful for carbon dioxide displacement, comprises reacting monomers to obtain intermediate, reacting with halogenated hydrocarbon, undergoing polymerization reaction under initiator, and reacting with hydrogen peroxide.
AU FANG J
   GUO X
   GUO Y
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI PETROLEUM EQUIP CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
   SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022044605
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing foaming agent comprises reacting first monomer, second monomer and third monomer to obtain the first intermediate, reacting first intermediate and fourth monomer to obtain the second intermediate, and reacting second intermediate with halogenated hydrocarbon to obtain the third intermediate, making third intermediate to undergo a polymerization reaction under an initiator to obtain fourth intermediate, and reacting fourth intermediate with hydrogen peroxide, where, the first monomer is 4-penten-2-one, 1-penten-3-one, 4-methyl-4-penten-2-one and/or 5-phenyl-4-pentene-2-ketone, the second monomer is diethyl amine, dibutyl amine and/or dihexylamine, the third monomer is formaldehyde, trioxymethylene and/or paraformaldehyde, and the fourth monomer is methyl magnesium bromide, ethyl magnesium bromide and/or n-propyl magnesium bromide.
   USE - The foaming agent is useful for carbon dioxide displacement (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foaming agent has strong foaming ability and foam-stabilizing ability, and performance is little affected by temperature, has good formation compatibility, forms a foam system with supercritical carbon dioxide and injects into the formation, can efficiently block the formation pores, blocks the gas channeling layer of the gas injection well, increases the sweep volume of carbon dioxide, and improves the oil displacement efficiency and recovery rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a foaming agent prepared by the above method; and
   (2) a displacement method, comprising mixing supercritical carbon dioxide and foaming agent to form a foam system and injecting into the oil reservoir, where the weight ratio of the supercritical carbon dioxide and the foaming agent is 100-1000:1, and the injection speed of the foam system is 1-10 ml/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of the first monomer, the second monomer and the third monomer is 5-11:20-40:3-7, the weight ratio of the first intermediate and the fourth monomer is 18-28:30-42, the weight ratio of the second intermediate and halogenated hydrocarbon is 20-30:6-16, and the weight ratio of the fourth intermediate and hydrogen peroxide is 60-100:2-4. The first monomer is 4-penten-2-one. The second monomer is dibutyl amine. The third monomer is formaldehyde. The fourth monomer is methyl magnesium bromide.Preferred Method: The method comprises under the protection of inert gas, adding the first monomer, the second monomer and the third monomer into the first solvent, adding a catalyst, adjusting the pH to be 4-6, and heating to 70-100degrees Celsius, and reacting for 5-7 hours to obtain first intermediate, where the catalyst is zinc fluoroborate, zirconia, aluminum chloride, niobium pentachloride and/or indium(III) chloride, and the first solvent is water, methanol and/or ethanol; under the protection of inert gas, adding the first intermediate into the second solvent to obtain a mixed solution, adding the fourth monomer dropwise into the mixed solution at -50 to -20degrees Celsius, after the dropwise addition is completed, reacting at 10-50degrees Celsius for 4-8 hours to obtain the second intermediate, where, the second solvent is ether and/or tetrahydrofuran; under the protection of inert gas, adding the second intermediate into ethylene glycol dimethyl ether, adding sodium hydroxide solution at 50-90degrees Celsius, adding halogenated hydrocarbon dropwise, and reacting at 60-100degrees Celsius for 20-28 hours to obtain the third intermediate, where, the halogenated hydrocarbon is bromomethane, bromoethane and/or bromopropane; under the protection of inert gas, adding the third intermediate and the initiator into the third solvent, heating to 50-90degrees Celsius, and reacting for 1-5 hours to obatin the fourth intermediate, where, the third solvent is hexane, benzene, toluene, xylene, ether and/or acetone; under the protection of inert gas, adding the fourth intermediate into the fourth solvent, adding disodium EDTA, heating to 55-75degrees Celsius, adding hydrogen peroxide dropwise to react for 1-2 hours, continuously heating to 75-80degrees Celsius and reacting for 2-6 hours to obtain the foaming agent, where, the fourth solvent is water, methanol, ethanol and/or isopropanol.

   ADVANTAGE - The carbonated beverage has refreshing and natural flavour, natural fusion of bubble flavor and wine aroma and has a broad market prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing carbonated beverages comprising (a) mixing of the drinking wine and purified water (2-3 times), where the temperature of the purified water is 70-98degreesC, (b) insulating first mixed product at a temperature not lower than 40degreesC for 2-10 minutes and cooling to temperature lower than 15degreesC, (c) performing second mixing between the fructose syrup and the product obtained in step (b), (d) performing third mixing of the optional sugar and small material with purified water until completely dissolved, where the small ingredients are the remaining raw materials in the raw materials of the carbonated beverage except for the carbon dioxide, the drinking wine, the fructose syrup, the sugar, the food flavor and purified water, (e) performing fourth mixing between the third mixed product and the second mixed product, (f) adding edible flavor to the product obtained in step (e), (g) subjecting the product obtained in the previous step to sterilization treatment and (h) subjecting the sterilization treatment product to carbonation to obtain the carbonated beverage, where, content of each raw material other than carbon dioxide and content of carbon dioxide used in the carbonation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The sugar includes at least one of crystalline fructose, glucose and white granulated sugar. The sugar alcohol includes at least one of erythritol, xylitol and sorbitol. The organic acid includes at least one of tartaric acid, lactic acid, acetic acid, citric acid and malic acid. The degree of the rice wine is 2-15 vol.%. The rice wine is rice wine, rice wine that has not been sterilized in advance. The edible salt is uniodized edible salt. The carbonated beverage further comprises 0.01-50 g, preferably 5-25 g crystalline fructose, 0.01-50 g, preferably 2-20 g glucose, 0.01-5 g, preferably 0.01-1 g edible flavor. The carbonated beverage comprises 2-20 g, preferably 2-8 g carbon dioxide, 5-150 g, preferably 10-80 g fruit, 2-50g, preferably 5-25 g erythritol, 0-50 g, preferably 5-25 g crystalline fructose, 0-50 g, preferably 2-20 g glucose, 1-50 g, preferably 2-20 g xylitol, 0.01-0.5 g, preferably 0.01-0.1 g tartaric acid, 0.001-0.5 g, preferably 0.01-0.1 g lactic acid, 0.01-0.5 g, preferably 0.01-0.2 g edible salt, 0-5 g, preferably 0-1.0 g edible flavor and purified water, where the carbon dioxide is in the form of carbonic acid, rice wine, rice wine, rum, wine, white wine or sake 5-200 g, preferably 40-80 g. The sterile thread adopts a sterile carbonic acid thread. In the step (a), drinking wine has not been sterilized in advance. The step (b) further comprises filtering the heat preservation product through 1 microm cloth bag filter after the heat preservation and before the cooling. The step (d) further comprises filtering the completely dissolved product through 1 microm cloth bag filter after the complete dissolution. The step (g), the sterilization is UHT sterilization performed at 90-120degrees Celsius (+2, -3) for 5-20 seconds, filtering the product obtained in the previous step is in advance before the sterilization treatment. The step (h), the carbonation is realized by a carbonation machine. The preparation method is realized by a sterile line and preferably, the aseptic line adopts a sterile carbonation line. The preparation of carbonated beverages comprises (a1) pouring the fructose syrup into the blending barrel immediately after the heat preservation of the drinking wine is over, (b1) pouring reverse osmosis water (2-3 times) at 70-98degrees Celsius into the barrel, initiating the timing when the drinking wine is completely brewed, insulating at 40degrees Celsius for 2-10 minutes, driving into the mixing barrel through plate cooler (the outlet temperature is less than 15degrees Celsius), (c) adding room temperature deoxygenated reverse osmosis water to the dissolving tank, stirring, adding optional sugar and small materials respectively, stirring until completely dissolved, driving into the mixing tank (the outlet of the dissolving tank passes via 1 microm cloth bag filter, (d) adding edible flavors to the mixing barrel afterwards, (d) performing UHT sterilization after the deoxygenation and (f) carbonating the UHT sterilized product to obtain the carbonated beverage.

AU HE H

GA 201999366F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing (1) a carbon dioxide emission right allocation model based on the power generation quota allocation method. The carbon dioxide emission right allocation model includes a carbon dioxide emission right quota allocated to a power generation component and planned power generation capacity of the power generation component in a unit time period. PN CN110354765-A; CN212017745-U
TI Photothermal fixed bed reaction experimental device for carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation reaction, comprises a reaction box body and a fixed bed reaction tube, where fixed bed reaction tube is inserted into the reaction chamber.
AU XU Y
   DU X
   ZHUANG W
   DONG L
   ZHOU J
   GONG G
   XUE S
   GUO S
AE UNIV XUZHOU TECHNOLOGY (XUZH-C)
GA 201988896P
AB    NOVELTY - Photothermal fixed bed reaction experimental device comprises a reaction box body and a fixed bed reaction tube (3), where fixed bed reaction tube is inserted into the reaction chamber (5), and both ends extend to the outside of the reaction chamber. The fixed bed reaction tube is provided with a catalyst bed, and the catalyst bed is located in the reaction chamber. The catalyst (4) comprises a carbon dioxide hydromethanation catalyst active component and a photocatalyst. The two ends of the fixed bed reaction tube are respectively provided with an air inlet and an air outlet. The outlet port of the fixed bed reaction tube is connected to the cold head. The multiple xenon lamps (8) are set in the reaction chamber.
   USE - Photothermal fixed bed reaction experimental device for carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photothermal fixed bed reaction experimental device improves single light of low catalysis efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing carbon dioxide hydromethanation reaction for a photothermal fixed bed reaction experimental apparatus, which involves:
   (A) introducing hydrogen into the fixed bed reaction tube, opening the xenon lamp, heating the temperature of the catalyst bed to 500-600 degrees C, and performing photothermal reduction activation on the catalyst;
   (B) turning off the xenon lamp, and passing cooling water to the casing to cool the catalyst bed to below 50 degrees C; and
   (C) passing hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and turning on the xenon lamp, and heating the catalyst bed to 200-600 degrees C to carry out carbon dioxide hydromethanation reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of photothermal fixed bed reaction experimental device.
   Fixed bed reaction tube (3)
   Catalyst (4)
   Reaction chamber (5)
   Reflector (6)
   Xenon lamps (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active component is Nickel, Cobalt, Platinum, Ruthinum, rhodium and titanium dioxide. The photocatalyst is a carrier for a carbon dioxide hydromethanation catalyst. Preferred Composition: The loading amount of the active component is 10-30 wt.% of the transition metal and 0.5-3 wt.% of the noble metal. Preferred Condition: The ratio of the hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 2-5.

The gas                discharge units (16,16a,16b) are configured to                discharge a third gas having a higher carbon                dioxide concentration and relative humidity than                the second gas while the second gas is introduced                from the gas inlet unit. The                equipment structure is simplified, such that the                growth of the plant is promoted. The carbon dioxide                concentration and the relative humidity are                adjusted in accordance with the timing of the start                and stop of photosynthesis by the plant. Thus, the                growth of plants is promoted by reducing the time                lag between the start and stop of photosynthesis                and regulation. Since the plant cultivation system                is provided with the gas supply device, the                facility configuration is simplified.

AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a harvest chamber (102) comprising an opening and a sorbent regeneration system comprising a release medium i.e. steam, a release medium emitter, and a liquid extractor. A capture body (104) is coupled to and movable by a support structure, where the support structure includes first portion inside of the harvest chamber and second portion outside of and above the harvest chamber at height. A product outlet is in fluid communication with the inside of the harvest chamber, and receives a product stream of enriched gas displaced by sweep gas inside the harvest chamber, where the sweep gas is introduced to the harvest chamber. A control system is communicatively coupled to the support structure, and cycles the capture body through a collection configuration and a release configuration. The system prevents or minimizes loss of carbon dioxide from the harvest chamber.

PN WO2018047032-A2; WO2018047032-A3
TI Conversion of hydrocarbon source to hydrocarbon e.g. ethylene, involves mixing obtained combustion products with hydrocarbon source to produce acetylene, ethylene and carbon dioxide, and subjecting quenched acetylene to hydrogenation.
AU GAUTAM P S
   LENGYEL I
   MAMEDOV A
   NAIR B
   PANNALA S
   SANKARANARAYANAN K
   WEST D
AE GAUTAM P S (GAUT-Individual)
   LENGYEL I (LENG-Individual)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   NAIR B (NAIR-Individual)
   PANNALA S (PANN-Individual)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SANKARANARAYANAN K (SANK-Individual)
   WEST D (WEST-Individual)
GA 201821654E
AB    NOVELTY - Energy and carbon efficient method of converting hydrocarbon source to 2C hydrocarbon involves combusting a hydrocarbon source (s1) comprising natural gas in the presence of oxygen in a reaction zone (z1) to produce combustion products, mixing the combustion products with a hydrocarbon source (s2) in a reaction zone (z2) to produce pyrolysis reaction products comprising acetylene, ethylene and carbon dioxide, quenching the pyrolysis reaction products, subjecting acetylene to hydrogenation to form additional ethylene, and subjecting carbon dioxide to hydrogenation.
   USE - Conversion of hydrocarbon source e.g. mixture of natural gas and heavy hydrocarbon, mixture of natural gas and petroleum coke, mixture of natural gas and heavy residue, mixture of natural gas and heavy oil, mixture of natural gas and naphtha, ethane, propane and naphtha to 2C hydrocarbon e.g. acetylene and ethylene (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently converts a wide range of hydrocarbon feedstock into high molecular hydrocarbon and syngas. The method efficiently produces acetylene with a yield of at least 25% and utilizes carbon dioxide byproducts of methane for generating syngas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Energy and carbon efficient method of converting hydrocarbon source to 2C hydrocarbon involves combusting a hydrocarbon source (s1) comprising natural gas in the presence of oxygen in a reaction zone (z1) to produce combustion products, mixing the combustion products with a hydrocarbon source (s2) in a reaction zone (z2) under specified conditions to produce pyrolysis reaction products comprising acetylene, ethylene and carbon dioxide, quenching the pyrolysis reaction products, subjecting acetylene to hydrogenation to form additional ethylene, and subjecting carbon dioxide to hydrogenation to form synthesis gas product stream. The reaction zone (z2) is heated by combustion in the reaction zone (z1).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the combustion or pyrolysis method in combination with an amine separation system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method further involves separating acetylene from the pyrolysis products by contacting acetylene with N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, recycling unreacted hydrogen in hydrogenation reaction to the combustion zone, and converting separated carbon dioxide to methanol or olefin.

PT P
PN CN114436297-A
TI Preparing pure alkali by mirabilite involves preparing carbon dioxide-containing gas into ammonia water, preparing bicarbonate aqueous solution B, mixing ammonium bicarbonate water solution obtained with mirabilite, and reacting filtering to obtain sodium bicarbonate wet material and mother liquid A.
AU ZHANG T
   ZHANG Q
   LI X
   JI L
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
   HENGXIN CREATIVE TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO (HENG-Non-standard)
GA 202266054U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing pure alkali by mirabilite involves preparing the carbon dioxide-containing gas into ammonia water, preparing bicarbonate aqueous solution B, mixing the ammonium bicarbonate water solution obtained with mirabilite, reacting filtering to obtain the sodium bicarbonate wet material and mother liquid A, dry C calcining the sodium bicarbonate wet material obtained, obtaining calcined soda, the carbon dioxide generated in the calcining process is returned, D heating the mother liquid A obtained, returning the generated carbon dioxide and ammonia, and obtaining the mother liquid B, E introducing ammonia gas into the mother solution B obtained, then crystallizing at low temperature, crystallizing and filtering to obtain sodium sulfate filter cake and mother liquid C, returning the obtained sodium sulfate filter cake, F heating the mother solution C obtained to steam ammonia, returning the obtained ammonia, adding acid to the rest mother liquor to adjust the pH value.
   USE - Method for preparing pure alkali by mirabilite
   ADVANTAGE - The method has to recycle the carbon dioxide and ammonia generated, has improving the conversion rate of the ammonium bicarbonate, has recycling the mother liquor and sodium sulfate, has improving the utilization rate of the sodium sulfate in the process of preparation, has improving the single-pass recovery rate of sodium sulfate, has avoiding the problem of difficult separation of sodium sulfate and ammonium sulfate complex salt, and has reducing the separation energy consumption, at the same time obtaining the ammonium sulfate with higher purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing pure alkali by mirabilite involves preparing the carbon dioxide-containing gas into ammonia water, preparing bicarbonate aqueous solution B, mixing the ammonium bicarbonate water solution obtained with mirabilite, reacting filtering to obtain the sodium bicarbonate wet material and mother liquid A, dry C calcining the sodium bicarbonate wet material obtained, obtaining calcined soda, the carbon dioxide generated in the calcining process is returned, D heating the mother liquid A obtained, returning the generated carbon dioxide and ammonia, and obtaining the mother liquid B, E introducing ammonia gas into the mother solution B obtained, then crystallizing at low temperature, crystallizing and filtering to obtain sodium sulfate filter cake and mother liquid C, returning the obtained sodium sulfate filter cake, F heating the mother solution C obtained to steam ammonia, returning the obtained ammonia, adding acid to the rest mother liquor to adjust the pH value, heating and concentrating, filtering when it is hot, precipitating the ammonium sulfate, and returning the filtrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The method further involves preparing carbon dioxide content in the gas containing carbon dioxide is 80-100 wt.%. The mass fraction of the ammonium hydrogen carbonate in the ammonium bicarbonate aqueous solution is 16-23 wt.%. The method further involves obtaining the mole ratio of ammonium hydrogen carbonate to sodium sulfate in the mirabilite is 2-3:1, and the reaction temperature is 30-35 degreesC for 1-2 hours, where the ammonium hydrogen carbonate solution is prepared by dissolving solid ammonium hydrogen carbonate and water.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E35

PN CN111019755-A
TI Carbon dioxide supercritical extracting and            separating flaxseed oil, by drying flaxseed, crushing            flaxseed, performing carbon dioxide supercritical            extraction, collecting separation substance, taking            upper-layer oil liquid and canning.
AU FU Z
AE GANSU JINHAO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 202036178F
AB    NOVELTY - Method for carbon dioxide supercritical                extracting and separating flaxseed oil, involves                (A) selecting flaxseed, sieving, screening out                impurities, washing with water, and drying, (B)                putting the obtained dried flaxseed into a crusher                for crushing to obtain the flaxseed with the mesh                number of 30-50 meshes, (C) performing carbon                dioxide supercritical extraction, (D) collecting a                separation substance in a primary separation kettle                and a second-stage separation kettle, directly                serving as the separation substance in the primary                separation kettle as flaxseed oil, performing                centrifugal separation in the secondary separation                kettle, taking an upper oil layer, collecting the                flax seed oil, and uniformly mixing to prepare the                flaxseed oil, and (E) allowing the obtained                flaxseed oil to stand for 24-36 hours at normal                temperature, and taking an upper-layer oil liquid,                and canning the flaxseed oil.
   USE - The method is used for carbon dioxide                supercritical extracting and separating flaxseed                oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for carbon dioxide supercritical                extracting and separating flaxseed oil, involves                (A) selecting flaxseed, sieving, screening out                impurities, washing with water, and drying at                normal temperature for later use, (B) putting the                obtained dried flaxseed into a crusher for crushing                to obtain the flaxseed with the mesh number of                30-50 meshes, (C) performing carbon dioxide                supercritical extraction comprising (a)                pre-introducing carbon dioxide into an extraction                kettle for washing, putting the flaxseed in the                step (B) into an extraction kettle for extraction,                extracting the pressure of 25-43 degrees C, and the                extraction temperature is carried out at 40-50                degrees C for 60-80 minutes, and (b) separating the                obtained flaxseed oil slurry, where the separation                mode is secondary separation, the pressure of the                primary separation kettle is 8.9-9.3 MPa, the                pressure of the secondary separation kettle is                6.7-7.5 MPa, the temperature of the primary                separation kettle is regulated to 45 degrees C, the                temperature of the secondary separation kettle is                regulated to 50 degrees C, and the extraction time                is 1-2 hours, (D) collecting a separation substance                in a primary separation kettle and a second-stage                separation kettle, directly serving as the                separation substance in the primary separation                kettle as flaxseed oil, carrying out centrifugal                separation by using a centrifuge in the secondary                separation kettle, taking an upper oil layer,                collecting the flax seed oil which is collected in                the primary separation kettle, and uniformly mixing                to prepare the flaxseed oil, and (E) allowing the                obtained flaxseed oil to stand for 24-36 hours at                normal temperature, and taking an upper-layer oil                liquid, and canning the flaxseed oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crushing time in step                  (B) is controlled at 30-40 minutes. The specific                  operation of washing the kettle in the step (C)                  involves introducing each time 10 minutes of                  carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle,                  discharging, and repeating for 2-3 times. In step                  (D), the centrifugal speed is carried out at                  5000-6000 rpm for 15 minutes. Preferred                  Components: The water content of flaxseed is not                  greater than 8%.

TI Apparatus for capturing carbon emissions from            vehicle, comprises exhaust feeder comprising first and            second ends, and housing connected to second end of            exhaust feeder and secured to exterior surface of            vehicle.
AU KENNEDY P
   THAKKAR H
AE MOTE INC (MOTE-Non-standard)
GA 201960411T
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus for capturing carbon emissions from                a vehicle (300), comprises: an exhaust feeder (304)                comprising a first end (301) and a second end                (303), where the first end of the exhaust feeder                connects to the vehicle and receives exhaust gases                emitted from the vehicle; and a housing connected                to the second end of the exhaust feeder and secured                to an exterior surface of the vehicle, where the                housing receives the exhaust gases emitted from the                vehicle via the exhaust feeder, the housing                comprises at least one gas deflector surface that                directs the exhaust gases to a sorbent material                configured to capture carbon dioxide from the                exhaust gases, a detachable section that allows for                removal and installation of the sorbent material,                and an exit port that allows for release of                residual gases after the sorbent material captures                carbon dioxide from the exhaust gases.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for capturing carbon                emissions from vehicle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus utilizes: detachable section                that allows for removal and installation of the                sorbent material; and exit port that allows for                release of residual gases after the sorbent                material captures carbon dioxide from the exhaust                gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                reducing carbon emissions from a vehicle,                comprising receiving exhaust gases emitted from a                vehicle at a first end of an exhaust feeder,                delivering exhaust gases from the first end of the                exhaust feeder to a second end of the exhaust                feeder, transporting the exhaust gases from the                second end of the exhaust feeder into a housing                secured to an exterior surface of a vehicle,                directing the exhaust gases in the housing to a                sorbent material using gas deflector surfaces,                adsorbing carbon dioxide from the exhaust gases                using the sorbent material, expelling residual                gases using an exit port in the housing after the                sorbent material captures carbon dioxide from the                exhaust gases, removing a section of the housing to                allow for extraction of the sorbent material,                heating the sorbent material after extraction to                regenerate the sorbent material, storing carbon                dioxide expelled from the sorbent material during                heating of the sorbent material in a storage tank,                utilizing the carbon dioxide for algae development                including usage in biofuels, biomass generation,                wastewater treatment, and fertilizers and                reinstalling the sorbent material in the housing                after regeneration of the sorbent material from                heating.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates a carbon dioxide                capture system secured to a vehicle.

The pressure of the raw material gas                  is 0.1-6 MPa, and the reaction temperature is                  350-450degrees Celsius.

TI Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump unit group detection and maintenance method, involves opening solenoid valve to supplement charging amount of unit if there is leak in unit, and feeding back number of leaked unit to center console.
AU ZHANG H
   MAO E
AE NINGBO MEIKE CARBON DIOXIDE HEAT PUMP (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202170899K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves numbering a machine set. The machine set is connected with a liquid storage tank through electromagnetic valves. The liquid storage tank is filled with coolant. An air suction end of a compressor is connected with the machine set that is connected with a temperature sensor. Suction temperature of the machine set is measured. Air suction temperature of the compressor is detected. Air suction temperature value of the machine set is compared with average suction temperature value. A solenoid valve is opened to supplement the charging amount of the unit if there is a leak in the unit. The number of the leaked unit is fed back to a center console to notify the unit to be overhauled.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump unit group detection and maintenance method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effectively monitoring the refrigerant consumption condition of each machine set in the carbon dioxide heat pump set group, and timely adding refrigerant to the under-charge set so as to ensure the normal operation of the carbon dioxide heat pump set group.

GA 202117082V
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical cell (10) comprises cathode                (11), anode (12), and an ion exchange membrane (13)                arranged between cathode and anode. The cathode                comprises catalyst (C1) which is capable of                catalyzing a reduction reaction for reducing carbon                dioxide into carbon monoxide. The anode comprises                catalyst (C2) which is capable of catalyzing                carbonylation reaction for producing carbonate                compound from carbon monoxide and alcohol                compound.
   USE - Cell used in electrochemical system for                producing carbonate compound (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical cell produces carbonate                compound by effectively utilizing electrical energy                when reducing carbon dioxide. The electrochemical                system has improved conversion rate of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) electrochemical system, which comprises at                least two electrochemical cells as electrochemical                cells (E1) and (E2), and feed path (P1) which is                capable of feeding a product produced at a cathode                of the electrochemical cell (E1) to an anode of the                electrochemical cell (E2); and
   (2) production of carbonate compound in the                electrochemical cell, which involves applying                voltage between the anode and cathode to reduce                carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide at the cathode                and producing carbonate compound from carbon                monoxide and alcohol compound at the anode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                electrochemical cell.

The cathode of the                  electrochemical cell (E1) and anode of                  electrochemical cell (E2) are located in one                  section separated by the ion exchange membrane. The                  anodes of the electrochemical cells (E1) and (E2)                  comprise catalyst (C1). The electrochemical cells                  (E1) and (E2) comprise both of a cathode                  compartment and an anode compartment. The cathode                  is arranged in each cathode compartment. The                  anode is arranged in each anode compartment. The                  connecting path enables gas to flow from the                  anode compartment into the cathode                  compartment.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Carbon monoxide and                  water are produced by reacting carbon dioxide,                  two protons and two electrons at cathode. Carbon                  monoxide is reacted with alcohol-containing                  compound of formula: 2R1OH (I) at anode to give                  ester-containing compound of formula: CO(OR1)2                  (II). Carbon monoxide is reacted with                  alcohol-containing compound of formula: HO-R11-OH                  (III) at anode to give carbon-containing compound                  of formula (IV), two protons and two electrons.                  The carbon monoxide is reacted with                  alcohol-containing compound of formula: R2-OH (V)                  and R3OH (VI) at anode to give carbon-containing                  compound of formula: (R2O)CO(OR2) (VII), two                  protons and two electrons. The carbonate compound                  is at least one chosen from dimethyl carbonate,                  diethyl carbonate, dipropyl carbonate, dibutyl                  carbonate, diphenyl carbonate, ethylene                  carbonate, propylene carbonate, 1,2-butylene                  carbonate, 1,3-dioxan-2-one, triphosgene, ethyl                  methyl carbonate, methyl phenyl carbonate, and                  butyl methyl carbonate.R1, R2, R3, R11 = 1-15C organic group.Preferred Process: The method involves                  preparing at least two of the electrochemical                  cells as electrochemical cells (E1) and (E2),                  feeding carbon monoxide produced at the cathode                  of the electrochemical cell (E1) to the anode of                  the electrochemical cell (E2), feeding carbon                  monoxide produced at the cathode of the                  electrochemical cell (E2) to the anode of the                  electrochemical cell (E1), reducing carbon                  dioxide into carbon monoxide at least one of the                  cathodes of the electrochemical cells (E1) and                  (E2), producing a carbonate compound from carbon                  monoxide and an alcohol compound at least one of                  the anodes of the electrochemical cells (E1) and                  (E2), feeding carbon monoxide produced at the                  cathode of the electrochemical cell (E2) to the                  anode of the electrochemical cell (E1), flowing                  at least one of carbon dioxide and carbon                  monoxide from at least of the anode compartments                  into at least of cathode compartments. PT P
PN CN112125313-A
TI Preparing spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent by dripping precursor slurry having silica sol and lithium oxalate powder into liquid nitrogen to freeze droplets, sublimating ice ball to obtain precursor green ball and calcining.
AU YANG Y
   LIU W
   XU M
   YAO S
   LI Q
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202102684T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation method of spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, involves (a) preparing a precursor slurry, which is a mixed solution of silica sol and lithium oxalate powder, (b) dripping the uniformly mixed precursor slurry in step (a) into a container containing liquid nitrogen, so that the droplets are rapid-frozen into ice balls containing the precursor slurry, (c) putting the ice ball obtained in step (b) into a freeze-drying device until the water is completely sublimated, and obtaining a precursor green ball composed of a lithium source and a silicon source, and (d) calcining the precursor green balls obtained in step (c) in an aerobic environment until the precursor completely reacts to lithium orthosilicate, to prepare the target spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed) in the actual industrialized fluidized circulation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is ingeniously designed, and the size of the spherical adsorbent finally obtained in step (d) can be adjusted in real time by changing the solid-to-liquid ratio of step (a) without stopping the production line. The prepared adsorbent has high sphericity, outstanding carbon dioxide cyclic absorption and desorption capacity, and excellent mechanical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The mass fraction of silica in the silica sol in step (a) is 20-40 wt.%, when the mass part of silica is recorded as 1 part mass, the part mass of lithium oxalate in the precursor slurry is 2-2.5 parts mass. In step (d), the calcination is performed at 600-1200 degrees C, preferably 800-950 degrees C for 3-6 hours, preferably 4-5 hours. The shrinkage rate of the green balls in step (d) during the calcination process is adjusted to obtain spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbents of different sizes by adjusting the solid-liquid ratio of the precursor slurry in step (a). Preferred Composition: The solid-liquid ratio of the precursor slurry is (1:2)-(1:8) or (1:2.5)-(1:7.5). Preferred Device: The freeze drying device in step (c) includes a freeze dryer or a vacuum freeze dryer. Preferred Properties: In step (c), the green balls obtained by drying have a particle size of 3-4 mm. The particle size of the spherical lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent obtained in step (d) is 0.5-2 mm, preferably 0.9-1.5 mm.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

AE UNIV SHANGHAI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USHS-C)
GA 201946452P
AB    NOVELTY - Seawater desalination system based on ammonia-carbon dioxide cascade refrigeration comprises seawater inlet valves, sea water pump, hydrate reactor and hydrate storage tank. The seawater inlet valve is connected to the bottom of the hydrate reactor via a sea water pump. The hydrate reactor and hydrate storage tank are connected by a pressure regulating valve. The fresh water outlet is arranged at the bottom of the hydrate storage tank. The system further comprises cascade refrigeration unit, heat exchange coil, flow regulator, first valve, second valve, third valve, fourth valve, first dryer, and flow meter. The heat exchange coil is disposed in the hydrate storage tank. The high temperature stage input end and the output end of the cascade refrigeration unit are connected to the two ends of the heat exchange coil. The low temperature stage output end of the cascade refrigeration unit is connected to the bottom of the hydrate reactor via the flow regulator and the first valve.

   ADVANTAGE - The seawater desalination system based on ammonia-carbon dioxide cascade refrigeration has simple structure; and environmentally friendly.

PN CN109046311-A; CN109046311-B
TI New solid alkali catalyst used for conversion of carbon dioxide, prepared by mixing aqueous solution of zinc salt and aluminum salt, synchronously dropping alkaline solution to adjust pH, adding glycerol, stirring and freeze-drying.
AU XU J
   LI Y
   GUO Y
   JIN T
   WU Y
   YE M
   WU G
   WANG X
AE NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 201901089D
AB    NOVELTY - A solid alkali catalyst of formula M-N-ZnOx-Al2O3 (I) is new. The ZnOx-Al2O3 is carrier.
   USE - Solid alkali catalyst used for conversion of carbon dioxide (claimed) for power plant, steel plant or cement plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The product is not easy to contain small amount of sulfur dioxide, and has good stability and yield of carbon dioxide conversion product reaching 10-96%. The method is simple, and has low cost and no environment pollution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A solid alkali catalyst of formula M-N'-ZnOx-Al2O3 (I) is new. The ZnOx-Al2O3 is carrier.
   x=1 or 2;
   M=rare earth metal; and
   N'=alkali metal ion.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the compound (I) comprising mixing aqueous solution of zinc salt and aluminum salt, synchronously dropping alkaline solution to adjust pH, adding glycerol, stirring and mixing, freeze-drying solid sample, calcining under oxygen-free condition to obtain composite oxide colloid aqueous solution, adding rare earth metal salt, mixing, stirring and drying, and calcining under oxygen-free condition; and
   (2) using method of compound (I) comprising passing epoxide or alcohol and carbon dioxide, adding compound (I), reacting, and converting carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rare earth metal is neodymium, ytterbium, cesium, cerium, lithium and lanthanum, or sodium, potassium or ammonium. The molar ratio of zinc to aluminum is 1-10:1. The zinc salt and aluminum salt are nitrates, hydrochloride and/or sulfate. The ammonium salt is ammonium carbonate, ammonium fluoride, ammonium chloride, ammonium bromide and/or ammonium iodide. The alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, ammonium hydroxide and urea. The rare earth metal salt is nitrate and/or hydrochloride. Preferred Method: The method involves mixing aqueous solution of zinc salt and aluminum salt, synchronously dropping 0.015-0.03 mol/L alkaline solution to adjust pH to 8-10 for 1-3 hours, adding 0-0.1 mol/L glycerol, stirring and mixing, freeze-drying solid sample, calcining under oxygen-free condition to obtain composite oxide colloid aqueous solution, adding rare earth metal salt, mixing, stirring and drying, and calcining under oxygen-free condition. The use method involes passing carbon dioxide at pressure of 0.1-4 MPa, adding compound (I), and reacting at 80-170 degrees C for 8-48 hours.

   ADVANTAGE - The device: prepares rose fragrance grape wine with simple process and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing rose fragrance grape wine comprising (1) adding the wine in the absorption tank, and placing the rose flowers in the sealed cabinet; (2) introducing carbon dioxide gas into the sealing cabinet and absorption tank through carbon dioxide inflation port; (3) absorbing aroma component of the rose flower by the wine with the carbon dioxide gas entering the absorption tank through the aroma introduction tube, and returning the unabsorbed portion to the sealed cabinet along with the carbon dioxide gas through the return pipe; and (4) wine absorbs the aroma components of the rose flowers carried by the circulating gas at 0-20 degrees C for 3-10 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the wine to the rose flower is (5-20): 1. Preferred Method: In the method, the flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas is 5-20 l/minute. In the step (4), the wine absorbs the aroma component of the rose flower carried by the circulating gas, and the stirring speed is 10-60 revolutions/minute.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The plurality of flower trays are vertically provided in the sealed cabinet; the plurality of vent holes are provided at the bottom of the tray. The vertical spacing of adjacent flower trays is 8-12 cm. The stirring paddle is provided in the absorption tank. The gas circulation pump is provided on the return pipe.

PN CN114961899-A
TI Ocean LNG power ship afterheat and cold energy            comprehensive utilization system, has low temperature            carbon trapping module that is provided with            evaporation side, organic Rankine cycle and carbon            trapping side.
AU LANG J
   WU J
   QIAO X
   ZHANG C
   LI C
   YAO S
AE UNIV JIANGSU SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJG-C)
GA 2022B53940
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an air separating module, a                flue gas waste heat utilization module and a                low-temperature carbon collecting module. The air                separation module is provided with a flow                sequentially for performing multi-stage cooling                pressurizing and two-stage rectification process. A                carbon capturing side is connected with a                third-stage circulating working medium heated by                flue air discharged from the flue exhaust module                through a third stage circulating pre-heater and                divided into two streams. The carbon dioxide is                discharged from a carbon collecting side of a flow                path to enter a transmitter. The carbon dioxide of                the other flow path is discharged by the air                separation modules. The waste nitrogen and waste                argon are cooled and liquefied.
   USE - Ocean LNG power ship afterheat and cold energy                comprehensive utilization system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system does not need to separate the                carbon dioxide from the flue gas without the                physical adsorption method. It has high-efficiency                energy-saving and zero-carbon discharge                characteristics. It fully recycles the ship waste                heat and cold energy recycling, by gradually                cooling pressurizing, two-stage rectifying method                to prepare the low cost, high purity oxygen, the                obtained high-purity oxygen and circulating carbon                dioxide enter the engine to burn, the natural gas                combustion reaction in pure oxygen needs to mix a                certain amount of carbon dioxide to satisfy the gas                cylinder charge quantity and reduce the combustion                temperature, the process product is only carbon                dioxide and a small amount of argon gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                ocean LNG power ship afterheat and cold energy                comprehensive utilization system.(Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN CN114961828-A
TI Method for processing spontaneous combustion of            coal bed used in coal mining technical field, involves            putting carbon dioxide induced cracking machine in            blast hole, denoting carbon dioxide induced cracking            device for forming collapse area, and leveling surface            of collapse area.
AU YUN Z
   ZHANG Y
   GE C
   GU L
   LIU X
AE TIANJIN GUOHUAN ENERGY SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B6503R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves observing (S100) a surface                area of a coal mine and determining a smoke point.                A blasting area is defined (S200) according to the                determined smoking point. A hollow surface on the                blasting area is set (S300). A periphery of the                hollow surface is provided (S400) with blast holes.                A carbon dioxide induced cracking machine is put                (S500) in the blast hole. A carbon dioxide induced                cracking device is denoted (S600) for forming a                collapse area. A surface of the collapse area is                leveled (S700). Temperature of a surrounding area                of the smoking point is detected. Multiple                temperature measuring holes are opened in the                surrounding area of the smoking point.
   USE - Method for processing spontaneous combustion                of a coal bed used in a coal mining technical                field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables controlling spontaneous                combustion phenomenon of coal seams, collapse                cracks and cavities by the combustion at a bottom                portion of the blasting area by shock wave formed                by explosion to isolate oxygen and prevent the                combustion, leveling the surface of the collapsed                area to increase sealing, and isolating                oxygen.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a method for processing spontaneous combustion of a                coal bed used in a coal mining technical field.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
   S100Observing surface area of coal mine and                determining smoke point

   S400Providing periphery of hollow surface                with blast holes

   S600Denoting carbon dioxide induced cracking                device for forming collapse area
   S700Leveling surface of collapse area

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) comprises an upper liquid                inlet that is connected to water feed rods located                in an upper area of an internal chamber of the                apparatus. A lower gas inlet is connected to a gas                feed mechanism located in a lower area of the                internal chamber. A porous membrane separates a top                side of the chamber from a bottom side. The porous                membrane extends over each of the feed rods and                under each of lower rods in an alternating pattern,                such that each feed rod is located on the bottom                side and each lower rod is on the top side. Each                feed rod extends horizontally across the                chamber.
   USE - Gas exchange apparatus for exchanging carbon                dioxide from a flue gas to a water supply in algal                growth systems or carbon sequestration and disposal                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas exchange apparatus can efficiently                sequester carbon dioxide into water while incurring                minimal additional carbon release. The porous                membrane can be used to increase the water's                exposure to air, or another gas, with the intended                purpose of achieving faster or more efficient                transfer of carbon dioxide from the gas into the                water. The use of power consuming devices to                increase such exposure can result in minimal net                carbon benefit once the carbon dioxide generated by                the power consuming device are considered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method of exchanging carbon dioxide from a flue gas                to a water supply using a gas exchange                apparatus.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a gas                exchange apparatus.

PT P
PN CN111298812-A; CN111298812-B
TI Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst            prepared by e.g. dispersing styrene monomer and acrylic            acid in distilled water, adding potassium persulfate,            dispersing selenium powder and obtained product in            ethanol, and centrifuging.
AU ZHANG Y
   WANG J
AE UNIV ZHAOQING EAST CHINA NORMAL PHOTOELE (UYZH-Non-standard)
GA 202058188G
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction                catalyst, is claimed. The catalyst is copper                selenide hollow microspheres.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide electrochemical                reduction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has high electrocatalytic                activity and selectivity for carbon dioxide                reduction; and improves resource utilization of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the carbon dioxide electrochemical                reduction catalyst, comprising (i) adding styrene                monomer and acrylic acid into distilled water,                stirring and dispersing, then heating the reaction                system at 50-70 degrees C in a water bath, passing                nitrogen gas for 20-40 minutes to remove oxygen                from the solution, then adding aqueous potassium                persulfate solution under stirring condition of                200-300 revolutions/minute, and reacting for 10-24                hours to obtain emulsion, centrifuging obtained                emulsion, washing centrifuged product for three                times using ethanol, and then freeze-drying using                vacuum freeze dryer under not greater than 50 Pa                and less than -30 degrees C to obtain polystyrene                microspheres, (ii) dispersing copper sulfate,                sodium hydroxide and polystyrene microspheres                prepared in step (i) in deionized water, then                carrying out reaction in oil bath at 90-150 degrees                C for 2-5 hours, centrifuging, and collecting the                product, washing for 3-5 times using deionized                water, then placing in oven and drying at 60                degrees C for 24 hours to obtain precursor, and                calcining obtained precursor in muffle furnace to                obtain copper oxide hollow microspheres, and (iii)                dispersing selenium powder and copper oxide hollow                microspheres prepared in step (ii) in absolute                ethanol, then dispersing uniformly under ultrasonic                condition, centrifuging, and collecting the                product, placing the product on the bottom of                porcelain boat, then placing the porcelain boat in                tube furnace, passing argon gas, and calcining                under argon atmosphere, cooling, and collecting the                product to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the raw                  material includes 50-100 ml styrene monomer, 5-10                  ml acrylic acid, 300-500 ml distilled water and                  10-25 ml aqueous potassium persulfate solution,                  where the concentration of aqueous potassium                  persulfate solution is 30-50 mmol/l. In step                  (ii), the raw material includes 1-3 g copper                  sulfate, 0.1-0.5 g sodium hydroxide, 1-2 g                  polystyrene microspheres and 100-200 ml deionized                  water. In step (iii), the raw material includes                  0.1-1 g selenium powder, 0.5-1 g copper oxide                  hollow microspheres and 10-20 ml absolute                  ethanol. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the                  calcination is carried out at 300-600 degrees C                  for 3-5 hours. In step (iii): the argon gas is                  introduced at a rate of 100-200 ml/minute; and                  the calcination is carried out by heating to                  200-500 degrees C at a rate of 1-5 degrees                  C/minute, and maintaining the temperature for 1-3                  hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A13 (Polymers of aromatic mono-olefins; including polystyrene.); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); W06 (Aviation, Marine and Radar Systems)

PT P
PN US2020176784-A1; WO2020112806-A1
TI Method for producing electricity in a molten            carbonate fuel cell, by contacting anode of fuel cell            with anode input stream, and contacting second cathode            layer of fuel cell with cathode inlet stream comprising            oxygen and carbon dioxide.
AU HILMI A
   KISS G
   BLANCO G R F
   GEARY T C
   DEMETER E L
   YUH C
   BLANCO GUTIERREZ R F
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
   FUELCELL ENERGY INC (FUEN-C)
GA 202048921P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting an anode of a                molten carbonate fuel cell with an anode input                stream, and contacting a second cathode layer (156)                of a cathode (102) of the molten carbonate fuel                cell with a cathode inlet stream comprising oxygen                and carbon dioxide. The second cathode layer has an                average pore diameter of 5.5 micrometers or more.                The cathode includes a first cathode layer (154)                adjacent to an electrolyte (142) of the fuel cell,                the first cathode layer having an average pore                diameter of 4.5 micrometers or less. The molten                carbonate fuel cell is operated at a current                density of 60 mA/cm2 or more to generate                electricity, an anode exhaust comprising hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, and a cathode                exhaust comprising carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for producing electricity in a molten                carbonate fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The layered cathode structure has an increased                pore size adjacent to the cathode collector to                facilitate diffusion of carbon dioxide into the                electrolyte interface, while also having a smaller                pore size adjacent to the electrolyte to allow for                improved electrical contact at the interface                between the electrolyte and the cathode. By                transporting alternative ions across the                electrolyte, the fuel cell can continue to operate,                even though a sufficient number of carbon dioxide                molecules are not kinetically available.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a molten carbonate fuel cell; and
   (2) a method for forming a molten carbonate                fuel cell.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                dual-layer cathode for a molten carbonate fuel                cell.
   Cathode (102)
   Electrolyte (142)
   First cathode layer (154)
   Second cathode layer (156)
   Cathode collector (160)
