PT P
PN US2019321268-A1
TI Constructing cosmeceutically bioactive compositions involves forming a solution or mixture of cosmeceutically benevolent phospholipids, where solution is hydrophobic or hydrophilic with an aqueous phase, removing any constituent attributes.
AU RICHARD D A
AE PPP & C INC DBA ROBIN MCGRAW REVELATION (PPPC-Non-standard)
GA 201988047N
AB    NOVELTY - Constructing cosmeceutically bioactive compositions involves forming a solution or mixture of cosmeceutically benevolent phospholipids, where solution is hydrophobic or hydrophilic with an aqueous phase, removing any constituent attributes of water-insolubility from the solution, while operating under conditions to preserve activity of labile biomolecules. The solution and desired hydrophobic bio-actives are loaded into an organic solvent residing in a pressure reactor. The pressure reactor has previously driven to a pre-determined working temperature. The reactor is pressurized with compressed carbon dioxide until reaching a pre-determined working pressure to produce a resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution. The resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution is depressuried over an aqueous phase to form vesicular conjugates. The resulting solution containing water soluble cationic or non-ionic surfactants and hydrophilic or hydrophobic bio-actives.
   USE - Method for constructing cosmeceutically bioactive compositions.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to construct cosmeceutically bioactive compositions that has environmental and physiological impact.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Constructing cosmeceutically bioactive compositions involves forming a solution or mixture of cosmeceutically benevolent phospholipids, where solution is hydrophobic or hydrophilic with an aqueous phase, removing any constituent attributes of water-insolubility from the solution, while operating under conditions to preserve activity of labile biomolecules. The solution and desired hydrophobic bio-actives are loaded into an organic solvent residing in a pressure reactor. The pressure reactor has previously driven to a pre-determined working temperature. The reactor is pressurized with compressed carbon dioxide until reaching a pre-determined working pressure to produce a resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution. The resulting carbon dioxide-expanded solution is depressuried over an aqueous phase to form vesicular conjugates. The resulting solution containing water soluble cationic or non-ionic surfactants and hydrophilic or hydrophobic bio-actives. The elastic vesicles contain a cosmetically and topically effective active agent, and a cosmetically acceptable carrier or diluent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The vesicle content is selected from natural cosmetic ingredient and a synthetic cosmetic ingredient. The vesicle content is used for treating or correcting a therapeutic effect of the skin surface and epi-dermal layers. The vesicle content creating effects from the group consisting of hydration, skin lightening, anti-wrinkle, skin smoothing, antioxidant activity, free radical scavenger, anti-inflammatory, anti-irritant, collagen stimulation, cell regeneration, stimulation, sebum regulation, anti-cellulite, antimicrobial, anti-acne, and antibacterial results. The vesicle contains naturally derived cosmetic beneficial ingredients that originate from one of a group consisting of plants, herbs, roots, flowers, fruits, leaves or seeds, aloe vera, almond oil, avocado oil, coconut oil, hazelnut oil, jojoba oil, olive oil, palm oil, pumpkin seed oil, sesame oil, sunflower oil, tamanu oil, candeia oil, arnica, chamomile, oat extract, Hibiscus flower, Boswellia serrata, cocoa powder, green and white tea, gotu kola, chamomile extract, L-arginine, glutamine, pantothenic acid, white willow bark extract, tetrahydrocurcuminoids, alpha-arbutin, aloesin, alpha glucosyl hesperidin, niacinimide, fucoidan, magnesium asorbyl phosphate, azelaic acid, N-acetyl-D-glucosamine, glutathione, mulberry, pomegranate seed oil, cyprus rotund root extract, licorice, licorice-glabrin root extract, kojic acid, panax ginseng root extract, ginko bilbao, salicylic acid, Lauric acid, glycerin, caffeine, tocopheryl acetate, copper peptide, retinyl palmitate, asorbyl palmitate, wakame, dimethylethanolamine, beta glucan, and triglyceride. The elastic vesicles contain a pharmaceutically and topically effective active agent, and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier or diluent. The active agent is lipid soluble and is present in a wall or membrane of the elastic vesicle. The active agent is water or oil soluble and is encapsulated within a wall or membrane of the elastic vesicle. The vesicle comprises an oil. The oil is selected from the group consisting of castor oil, soybean oil, squalene oil, squalane oil, sesame oil, olive oil, canola oil, corn oil, rapeseed oil, safflower oil, sunflower oil, fish oils, avocado oil, water insoluble vitamins, and mixtures. Preferred Condition: The pre-determined working temperature is less than 31.1 degrees C and the pre-determined working pressure is less than 73.8 bar. Preferred Process: The method includes solubilizing a vesicle-forming lipid in a hydrophilic pharmaceutically acceptable solvent other than water. The penetration enhancer is added to a solubilized vesicle forming lipid. The elastic vesicles further comprise a biologically active agent in entrapped form. The therapeutic agent is added to the lipophilic phase of emulsion, the therapeutic agent is lipophilic. The method further involves preparing liposomes having a central core compartment containing an oil-in-water emulsion, preparing an oil-in-water emulsion which is stabilized by a surfactant, solubilizing an elastic vesicle-forming lipid in a pharmaceutically acceptable solvent other than water, adding the oil-in-water emulsion to the solubilized vesicle-forming lipid, and mixing to form liposomes having a lipid-bilayer membrane composed of a vesicle-forming lipid and containing the oil-in-water emulsion in the central core compartment. The sterol is selected from cholesterol, cholesterol derivatives, and mixtures. The elastic vesicles are paucilamellar lipid vesicles having nonionic amphiphiles as major structural component consisting of 2-8 lipid bilayers. The unilamellar lipid vesicles comprises short-chain phospholipids and long-chain phospholipids.

PT P
PN WO2019021181-A1
TI Metal modified carbon nitride material used for hydrocarbon conversion process to form e.g. epoxides, and as catalyst for oxidation of hydrocarbons comprises metal and two-dimensional or three-dimensional mesoporous carbon nitride structure.
AU KIM I Y
   SCARANTO J
   RAMADASS K
   KIM M
   ALBAHILY K
   RAVON U
   VINU A
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201912272N
AB    NOVELTY - Metal modified carbon nitride material comprises a metal and a two-dimensional (2-D) or three-dimensional (3-D) mesoporous carbon nitride structure having anatomic nitrogen to atomic carbon ratio of 1.36-2.35.
   USE - The material is useful: for hydrocarbon conversion process (claimed) to form epoxides, alcohols and carbonates; and as a catalyst for oxidation of hydrocarbons using carbon dioxide or a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen as a mild oxidant.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: exhibits an average pore diameter of 1-15 nm, and a surface area of 50-500 (preferably 200-310) m2/g (claimed); lowers or eliminates the quantity of epoxide in the final reaction mixture or intermediate reaction mixtures, thus providing safer reaction conditions and product handling (e.g. reducing explosions); results in carbon nitrides with increased carbon dioxide conversion activity at temperatures lower than conventional processes; and utilizes the 2-D or 3-D nitrogen rich carbon nitride material that adsorbs carbon dioxide and catalyzes hydrocarbon oxidation reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing the material, comprising subjecting 2-D or 3-D carbon nitride material and metal precursor to conditions to form a carbon nitride/metal precursor material, and heat-treating the metal/carbon nitride material at 300-500 degrees C to produce material; and
   (2) a hydrocarbon conversion process comprising contacting the material with carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide and oxygen mixture, and a reactant feed source comprising an olefinic compound or an aromatic compound under conditions to form a product stream comprising an epoxide, a phenol, a carbonate or an alcohol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal comprises a transition metal, or a metal or metalloid from column 13 of the periodic table including iron, ruthenium (both preferred), zinc, titanium, vanadium, zirconium, tantalum, tungsten, aluminum, gallium, indium and/or thallium or their oxides or alloys. The metal is bonded to the carbon nitride structure, preferably, bonded to a nitrogen atom in the structure. The carbon nitride structure comprises a metal modified 2-D carbon nitride material or a metal modified 3-D carbon nitride material comprising a tetrazole moiety and/or an amino-tetrazole moiety. The metal modified carbon nitride material comprises having 1-10 wt.% of metal, based on the total weight of the material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon nitride structure comprises a tetrazole moiety and/or an amino tetrazole moiety. The tetrazole moiety or amino-tetrazole moiety is derived from 5-amino-1H-tetrazole, 1,5-diamino-1H-tetrazole, 1,5-(aminomethyl)tetrazole and/or 1-(3-aminophenyl)tetrazole. The reactant feed comprises an olefinic compound and the product comprises at least one of ethylene oxide, ethylene carbonate, propylene oxide, propylene carbonate, styrene carbonate, styrene oxide, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol or dimethyl carbonate, preferably ethylene carbonate or propylene carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - 2-D Mesoporous tetrazole-based carbon nitride materials were suspended in ethanol solution of iron nitrate nonahydrate (Fe(NO3).9H2O) corresponding to a desired quantity of iron metal in the final catalyst (e.g. 1-5 wt.% of iron metal) based on the total weight of the catalyst. The mixture was stirred at room temperature (e.g. 20-30 degrees C) for 20 hours and the solvent evaporated by heating to 50 degrees C. The resulting sample was calcined in air for 5 hours at 400 degrees C to obtain iron-modified 2-D carbon nitride materials.

PN WO2018226639-A1; US2020093399-A1
TI Breath analyzer device for analyzing lung function            e.g. spirometer, has carbon dioxide sensor that is            partially within tube and controller adapted to receive            data from oxygen sensor and carbon dioxide sensor.
AU MILLER T
   MILLER T P
AE MILLER T (MILL-Individual)
   MILLER T P (MILL-Individual)
GA 2018996003
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) has a tube (16) for                communicating a breath sample from a user of a                breath analyzer device. The tube has a first                opening at which the breath sample enters and a                second opening at which the breath sample exits,                and where the tube is adapted to provide minimal                restrictions on a flow of the breath sample. An                oxygen sensor (26) arranged partially within the                tube and adapted to detecting an amount of oxygen                present in the breath sample. A carbon dioxide                sensor (18) is arranged partially within the tube                and adapted to detecting an amount of carbon                dioxide present in the breath sample. A controller                (20) adapted to receive data from the oxygen sensor                and the carbon dioxide sensor. The device is                adapted to provide a differential pressure lower                than 0.8 cm H2O.
   USE - Breath analyzer device for analyzing lung                function such as spirometer.
   ADVANTAGE - The PVC is an ideal material due to durability                that is enough to withstand the downward force of                the two sensors attached and is bored easily                without the risk of stress fractures. The flat                sides facilitate the attachment of the attachment                portions that will encapsulate the two sensors. The                tests lack sensitivity to the small airways, thus                preventing them from effective in early detection                of lung diseases such as chronic obstructive                pulmonary disease (COPD). The addition of a                positive end expiratory pressure can cause                hyperinflation and result in inaccurate CO2 and O2                measurements, therefore a low differential pressure                from the device is needed to ensure additional                dynamic hyperinflation does not occur.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a rear perspective view of                a breath analyzer device.
   Breath analyzer device (10)
   Tube (16)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (18)
   Controller (20)
   Oxygen sensor (26)

PN CN108224832-A
TI Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump unit has            rear side panel that includes rear lower side panel            whose bottom is provided with linear uniform second            waist-shaped heat dissipation hole.
AU LIU R
   ZHANG B
AE ANHUI HUANQIUXING NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201854424S
AB    NOVELTY - The pump unit has an intermediate partition                plate (7) that is fixed between a front side panel                (3) and a rear side panel. The front side panel                includes a first evaporator side panel (31) and a                programmable logic controller (PLC) display cabinet                door (32). The two sides of the PLC display cabinet                door are respectively hinged on the intermediate                partition plate and a support column (10). A                linearly uniform first waist-shaped heat                dissipation hole (33) is opened at a bottom of the                PLC display cabinet door. The rear side panel                includes a second evaporator side panel, a rear                lower side panel, and a sealing panel. The bottom                of the rear lower side panel is provided with a                linear uniform second waist-shaped heat dissipation                hole.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump                unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The pump unit has simple structure and                reasonable design, and can effectively install                various components of the trans-critical carbon                dioxide heat pump reasonably. The internal and                external air circulates to form a heat replacement,                which ensures that the heat of the pump and                electrical equipment is fully dissipated when the                trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump is running.                The pump unit is very convenient to install and                debug PLC controllers. The pump unit is easy to                install and disassemble. The pump unit is very                convenient for daily maintenance. The pump unit is                an ideal matching device for the automatic                operation of trans-critical carbon dioxide heat                pumps.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view                of the trans-critical carbon dioxide heat pump                unit.

PN CN107589201-A
TI Determining amino acid content by reaction-headspace gas chromatography comprises e.g. configuring amino acid standard solution in headspace bottle, and then adding excess of ninhydrin solution, and diluting amino acid sample solution.
AU WAN X
   YU T
   CHAI X
   LI Y
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2018070967
AB    NOVELTY - Determining amino acid content by reaction-headspace gas chromatography comprises e.g. (i) configuring amino acid standard solution in headspace bottle, and then adding excess of ninhydrin solution, and (ii) diluting amino acid sample solution to be measured, placing solution in auto-sampler tray on headspace gas chromatograph, measuring produced carbon dioxide by gas chromatography, and measuring amino acid concentration by the standard curve of carbon dioxide signal value, multiplying by dilution factor to obtain concentration of amino acids in the sample to be tested.
   USE - The method is useful for determining amino acid content by reaction-headspace gas chromatography.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation and high accuracy, improves the operability, shortens the batch sample analysis and detection time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Determining amino acid content by reaction-headspace gas chromatography comprises (i) configuring certain concentration gradient of amino acid standard solution in the headspace bottle, and then adding excess of ninhydrin solution, placing airtight mixed sample on the top of the auto-sampler tray on the gas chromatograph, set the temperature at 50-95 degrees C, reacting for 0.5-2 hours, measuring produced carbon dioxide by gas chromatography, drawing standard curve based on the carbon dioxide signal value and amino acid concentration, and (ii) diluting amino acid sample solution to be measured within measurement range, adding same amount of ninhydrin solution as standard curve, placing the solution in auto-sampler tray on headspace gas chromatograph, setting same reaction temperature and time as standard curve, measuring produced carbon dioxide by gas chromatography, and measuring amino acid concentration by the standard curve of carbon dioxide signal value, multiplying by the dilution factor to obtain concentration of amino acids in the sample to be tested.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ninhydrin solution is ninhydrin in ethanol solution, ethylene glycol monomethyl ether solution or ethylene glycol monoethyl ether solution. The excess ninhydrin is ninhydrin/amino acid in a molar ratio greater than 15. The amino acid standard solution and the amino acid sample solution are phosphate buffer solution, and then adjusting the pH to 4.2. The amino acids are suitable for all amino acids that can be detected by ninhydrin spectroscopy including lysine or alanine. Preferred Method: In steps (i) and (ii), reaction temperature is 60 degrees C and the reaction time is 1 hours. The gas chromatographic measurement conditions are: carrier gas is nitrogen, flow rate is 2.7 ml/minutes, pressure of 30 psi, post-injection temperature is 200 degrees C, capillary temperature is 105 degrees C, shunt ratio is 0.1, detection time is 3 minutes using thermal conductivity detector.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PN WO2022170258-A1
TI Polymer composite useful in hybrid composite and            for capturing carbon dioxide from a quantity of air,            comprises polymer substrate, activated carbon and            carbonate salt.
AU GREEN M
   LACKNER K
   LEE J S
   WANG M
   KORAH M M
   MITHAIWALA H
   WRIGHT A
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 2022A0950T
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer composite comprises: a polymer                substrate; activated carbon; and a carbonate salt,                where the activated carbon is infused with the                carbonate salt.
   USE - The composite is useful: in hybrid composite;                and for capturing carbon dioxide from a quantity of                air (all claimed) and other gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite has high degree of porosity and                fast rate constants for carbon dioxide uptake (on                the order of 0.03 L/second assuming a first-order                sorption process), and high surface area for                contact with air (and thus carbon dioxide in the                atmosphere) to volume ratio; can be regenerated                after use and fabricated with a variety of                morphologies; and enables high loading and fast                reaction kinetics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included                for:
   (1) capturing (M1) carbon dioxide from a                quantity of air, comprising contacting the polymer                composite with the quantity of air in presence of                water vapor to yield potassium bicarbonate, where                the potassium bicarbonate is sorbed on the polymer                composite;
   (2) hybrid composite comprising a fibrous mat                comprising activated carbon, and potassium                carbonate crystals adhered to the fibrous mat;                and
   (3) making (M2) the polymer composite,                comprising combining activated carbon with a                polymer to yield a mixture, where the activated                carbon is infused with a carbonate salt, and                electrospinning the mixture to yield nanofibers,                where the carbonate salt is adhered to or embedded                in the nanofibers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate salt                  comprises potassium carbonate or sodium                  carbonate. The composite comprises up to 40 wt.%                  the activated carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer substrate                  comprises: a thermoset, a thermoplastic, or a                  thermoplastic elastomer; cured epoxy resin; a                  film; fiber; a fibrous mat; and a foaming agent.                  The thickness of the film is 50 mu m and 10 mm.                  The fiber is a hollow fiber. The polymer                  substrate is in the form of a foam. The activated                  carbon: is dispersed throughout the polymer                  substrate; and is adhered to a surface of the                  polymer substrate. The activated carbon is in                  powder form. In the method (M2), the polymer                  comprises polyacrylonitrile, polysulfone,                  polyvinylidenefluoride (PVDF), polystyrene,                  polycarbonate, poly(ethylene terephthalate), and                  nylon. Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises heating the polymer composite on which                  the potassium bicarbonate is sorbed to release                  carbon dioxide and regenerate the polymer                  composite.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing olefin by zero discharge coal                comprises preparing and purifying synthesis gas,                subjecting to high carbon hydrocarbon                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis, reforming methane and                carbon dioxide and carrying out low carbon alcohol                synthesis, where subjecting the raw coal to the                synthesis gas preparation and purification steps to                obtain pure synthesis gas and methane, subjecting                the pure synthesis gas into high carbon hydrocarbon                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis to obtain liquid-phase                product containing high carbon hydrocarbon and gas                phase product containing unreacted synthesis gas                and carbon dioxide, subjecting carbon dioxide                obtained in gas phase and methane obtained in into                methane and carbon dioxide reforming step to obtain                a reforming product, then subjecting the reformed                product into the low carbon alcohol synthesis step,                generating the purge gas by the low carbon alcohol                synthesis step and recycling in the low carbon                alcohol synthesis step.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing olefin by                zero discharge coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple process flow, so that                each component in the coal gas is used, reducing                the emission of green house gas, resizing the                economic, environment, and coordination development                of energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow diagram of                a preparing olefin by zero discharge coal. (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of preparing and                  purifying the synthesis gas includes mixing raw                  coal and oxygen for coal gasification, and the                  obtained crude gas undergoes sulfur-tolerant                  shift and then carrying out alcohol washing and                  methane separation in sequence to obtain pure                  synthesis gas and methane, preferably, the raw                  coal is 15-30 wt.% moisture content, 9-25 wt.%                  ash content, 28-37 wt.% volatile content, 49-61                  wt.% inferior coal crushed coal with a carbon                  content, preferably, the particle size of the raw                  coal is 5-50 &#181;m, preferably, the oxygen                  is provided by air, preferably, the coal                  gasification is carried out by fixed bed                  gasification, fluidized bed gasification and/or                  entrained bed gasification, preferably, the                  sulfur-tolerant shift adopts a cobalt-molybdenum                  catalyst, preferably a cobalt- molybdenum                  catalyst whose carrier is activated alumina,                  magnesium-aluminum spinel and/or                  aluminum-titanium-magnesium complex carrier,                  preferably, the step of alcohol washing adopts                  low temperature methanol washing and/or washing                  with polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether,                  preferably the temperature of the low-temperature                  methanol washing is -30 to -50&#8451;,                  preferably, the methane separation adopts                  cryogenic separation and/or adsorption                  separation. The conditions of the coal                  gasification include a temperature of                  1000-1500&#8451;, pressure of 4-6 MPa, the                  volume ratio of raw coal and oxygen is 1:1-5,                  preferably 1:2-3, and/or the crude gas contains                  0.05-0.1 vol.% methane, 55-65 vol.% carbon                  monoxide, 21-31 vol.% hydrogen, and 3-8 vol.%                  carbon dioxide, and/or the conditions for the                  sulfur-resistant shift includes a temperature of                  220-450&#8451;, pressure of 2-5 MPa, the                  volumetric space velocity of crude gas is                  1000-3000/hour, and/or the conditions of the                  alcohol washing includes a temperature of -33 to                  -55&#8451; and a pressure of 2-6 MPa,                  preferably, the alcohol washing is such that the                  hydrogen sulfide content in the crude gas after                  the alcohol washing is below 0.1 parts per                  million, carbon dioxide is below 20 parts per                  million, and/or the conditions of the cryogenic                  separation includes a temperature of -145 to                  -175&#8451;, pressure of 3-8 MPa. The                  sulfur-tolerant shift includes subjecting part of                  the crude gas to sulfur-resistant shift, and                  carrying out alcohol washing with the mixture of                  the remaining part of the crude gas without                  desulfurization and the shifted crude gas,                  preferably, the crude gas subjected to                  sulfur-tolerant shift accounts for the total                  amount of crude gas 25-50 vol.%, preferably, the                  step of alcohol washing further includes the                  operation of recovering the washed out hydrogen                  sulfide concentrated gas. The step of                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis of high-carbon                  hydrocarbon includes using the pure synthesis gas                  obtained in the synthesis gas preparation and                  purification steps as a raw material to carry out                  a Fischer-Tropsch reaction, preferably the                  conditions of the Fischer-Tropsch reaction                  includes 2-4 MPa pressure, temperature                  220-350&#8451;, molar ratio of hydrogen and                  carbon monoxide is 2-5:1, gas volume space                  velocity is 5000-50000/hour, preferably, the                  catalyst used in the Fischer-Tropsch reaction is                  iron-manganese-copper-potassium series catalyst                  or iron-manganese-coper-potassium-M series                  catalysts, where M is boron, carbon, nitrogen,                  zinc, gallium and tin, preferably, the step of                  Fischer-Tropsch synthesis of high-carbon                  hydrocarbon further includes gas-liquid                  separation of the reaction product to obtain a                  liquid-phase product and a gas-phase product. The                  step of synthesizing low-carbon alcohol includes                  synthesizing low-carbon alcohols on at least part                  of the reformed product obtained in the reforming                  step of methane and carbon dioxide, and the                  synthesis products were isolated to obtain low                  crude carbon alcohol and separation synthesis                  gas, preferably, the conditions for the synthesis                  of the low-carbon alcohols include: temperature                  250-290&#8451;, pressure 2-6 MPa, airspeed                  10000-30000/hour, preferably, the separation                  method includes atmospheric rectification or                  vacuum rectification, preferably, the conditions                  of the atmospheric rectification includes a                  temperature of 80-150&#8451;, preferably, the                  conditions of the vacuum rectification includes a                  temperature of 80-150&#8451;, and pressure of                  -0.5 to -0.7 MPa.Preferred Component: The liquid phase                  product includes 8-12 C alpha-olefin and naphtha,                  preferably, the content of &#945;-olefin is                  35-75 mol.% and/or the gas phase product includes                  methane and/or carbon dioxide, preferably, the                  method further includes decarbonizing the gas                  phase product to obtain an operation of                  recovering synthesis gas and carbon dioxide,                  preferably recycling the recovered synthesis gas                  to the Fischer-Tropsch reaction, more preferably,                  the method of the decarburization treatment                  includes liquid absorption and/or molecular sieve                  adsorption, preferably liquid absorption, more                  preferably potassium carbonate aqueous solution                  and/or sodium carbonate aqueous solution for                  absorption and decarburization. The reformed product includes                  carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide,                  methane and water, preferably, the method further                  includes dehydrating the reformed product to                  obtain the reformed product after dehydration,                  preferably by cooling and dehydrating, more                  preferably, the reformed product after                  dehydration, the content of water is not more                  than 0.01 mol.%, preferably 0.001-0.005 mol.%,                  and the content of methane is not more than 8                  mol.%, the content of carbon dioxide is not more                  than 12 mol.%, more preferably, the reformed                  product after dehydration, the molar ratio of                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen is 1:0.5-1.5. The                  low-carbon alcohol crude product includes 1-6 C                  low-carbon alcohol, 1-6 C low carbon aldehyde,                  1-3 C organic and/or water, preferably, the                  content of low-carbon alcohol is 65-95 mol.%,                  preferably, the method further includes refining                  the crude product of the low-carbon alcohol, so                  as to obtain the operation of refining the                  low-carbon alcohol, preferably in the refined                  low-carbon alcohol content of the low-carbon                  alcohol is more than 99 mol.%, more preferably,                  the method further includes the step of recycling                  the separated synthesis gas to the synthesis of                  lower carbon alcohol.

PN CN113026057-A; CN113026057-B
TI Online soft measurement method for current efficiency of aluminum electrolytic cell, involves calculating corresponding concentration of carbon monoxide and concentration of carbon dioxide substituted into correction formula to obtain soft measurement data of online current efficiency.
AU LIU D
   WANG C
   YI S
   LI X
   YANG Y
   GUO J
   WANG J
AE ZHEJIANG JETRON INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU HUAREN NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 202176913G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a current efficiency correction formula based on atmospheric carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration constant in online production relating to the parameters of concentration of carbon monoxide (CO), the concentration of carbon dioxide, atmospheric CO2 concentration constant, and correction factor. The collected CO and CO2 online concentration data in the electrolytic cell are substituted into the concentration of carbon monoxide and concentration of carbon dioxide of the correction formula, and the iterative regression of the current efficiency online soft measurement function model coefficients is performed to obtain the correction coefficient. The again the online concentration data of CO and CO2 in the electrolytic cell are collected again, and the corresponding concentration of carbon monoxide and concentration of carbon dioxide substituted into the correction formula is calculated to obtain the soft measurement data of the online current efficiency.
   USE - Online soft measurement method for current efficiency of aluminum electrolytic cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing online soft measurement of the current efficiency of the aluminum electrolytic cell with simple measurement, high precision and better stability to realize fine management of the electrolytic cell.

PN CN113019383-A
TI Preparing nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst useful for dry reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide, comprises performing co-current co-precipitation reaction on mixed reaction system containing precipitant, nickel salt and calcining.
AU SONG H
   CHOU L
   GU T
   ZHAO H
   LIU B
   ZHAO J
   YANG J
   YAN L
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202176875Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst comprises performing co-current co-precipitation reaction on mixed reaction system containing precipitant, nickel salt, lanthanum salt and solvent to obtain catalyst precursor, where the pH of the mixed reaction system is 7.5-8.5, and calcining the catalyst precursor.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for dry reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes simultaneous precipitation of nickel and lanthanum, ensures nickel is highly uniformly dispersed in the carrier, and is simple, environmentally-friendly and saves energy-saving. The catalyst has high activity and catalytic efficiency, significantly suppress the formation of carbon deposits, and realizes the conversion rate of methane and carbon dioxide are very high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for dry reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide, comprising preparing nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst, subjecting the nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst into reduction treatment at 600-800 degrees C for 1-3 hours in a reducing atmosphere, continuously adding methane and carbon dioxide into a tubular reactor equipped with a reduction-treated nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst, and reacting at 600-800 degrees C and space velocity of 10,000-50,000 per hour to generate carbon monoxide and hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises dissolving nickel salt and lanthanum salt in water to form first mixed solution, dissolving the precipitant in water to form precipitant solution, adding the first mixed solution and the precipitant solution into the solvent simultaneously at 20-50 degrees C to form mixed reaction system and controlling the pH of the mixed reaction system to be 7.5-8.5, stirring and reacting for 10-30 minutes at 20-50 degrees C, and allowing to stand for 10-30 minutes to obtain catalyst precursor. The temperature of the calcination treatment is 600-800 degrees C, and the time is 2-4 hours. The method also includes filtering, washing, and drying the obtained mixture after the co-current co-precipitation reaction is completed, where the temperature of the drying treatment is 80-120 degrees C, and the time is 8-12 hours. The reducing atmosphere includes a hydrogen atmosphere and/or, the molar ratio of methane to carbon dioxide is 1. Preferred Components: The nickel salt includes nickel chloride, nickel nitrate, nickel acetate, and nickel sulfate, the lanthanum salt includes lanthanum chloride and/or lanthanum nitrate, the precipitating agent includes ammonium carbonate and/or ammonium bicarbonate, the solvent includes water and/or ethanol aqueous solution and the concentration of the ethanol aqueous solution is less than 20 wt.%. The molar ratio of nickel salt to lanthanum salt in the first mixed solution is 0.02-0.5:1, the concentration of the precipitant in the precipitant solution is 0.1-2 mol/l and the volume ratio of the solvent to the sum of the first mixed solution and the precipitant solution is 1:1-1:2. The phase of the nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst includes monoclinic La2O2CO3, hexagonal La2O2CO3, La2O3, and La2NiO4, and the content of nickel in the nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst is 0.1-5.0 wt.%, and the content of nickel in the nickel/lanthanum oxide catalyst is 0.2-4 wt.%.

Confining pressure is                applied to a core holder by a hand pump and set to                formation pressure of a core block. A displacement                oil sample is injected into a core at constant                speed, where displacement pressure and water output                at a core outlet are recorded. The formation                conditions and conduct simulated accumulation aging                are maintained. Volume of carbon dioxide to                original pressure is converted to calculate return                rate.
   USE - Supercritical carbon-dioxide full-cycle                fracturing energy storage and backflow effect                evaluating experimental method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing multi-mode                energy storage experiments and flowback experiments                on a core, so as to simple and convenient to use                and accurately evaluating the energy-enhancing                effect and flowback efficiency of different                modes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                supercritical carbon-dioxide full-cycle fracturing                energy storage and backflow effect evaluating                experimental device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                supercritical carbon-dioxide full-cycle fracturing                energy storage and backflow effect evaluating                experimental device.

PT P
PN CN109704317-A
TI Preparation of graphene by detonation method involves preparing ferric chloride graphite intercalation compound and explosive into drug column by injection molding or press-molding, and assembling electric detonator.
AU MIAO Z
   TIAN H
AE UNIV YANCHENG TEACHERS (UAET-C)
GA 2019423231
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of graphene involves (1) adding uniformly mixed anhydrous iron (III) chloride and graphite, vacuum sealing in a vacuum ampule, heating, and taking black product and dissolving in a dilute hydrochloric acid solution to be washed, and drying to obtain a first-order ferric chloride graphite intercalation compound, (2) preparing the ferric chloride graphite intercalation compound (I) and the explosive into a drug column by injection molding or press-molding, and assembling the electric detonator, the booster charge column and the raw material drug column into a detonation sequence, (3) evacuating and filling the inside of explosion container with carbon dioxide as a protective medium, (4) making the carbon dioxide gas in the container to immediately enter the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid state after the explosion, cooling, collecting the product to obtain graphene.
   USE - Preparation of graphene by detonation method using carbon dioxide as protective medium.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide can use the heat explosion to enter the supercritical state, without the heating process, and continuously transfer heat generated by the explosion to undecomposed completely ferric chloride molecules. At the same time, the graphite sheet layer is continuously stripped by using the characteristics of supercritical fluid, and small layer graphene can be efficiently prepared, which is suitable for industrial production, easy to control, and simple in steps.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of anhydrous iron (III) chloride to graphite is (5:1)-(6:1). The mass ratio of the ferric chloride graphite intercalation compound (I) to explosive is (1:0.5)-(1:10). Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature is 380 degrees C, and the heating time is 12-24 hours. Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide is filled as a protective medium. The carbon dioxide is injected in an amount to make the carbon dioxide pressure in the explosion reactor close to or greater than 7.38 MPa. The explosion time is extremely short, the carbon dioxide gas absorbs heat into the supercritical fluid state, and continues to strip the ferric chloride graphite intercalation compound (I).
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AU XIA C
   SHI M
   ZHONG Y
   LIU Z
   YUAN H
AE YANGTZE RIVER PHARM GROUP CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
   YANGTZE RIVER PHARM GROUP CO LTD (YANG-Non-standard)
GA 2018730526
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises thymalfasin and polymer material in a weight ratio of 1:1-3, where the composition is prepared by dissolving thymalfasin and polymer material in a mixed solvent of water/trifluoroacetic acid/dichloromethane, subjecting to supercritical fluid crystallization and precipitating.
   USE - Used as thymalfasin composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has good stability and can be stored for a long time at room temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the composition, comprising (i) adding raw materials into mixed solvent of water/trifluoroacetic acid/dichloromethane and dissolving completely, (ii) continuously introducing liquefied carbon dioxide into a high-pressure crystallizer of supercritical fluid crystallization equipment system through carbon dioxide channel via high-pressure pump and regulating the temperature and pressure in the kettle by a heating device and a pressure regulating valve, so that carbon dioxide in the kettle reaches to a supercritical state, (iii) introducing mixed solvent of water/trifluoroacetic acid/dichloromethane through the solution channel, reaching the system to equilibrium, replacing the mixed solvent with the solution configured in step (i) for continuous injection, quickly mixing the solvent with supercritical carbon dioxide and recovering through the bottom of the crystallizer, so that solute particles settle at the bottom of the crystallizer, and (iv) continuously passing carbon dioxide and removing residual solvent, relieving pressure, and autoclaving and collecting the sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The composition further comprises hydroxypropyl methylcellulose and sodium L-ascorbate.Preferred Components: The polymer material is polyethylene glycol 6000 or Pluronic F-127 (RTM: Poloxamer 407). The weight ratio of thymalfasin, polymer material and hydroxypropyl methylcellulose is 1:1.5:2. The polymer material is polylactic acid or a polylactic acid-glycolic acid copolymer. The weight ratio of thymalfasin and polymer material is 1:2.5. The composition can be prepared in form of capsules, dry suspension, dry powder inhaler or injection.Preferred Method: The volume ratio of water/trifluoroacetic acid/dichloromethane in the mixed solvent is 4:2:4.    USE - Anti-freezing isolation liquid used for coordinated transformation of liquid carbon dioxide dry fracturing and hydraulic fracturing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The isolation liquid uses simple raw materials, has simple preparation process and convenient operation process, improves low permeability gas reservoir liquid carbon dioxide dry fracturing and hydraulic fracturing, does not generate hydrate or obstruction and not change viscosity of fracturing fluid, cannot be frozen at - 51.5 degrees C, and has low viscosity and friction, excellent fluidity, and good anti-freezing effect.

   (1) preparation of the anti-freezing isolation liquid comprising mixing dimethyl sulfoxide, ethylene glycol and diethylene glycol with water at room temperature and dissolving to form composite antifreezing liquid; and adding cosolvent and stirring until dissolved completely; and
   (2) use method of anti-freezing isolation liquid comprising injecting liquid carbon dioxide in coordinated transformation of liquid carbon dioxide dry fracturing and hydraulic fracturing in low permeability oil and gas reservoirs, injecting antifreezing isolation liquid,, and water-based fracturing at 0 degrees .

AU JEON Y W

GA 201855821R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a biogas plant portion which produces the biogas by digesting organic wastes. A carbon dioxide separating portion is provided for separating the biogas produced in the biogas plant portion. A heat supply unit is provided for heating the biogas remaining after separated from the carbon dioxide portion. An ammonia deasphalting portion is provided for degassing the ammonia of the filtrate separated in the solid-liquid separator. An ammonia absorber is provided for absorbing ammonia deaerated at an ammonia deaeration section. A bicarbonate ammonium production unit is provided for producing the ammonium bicarbonate by reacting ammonia absorbed in the ammonia absorption unit with carbon dioxide separated from the carbon dioxide separation unit. A positive osmosis treatment unit is provided for moving the water of the ammonia deaeration unit to the ammonia absorption unit.
   USE - Recovery system of resources from biogas plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The utilization ratio is increased and the greenhouse gas such as methane and carbon dioxide are reduced. The wastewater disposal facilities are simplified and the economical efficiency of the biogas plant is improved.

AU YU Q
   LIU G
   LIU Z
   LI Z
AE SINOPEC CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2018049009
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises (a) 0.9-10 pts. wt. metal zirconium and/or titanium elements, (b) 1-10 pts. wt. alkaline-earth metal element, and (c) 0.1-5 pts. wt. metal iron and/or manganese elements.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) comprising preparing catalyst comprising (i) preparing zirconium and/or titanium elements of the precursor salt solution A, (ii) preparing a precursor salt solution obtained from an alkaline earth metal element B, (iii) preparing iron and/or manganese of the precursor salt solution C, (iv) taking A, B and C, mixing, uniformly stirring, adding into the container with the precipitating and fast stirring, placing into water bath at 60-80 degrees C, where the pH value is controlled at 8-9, (v) separating out mother liquid, carrying out hydro-thermal treatment the precipitate after drying and roasting; and
   (2) hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide comprising taking carbon dioxide and hydrogen as feed gas, reacting at 550-600 degrees C, where the reaction pressure is atmospheric pressure ~ 1MPa, where the volume ratio of the dihydrogen/carbon dioxide is (1:1)-(3:1) and the space velocity is 6000-15000 h1, where feed gas is contacted with the catalyst to obtain carbon monoxide and water vapor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The content of the zirconium and/or titanium is 1-5 pts. wt.. The molar ratio of zirconium and/or titanium is (10:1)-(1:5), preferably (5:1)- (1:2). The content of alkaline earth metal is 1-5 pts. wt.. The content of the iron and/or manganese is 0.1-1 pts. wt.. The alkaline earth metals are obtained by magnesium, calcium or barium. The generated carbon monoxide and water vapor and un-reacted raw material gas through the cooler, gas-liquid separator, separate the water, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas mixture.

PT P
PN CN113981478-A
TI Performing bioelectrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, by performing aminization of mesoporous material i.e. SBA-15 by 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane modification, immobilizing formate dehydrogenase, and regenerating neutral red coenzyme.
AU GAO J
   JIANG Y
   ZHOU L
   WANG Z
   YANG M
   CHEN H
   CHEN R
   LIU G
AE UNIV HEBEI TECHNOLOGY (UYHT-C)
GA 202223522Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for performing bioelectrocatalytic carbon dioxide (CO2) reduction by immobilizing an enzyme on a mesoporous material involves using SBA-15 as mesoporous material, performing aminization of the mesoporous material by 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES) modification and glutaraldehyde covalently combined with polyethyleneimine (PEI), immobilizing formate dehydrogenase by shaking and mixing, using neutral red coenzyme to regenerate it, and realizing the bioelectrocatalytic CO2 reduction at the cathode.
   USE - The method is useful for performing bioelectrocatalytic CO2 reduction by immobilizing enzyme on mesoporous material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is easy to operate and easy to implement, increases CO2 adsorption capacity of the substrate, and performs bioelectrocatalytic CO2 reduction with higher formate yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of a mesoporous material-immobilized enzyme for performing bioelectrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, where the carbon dioxide reduction method is used for resource treatment of CO2 for reducing CO2 to formic acid, where concentration of formic acid produced after 180 minutes is 0.88-1.12 mM.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves (a) preparing 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane-modified mesoporous material by weighing mesoporous material SBA-15 into a container, adding mesoporous material SBA-15 with anhydrous toluene in a mass-volume ratio of 1 g/30 ml, ultrasonically dispersing into a homogeneous solution, slowly adding APTES with mass volume ratio of SBA-15 and APTES of 1 g/ml, stirring at 110degreesC, condensing and refluxing for 24 hours, cooling reaction solution to room temperature, centrifuging, washing three times with absolute ethanol to remove unreacted 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane, and drying in an oven at 60degreesC for 12 hours to obtain APTES-modified mesoporous material, i.e. APTES-modified mesoporous material, (b) performing amination of the mesoporous material polyethyleneimine by weighing the APTES-modified mesoporous material and placing it in a container, adding phosphate buffer with mass-volume ratio of APTES-modified mesoporous material to phosphate buffer of 0.3 g/10 ml, ultrasonically dispersing, adding glutaraldehyde solution to the above solution with mass volume ratio of APTES-modified mesoporous material and glutaraldehyde solution of 0.3 g/ml, stirring, adding 30-80% polyethyleneimine phosphate buffer, stirring the mixture at room temperature, cooling reaction solution to room temperature, centrifuging, washing several times with deionized water, and drying in an oven at 60degreesC for 12 hours, and performing amination of mesoporous polyethyleneimine to obtain an orange-red solid, i.e. PEI-SBA-15, (c) preparing mesoporous material immobilized enzyme by dispersing 100 mM PEI-SBA-15 in a phosphate buffer having pH of 6 with mass-volume ratio of PEI-SBA-15 to phosphate buffer of 0.05 g/ml, adding formate dehydrogenase with mass ratio of formate dehydrogenase to PEI-SBA-15 of 1/50, performing electrostatic adsorption by shaking and mixing on a shaker, washing three times with phosphate buffer, centrifuging to get the precipitate and drying to obtain the immobilized enzyme in mesoporous material, and recording as FDH-PEI-SBA-15, and (d) adding 100 mM phosphate buffer having a pH of 6 to the cathode tank of the electrocatalytic system, adding FDH-PEI-SBA-15 with neutral red to make the concentration of neutral red in the solution to 0.1 mM, adding NADH to make the concentration of NADH in the solution to 2 mM, introducing CO2 at a flow rate of 100 ml/minute, adding water equal to the volume of phosphate buffer in the cathode cell to the anode cell of the electrocatalytic system, and applying voltage to -0.7 V (vs. Ag/AgCl), for realizing bioelectrocatalytic CO2 reduction. The step (c) involves shaking for 4 hours, and drying by freeze-drying after centrifuging the precipitate. In step (d), the mass ratio of FDH-PEI-SBA-15, neutral red and NADH is 25:1:40.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN EP3868865-A1
TI Producing Haematococcus pluvialis used to produce            astaxanthin, involves inoculating Haematococcus            pluvialis into medium supplemented with carbon dioxide,            adding sodium acetate followed by first photo-culture,            and adding calcium ions.
AU SIM S J
   YU B S
   SUNG Y J
   HONG M E
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2021989092
AB    NOVELTY - Producing Haematococcus pluvialis, comprises                (a) supplementing a medium with carbon dioxide to                form bicarbonate ions, (b) inoculating H. pluvialis                into the medium supplemented with the carbon                dioxide, (c) adding sodium acetate to the medium                inoculated with the H. pluvialis, followed by first                photo-culture, and (d) adding calcium ions to the                medium added with the sodium acetate to form                calcium carbonate, followed by second                photo-culture.
   USE - The method is useful for producing H.                pluvialis, which is useful for producing                astaxanthin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes biomineralization, hence                enables the mass production of pure biomass                containing high density of cells and a large amount                of astaxanthin (of high antioxidant activity) in a                short period of time; is capable of removing                contamination factors and efficiently reduces                carbon dioxide compared to conventional                photo-culture methods; and enables the separation                of calcium carbonate from biomass.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (c), the                  sodium acetate is added at 5-15 mM. In the step                  (d): the calcium ions are added at 8-18 mM; and                  the calcium ions are added by supplementing the                  medium with calcium chloride. The step (d) is                  carried out when the density of the H. pluvialis                  cells is greater than or equal to 1 g/l in the                  step (c). At least one microorganism comprising                  bacteria, fungi or microalgae other than H.                  pluvialis is generated in at least one of the                  steps (a)-(c) and is killed by encapsulation with                  the calcium carbonate. The calcium carbonate                  formed on the surface of the H. pluvialis cells                  induces an increase in the size of the H.                  pluvialis and is separated from the surface of                  the H. pluvialis cells with increased size. In                  the step (d), a basic solution is added to the                  medium to maintain the pH of the medium at                  7.5-8.5. The method further comprises separating                  the cultivated H. pluvialis and the calcium                  carbonate after the step (d). Preferred                  Components: The zeta potential of the H.                  pluvialis is -30 eV to -34 eV in step (c) and -6                  eV to -10 eV in step (d).
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide experimental details in                production of Haematococcus pluvialis that do not                support the claims.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E24 (Other dyes, all precursors.)

PT P
PN CN113082935-A
TI Capturing carbon dioxide at high temperature comprises e.g. dissolving sodium hydroxide in deionized water, dropping sodium metasilicate, heating for dehydration, calcining, grinding solid calcined sample, and heating.
AU TANG Z
   LING C
   WANG Z
   DI W
   WANG J
   QIAN Y
AE SHANGHAI INST APPLIED PHYSICS CHINESE AC (CAYW-C)
GA 202179883X
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide at high temperature, comprises (i) dissolving sodium hydroxide in deionized water to obtain an aqueous solution of sodium hydroxide, (ii) dropping sodium metasilicate into the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution and stirring and dissolving to obtain a mixture, (iii) heating the mixture for dehydration to obtain a dehydrated sample, (iv) calcining the dehydrated sample at high temperature to obtain a solid calcined sample, (v) grinding the solid calcined sample to obtain a sodium orthosilicate adsorbent, and (vi) heating the sodium orthosilicate adsorbent to an adsorption temperature of 400-900 degrees C to capture carbon dioxide gas in the mixed gas.
   USE - The method is useful for capturing carbon dioxide at high temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, safe, and economical, obtains sodium orthosilicate adsorbent with good high temperature stability, meets the emission temperature of the factory after combustion, utilizes easily available raw materials, and is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (vi), the sodium orthosilicate adsorbent adsorbs carbon dioxide gas in the mixed gas under constant temperature and variable temperature conditions. The adsorption temperature is 700-800 degrees C. In the step (iii), the mixture is heated on a heater and stirred until the dehydration is complete, and a dehydrated sample is obtained. In the step (iv), the dehydrated sample is put into a crucible, and then calcined in a high-temperature resistance furnace at a high temperature to obtain a solid calcined sample. The target temperature of high-temperature calcination is 400-700 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-12 hours. Preferred Components: The mixed gas is the mixed atmosphere of carbon dioxide and nitrogen. In the step (ii), the molar ratio of sodium metasilicate to sodium hydroxide is 1:(2-1):(3).

TI Preparation of electrode used for lithium battery, e.g. liquid lithium ion battery by putting electrode material, conductive agent and adhesive, reacting with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, drying product, and hot pressing.
AU MAO Z

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of electrode comprises putting electrode material, conductive agent and adhesive in high pressure reactor, adding supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, reacting at 100-150 degrees C, stirring for 1-20 hours in a closed environment at high temperature and high pressure of 1-20 MPa, releasing pressure to convert supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into gaseous carbon dioxide, and discharging from the reactor; and drying product, and hot pressing.

   Preferred Definitions:
   n=6;
   A1, A2=H; and
   X1=O.

PN CN110200125-A
TI Preparing instant porous protein powder comprises preheating reaction vessel, adding protein powder and water, stirring to obtain protein slurry, carrying out high pressure carbon dioxide treatment, and spray drying to obtain the product.
AU YU L
   WU W
   CUI F
   LIU H
AE SINO-SINGAPORE INT JOINT RES INST (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 201978619J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing instant porous protein powder comprises (a) preheating reaction vessel, adding protein powder and water and stirring uniformly to obtain protein slurry, (b) pumping carbon dioxide into reaction vessel for high pressure carbon dioxide treatment, and (c) connecting the reactor vent to spray drying tower through a conduit, opening pressure reducing valve to allow the flow of protein slurry to spray drying tower with carbon dioxide, and drying to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing instant porous protein powder.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly; has low energy consumption; high reaction efficiency; and reduces the flavor loss of protein powder. The protein powder: has good water solubility; is rich in nutrient content; and can be easily absorbed by human body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for instant porous protein powder prepared by the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The preheating temperature in step (a) is 20-80 degrees C. The pressure of the high pressure carbon dioxide treatment in step (b) is 100-4000 Psi. The temperature of high pressure carbon dioxide treatment in step (b) is 20-80 degrees C. The time of high pressure carbon dioxide treatment in step (b) is 50-60 minutes. The temperature of drying treatment in step (c) is 40-300 degrees C and the time of drying treatment is 1-60 minutes. Preferred Components: The protein powder in step (a) is soybean protein powder, whey protein powder, peanut protein powder, casein powder, broad bean protein powder and/or pea protein powder. Preferred Composition: The percentage of protein powder in protein slurry of the step (a) is 5-30 wt.%. The weight of moisture in instant porous protein powder in step (c) is 0-15 wt.%.

PN US2019194583-A1
TI Making protective coating on an alkali metal hydroxide containing powder comprises exposing alkali metal hydroxide particles to carbon dioxide atmosphere which are coated with carbonate or bicarbonate and preparing detergent composition.
AU SMITH K R
   PETERSON M P
   OLSON K E
AE ECOLAB USA INC (ECON-C)
GA 201952868R
AB    NOVELTY - Making protective coating on an alkali metal hydroxide containing powder, comprises exposing alkali metal hydroxide particles to carbon dioxide atmosphere comprising at least 60% of carbon dioxide to form coated alkali metal hydroxide particles, where the alkali metal hydroxide particles are coated with a carbonate or bicarbonate, and preparing a detergent composition including at least 15 wt.% coated alkali metal hydroxide particles.
   USE - The method is useful for making a protective coating on an alkali metal hydroxide-containing powder (claimed) which is used in cleaning compositions e.g. washing objects dishes in automatic dishwashing detergents, laundry detergents and drain cleaners.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the composition that is free of phosphorus and inexpensive carbon dioxide source to form the protective coatings on alkali metal hydroxide; and provides the coating that does not affect, either positively or negatively, cleaning ability of the composition it protects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises forming the detergent composition into a solid block. The exposing occurs by dropping or accelerating the alkali metal hydroxide particles through a carbon dioxide atmosphere or by blending the alkali metal hydroxide particles with carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The alkali metal hydroxide is sodium hydroxide and/or potassium hydroxide. The solid block is a multiple-use solid having weight of 1-50 kg. The particles comprise an inner core comprising hygroscopic alkali metal hydroxide. The weight ratio of alkali metal hydroxide in the inner core to the sodium bicarbonate or sodium carbonate in the coating is at least 4:1. The particles are powder or beads. The detergent composition comprises 1-99 (preferably 10-60) wt.% coated particles, 0-30 (preferably 5-20) wt.% of chelating agent, 2-10 (preferably 1-5) wt.% of surfactant, 2-10 (preferably 5-10) wt.% of water; and 0-10 (preferably 2-10) wt.% of binder. The composition is a pressed solid block. Preferred Composition: The detergent composition further comprises at least one of a surfactant, chelating agent, water conditioner, binder, rinse aid, bleaching agent, antimicrobial agent, bleaching agent activator, detergent builder or filler, defoaming agent, antiredeposition agent, optical brightener dye and/or fragrance.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109911851-A
TI Purification of ultrapure hydrogen used in production of e.g. semiconductor, involves treating hydrogen by combined process of catalytic oxidation and adsorption.
AU LI H
   XU H
   TANG C
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201957285S
AB    NOVELTY - The purification of ultrapure hydrogen involves treating hydrogen by combined process of catalytic oxidation and adsorption. The catalytic oxidation is heating catalytic process in which hydrocarbon such as methane is converted to carbon dioxide and water. The adsorbent is a composite material, which simultaneously removes oxygen, carbon dioxide and water. The adsorption process is carried out at normal temperature.
   USE - Purification of ultrapure hydrogen used in production of semiconductor and LED.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of ultrapure hydrogen with long service life by simple, economical and industrial process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The purification of ultrapure hydrogen involves treating hydrogen by combined process of catalytic oxidation and adsorption. The catalytic oxidation is heating catalytic process in which hydrocarbon of formula: (CxHy) such as methane is converted to carbon dioxide and water. The adsorbent is a composite material, which simultaneously removes oxygen, carbon dioxide and water. The adsorption process is carried out at normal temperature.
   x = 1-30;and
   y = 4-62.
   .
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The two-stage catalytic oxidation reaction tube (I) and two-stage parallel adsorption tube (I) are used in two-stage series combination process, and the two parallel adsorption tubes are alternately adsorbed and regenerated. The material used for catalytic oxidation is catalyst supporting active components of platinum, ruthenium, or palladium. The mass loading amount of active component is 1-5%. The carrier is aluminum oxide, cerium oxide, manganese oxide or two or more kinds such as platinum/aluminum oxide, ruthenium/cerium oxide, palladium/cerium oxide. The composite adsorbent comprises 10-20 %mass zinc oxide, 10-15 %mass nickel oxide 20-40 %mass manganese oxide, 20-30 %mass copper oxide and 30-40 %mass calcium oxide. Preferred Process: The catalyst used for catalytic oxidation is prepared by a dipping process including immersing carrier of 20-30 mesh in solution of de-ionized water containing soluble active component compound, drying in water bath overnight at 100-120 degrees C, calcining at 600-800 degrees C for 6-10 hours, and reducing under hydrogen atmosphere at 400-600 degrees C for 3-10 hours to obtain platinum/aluminum oxide catalyst. The heating and cooling processes are carried out by heating mixture of zinc carbonate, nickel carbonate, manganese carbonate, copper carbonate and calcium carbonate at 400-600 degrees C, and cooling room temperature for 6 hours under a hydrogen atmosphere.

PN CN109761256-A
TI Preparation of calcium hydroxide from shell involves washing shell with alkali, calcining, injecting air or oxygen, obtaining calcium oxide powder, placing in closed space, and using carbon dioxide-free water to bring humidity.
AU WANG P
   XIE L
   HUANG X
   ZHU C
   ZHOU Y
   LI X
AE JINJIANG SHANJUNSHUIJIE TECHNOLOGY CO (JINJ-Non-standard)
   QINGDAO RONGCHUANG NEW MATERIAL ENG RES (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201946453S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of calcium hydroxide from shell involves washing shell with alkali, pickling, crushing, subjecting the obtained shell powder to vacuum negative pressure low-temperature calcination at 650-750 degrees C for 2-8 hours under degree of vacuum of (-80)-(-50) KPa, injecting air or oxygen in furnace, subjecting obtained calcium oxide powder to dry ultra-fine pulverization, placing in closed space without carbon dioxide, ultrasonically atomizing, using carbon dioxide-free water to bring the humidity of the enclosed space to 90-95% in the absence of carbon dioxide, and carrying out flexible slow reaction in closed space system for 6-48 hours.
   USE - Preparation of calcium hydroxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared highly active porous calcium hydroxide has improved whiteness, and BET specific surface area, and the process is carried out adopting vacuum negative pressure low-temperature calcination for saving energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The alkaline washing process preferably involves using shell to alkali in a weight ratio of 100:1-3, dissolving shell with water in a weight ratio of 1:2-4, heating, cooking to break the film containing phosphorus on the inner wall of the shell, washing shells, subjecting to centrifugal dehydration, and drying. The pickling process preferably involves placing washed alkali shell to acid solution in a weight ratio of 1:1.5-2.0, stirring to remove the impurities on the outer wall of the shell, washing, subjecting to centrifugal dehydration, drying, and pulverizing to 1-5 mm, subjecting obtained calcium oxide powder to dry ultra-fine pulverization to 300 nm-20 mu m. Preferred Component: The base used in alkaline washing process is potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide. Preferred Composition: The acid used in pickling process is hydrochloric acid solution having concentration of 1-5 %mass, sulfuric acid solution having concentration of 1-5 %mass, or nitric acid solution having concentration of 1-5 %mass. Preferred Properties: The calcium hydroxide having activity of more than 400, whiteness of more than 80, and BET specific surface area of more than 25 m2/g.

GA 201944519K

The structured                structure adsorbent is shape-regulating adsorbent                having a hydraulic equivalent diameter of 10-5000                mm, length of more than 30 mm, and an internal                diffusion distance of less than 0.3 mm. The                structured structure adsorbent is assembled from                multiple adsorbents, and is sequentially overlapped                according to the flow of raw materials of                dehydrated adsorbent, carbon dioxide adsorbent,                methane adsorbent, carbon monoxide and nitrogen                adsorbent, where the volume of dehydrated adsorbent                is 20-35 wt.%, and not less than 5 wt.% carbon                dioxide adsorbent and methane adsorbent of the                total volume of composite adsorbent.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The regular structure                  adsorbent shape comprises honeycomb shape, fiber                  fabric shape and foam structure. The                  methane adsorbent is Ca-A molecular sieve or Na-X                  molecular sieve. The carbon monoxide and nitrogen                  adsorbent adsorbents are LiX molecular sieves                  and/or Li-LSX molecular sieve. The activated                  alumina has a specific surface area of more than                  250 m2/g. The NaY molecular sieve comprises                  silicon dioxide/alumina oxide is greater than 3                  and has a specific surface area of greater than                  450 m2/g. The structured adsorbent is                  sequentially overlapped according to the flow                  direction of raw material order of activated                  alumina, NaY molecular sieve, Ca-A molecular                  sieve, Na-X molecular sieve, LiX molecular sieve                  and Li-LSX molecular sieve, and mixed gas passes                  through the bed, water, carbon dioxide, methane,                  carbon monoxide and nitrogen adsorption to obtain                  high concentration of hydrogen gas outlet. The                  hydrogen-efficient purification adsorbent has a                  porosity of less than 80%, preferably less than                  60% of the adsorbent module. The adsorption and                  separation process of the hydrogen includes PSA,                  VSA, VPSA, fast cycle PSA, fast cycle VSA and                  fast cycle VPSA process. Preferred Method: The                  adsorbent is used for hydrogen purification,                  separating hydrogen with molar concentration of                  more than 20% from impurities to obtain hydrogen                  product having concentration of greater than 99%,                  where the impurities are carbon dioxide,                  nitrogen, methane and small amount of carbon                  monoxide and water. The molar concentration of                  carbon monoxide impurity is less than 1%. The                  molar concentration of water impurity is less                  than 5%.
