PT P
PN US2019037834-A1; US11284616-B2
TI Composition used to reduce transfusion-associated            graft-versus-host disease comprises irradiated oxygen            and carbon dioxide-depleted red blood cells and free of            carnitine and the red blood cells are stored for time            period before irradiation.
AU YOSHIDA T
   VERNUCCI P J
AE NEW HEALTH SCI INC (NEWH-Non-standard)
   HEMANEXT INC (HEMA-Non-standard)
GA 201913612L
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises irradiated oxygen and                carbon dioxide-depleted red blood cells and not                comprising L-carnitine or an alkanoul derivative,                where oxygen and carbon dioxide-depleted red blood                cells are stored for a period of time prior to                irradiation.
   USE - The composition is useful for reducing                transfusion-associated graft-versus-host disease in                a patient, where the patient is receiving                chemotherapy and having immunocompromised blood                (all claimed). No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: is free of L-carnitine;                utilizes the oxygen and carbon dioxide depleted red                blood cells having increased deformability and                reduced hemolysis, improved post-transfusion                recovery as compared to red blood cells                conventionally stored for an equivalent period of                time and then irradiated; exhibits improved                post-transfusion recovery is after 6 weeks of                storage; leuko reduced, free of platelets                (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition                  comprises reduced lesions and reduced oxidative                  damage compared to red blood cells conventionally                  stored for an equivalent period of time and then                  irradiated. The composition further comprises an                  additive solution. Preferred Components: The                  irradiated oxygen and carbon dioxide depleted red                  blood cells comprise reduced potassium leakage                  compared to red blood cells conventionally stored                  for an equivalent period of time and then                  irradiated. The oxygen and carbon dioxide                  depleted red blood cells comprise less than 20%                  sulfur dioxide. The oxygen and carbon dioxide                  depleted red blood cells comprise reduced                  irradiation damage compared to red blood cells                  conventionally stored for an equivalent period of                  time and then irradiated. The oxygen and carbon                  dioxide depleted red blood cells comprise less                  than 10 mm of mercury. The additive solution is                  saline-adenine-glucose solution (AS-3) or OFAS3.                  The composition is stored within a storage vessel                  substantially impermeable to oxygen or oxygen and                  carbon dioxide, where the storage vessel                  comprises an inner layer compatible with blood                  and an outer layer substantially impermeable to                  oxygen or oxygen and carbon dioxide.
   ACTIVITY - Immunosuppressive.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC B06 (Inorganics - including fluorides for toothpastes etc.); B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); P33 (Medical aids, oral administration (A61G, H, J).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).)
MC B04-F04A; B05-C04; B05-C05; B05-C08; B12-R; B14-G02C; B14-S18; B14-S25; D05-H08B; P33-B; P34-A01

TI Intelligent rotary vegetable planting device, has carbon dioxide output mechanism connected with carbon dioxide output conduit, and control panel for controlling temperature control mechanism and cooling water control mechanism.
AU LI H
AE LI H (LIHH-Individual)
GA 201901193F
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a tray rotating mechanism connected with a multi-layer tray frame. A temperature control mechanism controls temperature of a vegetable tray. A cooling water control mechanism controls supply of moisture to the vegetable tray and is connected with the temperature control mechanism. A small hole is uniformly formed on an air inlet pipe. A carbon dioxide output mechanism is connected with a carbon dioxide output conduit. A control panel controls a lateral magnetic attraction mechanism, the temperature control mechanism and the cooling water control mechanism.
   USE - Intelligent rotary vegetable planting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can provide light, temperature and moisture of a vegetable tray for growth of vegetable plant so as to effectively provide soil free vegetable cultivation, thus improving degree of automation and ensuring wide range of application.

PT P
PN CN109022495-A; CN109022495-B
TI Reducing carbon dioxide to produce methane by microorganisms, involves constructing microbial electrolysis cell, adding methanogen culture medium, inoculating methanogen, culturing, loading cathode cell with voltage and harvesting methane.
AU MU B
   ZHOU L
   LIU J
   YANG S
   BAI Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 2018A5145C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reducing carbon dioxide to produce methane by microorganisms, involves (a) constructing a microbial electrolysis cell comprising a cathode cell and an anode cell, where the cathode cell comprises a working electrode and a reference electrode, the anode cell comprises a counter electrode, and the cathode and anode cells are separated by a proton exchange membrane, (b) adding a methanogen culture medium to the cathode cell and the anode cell, (b) inoculating 1.5-5% methanogen bacteria in the cathode cell, and culturing, (d) loading the cathode cell with a voltage of -0.6 v (vs SHE), and (e) harvesting methane from the headspace of the cathode cell.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing carbon dioxide to produce methane by microorganisms.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is performed without using any chemical catalyst, reduces carbon dioxide cost-effectively, and does not produce any other by product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The proton exchange membrane in step (a) is an N117 proton exchange membrane. The working electrode is made of a nitric acid-acetate mixed fiber film adhered to the multi-walled carbon nanotube. The amount of multi-walled carbon nanotubes adhered to the nitric acid-acetic acid mixed fiber film is 0.1-10 mg/cm2, and the length of the multi-walled carbon nanotubes is 0.5-50 mu m. The nitric acid-acetic acid mixed fiber membrane has a pore size of 0.2-2 mu m. Preferred Medium: The methanogen culture medium comprises 0.35 g/l dipotassium phosphate, 0.23 g/l monopotassium phosphate, 0.5 g/l ammonium chloride, 0.5 g/l magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, 0.25 g/l calcium chloride, 2.25 g/l sodium chloride, 0.85 g/l sodium bicarbonate, 0.5 g/l sodium sulfide hydrate, 10 ml/l trace element solution and 1 ml/l of vitamin solution, where the headspace of the electrolytic cell is a mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen in a ratio of 1:4 (n/n). The trace element solution comprises 1500 mg/l ferrous chloride tetrahydrate, 70 mg/l zinc chloride, 100 mg/l manganese chloride tetrahydrate, 6 mg/l boric acid, 190 mg/l cobalt chloride hexahydrate, 2 mg/l copper chloride hydrate, 24 mg/l nickel chloride hexahydrate, 36 mg/l sodium molybdate dehydrate and 10 mg/l 25% hydrochloric acid. The vitamin solution comprises 2 mg/l biotin, 2 mg/l folic acid, 10 mg/l vitamin B6, 5 mg/l vitamin B1, 5 mg/l vitamin B2, 5 mg/l niacin, 5 mg/l pantothenic acid, 0.1 mg/l vitamin B12, 5 mg/l aminobenzoic acid and 5 mg/l lipoic acid. The methanogen is Methanosarcina barkeri, Methanobacterium bryantii and Methanospirillum hungatei. Preferred Method: The methanogen is cultured at room temperature.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)

PN CN109030715-A; CN109030715-B
TI Indoor human body detecting method, involves determining indoor space by electronic device, judging whether human is checked with indoor space, and determining negative change in concentration to judge indoor space is unmanned.
AU XU C
   CHEN X
   DING J
   YAO C
   LAI W
AE ELITEGROUP COMPUTER SYSTEMS CO LTD (ECSC-C)
   ELITEGROUP COMPUTER SYSTEMS CO LTD (ECSC-C)
GA 2018A4932X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting an initial value of carbon dioxide concentration in indoor space by a sensor. A final value of the carbon dioxide concentration is detected after detecting time period. Concentration changing amount is calculated by an electronic device according to the received initial value and the final value of the carbon dioxide concentration. The indoor space is determined by the electronic device according to positive change in the concentration. Judgment is made to check whether human is checked with the indoor space. Negative change in the concentration is determined to judge the indoor space is unmanned.
   USE - Indoor human body detecting method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing indoor carbon dioxide changing process to judge a person in a room, tracking process for sensing process and specific function monitoring process to protect privacy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating an indoor human body detecting method. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PT P
PN CN108840955-A
TI Preparing cellulose carbonate carboxylic acid mixed ester comprises e.g. mixing cellulose, organic base and organic solvent, passing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction system, reacting, filtering, washing, purifying and then drying.
AU XIE H
   CAI L
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201894341Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cellulose carbonate carboxylic acid mixed ester comprises (i) mixing cellulose, organic base and organic solvent, passing carbon dioxide gas into the reaction system at a atmosphere pressure of 0.1-8 MPa, reacting at 20-80 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours to obtain cellulose solution, (ii) adding alkyl halide into the cellulose solution prepared in step (i), passing carbon dioxide gas at a pressure of 0.1-8 MPa, reacting at 20-80 degrees C for 0.5-48 hours, (iii) releasing carbon dioxide gas, adding acylating reagent into the reaction solution and reacting at 20-100 degrees C for 0.1-10 hours, and (iv) adding an anti-solvent into the reaction solution, filtering the precipitate, washing, purifying and then drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cellulose carbonate carboxylic acid mixed ester.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cellulose is microcrystalline cellulose, alpha -cellulose, cotton, wood pulp, bamboo pulp, from agricultural straw and/or crop seed husk, where cellulose is (3S,4R)-6-(hydroxymethyl)-2-methoxy-5-methyltetrahydro-2H-pyran-3,4-diol compounds of formula (I). The amount of cellulose in the mixture of cellulose, organic base and organic solvent is 2-15 wt.%, amount of organic base is 2-20 wt.%, and amount of organic solvent is balance amount. TI Method for preparing calcium-based carbon dioxide absorber, involves utilizing calcium zirconate, followed by slowly adding zirconium nitrate solution to calcium acetate solution, adding acetic acid, stirring and freeze drying.
AU ZHAO M
   HE X
   WANG F
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 201849553X
AB    NOVELTY - A calcium-based carbon dioxide absorber preparing method involves utilizing calcium zirconate, slowly adding zirconium nitrate solution to the calcium acetate solution, adding acetic acid and stirring until clear solution with magnetic stirrer to obtain the precursor solution. The precursor solution is placed into refrigerator to freeze until the solution is completely solidified, put in vacuum drier to dry completely sublimed to water to obtain the high temperature solid phase reaction sample, which is placed in the muffle furnace, naturally cooled the sample after calcining and ground into powder in a mortar to obtain the final product.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium-based carbon dioxide absorber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing the calcium-based carbon dioxide absorber with better effect of ensuring the stabilizing agent uniformly distributed in the absorbent, and better cycle stability in simple manner.

The method for preparing supported iron-based                  catalyst involves bombarding the iron target                  material for 1-30 minutes by using argon ions                  generated by a plasma generator, placing the                  carrier in the polygonal rotating barrel,                  vacuumizing the polygonal rotating barrel,                  introducing argon gas into the polygonal rotating                  barrel, and adjusting the rotating speed of the                  polygonal rotating barrel to enable metal iron                  ions to be deposited on the surface of the                  carrier, to obtain the supported iron-based                  catalyst. The supported iron-based catalyst                  comprises 10-30 wt.% active component, having                  particle size of 3-20 nm. The volume ratio of the                  carbon dioxide to hydrogen in the raw material                  gas is 1:1 to 1:6. The reaction temperature is                  280-320&#8451;. The volume space velocity of                  the feed gas is 2000-10000/hour, and the reaction                  pressure is 1-4 megapascals.

TI Preparing nano tin dioxide is used as catalyst for            electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves            dissolving the copper salt in methanol to obtain            solution A, mixing solution A with methanol solution of            the ligand.
AU LIN Y
   HE P
   CHEN L
   HAO F
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 2020611670
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano tin dioxide is used as catalyst                for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide,                involves dissolving the copper salt in methanol to                obtain solution A, mixing solution A with methanol                solution of the ligand to obtain mixed solution,                centrifuging and drying to obtain the                copper-organic framework material. The                copper-organic framework material, tin salt, and                aqueous solution obtained are stirred and mixed,                and then centrifuged and dried to obtain precursor                mixture. The precursor mixture obtained is                calcined. The temperature is raised to above 300                degrees C in air atmosphere for certain period of                time, and then naturally cooled to room                temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing nano tin dioxide is used                as catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare nano tin dioxide                that has good catalytic selectivity and catalytic                activity for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper salt is                  selected from one or more of copper chloride                  dihydrate, copper nitrate trihydrate, copper                  sulfate pentahydrate, and copper nitrate                  hexahydrate. The ligand is selected from trimesic                  acid, biphthalic acid and terephthalic acid,                  preferably trimesic acid. The surfactant is                  selected from one or more of                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, hexamethylenetetramine,                  cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The tin salt                  includes one or more of stannous chloride                  dihydrate, stannous oxalate, stannous sulfate.                  Preferred Conditions: The ratio of the molar                  amount of copper in the copper-organic framework                  material to the molar amount of tin in the tin                  salt is greater than or equal to 1:1. The                  potentiometric electrolysis of potassium                  bicarbonate electrolyte solution saturated with                  carbon dioxide gas is performed in potential                  range of (-)1.2-(-)1.8 volt relative to the                  silver/silver chloride electrode, main products                  are formic acid, carbon monoxide and                  hydrogen.

PN CN110923042-A
TI Method for removing heavy metal in coal includes adding mixture of coal powder and water in reactor, and continuously introducing water and carbon dioxide gas to saturate carbon dioxide gas in water and form acidic environment.
AU LI X
   SUN M
   XIONG Z
   YAO H
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 202026207G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing heavy metal in coal comprises crushing coal sample into coal powder, mixing with water, adding in reactor, continuously introducing water into the reactor, and feeding continuously carbon dioxide gas into the reactor to saturate carbon dioxide gas in water and form acidic environment; soaking fully the coal powder to remove water-soluble and acid-soluble inorganic heavy metal elements from the coal powder and replace organic heavy metal element in coal powder by hydrogen ion in acidic environment, and discharging continuously water containing heavy metal from the reactor; and filtering mixture of coal powder and water in reactor to obtain coal powder after removing heavy metal, and drying.
   USE - The method is for removing heavy metal in coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, mild condition, low requirements on reactor, low cost, and good removing effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Method: The discharged water containing heavy metal is passed through centrifugal sedimentation and filter processing, and recycled into the reactor. The flow rate of water into the reactor is 10-30 ml/minute. The flow rate of carbon dioxide gas into the reactor is 150-250 ml/minute. The time for soaking and washing coal powder is 1-6 hours. The drying is carried out at 90-120 degrees C in vacuum for 8-12 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The particle size of coal powder is 150-450 mu m.

   USE - Flue gas carbon dioxide trapping material.
   ADVANTAGE - The product effectively solves the problem of small specific surface area of adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of flue gas carbon dioxide trapping material comprising adding deionized water to adjust mass fraction of m-cresol and formaldehyde in whole system, adding alkaline catalyst, ultrasonically mixing, heating to form transparent gum, aging at room temperature, normal pressure, and under the protection of nitrogen to obtain porous carrier, preparing 0.1-60 wt.% organic amine aqueous solution, and using impregnation method to load organic amine on porous carrier.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline catalyst is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, magnesium carbonate, potassium carbonate, and/or ammonia water, and added amount of alkaline catalyst is 0.1-1 wt.%. Preferred Method: The heating step is done at 60-80 degrees C. The ultrasonic generator used is probe-type or slot-type ultrasonic processor, and the ultrasonic transducer is piezoelectric ceramic ultrasonic transducer or magnetostrictive ultrasonic transducer. The ultrasound frequency is low-frequency ultrasound with frequency of 10-100 kHz. The ultrasonic frequency is set to 10-40 kHz with ultrasonic sound intensity of 0.01-10 W/m2, and ultrasonic irradiation time of 24-48 hours. The ultrasonic continuous process uses ultrasonic sound intensity of 0.1-1.0 W/m2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount ratio of mixed substance of m-cresol and formaldehyde is 1:2. The mass fraction of m-cresol and formaldehyde in whole system is adjusted to 20-40%. Preferred Components: The organic amine aqueous solution is monoethanolamine, aminomethyl propanol, monomethylethanolamine, diethanolamine, piperazine, and/or hydroxyethylpiperazine.

GA 201990268A

   R = primary, secondary or tertiary alkyl                group.

   LANG X
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201899469R
AB    NOVELTY - A natural gas hydrate extracting method involves utilizing power plant flue gas replacement, performing the ash separation of the flue gas generated by the power plant and referring to the concentration of carbon dioxide to obtain a mixed gas of carbon dioxide and nitrogen. AU LIANG Z
   ZHANG X
   LIU H
   TONG B
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 201865331G
AB    NOVELTY - Use of oxide molecular sieve catalyst                comprising oxide and HZSM-5 molecular sieve, in                catalyzing carbon dioxide desorption, is claimed.                The oxide is an amphoteric metal oxide. The                catalyst is obtained by dissolving a soluble metal                salt in water, adding HZSM-5 molecular sieve powder                to obtain mixed solution, ultrasonically processing                for 0.5-2 hours, adding an alkaline substance to                improve pH to 8-12, carrying out metal salt                hydrolysis precipitation for 2-8 hours, aging for                6-24 hours, solid-liquid separating, water-washing                the solid, drying at 60-200 degrees C for 8-36                hours, and calcining at 300-800 degrees C for 2-12                hours.
   USE - Use of oxide molecular sieve catalyst in                catalyzing carbon dioxide desorption.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can be prepared by a simple                method, separated easily, and repeatedly recycled,                has excellent stability, and has excellent carbon                dioxide desorption performance.

The                  alkaline substance is aqueous ammonia or sodium                  hydroxide. The amphoteric metal oxide is                  zirconia, ferric oxide, alumina, chromium oxide,                  tin oxide, titania, zinc oxide, and/or                  germania.

PT P
PN TW201825004-A
TI Preservation processing method for porous baking food capable of increasing days for preservation without placing deoxidizer to enhance eating safety.
AU CAI J
   LIN Z
   HUANG Y
   YAN S
AE UNI-PRESIDENT ENTERPRISES CORP (SNPH-C)
GA 201876509P
AB    NOVELTY - The present invention provides a porous baking food and a preservation processing method for the same. The porous baking food includes a bag and the bag is received with a main body and filled with preservation gas composed of nitrogen and carbon dioxide. During the packaging process, the preservation processing method for porous baking food is injecting nitrogen and carbon dioxide by using a gas input device; and, using a gas regulation monitoring device to monitor the volume percentages of nitrogen and carbon dioxide of the preservation gas. Carbon dioxide in the preservation gas and water content contained in the main body will perform chemical reaction to generate carbonic acid. Because carbonic acid may lower pH value and the chemical reaction requires water, the present invention may reduce water content in the bag to achieve the purpose of increasing days for preservation without placing deoxidizer, so that eat the deoxidizer by mistake would not happen for enhancing eating safety.

   LI J

GA 201854540D
AB    NOVELTY - Continuous production of active magnesium oxide (MgO) and recovery of carbon dioxide (CO2) comprises feeding magnesite particles having particle size of less than 1 mm into preheating chamber by distributing hopper, charging into feed vacuum chamber, feeding into exhaust chamber, continuously microwave heating until decomposition temperature of magnesite particles to decompose CO2 gas, continuously heating to maximum temperature limit and keeping at this temperature until magnesite particles decompose into CO2 and become MgO particles, discharging MgO particles into cooling and collection chamber through discharge vacuum chamber with inlet and outlet doors; and cooling, collecting and packaging MgO particles. The entire production process is performed by programmable logic controller-controlled drive actuator.
   USE - Method for continuously producing active magnesium oxide and recovering carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not cause pollution due to fuel combustion, recovers CO2 during the process, and achieves zero discharge. The purity of recovered CO2 is more than 99%.

PN CN108060012-A
TI Method for extracting peony seed oil, involves            drying peony seeds, crushing, placing peony seed powder            in extraction kettle, injecting carbon dioxide gas and            dissolving peony seed oil components in supercritical            fluid.
AU TENG C
   PAN J
AE GUIZHOU AEROSPACE WUJIANG ELECTRO-MECHAN (CAES-C)
GA 2018421113
AB    NOVELTY - The method for extracting peony seed oil,                involves drying peony seeds before extraction,                crushing, placing the peony seed powder into                material bucket of extraction kettle, when the                extract is properly dried, injecting carbon dioxide                gas into extraction kettle, maintaining the                temperature and pressure in the extraction kettle                in contact with the natural materials in the                supercritical state of carbon dioxide gas,                dissolving peony seed oil components in the                supercritical fluid, and collecting fluid                containing-peony seed oil from the outlet of the                extraction kettle. The method uses supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid to extract peony seed oil from                peony seeds.
   USE - Method for extracting peony seed oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The economical and environmentally-friendly                method enables simple extraction of odorless peony                seed oil with low solvent consumption, low                extraction cost, excellent quality, high purity,                excellent color, shortened extraction cycle and low                energy consumption, and contains no solvent                residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The supercritical                  reaction is carried out at temperature of 40-50                  degrees C and pressure of 25-30 MPa. The flow                  rate of carbon dioxide gas through the extraction                  kettle is 200-400 kg/hour. The extraction process                  is carried out for 1.5-3 hours. Preferred                  Process: The drying process is carried out by                  placing the screened peony seeds in the oven and                  drying to a moisture content of 10-15% at a                  temperature of 50-60 degrees C. The pulverization                  process is carried out by placing the pulverized                  dried peony seeds in pulverizer and pulverizing                  to powder with particle size of 40-60 mesh.

TI Organic amine functionalized macroporous silica carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises macroporous silica material and organic amine loaded on the macroporous silica material.
AU LIU L
   LIU C
   LIU S
   ZHANG X
   ZHU Y
   ZHU B
AE UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
   UNIV HUAIBEI NORMAL (UYHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018124222
AB    NOVELTY - Organic amine functionalized macroporous silica carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprises macroporous silica material and organic amine loaded on the macroporous silica material, where the pore volume of the macroporous silica material is 1.1-2.2 cm3/g.
   USE - Used as organic amine functionalized macroporous silica carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: has excellent adsorption capacity, dynamic adsorption capacity of 208 mg/g, excellent regeneration cycle stability, and high absorption capacity even after 30 cycles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing organic amine functionalized macroporous silica carbon dioxide adsorbent, comprising (i) taking tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS) as silicon source, and taking pluronic triblock copolymer poly(ethylene oxide)-poly(propylene oxide)-poly(ethylene oxide) (P123) as template, mixing uniformly tetraethyl orthosilicate, P123, hydrochloric acid and pore-expanding agent, carrying out crystallization reaction, then drying, and calcining to obtain macroporous silica material, and (ii) loading organic amine on the macroporous silica material by impregnation method, and drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is polyethylene imine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine and/or triethanolamine. The mass percentage of the organic amine is 20-75%. The pore-expanding agent is benzene, toluene, dimethylbenzene and/or trimethylbenzene. In step (i), the molar ratio between P123, TEOS and pore-expanding agent is 4:8.6:1-4. In step (i), the crystallization reaction temperature is 100-120 degrees C, and the crystallization reaction time is 12-24 hours. In step (i), the calcination reaction conditions are: heating to 450-850 degrees C at a heating rate of 0.5-5 degrees C/minute, and calcining for 3-10 hours. In step (ii), the impregnation method utilizes impregnation solvent as organic compound having a boiling point of 50-80 degrees C, and the impregnation time is 0.2-3 hours. In step (ii), the drying process is carried out at 40-90 degrees C for 3-15 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves acquiring (101) the shear                stress generated at the second interface of                cementing under the coupled action of chemistry and                mechanics in the carbon dioxide (CO2) injection                well. The second interface of the cementing is                whether cracked is determined (102), based on the                shear stress and the cementing strength of the                cement sheath. The cracking length of the second                cementing interface is obtained (103), in response                to the occurrence of cracking at the second                cementing interface, according to the principle of                energy conservation. The failure length of the                second cementing interface is obtained (104), based                on the cracking length, and the integrity of the                second cementing interface is detected by the                failure length.

The method                more accurately obtain the failure length of the C2                interface under the condition of injecting CO2, by                analyzing the energy transfer process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide injection well cementing interface                integrity detection device.

   102Step for determining second interface of                the cementing is whether cracked, based on the                shear stress and the cementing strength of the                cement sheath
   103Step for obtaining cracking length of the                second cementing interface, in response to the                occurrence of cracking at the second cementing                interface, according to the principle of energy                conservation

GA 2022884576
AB    NOVELTY - Low energy consumption carbon dioxide capture                and compression refining process involves centering                absorption process on an absorption tower,                supplementing by a deep purification tower and                pressurization equipment, entering flue gas to the                absorption process after passing through the deep                purification tower. The desorption process is                centered on a desorption tower and a boiler,                supplemented by a lean-rich liquid heat exchanger,                an MVR semi-rich liquid generator and a                liquefaction system. The rich liquid produced by                the absorption process enters the desorption tower                through the lean-rich liquid heat exchanger and the                MVR semi-rich liquid generator to generate desorbed                gas and lean liquid. The liquefaction system is                connected with the desorption tower and other                process is the part between the above two,                including rich liquid recovery system and amine                recovery system.
   USE - Low energy consumption carbon dioxide capture                and compression refining process used for a carbon                dioxide-rich gas washing liquid and an amine                recovery system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high trapping and desorption                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low energy consumption carbon dioxide capture                and compression refining process involves centering                absorption process on an absorption tower,                supplementing by a deep purification tower and                pressurization equipment, entering flue gas to the                absorption process after passing through the deep                purification tower. The desorption process is                centered on a desorption tower and a boiler,                supplemented by a lean-rich liquid heat exchanger,                an MVR semi-rich liquid generator and a                liquefaction system. The rich liquid produced by                the absorption process enters the desorption tower                through the lean-rich liquid heat exchanger and the                MVR semi-rich liquid generator to generate desorbed                gas and lean liquid. The liquefaction system is                connected with the desorption tower and other                process is the part between the above two,                including rich liquid recovery system and amine                recovery system. The rich liquid recovery system is                connected with the absorption tower, and the cafbon                dioxide-rich gas scrubbing liquid produced in the                absorption tower is collected and transported to                the desorption tower for processing. The amine                recovery system is connected to the desorption                tower, and the amine vapor is recovered to the                desorption tower for reuse.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The flue gas                  temperature is reduced to 30-50degrees Celsius by                  sodium hydroxide dilute solution circulating                  spray. The flue gas treated by the absorption                  tower is discharged from the top of the tower to                  the nitrogen production system, and nitrogen is                  collected. The absorption process is also                  provided with a washing liquid pump, the washing                  liquid pump passes the washing liquid through a                  heat pump and a cooler to a temperature of                  30-50degrees Celsius, and enter the top of the                  absorption tower again. The rich liquid after                  absorbing carbon dioxide is divided into three                  parts after passing through the lean-rich liquid                  heat exchanger 1 at the bottom of the tower enter                  the desorption tower entering the MVR semi-rich                  liquid generator 2 after the second portion of                  the rich liquid pump, the liquid portion returns                  to the poor and rich liquid heat exchanger 1. The                  other portion passes through the semi-rich liquid                  heat exchanger, and entered the MVR semi-rich                  liquid generator 1 for reaction. The gas portion                  is sent to the desorption tower after the rich                  liquid gas compressor, and the liquid portion is                  enriched. The liquid pump is sent to the                  desorption tower. The gas portion is sent to the                  desorption tower after the rich liquid gas                  compressor. The third part is sent to the                  desorption tower after entering the second                  lean-rich liquid heat exchanger. The stripping                  gas is divided into two portions at the top of                  the tower includes entering the MVR semi-rich                  liquid generator 2 after the desorption gas                  compressor, and the gas portion contains amine                  vapor and water vapor, and directly entering the                  desorption tower for reaction. The desorption gas                  heat exchanger 2, the carbon dioxide compressor,                  the semi-rich liquid heat exchanger, the dryer,                  and then sent to the carbon dioxide storage tank                  after passing through the liquefaction system                  through the desorption gas heat exchanger 1. The                  heat generated by the heat exchange of the heat                  pump, the cooler, and the condensate heat                  exchanger is used for power plant generator                  rental. The lean liquid is divided into two                  portions at the bottom of the tower includes                  temperature is 60-65degrees Celsius after heat                  exchange with the lean-rich liquid heat exchanger                  2 and dividing into two portions, 90% of which                  pass through the lean-liquid heat exchanger and                  the lean-liquid heat exchanger, lowered to                  40degrees Celsius, and returned to the absorption                  tower to react again. The other 10% is sent to                  the amine recovery heater, and the gas portion                  contains amine vapor and water vapor, which                  directly enters the desorption tower. The liquid                  portion goes to the furnace after being reacted                  by the crystallizer, and the water vapor directly                  enters the desorption tower, heated to                  110-120degrees Celsius by the boiler and returned                  to the desorption tower to react. The condensed                  water in the boiler is deoxygenated after passing                  through the condensed water heat exchanger.

TI Gas filter for removing carbon dioxide from gas            stream, comprises active element and its housing            structure comprising gas inlet and gas outlet, active            element consists of matrix material, carbon dioxide            sorbent, and water.
AU G R
   CHEW Y
   CAMUS O
   CRITTENDEN B D
   PERERA S P
AE UNIV BATH (UBAT-C)
GA 202279578E
AB    NOVELTY - A gas filter comprises an active element and a                housing structure for the active element, where the                housing structure comprises a gas inlet and a gas                outlet, the active element consists of a matrix                material, a carbon dioxide (CO2) sorbent, and                water, where at least some of the CO2 sorbent is                embedded within the matrix material.
   USE - Gas filter for removing carbon dioxide from a                gas stream (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The filter helps reduce levels of carbon                dioxide in the atmosphere and from various other                environments such as exhaust gases or waste gases                generated in industry, business or in the home, and                more generally in enclosed environmental spaces,                e.g. in cars and offices, has economy of size,                higher loading, improved efficiency, and are                readily and efficiently regenerated for repeat                use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for
   (1) a method for removing carbon dioxide from                a gas stream comprising passing the gas stream                through the gas filter;
   (2) an active element suitable for use in the                gas filter which comprises a matrix material, a CO2                sorbet, and water, where at least some of the CO2                sorbent is embedded within the matrix                material;
   (3) a method of making an active element                consisting of a ceramic matrix material, a CO2                sorbet, and water, which involves combining at                least one clay material, a pore former, a CO2                sorbent or its precursor, and water to form a                paste, shaping the paste to form a green article,                and firing the green article to form the active                element, or involves combining at least one clay                material, a pore former, and water to form a paste,                shaping the paste to form a green article, and                firing the green article to form the active                element, impregnating the active element with a CO2                sorbent or its precursor, optionally wherein the                CO2 sorbent or its precursor is in solution;                and
   (4) a method of making an active element                comprising a polymer matrix material, a CO2                sorbent, and water, which involves forming a first                mixture comprising a first monomer, a solvent for                the first monomer, a first catalyst, water and                optionally at least one additive, forming a second                mixture comprising a second monomer, a CO2 sorbent                or its precursor, a second catalyst, combining the                first and second mixtures to form the polymer                matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The matrix material                  comprises a ceramic material and/or a polymer,                  where the matrix material is a ceramic material                  and the ceramic material comprises an inorganic                  mineral, the ceramic material comprises a clay                  material, the clay material is selected from                  kaolinite, montmorillonite-smectite, ilite,                  chlorite clay clays, the clay material is                  selected from clinoptalite, palygorskite,                  attapulgite, and/or bentonite. The matrix                  material comprises essentially of a polymer,                  where the polymer is a foamed polymer. The                  polymer polyurethane (PU), poly(vinylpyrrolidone)                  (PVP), polyimide (PI), polymers of intrinsic                  microporosity (PIMs), polyvinylidene difluoride                  (PVDF), polyethersulfone (PES), cellulose or                  biodegradable polymers such as polylactic acid                  (PLA) and/or poly (lactic-co-glycolic acid)                  (PLGA). The matrix material comprises mesopores                  and/or macropores. The CO2 sorbent is selected                  from an alkali metal carbonate, for e.g.                  potassium carbonate (K2CO3), sodium carbonate                  (Na2CO3), lithium carbonate (Li2CO3). The water                  is embedded within the matrix material. The water                  at least some or all of it is provided by an                  additional solid material. The additional solid                  material is particulate and is selected from                  aluminium oxide, zeolite, silicate, (e.g. silica                  gel), metal organic framework (MOF), and/or clay,                  (e.g. kaolinite). The active element comprises a                  combination of a matrix material which is a                  ceramic material, a CO2 sorbent, and water, plus                  a matrix material which is a polymer, a CO2                  sorbent, and water, where at least some CO2                  sorbent is embedded within the matrix. The CO2                  sorbent is present in the form of its precursor,                  for e.g. at least one bicarbonate. The gas filter                  or active matrix is heated in order to                  (re)generate the CO2 sorbent.

TI Supercritical energy-saving extraction device for            medicinal materials, has carbon dioxide pipe that            surrounds outer wall of gas-liquid separation device,            and carbon dioxide pipe is connected to grinding            chamber, and third valve is arranged on carbon dioxide            pipe.
AU YE X
AE GUANGDONG FENGCHUN PHARM CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202276167Y
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a feeding port (18) that is                provided with a sealing cover. A discharge pipe                (13) and a circulation pipe (24) are drawn out from                bottom of an extraction chamber (10). The                circulation pipe is provided with a circulating                pump (26) and a first valve (23). The discharge                pipe is connected to a solid-liquid separation                device (14). The discharge pipe is provided with a                second valve (12). The solid-liquid separation                device leads out a liquid outlet pipe (28) and a                medicine residue outlet pipe (15). The liquid                outlet pipe is connected to a gas-liquid separation                device (29). The gas-liquid separation device leads                out a gas outlet pipe (33) and a medicinal                component outlet pipe (30). The outlet pipe is                connected to a carbon dioxide (CO2) compressor                (34). The CO2 compressor leads out a CO2 pipe (32).                The CO2 pipe surrounds outer wall of gas-liquid                separation device. The CO2 pipe is connected to a                grinding chamber (3). A third valve (22) is on CO2                pipe.
   USE - Supercritical energy-saving extraction device                for medicinal materials for extracting medical                components of Chinese medicinal material in                pharmacy.
   ADVANTAGE - The device effectively reduces the system                energy consumption, and the material is repeatedly                used. The extraction efficiency is improved. The                device cost is effectively reduced, and the                refrigeration energy consumption is reduced. The                carbon dioxide is recycled, and has good                environment-friendly effect. The circulating                supercritical carbon dioxide is used as the coolant                between the grinding plate and the grinding block,                and avoids the problem of heat absorption of the                gas-liquid separating device.

TI Device for testing solid carbon content of concrete, in which carbon dioxide concentration data is recorded and stored at beginning, process and end of test after entering volume parameters of test chamber, and test value of carbon sequestration is displayed directly.

   WANG J
   ZHANG Y

   CHINA WEST CONSTR HUNAN CO LTD (CSCE-C)
GA 2022363521
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a sensor (8) that is provided with a temperature sensor, a humidity sensor, an air pressure sensor and a carbon dioxide concentration sensor. The sensor acquires the temperature, humidity, air pressure and carbon dioxide gas concentration data of a gas pretreatment chamber (7) and a test chamber (1) in real time. A control module (11) controls the opening and closing of the gas delivery pipeline and an electric valve (9) according to the input test chamber condition parameters. The start and stop of an air humidity regulator (3), an air temperature regulator (4), an air compressor (5) and a UV generator (6) is provided. The carbon dioxide concentration data is recorded and stored at the beginning, process and end of the test after entering the volume parameters of the test chamber and the weight parameters of the concrete specimen. The test value of carbon sequestration is displayed directly after the calculation.
   USE - Device for testing solid carbon content of concrete for industrial applications or in underground related technology, carbon, capture and storage technology for reducing global carbon dioxide emission.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can independently set the temperature, humidity, air pressure, ultraviolet, the influence of the concrete carbon amount of the gas concentration of the factor size, can measure different working conditions, and the solid carbon amounts of different concrete proportion. The device can set the test chamber to satisfy the temperature and humidity of gas, concentration and pressure. The concrete test block to be tested in the sealed test chamber for different age of carbon absorption process can be tested according to test chamber carbon dioxide gas concentration data recorded by control module to obtain concrete carbon amounts data.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a testing method of concrete carbon fixing amount testing device.

PN WO2020158752-A1
TI Regenerating zeolite adsorbent used in pressure swing adsorption device for reducing carbon gas concentration, using synthesis gas comprising hydrogen gas, carbon monoxide gas and carbon dioxide gas as main components and treating.
AU KOKORO H
   NORITOSHI Y
   HARUKA N
   KAZUTO N
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 202075248L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for regenerating zeolite adsorbent involves using synthesis gas comprising hydrogen gas, nitrogen gas, carbon monoxide gas and carbon dioxide gas as main components, discharging the main components from the gasification furnace, supplying carbon dioxide gas to adsorb in the synthesis gas, recovering used zeolite adsorbent from the pressure swing adsorption device, treating the zeolite adsorbent under an oxygen atmosphere, reusing the regenerated zeolite adsorbent as an adsorbent of pressure swing adsorption device, and passing the unused zeolite adsorbent until the specific surface area calculated by Brunauer, Emmett and Teller (BET) method using nitrogen gas becomes -15 to -1 %.
   USE - The method is used in a pressure swing adsorption device for reducing carbon gas concentration, recycling general waste and industrial waste introduced, and carbon dioxide by-produced by various industries and the organic substance is used as raw material for plastics, and resins, and as additive for cosmetics, beverages, chemical substances, fuel (jet fuel), and foods.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the carbon dioxide gas concentration, has sufficient purification effect and improves the reusability of the adsorbent capable of stably exhibiting the purifying ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a regenerated adsorbent prepared using calcined product of used zeolite adsorbent, attaching the carbon compounds and sulfur compounds to the pores in the zeolite crystal structure;
   (2) a method for producing an organic substance from a synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide by microbial fermentation involving using the regenerated adsorbent to reduce the carbon dioxide gas concentration in the synthesis gas and purification process, supplying the synthesis gas by the synthesis gas purification process to the microbial fermentation tank, performing microbial fermentation process to obtain organic substance-containing solution by the microbial fermentation, heating the organic substance-containing liquid to normal temperature or higher to 500 degrees C under a condition of 0.01-1000 kPa to separate liquid or solid component containing microorganisms and gas component containing organic substances, performing liquefaction process of condensing and liquefying a gas component obtained by the separation process, and purification process of purifying an organic substance from the liquefaction product obtained by the liquefaction process; and
   (3) organic substance obtained using the above-mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method involves attaching the used zeolite adsorbent, carbon compounds and sulfur compounds to the pores in the zeolite crystal structure, where the specific surface area calculated by BET method using nitrogen gas is -15 % or more based on the unused zeolite. The method involves reusing the heat of condensation generated in the liquefaction process as heat source when purifying an organic substance from the organic substance-containing liquid. The method involves obtaining the synthesis gas by gasifying a carbon source. Preferred Components: The syngas discharged from the gasification furnace comprises tar component. The zeolite used is an LSX type zeolite. The carbon source comprises waste. The organic substance comprises alcohol having 1-6 carbon atoms.

   ADVANTAGE - The composition exhibits zero or near zero carbon emissions, and reduces potential environmental impact when compared to materials produced using fossil fuel sources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A blended polymer composition comprises a component (A) comprising one or more bio-based polymer composition (a1), a component (B) comprising one or more recycled polymer compositions (b1), and an optional component (C) comprising one or more virgin petrochemical polymer compositions (c1). The blended polymer composition exhibits an emission factor of 1.0 kg carbon dioxide/kg or less calculated from weight percentage and emission factor of each component using the formula: Eblend = pa1.Epa1+pb1.Epb1+pc1.Epc1 (I), where pa1 is the weight percentage of the one or more bio-based polymer compositions, pb1 is the weight percent of the one or more recycled polymer compositions, pc1 is the weight percent of the one or more virgin petrochemical polymer compositions, Epa1 is the calculated emission for the one or more bio-based polymer compositions in kg carbon dioxide/kg polymer, Epb1 is the calculated emission for the one or more recycled polymer compositions in kg carbon dioxide/kg polymer, Epc1is the calculated emission for the one or more virgin petrochemical polymer compositions in kg carbon dioxide/kg polymer, and Eblend is the calculated emission for the blended polymer composition in kg carbon dioxide/kg blended polymer composition. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) article, which comprises the blended polymer composition; and
   (2) manufacture of blended polymer composition, which involves preparing a blended polymer composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The blended polymer composition comprises 2.4-59.3 wt.% bio-based polymer composition, and 40.7-97.6 wt.% recycled polymer composition. The bio-based polymer composition comprises 2.6-55.5 wt.% linear low density polyethylene having melt flow index (MFI) of 0.1-40 g/10 minutes according to ASTM D1238 at 190 degrees C/2.16 kg and density of 0.905-0.955 g/cm3, 2.5-54.3 wt.% low density polyethylene having MFI of 0.1-40 g/10 minutes according to ASTM D1238 at 190 degrees C/2.16 kg and density of 0.905-0.945 g/cm3, and 2.4-51.3 wt.% high density polyethylene having MFI of 0.1-50 g/10 minutes, and density of 0.945-0.963 g/cm3. Preferred Properties: The bio-based polymer composition has MFI of 0.05-400 g/10 minutes according to ASTM D1238 at 190 degrees C/2.16 kg and 0.1-130 g/10 minutes according to ASTM D1238 at 230 degrees C/2.16 kg, and density of 0.800-0.970 g/cm3 according to ASTM D1505/D792. The blended polymer composition has an emission factorblend of (-1)-1 g carbon dioxide/kg. Preferred Components: The recycled polymer composition comprises post-industrial polymer resin, post-consumer polymer resin, and reground polymer resin. The blended polymer composition is prepared by a melt blending process. The bio-based polymer composition comprises bio-based polyethylene, bio-based polypropylene or bio-based ethylene vinyl acetate copolymer. The recycled polymer composition comprises recycled polyethylene, recycled polypropylene or recycled ethylene vinyl acetate copolymer. The bio-based polymer composition is one or more chosen from high density polyethylene, low density polyethylene, and linear low density polyethylene. Preferred Process: The melt blending process involves combining the pellets or flake-shaped one or more biobased polymer compositions and pellets or flake-shaped recycled polymer compositions. The article is prepared by extrusion molding, coextrusion molding, extrusion coating, injection molding, injection blow molding, inject stretch blow molding, thermoforming, cast film extrusion, blown film extrusion, foaming, extrusion blow-molding, injection stretched blow-molding, rotomolding, pultrusion, calendering, additive manufacturing, and lamination. The preparation of composition involves extrusion molding, coextrusion molding, extrusion coating, injection molding, injection blow molding, inject stretch blow molding, thermoforming, cast film extrusion, blown film extrusion, foaming, extrusion blow-molding, injection stretched blow-molding, rotomolding, pultrusion, calendering, additive manufacturing, and/or lamination. The recycled polymer composition is obtained from recycling process of selecting a polymer composition from post-consumer residue, processing the polymer composition to generate polymer composition flakes, cleaning the polymer composition flakes, and extruding the polymer composition flakes to generate polymer composition pellets. The preparation of the blended polymer composition further involves adding the one or more bio-based polymer compositions and the one or more recycled polymer compositions to an extruder, melt extruding the one or more bio-based polymer compositions and the one or more recycled polymer compositions as a blended polymer composition, and forming pellets, films, sheets, or molded articles from the blended polymer composition.

PN PL418090-A1
TI Fluorescent thermo-chemical treatment of high-speed steel tools for metal processing, comprises e.g. placing tool in vacuum tank, applying voltage between tool and tank, introducing hydrogen, heating tool, and creating reactive atmosphere.
AU SOBIESKI S
   BABUL T
   TROJANOWSKI J
AE MECHANIKI PRECYZYJN INST (MECH-Non-standard)
   TIZ IMPLEMENTS SP ZOO (TIZI-Non-standard)
GA 201830945E
AB    NOVELTY - Fluorescent thermo-chemical treatment of high-speed steel tools for metal processing, comprises: placing tool in vacuum tank of 2x 10-1 mbar; applying voltage of 400-1200 V between tool and tank housing; then gradually introducing hydrogen; heating tool to temperature of 420-470 degrees C; introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide into the tank; and creating reactive atmosphere composed of 90% hydrogen, 8% nitrogen and 2% carbon dioxide, where the total flow is set at not more than 200 liters/hour and pressure is set at 0.1-0.5 mbar and the process is carried out time for 0.5-1.5 hours.
   USE - The methods are useful for fluorescent thermo-chemical treatment of high-speed steel tools for metal processing and wood processing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Fluorescent thermo-chemical treatment of high-speed steel tools for metal processing, comprises: placing a tool in a vacuum tank of 2x 10-1 mbar; applying a voltage of 400-1200 V between the tool and the tank housing; then gradually introducing 50-200 liters/hour of hydrogen; heating the tool to a temperature of 420-470 degrees C; introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide into the tank; and creating a reactive atmosphere composed of 90% hydrogen, 8% nitrogen and 2% carbon dioxide, where the total flow is set at not more than 200 liters/hour and pressure is set at 0.1-0.5 mbar and the process is carried out time for 0.5-1.5 hours. Fluorescent thermo-chemical treatment of high-speed steel tools for wood processing, comprises placing a tool in a vacuum tank of 2x 10-1 mbar, applying a voltage of 400-1200 V between the tool and the tank housing, then gradually introducing 100-400 ml/hour of hydrogen by heating the processed tool to a temperature of 470-510 degrees C, introducing nitrogen and carbon dioxide into the tank to form a reactive atmosphere composed of 75% hydrogen, 20% nitrogen and 5% carbon dioxide, where the total flow is determined at a level of not less than 400 ml/hour and its pressure is 1.5-3 mbar and the process is carried out within 1-2 hours.

AU ZHU J
   ZHU Z
   XU Y
   YUAN Y
AE SHANGHAI BONASAIEN PHARM R & D CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202263169A
AB    NOVELTY - Regulating pH value of calcium D-pantothenate comprises contacting solution containing calcium D-pantothenate with carbon dioxide, and subjecting contact product to solid-liquid separation to obtain liquid phase containing calcium D-pantothenate.
   USE - The method is useful for regulating pH value of calcium D-pantothenate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adjusts the pH value of calcium D-pantothenate with carbon dioxide, which can effectively avoid the problem of introducing acidic impurities and improve the purity of the calcium pantothenate product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amount of the carbon dioxide is such that the pH value of the contact product reaches 6.8-8.Preferred Method: The temperature of the contact is 15-40degrees Celsius. The preparation method of the solution containing calcium D-pantothenate comprises: mixing methanol crystals of calcium D-pantothenate with water, where with respect to 500g D-pantothenate calcium methanol crystals, the consumption of water is 750-1500g, and/or, the content of D-calcium pantothenate in the methanol crystal of D-calcium pantothenate is 78-80 wt.%, and the content of methanol is 17.4-19.3 wt.%, and/or, the mixing conditions include: a temperature of 15-40degrees Celsius and time of 10-60 minutes, and/or, the preparation method of the solution containing calcium D-pantothenate further comprises: removing methanol from the mixture obtained by mixing the methanol crystals of calcium D-pantothenate with water. The preparation method of the solution containing D-calcium pantothenate further comprises: removing methanol in the mixture obtained by mixing D-calcium pantothenate methanol crystals with water, and the method for removing methanol is concentration under reduced pressure. The condition for the concentration under reduced pressure includes: the temperature is 45-50degrees Celsius, and the vacuum gauge pressure is 0.08-0.09 MPa. The method further comprises drying the liquid phase containing calcium D-pantothenate. The drying temperature is 80-120degrees Celsius.

PN CN108587774-A
TI Preparation of peanut oil involves frying peanut kernels, crushing to obtain peanut powder, subjecting peanut powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and decontaminating peanut oil.
AU OUYANG D
AE ANHUI LAOOUMOFANG FOOD TRADE CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018838194
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of peanut oil involves (1) frying peanut kernels and crushing to 130-150 mesh to obtain peanut powder, (2) subjecting the peanut powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under pressure of 28-32 MPa at 45-55 degrees C for 150-180 minutes and controlling carbon dioxide flow rate at 23-30 L/hour to obtain peanut oil, and (3) decontaminating peanut oil.
   USE - Preparation of peanut oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of peanut oil ensuring high yield, enhanced quality and improved nutritional value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of peanut kernel, tea and lemongrass is 100:(2-4):(1-1.5).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adsorbent comprises diatomaceous earth, montmorillonite and activated carbon at weight ratio of (6-8):(6-7):1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (2), the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction uses mixture of diethyl ether, ethanol and methanol at mass ratio of (0.5-0.8):(4-6):1 as entrainer. The content of adsorbent is 0.8-1.5 wt.% of peanut oil. Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of entrainer is controlled at 7-10%. Preferred Process: In step (1), the method of frying involves mixing peanut kernel, tea and lemon grass evenly, frying at 55-65 degrees C for 25-35 minutes, screening out tea leaves and lemongrass and stirring at 1300-1500 degrees C for 3-5 minutes. The method of removing impurities involves adding adsorbent to peanut butter, stirring for 30-40 minutes and filtering to obtain peanut oil.

TI Improving microalgae oil content comprises adding            microalgae culture medium and Scenedesmus acutus HY-D3            seed liquid to photobioreactor, introducing carbon            dioxide gas and adding Parachlorella kessleri FSH-Y3 to            culture microalgae.

   LI X
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP DALIAN RES (SNPC-C)
GA 202159103N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving microalgae oil content                comprises adding a microalgae culture medium and                Scenedesmus acutus HY-D3 (CGMCC No. 15298) seed                liquid to a photobioreactor, and introducing carbon                dioxide gas, at the same time, adding Parachlorella                kessleri FSH-Y3 (CGMCC No. 9238) to the culture                system to culture the microalgae.
   USE - Method for improving microalgae oil                content.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves solid carbon fixation                efficiency of the microalgae culture system to high                concentration carbon dioxide and improves yield of                microalgae.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae culture                  medium comprises BG-11, SE or Bold's Basal Medium                  (BBM) liquid medium for culturing microalgae. The                  carbon dioxide gas is waste gas or flue gas                  containing sulfur dioxide and carbon dioxide,                  where sulfur dioxide volume content is not                  greater than 0.06%. Preferred Composition: The                  ratio of S.acutus HY-D3 seed liquid to the                  microalgae culture medium added to the                  photobioreactor is 1:20-1:5. The method involves                  introducing 5-45 vol.%, preferably 10-30 vol.%                  carbon dioxide gas. The ratio of P.kessleri                  FSH-Y3 seed liquid to the microalgae culture                  medium added to the photobioreactor is 1:40-1:20.                  Preferred Method: The preparation method of the                  S.acutus HY-D3 seed liquid involves inoculating                  S.acutus HY-D3 to a microalgae culture medium at                  pH value of 7-9 and temperature of 20-30 degrees                  C, where the illumination period is 24 hours, the                  light-dark time ratio is 14:10, and the                  illumination intensity is 2000-20000 Lux,                  vibrating and culturing to logarithmic phase to                  obtain S.acutus HY-D3 seed liquid. The                  preparation method of P. kessleri FSH-Y3 seed                  liquid involves adjusting the pH of the                  microalgae culture medium to 10-12, maintaining                  the temperature at 20-30 degrees C, where the                  illumination period is 24 hours, the light-dark                  time ratio is 14:10, and the illumination                  intensity is 2000-10000 Lux, shaking and                  culturing to logarithmic phase to obtain the                  P.kessleri FSH-Y3 seed liquid. The FSH-Y3 seed                  liquid is added to the photobioreactor in batches                  every 24-72 hours and the amount of each addition                  is averaged according to the total amount added.                  The method involves culturing until the growth                  stable period is over, and harvesting the                  microalgae cell by centrifugation or                  sedimentation. Preferred Condition: The                  temperature of the whole microalgae culture                  system is maintained at 10-30 degrees C, the                  illumination period is 24 hours, the light-dark                  time ratio is 14:10, and the illumination                  intensity is 2000-20000 Lux.

PN CN112390803-A; CN112390803-B
TI Preparation of porous organic molecular cage material used as adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide gas, involves using 1,3,5-trialdehyde benzene and aminocyclohexenediamine as precursors in dichloromethane solution.
AU OU J
   LI Y
   MA S
   YE M
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 2021212162
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of porous organic molecular cage material involves using 1,3,5-trialdehyde benzene and aminocyclohexenediamine as precursors in a dichloromethane solution.
   USE - Preparation of porous organic molecular cage material used as adsorbent for adsorbing carbon dioxide gas in gas adsorption process (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared porous organic molecular cage has excellent stability and large specific surface area, and is capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide gas even at 273-298K.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the porous organic molecular cage material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves mixing 17-50 mg 1,3,5-trialdehyde benzene and 18-52 mg aminocyclohexenediamine, reacting in 0.5-1 mL dichloromethane solution at room temperature, performing amine-aldehyde condensation reaction at 20-25 degrees C for 3-5 days, carrying out the reaction in the presence of catalyst trifluoroacetic acid, evaporating the solvent, connecting the porous organic molecular cage material by imine bonds, and drying the product. The material is formed by stacking single molecular cage containing amine-containing compound of formula (I). Preferred Properties: The catalyst trifluoroacetic acid has a volume of 0.5-1 mu L. The porous organic molecular cage has a specific surface area of 900-1100 m2/g and an average pore diameter of 0.5-1 nm. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of aldehyde in 1,3,5-trialdehyde benzene to amine in amino cyclohexenediamine is 0.5-1.5. Preferred Process: The process of forming a porous organic molecular cage involves covalently bonding molecular cage is in the manner of (4+6), which is four monomers containing three aldehyde functional groups and six monomers containing two amino functional groups, and forming crystalline structure having high uniformity, based on this uniformity, the pore size distribution of the molecular cage material is more uniform.

The pore volume occupied by pore diameter more than 15 nm in aluminum hydroxide is 12-20% of the total pore volume. The aluminum hydroxide has pore volume of greater than or equal to0.90 mL/g, the specific surface area of 290-310 m2/g, the infrared total acid content greater than or equal to0.360 mmol/g, and Bronsted acid to Lewis acid ratio of greater than or equal to0.04.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The method comprises                  (1) adding the precursors of magnesium,                  lanthanum, iron and copper respectively into the                  water to obtain 0.9-1.2 mol/l solutions of                  magnesium, lanthanum, iron and copper                  respectively, and mixing the solutions of                  magnesium, lanthanum, iron and copper to obtain                  precursor solution, (2) adding the precursor                  solution to the saturated complexing agent                  solution or the precipitating agent solution,                  post-processing, obtaining a dry sample, and                  grounding the dry sample and calcining to obtain                  the oxygen carrier for the production of syngas                  and the production of carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen.Preferred Method: The method comprises when                  the complexing agent is citric acid,                  post-processing, and the specific process of                  obtaining the dried sample is: stirring and                  evaporating to a gel state, placing in a drying                  oven at 120degrees Celsius for drying, and                  grinding the obtained material into dry powder;                  when the complexing agent is glycine,                  post-processing, and the specific process of                  obtaining the dry sample is: stirring and                  evaporating to a gel state, and placing in a                  muffle furnace at 250degrees Celsius for                  combustion, grinding the resulting material to a                  dry powder; when the precipitating agent is                  ammonium carbonate, post-processing, and the                  specific process of obtaining the dry sample is                  as follows: adjusting the pH to 8-11, and                  stirring, obtaining the precipitate by                  filtration, and drying the precipitate to obtain                  a dry sample. The specific process of calcination                  is as follows: heating from room temperature at a                  heating rate of 1-10degrees Celsius/minutes to                  400-500degrees Celsius for 2-6 hours, heating to                  600-1000degrees Celsius at a heating rate of                  1-10degrees Celsius/minutes and calcining for 2-6                  hours.Preferred Components: The ratio of (1-x) to                  y is 6:2-8. The value of x is 0.2-0.6. The                  precursor of iron is ferric chloride, ferric                  nitrate and ferric sulfate; the precursor of                  copper is cupric nitrate; the precursor of                  magnesium is magnesium nitrate; and the precursor                  of lanthanum is lanthanum nitrate. The complexing                  agent is citric acid or glycine; and the                  precipitating agent is ammonium carbonate. When                  the complexing agent is citric acid, the ratio of                  the amount of citric acid to the total amount of                  cationic substances in the precursor solution is                  1.5-2:1; when the complexing agent is glycine,                  the ratio of the amount of ammonia in the                  saturated glycine solution to the amount of                  nitrate in the precursor solution is 1.1-1.5:1;                  and when the precipitant is ammonium carbonate,                  the amount of ammonium carbonate is 1.2-2.0 times                  the total amount of magnesium, lanthanum, iron                  and copper.

AB    NOVELTY - Load-type Ru-based catalyst comprises titanium oxide catalyst carrier, where the titanium oxide catalyst carrier is added with active component Ru occupying 1-3% of the weight of titaniu oxide catalyst carrier and chromium ion auxiliary agent with the ratio of Cr ion mol amount and Cr ion mol amount and titanium ion mol amount of not more than 8 %.
   USE - Load-type Ru-based catalyst for carbon dioxide methanation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The load-type Ru-based catalyst exhibits excellent low-temperature activity in the carbon dioxide methanation reaction, selectivity and excellent stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing load-type Ru-based catalyst, which involves weighing chromium nitrate nonahydrate dissolved in a small amount of distilled water, then adding tetrabutyl titanate and a little nitric acid in the chromium nitrate nonahydrate solution, mixing uniformly, and then transferring the mixed solution to the hydrothermal reaction kettle, then putting the hydrothermal reaction kettle in the oven for heating, entrifuging and washing drying, roasting, grinding to obtain chromium doped titanium oxide catalyst carrier, weighing ruthenium chloride dissolved in distilled water to prepare water solution, adding the catalyst carrier obtained, stirring at room temperature, then water bath heating and stirring to dryness, drying, roasting, grinding and reducing to obtain load type Ru-based catalyst for carbon dioxide methanation reaction.
