   ADVANTAGE - The method: can accurately adjust the water quality, can reduce the generation of air bubbles, and improve the utilization rate of carbon dioxide gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises carbonic acid solution pipeline between the bottom outlet of the reaction tank and the inlet of the diffuser is provided with a control valve or a flow limiting hole plate or a throttle hole capable of precisely controlling the flow rate and the pressure; the diffuser is set in the water to be processed, the diffuser is a hollow cylindrical object whose one end is opened and the other end is sealed, a channel for releasing carbonic acid solution is opened on the cylindrical side wall of said diffuser, the channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution is a small hole or a narrow gap, or a combination of two kinds of small hole and narrow gap; the channel capable of releasing the carbonic acid solution can generate a certain back pressure and make the supersaturated carbonic acid solution to be sprayed to the water to be processed with a certain outlet pressure; the pressure difference between the outlet pressure of the diffuser and the pressure of the water to be treated is more than 2 bar, diffuser is set in the raw water or water pipeline to be processed with water flow, the upstream of the water flow; half side wall of the diffuser is opened with channel for releasing carbonic acid solution, the channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution is one or more, or one row or multiple rows, the diffuser is inserted into the raw water or water pipe in the direction vertical to the water flow direction; one side of the diffuser opened with the channel for releasing the carbonic acid solution is opposite to the upstream of the water flow; the carbonated solution in the diffuser is reversely sprayed from the channel releasing the carbonic acid solution to the water flow and capable of generating vortex strengthening mixing effect, or the diffuser is set in the water pool without water flow to be processed; the water pond or sewage tank; the double-side wall of the diffuser or the whole cylindrical side wall is opened with multiple or multiple rows of channels for releasing carbonic acid solution; (ii) using carbon dioxide gas that maintains a temperature and pressure, the carbon dioxide gas pipeline is conveyed to the reaction tank; (iii) carbon dioxide gas with temperature and pressure, the top of the reaction tank is divided into two paths, one path enters into the Venturi nozzle of the reaction tank top through the gas pipeline, the other path directly enters the reaction tank from the middle upper part of the reaction tank, at the same time, the reaction water is sent to the Venturi nozzle on the top of the reaction tank from the tank top through the water pipeline after being pressurized to pressure by the water pump, in the Venturi nozzle of the reaction tank top, the carbon dioxide gas is pre-mixed with the pressurized reaction water, after forming the gas-water mixture with pressure, it is injected into the reaction tank filled with carbon dioxide gas in fog shape, because the pressure in the reaction tank is large, carbon dioxide gas is quickly dissolved in the gas-water mixture to generate carbonic acid solution, along with the continuous feeding of the carbon dioxide gas, or continuously entering the tank along with the gas-water mixture with pressure; lifting the pressure in the tank to the set pressure; converting the carbonic acid solution in the reaction tank into saturated carbonate solution; the saturated carbonic acid solution is pressed to a control valve or a flow limiting hole plate or a throttle hole capable of accurately controlling the flow and pressure through the pipeline of the reaction tank bottom; the control valve or the flow limiting hole plate or the throttle hole seals the saturated carbonic acid solution between the reaction tank and the control valve or the flow limiting hole plate or the throttle hole, because the continuous pressure and carbon dioxide gas in the reaction tank are dissolved, saturated carbonic acid solution is supersaturated carbonic acid solution; supersaturated carbonic acid solution through the valve core or limiting hole plate or throttle hole in the open control valve, conveying to the diffuser through the pipeline; supersaturated carbonic acid solution is designed on the diffuser to release carbonic acid solution channel, namely small hole and/or narrow gap to form high-concentration carbonic acid solution and little carbon dioxide micro-bubble mixed liquid is reversely sprayed into the water, the carbonic acid solution and the alkaline substance in water meet and begin to perform acid-base neutralization reaction, or the carbonate solution reacts with calcium ion or calcium carbonate in water and small amount of carbon dioxide micro-bubble is absorbed by water flow; and (iv) using downstream of the water flow in the raw water or water pipe or the water pool, the water outlet of the water pool or sewage tank is set with water quality online detector, the water quality online detector is connected with the signal receiver; the signal receiver is connected with the input end of the PLC controller, the output end of the PLC controller is connected with the control valve, the water quality online detector transmits signal to the signal receiver in real time, the signal received by the signal receiver controls the opening size of the control valve after being processed by the PLC to control the feeding amount of the carbonic acid solution.

AU ZHANG L
   WU J
AE UNIV FUDAN (UYFU-C)
GA 2021804529
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of urea comprises preparing carbon                black loaded catalyst by water phase synthesis                method and loading the catalyst on carbon paper;                clamping the carbon paper loaded with the catalyst                and fixing by glassy carbon electrode as working                electrode, placing in an electrochemical reaction                tank, setting reference electrode and counter                electrode, and adding electrolyte; and introducing                nitric oxide and carbon dioxide into the                electrochemical reaction tank under the condition                of ensuring the reaction environment without                oxygen, applying preset reducing potential to the                working electrode, reacting for a preset time under                the condition of constant potential, and realizing                the electrochemical catalytic nitric oxide and                carbon dioxide synthesis urea under normal                temperature and normal pressure.
   USE - The method is used for synthesizing                urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method avoids the harsh high temperature                and high pressure reaction condition; it has high                efficiency, mild reaction condition, small energy                consumption, and the reaction is not limited by                liquid phase reactant consumption; the gas phase                reactant can be continuously supplied; using                exhaust gas nitrogen oxide and greenhouse gas                carbon dioxide as raw material, synthesizing the                product urea with high added value; and at the same                time, it creates a new direction for industrial                production of urea and environment treatment.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon black                  loaded catalyst comprises copper palladium nano                  particles loaded on carbon black, nickel                  nanoparticles loaded on carbon black, copper                  phthalocyanine loaded on carbon black, carbon                  black loaded on carbon black, and cobalt                  phthalocyanine loaded on carbon black. The                  reference electrode is silver/silver chloride.                  The counter electrode is platinum sheet. The                  electrolyte is 0.1 M potassium bicarbonate..                  Preferred Method: The copper-palladium                  nanoparticle loaded on carbon black is prepared                  by ultrasonically dispersing carbon black in                  deionized water, introducing argon gas to remove                  oxygen, adding hydrochloric acid solution of                  copper chloride solution and palladium chloride,                  slowly dripping sodium borohydride aqueous                  solution under stirring condition, reacting, and                  ultrasonically dispersing in ethanol solution                  containing perfluorinated sulfonic acid film. The                  cobalt phthalocyanine loaded on carbon black is                  prepared by ultrasonically dispersing cobalt                  phthalocyanine and carbon black in ethanol                  solution containing perfluorinated sulfonic acid                  film.

Mixing of liquefied hydrocarbon gases with absorbent is carried out in an amount required for formation after completion of mixing of absorbent water layer, containing molecular sulphur extracted from purified hydrocarbon liquid, sulphur compounds and carbon dioxide, and a layer of hydrocarbon fluid with no or low content of molecular sulphur, sulphur compounds and carbon dioxide, which is then decanted from the aqueous layer of the absorbent to obtain a purified product.

PN CN110004113-A
TI Constructing tissue engineering bone research model, by placing bone cells in culture medium, fusing cells, expanding and culturing, inoculating cells on scaffold carrier, placing in culture medium and incubating under micro-vibration.
AU ZHI W
   WU J
   WANG J
   QU S
   WENG J
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST JIAOTONG (UYSJ-C)
GA 201963527V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing a tissue engineering bone research model, involves (i) placing bone cells in a culture medium, culturing in an environment at 35-40 degrees C, 5-10% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity, and after the cells are fused to 80-90%, expanding and culturing the cells, (ii) adjusting the cell density to increase the passage amplification to 3-7 th generation, inoculating cells on the prepared scaffold carrier, placing the scaffold carrier in a culture medium, and incubating in an environment at 35-40 degrees C, 5-10% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity for 20-24 hours, and (iii) placing the incubated scaffold carrier in a device having a frequency of 1-100 Hz, amplitude of less than or equal to 50 mu m and intensity of less than 1 g, incubating under micro-vibration at 35-40 degrees C, 5-10% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity for 30-60 minutes, and continuously incubating at 35-40 degrees C, 5-10% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity for 1-21 days.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing a tissue engineering bone research model (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method accurately reflects the development and biological activity of the tissue engineered bone construct under the physiological stress environment in vivo.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Cells: The bone cell in step (i) is a mesenchymal stem cell, an osteoblast or a 3-day transfer, inoculum 3x 105 cell. Preferred Method: The micro-vibration incubation in step (ii) involves placing the incubated saffold carrier in a device having a frequency of 1-100 Hz, an amplitude of 0-50 mu m, an intensity of 0-1 g, a waveform comprising sine wave/triangle wave/square wave, and periodic force addition and culturing process. Preferred Composition: The culture medium in step (ii) comprises a alpha -dulbecco's modified essential medium (DMEM)/Ham's F-12 medium/DMEM medium comprising 10-15% fetal bovine serum or fetal calf serum, and 1-5% double antibody, which is suitable for culturing bone cells. Preferred Components: The scaffold carrier is made of chitosan, sodium alginate, gelatin, type I collagen, polyvinyl chloride, natural rubber, polyethylene, polyamide, polypropylene, polystyrene, silicone rubber, polyester, polytetrachloroethylene, polymethyl methacrylate, polyurethane, calcium phosphate, hydroxyapatite, decalcified bone matrix, titanium, tantalum, stainless steel, cobalt-based alloy, magnesium alloy, titanium alloy, aluminum alloy or bioinert ceramic. Preferred Conditions: The inoculation amount of the cells on the scaffold carrier in step (ii) is 0.1-100x 105. The culturing and incubation in steps (ii) and (iii) are carried out at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity at 37 degrees C.

TI Polyion liquid-oxide compound based catalyst useful in preparing cyclic urea compound from carbon dioxide and diamine, comprises polyion liquid as carrier for loading oxide compounds e.g. cerium(IV) oxide and cerium oxycarbonate hydrate.
AU LI J
   LI M
   WANG Y
   WANG H
   HONG Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201953431N
AB    NOVELTY - Polyion liquid-oxide compound based catalyst comprises polyion liquid as carrier for loading oxide compounds, where oxide compounds is composed of cerium(IV) oxide and cerium oxycarbonate hydrate, where Ce3+6 has mol content of 30-60% of the gross of cerium element.
   USE - The catalyst is useful in preparing cyclic urea compound from carbon dioxide and diamine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high oxygen vacancy concentration and catalytic active site.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polyion liquid-oxide compound based catalyst comprises polyion liquid as carrier for loading oxide compounds, where oxide compounds is composed of cerium(IV) oxide and cerium oxycarbonate hydrate (Ce2O(CO3)2H2O), where Ce3+6 has mol content of 30-60% of the gross of cerium element. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing polyion liquid-oxide compound based catalyst, comprising adding soluble trivalent cerium salt and water to the polyionic liquid, then adjusting pH to 7-10, stirring, filtering, precipitating, washing, drying, and then calcining to obtain final product; and
   (2) use method of polyion liquid-oxide compound catalyst in preparing cyclic urea compound from carbon dioxide and diamine, comprising reacting ethane-1,2-diamine compounds of formula (I) carbon dioxide under action of catalyst to obtain cyclic urea compounds of formulae (II) or (III).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyionic liquid is of formula (PDVPy(CnDVBTMA)(X)) (I), EGDMA-crosslinked-(P(VBTMA)(X)) (II) or (PVIm-pX) (III). The molar ratio of A to A1 is 1-20:1, and the mass ratio of monomer A4 or monomer E to A3 is 1-30:1. Preferred Method: The polyionic liquids is prepared by adding A and A1 into a reactor, and dissolving under nitrogen at 50-70 degrees C and reacting for 12-36 hours to prepare monomer A2, then adding A3 and stirring to obtain polyion liquid gel; or reacting A4 and A5 ion exchange at 40-120 degrees C for 12-36 hours to obtain monomer E, then adding A3 and stirring to obtain polyion liquid gel, where A is 4-vinylbenzyl chloride or 4-vinylbenzyl bromide, (VBTMA)(Cl) or (VBTMA)(Br), 1,3,5-tris(bromomethyl)benzene, 1,2,4,5-tetrakis(bromomethyl)benzene or 1,2,3,4,5,6-hexa(bromomethyl)benzene, A1 is TMDA, TMEDA, DABCO, EGDMA or Vim, solvent is acetonitrile, water, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran or acetone, A3 is azobisisobutyronitrile and 5,5-divinyl-2,2-bipyridine, and A5 is tetrafluoroborate or ammonium hexafluorophosphate. pH is adjusted using sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate or ammonium bicarbonate solution. The molar ratio of trivalent phosphonium salt to polyionic liquid is 4-10:1, the stirring time is 6-10 hours, the calcining temperature is 50-300 degrees C, and the calcining time is 2-5 hours.n = 1, 2 or 3;X = Cl, Br, BF4 or PF6;p = number of substitutions of VIm (3, 4 or 6);DVPy = 5,5-divinyl-2,2-bipyridine;(VBTMA)(Cl) = benzylvinyltrimethylammonium chloride;(VBTMA)(Br) = benzyl vinyltrimethylammonium bromide;TMDA = tetramethylmethylenediamine;TMEDA = tetramethylethylenediamine;TMPDA = tetramethylpropylenediamine;EGDMA = ethylene glycol dimethacrylate; andVim = 1-vinyl-1H-imidazole.

TI Method for preparing iron-nickel battery anode material, involves coating composite nano material slurry on copper mesh, drying under vacuum, and crushing and slicing to obtain negative electrode sheet of iron-nickel battery.
AU ZHU A
   GU S
   JIANG Y
   RUI M
AE UNIV YANGZHOU (UYYZ-C)
   UNIV YANGZHOU ZHENJIANG HIGH TECHNOLOGY (UYYZ-C)
GA 201938331V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves putting carbon nanotubes, graphene, nano-ferric oxide and calcium hydroxide into water to prepare a mixed suspension. The carbon dioxide is passed into the mixed suspension for gas-solid reaction. The carbon dioxide is introduced into the mixed suspension for gas-solid reaction. The carbon dioxide gas is stopped, when the pH of the suspension drops to 7. A composite nano material filter cake of carbon nanotubes loaded is filtered with carbon nanotubes, graphene and triiron tetroxide. The filter cake is mixed with an aqueous solution of hydroxyethyl cellulose to obtain composite nano material slurry. The composite nano material slurry is coated on a copper mesh, dried under vacuum, and crushed and sliced to obtain a negative electrode sheet of iron-nickel battery.
   USE - Method for preparing iron-nickel battery anode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts a gas-solid reaction of carbon dioxide and calcium hydroxide, so that the submicron calcium carbonate supports the composite material of graphene, carbon nanotubes and ferroferric oxide nano nanometer, thus preparing high gram capacity, low electrical resistance and good cycle stability.

The control is 1 m/second, and the indoor circulation ventilation system is 2000 m3/hour. The temperature, humidity and air volume are independently controlled. PN WO2018211372-A1; AU2018268375-A1; CA3063829-A1; CN110650725-A; EP3624775-A1; US2020093741-A1; BR112019023848-A2; JP2020520934-W; HK40012394-A0; IN201917044450-A; US11083691-B2; IN382463-B; EA38832-B1; JP7157085-B2
TI Composition useful for releasing carbon dioxide            following rectal administration and treating            constipation, comprises mixture of e.g. plant juice            partially neutralized with alkaline substance supported            on gum and salt of carbonic acid.
AU MERCATI V
   RAMPOLDI L
   PELUCCHINI C
AE ABOCA SOC AGRIC SPA (ABOC-Non-standard)
   ABOCA SOC AGRIC SPA (ABOC-Non-standard)
   ABOCA AGRIC SPA (ABOC-Non-standard)
   ABOCA SOC AGRIC SPA (ABOC-Non-standard)
   ABOCA SOC AGRIC SPA (ABOC-Non-standard)
GA 201891342Q
AB    NOVELTY - Composition for the release of carbon dioxide                following rectal administration, comprises a                mixture of (a) acid plant juice supported on gum                and a salt of carbonic acid, (b) plant juice,                partially neutralized with an alkaline substance,                supported on gum and a salt of carbonic acid, or                (c) acid plant juice and plant juice partially                neutralized with an alkaline substance supported on                gum and a salt of carbonic acid, where the                composition is in the form of suppository or                ovule.
   USE - The composition is useful for: the release of                carbon dioxide following rectal administration; and                treating constipation (claimed). No biological data                given.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition provides an optimum kinetics                of carbon dioxide release.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the composition comprising (i) preparing                at least one of acid plant juice supported on gum                and a salt of carbonic acid, plant juice partially                neutralized with an alkaline substance supported on                gum and a salt of carbonic acid, acid plant juice                and plant juice partially neutralized with an                alkaline substance both supported on gum and a salt                of carbonic acid, and (ii) adding to the mixtures                at least one substance for the preparation of                rectal administration formulations.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composition:                  further comprises cocoa butter, honey and/or                  beeswax; comprises hydrophilic agents, in                  particular polyethylene glycol and/or glycerin;                  and comprises a lipophilic portion consisting of                  mono-, di- and tri-glycerides of natural and/or                  synthetic fatty acids. Preferred Method: The step                  (i) for preparing the mixture comprises preparing                  a solution comprising the acid plant juice and/or                  the partially neutralized plant juice and the gum                  support, and drying by spray drying or                  freeze-drying the solution. The process comprises                  (a1) melting lipids and/or other components                  soluble or dispersible in the melt lipids, (b1)                  adding to the molten mass obtained in step (a1)                  the mixture and at least one substance for the                  preparation of rectal administration                  formulations, (c1) pouring into molds or                  containers the product obtained in step (b1) and                  cooling down until complete solidification.                  Preferred Components: The alkaline substance is                  sodium, potassium and magnesium salts of                  carbonic, citric, phosphoric, sulfuric, tartaric                  and/or malic acid. The carbonic acid salt is                  sodium acid carbonate, potassium acid carbonate,                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, calcium                  carbonate or magnesium carbonate. The plant juice                  is supported on a natural gum comprising Arabic                  gum, xanthan gum, konjac gum, tare gum, guar gum                  and/or ghatti gum. The plant juice is a fruit                  juice. The fruit juice is orange juice, lemon                  juice, pineapple juice, apple juice, blackberry                  juice, blueberry juice, grapefruit juice and/or                  pear juice. The plant juice supported on gum or                  the mixture is dried by spray dryer or                  freeze-drying. The mixture is present at 5-50                  wt.%.
   ACTIVITY - Laxative.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - 100 kg Lemon juice were placed in a dissolver                and kept under stirring at room environment. 15 kg                Sodium bicarbonate were added to the lemon juice                and the whole was kept under stirring until                reaching a pH of 4.5. After the reaction took                place, 15 kg of Arabic gum were added and the whole                was kept under stirring until complete dissolution                of gum. After the dissolution took place, the                mixture was brought to the dry state by spray                drying (nebulization in atomizer). Thus the                composition was obtained.

PN CN207853163-U
TI Micro-carbon dioxide laser tube heat dissipation device, has cooling sleeve provided with water inlet and water outlet, where water outlet is connected with water return port of water storage tank through water pipe.
AU FENG Z
AE GUANGZHOU HANNIU MACHINERY EQUIP CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201873103T
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a micro carbon dioxide laser tube radiator, comprising a shell, a water storage tank, a water pump, a condensing pipe, a water storage tank, a water pump, a condensing pipe are arranged in the shell; the water storage tank is provided with a first water inlet, a first water outlet, the first returning port, the water storage tank is filled with cooling liquid, a water pump connected between the and the condensing pipe of the water storage tank of carbon dioxide laser tube is provided with a cooling sleeve, the cooling sleeve is provided with a second water inlet and a second water outlet, a second water inlet connected with the condensing pipe through the water pipe, the second water outlet connected with first water return port of the water storage tank through the water pipe. The utility model realizes cooling by cooling the carbon dioxide laser tube, its cooling efficiency, good frost resistance, curing is not easily frozen, avoids freezing caused by laser tube damage, which effectively protects the service life of the laser tube, small volume, convenient installation, and can be directly built in the laser cutting device, it does not need external, convenient and energy saving.
DC V08 (Lasers and Masers)
MC V08-A01D; V08-A02; V08-A04B; V08-A05

PT P
PN CN108218783-A
TI Functional ionic liquid used as absorbent for absorbing carbon dioxide gas in biogas, is 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolysine ionic liquid or 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium ionic liquid.
AU WANG Q
   DING B
   XIA P
   DENG G
   ZANG S
AE UNIV LIAONING SHIHUA (ULNS-C)
GA 2018533714
AB    NOVELTY - A functional ionic liquid is 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolysine ionic liquid, 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium ionic liquid or 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium glycine ionic liquid.
   USE - Functional ionic liquid is used as absorbent for absorbing carbon dioxide gas in biogas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The functional ionic liquid having excellent absorption ability, is prepared by simple method with low consumption of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of functional ionic liquid, which involves anion exchanging 1-aminopropyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide salt through an ion exchange column, obtaining intermediate, adding equimolar amount of amino acid to resultant intermediate, magnetically stirring for 24 hours under constant temperature water bath at 30 degrees C, removing water by rotary evaporator at 70 degrees C, drying the resultant solution in vacuum oven for 48 hours, extracting with methanol and ethyl acetate, removing unreacted amino acid and vacuum drying; and
   (2) method of using functional ionic liquid as absorbent for absorbing carbon dioxide gas in biogas, which involves adding 20 ml functional ionic liquid as absorbent into absorption chamber, vacuumizing the system, filling the gas storage chamber with preset amount of biogas simulation gas mixed with methane and carbon dioxide, connecting gas storage chamber with absorption chamber, controlling the temperature in absorption chamber at 30 degrees C, and controlling the pressure at 0.8 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: 20 %Mass functional ionic liquid is used as absorbent. The volume ratio of methane with respect to carbon dioxide is 6:4.

PN CN207476479-U
TI Gas adjusting device of fungus cultivating room,            has gas conveying pipe which is connected with carbon            dioxide pipeline, and carbon dioxide concentration            sensor and temperature sensor that are communicatively            coupled with control system.
AU LI B
AE HEBEI WANRUN AGRIC TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201848047X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a fungus culturing                room air adjusting device, belonging to the fungus                planting device technology field. A fungus                cultivating room of the air adjusting device,                comprising a culturing room outside of the                temperature adjusting device and air extraction                pipeline and air supply pipeline; the air inlet end                of the air suction pipeline is in the culturing                room outlet end is connected with the temperature                adjusting device is connected with air pipeline                located in the culturing room lower part; one end                of the air outlet of the gas conveying pipe of the                cultivating room and the other end connected with                the temperature adjusting device, an air conveying                pipeline located in the cultivating room is part of                the gas conveying pipe is connected with carbon                dioxide pipeline; the gas adjusting device further                comprises a control system and a carbon dioxide                concentration sensor and a temperature sensor                communicatively coupled with the control system.                gas adjusting device of the utility model can                adjust the culturing gas in the room by the exhaust                pipeline and the air pipeline and a temperature                adjusting device. The structure of this utility                model is simple, high in degree of automation, and                capable of promoting the good growth of the                fungus.

PT P
PN WO2018075237-A1; CA3040688-A1; CN109844060-A; US2019256763-A1; MX2019004244-A1; US10662370-B2
TI Recovery of crude oil from subterranean formation            penetrated by injection well and production well            involves injecting mixture of nonionic surfactants in            carbon dioxide into subterranean formation and            recovering crude oil from the formation.
AU GRZESIAK A L
   CAMPBELL R M
   CROSTHWAITE J M
   KATIYAR A
   KNIGHT T E
   PATIL P D
   PATEL R D
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 2018321533
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of crude oil from subterranean                formation penetrated by greater than or equal to 1                injection well and production well is performed by                (i) injecting mixture of greater than or equal to 2                nonionic surfactants in carbon dioxide into                subterranean formation, where each nonionic                surfactant is prepared by alkoxylation reaction                with double metal cyanide catalyst of 1st epoxide,                2nd epoxide, and optionally 3rd epoxide, in which                the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd epoxide are different from                one another, and linear or branched aliphatic                alcohol having 1-20 carbon atoms; and (ii)                recovering crude oil from the subterranean                formation from production well.
   USE - Recovery of crude oil from subterranean                formation penetrated by injection well and                production well.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixture of 2 nonionic surfactants                demonstrates improved partitioning between the                water and carbon dioxide than is predicted by                arithmetic average of the individual surfactants                alone and enhances oil recovery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for emulsion                comprising carbon dioxide, diluent, and mixture of                greater than or equal to 2 nonionic surfactants,                where each nonionic surfactant is prepared by                alkoxylation reaction with double metal cyanide                catalyst of 1st epoxide, 2nd epoxide, and                optionally 3rd epoxide, in which the 1st, 2nd, and                3rd epoxide are different from one another, and                linear or branched aliphatic alcohol having 6-9                carbon atoms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Step of injecting                  mixture of nonionic surfactants further includes                  (a) creating foam with nonionic surfactants in                  carbon dioxide and diluent and (b) injecting foam                  of nonionic surfactants in carbon dioxide and                  diluent into subterranean formation. Preferred                  Compositions: Each linear or branched aliphatic                  alcohol has 6-9 carbon atoms. For each                  surfactant, the 1st epoxide is selected from                  propylene oxide, butylene oxide, hexene oxide,                  and/or octene oxide. For each surfactant, the 1st                  epoxide is propylene oxide and the 2nd epoxide is                  ethylene oxide, in which for each surfactant, a                  1st stage of alkoxylation reaction adds the                  propylene oxide to the linear or branched                  aliphatic alcohol and a 2nd stage of alkoxylation                  reaction adds the ethylene oxide to provide the                  nonionic surfactant. For each surfactant, the 1st                  epoxide is propylene oxide and the alkoxylation                  reaction has molar ratio of 1.5-10 moles                  propylene oxide per mole of linear or branched                  aliphatic alcohol. For each surfactant, the 2nd                  epoxide is ethylene oxide and the alkoxylation                  reaction has molar ratio of 1-25 moles ethylene                  oxide per mole of linear or branched aliphatic                  alcohol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: Mixture of nonionic                  surfactants comprises 1st nonionic surfactant (A)                  which is a block copolymer reaction product of                  aliphatic alcohol of formula (R1OH) with                  propylene oxide (PO), ethylene oxide (EO), and                  optionally butylene oxide (BO) of formula                  R1-O-BOx-POy-EOz (I), in which R1OH may be                  reacted with PO, EO, and optionally BO (I) in any                  order, and 2nd nonionic surfactant (B) which is a                  block copolymer reaction product of aliphatic                  alcohol of formula (R2OH) with PO, EO, and                  optionally BO of formula R2-O-BOl-POm-EOn (II),                  in which R2OH may be reacted with PO, EO, and                  optionally BO (II) in any order.R1, R2=linear or branched 1-20C alkyl;l, x=0-5;m, y=0-10;z=0-25; andn=z+4 to 50.Preferred Parameters: Each nonionic                  surfactant has polydispersity of 1.01-1.1.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)
MC A12-W10B; H01-D06C
IP C09K-008/584; C09K-008/594; E21B-043/16; E21B-043/22

PT P
PN CN107955122-A; CN107955122-B
TI Carbon dioxide supercritical auxiliary foamed rigid polyurethane foam comprises e.g. combined polyether and polymeric MDI, combination polyether comprises polyol, catalyst, flame retardant, foaming agent and surfactant.
AU LUO Z
   CHEN C
   XIANG M
   CHEN J
   SHEN C
AE UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
   SHAOXING HUACHUANG POLYURETHANE CO LTD (SHAO-Non-standard)
   UNIV NANJING FORESTRY (UNJF-C)
GA 201835338C
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide supercritical auxiliary foamed rigid polyurethane foam comprises combined polyether and polymeric MDI. The combination polyether comprises polyol, catalyst, flame retardant, foaming agent and surfactant. The ratio of combined polyether to polymeric MDI is 1:1.1-1.3 and 100 pts. wt. polyol, foaming agent comprises chemical foaming agent and supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2), 0.5-3 pts. wt. supercritical CO2 in combined polyether, 1-4 pts. wt. chemical foaming agent in combination with polyether and chemical foaming agent is water.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide supercritical auxiliary foamed rigid polyurethane foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The rigid polyurethane foam has good strength, heat resistance and dimensional stability and preparation method is simple and reliable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing polyurethane foam, comprising (i) mixing polyol, catalyst, flame retardant, surfactant, chemical foaming agent and physical foaming agent by physical mixing; (ii) converting liquid CO2 in high-pressure tank into a supercritical state by means of vacuum gasification and cooling liquefaction; (iii) mixing supercritical CO2 obtained in step (ii) with mixture obtained in step (i) to form a combined polyether; (iv) combining polymeric MDI and combined polyether of step (iii) at a certain material temperature and ambient temperature and mixing tip by high pressure pump for foaming; and (v) curing after a certain.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The foaming agent further comprises 6-30 pts. wt. physical foaming agent combined with polyether and physical foaming agent is HFC-245fa, HFC-365mfc, HFO-1233zd(E), HFO-1336mzz. The polyol comprises 5-30 pts. wt. polyol A and 2-10 pts. wt. polyol B. The polyol A is ethylenediamine, propylenediamine, toluene diamine, diphenylmethane diamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, polyether polyol is a initiator or monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, triisopropanolamine or phenol, formaldehyde, diethanolamine additives obtained by Mannich reaction or starting agent polyether polyol and polyol B is di(dipropylene glycol) carbonate, di(ethylene glycol) carbonate. The starting agent comprises sucrose and diethylene glycol. The catalyst comprises organic amine catalysts for normal polyurethanes and organic metal catalysts, and combined polyether comprises 2-4 pts. wt. organic amine catalyst, 1-2.5 pts. wt. organometallic catalyst, 15-25 pts. wt. flame retardant, where flame retardant is for a conventional rigid polyurethane foam and 0.2-2 pts. wt. surfactant, where surfactant is a silicone surfactant. In the step (iii) fraction of supercritical CO2 in combined polyether is 0.5-3%. Preferred Method: In the step (iv) material temperature is 35-45 degrees C and ambient temperature in is -10 to 35 degrees C. In the strep (v) solidifying for 10-20 seconds.
DC A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.)
MC A02-A00A; A06-A00B; A08-B; A08-F01; A08-S01; A08-S02; A10-D; A11-A03; A11-B06; A11-C02D; A12-S02C; A12-S02D

PT P
PN CN107774234-A; CN107774234-B
TI New metal organic framework material useful for            gas adsorption, gas adsorption storage, gas adsorption            separation, and selective adsorption and separation of            methane from methane/carbon dioxide mixture.
AU FANG X
   WANG G
   WANG H
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO L
AE SINOPEC CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC FUSHUN RES PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC FUSHUN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
GA 2018237193
AB    NOVELTY - Metal organic framework material                [(M3(P)3(Q)2].5DMF.2H2O)n (I), is new.
   USE - The metal organic framework material is useful                for gas adsorption, gas adsorption storage, gas                adsorption separation, preferably in carbon                dioxide, hydrogen sulfide adsorption storage, and                methane/carbon dioxide, selective adsorption and                separation of methane from methane/carbon dioxide                mixture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The metal organic framework material: has good                water resistance and heat resistance stability;                contains 2',3'-dimethyl group, which limits the                interpenetrating network process to some extent and                prevents excessive pore narrowing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal organic framework material                [(M3(P)3(Q)2].5DMF.2H2O)n of formula (I) is                new.
   M=metal cation iron (II), cobalt (II), nickel                (II), copper (II) and/or zinc (II);
   P=organic ligand                2',3'-dimethyl-p-terphenyl-4,4''-dicarboxylic                acid;
   Q=secondary organic ligand 4,4'-bipyridine,                2-phenylpyridine, 4-phenylpyridine and/or                1,4-bis(p-pyridyl)benzene, preferably                1,4-di(p-pyridyl)benzene; and
   DMF=N,N'-dimethyl formamide
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing metal organic framework material,                comprising dissolving nitrate M, organic ligand,                and auxiliary organic ligand of M in N,N'-dimethyl                formamide, performing thermal synthesis reaction,                after finishing the reaction, cooling to room                temperature, washing and drying the obtained                product to obtain the metal organic framework                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The crystal structure                  of the metal organic framework material is                  monoclinic, the space group is P2/c, cell                  parameters are, a=18.231&#8491; ,                  b=20.032&#8491; , c=33.016&#8491; ,                  &#945; =89.001&#176; , &#946;                  =100.123&#176; , and &#947;                  =89.001&#176; . The main channel size along                  the metal-organic framework material in the                  a-axis direction of the crystal is 14.23?x                  13.05&#8491; . The specific surface area of                  the metal organic framework material is 500-                  m2/g, preferably is 650 m2/g, the total pore                  volume is 0.3-0.6 cm3/g, preferably 0.4-0.5                  cm3/g, the pore diameter holes take up 0.65-0.85                  nm of the pore volume of the total pore volume of                  55-85%, preferably from 60-80%. Preferred Method:                  The each M in the skeleton structural unit of the                  metal organic framework material is in the form                  of twisted tetragonal cone coordinated with                  oxygen atoms in four different carboxylates in                  the P2-organic ligand and occupies the basic                  position, simultaneously with the pyridine from                  Q, nitrogen atom of the group coordinates and                  occupies the top position of the complex, the                  carboxy group coordinates with M to form a                  turn-like secondary structure unit, and the                  secondary structure unit further extends into a                  two-dimensional grid structure, and then the net                  lattice structure forms a columnar overall                  structure via a part of Q. In the method, the mol                  ratio of M nitrate, organic ligand, auxiliary                  organic ligand and N,N'-dimethyl formamide is                  1:(0.01-5):(0.01-5):(75-350), preferably                  1:(0.1-1):(0.1-1):(185-320). In the method, the                  temperature of the hot synthesis reaction is                  50-150&#176; C, preferably 65-100&#176;                  C, and the time is 12-100 hours, preferably 50-90                  hours. In the method, cooling to room temperature                  is at cooling rate of 0.3-10&#176; C/minute,                  preferably 0.7-3&#176; C/minute, washing                  solvent is methanol, ethanol, chloroform,                  acetone, dimethylformamide and/or water,                  preferably dimethylformamide and acetone, the                  washing sequence preferably is with                  dimethylformamide, then washing by acetone,                  drying temperature is 20-150&#176; C,                  preferably 35-65&#176; C, and the drying time                  is 20-80 hours, preferably 30-60 hours. The                  synthesis of organic ligand                  2',3'-dimethyl-p-terphenyl-4,4''-dicarboxylic                  acid comprises subjecting ethylene glycol                  dimethyl ether, 1,4-dibromo-2,3-dimethylbenzene,                  4-methoxy-carbonylphenylboronic acid, potassium                  carbonate, and triphenylphosphine to first reflux                  reaction in an inert atmosphere, purifying after                  finishing reaction to obtain white solid, mixing                  the white solid with tetrahydrofuran, methanol,                  potassium hydroxide and first parts of water                  uniformly, carrying out second reflux reaction,                  dissolving the product in water after finishing                  second reflowing reaction, adding hydrochloric                  acid solution till not generating deposition,                  filtering and drying to obtain the organic ligand                  2',3'-dimethyl-p-terphenyl-4,4''-dicarboxylic                  acid, where the mol ratio of ethylene glycol                  dimethyl ether, 1,4-dibromo-2,3-dimethylbenzene,                  4-methoxy-carbonylphenylboronic acid, potassium                  carbonate, and triphenylphosphine is                  (80-230):(0.1-10):(1):(0.1-10):(0.001-0.07),                  preferably (120-200):(1-5):(1):(2-7):(0.01-0.05),                  the mol ratio of white solid, tetrahydrofuran,                  methanol, potassium hydroxide and first parts of                  the water is                  1:(0.05-10):(0.1-10):(0.001-0.08):(0.1-10),                  preferably 1:(0.1-1):(1-5):(0.01-0.05):(1-5), the                  first reflux reaction time is 20-100 hours,                  preferably 8-20 hours and temperature is                  50-80&#176; C, preferably 50-80&#176; C,                  the second reflux reaction time is 20-100 hours,                  preferably 2-20 hours, and the temperature is                  50-80&#176; C, preferably 50-80&#176; C.                  The purifying method after the first reflux                  reaction comprises cooling first refluxing                  reaction product to room temperature, distilling                  to remove solvent and the residue, cleaning with                  water and acetone in turn, and then further                  purifying by column chromatography to obtain                  white solid. In the method, after the second                  reflux reaction, before dissolving the product in                  second part of water, removing the                  tetrahydrofuran and methanol solvent in the                  product by distillation.
DC E12 (Organometallics - ie containing other than H, C, N, O, S, halogens, Si and P.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Device for recycling carbon dioxide gas, has cooling tank, drying bed, humidity detecting tank, adsorption bed, liquefier, refrigerator and finished product tank that are connected by connecting pipe.
AU LI G
   WANG Y
   KONG Q
   YE M
AE HEILONGJIANG PROVINCE FORESTRY PROD IND (HEIL-Non-standard)
GA 201814105P
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a device for recycling carbon dioxide gas, comprising a diversion tank, separating tank is provided with a compressor, a compressor is provided with a cooling tank, one side of the cooling tank is provided with a drying bed, one side of the drying bed is provided with a humidity detecting tank; one side of the humidity detecting tank set with adsorption bed, one side of the adsorption bed is provided with a liquefier, liquefier bottom is provided with one side of the refrigerator, a liquefier is provided with a finished product tank, a humidity detecting tank is set with a pipeline, the pipeline is provided with a humidity sensor; one side of the humidity sensor is provided with a circuit board, the bottom of the pipeline are respectively provided with a first output pipe and the output pipe, the first output pipe and the surface of the second output pipe are respectively provided with a first electromagnetic valve and a second electromagnetic valve. the device solves the problem that the current device for recycling carbon dioxide gas after the drying bed drying cannot detect the drying effect, resulting in incomplete drying, cause recycling problem of low purity of the carbon dioxide gas.

   NING Z
   WANG Y
   YANG L
   WANG T
   LIU K
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV FUDAN ZHONGSHAN HOSPITAL (UYFU-C)

AU STAM W
   VAN DEN HAM E J
   WOLTERBEEK T K T

AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)

AB    NOVELTY - The downhole seal has a CO2 swellable elastomer which swells when in contact with CO2. The CO2 swellable elastomer comprises a halogenated elastomer, a perhalogenated elastomer, and EPDM. The CO2 swellable elastomer comprises inclusions comprising a metal oxide. The metal oxide is susceptible to chemically react with CO2 and water. A reaction product from a chemical reaction of the metal oxide with water is susceptible to chemically react with CO2. The CO2 swellable elastomer further comprises a salt that is hydroscopic and soluble in water.
   USE - Downhole seal for use in subsurface storage of carbon dioxide in a carbon capture storage well.

PN RU2757402-C1
TI Apparatus for indoor cooling and method for cooling has sealed sublimation container equipped with a door.
AU GROSUL M R
   NASLEDNIKOV G A
   NASLEDNIKOV V E
AE UNIV BELGOROD STATE AGRARIAN (UYBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021C1103M
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to systems for cooling rooms for various purposes using gases. The apparatus for indoor cooling consists of a sealed sublimation container equipped with a door, a pressure gauge, and a valve, as well as a main pipe, an outlet pipe, and heat exchangers. The valve is equipped with an adapter. A diaphragm is installed on the outlet pipe. The sublimation container, the valve, the adapter, the main pipe, the heat exchangers, the outlet pipe with the diaphragm are connected in series. Carbon dioxide is sublimated in the sealed container. The sublimated gaseous carbon dioxide increased in volume at a temperature of -78.4C is released from the container through the valve and the adapter into the main pipe, then passed through one or several heat exchangers, the outlet pipe, and then released outside the apparatus and the room.
   USE - Cooling.
   ADVANTAGE - Expanded possibilities of using carbon dioxide for cooling facilities for various purposes.

TI Preparation of secondary battery cathode material            involves separating carbon dioxide from lithium            carbonate powder to prepare lithium oxide powder,            mixing with nickel-cobalt-manganese precursor powder,            and sintering using rotary kiln.
AU YOU K S
   YOO K
   LIU Q
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
   RES INST IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (POSC-C)
   IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES INST (POSC-C)
   POHANG INST IND SCI (POSC-C)
   POSCO (POSC-C)
   RES INST IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (POSC-C)
GA 2021703952
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of a secondary battery cathode                material involves separating carbon dioxide (S100)                from a lithium carbonate powder to prepare a                lithium oxide powder, mixing (S200) the lithium                oxide powder with a nickel-cobalt-manganese (NCM)                precursor powder to form a mixed powder, and                sintering (S300) the mixed powder by using a rotary                kiln.
   USE - Preparation of secondary battery cathode                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical due to usage of                lithium carbonate instead of lithium hydroxide,                manufactures high nickel NCM positive electrode                material having a nickel content of 70% or more,                and ensures production of cathode material in large                quantities by using rotary kiln.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the                manufacturing method. (Drawing includes non-English                language text)
   Carbon dioxide separation process                (S100)
   Mixing process (S200)
   Sintering process (S300)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide                  separation step involves charging the lithium                  carbonate powder into a high temperature kiln,                  supplying air or oxygen into the kiln, and                  preparing the lithium oxide powder. The method                  further involves forming the mixed powder by                  mixing the lithium oxide powder and the NCM                  precursor powder, grinding the mixed powder, and                  molding the mixed powder in the form of granules                  or briquettes in a closed space under nitrogen                  atmosphere. The sintering step involves forming a                  refractory coating layer on the inner surface of                  the kiln, installing a spiral baffle inside the                  kiln in a high-temperature atmosphere, charging                  the mixed powder into the kiln, and then                  sintering the mixed powder in the rotary                  kiln.

PN CN111905692-A
TI Preparing semi-coke-based carbon adsorption material by dissolving organic matter in water, adding semi-coke powder, stirring, fermenting and performing high-temperature carbon dioxide activation and vacuum drying.
AU YANG G
   YANG X
   YAN X
   XU B
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2020B5977V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing semi-coke-based carbon adsorption material by organic fermentation coupled with carbon dioxide activation involves (a) dissolving organic matter in water, adding semi-coke powder, and stirring evenly to obtain a mixed sample, and fermenting to obtain fermented semi-coke, and (b) performing high-temperature carbon dioxide activation and vacuum drying the fermented semi-coke to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is used for preparing semi-coke-based carbon adsorption material, which is used for treatment of volatile organic chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures excellent activation effect and friendliness to environment and equipment. The semi-coke-based carbon adsorption material has improved specific surface area of 500-800 m2/g, an average pore diameter of 2-4 nm, and the adsorption capacity of butane is 8-16.5 mg/g.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic matter in step (a) is one or a mixture of starch, glucose, maltose, fructose, citric acid, glycine, cysteine, urea and polyvinylpyrrole. The mesh number of the semi-coke powder in step (a) is less than or equal to 60 mesh. Preferred Method: The method involves adding a baking powder during the fermentation process. The step (b) involves using a tube furnace to carry out high-temperature carbon dioxide activation on the fermented semi-coke, vacuum drying after activation, sealing and storing. In the process of high-temperature carbon dioxide activation, the heating time of the tube furnace is 30-120 minutes, and the activation temperature is 700-1000 degrees C, the activation time is 60-300 minutes, the activation gas is carbon dioxide gas, and the gas flow is 20-60 ml. Preferred Composition: The ratio of baking powder, organic matter, water and semi-coke is (0.01-0.1) g:(0.05-0.5) g:(0.5-1) g:(0.5-2) g. Preferred Condition: The temperature during the fermentation process is 20-60 degrees C, and the fermentation time is 5-30 days. The temperature during the vacuum drying process is 120 degrees C, and the drying time is 12 hours.

   R = 16-18C fatty alcohol residues;and
   n = 10.

The amount of the fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether is 0.5-1.5 g/l calculated based on the volume of the carbon dioxide fluid. PN CN111218519-A
TI Bio-electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to            produce methane by reverse transcription on RNA into            cDNA, calculating expression level of functional genes,            and selecting reaction condition corresponding to            methane production pathway.
AU MU B
   ZHOU L
   LIU J
   YANG S
   BAI Y
   LIANG T
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202051438W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for bio-electrochemical reduction of                carbon dioxide to produce methane involves (a)                extracting RNA of methanogens in biochemical                electrochemical reaction system and control system                to be tested, (b) performing reverse transcription                on extracted RNA into cDNA, (c) using specific                primers of functional genes ccdA and frhB to                perform fluorescent quantitative PCR on obtained                cDNA, (d) calculating a ratio of expression levels                of two functional genes, and comparing with control                system to determine methane production pathway and                selecting reaction conditions corresponding to the                pathway to produce methane.
   USE - The method is useful for performing                bio-electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide to                produce methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exhibits characteristics of strong                specificity, convenient operation, accurate                detection and high efficiency of methane production                as compared with existing method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Species: The methanogen is                  Methanosarcina barkeri, where inoculation volume                  of methanogenic bacteria is 10-30%. Preferred                  Method: The step (a) involves adding culture                  solution to body of bioelectrolysis cell,                  inoculating methanogenic bacteria, connecting to                  electrochemical workstation, and setting reaction                  conditions, which constitutes bioelectrochemical                  reaction to be tested system. The set reaction                  condition is to apply a potential of -1.2 to 0 V                  to the reaction system, and react for 48-72                  hours. The step (a) involves extracting RNA by                  collecting bacterial liquid, extracting bacterial                  liquid sample obtained after the electrochemical                  reaction of the bio-electrochemical reaction                  system to be tested and the control system,                  adding 10% ratio of 95:5 volumetric absolute                  ethanol TRIzol (RTM: Acid-guanidinium-phenol                  based solution) for fixation, placing the fixed                  bacterial solution in a centrifuge tube in an                  anaerobic glove box, centrifuging at 12000 g at 4                  degrees C, and collecting cells for 20 minutes                  for RNA extraction. The method involves RNA                  extraction by TRIzol (RTM:                  Acid-guanidinium-phenol based reagent) method.                  The step (b) involves reverse transcribing the                  extracted RNA into cDNA by preparing digestion                  and degradation DNA system by mixing complex DNA                  degrading enzyme I, 10 times DNAse I buffer,                  complex RNA degrading enzyme inhibitor and                  extracted RNA in a volume ratio of 2:2:0:2:15.8,                  taking digested and degraded DNA system into a                  new PCR tube, adding random primers (Random6mers                  sequence: pd(N)6), heating it in a water bath at                  75 degrees C for 10 minutes, quickly cooling on                  ice for 2 minutes, and centrifuging for 5                  seconds, where the volume ratio of digested and                  degraded DNA system to random primers is 6.5:0.5,                  preparing 10 mu l RNA reverse transcription                  system by 5 times reverse transcriptase M-MLV                  buffer, 2'-deoxynucleotide-5'-triphosphate                  mixture, reverse transcriptase M-MLV (RNase H-),                  recombinant RNase inhibitor and system obtained                  in step (b) in a volume ratio of                  2:0.5:0.25:0.25:7, placing it in a PCR instrument                  for reverse transcription, performing PCR at 30                  degrees C for 10 minutes, 42 degrees C for 60                  minutes, and 70 degrees C for 15 minutes, and                  cooling to room temperature to obtain a cDNA                  solution. The amplification procedure of the                  fluorescent quantitative PCR is performed at 95                  degrees C for 5 minutes, 94 degrees C for 45                  seconds, and 62 degrees C for 45 seconds, which                  are repeated for 45 cycles, and 72 degrees C for                  45 seconds. The method involves calculating a                  ratio of the expression levels of the two                  functional genes described in step (d) is                  R=ccdA(copies/mL)/frhB(copies/ml). The step (d)                  involves selecting two systems of -0.35 V without                  hydrogen i.e. only a direct electron transfer                  pathway, and 0 V, with hydrogen, which is only a                  hydrogen-mediated indirect electron transport                  methanogenesis pathway for control system.                  Preferred Biomolecule: The specific primer                  sequences of the functional genes ccdA and frhB                  described in step (c) are ccdAF having                  tgacagagactcgagacgga (SEQ ID NO: Not defined),                  ccdAR having gcctgctgccagaagtaaga (SEQ ID NO: Not                  defined), frhBF having gcccggtcttggaattgtcaaaaa                  (SEQ ID NO: Not defined), and frhBR having                  cgccttattaaccccaaaattcct (SEQ ID NO: Not                  defined). The fluorescence quantitative PCR                  amplification reaction system comprises sterile                  water, 12.5 mu M forward and reverse primers, TB                  green premix Ex Taq II and cDNA sample                  amplification system cDNA sample content of 20                  ng/ mu l with a volume ratio of                  3.6:0:2:5:1.

TI Preparing carbonate gelling material comprises crushing the limestone, grinding, adding water, reacting calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide and forming mixture of calcium bicarbonate supersaturated solution containing solid impurities.
AU YIN X
AE CHANGSHA ZICHEN TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201976038V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonate gelling material comprises (i) crushing the limestone and grinding to obtain raw material powder; and (ii) placing the raw material powder in a pressure reaction crucible with a stirring mechanism, adding water, pressing carbon dioxide gas into the pressure reaction under pressure, reacting the main mineral calcium carbonate in the raw material with carbon dioxide, converting to a water-soluble calcium bicarbonate supersaturated solution with impurity compounds of silicon, aluminum and iron in raw material powder become insoluble precipitate solids and forming a mixture of calcium bicarbonate supersaturated solution containing solid impurities.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbonate gelling material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple economical and environmentally friendly; and has simple equipment, low energy consumption and waste of raw materials and waste.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), in the limestone, the calcium oxide mass content is greater than or equal to 48%, and the aluminum oxide mass content is less than or equal to 2.2%. The raw material has a particle size of less than or equal to 80 mu m, preferably less than or equal to 74 mu m. In the step (2), the water is added in an amount of 2.5-20 times, preferably 4-12 times the mass of the raw material and the temperature at which carbon dioxide is injected is 0-45 degrees C, preferably 8-25 degrees C. The pressure in the pressure state is 1-15 MPa, preferably 2-7 MPa. The reaction time is 0.5-3 hours, preferably 0.7-2.5 hours. The stabilizer is non-biotoxic substance capable of alleviating the dissociation of calcium bicarbonate, preferably triethanolamine and sodium bicarbonate and the amounts of the triethanolamine and sodium bicarbonate are 0.2-2.0% and 0.1-1.0%, respectively, based on the mass of the calcium bicarbonate. Preferred Method: The solid-state impurity-containing carbonate gelling material is filtered under a pressure of 1.0-15.0 MPa to obtain a filter residue and a carbonate gelling material solution. The method comprises adding a stabilizer into the carbonate gelling material solution under a pressure of 1-15 MPa and releasing the carbon dioxide pressure to atmospheric pressure by the stabilizer cement solution of the carbonate gelling material to obtain a solid-liquid mixture gelling material containing calcium bicarbonate solid.

TI Ternary non-aqueous solid-liquid phase change absorption system useful for capturing carbon dioxide, comprises 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, dipropylene glycol dimethyl ether comprising solvent and piperazine.
AU LV B
   JING G
   CHEN Z
   ZHOU Z
AE UNIV HUAQIAO (UYHQ-C)
GA 201977329E
AB    NOVELTY - Ternary non-aqueous solid-liquid phase change absorption system comprises 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, dipropylene glycol dimethyl ether and piperazine. The molar ratio of 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol and piperazine is 7:3-9:1 and the concentration is 1-2 mol/l. The dipropylene glycol dimethyl ether is a solvent.
   USE - The absorption system is useful for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorption system reduces the energy consumption, is easy to separate than the conventional solid-liquid phase change system solid phase viscous rich liquid, and has phase change system regeneration temperature which is much lower than the solid-liquid phase change system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the use of absorption system for capturing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The solid phase in the system absorbs carbon dioxide and becomes solid-liquid two-phase which is separated by filtration and the solid-liquid two-phase solid phase is regenerated by thermal desorption. Preferred Components: The ternary mixed system has a phase separation property, is a homogeneous solution before absorbing carbon dioxide and becomes a solid-liquid two phase after absorbing carbon dioxide, where the solid phase is a granular crystal, and the carbon dioxide is enriched in the solid phase. The 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol is used as a main absorbent, the dipropylene glycol dimethyl ether is used as a phase separation reagent and the piperazine is used as a proton-removing agent for carbon dioxide absorption reaction, where the three components constitute a ternary component. Preferred Composition: The ternary mixed system is used for absorbing carbon dioxide in a pure carbon dioxide or a mixed gas, where the amount of 5-20 vol.% carbon dioxide. The amount of solid-liquid two-phase enriched liquid is 20-50 vol.%. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide absorption temperature of the ternary mixed system is controlled at 30-60 degrees C, where the absorption load is 0.5-1 mol carbon dioxide/mol of absorbent. The regeneration is carried out at 90-120 degrees C for 50-100 minutes, where the regeneration efficiency is 40-95%.

PN CN109999779-A
TI Preparation of indium(III) oxide photocatalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to form carbon monoxide, involves mixing urea and indium(III) chloride in distilled water, centrifuging, washing and drying solution.
AU ZHU X
   XU H
   LI H
   YANG J
   ZHOU G
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2019628707
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of indium(III) oxide photocatalyst, involves (1) mixing urea and indium(III) chloride in distilled water uniformly, reacting obtained mixture in an oil bath, (2) centrifuging, washing and drying solution after the reaction of step (1) to obtain indium(III) hydroxide, (3) placing indium(III) hydroxide obtained in the step (2) in a tube furnace and calcining to obtain indium(III) oxide, and (4) subjecting indium(III) oxide obtained in the step (3) to plasma treatment.
   USE - Preparation of indium(III) oxide photocatalyst used for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to form carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen-free vacancy-type indium(III) oxide nanotube catalyst has large surface area, and exposes more active sites, adsorbs carbon dioxide, and effectively inhibits photo-generated carrier recombination.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The ratio of urea, indium(III) chloride and deionized water is 25 mg: 3 mg: 100 mL. Preferred Condition: The stirring time is 5-10 minutes and the oil bath temperature is 80 degrees C, in step (1). The reaction is carried out for 10-20 hours under magnetic stirring. The centrifugal rotation speed is 10000 rpm, in step (2). The calcination temperature is 300 degrees C, and calcination is carried out in an air atmosphere, and the calcination time is 2 hours, in step (3). The dielectric barrier discharge power is 50-150 W, the treatment atmosphere is ammonia gas, argon gas or hydrogen gas, the treatment time is 5-15 minutes, and the gas flow rate is 300 mL/minute, in step (4). Preferred Process: The solution is washed with deionized water 3-5 times, dried at room temperature for 48 hours.

PN CN109717335-A
TI Sterile cold juicing process by washing fruit, freezing, thawing, pressing to obtain pulp, adding water to pulp, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide, discharging fruit liquid from separating kettle, processing and sterilizing.
AU ZHOU C
   ZHOU M
   ZHOU Z
   CHEN R
AE GUANGDONG CONGMINGREN GROUP CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201942956L
AB    NOVELTY - A sterile cold juicing process involves washing fruit, placing in a freezer at (-20)-(-30) degrees C for 6-48 hours, taking out, thawing at 30-40 degrees C for 4-6 hours, pressing the thawed fruit using a juicer to obtain a pulp, adding appropriate amount of water to the pulp, adding the mixture to an extraction kettle of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device, carrying out extracting process using supercritical carbon dioxide extracting agent, discharging fruit liquid from the separating kettle, processing the fruit liquid and sterilizing.
   USE - The method is used for preparing juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method fully extracts the nutrients in the pulp, avoids the degradation of nutrients, ensures the taste of the pulp and prolongs the shelf life of the juice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The fruit comprises apple, strawberry, kiwi, pear, watermelon, orange and/or mandarin orange. Preferred Method: The method involves freezing the fruit at (-24)-(-26) degrees C for 8-10 hours and thawing at 35-38 degrees C for 5 hours. The process involves chopping the fruit, pressing the fruit in the juicer to obtain the pulp, adding 1-2 ml water per gram pulp and mixing uniformly. The extraction kettle is maintained at 45-55 degrees C, where the pressure of the extraction kettle is 30-40 MPa, and the extraction is carried out for 30-120 minutes. The separation kettle is maintained at 30-40 degrees C, and the pressure of the separation kettle is 20-25 MPa. The process involves pasteurizing the fruit liquid, rapidly cooling and aseptic filling during sterilization. The pasteurization is carried out at 100-105 degrees C for 5-10 seconds using a tube-type sterilizer and rapidly cooling to normal temperature using a heat exchanger.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

   USE - Used as curcumin composite nano-particle.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing curcumin composite nanoparticle, comprising (i) adding a proportion of curcumin and piperine into the liquefied carbon dioxide and dispersing uniformly to obtain a mixture, (ii) pumping the mixture obtained in step (i) into a high pressure crystallizing kettle, adjusting the temperature and pressure to a preset value, and pumping a ratio of the aqueous solution of the plant polysaccharide into the high pressure crystallizing kettle, (iii) passing carbon dioxide into the high pressure crystallizing kettle, and maintaining the temperature and pressure in the autoclave until the water is dried, stopping the pumping of carbon dioxide and obtaining a dried sample after the pressure is reduced to normal pressure.

Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the preset temperature of the autoclave during the reaction is 25-40 degrees C. In the step (iii), the pressure of the autoclave at the time of the reaction is 15-30 MPa. In the step (ii), the flow rate of the aqueous solution into the autoclave is 3 ml/min.

PN CN109232775-A
TI Preparing cyclodextrin carbonate comprises e.g. taking cyclodextrin, mixing cyclodextrin, super strong organic base and organic solvent, then adding into the reactor, passing carbon dioxide and then reacting.
AU XIE H
   CAI L
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201913311U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclodextrin carbonate comprises (i) taking cyclodextrin, (ii) mixing cyclodextrin, super strong organic base and organic solvent to obtain a mixed solution, then adding into the reactor, passing carbon dioxide gas at a pressure of 0.1-8 MPa into the reactor, reacting at 20-80 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours to obtain a cyclodextrin solution, (iii) adding halogenated methyl compound into the cyclodextrin solution and reacting at 20-80 degrees C for 0.5-48 hours under carbon dioxide atmosphere at a pressure of 0.1-8 MPa, and (iv) adding anti-solvent into the reaction liquid of step (iii), filtering the precipitate, washing, purifying and drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclodextrin carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyclodextrin carbonate: has high preparation efficiency and universality; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cyclodextrin compounds of formula (I) is a-cyclodextrin, beta -cyclodextrin and/or gamma -cyclodextrin, where number of repeating glucose units of cyclodextrin are 6-12. In step (ii), mass concentration of the cyclodextrin in the mixed solution is 2-50 wt.%, and mass concentration of super strong organic base is 2-30 wt.%. The super strong organic base is 2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepine, 2,3,4,6,7,8-hexahydropyrrolo(1,2-a)pyrimidine, 1,2-N',2-N',2-N,2-N,4-N',4-N',4-N,4-N,6-decamethyl-1,6-dihydro-1,3,5,2(5),4(5)-triazadiphosphinine-2,2,4,4-tetramine, 3,5,6,7-tetrahydro-2H-pyrrolo(1,2-a)imidazole compounds of formula (II), 2-methylprop-1-ene compounds of formula (III), 2-(((diaminomethylidene)amino)methyl)guanidine compounds of formula (IV) and imidazolidin-2-imine compounds of formula (V). The organic solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone, tetramethyl hydrazine, N,N-dimethylimidazolidinone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-diethylacetamide, 2-pyrrolidone, 2-azacyclohexanone, epsilon -caprolactam, N,N-dimethylpropenyl urea, sulfolane or piperylene sulfone. The halogenated methyl compound is saturated halogenated alkanes (XCnH(2n+1)), halogenated olefin or halogenated cycloalkane having a double bond (XCnH(2n-1)), double-bonded halogenated olefin or a halogenated cycloalkane (XCnH(2n-3)), halogenated olefins or halogenated cycloalkanes containing three double bonds (XCnH(2n-5)), halogenated alkyne containing a triple bond (XCnH(2m-3)) and aromatic substance containing a halogenated methyl group i.e. toluene compounds of formula (VI), ester organics containing a halogenated methyl group i.e. acetate compounds of formula (VII), amide compounds containing a halogenated methyl group i.e. acetamide compounds of formula (VIII), nitrile compound containing a halogenated methyl group (X- (CH2)nCN), epoxy compounds containing a halogenated methyl group i.e. 2-ethyloxirane compounds of formula (IX), perfluoroalkane compound containing a halogenated methyl group (XCH2(CF2)nCF3), fluorobenzene compound containing a halogenated methyl group i.e. 1,2,3,4,5-pentafluoro-6-methylbenzene compounds of formula (X), polyethylene glycol derivative containing a halogenated methyl group (X-CH2OCH3), (X-CH2CH2(OCH2CH2)nOCH3), (XCH2CH2(OCH2CH2)nOCH2CH3), aldehydes and ketone derivatives containing a halogenated methyl group i.e. propionaldehyde compounds of formula (XI), dihalogenated compound containing a halogenated methyl group i.e. propane compounds of formula (XII), halogenated methyltetrahydrofuran compound i.e. benzene compounds of formula (XIII) and 2-methyltetrahydrofuran compounds of formula (XIV), and silane compound containing a halomethyl group of formula (XV). In step (iii), molar ratio between halogenated methyl compound and hydroxy group on the cyclodextrin is 0.1-4:1, preferably 0.5-2:1. The anti-solvent is water, 1-3C lower aliphatic alcohol, ether or acetone; and volume ratio between anti-solvent and reaction liquid is 0.5-10:1.n = 6, 7, or 8;p = 1 or 2;m = 1-6;R = H, methyl or ethyl;R1 = H, methyl, 1-6C alkyl, 3-10C ethoxy polyethylene glycol, methoxy polyethylene glycol base, 1-6C hydroxylated alkyl group or 4-8C polyethylene glycol group;R2-R-5 = H, methyl or ethyl;X = Cl, Br or I;n = 1-20;m = 1-5; andR = methyl, ethyl, propyl, isopropyl, methoxy, trifluoromethyl, nitro, cyano, aldehyde, methyl formate, ethyl formate or trifluoromethoxy.Preferred Method: In step (iv), distilling off reaction liquid obtained in step (iii) under direct reduced pressure to remove the solvent and the unreacted monomer, adding water, 1-3C lower aliphatic alcohol, soaking in ether or acetone, stirring, filtering and then drying to obtain cyclodextrin carbonate.
