PN CN108774384-A; CN108774384-B
TI Preparing polylactic acid-based composite material comprises adding a filler, a reinforcing agent, a toughening agent and a part of polylactic acid into a mixer, premixing to obtain premix and dissolving nucleating agent.
AU GAO D
   ZHOU Y
   WANG D
   WEN Y
   LI H
   LI Z
   LIAO S
   LI C
   WEI X
AE WUHAN KINGFA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
   WUHAN KINGFA TECHNOLOGY ENTERPRISE TECHN (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201890017N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polylactic acid-based composite material comprises adding a filler, a reinforcing agent, a toughening agent and a part of polylactic acid into mixer, premixing to obtain premix, dissolving the nucleating agent in supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain supercritical carbon dioxide solution, adding the premix into the extruder through the main feed port, adding the remaining part of the polylactic acid from the side feed port to the extruder, adding the supercritical carbon dioxide solution from the middle of the extruder, melting, extruding, and granulating through extruder to obtain polylactic acid-based composite material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polylactic acid-based composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has high toughness and high strength and is biodegradable and recycled.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The weight ratio of the polylactic acid, the filler, the reinforcing agent, the toughening agent and the nucleating agent is 60-90:5-20:1-4:3-12:1-4. The polylactic acid has weight average molecular weight of 5000-10000 g/mol. The filler includes calcium carbonate, talc, barium sulfate, diatomaceous earth or mica powder. The reinforcing agent is graphene. The toughening agent is polyadipate-1,4-butanediol ester or polybutylene succinate. The nucleating agent is methylene bis(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl)aluminum phosphate or 2,2'-methylene-bis(4,6-di-n-butylphenol)phosphoric acid sodium. The partial polylactic acid accounts for 70-90% by weight of the total polylactic acid. The coupling agent is a silane coupling agent, a titanate coupling agent or a borate coupling agent. The antioxidant is tetrakis( beta -(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionic acid)pentaerythritol ester, 1,1,3-tris(2-methyl-4-hydroxy-5-tert-butylphenyl)butane, tris(2,4-di-tert-butylphenyl) phosphite, thiodiethylene bis(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate) or N,N-bis-(3-(3,5-di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionyl)hexanediamine. The plasticizer is ethylene hydroxy bis stearamide. The extruder comprises 12 zones, and the premix is added to the extruder through a main feed port provided in the first zone. The remaining portion of polylactic acid is added into the extruder from the side feed port provided in the fifth zone, and the SCCO2 solution was added from the seventh zone of the extruder. Preferred Method: The preparation of the premix comprises (i) adding a part of the filler and a part of the polylactic acid into the mixer, melting and extruding the mixed mixture and granulating through extruder to obtain master batch; (ii) adding the master batch, the reinforcing agent, the toughening agent and a part of the polylactic acid into a mixer and premixing to obtain a premix. In the step (i), the polylactic acid added into the mixer accounts for 20-40% of the total weight of the polylactic acid.

PN CN108409544-A
TI Producing triglyceride stabilizer comprises e.g. dissolving sodium hydroxide in refined glycerol, evaporating water, condensing, passing carbon dioxide gas to remove generated water, introducing argon gas, and evaporating unreacted glycerol.
AU LI H
AE CHENGDU MONITA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201865921U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing triglyceride stabilizer comprises dissolving 10 kg sodium hydroxide in 1200-1300 kg refined glycerol, evaporating water, condensing at 210-220 degrees C for 7-10 hours, passing carbon dioxide gas to remove generated water, introducing argon gas at 230-240 kPa at 190-195 degrees C, evaporating unreacted glycerol, cooling to 6-8 degrees C under nitrogen protection, mixing 900 kg stearic acid and 1000-1100 kg triglycerin, reacting at 190-196 degrees C for 70-90 minutes, cooling in carbon dioxide gas stream for 2-3 hours, washing in cyclohexane solution for 60-90 minutes, recrystallizing in an isopropanol solution, dehydrating using dehydrating agent to obtain a finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for producing triglyceride stabilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentrstion of cyclohexane solution is 40-43%, and isopropanol solution is 50-54%.

TI Carbonating mineral, comprises e.g. precipitating reducing slag in a reaction tank containing water, reacting free lime in reducing slag with carbon dioxide to form calcium carbonate film on surface of water, and removing film.

   JUN H B
   SOO C C
AE KIGAM KOREA INST GEOSCIENCE & MINERAL (KGEO-C)
GA 201821266Q
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonating mineral, comprises (a) precipitating reducing slag in a reaction tank containing water, reacting free lime in the reducing slag with carbon dioxide in the atmosphere to form calcium carbonate film on surface of the water, and removing the calcium carbonate film from the surface of the water, (b) separating the reducing slag and water from each other through solid-liquid separation, (c) introducing the reducing slag into solvent to extract calcium from the reducing slag, and (d) precipitating calcium carbonate by injecting carbon dioxide into a solvent in which calcium is extracted.
   USE - The method is useful for carbonating mineral.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves solvent extraction efficiency, removes free lime from the reducing slag, and recycles as material for construction and civil engineering.

The reducing slag is rapdly air-cooled ladle furnace slag (RA-LFS). Preferred Method: The step (a) is carried out by stirring aqueous solution containing water and a reducing slag at rate of greater than or equal to 100 revolutions/minute for several minutes to several tens minutes. The method further comprises removing free calcium oxide component, and recycling as concrete material after precipitating the calcium carbonate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN KR2429711-B1
TI Circulation process for recycling cement kiln dust            generated in manufacturing of cement, involves            filtering sludge containing cement kiln dust and water            and obtaining potassium chloride through drying by            calculating potassium chloride crystals from stored            filtered liquid by evaporation.
AU LEE J
   KIMHAEKI
   HYUN P B
   CHOI J S
AE ANYTECH (ANYT-Non-standard)
GA 2022A1564F
AB    NOVELTY - Circulation process for recycling cement kiln                dust involves (a) injecting cement kiln dust and                water into a stirring tank to obtain sludge while                stirring, (b) supplying the obtained sludge to a                filter press, and separating the solid and liquid                contained in the sludge by filtration, (c) storing                the filtered liquid in a storage tank (t1), (d)                obtaining potassium chloride through drying by                calculating potassium chloride crystals from the                stored liquid by evaporation and (e) supplying the                condensed water generated due to evaporation and                concentration in step (d) to the stirred tank of                step (a) while storing it in a storage tank (t2).                In step (a), during the stirring, carbon dioxide                gas generated in the cement manufacturing process                is injected together into the tank, and according                to the injection, chlorine and potassium components                in the cement kiln dust are eluted, and the heavy                metal is adsorbed by allowing the mineralization to                proceed to produce calcium carbonate.
   USE - Circulation process for recycling cement kiln                dust generated in manufacturing of cement.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables circulation process for                recycling cement kiln dust with minimized                generation of industrial waste through the                configuration of continuously circulating processes                that enable the recovery of industrial resources                such as calcium carbonate and potassium chloride                while voluntarily recycling carbon dioxide                generated during cement kiln dust and cement                manufacturing. The method prevents environmental                pollution and circulation process for cement kiln                dust resource conversion, thereby achieving more                efficient and economical industrial waste treatment                system. By consuming carbon dioxide gas generated                in the cement manufacturing process, thereby                contributing to carbon neutrality, and by                reintroducing and using the sludge that has                undergone the filtration process for the recovery                of potassium chloride is used as raw material for                the manufacturing process of cement. Process water                that is undergone the evaporation and concentration                process can able to build eco-friendly recycling                system of infinite repetition through configuration                in which condensed water is re-introduced into the                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Circulation process for recycling cement kiln                dust involves (a) injecting cement kiln dust and                water in an arbitrary weight ratio into a stirring                tank to obtain sludge while stirring for a preset                time, (b) supplying the obtained sludge to a filter                press, and separating the solid and liquid                contained in the sludge by filtration using a                filter press, (c) storing the liquid filtered                through step (b) in a storage tank (t1), (d)                obtaining potassium chloride through drying by                calculating potassium chloride crystals from the                liquid stored in step (c) by evaporation using                vacuum evaporation equipment and (e) supplying the                condensed water generated due to evaporation and                concentration in step (d) to the stirred tank of                step (a) while storing it in a storage tank (t2) so                that it is circulated again from step (a). In step                (a), during the stirring, carbon dioxide gas                generated in the cement manufacturing process is                injected together into the stirring tank, and                according to the injection, chlorine and potassium                components in the cement kiln dust are eluted, and                the heavy metal is adsorbed by allowing the                mineralization to proceed to produce calcium                carbonate. In step (c), the sludge from filtration                in step (b), is re-injected as a raw material for                the manufacturing process of cement. In step (a),                the cement kiln dust and the water is in weight                ratio of 1: 2.5-1: 5 and preset time for the                stirring in step (a) is 30-40 minutes. In step (c),                the liquid filtered through step (b) is reused                during the stirring of step (a), and the number of                reuse is 2-4 times. In step (e), the condensed                water reuses the filtered liquid in step (c). The                sludge passed through the filtration in step (b) in                response to the amount of moisture loss in the                sludge, is supplied to the stirred tank to have a                moisture content of 20-30%. The cement kiln dust is                a bypass dust containing chlorine generated in the                manufacturing process of cement.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

PT P
PN CN113788472-A
TI Three-dimensional printing molding method of            three-dimensional graphene composite material by            substrate processing, in which one side of double-sided            tape is fixed on bottom plate and other side is adhered            to polymer powder, laying polymer powder on surface,            and laser selective processing.
AU WANG G
   LIU F
   LUO S
AE UNIV BEIHANG (UNBA-C)
GA 202200206F
AB    NOVELTY - Three-dimensional printing molding method of a                three-dimensional graphene composite material                involves (a) substrate processing, in which one                side of the double-sided tape is fixed on the                bottom plate and other side is adhered to the                polymer powder, (b) laying the polymer powder on                the surface of the sintered layer base, (c) laser                selective processing using a 10.6 microm carbon                dioxide laser to selectively carbonize or sinter                polymer powder according to a pre-designed path to                form single-layer graphene, single-layer sintered                polymer or single-layer composite layer, (d)                spreading a layer of polymer powder on the surface                of the formed structural layer, (e) laser selective                processing using a 10.6 microm carbon dioxide laser                to selectively carbonize or sinter polymer powder,                (f) repeating the steps (e) and (d) until the                printing is completed to obtain the material, and                (g) cleaning.
   USE - The method is useful for three-dimensional                printing molding of a three-dimensional graphene                composite material.
   ADVANTAGE - The invention provides a 3D printing molding                method of a three-dimensional graphene composite                material. The method utilizes the laser selective                carbonization technology to sinter and carbonize                the polymer powder respectively, and then                accumulates layer by layer, so that the                three-dimensional graphene composite material can                be prepared in situ. The method can realize the                formation of a three-dimensional graphene structure                on the surface and inside of the polymer sintered                body, which not only has the excellent special                properties of graphene, but also has the                functionality of a polymer entity. At the same                time, the shape of the three-dimensional entity can                be freely customized, and the integration position                of graphene in the composite material can be                arbitrarily designed. The three-dimensional                graphene composite material forming method of the                present invention is simple, fast, and has high                forming precision, and the prepared                three-dimensional device can meet the requirements                of different usage scenarios.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Three-dimensional printing molding method of a                three-dimensional graphene composite material                involves (a) substrate processing, in which one                side of the double-sided tape is fixed on the                bottom plate, the other side is adhered to the                polymer powder, and the polymer powder is                irradiated by laser to generate the polymer                sintered layer substrate, (b) laying the polymer                powder on the surface of the sintered layer base,                if a single graphene layer structure is formed, the                thickness of the powder layer is 60-80% of the                graphene growth height, if a single polymer                sintered layer is formed, the thickness of the                powder layer is 20-60 microm, if a partially                sintered and partially carbonized composite layer                is formed, the thickness of the powder layer is                40-60 microm, (c) laser selective processing, using                a 10.6 microm carbon dioxide laser to selectively                carbonize or sinter polymer powder according to a                pre-designed path to form single-layer graphene,                single-layer sintered polymer or single-layer                composite layer, (d) spreading a layer of polymer                powder on the surface of the formed structural                layer, if a single graphene layer structure is                formed, the thickness of the powder layer is 60-80%                of the graphene growth height, if a single polymer                sintered layer is formed, the thickness of the                powder layer is 20-60 microm, if a composite layer                of partial sintering and partial carbonization is                formed, the thickness of the powder layer is 40-60                microm, (e) laser selective processing, using a                10.6 microm carbon dioxide laser to selectively                carbonize or sinter polymer powder according to a                pre-designed path to form single-layer graphene,                single-layer sintered polymer or single-layer                composite layer, (f) repeating the steps (e) and                (d) until the printing is completed to obtain the                material, and (g) cleaning the powder to obtain the                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The graphene is located                  inside the three-dimensional graphene composite                  material, preferably located on the surface of                  the three-dimensional graphene composite                  material. In the step (c) and (e), the scanning                  speed and printing resolution are respectively                  33.1-43.1 mm/second, 2.54-203.2 mm/s and 10-1000                  ppi/inchm, when forming a single graphene layer                  structure, the laser carbonization uses 0.5-25 W,                  when forming a single sintered layer, the laser                  power is 0.05-0.3 W, when forming a composite                  layer of partial sintering and partial                  carbonization, the power used for laser                  carbonization is 0.5-1 W, and the power used for                  laser sintering is 0.2-0.3 W, the processing can                  be carried out at room temperature and                  atmospheric conditions. The step of laser                  selective carbonization or sintering the step (c)                  and (e) adopts a single induction. The particle                  size of the polymer powder is less than or equal                  to 10 microm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer powder                  comprises thermoplastic powder, comprising                  polyimide, polyphenylene sulfide, polyetherimide                  or polyetheretherketone powder.

MC A11-B14; A11-B16; A11-C01D; A11-B09; A11-C; A12-A01; A12-A04D; A08-R03; E05-U05C

PN CN113337219-A
TI Antibacterial and antiviral aldehyde-removing coating involves e.g. nano inorganic aldehyde-removing resin, water, barium sulfate, calcium powder, talc, nano carbon dioxide, propylene glycol, antifoaming agent 4210, and thickening agent 935.
AU CHENG Q
   LIU D
   ZHANG J
   LI Z
   WU Z
AE ANHUI QINGFENG COATING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021A4528K
AB    NOVELTY - Antibacterial and antiviral aldehyde-removing coating comprises 140-160 pts. wt. nano inorganic aldehyde-removing resin, 110-130 pts. wt. water, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent 6154, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. odor-removing agent 11 G, 3-5 pts. wt. sodium salt dispersant, 1-3 pts. wt. non-ionic wetting agent, 1-3 pts. wt. cellulose 250, 1-3 pts. wt. odor removing multifunctional 168, 20-30 pts. wt. negative ion powder, 40-60 pts. wt. barium sulfate, 140-160 pts. wt. titanium pigment, 120-130 pts. wt. calcium powder, 20-30 pts. wt. talc, 70-80 pts. wt. talc, 40-60 pts. wt. silicon ash powder, 5-15 pts. wt. nano carbon dioxide, 5-15 pts. wt. propylene glycol, 180-220 pts. wt. odor removing emulsion 380, 3-5 pts. wt. odor removing auxiliary agent, 1-2 pts. wt. antifoaming agent 4210, 6-8 pts. wt. dry film mildew preventive, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. thickening agent 621, 1-3 pts. wt. thickening agent 935, and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. thickening agent 80.
   USE - Used as antibacterial and antiviral aldehyde-removing coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating: has high activity, has strong oxidation-reduction effect; which has capable of destroying cell membrane or cell protoplasm active enzyme activity of the cell membrane or cell; achieves the purpose of antibacterial and sterilizing; eliminates the indoor peculiar smell and various harmful gas e.g. benzene, formaldehyde, ketone, ammonia and pungent gas and residual rice acid smell in daily life, which is used in the decoration material volatilization. The method: adds barium sulfate, barium sulfate mesoscopic microcrystal particles, which can separate the agglomerated titanium dioxide particles; uses barium sulfate particle, which has enough surface energy; can absorb 410 titanium dioxide particles around; ensures the space position, so that the scattering rate of each titanium dioxide particle reaches the maximum; uses nano inorganic aldehyde-removing resin, which has a capable of quickly absorbing formaldehyde and has good fireproof effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Antibacterial and antiviral aldehyde-removing coating comprises 140-160 pts. wt. nano inorganic aldehyde-removing resin, 110-130 pts. wt. water, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. antifoaming agent 6154, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. odor-removing agent 11 G, 3-5 pts. wt. sodium salt dispersant, 1-3 pts. wt. non-ionic wetting agent, 1-3 pts. wt. cellulose 250, 1-3 pts. wt. odor removing multifunctional 168, 20-30 pts. wt. negative ion powder, 40-60 pts. wt. barium sulfate, 140-160 pts. wt. titanium pigment, 120-130 pts. wt. calcium powder, 20-30 pts. wt. talc, 70-80 pts. wt. talc, 40-60 pts. wt. silicon ash powder, 5-15 pts. wt. nano carbon dioxide, 5-15 pts. wt. propylene glycol, 180-220 pts. wt. odor removing emulsion 380, 3-5 pts. wt. odor removing auxiliary agent, 1-2 pts. wt. antifoaming agent 4210, 6-8 pts. wt. dry film mildew preventive, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. thickening agent 621, 1-3 pts. wt. thickening agent 935, and 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. thickening agent 80, where the antibacterial and antiviral aldehyde-removing coating is prepared by taking above raw materials, dispersing the raw materials uniformly to obtain the aldehyde-removing coating. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing antibacterial and antiviral aldehyde-removing coating comprising (i) adding nano inorganic aldehyde removing resin, water, antifoaming agent 6154, odor removing agent 11G, sodium salt dispersant, non-ionic wetting agent, cellulose 250, odor removing multifunctional 168, negative ion powder, barium sulfate, titanium pigment, heavy calcium powder, talc, silicon ash powder, nano carbon dioxide, propylene glycol, odor removing emulsion 380, odor removing auxiliary agent, antifoaming agent 4210, dry film mildew preventive, thickening agent 621, thickening agent 935, thickening agent 80, and (iii) stirring uniformly at a rate of 800-1000 revolutions/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The non-ionic wetting agent comprises polyoxyethylene alkyl phenol ether, polyoxyethylene fatty alcohol ether, and/or polyoxyethylene polyoxypropylene block ester. The dry film mildew preventive comprises AEM-5700T mildew preventive, HY-606- 1 mildew preventive, and/or WL-3 coating mildew-proof agent. The mass ratio of the propylene glycol ether, methyl 2-butenoate, and azobisisobutyronitrile in the step (i) 10:10:0.3. The mass ratio of intermediate 1, hydrazine hydrate and tetrabutylammonium bromide is 13.4:15.52:2. The dosage ratio of intermediate 2, tetrahydrofuran, benzoyl peroxide, and N-bromosuccinimide is 0.012 mol: 30 ml: 0.2 g:0.015 mol. The amount ratio of carbon nanotubes and mixed acid in step (ii) is 1.5 g: 80 ml. The mixed acid is 95 wt.% concentrated sulfuric acid and 68 wt.% concentrated nitric acid in a volume ratio of 3:1. The dosage ratio of oxidized carbon nanotubes, manganese sulfate solution, potassium sulfate solution, and potassium permanganate solution is 0.27 g:10 ml:20 ml:20 ml. The concentration of manganese sulfate solution is 0.45 mol/l. The concentration of potassium sulfate solution is 0.125 mol/l. The concentration of potassium permanganate solution is 0.15 mol/l. The dosage ratio of phenylphosphoryl dichloride and mixed acid described in step (iii) is 1 g:3 ml. The mixed acid is 68% concentrated nitric acid and 95% concentrated sulfuric acid in a volume ratio of 1:1.1. The dosage ratio of intermediate 4, tin powder and concentrated hydrochloric acid is 3.5 g:4.8 g:60 ml. The amount-mass ratio of pre-carrier, intermediate 5, and 1-hydroxybenzotriazole is 5:1:1.2. The mass ratio intermediate 3, potassium carbonate, tetrahydrofuran, and tetraethylammonium bromide is 5 g: 10 g: 80 ml: 3.5 ml. The mass ratio of intermediate 7, bromine benzyl alcohol, sodium hydroxide, and modified carrier is 3:5:1.5:10. Preferred Method: The nano inorganic aldehyde-removing resin is prepared by (i) adding propylene glycol ether, 2-methyl crotonate, azodiisobutyronitrile into a reaction kettle, introducing nitrogen, reacting at a speed of 150-200 revolutions/minute at 80-90 degrees C for 10-15 hours to obtain the intermediate 1, adding intermediate 1, tetrahydrofuran, hydrazine hydrate, tetrabutyl ammonium bromide into a reaction kettle at 85-90 degrees C, reacting for 10-15 hours to obtain the intermediate 2, adding intermediate 2, tetrahydrofuran, benzoyl peroxide, N-bromine succinimide into the reaction kettle under the condition of 80-90 degrees C, performing reflux reaction for 3-5 hours to obtain intermediate 3, (ii) adding the carbon nano-tube and mixed acid into the reaction kettle, stirring at 25-30 degrees C at a rate of 200-300 revolutions/minute for 20-25 hours, filtering the filter cake with deionized water, washing to neutral to obtain the carbon oxide nano-tube, adding carbon oxide nano-tube, manganese sulfate solution, potassium sulfate solution into the reaction kettle, dropping potassium permanganate solution, performing reflux reaction at 100-110 degrees C for 20-25 hours, filtering the filter cake with deionized water, washing for 2-3 times, each time for 20-30 seconds, drying to obtain the pre-carrier, (iii) adding phenyl phosphoryl chloride and chloroform into the reaction kettle at a rate of 150-200 revolutions/minute, stirring to phenyl phosphoryl chloride completely dissolved, dropping mixed acid at 50-55 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours, reacting for 20-30 minutes to obtain intermediate 4, adding intermediate 4, tin powder, and concentrated hydrochloric acid into the reaction kettle, reacting at 35-40 degrees C for 30-40 minutes, adjusting the pH value of the reaction liquid to 10-11 to obtain intermediate 5, dispersing pre-carrier in the deionized water, adding intermediate 5 and 1-hydroxy benzotriazole, reacting at 25-30 degrees C for 2-3 hours, filtering to remove the filtrate, preparing the modified carrier, (iv) adding intermediate 3, potassium carbonate, tetrahydrofuran, tetraethylammonium bromide into the reaction kettle, carrying out reflux reaction at a rate of 150- 200 revolutions/minute at 70-80 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours to obtain intermediate 7, dissolving the intermediate 7 in tetrahydrofuran, adding p-bromobenzene methanol and sodium hydroxide, performing reflux reaction for 0.5-1.5 hours at 90-95 degrees C, adding modified carrier, continuously reacting for 1-1.5 hours, filtering to remove the filtrate, drying the filter cake.

PN CN111205894-A; CN211896821-U
TI Carbon-based energy supercritical water gasification hydrogen production and carbon dioxide energy utilization system comprises main reaction module comprising supercritical water gasification reactor attached with separator.
AU WANG S
   LI Y
   JIANG Z
   CUI C
   KONG W
   HE C
   WANG T
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
   UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2020514660
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-based energy supercritical water gasification hydrogen production and carbon dioxide (CO2) energy utilization system comprises a main reaction module comprising a supercritical water gasification reactor (1), which has bottom outlet attached with a separator through a hot fluid side of a material heat regenerator (2). Liquid phase outlet of the separator is connected with an inlet of supercritical water oxidation reactor (10) through a component recovery unit (7), pressurizing slurry pump (8) and cold fluid side of slurry heat regenerator (9) in sequence. A supercritical water oxidation reactor outlet is connected with an inlet of a three-phase separator through a hot fluid side of the slurry heat regenerator, economizer and pressure reducer in sequence. A three-phase separator liquid phase outlet is connected with a coal slurry preparation unit.
   USE - Carbon-based energy supercritical water gasification hydrogen production and CO2 energy utilization system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system integrates supercritical water thermal combustion, supercritical water gasification and supercritical water oxidation, and realizes high-value clean conversion and utilization of coal in the whole process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon-based energy supercritical water gasification hydrogen production and carbon dioxide (CO2) energy utilization system comprises a main reaction module comprising a supercritical water gasification reactor (1), which has bottom outlet connected with a separator through a hot fluid side of a material heat regenerator (2), where liquid phase outlet of the separator is connected with an inlet of supercritical water oxidation reactor (10) through a component recovery unit (7), pressurizing slurry pump (8) and cold fluid side of slurry heat regenerator (9) in sequence. An outlet of the supercritical water oxidation reactor is connected with an inlet of a three-phase separator through a hot fluid side of the slurry heat regenerator, economizer and pressure reducer in sequence. A liquid phase outlet of the three-phase separator is connected with a coal slurry preparation unit. The energy-regeneration carbon sequestration module comprises a high-carbon gas centralized supply unit, which has input port connected with CO2 separator and three-phase separator. An outlet of the high-carbon gas centralized supply unit is communicated with a first inlet of microalgae solid carbon reactor, which has an outlet connected into a centrifugal separator having bottom outlet communicated with a hydrothermal liquefaction reactor. A top outlet of the centrifugal separator is communicated with a second inlet of the microalgae solid carbon reactor. An oxidant supply module comprising an air separation device is provided. An oxygen-enriched gas outlet of the air separation device is communicated with an inlet of an oxygen compressor, which has an outlet divided into two paths, where one path is connected to an inlet at the top of the supercritical water gasification reactor, and the other path is communicated with an inlet of the supercritical water oxidation reactor. A material supply module comprising a coal slurry preparation unit is provided. An outlet of the coal slurry preparation unit is connected to a cold fluid side inlet of a material heat regenerator through a high-pressure material pump. The cold fluid side outlet of the material heat regenerator is divided into two paths, where one path is connected to a top inlet of a supercritical water gasification reactor, and the other path is connected to an upper side inlet of the supercritical water gasification reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon-based energy supercritical water gasification hydrogen production and CO2 energy utilization using the system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing is a schematic flow diagram showing a carbon-based energy supercritical water gasification hydrogen production and carbon dioxide energy utilization system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Supercritical water gasification reactor (1)
   Material heat regenerator (2)
   Component recovery unit (7)
   Pressurizing slurry pump (8)
   Slurry heat regenerator (9)
   Supercritical water oxidation reactor (10)

PN CN109943202-A; CN109943202-B
TI Aqueous epoxy nanofiber heavy anticorrosive coating comprises component comprising e.g. aqueous epoxy curing agent, dispersing agent and antifoaming agent, and component B comprising e.g. emulsifier, inhibitor and deionized water.
AU JIANG Y
   REN M
AE CHENGDU BAYDI NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (CHEN-Non-standard)
   JIANG Y (JIAN-Individual)
GA 201958423T
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous epoxy nanofiber heavy anticorrosive coating comprises a component comprising 6-10 wt.% aqueous epoxy curing agent, 0.5-1 wt.% dispersing agent, 0.3-0.6 wt.% antifoaming agent, 10-14 wt.% auxiliary solvent, 2-4 wt.% aqueous zinc phosphate, 1-10 wt.% nano-barium sulfate, 2-6 wt.% titanium dioxide, 0.1-0.3 wt.% carbon black, 0.3-0.5 wt.% organic bentonite, 0.5-0.8 wt.% fumed silica, 2-4 wt.% aqueous wax slurry, 40-66 wt.% zinc powder, 2-10 wt.% nanofiber pulp and 1-3 wt.% graphene pulp, and component B comprising 80-90 wt.% aqueous epoxy resin emulsion, 2-5 wt.% liquid polysulfide rubber solution, 0.1-0.2 wt.% emulsifier, 0.5-1 wt.% inhibitor and 6.2-7.3 wt.% deionized water.
   USE - Used as aqueous epoxy nanofiber heavy anticorrosive coating.
   ADVANTAGE - The coating has excellent corrosion resistance, chemical resistance, impact toughness, rapid cycle resistance to cold and heat, and excellent thick coating workability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the aqueous epoxy nanofiber heavy anticorrosive coating, comprising (i) preparing zinc oxide solution by taking 6-10 pts. wt. zinc acetate dihydrate, adding 40-45 pts. wt. isopropanol, heating to 70-72 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, then adding 3-5 pts. wt. ethanolamine stabilizer, mixing for 10-12 minutes, cooling, then dripping 5-8 pts. wt. ethanol, then heating to 70-72 degrees C for 1-1.5 hours to form a sol, allowing to stand, and aging for 48 hours to obtain the, (ii) preparing sodium aluminate solution by slowly adding aluminum oxide to 80-85 degrees C 6-8 mol/l aqueous sodium hydroxide solution in the molar ratio of 1.5-2.2:1, heating the solution to 95-98 degrees C, and reacting for 1.5-3 hours, (iii) preparing aluminum hydroxide-supported zinc oxide nanoparticles by uniformly mixing the sodium aluminate solution and deionized water in a mass ratio of 1:3-4:1.5-2, slowly introducing carbon dioxide into the mixture, reacting, where the volume ratio of the bubble volume of the carbon dioxide and the mixed liquid per minute is 3-4:1 and the pressure of the carbon dioxide gas source is 0.1-0.12 MPa, blasting the carbon dioxide at 30-35 degrees C for 30-45 minutes, then maintaining the temperature for 20-25 hours, filtering-off the formed solid matter, washing with deionized water, and drying under vacuum, (iv) preparing inorganic nanofibers by dispersing the nanoparticles, conductive carbon nanotubes, polyvinyl alcohol thickener, methylcellulose thickener, water-soluble starch pore-forming agent, polyvinylpyrrolidone and deionized water, uniformly mixing at a mass ratio of 50:3-5:4-8:6-10:7-12:5-12:35-45, firstly ultrasonically dispersing for 1-2 hours, then stirring by using a magnetic stirrer at room temperature for 2-3 hours to obtain a spinnable solution, charging the spinnable solution into a liquid storage device of an electrospinning device at 25-35 degrees C, where the feeding rate of the electrospinning solution is adjusted to 0.5-1.5 ml/minute, the distance between the spinneret of the electrospinning equipment and the grounded collector is 10-15 cm, and the ambient temperature is 20-25 degrees C, then turning on the high voltage power supply, where the static voltage is 25-30 kV, opening a syringe pump, spraying the electrospinning solution jet onto the collector to obtain a fiber filament, calcining the fiber filament at 280-340 degrees C for 2-3 hours in a nitrogen atmosphere, then taking out, and grinding to obtain an inorganic nanofiber, and (v) dispersing 10-15 wt.% inorganic nanofibers and 1-3 wt.% aqueous dispersant by using deionized water as a solvent, performing high-speed stirring, and uniformly dispersing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organotin catalyst is di-n-octyltin dilaurate. The chain extender is 1,4-butanediol, neopentyl glycol, ethylene glycol, diethylene glycol, glycerin, maleic anhydride, trimethylolpropane, ethylenediamine, ethylene triamine, triethylenetetramine and/or dimethylolpropionic acid. The neutralizing agent is triethylamine, methylamine or triethanolamine. The aqueous dispersion is polyvinylpyrrolidone, triammonium citrate or beta-alanine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of component A and component B is 100:30-40. The nanofiber slurry is a slurry prepared from inorganic nanofibers. Preferred Method: The vinyl terminated modified E44 epoxy resin emulsion is prepared by (a) adding a polyol and an isocyanate to a reactor, adding an organotin catalyst, reacting under the protection of nitrogen at 80-85 degrees C for 2-2.5 hours, then adding a chain extende, continuously reacting for 0.6-0.8 hours to obtain a prepolymer, where the molar ratio of polyol, isocyanate and chain extender is 4:3.5-5.5:1-1.3, (b) mixing the prepolymer, E44 epoxy resin, 2-(tert-butylamino)ethyl methacrylate and azobisisobutyronitrile in a mass ratio of 1:5.5-6.5:1.2-1.4:0.05-0.1, reacting under the nitrogen protection at 82-86 degrees C for 0.8-1.1 hours, adding vinyl-terminated polymethylphenylsiloxane, continuously reacting by performing end-capping reaction for 1.2-1.6 hours, where the mass ratio of the vinyl-terminated polymethylphenylsiloxane and the prepolymer is 0.3 1.8-2.5, adding neutralizing agent, performing neutralization, adding deionized water, vigorously stirring to obtain an emulsion, and adding deionized water and neutralizing agent in an amount of 15-18 times the mass of the prepolymer and 0.06-00.08 times the mass of the prepolymer. The liquid polysulfide rubber solution is prepared by taking liquid polysulfide rubber, xylene, dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate and azobisisobutyronitrile in a mass ratio of 20:30-45:2.5-4:0.3-0.6, uniformly mixing, under the protection of nitrogen, heating to 85-90 degrees C, refluxing and reacting for 2-4 hours, releasing the cold discharge, removing the upper solvent layer, and distilling-off the lower thick layer by using a solvent under reduced pressure. The component A is prepared by (A1) adding an aqueous epoxy curing agent, a dispersing agent, an antifoaming agent and a co-solvent to a production cylinder, and stirring at 400-600 rpm for 5 minutes, (A2) adding aqueous organic bentonite, fumed silica, and aqueous wax slurry under stirring conditions, and stirring at 400-600 rpm for 5 minutes, (A3) adding titanium dioxide, carbon black, aqueous zinc phosphate and nano barium sulfate under stirring conditions, and stirring at 600-800 rpm for 10 minutes, (A4) grinding to a fineness opening 30 microns by using a sand mill, and (A5) slowly adding zinc powder, nanofiber pulp and graphene pulp under stirring at 400-600 rpm and continuously stirring at 1000-1200 rpm for 25-30 minutes. The component B is prepared by (B1) adding an aqueous epoxy resin emulsion, a liquid polysulfide rubber emulsion, an emulsifier, a corrosion inhibitor and a flash-proof rust agent to a production cylinder, and stirring at 400-600 rpm for 10-20 minutes.

PN CN107916191-A
TI Producing organic wine, by interplanting e.g. garlic in orchard, applying fertilizer with ecological bio-stimulating hormone, harvesting grapes, heating adding enzyme, squeezing mash, fermenting juice, purifying and filtering product.
AU LIANG H
   WANG Y
AE YANTAI ZHIQI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (YANT-Non-standard)
GA 201833444R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing organic wine involves interplanting plants having insecticidal effect e.g. garlic in orchard, applying fertilizer with ecological bio-stimulating hormone, harvesting healthy grapes, heating grape pulp, adding enzyme and grape pulp into a container, filling nitrogen, soaking grape mash, squeezing grape mash under flow of nitrogen or carbon dioxide, purifying juice, inoculating S.cerevisiae, fermenting juice, inoculating lactic acid bacteria, fermenting product, purifying and filtering product. The grape variety includes Syrah and Riesling.
   USE - The method is useful for producing organic wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce organic wine without adding sulfur dioxide, and increases nutrient content of organic wine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for producing organic wine involves interplanting plants having insecticidal effect such as garlic, lavender, Geranium, pitcher plant, Drosera indica and mugworts in orchard, applying pesticide, and fertilizer mixed with ecological bio-stimulating hormone, sampling and measuring sugar content and acidity of grapes, harvesting fresh and healthy grapes having sugar content of 160-230 g/l and acidity of 6-6.5 g/l, crushing stems at 5-20 degrees C, heating grape pulp at 50-65 degrees C for 20-60 minutes to inactivate oxidase, cooling grape pulp to 15-25 degrees C, adding 20-60 mg/l enzyme and grape pulp into a container, filling nitrogen or carbon dioxide, cooling mash to 5-8 degrees C and soaking grape mash for 12-96 hours, spraying water for 1-2 times every day, where spraying volume is 1/2-1/3 times of volume of the grape mash, squeezing grape mash under flow of nitrogen or carbon dioxide, adding purifying agent, purifying juice for 24-48 hours until turbidity reaches 150-250 nephelometric turbidity unit, inoculating aroma-producing yeast except Saccharomyces cerevisiae at a concentration of 106 colony forming unit/ml, fermenting juice at 10-12 degrees C for 24-72 hours, inoculating S.cerevisiae, again fermenting juice at 16-20 degrees C for 24-72 hours, when specific gravity is reduced to 0.993 during alcohol fermentation, cooling fermented product to 5-10 degrees C, filling product in a container under flow of nitrogen or carbon dioxide gas, inoculating 20-60 mg/l lactic acid bacteria, fermenting product at 20-25 degrees C for 24-480 hours, storing fermented product at 10-15 degrees C for 3-24 months, purifying product using purifying agent, and filtering product using 0.45 mu m and 0.20 mu m filter membrane. The grape variety is chosen from Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Gernischet, Cabernet Franc, Syrah, Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, Italian Riesling, and Riesling, the enzyme is chosen from pectinase, cellulose, papain, alginase, and amylase, and the purifying agent is chosen from gelatin, bentonite, polyvinylpolypyrrolidone, and egg white, and the fertilizer is organic fertilizer, microbial fertilizer, organic-inorganic fertilizer or bioorganic fertilizer. The ecological bio-stimulating hormone is prepared by (a) washing and crushing seaweed, adding water, sterilizing mixture at 60-120 degrees C for 5-40 minutes, adding enzymes and hydrolyzing mixture for 30-60 minutes, where mass ratio of seaweed, water and enzyme is 5-30:70-95:0.02-1, and filtering hydrolyzate, (b) mixing residues with urea, magnesium sulfate, sodium chloride, potassium dihydrogen phosphate and glucose in a mass ratio of 95.5:1:0.5:1:1:1, adjusting water content to 50-70%, sterilizing mixture at 70-150 degrees C for 5-40 minutes, cooling product to 27-36 degrees C, inoculating Rhodotorula mucilaginosa, culturing strain at 27-36 degrees C for 6-144 hours, maintaining oxygen concentration in fermentation tank at 8-21%, sterilizing fermentation broth at 70-150 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, and cooling product to 20-40 degrees C, and (c) mixing products obtained in steps (a) and (b), inoculating Corynebacterium ammoniagenes, maintaining oxygen content at 1-8 mg/l, fermenting strain at 25-35 degrees C for 1-6 days, filtering fermentation broth, concentrating filtrate and spray drying concentrate, where the seaweed is chosen from Enteromorpha, Laminaria japonica, Sargassum, Undaria pinnatifida, and Kombu.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Biomolecule: The enzyme comprises lysozyme and pectinase in mass ratio of 5:1. Preferred Method: The lysozyme is prepared by adding 5 times water into egg white or whole egg liquid, uniformly stirring and homogenizing mixture at 30-60 degrees C and 2-40 MPa using homogenizer for 10-40 minutes, filtering homogenate, again filtering filtrate using ultra-filtration membrane having molecular weight cutoff of 15000-30000 Da, diluting and eluting filtrate through adsorption resin using 0.001-0.5 mol/l aqueous sodium chloride solution, desalting eluate using ultra-filtration membrane having molecular weight of 2000-10000 Da, drying filtrate. The filtration method is chosen from pressure filtration, and centrifugal separation.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pesticide is chosen from lime sulfur mixture, bordeaux mixture, mineral oil, Trichoderma harzianum, quinolinone suspension, ethanol, soda, azadirachtin, rotenone, pyrethrum emulsifiable concentrate, and matrine. The adsorption resin is a cation exchange resin or macroporous resin, where the cation exchange resin is chosen from 001asterisk7(732) (RTM: Strongly acidic styrene cation exchange resin), Amberlite IR-120 (RTM: Strongly acidic cation exchange resin), Dowex-50 (RTM: High capacity cation exchange resin), Lewatit-100 (RTM: Strongly acidic gelular cation exchange resin), Diaion SK-1 (RTM: Strongly acidic cation exchange resin), Allassion CS (RTM: Strongly acidic cation exchange resin), Duolite C-20 (RTM: Gel type strongly acidic cation exchange resin of sulfonated polystyrene type), and styrene-divinylbenzene-3, and the macroporous resin is AB-8, D101, D3520, X-5, NKA-II, NKA-9, S-8, XDA-1, H-20, H-30, and H-40 Preferred Microorganisms: The R.mucilaginosa is R.mucilaginosa strain (CICC 31192), and the C.ammoniagenes is C.ammoniagenes strain (CICC 10168).
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

PT P
PN IN201921024595-A; IN391690-B
TI Producing olefins comprises heating feed comprising carbon dioxide and alkane in reformer, contacting first stream comprising syngas with second catalyst, contacting second stream comprising dimethyl ether with catalyst and separating water.
AU BHATT U B
   KALANTRI P B
   KELKAR J V
   DINGANKAR S B
AE RELIANCE IND LTD (RELN-C)
GA 202101986N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing olefins, comprises: (a) heating feed comprising carbon dioxide and at least one alkane in a reformer, at a first predetermined temperature and at a first predetermined pressure, in presence of first catalyst, to produce first stream comprising syngas and unused feed; (b) contacting the first stream comprising syngas with at least one second catalyst in a first reactor, at second predetermined temperature and at second predetermined pressure, to produce second stream comprising dimethyl ether (DME), and first mixture of unconverted syngas and carbon dioxide; (c) contacting second stream comprising DME with third catalyst in a second reactor, at third predetermined temperature and at third predetermined pressure, to produce third stream comprising olefins water, unconverted DME, and a second mixture of alkanes comprising the alkane; and (d) separating the water, unconverted DME, and second mixture of alkanes from the third stream in fractionation column to obtain the olefins.
   USE - The process is useful for producing olefins.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing olefins, comprises: (a) heating a feed comprising carbon dioxide and at least one alkane in a reformer, at a first predetermined temperature and at a first predetermined pressure, in the presence of at least one first catalyst, to produce a first stream comprising syngas and unused feed, where the ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas is 1:1; (b) contacting the first stream comprising syngas with at least one second catalyst in a first reactor, at a second predetermined temperature and at a second predetermined pressure, to produce a second stream comprising dimethyl ether (DME), and a first mixture of unconverted syngas and carbon dioxide; (c) contacting the second stream comprising DME with a third catalyst in a second reactor, at a third predetermined temperature and at a third predetermined pressure, to produce a third stream comprising olefins water, unconverted DME, and a second mixture of alkanes comprising the alkane; and (d) separating the water, the unconverted DME, and the second mixture of alkanes from the third stream in a fractionation column to obtain the olefins. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for apparatus for producing olefins comprising (i) a reformer (R) configured for receiving a feed comprising carbon dioxide and at least one alkane, and heating the feed in the presence of a first catalyst to produce a first stream comprising syngas and unused feed, (ii) a first separator (S1) configured for receiving the first stream and separating from the first stream, the unused feed to obtain a stream of syngas, (iii) a first conduit for leading the first stream from the reformer (R) to the first separator (S1), (iv) a second conduit for recycling the unused feed from the first separator (S1) to the reformer (R), (v) a first reactor (D) configured for receiving the stream comprising syngas and contacting the stream comprising syngas with a second catalyst to obtain a second stream comprising DME and a first mixture comprising unconverted syngas and carbon dioxide, (vi) a third conduit for leading the stream comprising syngas from the first separator (S1) to the first reactor (D), (vii) a second separator (S2) configured for receiving the second stream, and separating from the second stream, the first mixture to obtain a stream of DME, (viii) a fourth conduit for leading the second stream from the first reactor (D) to the second separator (S2), (ix) a third separator (S3) configured for receiving the first mixture, and separating the unconverted syngas and the carbon dioxide from the first mixture, (x) a fifth conduit (v) for leading the first mixture from the second separator (S2) to the third separator (S3), (xii) a sixth conduit for recycling the unconverted syngas from the third separator (S3) to the first reactor (D), (xiii) a seventh conduit for recycling the carbon dioxide from the third separator (S3) to the reformer (R), (xiv) a second reactor (O) configured for receiving the stream comprising DME and contacting the stream comprising DME with a third catalyst to obtain a third stream comprising olefins, water, unconverted DME, and a second mixture of alkanes comprising the alkane, (xv) an eighth conduit for leading the stream comprising DME from the second separator (S2) to the second reactor (O), (xvi) a fractionation column (Dw) configured for receiving the third stream, and separating the water, the unconverted DME, the second mixture of alkanes from the third stream to obtain a stream of olefins, (xvii) a ninth conduit for leading the third stream from the second reactor (O) to the fractionation column (Dw), (xviii) a tenth conduit (x) for recycling the second mixture of alkanes from the fractionation column (Dw) to the reformer (R), and (xix) an eleventh conduit for recycling the unconverted DME and heavies from the fractionation column (Dw) to the second reactor (O).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process comprises: separating from the first stream the syngas and the unused feed, in a first separator and recycling the unused feed to the reformer; and separating from the second stream, the DME and the first mixture of unconverted syngas and carbon dioxide in a second separator, separating the unconverted syngas and the carbon dioxide in a third separator and recycling the separated carbon dioxide to the reformer, and the unconverted syngas to the first reactor. The process steps (a)-(c) are carried out in a single reactor by heating the feed comprising carbon monoxide and the alkane, at the first predetermined temperature and at the first predetermined pressure, in the presence of at least one tri-functional catalyst, to produce the third stream comprising water, unconverted syngas, unconverted carbon dioxide, unconverted alkane, olefins, and unconverted DME and separating the water, unconverted syngas, unconverted carbon dioxide, unconverted alkane, and unconverted DME from the third stream in the fractionation column to obtain the olefins. The tri-functional catalyst is at least one comprising copper oxide, chromium oxide, zinc oxide, aluminum oxide, zeolites, aluminophosphate molecular sieve, and silicoaluminophosphate (SAPO). The first predetermined temperature of 300-1000 degrees C and the first predetermined pressure of 1-80 kg/cm2. The second predetermined temperature is 100-400 degrees C and the second predetermined pressure of 1-60 kg/cm2. The third predetermined temperature is 200-600 degrees C and the third predetermined pressure is 0.5-10 kg/cm2. The process comprises recycling the unconverted DME to the second reactor and the mixture of alkanes to the reformer. The olefin is at least one of ethylene and propylene. The alkane is a 1-20C alkane. The ratio of the carbon dioxide to the amount of the alkanes is 1:1-4:1.11. Preferred Components: The first catalyst comprises nickel, cerium or cobalt. The second catalyst comprises copper oxide, chromium oxide, zinc oxide and or aluminum oxide. The third catalyst comprises zeolites, aluminophosphate molecular sieve or silicoaluminophosphate (SAPO).

AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SCI-TECH (UZST-C)
   ZHEJIANG LVRAN ENVIRONMENTAL ENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021870801
AB    NOVELTY - Reaction device comprises an insulating tube (1), in which the top is provided with an air inlet (2), and the bottom is provided with an air outlet (3). The insulating tube is divided from top to bottom into a first discharge area (4) and a second discharge area (5), the volume ratio of the first discharge area and the second discharge area is 3:1-1:3. The first discharge area and the second discharge area are separated by a first mesh plate and a first catalyst is placed on the first mesh plate. An air inlet pipe penetrates the upper wall of the insulating pipe and extends to the bottom of the insulating pipe, and the air inlet pipe is coaxial with the insulating pipe. The air inlet pipe is provided with a first exhaust hole and a second exhaust hole. The first exhaust hole is located in the first discharge area, and the second exhaust hole is located in the first discharge area. the material of the intake pipe is metal.
   USE - Reaction device for generating methanol by using carbon dioxide and hydrogen (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reaction device comprises an insulating tube, in which the top is provided with an air inlet, and the bottom is provided with an air outlet. The insulating tube is divided from top to bottom into a first discharge area and a second discharge area, the volume ratio of the first discharge area and the second discharge area is 3:1-1:3. The first discharge area and the second discharge area are separated by a first mesh plate and a first catalyst is placed on the first mesh plate. An air inlet pipe penetrates the upper wall of the insulating pipe and extends to the bottom of the insulating pipe, and the air inlet pipe is coaxial with the insulating pipe. The air inlet pipe is provided with a first exhaust hole and a second exhaust hole. The first exhaust hole is located in the first discharge area, and the second exhaust hole is located in the first discharge area. the material of the intake pipe is metal. The metal mesh is located on the outer surface of the first discharge zone of the insulating tube, and the metal mesh is connected to the grounding device. Cooling device is located on the outer surface of the second discharge area of the insulating tube, and a second screen is located at the bottom of the second discharge area, and a second catalyst is placed on the second screen. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for generating methanol from carbon dioxide and hydrogen based on the reaction device, which involves passing hydrogen into the intake pipe, passing carbon dioxide into the intake port, and energizing the intake pipe, discharging hydrogen and carbon dioxide from the first exhaust hole in the first discharge zone, performing a pre-reaction to obtain a pre-reaction gas produced, where the pre-reaction gas produced includes carbon monoxide and methanol, entering pre-reaction gas and unreacted carbon dioxide into the second discharge zone, under the action of the cooling device carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide undergo a reforming reaction with the hydrogen discharged from the second exhaust hole to obtain methanol.

PT P
PN WO2021137862-A1; CN115087599-A; EP4085009-A1
TI Package used in packaged product e.g. food,            contains self-sterilizing components comprising            chlorite ions that are substantially free of            energy-activated catalyst and of acid-releasing            compound, and headspace comprising carbon dioxide.
AU MCGINNIS J E
   YAGER T C
AE BEMIS CO INC (BEMI-C)
   AMCOR FLEXIBLES NORTH AMERICA INC (AMCL-C)
   AMCOR FLEXIBLES NORTH AMERICA INC (AMCL-C)
GA 2021813392
AB    NOVELTY - Package (300) comprises: a sterile barrier                packaging film; self-sterilizing components                comprising many chlorite ions and water, where the                self-sterilizing components are substantially free                of an energy-activated catalyst and of an                acid-releasing compound; a package interior (302)                formed by hermetically sealing the sterile barrier                packaging film; and a headspace within the package                interior comprising carbon dioxide present in an                amount of greater than or equal to 5% by volume of                the headspace.
   USE - The package is useful in a packaged product                i.e. a medical device or a medical supply, or a                food (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The package: is self-sterilizing by providing                controlled-release of chlorine dioxide gas for                sterilizing various products; and offers a low-cost                solution with minimal changes to existing packaging                lines and procedures to achieve benefits in control                and/or efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a packaged product comprising a packaging                film, many chlorite ions, water, the package                interior formed by hermetically sealing the                packaging film, a product within the package                interior, and the headspace within the package                interior comprising greater than or equal to 5%                carbon dioxide by volume of the headspace, where                self-sterilizing components comprise the water and                the chlorite ions, and are substantially free of                energy-activated catalyst and are substantially                free of an acid releasing compound; and
   (2) sterilizing the packaged product,                comprising providing an unsealed package comprising                a sterile barrier packaging film comprising the                primary layer and chlorite ions and the package                interior, and introducing water to the package                interior, introducing a product to be sterilized to                the package interior, introducing a gas comprising                carbon dioxide to the package interior of the                package, hermetically sealing an open end of the                unsealed package after introducing the gas                comprising carbon dioxide to form a                self-sterilizing package containing a packaged                product and a headspace, and exposing the                self-sterilizing package to UV radiation, where                self-sterilizing components comprise the water and                the chlorite ions, and are substantially free of an                energy-activated catalyst and of the acid-releasing                compound.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic sectional view                of the package.
   Patch (100)
   Package (300)
   Package interior (302)
   First side of package (307)
   Second side of package (309)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: When the package or                  the packaging film is exposed to UV light having                  a wavelength of 254 nm, reaction of the chlorite                  ions with the water generates chlorine dioxide                  which is released into the headspace, and is                  effective to sterilize the product within the                  package interior. In the absence of any UV light,                  there is not chlorine dioxide (ClO2) generation.                  The water is proximate to the chlorite ions. The                  package has a relative humidity in the package                  interior of 20-100% at 72.5 degrees F. A salt                  comprising sodium chlorite, potassium chlorite,                  calcium chlorite, magnesium chlorite, lithium                  chlorite and/or ammonium chlorite supplies the                  chlorite ions. The sterile barrier packaging film                  is substantially UV-light transparent. The                  sterile barrier packaging film comprises a                  primary layer and a patch (100) fixed to the                  primary layer, where the patch supplies the                  chlorite ions. An amount of at least 1.39 mu g/ml                  of chlorine dioxide is generated within the                  package within a timeframe of 5 minutes. The UV                  radiation includes a 254 nm wavelength.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The patch comprises: a                  patch support that is permeable to chlorine                  dioxide and that defines a first major surface of                  the patch; and a patch sealing layer comprising                  the chlorite ions dispersed in a polymer                  composition, where the patch sealing layer is in                  contact with the patch support and defines a                  second major surface of the patch, and the patch                  sealing layer is fixed to the primary layer. The                  polymer composition comprises a polyethylene, a                  polypropylene, a polyacrylate, and/or their                  copolymer. The chlorite ions are present in a                  coating on of the self-sterilizing packaging                  film, and the coating comprises a chlorite salt                  in combination with a polymer composition. The                  polymer composition comprises a polyethylene, a                  polypropylene, a polyacrylate and/or their                  copolymer.

PT P
PN CN112479201-A; CN112479201-B
TI Purifying artificial diamond, comprises e.g.            placing rough artificial diamond into an atmosphere            furnace and blowing nitrogen gas, heating, then passing            carbon dioxide gas, reacting, cooling and then taking            out the material.
AU MAN W
   ZHU C
   GONG C
   WU J
   JIANG J
AE SHANGHAI ZS TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI ZHENGSHI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202130851Y
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying artificial diamond, comprises (i)                placing rough artificial diamond into an atmosphere                furnace and blowing nitrogen gas so that the oxygen                volume concentration in the atmosphere furnace is                lower than 5 ppm and heating, then passing carbon                dioxide gas so that the oxygen volume concentration                in the atmosphere furnace is lower than 2 ppm,                reacting in this atmosphere and then cooling and                then taking out the material to obtain reaction                material, the carbon dioxide volume concentration                is greater than 99%, (ii) adding reaction materials                into the autoclave, heating, passing carbon                monoxide to make the pressure 1-10 MPa, then                reacting, releasing the pressure, and passing the                generated gas into ethanol, cooling to less than 25                degrees C, and then adding materials in the                autoclave to absolute ethanol, mixing and stirring                to obtain solid-liquid mixed material, and (iii)                subjecting solid-liquid mixed material to                solid-liquid separation, washing with absolute                ethanol and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying artificial                diamond.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: effectively purifies the metal and                unconverted carbon; and has high efficiency and                high final diamond purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Purifying artificial diamond, comprises (i)                placing rough artificial diamond into an atmosphere                furnace and blowing nitrogen gas so that the oxygen                volume concentration in the atmosphere furnace is                lower than 5 ppm and heating at 700-800?OC, then                passing carbon dioxide gas so that the oxygen                volume concentration in the atmosphere furnace is                lower than 2 ppm, reacting in this atmosphere for                30-60 minutes and then cooling to less than 100                degrees C and then taking out the material to                obtain reaction material, the carbon dioxide volume                concentration is greater than 99%, (ii) adding                reaction materials into the autoclave, heating at                50-120 degrees C, passing carbon monoxide to make                the pressure 1-10 MPa, then reacting for 30-60                minutes, releasing the pressure, and passing the                generated gas into ethanol, cooling to less than 25                degrees C, and then adding materials in the                autoclave to absolute ethanol, mixing and stirring                for 10-20 minutes to obtain solid-liquid mixed                material, and (iii) subjecting solid-liquid mixed                material to solid-liquid separation, then washing                with absolute ethanol, and drying in a vacuum to                obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  volume concentration of oxygen in the nitrogen is                  less than 1 ppm, and the nitrogen preparation                  method is cryogenic air separation nitrogen                  production, pressure swing adsorption nitrogen                  production or membrane separation nitrogen                  production. In the step (iii), the equipment used                  for solid-liquid separation is a centrifuge, the                  drying temperature in the drying process in the                  vacuum drying oven is 40-60 degrees C and the                  vacuum degree in the drying process in the vacuum                  drying oven is -0.08 to -0.05 MPa. The step (ii)                  comprises absorbing the ethanol of the gas and                  carrying out distillation under reduced pressure                  to separate the gas from the ethanol. Preferred                  Components: In the step (ii), the mass ratio of                  the materials in the autoclave to the absolute                  ethanol is 1:3-5.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN CN111744375-A; CN111744375-B
TI Mixed matrix membrane with highly selective gas channel for separation of mixed gas containing carbon dioxide, comprises hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane, hydrophilic polymer membrane and metal composite hydrophobic filler.
AU QIAO Z
   JIA X
   ZHONG C
   SUN Y
   HUANG H
   GUO X
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2020A11793
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed matrix membrane with highly selective gas channels comprises hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane, hydrophilic polymer membrane and metal composite hydrophobic filler particles. The hydrophilic polymer membrane is combined with hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane through hydrogen bonding. The metal composite hydrophobic filler particles are evenly and vertically distributed on the hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane and penetrate each other with the hydrophilic polymer membrane. The hydrophilic polymer membrane and metal composite hydrophobic filler particles are far away from each other due to the difference in electronegativity and forming gas channel gap. The hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane is prepared by coating polydimethylsiloxane and polyvinyl alcohol on surface of polysulfone. The hydrophilic polymer membrane is formed by coating polyvinylamine on the surface of metal composite hydrophobic filler particles to perform interface self-assembly.
   USE - Mixed matrix membrane with highly selective gas channel for separation of mixed gas containing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed matrix membrane has carbon dioxide permeability of 1800-3400 GPA, carbon dioxide permeability coefficient of 490-995 Barrer, carbon dioxide/nitrogen gas selectivity of 65-70.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mixed matrix membrane with highly selective gas channels comprises hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane, hydrophilic polymer membrane and metal composite hydrophobic filler particles. The hydrophilic polymer membrane is combined with hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane through hydrogen bonding. The metal composite hydrophobic filler particles are evenly and vertically distributed on the hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane and penetrate each other with the hydrophilic polymer membrane. The hydrophilic polymer membrane and metal composite hydrophobic filler particles are far away from each other due to the difference in electronegativity and forming gas channel gap. The hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane is prepared by coating polydimethylsiloxane and polyvinyl alcohol on the surface of polysulfone. The metal composite hydrophobic filler particles are synthesized in situ on the surface of the hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane by using low-temperature solid-phase synthesis method using metal salt and ligand. The hydrophilic polymer membrane is formed by coating polyvinylamine on the surface of metal composite hydrophobic filler particles to perform interface self-assembly. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the mixed matrix membrane, which involves using polydimethylsiloxane solution , polyvinyl alcohol solution, polysulfone, metal salt solution, ligand-methanol solution and polyvinylamine aqueous solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The metal salt is anhydrous copper acetate. The ligand is 4-fluorobenzoic acid, 4-chlorobenzoic acid, 4-bromobenzoic acid or 4-iodobenzoic acid. Preferred Composition: The concentration of ligand-methanol solution is 60 mmol/L and metal salt aqueous solution is 0.25 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polysulfone is commercial ultrafiltration membrane with average pore diameter of 20-50 nm. The polydimethylsiloxane solution comprises polydimethylsiloxane and n-heptane. The polydimethylsiloxane is of formula (I).n = 300-500.Preferred Properties: The polyvinyl alcohol has polymerization degree of 1700 and alcoholysis degree of 87-89%. The polyvinylamine has weight average molecular weight of 100000-500000. The thickness of hydrophilic modified polysulfone membrane is 50-100 nm, hydrophilic polymer membrane is 270-300 nm and metal composite hydrophobic filler particles are 480-740 nm. Preferred Composition: The mixed matrix membrane comprises 0.1-1.0 wt.% polydimethylsiloxane solution, 0.01-0.5 wt.% polyvinyl alcohol aqueous solution and 0.1-3.0 wt.% polyvinylamine aqueous solution.
