PT P
PN CN111440946-A; CN111440946-B
TI Rare earth extraction method for realizing            recycling of magnesium bicarbonate by saponifying            acidic extractant and magnesium bicarbonate, mixing            saponified extractant with rare earth material liquid,            extracting and separating.
AU SUN X
   LIU X
   WEI Y
   FENG Z
   HUANG X
   PENG X
   XU Y
   WANG M
   XIA C
AE GRIREM ADVANCED MATERIALS CO LTD (BFER-C)
GA 2020724530
AB    NOVELTY - Rare earth extraction method for realizing the                recycling of magnesium bicarbonate, involves (a)                mixing the acidic extractant and the magnesium                bicarbonate solution to perform the saponification                reaction, and separately recovering the carbon                dioxide and saponification wastewater generated in                the saponification process for use, (b) mixing the                saponified extractant with the rare earth material                liquid to perform the rare earth soap reaction, and                extracting and separating to obtain magnesium                chloride wastewater and the required rare earth                products, respectively, (c) adding the                saponification wastewater collected in the step (a)                to the magnesium chloride wastewater collected in                the step (b) for dilution, and adding an amine                extractant for mixing, and passing the carbon                dioxide gas into the carbonization reaction, and                (d) clarifying the reaction solution and separating                into phases, and recycling the magnesium                bicarbonate solution for the saponification                reaction of step (a).
   USE - The rare earth extraction method is used for                realizing the recycling of magnesium                bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The rare earth extraction method reduces the                salt discharge in the rare earth extraction                process, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rare earth extraction method for realizing the                recycling of magnesium bicarbonate, which involves                combining the magnesium chloride wastewater                recovered in the rare earth soap process with an                organic amine extractant and recovering carbon                dioxide to prepare a magnesium bicarbonate solution                through extraction-carbonization. The method                involves (a) mixing the acidic extractant and the                magnesium bicarbonate solution to perform the                saponification reaction, and separately recovering                the carbon dioxide and saponification wastewater                generated in the saponification process for use,                (b) mixing the saponified extractant with the rare                earth material liquid to perform the rare earth                soap reaction, and extracting and separating to                obtain magnesium chloride wastewater and the                required rare earth products, respectively, (c)                adding the saponification wastewater collected in                the step (a) to the magnesium chloride wastewater                collected in the step (b) for dilution, and adding                an amine extractant for mixing, and passing the                carbon dioxide gas generated in the step (a) into                the carbonization reaction, and (d) clarifying the                reaction solution and separating into phases, where                the resulting organic phase is the extract                containing the amine extractant and hydrochloric                acid, and the resulting aqueous phase is the                desired magnesium bicarbonate solution, and                recycling for the saponification reaction of step                (a).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (b) further                  involves adjusting the concentration of the                  magnesium chloride wastewater to 0.1-5 mol/l,                  and/or adjusting the pH of the magnesium chloride                  wastewater to 2-7. In the step (c), the volume                  ratio of the saponification wastewater used for                  dilution to the magnesium chloride wastewater is                  5:1-20:1. The step (d) further involves adding                  pure water or the saponification wastewater to                  the separated organic phase to perform mixing and                  back extraction, where the ratio of the organic                  phase to the pure water or the saponification                  wastewater is 0.5 :1-5:1, and the time of mixing                  and stripping is 1-60 minutes. Preferred                  Components: In the step (a), the acidic                  extractant includes P507, P204, P229, and                  bis(2,4,4-trimethylpentyl)phosphinic acid                  (HBTMPP). In the step (c), the amine extractant                  includes a primary, secondary, or tertiary amine                  organic amine extractant dissolved in a diluent,                  where the diluent includes benzene, monohydric                  alcohol, polyhydric alcohol or kerosene, and the                  volume ratio of the amine extractant to the                  diluent is 0.2:1-2:1. In the step (a), the                  concentration of the magnesium bicarbonate                  solution is 0.01-0.5 mol/l. In the step (a), the                  dosage ratio of the acidic extractant to the                  magnesium bicarbonate solution is 0.2:1-2:1. In                  the step (b), the concentration of the rare earth                  material liquid is 0.5-2 mol/l, and the volume                  ratio of the saponified extractant to the rare                  earth material liquid is 3:1-15:1. In the step                  (c), the mixing volume ratio of the magnesium                  chloride wastewater diluted with the                  saponification wastewater and the amine                  extractant is 0.2:1-5:1, and the purity of the                  injected carbon dioxide is controlled to be                  30-100%, and the carbonization time 5-300                  minutes.

PN CN108675735-A
TI Preparing solid waste carbonized foamed concrete by injecting carbon dioxide gas into hot slurry prepared using solid wastes, heat preserving, stirring, adding aluminum powder foaming agent, then injecting into mold, foaming and curing.
AU LIAO H
   REN G
   GAO H
   CHENG F
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 201884289A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid waste carbonized foamed concrete involves: (1) mixing and stirring 3 kinds of solid wastes to form solid waste-based cementing material; (2) adding water to the solid waste-based cementing material at 40-85 degrees C and heating to maintain the slurry temperature at 40-85 degrees C to obtain a hot slurry; (3) injecting carbon dioxide gas into the hot slurry, heat preserving and stirring to form a carbonated slurry; (4) adding an aluminum powder foaming agent to the carbonated slurry and continuously stirring to form a foaming slurry; (5) injecting the foaming slurry into a mold to complete grouting and foaming to prepare a formed concrete initial setting block; (6) placing the initial setting block in the carbonation curing room, and curing to form a carbonated curing block; and (7) placing the carbonated curing block in an autoclave curing room for curing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing solid waste carbonized foamed concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical preparation of solid waste carbonized foamed concrete using wide source of raw materials with reduction carbon dioxide emission and solid waste resource utilization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing solid waste carbonized foamed concrete involves: (1) mixing and stirring 3 kinds of solid wastes according to the element content including calcium and magnesium, silicon and aluminum, and iron and sulfur in a mass ratio of (55-60):(35-40):(5-10) to form solid waste-based cementing material; (2) adding water to the solid waste-based cementing material at 40-85 degrees C, where the mass ratio of solid waste-based cementing material and water is 1:(0.35-0.65), and heating to maintain the slurry temperature at 40-85 degrees C to obtain a hot slurry; (3) injecting carbon dioxide gas into the hot slurry, heat preserving and stirring to form a carbonated slurry; (4) adding an aluminum powder foaming agent to the carbonated slurry, and continuously stirring to form a foaming slurry; (5) injecting the foaming slurry into a mold to complete grouting and foaming to prepare a formed concrete initial setting block; (6) placing the initial setting block in the carbonation curing room, and curing under the condition of carbon dioxide concentration to form a carbonated curing block; and (7) placing the carbonated curing block in an autoclave curing room for curing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The solid waste-based cementing material is a powdery material or muddy material. The solid waste comprises coal slag powder, steel slag powder, slag powder, calcium carbide mud and/or desulfurized gypsum. Preferred Properties: The powdery material has an average particle size of less than 75 mu m.

PN CN108609642-A; CN108609642-B
TI Comprehensive utilization of chlorine waste salt mud by mixing chlorine waste salt mud with water, adding calcium chloride solution, passing through carbon dioxide gas, leaching, filtering, and adding filtrate with sodium hydroxide solution.
AU YAN P
   LIU Z
AE NORTH CHINA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NCSI-C)
   YINGCHUANG SANZHENG YINGKOU FINE CHEM CO (YING-Non-standard)
   NORTH CHINA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NCSI-C)
GA 2018804317
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensive utilization of chlorine waste salt mud includes mixing chlorine waste salt mud with water, adding calcium chloride solution, transferring slurry to reaction kettle, passing through carbon dioxide gas, performing selective leaching reaction, suction filtering, adding filtrate to stirring tank, and subjecting filter cake to subsequent secondary selective leaching reaction; adding filter cake with water, adding calcium chloride solution, transferring slurry to reaction kettle, passing through carbon dioxide gas, performing secondary selective leaching reaction, suction filtering, pumping filtrate to step (A) stirring tank, washing secondary filter cake, and drying to obtain calcium carbonate product; adding filtrate with sodium hydroxide solution, stirring, reacting, pressure-filtering, repeatedly washing, and drying to obtain magnesium hydroxide product; and subjecting to high-efficiency distillation.
   USE - Method for comprehensive utilization of chlorine waste salt mud.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environment-friendly and recycles chlorine waste salt slurry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive utilization of chlorine waste salt mud comprises:
   (A) mixing chlorine waste salt mud with water at mass ratio of 1:20-1:10, adding 0.125-0.5 mol/L calcium chloride solution at ratio of 0.8-1.2:1, transferring 60-80 wt.% slurry to reaction kettle, passing through carbon dioxide gas at pressure of 0.3-0.8 MPa, performing selective leaching reaction at 20 degrees C for 1-3 hours, suction filtering, adding filtrate to stirring tank, and subjecting filter cake to subsequent secondary selective leaching reaction;
   (B) adding filter cake with water at mass ratio of 1:20-1:10, adding 0.125-0.3 mol/L calcium chloride solution at molar ratio of 1-1.2:1, transferring 60-80 wt.% slurry to reaction kettle, passing through carbon dioxide gas at pressure of 0.3-0.8 MPa, performing secondary selective leaching reaction at 20 degrees C for 1-3 hours, suction filtering, pumping filtrate to step (A) stirring tank, washing secondary filter cake, and drying to obtain calcium carbonate product;
   (C) adding filtrate with 1-3 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution at molar ratio of 1:2, stirring at speed of 400 revolutions/minute, reacting at room temperature for 40 minutes, pressure-filtering, repeatedly washing, and drying to obtain magnesium hydroxide product; and
   (D) subjecting to high-efficiency distillation.

The                cavity is connected with the micro-channel flat                pipe to absorb heat of the micro-channel flat pipe. The                regeneration tower is used for separating rich                liquid out of carbon dioxide to form barren liquid.    USE - Absorption regeneration decoupling carbon                capture system for use in coal-fired power plant,                chemical absorption method decarburization and                carbon dioxide emission reduction technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The absorption regeneration decoupling carbon                capture system has reasonable structure design, can                reduce the system floor area and reduce the energy                cost. Because the controller controls the turn on                tower and the regeneration tower according to the                difference of the energy price, the system can                selectively start or close the regeneration and                absorption towers, so as to reduce the power                consumption cost. Since the rich liquid cavity and                poor liquid cavity are integrated in the same                liquid storage tank, and the volume of the rich and                barren liquid cavities can be changed, the                absorption and purification efficiency of the                carbon dioxide can be improved.

TI Preparing nano-silica used in carbon dioxide            adsorbent, involves adding surfactant and templating            agent to water, and adding catalyst to obtain mixed            solution, and then adding silicon source into mixed            solution to generate precipitation.
AU QU F
   SHEN X
   ZHANG Z
   LI C
   YAN F
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 2022649762
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nano-silica, involves adding                surfactant and templating agent to water, and                adding catalyst to obtain a mixed solution, and                then adding silicon source into the mixed solution                to generate orthosilicic acid precipitation. The                organic alcohol solvent is added to the                orthosilicic acid precipitation for azeotropic                distillation to obtain the orthosilicic acid. The                orthosilicic acid is dried and calcined after the                hole expansion to obtain nano-silica.
   USE - Method for preparing nano-silica used in                carbon dioxide adsorbent (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-SiO2 is prepared by using a template                agent as a pore shape control agent and azeotropic                distillation to expand the pores. The preparation                method greatly improves the pore size, specific                surface area and pore volume of nano-SiO2. The CO2                adsorbent is prepared from the nano-SiO2 and                organic amines. The CO2 adsorbent has a faster                adsorption rate and higher adsorption capacity, and                is a very promising adsorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   A nano silica, is prepared by method.
   A carbon dioxide adsorbent, which comprises a                substrate and an active substance supported on the                substrate. The substrate is the nano-SiO2, the                active material is an amine material, and the                loading amount of the active material in the CO2                adsorbent is 10-80 wt.%.
   A method for using carbon dioxide (CO2)                adsorbent, which involves using CO2 adsorbent to                adsorb CO2 in a gas stream of 0.04-100 vol.% CO2 at                30-110&#8451;, where CO2 adsorption capacity of                the CO2 adsorbent is greater than or equal to 250                mg/g, and the CO2 adsorbent is used for 0.04-100                vol.% CO2 gas flow or desorption in argon gas flow                for 10-80 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The surfactant and the                  template agent are added to the water and                  stirring uniformly, then added the catalyst, and                  stirred uniformly at room temperature to obtain                  the mixed solution. The silicon source is added                  to the mixed solution and stirring the reaction                  at room temperature for 12-72 hours to generate                  the orthosilicic acid precipitation, where the                  stirring speed is 400-1000 r/min. The                  orthosilicic acid precipitation is washed with                  water and suction filtered to form a filter cake.                  The organic alcohol solvent is added to the                  filter cake, first distilled at the first                  temperature for 0.5-1 hours, then heated up to                  the second temperature for distillation for 0.5-1                  hours, or, firstly distilled at the third                  temperature for 0.2-0.8 hours, then raised the                  temperature to the first temperature and                  distilled for 0.5-1 hours, and finally raised the                  temperature and distilled at the second                  temperature for 0.5-1 hours. The first                  temperature is greater than or equal to the                  azeotropic point of the organic alcohol solvent                  and water. The second temperature is greater than                  or equal to the boiling point of the organic                  alcohol solvent, and the third temperature is                  greater than or equal to the boiling point of the                  templating agent. Preferred Components: The                  surfactant is linear alkylbenzene sodium                  sulfonate, fatty alcohol sulfate, long-chain                  alkylammonium salt or nonionic surfactant. The                  templating agent includes organics or carbonyl                  compounds containing active &#945;-hydrogen                  atoms. The catalyst includes vanadium phosphate                  oxide, niobic acid, alkali metal or alkaline                  earth metal hydroxide, bicarbonate, carbonate,                  carboxylate or ammonia water, and the silicon                  source is organosilane. Preferred Compositions:                  The molar ratio of the silicon source and                  surfactant is 5:1-30:1. The molar ratio of the                  silicon source to the template agent is                  1:5-1:100. The molar ratio of the silicon source                  and catalyst is 1:1-1:10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alcohol                  solvent includes ethanol, propanol and                  butanol.

PN CN113999170-A
TI New pyridinium based ionic liquid useful as catalyst for cycloaddition reaction of epoxy compounds e.g. oxiran-2-ylmethanol, 2-(allyloxymethyl)oxirane or 2-(chloromethyl)oxirane, and carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonates.
AU ZHANG Z
   REN T
   ZHANG J
   WANG L
   GUO X
AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 2022259205
AB    NOVELTY - Pyridinium based ionic liquids (A) i.e. bis(dimethylamino)methyleneammonium;3-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)propanoate;bromide (I), 3-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)propanoate;2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepin-1-ium;bromide (II) or benzyl(trimethyl)ammonium;3-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)propanoate;bromide (III) are new.
   USE - (A) are useful as catalyst for cycloaddition reaction of epoxy compounds including oxiran-2-ylmethanol, 2-(allyloxymethyl)oxirane, 2-(chloromethyl)oxirane, 2-(phenoxymethyl)oxirane, 2-methyloxirane, 2-phenyloxirane or 7-oxabicyclo(4.1.0)heptane and carbon dioxide to prepare cyclic carbonates (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (A): solves the problems of low catalyst performance, harsh reaction conditions and the use of organic solvents or co-catalysts in the existing methods for synthesizing cyclic carbonates by using carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds; and ensures the obtained cyclic carbonates with high yield of 98.1%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pyridinium based ionic liquids (A) i.e. bis(dimethylamino)methyleneammonium;3-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)propanoate;bromide of formula (I), 3-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)propanoate;2,3,4,6,7,8,9,10-octahydropyrimido(1,2-a)azepin-1-ium;bromide of formula (II) or benzyl(trimethyl)ammonium;3-(4-(dimethylamino)pyridin-1-ium-1-yl)propanoate;bromide of formula (III) are new.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use method, comprising adding (A) and epoxy compound in a mol ratio of 1-9:100 into a reactor, closing the reactor and introducing carbon dioxide, and performing cycloaddition reaction under the conditions of a reaction pressure of 0.1-0.5 MPa and reaction temperature of 30-90degrees Celsius for 1-12 hours to obtain cyclic carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) reacting 4-dimethylaminopyridine with 3-bromopropionic acid to obtain 1-(2-carboxyethyl)-4-(dimethylamino)pyridinium bromide (II), and (ii) reacting (II) with 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene or benzyltrimethylammonium hydroxide at room temperature for 6-18 hours in the presence of solvent, performing rotary evaporation, washing and drying to obtain final product.Preferred Components: The molar ratio of 4-dimethylaminopyridine to 3-bromopropionic acid is 1:1-1.2. The molar ratio between (II) and 1,1,3,3-tetramethylguanidine, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene or benzyltrimethylammonium hydroxide is 1:1-1.2. The solvent is methanol or ethanol. The molar ratio of (A) to epoxy compound is 5-7:100.Preferred Method: In the use method, the cycloaddition reaction is performed at 60-70degrees Celsius for 6-9 hours.

TI System for storing carbon dioxide, has third unit that fixes metal on carbide, and fourth unit adsorbs phosphorus-containing compounds and fifth unit produces fertilizer from carbides on which compound is adsorbed.

   FUKURO S
   KURASAWA H
AE FUJITA KK (FUKG-C)
GA 202112846R
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first unit that is provided for carbonizing biomass to generate carbides and a carbonization gas. A second unit is provided for generating electricity using the carbonization gas. A third unit is provided for fixing the metal on the carbide to the carbide on which the metal is fixed. A fourth unit adsorbs phosphorus-containing compounds. A fifth unit produces fertilizer from the carbides on which the compound is adsorbed. The sixth unit disperses the fertilizer in soil. The biomass is the material containing a lignocellulose. The first unit contains a gasifier provided with a carbonization furnace. The second unit contains the generator connected to a gas engine and gas engine. The third unit is a container configured to contain the salt-containing liquid and the carbides. A reducing device reduces the salt adsorbed on the carbide.
   USE - System for storing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon storage system can contribute to the suppression of global warming by storing carbon dioxide in the atmosphere as carbon in the ground.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for improving water quality. The first biomass is carbonized to produce carbides and a first carbonization gas. The carbides are immersed in a liquid containing a metal salt. The metal salt adsorbed on the carbide is reduced to produce metal-supported carbide. The first biomass is carbonized to produce carbides and a first carbonization gas. The carbide is mixed with a material comprising an element selected from manganese, silicic acid, and boron.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the block diagram of the system for storing carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

AU LIU R

AE LIYANG YIKECHUANG INSULATION MATERIAL (LIYA-Non-standard)
GA 202041602W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas protection welding gun glass fiber insulating piece comprises 50-70 pts. wt. alkali-free glass fiber, 28-35 pts. wt. silicone resin, 1-3 pts. wt. KP-1 cement, 2-5 pts. wt. silica aerogel, 1-4 pts. wt. quartz powder, 2-8 pts. wt. curing agent, 2-8 pts. wt. diluting agent and 2-8 pts. wt. reinforcing agent.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide gas protection welding gun glass fiber insulating piece.
   ADVANTAGE - The pipe fittings: has excellent mechanical properties, excellent high temperature resistance and insulation properties; and is suitable for the use of welding gun nozzle insulation pieces.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the carbon dioxide gas protection welding gun glass fiber insulating piece, comprising weaving alkali-free glass fiber into 10-13 silk fiber glass cloth, mixing silicone resin, KP-1 cement, silica aerogel, quartz powder, curing agent, diluting agent and reinforcing agent at 45 plus minus 5 degrees C, rotating at the speed of 700-800 rotations per minute to obtain a coating, applying glue on the surface of the glass fiber cloth, air drying for 12 plus minus 2 minutes to obtain the prefabricated cloth after 14-16 silk, wrapping the prefabricated cloth on the outer wall of the model pipe, and then wrapping the outermost of the prefabricated cloth with aluminum tape to obtain the prefabricated model pipe, placing the prefabricated model pipe at 260 plus minus 5 degrees C, heating and solidifying for 15 plus minus 1 hours, cooling to 90 plus minus 5 degrees C, removing the aluminum tape and model pipe, and cutting to obtain an insulating pipe fitting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The curing agent comprises 15-23 pts. wt., preferably 16 pts. wt. hexamethyldisilazane and 10-22 pts. wt., preferably 20 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate. The diluting agent comprises 15-20 pts. wt., preferably 17 pts. wt. talc and 20-35 pts. wt., preferably 32 pts. wt. methyl ethyl ketone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The insulating piece comprises 56 pts. wt. alkali-free glass fiber, 30 pts. wt. silicone resin, 2 pts. wt. KP-1 cement, 3 pts. wt. silica aerogel, 2 pts. wt. quartz powder, 5 pts. wt. curing agent, 4 pts. wt. diluting agent and 6 pts. wt. reinforcing agent. Preferred Components: The silicone resin comprises 20-35 pts. wt., preferably 25 pts. wt. methyl phenyl silicone resin, and 26-36 pts. wt., preferably 33 pts. wt. methyl MQ silicone resin. The reinforcing agent comprises 20-36 pts. wt., preferably 26 pts. wt. carbon fiber powder and 15-26 pts. wt., preferably 17 pts. wt. boron nitride. The specific surface area of the silica aerogel is 400-600 m2/g, preferably 470 m2/g and the density of the silica aerogel is 40-60 kg/m, preferably 52 kg/m. The particle size of the quartz powder is 200-300 meshes, preferably 230 meshes.

TI Cadmium complex with three-dimensional porous structure in carbon dioxide chemical fixation, is represented by linear formula, where basic structural unit of cadmium complex comprises one-half of crystallographically independent cadmium ion.
AU HE H
   LI C
   DU M
AE UNIV TIANJIN NORMAL (UTJN-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN NORMAL (UTJN-C)
GA 202000101G
AB    NOVELTY - Cadmium complex with three-dimensional porous structure is represented by linear formula.
   USE - Cadmium complex with three-dimensional porous structure in carbon dioxide chemical fixation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cadmium complex with three-dimensional porous structure is good catalyst, and has rich catalytic site and pore structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cadmium complex with three-dimensional porous structure is represented by (Cd2L(H2O)3)n, where L is deprotonated 3,3',5,5'-tetra (4-carboxyphenyl)-4,4'-biphenyldiamine anion ligand. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing cadmium complex with three-dimensional porous structure, which involves adding 3,3',5,5 -tetra (4-carboxyphenyl)-4,4 -biphenylenediamine and cadmium (II) metal salt and uniformly dispersing at 100-120 degrees C for 10-15 days, naturally reducing to room temperature of 20-25 degrees C to obtain yellow massive single crystal of cadmium complex, where molar ratio of the cadmium (II) metal salt and 3,3',5,5 -tetra (4-carboxyphenyl)-4,4 -biphenylenediamine is (4-4.4):1; and
   (2) a method for using cadmium complex in carbon dioxide chemical fixation, which involves dispersing epichlorohydrin and tetrabutylammonium bromide in a ratio of 1:(0.15-0.20) in acetonitrile, adding the cadmium complex as heterogeneous catalyst under an atmosphere of carbon dioxide, performing reaction at 80-85 degrees C for 24-36 hours, where conversion rate of the epichlorohydrin to the cyclic carbonate is up to 100%, where ratio of cadmium complex to epichlorohydrin is 50:(0.5-1.5), ratio of epichlorohydrin substance to acetonitrile is 1:(2-3), using cadmium complex as a heterogeneous catalyst, still retains the original skeleton structure after being recycled for 3-6 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The basic structural unit of the cadmium complex comprises one-half of crystallographically independent cadmium ion(Cd1), one-quarter of completely deprotonated L4-one quarter crystallographically independent coordinated water molecule H2O (4) and one quarter crystallographically independent coordinated water molecule H2O (3). The cadmium ion is connected to N1, O1, O2, O3, O4, O1' and O2', N1 comes from the first L4-, O1 and O2 come from two oxygens in the same carboxyl group of the second L4-, O1' and O2' are from two oxygens in the same carboxyl group of the third L4-, O3 comes from one water molecule H2O (3) and O4 comes from another water molecule H2O (4). The three-dimensional skeleton of the cadmium complex begins to decompose, and the remaining residue is a metal oxide cadmium oxide. Preferred Condition: The ratio of the amount of the cadmium (II) metal salt to N,N-diethylformamide is (4-4.4):(266-350).

AB    NOVELTY - Electrolysis system comprises a direct current power source (10), an electrolytic cell unit (20), and a heating unit (30) for heating the electrolytic cell unit, where electrolysis cell unit comprises a support layer, an electrolyte layer and a non-support layer. The electrolyte layer includes a first electrolyte layer and a second electrolyte layer, the first electrolyte layer is located on the upper surface of the support layer, and the second electrolyte layer is located on the lower surface of the support layer. The non-support layer includes a first non-support layer and a second non-support layer, the first non-support layer is located on the upper surface of the first electrolyte layer, and the second non-support layer is located on the lower surface of the second electrolyte layer.
   USE - Electrolysis system.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrolysis system is cost effective, has simple operation, safety and high efficiency, in relatively low electrolytic voltage to realize the advantages electrolytic conversion of water and carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for electrolyzing carbon dioxide, which involves:
   (A) using electrolysis system, heating electrolytic cell unit to make the temperature of the electrolytic cell unit reach 600 degrees C or higher;
   (B) contacting first cathode layer and the second cathode layer with the air, introducing mixed gas of carbon dioxide and water into the tunnel from the inlet of the tunnel; and
   (C) turning on the du=irect current power supply, energizing electrolytic cell unit to operate at a certain working voltage, electrolyze carbon dioxide and water, and obtain a synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide and hydrogen at the outlet of the tunnel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of electrolysis system.
   Direct current power source (10)
   Electrolytic cell unit (20)
   Heating unit (30)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The electrolyte material is Scandia-Stabilized Zirconia, Yttria stabilized zirconia, and Lanthanum strontium gallium magnesium oxide. The non-supporting layer material is Lanthanum strontium manganite, and lanthanum Strontium Cobalt Ferrite. Preferred Process: The method involves adjusting the temperature of the electrolytic cell unit, the working solution voltage, and the ratio of molar ratios of carbon dioxide to water in the mixed gas. Preferred Conditions: The temperature ofthe electrolytic cell unit is 600-850 degrees C. The mixed gas has a molar ratio of carbon dioxide to water of not less than 5%.

TI System for producing product gas containing carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen from source gas containing carbon dioxide and/or water, has redox conversion reactor, plasma generator, gas inlet and gas discharge unit.

GA 201983289H
AB    NOVELTY - A system has a redox conversion reactor (110, 110') having a space so as to be filled with a reversible redox converter, a plasma generator (120) capable of generating plasma in an inner space of the redox conversion reactor, a gas inlet (130, 130') for supplying inert gas, carbon dioxide and/or water vapor to an internal space of the redox conversion reactor, and a gas discharge unit (140, 140') configured to discharge the gas including the synthesis gas generated in the conversion/reduction conversion reactor to the outside.
   USE - System is used for producing product gas containing carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen from source gas containing carbon dioxide and/or water (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses plasma as heat source, which enables distributed and modular design in which the installation site can be applied. The plasma saves installation space and cost. The system can be used immediately when the floating power occurs, and surplus power generated by excess production in the power plant, surplus power at night and discontinuously produced renewable energy are used as energy source. In addition, flow current of the system is reduced to reduce the power quality and cost of maintaining efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of producing product gas containing carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen from source gas using a reversible redox converting agent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of system for converting carbon dioxide and water vapor using reversible redox converter. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Redox conversion reactor (110,110')
   Plasma generator (120)
   Heating device (121)
   Gas inlet (130,130')
   Gas discharge unit (140,140')
   Power supply (150)

TI Carbon dioxide (CO2) gas-discharge slab-laser used            for dental operation, has first and second elongated            electrodes which are spaced apart by first and second            elongated ceramic strips, laterally-separated and            parallel to each other.
AU MUELLER E R
   SEGUIN V A
   SHACKLETON C
   MILLER E R
AE COHERENT INC (COHE-C)
   COHERENT INC (COHE-C)
   COHERENT INC (COHE-C)
   COHERENT INC (COHE-C)
GA 201978263T
AB    NOVELTY - The gas-discharge slab-laser (40) has a                laser-housing which include an elongated hollow                extrusion having an interior surface which form a                first elongated electrode. A second elongated                electrode is located within the elongated                extrusion, where the second elongated electrode                spaced-apart and parallel to the first elongated                electrode. The spaced-apart first and second                elongated electrodes define a discharge-gap of the                slab-laser, neither the elongated extrusion nor the                second elongated electrode which include any                fluid-cooling unit. The first and second elongated                electrodes are spaced apart by first and second                elongated ceramic strips, laterally-separated and                parallel to each other. The slab-laser is a carbon                dioxide slab-laser having a carbon dioxide gas                mixture. The elongated ceramic strips are made from                one of a group of ceramics consisting of aluminum                nitride, aluminum oxide, and beryllium oxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide (CO2) gas-discharge slab-laser                used for dental operation.
   ADVANTAGE - The segmented arrangement has the benefits for                making the ceramic strips less expensive to                fabricate and to install within laser and also for                reducing acoustic noise. The cooling-plate is                generally most effective in conductively cooling                the live electrode, because of a relatively short                and unrestricted thermal path from the live                electrode through the ceramic spacers and the                extrusion. The conduit is replaced quickly and                easily. The arrangement prevents contact between                the ceramic spacers and the gas-discharge, so that                avoiding possible erosion of the ceramic spacers by                the gas-discharge.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                CO2 slab-laser.
   Laser (40)
   Gas-tight housing (42)
   Screw hole (50)
   Insulator Strip (64)
   Inductor (70)

PN CN108654686-A; CN108654686-B
TI Catalyst comprises diaminostilbene-disulfonic            acid, dinitrostilbene disulfonic acid or            co-intercalated hydrotalcite photocatalyst, in which            metal cation and hydroxide ion form octahedron by            covalent bond.
AU LU J
   XU S
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 201884837X
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises                4,4'-diaminostilbene-2,2'-disulfonic acid (DAS),                4,4'-dinitrostilbene-2,2'-disulfonic acid (DNS) or                co-intercalated hydrotalcite photocatalyst, in                which a metal cation and a hydroxide ion form a                octahedron by covalent bond. A curled piece is                formed by a common side structure, where anionic                4,4'-diaminostilbene-2,2'-disulfonic acid and                4,4'-dinitrostilbene-2,2'-disulfonic acid are                intercalated between hydrotalcite layers. The                intercalated supramolecular layered composite                material is uniformly dispersed.
   USE - Catalyst for photocatalytic reduction of                carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst realizes transfer of photo-charge                and solves the problem of rapid recombination of                electron-holes separated by semiconductor catalyst                after illumination, where the separated electrons                have enough time to participate in photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide, and shows high                efficiency and reduces carbon dioxide to methanol                with high yields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing catalyst for photocatalytic reduction                of carbon dioxide, which involves:
   (A) preparing a solution having a molar ratio                of a divalent nickel salt to a divalent manganese                salt of 2:1-3:1;
   (B) preparing                4,4'-diaminostilbene-2,2'-disulfonic acid,                4,4'-dinitrostilbene-2,2'-disulfonic acid and                sodium hydroxide mixed aqueous solution;
   (C) adding hydrogen peroxide in the sodium                hydroxide solution;
   (D) adding divalent manganese ions to obtain                solution B;
   (E) mixing the obtained solution A and                solution B in a constant pressure funnel;
   (F) stirring the resulting mixture                continuously for 10-50 minutes; and
   (G) washing the filter cake obtained by                centrifugation to obtain the co-intercalated                hydrotalcite photocatalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic scanning                electron microscopic image of the obtained                photocatalysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal cation is                  nickel (II) or manganese (II). The divalent                  nickel salt is nickel nitrate. The bivalent                  manganese salt is manganese nitrate.

PT P
PN CN108620079-A; CN108620079-B
TI Preparing nickel-based composite catalyst for pressurizing carbon dioxide to reform methane to syn gas, involves using catalyst as silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide and titanium dioxide, and active component is nickel-iron or nickel-cobalt.
AU LIU Z
   XIAO Y
   SONG Y
   LI T
   SHI X
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 201880288U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-based composite catalyst involves using catalyst as silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide and titanium dioxide, and active component is nickel, nickel-iron or nickel-cobalt. The agent is a composite oxide of ceric oxide-lanthanum oxide, ceric oxide-samarium oxide, ceric oxide-praseodymium oxide, ceric oxide-neodymium sesquioxide, and ceric oxide-yttrium oxide. The active component content is 5-15 wt.% based on 100 wt.% mass of catalyst. The content of auxiliary agent is 3-15 wt.%, and rest is a carrier. The catalyst is prepared by using composition of catalyst, active component precursor, auxiliary agent, and carrier precursor which are dissolved in ethanol and added aqueous solution of the combustion-supporting agent, and then stirred at room temperature for 2-4 hours. The solvent is distilled off to obtain viscous liquid, and liquid is heated and burned to obtain solid powder.
   USE - Preparing nickel-based composite catalyst for pressurizing carbon dioxide to reform methane to syn gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to preparing nickel-based composite catalyst which has high methane and carbon dioxideconversion to carbon dioxide reformed methane under pressurized condition and exhibits high activity, high stability and high carbon and anti-sintering ability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The combustion is uniformly polished, and then calcined in an air atmosphere of 550-800 degrees C for 3-6 hours, naturally cooled to room temperature, tableted, granulated, passed through 40-60 mesh sieve to obtain catalyst. The above-mentioned coordination-combustion agent is glycine, alanine, threonine, serine, ethylenediamine, Citric acid, urea, trimesic acid, nicotinic acid, and oxalic acid. The coordination-combustion agent is added in an amount of 0.5-2 times total amount of metal elements and metalloid elements in precursor of active component and carrier precursor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of active component is 10 wt.% based on mass of catalyst, and content of auxiliary agent is 5-10 wt.%. Preferred Condition: The coordination-combustion agent is added in a metal element of precursor of active component carrier precursor. The total molar amount of the metal element or metalloid element is 1.0-1.5 times. The calcination is carried out in an air atmosphere at 650-750 degrees C for 3-6 hours. The ratio of total molar amount of medium metal element is 0.015-0.02:1. Preferred Component: The active component is nickel, and precursor of active component is nickel nitrate or nickel oxalate. The composition is nickel-iron, and active component precursor is a mixture of nickel nitrate and nickel oxalate and ferric nitrate, iron oxalate and ferric ammonium citrate. The active component is nickel-cobalt, and active component precursor is a mixture of nickel nitrate and nickel oxalate and cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetylacetonate and cobalt oxalate. The carrier precursor is methyl silicate, ethylsilicate, propyl silicate, butyl silicate, aluminumisopropoxide, aluminum nitrate, boehmite, tetrabutyl titanate, and isopropyl titanate. The soluble salts of cerium are cerium nitrate, cerium ammonium nitrate, cerium acetate, cerium oxalate, and isopropyl ester. The soluble salt of rare earth element is a nitrate or chloride of rare earth element. Preferred Process: The auxiliary agent is prepared by using sol-gel method soluble salt, and dissolving soluble salt of rare earth element completely in absolute ethanol. The solvent is evaporated to dryness at 40-100 degrees C, and obtained dry gel is baked at 400-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours in an air atmosphere, and naturally cooling to room temperature, it is ground into powder to obtain auxiliary agent, where soluble salt of rare earth element is soluble salt of lanthanum, samarium, praseodymium, neodymium, and yttrium. The coordination-combustionimprover is glycine, alanine, and threonine.

PN US2018287221-A1; US10374230-B2
TI Method for providing electricity to vehicle e.g. car, involves electrolyzing aqueous, ionic solution to provide electricity, and powering vehicle with electricity.
AU BAIRAT A M
AE BAIRAT A M (BAIR-Individual)
GA 201876669Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing an aqueous, ionic solution of carbon dioxide or carbonic acid, or a combination of the two. The source of ions is an acid, an alkali metal salt, an alkaline earth metal salt. The aqueous, ionic solution is electrolyzed to provide electricity. The electrolysis process proceeds by the conversion of the carbon dioxide or carbonic acid to bicarbonate ions and carbonate ions. A vehicle is powered with the electricity. The acid is citric acid or a fruit juice solution. The alkali metal salt is sodium chloride. The graphite is present in the aqueous, ionic solution during electrolysis.
   USE - Method for providing electricity to vehicle (claimed) such as cars, trucks, motorcycles, bicycles, trains, planes, and hybrid car.
   ADVANTAGE - The ionic solution can increase the ionic conductivity of the cell, thus allowing for more efficient ion transfer during electrolyzing process in the electrochemical cell. The sodium chloride is especially suitable for electrolysis process because of low cost and high dissociation constant. The presence of acid can absorb carbon dioxide gas in the solution, allowing for higher carbon dioxide or carbonic acid concentration in the water, further increasing ionic conductivity and thus more voltage output from the electrochemical cell. The electrochemical structures are simple to construct and relatively inexpensive. The material used to make the electrochemical cells can be made of recyclable materials or disposable materials that lower the capital costs of electrochemical cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an electrochemical cell for electrolyzing the aqueous, ionic solution;
   (2) a vehicle; and
   (3) an electrical power station.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrochemical cell.
   Metal mesh sieve (3)
   Electrical circuitry (6)
   Electrical wiring (7,8)
   Electric structure (9)
   Electrochemical cell (11)

TI Catalyst for the decomposition of carbon dioxide plasma, comprises a spherical carrier and a non-noble metal oxide supported on the surface of the spherical carrier.
AU CHEN B
   CAO T
   ZHENG J
   LI X
   ZHANG N
   YE S
   CAO D
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201839503C
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a spherical carrier and a non-noble metal oxide supported on the surface of the spherical carrier, where the spherical carrier comprises \alumina, titanium oxide, 13X molecular sieves and/or silica, the mass of the non-noble metal oxide is 1-40% of the mass of the spherical carrier.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for the decomposition of carbon dioxide plasma (claimed) .
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst reduces the activation barrier of the plasma so as to improve energy in the plasma decomposition process and has the conversion rate of carbon dioxide of 25% and the energy efficiency of 25%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMs are also included for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst, comprising (i) immersing the spherical carrier in a non-precious metal salt solution to obtain a catalyst precursor, impregnating time for 0.5-2 hours, where the quality of the non-noble metal salt solution in the non-precious metal salt solution is based on the mass of the corresponding oxide and the mass of the non-precious metal oxide is 1-40% of the mass of the dried spherical carrier, (ii) drying catalyst precursor obtained in the step (i), and subjecting to a discharge treatment in an argon atmosphere; and
   (2) a catalytic system for the decomposition of carbon dioxide plasma, comprising upper air intake, the columnar cylinder of the lower air outlet and the sieve plate, the anode on the outer surface of the cylindrical cylinder and the ground electrode on the central axis of the column cylinder are characterized in that the column cylinder contains the sieve plate filled, the columnar cylinder is an insulating cylinder and the upper surface of the screen is flush with the bottom of the anode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The distance from the inner wall of the columnar cylinder to the grounding electrode is 3-8 mm and the height of the anode is 50-150 mm. The filling height of the catalyst is 10-100% of the height of the anode. The decomposition of carbon dioxide comprises under discharge conditions, carbon dioxide is passed into the catalytic system and the power of the discharge is 2-20 W.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The non-noble metal is molybdenum, cobalt, iron, copper and/or nickel. The particle diameter of the spherical carrier is 1-5 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the spherical carrier is obtained by drying the spherical carrier material and the drying temperature is 100-120 degrees C and the drying time is 3-5 hours. In the step (ii), the input power of the discharge treatment is 30-90 W and the discharge treatment time is 0.5-11 hours.

AU WILD H
   YARDENI G
   KRYSTAL E
   COHEN R
   KALMANOVICH G
   WILDER H
   COHEN E
AE STRAUSS WATER LTD (STRA-Non-standard)
   STRAUSS WATER SECTOR CO LTD (STRA-Non-standard)
   STRAUSS WATER LTD (STRA-Non-standard)
   STRAUSS WATER LTD (STRA-Non-standard)
   STRAUSS WATER LTD (STRA-Non-standard)
GA 2018222394
AB    (IL248295-A)   NOVELTY - Carbonation unit, system and method (Incomplete specification, abstract based on available information published by the patent office).
   USE - Carbonation unit, system and method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a continuous water carbonation method; and
   (2) a water dispenser.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a water carbonation unit.
   Water carbonation unit (100)
   Mixing chamber (102)
   Lugs (117,119)
   Water feed (128)
   Carbon-dioxide feed (130)
EA    NOVELTY - The unit (100) has a merging duct that is extending between a first closed end and a second end. A water feed (128) and a carbon-dioxide feed (130) opening into duct at first end. The second end opening into a mixing chamber (102) linked to a carbonated water outlet. The carbonated water outlet restricts outflow of carbonated water from the chamber to maintain pressure within the chamber while carbonated water flows out of the carbonated water outlet. A conduit controls the outflow and bubble size of the carbon-dioxide in the carbonated water.
   USE - Water carbonation unit of water dispenser (claimed) used for domestic use.
   ADVANTAGE - The diameters of the terminal segments are configured so as to provide proper relative inflow of two components to ensure proper carbonation. The shear forces flowing through conduit and the pressure maintained within the chamber act in combination to control the carbonation strength. The auxiliary adjustable valves permit fine-tuning of carbon dioxide bubbles' size through adjustment of the pressure differential between the carbonation unit outlet and the dispensing outlet. The adjustable valve is fitted with user-operated lever, which permits the user to adjust the strength of carbonation by operating the handle to change the clearance in valve.

PT P
PN CN107568060-A
TI Radiation breeding low protein and low phosphorus rice variety comprises carbon dioxide laser irradiation, screening low-protein and low phosphorus rice seed, cobalt-60 gamma irradiation, soaking in sodium azide, backcrossing and breeding.
AU ZHANG B
   ZHANG X
AE ANHUI POLIMI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2018063376
AB    NOVELTY - Radiation breeding low protein and low phosphorus rice variety comprises irradiation with carbon dioxide laser, sowing and harvesting M1 generation rice seed, screening, irradiating with cobalt-60 gamma rays, sowing and harvesting M2 rice seeds, continuously screening low protein and low phosphorus rice seed, soaking full grain rice seed in distilled water, soaking in sodium azide phosphate solution, washing with clean water, mutating M3 generation rice seed, hybridizing the parent of the screened low protein and low phosphorus content rice varieties and hybrid rice, backcrossing and breeding.
   USE - The method is useful for radiation breeding low protein and low phosphorus rice variety (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves mutation frequency due to carbon dioxide laser and cobalt-60 gamma rays; and uses sodium azide, which is a respiratory inhibitor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Radiation breeding low protein and low phosphorus rice variety comprises (a) using carbon dioxide laser irradiation for 5-7 seconds at a power density of 10-15 W/cm2, sowing and harvesting M1 generation rice seed (b) screening M1 generation rice seed, selecting low protein and low phosphorus rice seed, irradiating with cobalt-60 gamma rays at a dose of 200-250 Gy for 1-2 hours, sowing and harvesting M2 rice seeds (c) continuously screening low protein and low phosphorus rice seed, soaking full grain rice seed in distilled water for 10-12 hours, taking out, soaking in 2 mmol/l sodium azide phosphate solution for 2-5 hours, washing with clean water for 1 hour after sowing and harvesting rice seed, mutating M3 generation rice seed, detecting and screening low protein and low phosphorus content rice seed and (d) hybridizing the parent of the screened low protein and low phosphorus content rice varieties and hybrid rice, backcrossing and breeding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: The concentration of sodium azide phosphate solution is prepared using 0.04 mol/l phosphoric acid buffer solution.
DC C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. PN FR3118717-A1; WO2022152629-A1; WO2022152629-A9
TI Method for drying first wet gas stream, involves            purifying first stream of water in one of adsorbers to            produce dried gas while regeneration gas is sent to            other of adsorbers.
AU LECLERC M
   PERE F
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202290645R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing possibly                compression of the first gas stream which is wet                (1) up to a first pressure to obtain a first wet                gas stream. The first stream of carbon dioxide is                dried in a unit. The water adsorber of the type                implementing n adsorbers (17A, 17B) is provided.                The admission of first wet gas stream into the                adsorber through first end called inlet end of the                adsorber is provided with circulation of stream in                the adsorber and simultaneous withdrawal through                other end of the adsorber of gas purified into                water is provided. The first stream is purified of                water in one of the adsorbers to produce a dried                gas while the regeneration gas is sent to other of                the adsorbers. The portion of the regeneration gas                is a gas containing at least 90 percent mol of                carbon dioxide produced in a separation apparatus                (33) which separates a gas (31) containing carbon                dioxide and impurity by partial condensation and/or                distillation at a temperature below 0 degree.
   USE - Method for drying first wet gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The water resulting from the regeneration is                partially eliminated together with the condensates.                The latter can be pooled to separate the condensate                from the CO2 gas from the previous compressor                section and the hot gas from the regeneration of                the dryer. The use of a common compressor is                provided to compress the gas rich in carbon dioxide                originating both from a low-temperature separation                device and from device producing moist carbon                dioxide makes it possible to reduce the total                number of rotating machines on the site.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for drying a first wet gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                apparatus for drying a first wet gas stream.
   Wet (1)
   Adsorbers (17A, 17B)
   Gas (31)
   Separation apparatus (33)
   Duration cycle (T)

PN CN217640727-U
TI Rare earth aluminum alloy photovoltaic            flame-retardant cable, has fireproof cavity provided            with sodium bicarbonate powder, where inner part of            shielding layer is provided with multiple leads.
AU HAN P
AE ANHUI FEICHUN SPECIAL CABLE CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2022D4714X
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a rare earth                aluminium alloy photovoltaic flame-retardant cable,                relating to the technical field of photovoltaic                cable, comprising a first lead assembly and a                second lead assembly, one side of the second lead                assembly is provided with a second lead assembly,                the fireproof cavity is internally provided with                sodium bicarbonate powder, after the wearable layer                and the waterproof layer are burned, sodium                bicarbonate powder is heated and decomposed to                generate carbon dioxide, the carbon dioxide gas                layer is generated around the lead, reducing the                ambient air oxygen concentration, so as to reach                the effective flame-retardant effect, in addition,                sodium bicarbonate decomposition product sodium                carbonate is fixed granular, forming a barrier                shell in the fireproof cavity, it can protect the                lead, the carbon dioxide gas generated by sodium                bicarbonate enters into the metal box through the                guide pipe, so as to push the first to two sides,                the second piston moves, so as to increase the                distance between two lead components, avoiding one                lead component after burning wave and the other                lead component, effectively reducing the damage                caused by fire.

PN KR2447035-B1
TI Fumigation device for use with electric heater            type vaporizer, has distribution valve connected to            reservoir, in which fumigant and carbon dioxide in            liquid state are stored together with pipe, where            electric heater units are provided.
AU IL K
AE JEIL GAS CO LTD (JEIL-Non-standard)
   TOP FRESH CO LTD (TOPF-Non-standard)
GA 2022C4282R
AB    NOVELTY - The fumigation device has a distribution valve                (140) connected to a reservoir in which a fumigant                and carbon dioxide are stored together with a pipe.                The distribution valve distributes the fumigant in                a liquid state and the carbon dioxide in the liquid                state from the reservoir. Electric heater units                (156) directly heat and vaporize the distribution                valve. Multiple distribution pipes (141) are                provided between the electric heater units. A                combined valve (160) is provided for collecting and                discharging the vaporized fumigant. A quick coupler                (110) is installed in the pipe between the                reservoir and the distribution valves.
   USE - The fumigation device is useful with electric                heater type vaporizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can simply and efficiently produce                fumigation gas without using a heating medium such                as water or oil, so that safety accidents can be                prevented while simplifying an entire facility. The                device simply performs fumigation without                separately installing a liquid fumigator and a                gaseous inert gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the fumigation device.
   110Quick coupler
   140Distribution valve
   141Distribution pipe
   156Electric heater
   160Combined valve

PN CN216987001-U

PN IN201921016511-A
TI Making host cells for making biological compounds            by inoculating host cells in media, incubating host            cells in carbon dioxide incubator, adding at least one            stress inducer to first incubated host cells and            sub-culturing modified cell lines.
AU MEHRA S
   CHANDRAWANSHI V
   GUPTA S
AE INDIAN INST TECHNOLOGY BOMBAY (IITB-C)
   INDIA MIN SCI & TECHNOLOGY DEPT BIOTECHN (DBIO-C)
GA 202268736U
AB    NOVELTY - Producing host cells for producing biological                compounds, comprises: inoculating a pre-determined                amount of host cells in a pre-determined amount of                media for 72-96 hours, where the media has a pH of                7-7.2; incubating the host cells in a carbon                dioxide incubator to obtain first incubated host                cells, where the carbon dioxide concentration is                5-8%, the temperature is 36.9-37.1&#176; C and                relative humidity is 85-90%; adding in                pre-determined amounts of at least one stress                inducer to the first incubated host cells followed                by further incubation for 24-96 hours to obtain                modified cell lines; and sub-culturing the modified                cell lines for a pre-determined period in                increasing amounts of the stress inducer to obtain                host cells exhibiting enhanced productivity of                desired biological compounds.
   USE - The process is useful for producing host cells                for producing biological compounds.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides host cells which exhibit                enhanced productivity of desired biological                compounds; and enhances the ability of the host                cells to produce the biological compounds.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The first incubated host                  cells are incubated for at least 24 hours. The                  modified cell lines are sub-cultured: till the                  viability increases to greater than 70%; for a                  time period of 3-5 months; till a passage number                  of 25-45 at a similar viable inoculation cell                  density is obtained; till the cell density is                  similar to inoculation cell density; and/or till                  the titers/qP increase by at least a factor of                  2-fold. Preferred Components: The media is a                  serum and protein free media including CD-Chinese                  hamster ovary (CHO), PF-CHO or CHOSFM2. The host                  cells are mammalian cells including recombinant                  CHO cells derived from CHO-DG44 or CHO-K1. The                  stress inducer is an endoplasmic reticulum                  (ER)-stress inducer including tunicamycin,                  brefeldin A or thapsigargin. The increasing                  amount of the stress inducer is 30-500 ng/ml. The                  biological compounds are monoclonal antibodies                  including immunoglobulin G and other recombinant                  glycosylated protein drugs. The host cell attains                  an increased yield of 1.5-2-folds in the                  productivity of the desired biological                  compound.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of adaptation of                recombinant CHO cells in presence of tunicamycin                and comparison of growth and production                characteristics of modified cells in Batch culture                that do not support the claims.

TI Preparing super durable material has mixing ground            cement, graded fine sand, ultra-fine ash and additives,            adding water, stirring, curing preform, vacuum            degassing, dehydrating, introducing liquid carbon            dioxide, taking out preform, polishing surface and            spraying wear-resistant material.

   XI J

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing super durable material comprises                mixing 35-57% ground cement, 15-45% graded fine                sand, 15-45% ultra-fine ash and 0.3-1.5% additives,                adding water, stirring to obtain a mortar, making                the mortar into perform, steam curing the preform,                vacuum degassing and dehydrating in a carbonization                kettle, introducing gaseous and liquid carbon                dioxide into a carbonization kettle, controlling                the pressure and temperature so that the carbon                dioxide entering the carbonization kettle is in a                supercritical state, maintaining at a constant                temperature for 2-200 minutes, taking out the                preform from the carbonization kettle, polishing                surface and spraying anti-corrosion and                wear-resistant material on the surface of the                preform.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing super                durable material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method greatly improves the strength and                durability of high-performance cement-based                materials; can realize large-scale substitution of                plates, pipes and profiles made of metals e.g.                steel and aluminum and has the durability of                ceramic materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The fiber material is                  mixed into the raw material and the amount of the                  fiber material in the total raw material is less                  than 12%. The fiber material comprises steel                  fiber, basalt fiber and/or ceramic fiber. The                  part of ground cement and part of the ultra-fine                  ash in the raw materials are replaced with meta                  kaolin in the same amount. The fineness of meta                  kaolin is greater than 800 mesh and the amount of                  meta kaolin in the total raw materials is 5-35%.                  The raw materials are mixed with graphite,                  graphene or carbon nanotubes and the ratio of                  graphite, graphene or carbon nanotubes in the                  total raw materials is 0.03-15%. The specific                  surface area of ground cement is 380-780 m2/kg                  and the sieve residue of 80 microm fineness is                  less than 7%. The fineness of the graded fine                  sand is 0.1-0.8 mm, the apparent density is                  greater than 2400 kg/m3, the bulk density is                  greater than 1200 kg/m3, the crushing value is                  less than 6% and the mud content is less than                  0.1%. The graded fine sand comprises quartz sand,                  basalt machine-made sand, gold tailings and/or                  iron tailings. The ultra-fine ash is obtained by                  mixing slag and steel slag powder with silica                  fume in a ratio of (0.3-3):1 and the specific                  surface area of slag and steel slag powder is                  greater than 4500 cm2/g. The additive is a                  high-efficiency and early-strength water-reducing                  agent.Preferred Method: The method comprises                  adding styrene while passing gaseous and liquid                  carbon dioxide into the carbonization kettle and                  the amount of styrene in the total raw material                  is 0.5-6.5%. The method comprises adding ethanol                  or methanol when introducing gaseous and liquid                  carbon dioxide into the carbonization kettle,                  where the amount of ethanol mixed in liquid                  carbon dioxide is less than 8% and the amount of                  methanol mixed in liquid carbon dioxide is less                  than 6%. The method comprises coating zirconium                  oxide, aluminum oxide or silicon carbide material                  on the surface of the preform by physical vapor                  deposition, chemical vapor deposition or                  magnetron sputtering and the thickness of the                  coating layer is greater than 1 microm.

PN CN113546659-A; CN113546659-B
TI Preparing highly dispersed cerium-carbon            nitride-urea-nitrogen material by coordination process            used in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide,            involves putting solid cerium nitrate hexahydrate and            urea into absolute ethanol.
AU ZHU C
   WEI X
   ZOU W
   PU Y
   LI W
   DONG L
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
GA 2021C3764W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing highly dispersed cerium-carbon                nitride-urea-nitrogen material by coordination                process involves putting solid cerium nitrate                hexahydrate and urea into absolute ethanol to                obtain first dispersing and second dispersion                respectively dissolving completely, dropping slowly                the obtained first dispersion into the obtained                second dispersion, stirring to obtain the third                solution, putting the solid graphene carbon nitride                (g-C3N4) into absolute ethanol, stirring uniformly                to obtain fourth dispersion, dropping slowly the                obtained third solution into the obtained fourth                dispersion, heating in a water bath, stirring to                obtain a solid, putting the obtained solid in a                porcelain boat, placing the porcelain boat in a                tube furnace, calcining in a nitrogen (N2)                atmosphere, and cooling to room temperature after                completion to obtain a catalyst cerium-carbon                nitride-urea-nitrogen (CeCN-urea-N2).
   USE - Method for preparing highly dispersed                cerium-carbon nitride-urea-nitrogen material by                coordination process used in photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and the raw material is                cheap. The prepared material has strong                practicability and high environmental stability,                and has potential application prospect in the                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of solid                  graphene-carbon nitride (g-C3N4) involves putting                  the urea in the muffle furnace, heating up to                  550-600degrees Celsius, and calcining for 4-6                  hours to obtain g-C3N4 and the heating rate is                  2-3degrees Celsius/minutes. Preferred Conditions:                  The concentration of the first dispersion is 1-4                  g/L, second dispersion is 3-10 g/L, and the                  fourth dispersion is 17-20 g/L. The ultrasonic                  dispersion time for preparing the dispersion                  solution is 0.5-1 hours. The mass ratio of solid                  cerium nitrate hexahydrate, urea and solid g-C3N4                  is 1:1-2:4-6. The obtained third solution is                  dropping slowly into the obtained fourth                  dispersion, heating in a water bath at                  temperature of 100degrees Celsius. The obtained                  solid is placed in a porcelain boat, and                  calcining at temperature is 450-550degrees                  Celsius in nitrogen atmosphere (N2), the gas flow                  rate of nitrogen is 150-250 mL/minutes.

AB    NOVELTY - Processing fermentation exhaust gas and                wastewater by using active microalgae coupling                involves capturing the carbon dioxide waste gas                discharged from the fermentation to prepare the                first solution. The active microalgae are                inoculated in the microalgae culture solution, then                the carbon dioxide is introduced to culture to                obtain microalgae.
   USE - Method for processing fermentation exhaust gas                and wastewater by using active microalgae.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses active microalgae coupling                process fermentation discharge waste gas and waste                water. The microalgae absorbs the nutrient                substance in the fermentation wastewater, it can                purify water quality, it can realize zero discharge                of wastewater and waste gas in the fermentation                industrial production process, at the same time, it                reduces the production cost of the microalgae,                obtaining high-value microalgae liquid or                microalgae powder, realizing energy saving and                emission reduction green production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Condition: The chemical oxygen                  demand is 3-60 grams/liter (g/L)/L, solid                  suspended matter 2-30 g/L, total nitrogen 40-900                  milligrams (mg)/L, ammonia nitrogen 20-500 mg/L,                  total phosphorus 20-400 mg/L. In the second                  solution, the chemical oxygen demand is 70-400                  mg/L, the solid suspension is 2-50 mg/L, the                  total nitrogen is 4-45 mg/L, ammonia nitrogen                  1-35 mg/L, the total phosphorus is 1-10 mg/L. In                  the microalgae culture solution, carbon (C):                  nitrogen (N): phosphorus (P)= 1:(2-30):1. The                  adding amount of glucose is 0-30 g/L. The source of the                  fermentation wastewater comprises but is not                  limited to the alcohol fermentation industry,                  amino acid fermentation industry, citric acid                  fermentation industry, vitamin fermentation                  industry. The nutrient substance comprises                  glucose and/or inorganic salt. The floating                  Chlorella is preserved in the China General                  Microbiological Culture Collection Center                  (CGMCC), the preservation date is July 27, 2020;                  the preservation number is CGMCC No. 20291. The                  active microalgae comprise but are not limited to                  common Chlorella, floating Chlorella,                  protein-nucleic Chlorella, Oocystis algae, one or                  more of the Scenedesmus. Preferred Composition:                  The inorganic salt comprises but not limited to                  one or more of 75 mg/L magnesium sulfate                  hexahydrate (MgSO4x 7H2O), 36 mg/L calcium                  chloride dihydrate (CaCl2-2H2O), 6 mg/L ferric                  ammonium citrate, 6 mg/L citric acid, 1 mg/L                  ethylene diammine tetracetate (EDTA), 1                  milliliter (mL)/L A5 microelement solution.
