PT P
PN CN111173480-A; CN111173480-B
TI Mining natural gas hydrate involves drilling into            natural gas hydrate reservoir, forming horizontal,            which is fractured with fresh water and sand, the            fracturing pressure is set according to formation            characteristics.
AU SI N
   WANG M
   JIANG H
   YE H
   DENG H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC RES INST PETROLEUM ENG (SNPC-C)
GA 2020453324
AB    NOVELTY - Mining natural gas hydrate involves drilling                into the natural gas hydrate reservoir, forming                horizontal, which is fractured with fresh water and                sand. The fracturing pressure is set according to                the formation characteristics, and the fracture                exceeds the unstable area of the well wall to form                a channel, intermittently injected carbon dioxide                gas into the well to allow carbon dioxide to be                replaced in the reservoir, especially the fractures                formed by fracturing, to form carbon dioxide                hydrates, to limit the continued decomposition of                natural gas hydrates in fractures and channels                according monitored real-time gas production                decline, when no more gas is replaced, stop the                injection of carbondioxide, used continuous tubing                water jets to extend cracks or channels, installed                a flow control device in the production string of                the horizontal well section, and used pressure                reduction method to control the wellhead pressure                and the flow of each flow control device to extract                natural gas hydrate.
   USE - Method for mining natural gas hydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to min natural gas hydrate                ensures single crack natural gas hydrate slowly                production, and avoids the sand and stratum                sedimentation problem.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The fracturing                  pressure is greater than the formation fracturing                  pressure. The intermittent injection of carbon                  dioxide after continuous injection of carbon                  dioxide gas for a period of time, the injection                  is stopped when the wellhead pressure shows an                  increase after the pressure gradually decreases                  and becomes stable, injected carbondioxide gas                  again and repeat injection multiple times. The                  carbon dioxide gas is injected again, the                  wellhead pressure shows an increase, and the                  pressure no longer drops after stopping the                  injection of CO2 gas, indicating no the gas is                  replaced and the CO2 injection is stopped. The                  injection pressure is 20-150 MPa, and the                  flow-back speed is 0.5 m/s. The flow control                  device is installed at the crack. The flow rate                  of the flow device is controlled to ensure that                  the wellhead pressure is equal to the formation                  pressure during gas production and avoids sand                  formation.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

AU SONG Z

   HUANG G
   HUANG S
   PAN X
AE GUANGDONG JIANYU BUILDING TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202045909M
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the sewage treatment field, claims a prefabricated septic tank of explosion-proof, comprising a water inlet and a shell, the surface of one end of the shell is provided with a water inlet, the other end surface of the shell is provided with a water outlet, the utility model has outer shell, inner shell, a pressure gauge, a pressure sensor and an alarm lamp shell, solves the problem that the shell broken leakage is difficult to be found, cause septic tank near the methane content is raised, when fire source is easy to explode, personnel and property loss problem, the inner shell is set in outer shell, the middle is provided with a carbon dioxide, such that the inner shell leakage will cause carbon dioxide gas pressure of the middle change, pressure detector detecting the pressure change, activate an alarm, it can remind the worker to replace the septic tank, at the same time, carbon dioxide is an excellent flame retardant, and methane mixing can reduce methane concentration, at the same time, isolating air, preventing the biogas flame explosion.

TI SYNTHETIC GAS PRODUCTION METHOD. Invention relates to high-temperature catalytic oxidative methods of converting methane to synthesis gas and can be used in chemical engineering.
AU DEDOV A G
   LOKTEV A S
   VOLOSHIN YA Z
   BUZNIK V M
   SANDZHIEVA D A
AE UNIV GUBKIN RUSSIAN STATE OIL & GAS (UYGU-Non-standard)
GA 202034377L
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to high-temperature catalytic oxidative methods of converting methane to synthesis gas and can be used in chemical engineering. Method is implemented by feeding into reactor, in which catalytic system is placed, of initial gas mixture containing mixture of methane and carbon dioxide with volume ratio of methane:carbon dioxide equal to 0.971.1:1, and carrying out the process at temperature of 700950C and atmospheric pressure, wherein the catalytic system used is a macrocyclic ruthenium complex Ru(CwGm)3 (Bn-C4H9)2, where CwGm is a chelating bis-2-oxyphenoxyl gliocimate group and Bn-C4H9 is a cross-linking n-butyl-borate group supported on a heat-resistant, highly porous ceramic support in amount of 5.0% of the weight of the carrier.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result consists in provision of effective immobilisation of monolayers of used catalytic system on common surface of highly porous carrier, as well as in process of carbon dioxide conversion of methane without additional supply of inert gases and at atmospheric pressure.

TI System for manufacturing protein comprises methane production unit which manufactures methane by reaction of hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and protein manufacture unit which uses methane assimilation microbe and manufactures protein.
AU FUKUDA I
   FUJIMINE T
AE TOKYO GAS CO LTD (TOLG-C)
GA 2020066086
AB    NOVELTY - A protein manufacturing system (1) comprises electrolysis unit (10) which electrolyzes water and manufactures hydrogen and oxygen, methane production unit (20) which makes hydrogen and carbon dioxide react and to manufacture methane, protein manufacture unit (30) which uses methane assimilation microbe and to manufacture protein based on the methane, and supply unit (33) which supplies the oxygen manufactured by electrolysis unit, and methane manufactured by methane production unit to protein manufacture unit.
   USE - A system for manufacturing protein (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Protein can be manufactured in maintainable state without using limited resources. Emission of global warming gases is suppressed and environmental load is small.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing protein which involves electrolyzing water and manufacturing hydrogen and oxygen, making hydrogen and carbon dioxide react to manufacture methane, supplying oxygen manufactured by electrolysis process and methane manufactured by methane production process to methane assimilation microbe and manufacturing protein.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an explanatory view of the whole structure of protein manufacturing system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Protein manufacturing system (1)
   Electrolysis unit (10)
   Methane production unit (20)
   Protein manufacture unit (30)
   Supply unit (33)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The carbon dioxide used in methane production unit contains one discharged by methane assimilation microbe. The methane production unit manufactures water with methane, and the manufactured water is supplied to protein manufacture unit by a mixed state with methane. The protein manufacture unit is held in a predetermined temperature range using the heat generated by methane production unit. Preferred Devices: The protein manufacturing system further includes drying unit which dries protein manufactured by protein manufacture unit and dries using the heat generated by methane production unit. The drying unit further uses the heat generated using oxygen manufactured by electrolysis unit or by using heat generated by burning natural gas using oxygen manufactured by electrolysis unit. Electrolysis unit electrolyzes water using the electric power produced with renewable energy electric power generating apparatus. The system further includes control unit which combines amount of protein manufactured in protein manufacture unit, and controls electrolysis unit, methane production unit and supply unit.

TI Manufacture of carbonate ester used as e.g. gasoline additive, involves reacting monohydric alcohol and carbon dioxide in presence of catalyst structure comprising base material and catalyst layer, and wettable powder.
AU KIMIHITO S
   YOUSUKE S
   HONGYU L
   HIDEFUMI H
   YUZURU K
   KENTARO M
   ASUKA O
   SUZUKI K
   SHINKAI Y
   LIU H
   HARADA H
   KATO Y
   MORITA K
   OHASHI A
AE NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   NIPPON STEEL ENG KK (YAWA-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   NIPPON STEEL ENG KK (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   NIPPON STEEL ENG KK (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL ENG CO LTD (YAWA-C)
   NIPPON STEEL ENG KK (YAWA-C)
   MITSUBISHI GAS CHEM CO INC (MITN-C)
   NIPPON STEEL CORP (YAWA-C)
GA 202006530R
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of carbonate ester involves reacting monohydric alcohol and carbon dioxide in the presence of a catalyst structure and a wettable powder. The catalyst structure comprises a base material and a catalyst layer comprising solid catalyst and an inorganic binder formed on at least a portion of the surface of the substrate. The solid catalyst comprises cerium oxide in a supported amount of 15-200 g/m2. The inorganic binder comprises silica and/or alumina.
   USE - Manufacture of carbonate ester. Uses include but are not limited to gasoline additive, alkylating agent, pharmaceutical and agrochemical additives, diesel fuel additives, electrolyte solution of lithium cells, carbonylation agent, solvent, lubricating oil raw material, and deoxidation agent raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of carbonate ester having excellent efficiency, and suppressed powdering and desorption of the solid catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) catalyst structure; and
   (2) carbonate ester manufacturing apparatus, which consists of a supply path for supplying at least a monohydric alcohol and carbon dioxide to the catalyst structure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic binder preferably comprises silica. The catalyst structure further comprises an intermediate layer between the catalyst layer and the base material. The base material comprises a communication hole as a flow path of the monohydric alcohol and the carbon dioxide, and the catalyst layer is formed on a surface of the communication hole. The solid catalyst further comprises an oxide of a rare-earth element or an oxide of lanthanum. The base material comprises a metal honeycomb. The wettable powder comprises a nitrile compound or 2-cyanopyridine. Preferred Composition: The supported amount of solid catalyst is 15-150 g/m2, preferably 15-70 g/m2. Preferred Properties: The catalyst layer has an average thickness of 12-180 mu m. Preferred Apparatus: The apparatus further consists of a discharge path through which a carbonate produced from monohydric alcohol and carbon dioxide passes, catalyst structures arranged in series, and a catalyst structure having an integrated structure in which the catalyst structures are arranged in parallel. Preferred Condition: The apparatus circulates the reaction substrate containing the monohydric alcohol and carbon dioxide at a space velocity/minute of 0.005-5000.

AE UNIV ZACHODNIOPOMORSKI TECHNOLOGICZNY (UZAC-C)
GA 201913996T

PN CN108997241-A
TI New carbon dioxide-responsive N-prop-2-ynyl-1,3,5-triazin-2-amine compound useful for Click reaction.
AU YIN H
   FENG Y
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2018A3580S
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-responsive N-prop-2-ynyl-1,3,5-triazin-2-amine compound (I) is new.
   USE - The new compound (I) is useful for the Click reaction (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide-responsive N-prop-2-ynyl-1,3,5-triazin-2-amine compound of formula (I) is new.
   a, b = 1-5.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the compound (I), which involves: (1) adding cyanuric chloride and deionized water to a reaction vessel, and stirring in an ice water bath until the mixture is uniformly dispersed; (2) dripping N,N-dimethylalkyldiamine to the reaction solution obtained in the step (1), heating at 58-65 degrees C after the dripping is completed, and continuously reacting for 4-8 hours; (3) dripping propargylamine to the reaction solution obtained in the step (2), gradually heating at 80-95 degrees C, and continuously reacting for 4-8 hours; (4) adding sodium hydroxide solution to the reaction solution obtained in the step (3), and continuously reacting for 0.5-1 hour; and (5) removing the solvent from the reaction solution obtained in the step (4) and purifying the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The purification method in the step (5) involves: (a) dissolving the product in dichloromethane after removing the solvent from the reaction solution obtained in the step (4), collecting the solution by filtration, drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate for 12-24 hours and removing the solvent to obtain a crude product; and (b) dissolving the obtained crude product in dichloromethane, then adding petroleum ether to shake well, leaving still the mixture for separation, collecting dichloromethane layer, repeatedly adding petroleum ether, then removing the solvent and freeze-drying for 12-24 hours. Preferred Components: The mass ratio of cyanuric chloride to deionized water in the step (1) is 1:(8-20). The N,N-dimethylalkyldiamine in the step (2) is of formula (II). The molar ratio of propargylamine added in the step (3) to the cyanuric chloride in the step (1) is (1.02-1.10):1. The molar ratio of sodium hydroxide added in the step (4) to the cyanuric chloride in the step (1) is 3:1.c = 1-5.

PN CN108840360-A; CN108840360-B
TI Preparing low alkalinity nano calcium carbonate includes carbonizing, adding a crystal modifier, introducing carbon dioxide, adding auxiliary additives, surface modifying, pressure filtration, dehydrating, depolymerizing and sieving.
AU YANG H
   YANG J
AE ANHUI YANGLIUQING CALCIUM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
   ANHUI YANGLIUQING CALCIUM TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 201894797R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low alkalinity nano calcium carbonate comprises (a) carrying out carbonization by introducing carbon dioxide into the calcium hydroxide suspension to control the temperature, and simultaneously adding a crystal modifier until the pH of the slurry is not less than 7 to obtain a nano calcium carbonate slurry, (b) allowing to stand the prepared nano calcium carbonate slurry until the pH remains stable, (c) introducing carbon dioxide into the aged nano-calcium carbonate slurry, simultaneously adding the auxiliary additives and stirring, (d) carrying out surface modification by adding a surface coating agent to the slurry obtained in the step (c) and stirring, and (e) subjecting the surface-treated nano-calcium carbonate slurry to pressure filtration and centrifugal dehydration, drying, depolymerizing and sieving.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing low alkalinity nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The nano calcium carbonate: has stable low alkalinity, has good uniform diameter, has less diameter ratio span, has high purity crystal form; has good reinforcing properties as a filler; has high tensile strength and tensile elongation strength; and reduces the rate of tensile deformation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal modifier in the step (a) comprises potassium hydrogen sulfate, disodium hydrogen phosphate and sodium citrate at a mass ratio of 1-4:2-4:4-8. The auxiliary additive in the step (c) is a long-chain hydrocarbon ammonium salt. The long-chain hydrocarbon ammonium salt is dodecyl dimethyl benzyl ammonium chloride, dioctadecyldimethylammonium bromide or dimethyl octadecyl hydroxyethyl ammonium-nitrate. The surface coating agent is linoleic acid, arachidonic acid or 2-propenylacrylic acid. Preferred Method: The 0.1-1.5% crystal modifier in the step (a) is added to the mass of the nano calcium carbonate slurry. The carbonizing in the step (a) is carried out at 45-65 degrees C.

   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM (UYPE-C)

   UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES BEIJING (UYGJ-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)

GA 201867336K
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide (CO2)-sensitive                fracturing-discharging system comprises base liquid                comprising surfactant, auxiliary agent, and                water.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-sensitive                fracturing-discharging system used for development                of tight reservoirs.
   ADVANTAGE - The system does not return to the tank after                completion of fracturing construction, and the well                is drained after period of time to improve the                recovery rate of tight oil. The system utilizes                one-use multi-purpose function of sand-carrying,                realizes expansion of tightness of tight oil,                improves tight oil, reduces costs, and achieves the                goal of unified, efficient and green development of                oil and gas fields.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of the CO2-sensitive                fracturing-discharging system comprising                compounding surfactant and auxiliary agent to form                low viscosity base liquid, introducing CO2 into                base liquid to form worm micelles on microscopic                surface, increasing macroscopic viscosity of                system, and thickening system; and
   (2) applying fracturing-discharging system                comprising injecting fracturing-discharging system                into ground for fracturing, fracturing, breaking,                suffocating well, and returning glue to discharge,                where the breaker is hydrocarbon breaker or/and                alcohol breaker.
   The hydrocarbon breaker is kerosene, crude oil                or long-chain alkane. The alcohol breaker is                pentanol, hexanol or isopropanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base liquid                  comprises 1-15 wt.% surfactant, 0.1-10 wt.%                  auxiliary agent, and 10-70 wt.% liquid and/or                  supercritical CO2. The base liquid is nonionic                  base fluid containing CO2-sensitive nonionic                  surfactant, adjuvant and water; anionic base                  fluid containing anionic surfactant, auxiliary                  agent and water; and Zwitterionic base liquid                  containing zwitterionic surfactants, auxiliaries                  and water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The CO2-sensitive                  nonionic surfactant is long-chain amine-based                  surfactant, long chain sulfhydryl surfactant                  or/and long chain sulfhydryl surfactant. The                  long-chain amine-based surfactant is long-chain                  alkyl amide alkyl dimethylamine, oleic acid amide                  alkyl dimethylamine or erucamide alkyl                  dimethylamine. The long-chain thiol-based                  surfactant is N'-long-chain                  alkyl-N,N-dimethylacetamidine or N,N'-dual                  long-chain alkylethyl hydrazine. The long chain                  sulfhydryl surfactant is long chain alkyl                  tetramethyl hydrazine. The anionic surfactant is                  long chain carboxylate, long sulfate salt, or/and                  long chain alkyl sulfonate. The long-chain                  carboxylate is sodium oleate, sodium linoleate,                  sodium long-chain alkyl carboxylate or sodium                  fatty acid polyoxyethylene ether carboxylate. The                  long sulfate salt is long-chain sodium alkyl                  sulfate, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether                  sulfate or long-chain alkyl alcohol ether                  sulfate. The long chain alkyl sulfonate is sodium                  long chain alkyl sulfonate, sodium long chain                  alkyl benzene sulfonate or sodium fatty acid                  methyl sulfonate. The zwitterionic surfactant is                  sulfobetaine or/and carboxybetaine. The                  sulfobetaine is fatty acid amide hydroxypropyl                  sulfobetaine, long-chain alkyl sulfobetaine or                  fatty acid amide propyl sulfobetaine. The                  carboxy-caline is long-chain alkyl betaine or                  fatty acid amide betaine. The auxiliary agent is                  counter ion co-surfactant, liquid/supercritical                  CO2 emulsifier or/and other additives. The                  auxiliary agent is non-ionic base liquid. The                  counter-ionic co-surfactant is anion                  co-surfactant, preferably sodium salicylate,                  sodium p-toluenesulfonate, sodium lauryl sulfate,                  sodium dodecyl sulfate, sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate, sodium maleate, sodium                  benzoate or/and potassium terephthalate. The                  counter ion cosurfactant is CO2-sensitive                  nonionic cosurfactant, preferably                  N,N,N',N'-tetramethyl-1,3-propanamine,                  3-diethylaminopropylamine,                  N,N-dimethyl-p-phenylenediamine,                  tetramethyl-ethylenediamine,                  bis(2-hydroxypropyl)amine,                  N-(2-hydroxyethyl)ethyl diamine, triethanolamine,                  diisopropanolamine, 1,4-butanediamine,                  isopropanolamine, di-n-propylamine,                  diethanolamine, N,N-dimethylethanolamine or/and                  N-methyldiethanolamine. The liquid or/and                  supercritical CO2 emulsifier is sodium                  bis(2-ethylhexyl)succinate sulfonate, TX-100                  (RTM: nonionic surfactant), and/or fluorocarbon                  surfactant.

PN CN108237376-A
TI Method for processing ultra-long carbon dioxide cracking tube for blasting, involves welding spiral joint between two connecting steel pipes firmly by welding.
AU TANG Y
   GAO G
AE SHANDONG POLYTECHNIC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201853977D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using alloy steel bar as raw material, forging and punching, to make three steel pipes according to the required size. The outer surface of the tube, the end surface and the inner hole of the three steel tubes are machined to achieve the accuracy requirement. One of the steel pipes is used as the middle section for the external thread processing and the weld joint processing at the two ends, and the other two steel pipes are used as the left and right ends. The stepped holes are machined to the corresponding precision. The left and right sections and the middle section of the steel pipe are screwed to form a long pipe. The spiral joint between the two connecting steel pipes is welded firmly by welding.

   ADVANTAGE - The method for processing an ultra-long carbon dioxide cracking tube for blasting is simple and convenient, reduces processing requirements on machining equipment, machining fixtures and tools, improves the tightness and strength of the connection, effectively improves production efficiency, and satisfies product quality requirements.

PN CN108164497-A; CN108164497-B
TI Green synthesis of propylene carbonate comprises e.g. mixing the quaternary ammonium salt and polyethylene glycol, reacting and cooling stirring and introducing carbon dioxide.
AU LV H
   WU K
   DENG C
   REN W
AE UNIV YANTAI (UNYT-C)
   UNIV YANTAI (UNYT-C)
GA 201849203R
AB    NOVELTY - Green synthesis of propylene carbonate comprises (i) mixing the quaternary ammonium salt and polyethylene glycol in a molar ratio of 1:2, stirring at room temperature and heating to 80 degrees C, reacting for 1 hour and cooling to room temperature to obtain colorless transparent liquid of quaternary ammonium salt-polyethylene glycol low eutectic, where the quaternary ammonium salt is choline chloride or tetrabutylammonium chloride and the polyethylene glycol is PEG200, PEG400, PEG600, PEG1000, and/or ethylene glycol (EG), and (ii) adding the quaternary ammonium salt-polyethylene glycol low eutectic as a catalyst and placing the reaction medium in an autoclave, adding the propylene oxide is added, stirring, introducing carbon dioxide, reacting at 100-160 degrees C for 1-6 hours to obtain propylene carbonate, where the molar ratio of the quaternary ammonium salt-polyethylene glycol low eutectic to propylene oxide is 1.5:71, and the carbon dioxide pressure during the reaction is 0.4-1.5 Mpa.
   USE - The method is useful for green synthesis of propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple to perform, environmentally friendly and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (ii), comnprises heating the product after the reaction to temperature of 130 degrees C for 4-8 hours to distill out propylene carbonate and subjecting separated quaternary ammonium salt-polyethylene glycol eutectic to the steps of (ii), where the same operation was carried out by adding propylene oxide, passing carbon-dioxide to carry out repeated reaction to prepare propylene carbonate and recycling the quaternary ammonium salt-polyethylene glycol eutectic.

PN CN114225647-A; CN114225647-B
TI Recycling carbon monoxide low energy consumption            in low temperature methanol washing exhaust gas,            involves cooling carbon-rich and sulfur-rich methanol,            and sending it to flash tanks to carry out first-level            flash evaporation.
AU WANG H
   XIAO H
   ZHU Z
   FAN P
   YANG Z
   ZHOU X
   CHENG G
   LI H
   YANG J
   XIE B
   SONG J
   ZHANG J
AE PUCHENG CLEAN ENERGY CHEM CO LTD (PUCH-Non-standard)
GA 202248959D
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling carbon monoxide low energy                consumption in low temperature methanol washing                exhaust gas, involves cooling carbon-rich methanol                and sulfur-rich methanol from the methanol washing                tower (1) and sending to the first flash tank (2)                and the second flash tank (3) for an intermediate                pressure flash evaporation. The methanol after the                first-stage flash evaporation and the gas that                promotes the desorption of carbon mono oxide in the                methanol are carried out in the medium-pressure                flash column (4) for the second-stage                medium-pressure flash evaporation, after the                secondary medium-pressure flash distillation, the                methanol after CO removal is pumped by methanol                lift pump A (5) located at the top of the                medium-pressure flash column, and respectively into                carbon dioxide product column (6) and the hydrogen                sulfide concentration column (7) by methanol lift                pump C (8) at the bottom of the medium pressure                flash column for methanol regeneration.
   USE - Recycling carbon monoxide low energy                consumption in low temperature methanol washing                exhaust gas used in chemical production using coal                as raw material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce the carbon                monoxide (CO) content in the methanol entering                carbon dioxide product tower and the hydrogen                sulfide concentration tower, which increases the                methanol production capacity, and also effectively                improves the on-site operating environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling carbon monoxide low energy                consumption in low temperature methanol washing                exhaust gas, involves cooling carbon-rich methanol                and sulfur-rich methanol from the methanol washing                tower (1) and sending to the first flash tank (2)                and the second flash tank (3) for an intermediate                pressure flash evaporation. The methanol after the                first-stage flash evaporation and the gas that                promotes the desorption of carbon mono oxide in the                methanol are carried out in the medium-pressure                flash column (4) for the second-stage                medium-pressure flash evaporation, after the                secondary medium-pressure flash distillation, the                methanol after CO removal is pumped by the methanol                lift pump A (5) located at the top of the                medium-pressure flash column, and respectively into                the carbon dioxide product column (6) and the                hydrogen sulfide concentration column (7) by the                methanol lift pump C (8) at the bottom of the                medium pressure flash column for methanol                regeneration. The methanol from the methanol                washing system is sent to the gas-liquid separation                tank (10) for separation after heat exchange by the                first heat exchanger, and the separated gas is sent                to the carbon dioxide product tower. The separated                methanol is divided into two strands, and one                strand is sent into the hydrogen sulfide                concentration tower. The other strand exchanges                heat with the lean methanol at the inlet of the                lean methanol tank (11) through the second heat                exchanger (12). The rewarmed methanol is sent to                the flash tank (13) for flash evaporation under                reduced pressure. The flash steam is processed by                carbon dioxide product column, after flash                evaporation, methanol with less carbon dioxide is                pressurized by a pump and then cooled twice by a                deep cooler (14) and a cooler (15). The carbon                dioxide which is rewarmed and decomposed and the                methanol after cooling are sent to the medium                pressure flash tower, and reverse contact with the                flash steam from the medium pressure flash tower,                washed and absorbed carbon dioxide in the upper and                lower flash steam of the medium pressure flash                tower, and reduced the amount of flash gas. The                methanol from carbon dioxide is sent to the                hydrogen sulfide concentration tower by the                methanol lift pump B (9) located in the middle of                the medium pressure flashing column.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of a process                for treating ammoniacal methanol in a low                temperature methanol wash system.
   1Methanol washing tower
   2First flash tank
   3Second flash tank
   4Medium-pressure flash column
   5Methanol lifting pump A
   6CO2 product column
   7H2S concentration tower
   9Methanol lift pump B
   10Gas-liquid separation tank
   11Methanol-lean tank
   12Second heat exchanger
   13Flash tank
   14Deep cooler
   15Cooler
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas that promotes                  the desorption of carbon monoxide in methanol is                  hydrogen or purified gas.

PN CN111972523-A
TI Method for preparing high-selenium fermented solid            powdered tea beverage comprises using            selenium-containing green tea as raw material,            crushing, sieving, inoculating and performing            solid-liquid separation.
AU YUE P
   ZHANG J
   GAO X
   ZHANG B
AE HUZHOU DINGXIANG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020C19482
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high-selenium fermented                solid powdered tea beverage comprises (i) using                selenium-containing green tea as a raw material,                crushing, sieving, removing the sieve tea, and                drying to obtain selenium-containing green tea.                Broken green tea; (ii) carrying out supercritical                carbon dioxide treatment on selenium-containing                crushed green tea, where the pressure of                supercritical carbon dioxide treatment is 250-330                MPa for 1-3.5 hours; (iii) adding                selenium-containing green tea powder treated with                supercritical carbon dioxide to purified water at                80-100 degrees C; (iv) using green tea concentrate                as the medium to prepare the first-level seed                solution of Santocystis cristatum strain; (v)                inoculating the aseptic mixed tea solution with the                seed solution of Santocystis cristatum; and (vi)                performing solid-liquid separation of the fermented                high-selenium mixed tea liquid.
   USE - Used as method for preparing high-selenium                fermented solid powdered tea beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical extraction                technology that removes caffeine in                selenium-containing tea leaves, and ultra-high                pressure treatment that destroys cell walls of                selenium-containing tea leaves.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing high-selenium fermented                solid powdered tea beverage comprises (i) using                selenium-containing green tea as a raw material,                crushing, sieving, removing the sieve tea, and                drying to obtain selenium-containing green tea.                Broken green tea; (ii) carrying out supercritical                carbon dioxide treatment on selenium-containing                crushed green tea, where the pressure of                supercritical carbon dioxide treatment is 250-330                MPa for 1-3.5 hours; (iii) adding                selenium-containing green tea powder treated with                supercritical carbon dioxide to purified water at                80-100 degrees C, airtightly stirring, keeping the                temperature at 80-100 degrees C for 30-120 minutes,                and cooling to 20-30 degrees C in a closed                environment to obtain aseptic mixed tea liquid;                (iv) using green tea concentrate as the medium to                prepare the first-level seed solution of                Santocystis cristatum strain, and finally                inoculating and culture step by step to obtain the                seed solution above level 2 to obtain the seed                solution of Santocystis cristatum; (v) inoculating                the aseptic mixed tea solution with the seed                solution of Santocystis cristatum, where the                inoculation amount is 3-8% (vol.%/v vol.%),                completing inoculation and dissolved oxygen is                60-100% , fermenting for 5-11 days under the                fermentation conditions of pressure 0.02-0.05 MPa                at 20-30 degrees , under stirring speed 3200                revolution/minute to obtain fermented high selenium                mixed tea liquor; and (vi) performing solid-liquid                separation of the fermented high-selenium mixed tea                liquid, removing the tea residue, and then                filtering and concentrating, ultra-high temperature                instantaneous sterilization, spraying or                freeze-drying to obtain high-selenium solid                fermented tea beverage. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is                also included for method for increasing the                selenium content in a solid tea beverage comprises                performing supercritical carbon dioxide treatment                of selenium-containing green tea powder, where the                pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide                treatment is 250-500MPa for 1-3.5 hours, adding                selenium-containing green tea powder treated with                supercritical carbon dioxide to purified water at                80-100 degrees C, airtightly stirring, keep the                temperature at 80-100 degrees C for 30-120 minutes,                and cooling to 20-40 degrees C in a closed                environment, inoculating the aseptic mixed tea                solution with the seed solution of Santocystis                cristatum, where inoculation amount is 3-8% by                volume percentage, fermenting for 5-11 days under                fermentation conditions of 60-100% and pressure                0.02-0.05 MPa at 20-30 degrees C, and stirring                speed 30-200 revolutions/minute to obtain fermented                high-selenium mixed tea liquor, subjecting                fermented high-selenium mixed tea liquid to                solid-liquid separation,removing the tea residue                and then filtering and concentrating, sterilizing,                spray drying or freeze drying are performed to                obtain a solid black tea beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  drying is carried out at 60-120 degrees C. In the                  step (I), the sieving specifically comprises 40                  to 60 meshes sieve. In the step (iv), the                  preparation method of the first-grade seed liquid                  of the coronaria saccharomyces strain comprises                  mixing green tea with water at 60-90 degrees C,                  soaking for 30-120 minutes, soaking and                  concentrating to obtain a green tea concentrate,                  and adjusting the green tea concentrate after the                  medium solid content reaches 4-6%, using as a                  green tea liquid culture medium, cultivating                  coronatus sacculus strain on a PDA plate, eluting                  with water to prepare a spore suspension, under                  aseptic conditions, inoculating spore suspension                  into the green tea liquid medium, where the                  concentration of the spore suspension was                  4.0-9.0x 107/ml, the inoculation amount was 3-12%                  (vol.%/ vol.%), and the dissolved oxygen was                  60-100%, under pressure 0.02-0.05 MPa at 28-30                  degrees and stirring speed of 50-200                  revolutions/minute, performing fermentation and                  cultivation for 3-6 days. In the step (iv), the                  condition of concentrating is carried out under                  pressure is 0.01 -0.1 Mpa at 60-70 degrees C. In                  the step (vi), the ultra-high temperature                  instantaneous sterilizing comprises processing                  for 3-7 seconds under the condition of 135                  degrees . Preferred Components: In the step                  (iii), the selenium-containing crushed green tea                  and the purified water are in a ratio of                  1:15-1:35 according to the mass and volume of the                  liquid. In the step (iv), the preservation number                  of the Santocystis cristatumstrain is CGMCC                  NO.106100.

GA 201839390J
AB    NOVELTY - Essential oil fertilizer comprises fertilizer and essential oil composition.
   USE - Used as essential oil fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The essential oil fertilizer improves utilization ratio of fertilizer, and improves quality of crops.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing essential oil fertilizer comprising adding essential oil to fertilizer, uniformly mixing, to obtain essential oil fertilizer, where the essential oil preparation includes digesting plant essential oil using lysozyme, inactivated enzymes, inactivating the enzyme, extracting using liquid carbon dioxide, carrying out separation, to obtain essential oils, the separated residue is residue containing essential oil, the essential oil plant is lavender, rose, laurel, lemon, tea tree, cypress, Geranium, and sage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fertilizer and essential oils is in mass ratio of 50-99.99:0.01-50. The fertilizer includes single fertilizer, complex fertilizer, composite fertilizer, blended fertilizers, organic and inorganic fertilizer, bio-organic fertilizer, organic fertilizer and water soluble fertilizer. The essential oil is simple essential oil, composite essential oil, dilute essential oil, and essential oil residue. The plant essential oil includes fresh plants essential oil. The fresh essential oil plant is freshly picked plant essential oil and stored in - 20 degrees C to - 5 degrees C. The lysozyme enzymatic hydrolysis also includes water, fresh plants essential oil, and lysozyme in a mass ratio of 10-50:0.02-1:50-90, carrying out enzymolysis at 27-55 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. The enzyme inactivation is carried out at 80-120 degrees C for 5-20 minutes. The enzyme inactivation pressure is 0-5 MPa. The temperature of liquid carbon dioxide is 15-31.26 degrees C, and pressure is 5-10 MPa, the flow rate is 25-35 l/hour. The extraction time is 0.5-2 hours, adding essential oils to retaining agent in a mass ratio of 50-99.5:0.5-50. The retention agent is ethylene glycol, glycerol, and isopropanol. The separation includes a primary separation vessel separation and secondary separation kettle separation, the separation pressure of primary separation vessel is 6-8 MPa, separation temperature is 30-50 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The separation pressure of secondary separation vessel is 4-6 MPa, separation temperature is 25-40 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The bottom of primary separation vessel is left behind as mixture, transferring the mixture to water separator, adding flocculant to mixture, allowing to stand the mixture for 20-150 minutes, where the mixture and flocculant is in mass ratio of 99-99.99:0.01-1, carrying out oil-water separation at 30-50 degrees C, to obtain lavender wax and carrying out water treatment, filtering, to obtain water and essential oil residue. The flocculant is activated clay, polyaluminum chloride, sodium polyacrylate, and polyacrylamide.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN112851924-A; CN112851924-B
TI Preparing recyclable nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic comprises e.g. taking 3-pyrroline prepared by performing cyclization of furfural, reacting with dicarbonate or carbonochloridate compound, and reacting with carbon dioxide.
AU LIU Y
   YU Y
   LV X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202162057A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing recyclable nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic comprises (i) (a) performing hydrolysis, reduction, halogenation and cyclization of furfural derived from biomass to form 3-pyrroline compound (1a), dissolving (1a) in dichloromethane, adding dicarbonate compound (IIa) dropwise to react, drying with anhydrous sodium sulfate, spin-drying the solvent and separating by column chromatography to obtain colorless liquid substituted 2,5-dihydro-1H-pyrrole-1-carboxylate compound (IV), preparing five-membered ring structure alkylene oxide monomer (V) as colorless liquid, and (ii) performing the reaction of carbon dioxide with (V) in presence of bi-tetradentate or bi-tridentate Schiff base complex bimetallic catalyst that connects two metal centers through a biphenyl framework at 0-100 degrees C for 1-48 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing recyclable nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: produces non-toxic and harmless, biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate, which can be chemically degraded under heating conditions; realize the cycle of polycarbonate to alkylene oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing recyclable nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic comprises (i) (a) performing hydrolysis, reduction, halogenation and cyclization of furfural derived from biomass to form 3-pyrroline compound of formula (1a), dissolving (1a) in dichloromethane under nitrogen protection to make 3-pyrroline solution with concentration of 1 mol/l, placing it in an ice bath at 0 degrees C, adding dicarbonate compound of formula (IIa) dropwise to control the molar ratio of 3-pyrroline to (IIa) to 5:6, heating to room temperature and stirring for 3 hours, adding 2 mol/l dilute hydrochloric acid solution with dichloromethane and other volume and stirring for 0.25 hour, separating the organic phase, drying with anhydrous sodium sulfate, spin-drying the solvent and separating by column chromatography to obtain colorless liquid substituted 2,5-dihydro-1H-pyrrole-1-carboxylate compound of formula (IV), (b) dissolving 3-pyrroline in dichloromethane to make 3-pyrroline solution with concentration of 1 mol/l under nitrogen protection, placing it in an ice bath at 0 degrees C, adding triethylamine to control the molar ratio of triethylamine to (1a) to 2:1, adding carbonochloridate compound of formula (R-O-C(=O)-Cl) (IIb) dropwise, removing the ice bath after the dripping is complete, reacting at room temperature for 8 hours, and controlling the molar ratio of 3-pyrroline to (IIb) to 2:3, removing the solid by filtration, washing the filtrate with saturated brine, separating the organic phase and drying with anhydrous sodium sulfate, removing the solvent, and separating the product by column chromatography to obtain colorless liquid substituted 2,5-dihydro-1H-pyrrole-1-carboxylate compound of formula (IV), (c) dissolving (IV) in dichloromethane under nitrogen protection to make solution concentration of 0.25 mol/l, placing it in an ice bath at 0 degrees C, slowly adding m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid to control the molar ratio of compound to m-chloroperoxybenzoic acid to 5:7, removing the ice bath after 1 hour, and reacting for 24 hours at room temperature, adding saturated sodium thiosulfate solution to make the reaction solution neutral, stirring for 1 hours, separating the organic phase, and washing with saturated sodium bicarbonate solution, separating the organic phase, drying with anhydrous sodium sulfate, concentrating and separating by column chromatography to obtain five-membered ring structure alkylene oxide monomer of formula (V) as colorless liquid, and (ii) performing the reaction of carbon dioxide with (V) in presence of bi-tetradentate or bi-tridentate Schiff base complex bimetallic catalyst that connects two metal centers through a biphenyl framework at 0-100 degrees C for 1-48 hours to obtain final product, where the carbon dioxide pressure is 0.1-6 MPa, and the molar ratio between catalyst and (V) is 1:1000-1:200000.
   R = CH3, CH2CH3, CH2CH2CH3, CH2CH2CH2CH3, (CH2)4CH3, (CH2)5CH3, CHCH3CH2CH3, C(CH3)2CH2CH3, CH(CH3)2, CH2CH(CH3)2, C(CH3)3, CH2C6H5 or -CPh3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) catalytic degradation of nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic, comprising catalyzing and degrading the nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic into alkylene oxide monomer under the condition of solvent or no solvent in the catalysis of the chromium complex catalyst containing Schiff base structure, where the alkylene oxide monomer can be polymerized into polycarbonate again, the molar ratio of the catalyst to the repeating unit in the polycarbonate in the homogeneous degradation with solvent and the catalytic pyrolysis reaction system without solvent is 1:10-1:10000, the concentration of the repeating unit in the homogeneously degraded polycarbonate in the solvent is 0.01-0.1 mol/l, the reaction temperature is 50-150 degrees C, and the reaction time is 1-200 hours, and the reaction temperature of the solvent-free catalytic pyrolysis is 150-250 degrees C, and the reaction time is 0.1-3 hours; and
   (2) directly pyrolyzing nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic into alkylene oxide when heated, comprising pyrolyzign the nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic into alkylene oxide monomers at 200-350 degrees C for 0.5-10 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the preparation of recyclable nitrogen-containing polycarbonate plastic, the solvent comprises toluene, xylene, tetrahydrofuran, methylene chloride, dioxane and ethylene glycol dimethyl ether. In the catalytic degradation, the solvent comprises toluene, xylene, tetrahydrofuran, chloroform and o-dichlorobenzene.
DC A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A21 (Epoxides; aminoplasts; phenoplasts.)
MC A05-E06A; A10-E05; A10-E05B; A11-C03

PN EP4001381-A1
TI Production of biomethane used in industrial applications, involves upgrading biogas by performing intermediate removal of water, and performing direct methanation reaction of carbon dioxide present in biogas.
AU CANU P
   PAGIN M
AE K-INN TECH SRL (KINN-Non-standard)
GA 202272677M
AB    NOVELTY - Production of biomethane (10) from biogas (100) involves upgrading biogas by (A) performing intermediate removal of water, and (B) performing direct methanation reaction of carbon dioxide present in the biogas.
   USE - Production of biomethane used in industrial/chemical applications in renewable energy industry sector.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically and reliably provides biomethane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the production of biomethane from biogas.
   Biomethane (10)
   Biogas (100)
   Hydrogen (101)
   Condensers (C1,C2)
   Apparatus lines (L1,L2)
   Reactors (R1,R2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves introducing the biogas and hydrogen (101) into a reactor (R1), performing a methanation reaction to obtain a gaseous mixture (a), adding water in the mixture (a) in condenser (C1), separating water from the balance of the mixture (a) to obtain an impure biomethane, introducing the impure biomethane into a reactor (R2), performing a methanation reaction (A1) to obtain a gaseous mixture (b), adding water in the gaseous mixture (b) condensing in a condenser (C2), separating the water from the balance of gaseous mixture (b) to obtain biomethane, and swapping the reactors periodically during the methanation step (B) by change in a position of the valves (I and II). Preferred Properties: The reactors contain a catalytic converter and are operated at 200-350 degrees C in a pressure of 1-30 bar. The step (A) and (B) take place in two apparatus lines arranged in series, parallel, and fluidically connected to each other. The apparatus lines contain the reactors and condensers. The heat produced by the reactor (R1) is for compensating the dissipations in the reactor and allowing auto-thermal operation, and/or preheating the reactor (R2). The apparatus lines (L1, L2) are arranged upstream of the reactors and downstream of the condensers by two four-way valves. The valve (I) is fluidically connected in upstream of the reactors, and valve (II) is fluidically connected in downstream of the condensers.

PT P
PN CN113185684-A
TI Preparing poly (propylene carbonate) comprises            activating an organic solvent, propylene oxide and            catalyst, and polymerizing carbon dioxide and propylene            oxide in presence of activated catalyst and organic            solvent.
AU TANG J
   CUI Y
   HU H
AE SHANGHAI HUAFON NEW MATERIALS RES & DEV (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202190144A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing poly (propylene carbonate) comprises                (a) activating an organic solvent, propylene oxide                and catalyst to obtain an activated catalyst, (b)                polymerizing carbon dioxide and propylene oxide in                the presence of activated catalyst and organic                solvent to obtain the poly (propylene                carbonate).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing poly                (propylene carbonate).
   ADVANTAGE - The poly (propylene carbonate): has ultra-high                molecular weight and good mechanical                property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the                poly (propylene carbonate) prepared by the above                mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  activating at 65-75 degrees C under an ultrasound                  with a power of 1.5-20 watt (W)/cm2 for 0.5-2                  hours. The method comprises post-processing by                  freeze drying the activated catalyst at 20-55                  degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, and grinding using a                  ball mill in the presence of inert gas for 0.5-2                  hours. The method comprises polymerizing in a                  high pressure reaction kettle at 70-100 degrees C                  under a pressure of 4-15 MPa for 10-30 hours. The                  method comprises post-processing by cooling,                  depressurizing, washing and drying. The method                  specifically comprises (1) ultrasonically                  treating the organic solvent, propylene oxide and                  catalyst at 65-75 degrees C under the power of                  1.5-20 W/cm2 for 0.5-2 hours, freeze drying at                  20-55 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours, grinding for                  0.5-2 hours in the inert gas atmosphere to obtain                  the activated catalyst, and (2) mixing the                  activated catalyst and organic solvent in the                  reaction kettle, heating to 70-100 degrees C,                  adding propylene oxide and filling carbon                  dioxide, polymerizing under the pressure of 4-15                  MPa for 10-30 hours, cooling, depressurizing,                  washing, and drying to obtain the poly(propylene                  carbonate). The weight average molecular weight                  of the polypropylene carbonate is 1000-2000 kDa.                  Preferred Components: The organic solvent                  comprises at least one of 1, 4-dioxane,                  1,3-dioxolane or methylene dichloride. The volume                  ratio of the organic solvent and the propylene                  oxide in step (a) is (0.5-1.5):1. The total mass                  of propyleneoxide in step (a) and step (b) is 100                  wt.%. The mass of the propylene oxide in step (a)                  is 5-20 wt.%. The catalyst is a heterogeneous                  catalyst, preferably at least one of ternary rare                  earth catalyst, organic carboxylic acid zinc                  catalyst, double metal cyanide catalyst or                  modified double metal cyanide catalyst. The                  organic solvent in step (b) is at least one of                  1,4-dioxane, 1,3-dioxo-pentacene or                  dichloromethane. The mass ratio of the propylene                  oxide in step (b) and the catalyst in step (a) is                  (7-15):1. The volume ratio of the propylene oxide                  and the organic solvent in step (b) is 1:(1-1.5).                  The moisture content of the organic solvent and                  the propylene oxide in step (a) and step (b) is                  lower than 50 parts per million (ppm).

   ZHAO K

GA 2021271056

AU JING J

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing composite catalyst comprises (1) mixing mixed metal ion salt solution and precipitant solution to reacting, (2) carrying out aging crystallization of reaction product, solid-liquid separation, and precipitation, and (3) calcining precipitate to obtain composite catalyst.
   USE - The preparing composite catalyst is useful for reforming hydrogen production, and carbon dioxide adsorption intensified methane steam reforming hydrogen production reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves problems of low activity and poor cycle stability of catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mixed metal ion salt solution includes calcium ions, nickel ions and cerium ions, the concentration of the calcium ions is 0.05-1 mol/l, and the ratio of the amount of calcium ions to nickel ions and cerium ions is 1:0.01-0.5:0.01-1, the precursor salt of the calcium ion is calcium nitrate, calcium acetate, calcium sulfate, or calcium chloride, the precursor salt of nickel ion is nickel nitrate, nickel acetate, nickel sulfate, or nickel chloride, and the precursor salt of cerium ion is cerium nitrate, cerium sulfate, or cerium chloride. Preferred Method: The concentration of the precipitation agent in the precipitation agent solution is 0.1-3 mol/l, and the precipitation agent is sodium carbonate, urea, potassium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, and/or potassium hydroxide. The temperature of the aged crystallization is 60-120 degrees C, and the time of the aged crystallization is 8-24 hours. Before the roasting, washing precipitate and drying, the washing is to wash precipitate to neutrality, the drying temperature is 80-150 degrees C, and the drying time is 6-24 hours. The temperature of the calcination is 400-900 degrees C, and the time of the calcination is 3-20 hours. The composite catalyst includes an adsorption component, an active component and an auxiliary agent, the adsorption component is calcium oxide, the active component is nickel, and the auxiliary agent is cerium oxide.

AB    NOVELTY - Processing gas stream involves utilising gas stream in catalytic chemical vapor deposition process, reducing carbon dioxide content of the gas stream.
   USE - Method for processing gas stream.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a apparatus for processing gas stream, which comprises reactor for receiving catalyst, reactor has gas inlet and separate gas outlet, heat source for heating the reactor, and supply of gas stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas stream comprises hydrogen, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane and one or more n-C2 hydrocarbons. The gas stream further comprises one or more gases selected from oxygen, water, n-C3, n-C4, n-C5 and/or n-C6 hydrocarbons. The gas stream is produced by gasification of biomass. The catalyst is stainless steel or alumina supported metal catalyst. The metal is nickel, iron, molybdenum, manganese, cobalt, copper, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium, platinum, chromium, tungsten, cerium, silver, gold, or mixtures or alloys. The catalyst is coated stainless steel supported catalyst has metal loading of from 0.1-1.1 wt.%. The catalyst is an alumina supported catalyst having metal loading of from 2.5-10 wt.%. Preferred Process The method involves gasifying waste material to produce gas stream, and using gas stream as the gas stream in the CCVD process, processing waste material using low temperature conversion to produce output gas, and using output gas as the gas stream in the CCVD process, carrying out catalytic chemical vapour deposition process using gasified biomass stream or a LTC gas stream. The gasified biomass stream or LTC gas stream is used in catalytic chemical vapour deposition process.

The mixing reaction is carried out at                  20-40 degrees C for 20-200 minutes. The heating                  reaction is carried out at 200-320 degrees C for                  100-200 minutes. Preferred Properties: The                  manganese-doped indium sulfide nanosheet has                  average size of 50-100 nm and thickness of 0.6-2                  nm. The surface crystal plane of manganese-doped                  indium sulfide nanosheet is 022.

The dialkyldithiocarbamate is chosen from one or                  more of sodium dialkyldithiocarbamate, ammonium                  dialkyldithiocarbamate and diethylammonium                  dialkyldithiocarbamate. The amine solvent is                  chosen from one or more of octylamine,                  decylamine, dodecylamine, tetradecylamine,                  hexadecylamine and oleylamine. Preferred                  Composition: The mass ratio of precursor complex                  and amine solvent is 0.005-0.1: 1.

GA 201872344W
AB    NOVELTY - Two-step electro-deposition preparation of                copper-indium alloy modified copper indium sulfide                film electrode comprises depositing cuprous oxide                particles on copper indium sulfide thin film,                performing electrodeposition of metal indium                particles, reducing copper in cuprous oxide to                copper metal particles under illumination and                modifying.
   USE - Method for two-step electro-deposition                preparation of copper-indium alloy modified copper                indium sulfide film electrode used as photocathode                for carbon dioxide photoelectric catalytic reaction                to ethanol (claimed).

The                  indium salt is indium chloride, indium sulfate                  and/or indium nitrate with concentration of 5-50                  mM.

PN CN108298571-A
TI Preparation of high-purity metaaluminate, involves adding crude metaaluminate solution to alkali liquor, introducing carbon dioxide gas, carrying out carbonation precipitation treatment, aging, filtering and drying.
AU SHI H
   HE X
   XU Q
   WANG Y
   YE S
   MIAO S
   PENG J
   LI Y
   XU Y
   ZHOU Y
   YANG C
   REN S
   HE M
AE YUNNAN ALUMINIUM CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2018590864
AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of high-purity metaaluminate, involves adding crude metaaluminate solution to alkali liquor and introducing carbon dioxide gas into alkali liquor, carrying out carbonation precipitation treatment on crude metaaluminate solution to obtain metaaluminate slurry and performing aging treatment on metaaluminate slurry to obtain aged slurry, filtering and drying aged slurry.
   USE - Preparation of high-purity metaaluminate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of high-purity metaaluminate with high-purity, increased whiteness, large specific surface area and increased pore volume by simple and economical process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crude metaaluminate solution is sodium metaaluminate solution or potassium metaaluminate solution generated in discharge process of aluminum-air battery. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of aqueous ammonia, sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide is of 1-10 g/l and the pH of pure water is greater than or equal to 12. The temperature of alkali liquor is controlled at 40-85 degrees C. The concentration of crude metaaluminate solution is 75-200 g/l. The dripping rate of crude metaaluminate solution is 0.5-8 l/hour. The flow rate of carbon dioxide gas is 0.5-15 l/minute. The aging treatment is carried out at 55-90 degrees C for 48-240 hours.

TI Propylene carbonate synthesis involves using            propanediol, carbon dioxide and acetonitrile as            reaction solvent, and then adding catalyst, where            catalyst is methyl butylimidazolium hydroxide or            imidazole methyl butylimidazolium salt.
AU GAO Y
   WANG Y
   WANG C
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201845496X
AB    NOVELTY - Propylene carbonate synthesis involves using                1,2-propanediol, carbon dioxide and acetonitrile as                a reaction solvent in a mass ratio of 4-8:1, and                then adding catalyst. The catalyst and the                1,2-propanediol are provided in a molar ratio of                0.01-0.04:1. The reaction is carried out at 150-180                degrees C for 24 hours.
   USE - Method for synthesizing propylene                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method synthesizes the propylene carbonate                in a simple and cost-effective manner with high                yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is a                  basic ionic liquid. The basic ionic liquid                  catalyst is 1-methyl-3-butylimidazolium                  hydroxide, imidazole 1-methyl-3-butylimidazolium                  salt, imidazolate tetrabutylphosphonium salt,                  imidazolate tetrabutylammonium salt,                  1-methyl-3-butylimidazolium N,N-dimethyl-3-                  amino-1-propane sulfonate or tetrabutylammonium                  N,N-dimethyl-3-amino-1-propane sulfonate.

PT P
PN WO2022132366-A1
TI Converting hydrocarbon-containing feed by            pyrolysis by feeding hydrocarbon-containing feed and            heated particles into pyrolysis zone, contacting with            heated particles in pyrolysis zone, feeding into            gasification/combustion zone, feeding into pyrolysis            zone, and obtaining carbon dioxide-rich stream.
AU HARANDI M N
   KEUSENKOTHEN P F
   LIU Y
AE EXXONMOBIL CHEM PATENTS INC (ESSO-C)
GA 202281645S
AB    NOVELTY - Process for converting a                hydrocarbon-containing feed by pyrolysis involves                (i) feeding the hydrocarbon-containing feed and                heated particles into a pyrolysis zone, (ii)                contacting with the heated particles in the                pyrolysis zone to effect pyrolysis of at least a                portion of the hydrocarbon-containing feed, (iii)                obtaining from the pyrolysis zone effluent a first                gaseous stream rich in the olefins and a first                particle stream rich in the particles, (iv) feeding                into a gasification/combustion zone, (v) contacting                within the gasification/combustion zone to effect                gasification/combustion of at least a portion of                the coke arranged on the surface of the particles,                (vi) obtaining from the gasification/combustion                zone effluent a second gaseous stream rich in the                gasification/combustion gas mixture and a second                particle stream rich in the regenerated particles,                (vii) feeding into the pyrolysis zone, and (viii)                obtaining a carbon dioxide-rich stream from the                gasification/combustion gas mixture.
   USE - The process is used for converting a                hydrocarbon-containing feed by pyrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The process upgrades hydrocarbon-containing                feeds to produce valuable chemical products such as                olefins.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Process for converting a                hydrocarbon-containing feed by pyrolysis involves                (i) feeding the hydrocarbon-containing feed and                heated particles into a pyrolysis zone, (ii)                contacting the hydrocarbon-containing feed with the                heated particles in the pyrolysis zone to effect                pyrolysis of at least a portion of the                hydrocarbon-containing feed to produce a pyrolysis                zone effluent comprising olefins and the particles,                where coke is formed on the surface of the                particles, (iii) obtaining from the pyrolysis zone                effluent a first gaseous stream rich in the olefins                and a first particle stream rich in the particles,                (iv) feeding at least a portion of the first                particle stream, an oxidant stream, and an optional                steam stream into a gasification/combustion zone,                where the oxidant stream comprises molecular                oxygen, (v) contacting the first particle stream,                the oxidant stream, and the optional steam stream                within the gasification/combustion zone to effect                gasification/combustion of at least a portion of                the coke arranged on the surface of the particles                to produce a gasification/combustion zone effluent                comprising regenerated particles and a                gasification/combustion gas mixture comprising                carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide, (vi)                obtaining from the gasification/combustion zone                effluent a second gaseous stream rich in the                gasification/combustion gas mixture and a second                particle stream rich in the regenerated particles,                (vii) feeding at least a portion of the second                particle stream into the pyrolysis zone as at least                a portion of the heated particles fed into the                pyrolysis zone in step (i), and (viii) obtaining a                carbon dioxide-rich stream from the                gasification/combustion gas mixture, where the                carbon dioxide-rich stream, on a dry basis,                comprises carbon dioxide at a concentration of not                less than 90 vol.%, based on the total volume of                the carbon dioxide -rich stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant stream                  comprises nitrogen at a concentration not less                  than 15 vol.%, based on the total volume of the                  oxidant stream, the gasification/combustion zone                  is a gasification zone, where the second gaseous                  mixture comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide,                  carbon dioxide, and nitrogen. The second gaseous                  stream comprises a greater amount of nitrogen                  than a combined amount of hydrogen, carbon                  monoxide, and carbon dioxide. The second gaseous                  stream comprises 10-25 vol.% hydrogen, 15-30                  vol.% carbon monoxide, and not less than 3 vol.%                  carbon dioxide. The oxidant stream comprises                  oxygen at a concentration not less than 95 vol.%                  and nitrogen at a concentration not less than 5                  vol.%, based on the total volume of the oxidant                  stream, the gasification/combustion zone is a                  combustion zone, the second gaseous mixture is a                  flue gas comprising carbon dioxide and water. The                  heated particles in step (i) comprise: silica,                  alumina, titania, zirconia, magnesia, pumice,                  ash, clay, diatomaceous earth, bauxite, spent                  fluidized catalytic cracker catalyst, or their                  mixtures.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises combusting at least a portion of the                  carbon dioxide-lean gas stream to produce heat,                  (ix) combining a fuel with the carbon                  dioxide-lean gas stream to produce an adjusted                  gas stream, and (x) combusting at least a portion                  of the adjusted gas stream to produce heat. The                  step (viii) involves (viiia) reacting at least a                  portion of the second gaseous steam with                  additional steam under shifting conditions to                  produce a shifted gas stream, where the shifted                  gas stream, on a dry basis, comprises carbon                  dioxide at a concentration of not less than 20                  vol.%, based on the total volume of the shifted                  gas stream, and (viiib) obtaining from the                  shifted gas stream the carbon dioxide-rich stream                  and a carbon dioxide-lean gas stream comprising                  hydrogen and nitrogen. The step (iv) further                  comprises feeding a diluent stream into the                  gasification zone, where the diluent stream                  comprises a portion of the second gaseous stream                  obtained in step (vi). The step (viiia) involves                  (viiia1) indirectly transferring heat from the                  second gaseous stream to a cooling medium to                  produce a cooled second gaseous stream comprising                  water, (viiia2) separating at least a portion of                  the water produced in step (Viiia1), (viiia3)                  optionally separating at least one of: (A) at                  least a portion of any regenerated particles, if                  present in the second gaseous stream; and (B) at                  least a portion of any hydrogen sulfide from the                  cooled second gaseous stream, if present in the                  second gaseous stream, to produce a purified                  second gaseous stream, (viiia4) compressing at                  least a portion of the purified second gaseous                  stream to produce a compressed second gaseous                  stream, and (viiia5) mixing at least a portion of                  the compressed second gaseous stream with the                  additional steam to effect the reacting under the                  shifting conditions to produce the shifted gas                  stream. The step (iv) further involves feeding a                  diluent stream into the gasification zone, where                  the diluent stream comprises a portion of the                  compressed second gaseous stream obtained in step                  (viiia4), feeding a fuel stream into the                  gasification/combustion zone, where a first                  portion of the fuel stream is combusted within                  the gasification zone, and where a second portion                  of the fuel stream is converted into hydrogen and                  carbon monoxide. The step (viii) involves (viiic)                  indirectly transferring heat from the second                  gaseous stream to a cooling medium to produce a                  cooled second gaseous stream comprising water,                  and (viiid) separating at least a portion of the                  water from the cooled second gaseous stream to                  produce the carbon dioxide-rich stream                  comprising, on a dry basis, carbon dioxide at a                  concentration not less than 90 vol.% carbon                  dioxide, based on the total volume of the carbon                  dioxide-rich stream, abating at least a portion                  of fine particles, if any, from the second                  gaseous stream, abating at least a portion of                  sulfuric oxide, if any, from the second gaseous                  stream, and abating at least a portion of nitrous                  oxide, if any, from the second gaseous stream.                  The process further involves utilizing the carbon                  dioxide-rich stream, upon optional compressing,                  in an enhanced oil recovery process, sequestering                  the carbon dioxide-rich stream, converting at                  least a portion of the carbon dioxide-rich stream                  into another compound; and introducing the carbon                  dioxide-rich stream into a carbon dioxide                  pipeline, feeding a steam stream into the                  pyrolysis zone in step (i), where at least one of                  the following is met: (i) a weight ratio of the                  steam stream to the hydrocarbon-containing feed                  fed into the pyrolysis zone is 0.01:1-6:1, a                  velocity of gaseous components within the                  pyrolysis zone is at least 20% greater than a                  velocity of the particles within the pyrolysis                  zone, the pyrolysis zone is operated at a                  temperature of 800-l100 degrees C, a pressure                  within the pyrolysis zone is 100-7000                  kPa-absolute, a velocity of the gaseous                  components within the pyrolysis zone is 9-155                  m/second, a velocity of the particles within the                  pyrolysis zone is up to 15.5 m/second, a weight                  ratio of the particles to the                  hydrocarbon-containing feed stream fed into the                  pyrolysis zone in step (i) is 7:1-35:1, and                  (viii) the hydrocarbon-containing feed is                  contacted with the heated particles within the                  pyrolysis zone for a gas residence time of 10-700                  milliseconds, preferably in a downflow reactor.                  Preferred Conditions: The gasification/combustion                  zone is operated at a temperature of at least                  1000 degrees C such as 1200-1500 degrees C, and                  at a pressure of not less than 800 kPa-absolute.                  The gasification/combustion zone is operated at a                  temperature of at least 1000 degrees C such as                  1200-1500 degrees C, and at a pressure of not                  less than 800 kPa-absolute such as 800-7000                  kPa-absolute. Preferred Components: The fuel                  comprises methane, ethane, propane, butane, or                  their mixtures.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
