PN CN112322296-A
TI Preparing rosemary extract comprises e.g. taking 1KG rosemary, washing, placing in the drying equipment, drying, placing in crushing equipment for crushing, then placing the crushed rosemary in a mixing tank and adding aqueous solution.
AU MA T
AE YUZHOU ZHANYING MIDIEXIANG BIOTECHNOLOGY (YUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202118041T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing rosemary extract comprises (i) taking 1KG rosemary, washing and placing in the drying equipment for drying and placing the rosemary in the crushing equipment for crushing treatment, (ii) placing the crushed rosemary in a mixing tank and adding aqueous solution, mixing crushed rosemary with aqueous solution and using a filter to separating the rosemary from the mixed aqueous solution and allowing to stand, (iii) pouring the mixed aqueous solution into the extraction vessel and adding the composite carrier co-solvent and then sealing one end of the high-pressure pump with the extraction vessel, adjusting the immersion internal pressure of the extraction vessel and moving the carbon dioxide heater to the processing position, fixing a heat conduction fixing block with a fast heat conduction rate above the carbon dioxide heater, then placing the extraction solution on the heat conducting fixed block above the carbon dioxide heater and heating the extraction vessel.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rosemary extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The extract: has excellent physicochemical property and solubility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing rosemary extract comprises (i) taking 1KG rosemary, washing and placing in the drying equipment for drying and placing the rosemary in the crushing equipment for crushing treatment, (ii) placing the crushed rosemary in a mixing tank and at the same time adding 1000 ml of aqueous solution, then thoroughly mixing the crushed rosemary with 1000 ml aqueous solution, and then using a filter to separating the rosemary from the mixed aqueous solution and allowing to stand, (iii) pouring the mixed aqueous solution into the extraction vessel and adding the composite carrier co-solvent and then sealing one end of the high-pressure pump with the extraction vessel, adjusting the immersion internal pressure of the extraction vessel and moving the carbon dioxide heater to the processing position, fixing a heat conduction fixing block with a fast heat conduction rate above the carbon dioxide heater, then placing the extraction solution on the heat conducting fixed block above the carbon dioxide heater and heating the extraction vessel, (vi) pouring the resultant liquid extracted into the separation container used and then connecting the carbon dioxide storage tank with the separation container, allowing the separation container to stand, taking out the separated liquid in the lower part of the separation container, (v) pouring the resultant separated liquid into the measuring cup and then placing the measuring cup inside the odor absorption device, sucking the odor in the separated liquid out through the activated carbon block inside the odor adsorption device, (vi) storing the resultant liquid into the bottles and then placing the divided bottles in the disinfection and sterilization device for disinfection and sterilization, and (vii) sterilizing the bottle body, sealing and then covering the bottle body, packing and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i) the drying temperature of the drying equipment is at 80-100 degrees C, the drying time of the drying equipment is 30-60 minutes, the rotating speed of the crushing equipment is 600-1000 rotations/minute (rpm), the time of the crushing equipment is 25-35 minutes. In the step (ii) the stirring speed of the stirring tank is 400-600 rpm. The stirring time of the stirring tank is 8-10 minutes and the allowing to stand for 5-8 minutes. In the step (iii) the extraction pressure of the high-pressure pump is 20-40 MPa, the heating temperature of the CO2 heater is 50-80 degrees C, the extraction time of the extraction container is 60-90 minutes. In the step (iv) the allowing to stand time of the extraction solution is 5-10 minutes and the allowing to stand temperature is normal temperature. In the step (v), the odor absorption time of the odor adsorption device is 20-30 minutes. in In the step (vi), the sterilization time of the bottle body is 8-10 minutes. Preferred Components: The co-solvent of the composite carrier is E6K-10ET and the ratio of the composite carrier co-solvent to the mixed aqueous solution is 1:200.

PT P
PN CN108546825-A; CN108546825-B
TI Solid waste liquid waste three-phase extraction of            olefin carbon-enriched rare earth separation of            inorganic material, comprises mixing the raw materials            with water, transferring into a supercritical carbon            dioxide reactor and reacting.
AU CAO Y
AE SHANXI ZHONGPU ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201877265Q
AB    NOVELTY - Solid waste liquid waste three-phase                extraction of olefin carbon-enriched rare earth                separation of inorganic material, comprises: (i)                mixing the raw materials with water, transferring                into a supercritical carbon dioxide reactor,                reacting, extracting rare earth elements; (ii)                performing solid-liquid separation of the solution;                (iii) reacting, adding soluble sulfide,                thioacetamide or copper reagent into the reactor,                separating alkali metal, alkaline earth metal,                transition metal and heavy metal ions, desorbing;                (iv) adding an organic ester molecule into the                reaction vessel; (v) vacuum-concentrating the rare                earth element extraction storage tank, separating                carbon dioxide from rare earth and organic ester                molecular rare earth solvation complexes, pickling                and extracting organic ester molecule to enrich                rare earth, and performing simultaneous separation                and recovery of organic ester molecules; and (vi)                subjecting the carbon-containing olefin to olefin                extraction process.
   USE - Used as solid waste liquid waste three-phase                extraction of olefin carbon-enriched rare earth                separation of inorganic material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Solid waste liquid waste three-phase                extraction of olefin carbon-enriched rare earth                separation of inorganic material, comprises: (i)                mixing the raw materials with water, transferring                into a supercritical carbon dioxide reactor,                reacting at 283-423 K with a pressure of 3.5-35                MPa, extracting rare earth elements from the added                raw materials during stripping of olefin carbon;                (ii) performing solid-liquid separation of the                solution, where the separated solid is a bulk                silicon-aluminum solid material; (iii) maintaining                the temperature of the reactor at 283-373 K with a                pressure of 0.1-10 MPa, adding soluble sulfide,                thioacetamide or copper reagent into the reactor,                separating alkali metal, alkaline earth metal,                transition metal and heavy metal ions, desorbing                from the olefinic carbon and the rare earth in the                mixed liquid phase; (iv) adding an organic ester                molecule into the reaction vessel, where the                organic ester molecule forms a solvate complex with                the rare earth, the olefinic carbon remains in                aqueous phase, the organic ester molecules are                mutually soluble with carbon dioxide to form a                uniform phase gas extending liquid, layering the                gas expanding liquid and aqueous phase, separating                the gas expansion liquid with the rare earth                solvation complex of the organic ester molecule                from olefin carbon-containing aqueous phase,                feeding gas expansion liquid with rare earth                solvation complex of an organic ester molecule into                a rare earth element extraction storage tank; (v)                vacuum-concentrating the rare earth element                extraction storage tank (at 273-373 K with a                pressure of 0.1-9 MPa), separating carbon dioxide                from rare earth and organic ester molecular rare                earth solvation complexes, re-using the recovered                carbon dioxide and returning to step (i), pickling                and extracting organic ester molecule to enrich                rare earth, and performing simultaneous separation                and recovery of organic ester molecules; and (vi)                subjecting the carbon-containing olefin to olefin                extraction process in the reaction kettle to obtain                olefinic carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material is:                  coal gangue; inferior coal; fly ash; coal char;                  washing plant wastewater generated coal; coal                  tar; solid waste in the steel and smelting                  industry involving solid waste or liquid waste                  generated in the production process of all                  metallurgical industries; rare earth mine; or                  rare earth slag or liquid waste generated after                  extraction by other method. The reaction kettle                  is provided with a stirring device using stirring                  condition during the reaction. The organic ester                  molecule is a neutral orthophosphoric acid                  extractant. The neutral extraction agent is                  orthophosphoric acid tributyl phosphate or                  trioctylphosphine. Preferred Method: The                  step-by-step reaction comprises adopting                  microwave, ultrasonication, heating,                  acid-enhanced extraction and enhanced mass                  transfer means, where the mechanical force of the                  high-speed ball mill is used to enhance the                  reaction effect by strengthening the breaking and                  re-bonding of the chemical bond. The olefin                  extraction process comprises filtering via 0.1-10                  micron filter membrane, using Darcy index                  100-10000 as dialysis membrane for dialysis, and                  centrifuging the olefinic carbon-containing                  aqueous phase at 2000-50000 rotations/minute to                  obtain the olefin carbon.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

TI Preparing edge-carboxylated graphene involves grinding graphite powder in presence of supercritical carbon dioxide in high-pressure grinding kettle.
AU WANG X

   ZHANG H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 2020181552
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing edge-carboxylated graphene involves grinding graphite powder in the presence of supercritical carbon dioxide in a high-pressure grinding kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing edge-carboxylated graphene.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The high-pressure grinding disc kettle includes a shell (A) and a self-circulating grinding disc reaction device provided inside the shell (A). The self-circulating grinding disc reaction device includes a grinding disc support frame, which is fastened on the top wall of the shell (A). The bottom of the grinding disc support frame is fastened to a grinding disc (A). A grinding disc (B) is provided on the top of the grinding disc (A). The bottom of the grinding disc (B) is arranged opposite to the top of the grinding disc (A). A guide portion for discharging material between the grinding disc (A) and the grinding disc (B) is provided at the bottom of the grinding disc (B). The centrifugal force of the grinding disc (B) causes a vacuum negative pressure region to be formed between the grinding disc (A) and the grinding disc (B). A through hole is provided in the middle of the grinding disc (B). A diversion portion includes a diversion groove (A) and a diversion groove (B). Both ends of the diversion groove (A) penetrate the outside of the grinding disc (B) and the through hole, respectively. Both ends of the diversion groove (B) pass through the outside of the grinding disc (B) and a side groove wall of the diversion groove (A), respectively. The diversion grooves (A) are arranged at a circumferential interval on the bottom of the grinding disc (B). Two ends of each of the diversion grooves (A) are respectively penetrated with the outer peripheral edge position of the grinding disc (B) and the through hole. The diversion grooves (B) are arranged at intervals on the bottom of the grinding disc (B). Both ends of each of the diversion grooves (B) respectively penetrate the outer peripheral edge position of the grinding disc (B) and a groove wall of one side of the diversion groove (A). A cross-section of each of the diversion groove is set to be a triangle, and a slot width of each of the diversion grooves (B) is greater than a groove bottom width. The depth of a notch on each of the diversion grooves (A) near the through hole is greater than the depth of the notch on the diversion groove (A) near a peripheral edge position of the grinding disc (B). A distance adjusting spring is provided between the grinding disc (A) and the grinding disc support frame. The contact force between the grinding disc (A) and the grinding disc (B) is controlled by the distance-adjusting spring to be 15-25 Newtons. The top of the grinding disc (B) is fastened to the bottom of a transmission shaft. The bottom of the transmission shaft is close to the through hole and is fastened to the top of a stirring lifting paddle. The bottom of the stirring lifting paddle passes through the grinding disc (A) and the grinding disc (B), and is arranged in a deflector. The top of the deflector extends out of the grinding disc (A), and is arranged in the through hole of the grinding disc (B). The bottom of the deflector is arranged inside the shell (A). The top of the transmission shaft is connected to the power output shaft of the magnetic transmission device through a coupling. A shell (B) is fastened to the outside of the shell (A). An interlayer for containing liquid is provided at a position between the shell (A) and the shell (B). The shell (A) is a high-pressure inner kettle unit. End caps are respectively provided at both ends of the high-pressure inner kettle unit. The two ends of the kettle unit and the two end covers are connected by flanges. Sealing rings are respectively provided at positions, where the two ends of the kettle unit are connected to the two end caps. The shell (B) is a high-pressure outer kettle unit. A liquid outlet and a liquid inlet are arranged on the side wall of the kettle unit at intervals.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method involves: (S1) adding the purified or unpurified graphite powder to the high-pressure grinding kettle; (S2) passing carbon dioxide into the kettle and placing the kettle in a supercritical state to form a material containing graphite powder and supercritical carbon dioxide; and (S3) grinding the material containing graphite powder and supercritical carbon dioxide to obtain the edge-carboxylated graphene. Preferred Conditions: The operating conditions in the high-pressure grinding kettle are a temperature of 35-200 degrees C, preferably 35-70 degrees C, a pressure of 75-165 atmosphere, preferably 75-125 atmosphere, a grinding speed of 500-10000 rpm, preferably 500-5000 rpm, and a grinding time of 6-48 hours. Preferred Components: The graphite powder is a flake graphite powder and an expanded graphite powder. Preferred Property: The graphite powder has a particle size of 10-80 mesh, preferably 20-60 mesh.

TI Preparation of adsorbent material for removal of e.g. carbon dioxide and methane in confined space, involves preparing porous carbon material, preparing ionic liquid using organic amine compound, and loading ionic liquid on porous carbon material.
AU CUI Q
   ZHAO X
   WANG B
AE NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
   CHN ENERGY INVESTMENT GROUP CO LTD (SHGR-C)
GA 2021C4877X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of adsorbent material involves preparing porous carbon material, preparing ionic liquid using organic amine compound, and loading ionic liquid on porous carbon material.
   USE - Preparation of adsorbent material for removal of carbon dioxide, volatile organic compounds, sulfur dioxide and methane in confined space (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared adsorbent material effectively avoids defects like aging and crushing of material after absorption of carbon dioxide, effectively solves the impact of powdering of existing adsorbent and harm to human health, and is easy to regenerate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation involves mixing carbon material with alkali, carbonizing at 400-800degrees Celsius under heating rate of 5-15degrees Celsius/minute for 4-12 hours, washing carbonized sample with 0.5-1.5 mol/L acid solution and drying to obtain porous carbon material, diluting the organic amine compound with deionized water to obtain 30-70 wt.%, preferably 50-70 wt.% diluted organic amine compound, adding 20-50 wt.%, preferably 35-45 wt.% organic acid at 5-10degrees Celsius, and stirring at 20-30degrees Celsius for 6-10 hours to obtain ionic liquid, dissolving ionic liquid in solvent, adding porous carbon material, soaking and air drying.Preferred Component: The carbon material is carbon-containing solid waste, preferably coal liquefaction residue and/or asphalt. The organic amine compound is one or more of ethanolamine, diethanolamine, ethylenediamine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine and tetraethylenepentamine. The organic acid is 2-5C organic acid, preferably lactic acid. The solvent is methanol and/or ethanol.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of organic acid to the diluted organic amine compound is 1:1-1.5, preferably 1:1-1.2. The mass ratio of ionic liquid to porous carbon material is 1:10-20, preferably 1:10-15.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali is potassium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide, preferably potassium hydroxide. The acid solution is inorganic acid, preferably at least one of hydrochloric acid, nitric acid and sulfuric acid.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. GA 2021D9459L

TI Recovery of carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide source discharged from e.g. blast furnace, involves contacting gaseous carbon dioxide source with carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid, supplying carbon dioxide-rich absorbent liquid to regeneration tower, and recovering desorbed carbon dioxide.
AU TAKAOKA H
   HIRAKI M
AE COSMO ENG KK (MAZN-C)
GA 202265857D
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of carbon dioxide in a gaseous carbon dioxide source involves contacting the gaseous carbon dioxide supply source (1) with the carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid in the absorption tower, absorbing the carbon dioxide lean absorbent liquid and then passing to a carbon dioxide rich absorbent liquid storage tank and storing the liquid, supplying the carbon dioxide-rich absorbent liquid to the regeneration tower and heating the carbon dioxide-rich absorbent liquid, obtaining the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid, then recovering the desorbed carbon dioxide, and sending the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid to a carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid storage tank to store the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid, transferring the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid storage tank for the absorbing tower, and transferring the carbon dioxide-rich absorption liquid to the carbon dioxide-rich absorption liquid storage tank.
   USE - Recovery of carbon dioxide in gaseous carbon dioxide source discharged from blast furnaces, lime furnaces, heating furnaces, reactors, boilers, power plants, steel mills, cement factories, refineries, and chemical factories.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recovery of carbon dioxide from a low-pressure gaseous carbon dioxide source, and reduced site area of equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovery of carbon dioxide in a gaseous carbon dioxide source involves contacting the gaseous carbon dioxide supply source with the carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid in the absorption tower, absorbing the carbon dioxide lean absorbent liquid to obtain a carbon dioxide rich absorbent liquid, and then passing the carbon dioxide rich absorbent liquid to a carbon dioxide rich absorbent liquid storage tank for an absorption tower and storing the liquid, supplying the carbon dioxide-rich absorbent liquid in the carbon dioxide-rich absorbent liquid storage tank to the regeneration tower and heating the carbon dioxide-rich absorbent liquid, obtaining the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid, then recovering the desorbed carbon dioxide, and sending the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid to a carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid storage tank to store the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid, transferring the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid storage tank for the absorbing tower, and transferring the carbon dioxide-rich absorption liquid to the carbon dioxide-rich absorption liquid storage tank. The carbon dioxide lean absorber and the carbon dioxide rich absorber comprise an absorbent. The carbon dioxide concentration in the gaseous carbon dioxide source is 50.0 vol.% or less, and the pressure of the gaseous carbon dioxide source is 90-300 kPa.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide recovery system.
   Gaseous carbon dioxide source (1)
   Processing gas (2)
   Carbon dioxide rich absorbent (3)
   Carbon dioxide lean absorber (4)
   Carbon dioxide recovery system (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the absorption process, the gaseous carbon dioxide supply source is flowed in an upward flow and the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid is flowed in a downward flow in the absorption tower, where the carbon dioxide lean absorbing liquid and gaseous carbon dioxide source are contacted with a countercurrent. In the regeneration process, the carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid accumulated at the bottom of the regeneration tower is heated to generate an upward flow of vapor of the absorption liquid having a saturation temperature in the regeneration tower, carbon dioxide-rich absorbing liquid is heated by heating the inside of the regeneration tower and flowing the carbon dioxide-rich absorbing liquid in a downward flow. The absorption process, the regeneration process, the carbon dioxide lean absorption liquid transfer process, and the carbon dioxide rich absorption liquid transfer process is performed by the receipt/shipment management control system. Preferred Properties: The absorption rate of carbon dioxide in the absorption process is 25-75%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The absorbent comprises an amine compound.

PN EP4000657-A1; WO2022106488-A1
TI Making amino acids, preferably glycine and/or alanine, by contacting gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, methane and nitrogen in presence of water with catalyst comprising permanently polarized hydroxyapatite and brushite.
AU ALEMAN LLANSO C E
   PUIGGALI BELLALTA J
   SANS J
   TURON DOLS P
   SANZ BELTRAN V
   RODRIGUEZ RIVERO A M
AE BRAUN SURGICAL SA B (BINT-C)
   UNIV CATALUNYA POLITECNICA (UYCL-C)
   BRAUN SURGICAL SA B (BINT-C)
   UNIV CATALUNYA POLITECNICA (UYCL-C)
GA 202272694S
AB    NOVELTY - Producing amino acids, preferably glycine and/or alanine, comprises contacting a gas mixture comprising carbon dioxide, methane and nitrogen in presence of water with a catalyst comprising permanently polarized hydroxyapatite and brushite and/or a brushite-like material.
   USE - The process is useful for: producing amino acids, preferably glycine and/or alanine; and removing harmful gases e.g. carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, and/or methane from an atmosphere, preferably air (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: has lower levels of environmental contamination; and is cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The contacting step is carried out: in the presence of liquid water and/or water vapor; with a volumetric ratio of the catalyst to the water, preferably liquid water and/or water vapor, of 1000:1-0.01:1, preferably 300:1-350:0.01; with a volumetric ratio of the carbon dioxide to the methane to the nitrogen of 100:1:1, preferably 1:1:1; under a total pressure of 0-250 bar, preferably 0-10 bar; under a partial pressure of the carbon dioxide of 0-100 bar, preferably 0-10 bar; with a molar ratio of the carbon dioxide to the catalyst of 0-0.5, preferably 0.3-0.5; under UV irradiation or UV-Vis irradiation having a wavelength of 200-850 nm, preferably of 253.7 nm, and/or having a irradiance from 0.1-200 W/m2, preferably 2-10 W/m2; and at a temperature of 25-250 degrees C, preferably of 95 degrees C. Preferred Components: The catalyst: is in the form of a multi-phase catalyst, where the permanently polarized hydroxyapatite forms a phase, preferably a main phase, of the catalyst and the brushite and/or brushite-like material forms a further phase of the catalyst; has a wide angle x-ray scattering pattern as shown on figure 1(a) of the specification; and has a raman spectrum as shown on figure 1(b) of the specification. The permanently polarized hydroxyapatite has a proportion which is larger than a proportion of the brushite and/or the brushite-like material. The permanently polarized hydroxyapatite has a proportion of 50-99.9 wt.%, preferably 85-88 wt.%. The brushite and/or the brushite-like material has a proportion of 0-50 wt.%, preferably 12-15 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - High pressure stainless steel reactor was employed to perform the synthesis of amino acids. The reactor was also characterized by an inert reaction chamber coated with 120 ml perfluorinated polymer where both the catalyst and water were incorporated. The reactor was equipped with an inlet valve for the entrance of nitogen, methane, carbon dioxide and an outlet valve to recover the gaseous reaction products. A UV lamp was also placed in the middle of the reactor to irradiate the catalyst directly, the lamp being protected by a UV transparent quartz tube. All surfaces were coated with a thin film of a perfluorinated polymer in order to avoid any contact between the reaction medium and the reactor surfaces, in this way discarding other catalyst effects. The reactions were performed at 95 degrees C for a reaction time of 48 hours. Catalyst samples weighed 150 mg and 0.5 ml de-ionized liquid water were initially incorporated into the reaction chamber. The chamber was extensively purged with the first selected gas in order to eliminate the initial air content. Each selected gas was introduced to increase the reaction chamber pressure to the target pressure. In all cases the chamber pressure was increased up to 6 bar by introducing sequentially 2 bar of each feeding reaction gas.

TI Carbonating reactive magnesia cement involves producing bacteria generates carbon dioxide for duration to form aqueous mixture sufficient for carbonating reactive magnesia cement, mixing aqueous mixture with reactive magnesia cement to form blend, where nutrient is provided in aqueous suspension.
AU NGUYEN T D
   UNLUER C
   XIAO X
   YANG E
AE UNIV NANYANG TECHNOLOGICAL (UYTU-C)
GA 202268709D
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonating reactive magnesia cement involves producing bacteria generates carbon dioxide for duration to form aqueous mixture sufficient for substantially carbonating reactive magnesia cement, mixing the aqueous mixture with reactive magnesia cement to form a blend, where nutrient is provided in the aqueous suspension or in the reactive magnesia cement to sustain the carbon dioxide-producing bacteria in the reactive magnesia cement, and curing the blend to carbonate reactive magnesia cement.
   USE - Method for carbonating reactive magnesia cement.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method of providing the aqueous suspension includes dissolving the nutrient in water prior to dispersing the carbon dioxide-producing bacteria in the water. The method of mixing the aqueous suspension with the precursor includes dissolving the precursor in water prior to mixing the aqueous suspension with the precursor. The duration is 30 seconds. The mixing the aqueous mixture with the reactive magnesia cement is carried out with the water of the aqueous mixture and the reactive magnesia cement present in a weight ratio of 0.43-0.7. The method includes mixing the reactive magnesia cement with a hydration agent prior to mixing the reactive magnesia cement with the aqueous mixture. The method includes mixing the aqueous suspension with seed particles. The method includes mixing the aqueous suspension with the seed particles comprises dispersing the seed particles in water. The method includes mixing coarse aggregates with the blend prior to curing the blend. The coarse aggregates have a size ranging from 4-10 mm. The method of curing the blend at atmospheric pressure, temperature ranging from -10-50degrees Celsius, and a humidity ranging from 75-100%. The X-ray diffraction pattern further comprises one peak having a two theta value between 5-10?o which corresponds to dypingite, and/or one peak having a two theta value between 15-20degrees which corresponds to hydromagnesite. The X-ray diffraction pattern further comprises one peak having a two theta value between 30-35degrees which corresponds to artinite. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-producing bacteria comprises an alkaliphilic bacteria or ureolytic bacteria. The nutrient comprises yeast extract, soy broth, lysogeny broth, meat extract, and/or peptone. The precursor comprises urea or magnesium lactate. The aqueous mixture comprises water. The hydration agent comprises hydrochloric acid, magnesium acetate, or magnesium chloride. The seed particles comprise hydromagnesite, carbon nanotubes, nano-sized silicon dioxide, nano-sized titanium dioxide, or carbon nanofibers. The carbon dioxide-producing bacteria comprises Sporosarcina pasteurii, Bacillus sphaericus, Bacillus subtilis, and/or Bacillus cohnii. The reactive magnesia cement composite comprises a carbon dioxide-producing bacteria, X-ray diffraction pattern comprises one peak having a two theta value between 30-35degrees which corresponds to magnesite, one peak having a two theta value between 35-40degrees which corresponds to brucite, one peak having a two theta value between 40-45degrees which corresponds to periclase, where the intensity of the one peak which corresponds to brucite is lower in the presence of the carbon dioxide-producing bacteria as compared to another reactive magnesia cement composite absent of the carbon dioxide-producing bacteria.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

AU 
AE KUSE K (KUSE-Individual)
GA 2022557726
AB    NOVELTY - A structural arrangement comprises two or more panels or free-form structures made of concrete comprising carbon dioxide-calcined rock as filler.
   USE - Structural arrangement used as construction material e.g. reinforced concrete slab for house wall and floor panel of concrete building.
   ADVANTAGE - The arrangement is environmentally-friendly, and efficiently binds atmospheric carbon dioxide and stores it for long.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of reinforced concrete slab.
   Reinforced concrete slab (1)
   Stabilizing lamellae (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The rock is made of rock sources such as serpentine, olivine, dunite, basalt, gabbro, granite, quartzite, limestone, sandstone, old concrete and/or other rocks chemically weathered with aqueous carbon dioxide. The concrete further contains a binder made neutral with carbon dioxide (optional), a reinforcement made of weathered stone dust from carbon fiber stone (CFS) lamellae, basalt profiles, another material with high tensile strength, steel, other profile material or carbon profiles. The carbon fiber used in the CFS lamellae is made from carbon dioxide. The building material has an overall neutral or negative carbon footprint. The carbon fiber comes from fossil sources or regenerative sources.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The binding matrix of the lamella, which connects the carbon fiber with the stone layers, has an epoxy resin, polyester resin-phenolic ester resin, polyimide resin, cyanate ester resin, vinyl ester resin, melamine resin, polyurethane resin and/or silicone resin base having shrinking properties. The raw materials for carbon fibers, resins and/or binders contain vegetable oils, algae oils, yeast oils, lignin or other vegetable raw materials, and come from plant residues that are left over from paper production, preferably in the form of lignin and other plant residues, which are fermented with yeast using yeast oils for biogenic production of polyacrylonitrile (PAN)-based carbon fibers, graphene, resins and binders. The PAN-based carbon fibers, resins and/or binders are produced from carbon dioxide from natural or synthetic bio-methanol using synthetic processes, preferably water-gas shift reaction and Fischer-Tropsch synthesis. The arrangement further contains a mineral binder based on water glass for connecting carbon fiber and stone layer.

PN US2021094928-A1; WO2021061545-A1; US11091454-B2; CA3152089-A1; CN114450273-A; IN202217019616-A; EP4034531-A1; BR112022005842-A2
TI Synthesizing aromatic carboxylic acid compound            comprises providing aromatic compound or aromatic            compound with carboxylic group, providing metal            hydroxide and carboxylate and adding carbon dioxide to            mixture.
AU BRODERICK E M
   ABREVAYA H
   BARGER P T
   BHATTACHARYYA A
   BAGGER P T
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 202132562L
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing an aromatic carboxylic acid                compound comprises: (a) providing an aromatic                compound or an aromatic compound with at least one                carboxylic group; (b) providing a metal hydroxide                and at least one carboxylate to produce a mixture;                and (c) adding carbon dioxide to the mixture under                pressures from about atmospheric to 1000 psig and                sufficient heat for a time sufficient to produce an                aromatic carboxylic acid compound.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing an                aromatic carboxylic acid compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method synthesizes compounds from carbon                dioxide is the difficulty of forming carbon-carbon                bonds efficiently.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The metal hydroxide and                  carboxylate are in a solvent, where the solvent                  is removed prior to the carbon dioxide being                  added to the mixture. The sufficient heat is from                  ambient temperature to 400degrees Celsius. The                  temperatures are 200-300degrees Celsius. The time                  sufficient to produce the aromatic carboxylic                  acid compound is 1 second to 24 hours. The                  synthesis of the aromatic carboxylic acid                  compound is a continuous, semi-batch or batch                  reaction process.Preferred Components: The aromatic                  carboxylic acid compound comprises benzoic acid,                  toluic acid, furoic acid, furan dicarboxylic                  acid, 2,5-furan dicarboxylic acid, terephthalic                  acid, naphthalic acid, thiophene dicarboxylic                  acid, pyridine dicarboxylic acid, carbazole                  dicarboxylic acid, and dibenzothiophene                  dicarboxylic acid. The solvent is water or an                  alcohol. The alcohol comprises methanol, ethanol,                  propanol and butanol. The aromatic carboxylic                  acid monomer is furan-2,5-dicarboxylate. The                  dicarboxylates comprises oxalate, malonate,                  succinate and adipate. The tricarboxylates                  comprises citrate and isocitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal hydroxide                  comprises lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide,                  potassium hydroxide (preferred), rubidium                  hydroxide, cesium hydroxide, magnesium hydroxide                  and calcium hydroxide. The carboxylate consists                  of cations comprising lithium, sodium, potassium,                  rubidium, cesium, magnesium, and calcium and                  anions comprising formates, acetates,                  dicarboxylates and tricarboxylates. The metal                  hydroxide provided in step (b) is at a mole ratio                  of metal hydroxide to aromatic compound or an                  aromatic compound with at least one carboxylic                  group is 1:1-2:1. The mole ratio of metal                  hydroxide and carboxylate is 1:0.1-1:1. The                  anions are propionate, butyrate, isobutyrate or                  lactate.
   EXAMPLE - A water solution of 1.05 equivalents potassium                hydroxide was added to furoic acid. The water was                removed by a rotary evaporator at 120degrees                Celsius for 2 hours producing a white solid. In a                nitrogen atmosphere, the solid was then combined                with 1 equivalent potassium hydroxide and 0.37                equivalent potassium acetate. The solids were                crushed and mixed with a mortar and pestle to form                a powder. The powder was loaded into a glass lined                75 ml autoclave. The autoclave was backfilled 3x                with carbon dioxide then filled with carbon dioxide                (220 psig). The autoclave was heated to 280degrees                Celsius for 1-4 hours. After the allotted time, the                autoclave was cooled for 30 minutes, and the                reaction was vented into a hood.

TI New imidazolidine-based ion exchange resin compound for catalyzing carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide addition reactions.
AU YU F
   XIE T
   HE W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 2019350752
AB    NOVELTY - An imidazolidine-based ion exchange resin compound (I), is new.
   USE - New imidazolidine-based ion exchange resin compound for catalyzing carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide addition reactions (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An imidazolidine-based ion exchange resin compound of formula (I), is new.
   P' = macroporous nano composite resin matrix;
   M- = anion;
   POSS = cage silsesquioxane unit, preferably (-SiO1.5)m;
   m = 6, 8, 10 or 12;
   group A = imidazole cationic unit;and
   R = linking group between the POSS unit and the imidazole cation unit, preferably alkylene or arylene.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for compound (I), which is used to catalyze carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide addition reactions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nanomaterial is at least one of single layer graphene, multilayer graphene, graphene oxide, and/or graphene alkyne, preferably multilayer graphene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The styrene monomer is at least one of styrene, alpha -methyl styrene and/or 4-butyl styrene, preferably styrene. The comonomer is at least one of ethylene glycol dimethacrylate, dipropylene benzene, divinyl phenylmethane and/or divinylbenzene, preferably divinylbenzene. Preferred Conditions: The addition reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide of formula (II) is carried out at 60-180 degrees C and 0.1-10.0 MPa for 1-8 hours. The weight ratio of the ion exchange resin to the alkylene oxide is 0.001-1:1.R1-R4 = H or 1-6C alkyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The compound (I) contains 4-15 wt.% polyhedral oligomeric silsesquioxane (POSS). Preferred Process: The macroporous nanocomposite resin matrix is a nanoporous copolymer obtained by in-situ copolymerization of 85-95 pts. wt. styrene monomer, 2-5 pts. wt. comonomer, 0.1-3 pts. wt. nanomaterial, and 10-100 pts. wt. porogen. The porogen is at least one of an aliphatic hydrocarbon, polystyrene, gasoline, poly(propylene glycol), poly(ethylene glycol), polydimethylsiloxane, fatty acid and/or paraffin wax, preferably polystyrene.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M- = bromo ion;and
   R = ethylene, ethylene, propylene, phenylene, naphthylene or benzylene.

TI Preparation of mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve film doped with nitrogen carbide for gas permeability test, involves preparing polyamic acid mixed matrix film doped with melamine and performing carbon chain rearrangement by carbonization.
AU WANG Y
   LI J
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2021481674
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve film in-situ doped nitrogen carbide, involves preparing poly(amic acid) mixed matrix film doped with melamine in carbonization furnace, performing high temperature carbonization by programmed heat controlling where poly(amic acid) undergoes imidization and carbon chain rearrangement, achieving carbonization process and simultaneously synthesizing melamine in-situ with nitrogen carbide in poly(amic acid) mixed matrix film. The mixed matrix comprises polymer comprising poly(amic acid) polymer and melamine additive.

   ADVANTAGE - The mixed matrix has high carbon dioxide permeability coefficient and carbon dioxide-nitrogen and has excellent carbon dioxide-methane separation factor, chemical stability, performance, permeability and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application in gas permeability test by constant volume variable pressure method which involves vacuum treating both sides of membrane to adjust pressure less than 1000 Pa at temperature range 5-95 degrees C and at feeding pressure 0.1-0.5 MPa and simultaneously monitoring pressure on permeate side by pressure sensor at testing time 2-12 hours, where area for effective testing of mixed matrix carbon molecular sieve membrane is 0.3-10 cm2.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is any one of N,N-dimethyl formamide, N,N-dimethyl acetamide, tetrahydrofuran, acetone and dimethyl sulfoxide.

PT P
PN CN112453047-A
TI Method for increasing infiltration of shallow            contaminated soil, involves repeating injection of            high-viscosity fluid-liquid nitrogen-liquid carbon            dioxide at same injection point until cracks reach            expected requirements.
AU ZHOU D
   ZHANG G
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 202125279H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting a high-viscosity                fluid into the shallow soil, and taking the area of                the shallow contaminated soil as 700 m2. The                injection volume of the high-viscosity fluid is                5m3-10m3. Liquid nitrogen is injected into the same                injection point in the shallow soil, and the                injection volume of the liquid nitrogen is 2m3-5m3                based on the area of the shallow contaminated soil                as 700m2. Liquid carbon dioxide is injected into                the same liquid injection point in the shallow soil                body. The injection volume of the liquid carbon                dioxide is 5m3-10m3 based on the area of the                shallow contaminated soil being 700m2. The                injection of high-viscosity fluid-liquid                nitrogen-liquid carbon dioxide is repeated at the                same injection point until the cracks reach the                expected requirements. The liquid carbon dioxide is                replaced with nitrogen or air.
   USE - Method for increasing infiltration of shallow                contaminated soil for shallow contaminated soil                remediation process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The infiltration enhancement method                effectively increases the permeability of the soil,                thus improving the mass transfer capacity of the                repair agent, and improving the soil repair                effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                well pattern structure of the method for enhancing                the permeability of shallow contaminated                soil.
   Sand mixing tank (1)
   Liquid tank (2)
   Plunger pump (3)
   Wellhead valve (4)
   Booster pump (5)

PN EP3763848-A1; WO2021005216-A1; EP3997257-A1; US2022251717-A1; JP2022540185-W
TI Electrodicarboxylation of alkene, comprises e.g.            reacting carbon dioxide in presence of hydrogen with            formation of unsaturated dicarboxylic acid in reactor            comprising cathode as working electrode for cathodic            activation of carbon dioxide.
AU NEUMANN M
   SCHOMAECKER R
   STRASSER P
AE UNIV BERLIN TECH (UYBE-C)
   UNIV BERLIN TECH (UYBE-C)
   UNIV BERLIN TECH (UYBE-C)
GA 2021050925
AB    NOVELTY - Electrodicarboxylation of at least one alkene,                comprises reacting carbon dioxide in the presence                of hydrogen with the formation of at least one                unsaturated dicarboxylic acid in a reactor                comprising at least one cathode as a working                electrode for the cathodic activation of carbon                dioxide, and at least one anode is used as a                counter electrode for the anodic oxidation of                hydrogen, where the volumetric ratio of hydrogen to                carbon dioxide is 2:1-1:10, preferably 1:1-1:3, a                total pressure (pG) in the reactor is 1-5 MPa,                preferably 3-4 Mpa, and an average current density                (j) is 2-20 mA/cm2, preferably 10-12.5 mA/cm2, and                at least one alkene is at least one diene,                preferably linear conjugated diene, and Faraday                efficiency FEEC for the sum of all dicarboxylation                products is 10-55%, preferably 20-25%.
   USE - The method is useful for                electrodicarboxylation of at least one                alkene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method optimizes the anode, using hydrogen                as an anode reaction (counter reaction), which                greatly simplifies the entire reaction; avoids the                use of aluminum and the production of product                aluminum salts that are difficult to purify, which                saves cost in a corresponding process, avoids                constant maintenance of the reactor, and enables an                increase in the Faraday efficiency of the                products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                reactor utilized in the above mentioned                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The reaction is carried                  out in a dried, organic solvent containing at                  least one conductive salt. The organic solvent is                  dimethylformamide (DMF), dimethylpropyleneurea                  (DMPU), N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) or                  acetonitrile, preferably DMF. The conductive salt                  is alkylammonium bromide, preferably                  tetra-n-butylammonium bromide (TBAB). The                  electrodicarboxylation is carried out in the                  presence of a mediator. The mediator is a                  transition metal complex, preferably rhodium,                  platinum, palladium, ruthenium or iron                  complex.

GA 202037449Q
AB    NOVELTY - Field effect transistor (FET)-based carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor comprises a dielectric material located on a surface of a conductive material layer, a first electrode located on a first portion of the dielectric material, a second electrode spaced apart from the first electrode and located on a second portion of the dielectric material, and a conducting channel is located between the first and second electrodes. The conducting channel is coated with a chemical compound that is selective for carbon di-oxide sensing.
   USE - Field effect transistor (FET)-based carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The field effect transistor (FET)-based carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor is cost-effective, and has longer durability than infrared sensors, and also has a high selectivity for sensing carbon di-oxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of forming a field effect transistor (FET)-based carbon dioxide (CO2) sensor, which involves:
   (A) providing a structure that includes a dielectric material located on a surface of a conductive material layer, a first electrode located on a first portion of the dielectric material, a second electrode spaced apart from the first electrode and located on a second portion of the dielectric material;
   (B) conducting channel located between the first and second electrodes; and
   (C) coating the conducting channel with a chemical compound that is selective for CO2 sensing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical compound is an organic compound that contains an amine functional group. The organic compound that contains the amine functional group comprises monoethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol (AMP), 2-(aminoethyl)ethanolamine (AEEA), or piperazine. The organic compound that contains the amine functional group comprises a polymer containing monoethanolamine (MEA), a polymer containing diethanolamine (DEA), a polymer containing 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol (AMP), a polymer containing 2-(aminoethyl)ethanolamine (AEEA), or a polymer containing piperazine. The conducting channel is composed of a semiconductor material. The conducting channel is composed of one or more carbon nanotubes. The one or more carbon nanotubes comprise a network of carbon nanotubes. The chemical compound is an organic compound that contains an amine functional group. The conducting channel is composed of a semiconductor material, or one or more carbon nanotubes. The one or more carbon nanotubes comprise a network of carbon nanotubes. The network of carbon nanotubes is randomly orientated. Preferred Process: The coating includes providing a solution of the organic compound, and introducing the solution to the conducting channel to coat the conducting channel with the organic compound. The method further involves removing the solution.

PN CN110125156-A; CN110125156-B
TI Method for treating heavy metal pollution in soil by carbon dioxide filling technology, involves starting vacuum system to check tightness of vacuum pre-pressing system, and closing vacuum system after filtering in vacuum for specific range.
AU NA P
   WANG C
   QIAO B
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019728172
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a sand bag well on a surface of a to-be-processed soil. A lower end of the sleeve is located on a bottom of the sand well, where the sleeve comprises an inner pipe and an outer pipe. An upper end of the sleeve is exposed out of a working cushion layer. The outer pipe is connected with a carbon dioxide generating device through a soft pipe. The inner pipe is connected with a vacuum system through the soft pipe. The vacuum system is started to check a tightness of a vacuum pre-pressing system. The vacuum system is closed after filtering in vacuum for 30 to 90min.
   USE - Method for treating heavy metal pollution in soil by carbon dioxide filling technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing the soil in the heavy metal solution to react with carbon dioxide to form heavy metal ions transferred to the liquid in the soil, and extracting the liquid by vacuum extraction, so as to effectively remove the heavy metal in the soil, and avoiding water containing heavy metal ion from moving downward to contaminated groundwater.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device for treating heavy metal pollution in soil by carbon dioxide filling technology. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PN CN108707131-A; CN108707131-B
TI Synthesizing catalyst-based cyclic carbonate comprises e.g. taking epoxy compound and carbon dioxide, morpholine ionic liquid or ionic liquid crystal, and metal inorganic salt, then reacting and carrying out cycloaddition reaction.
AU XIAOHAI Z
   ZHOU X
   ZHANG H
   GONG Q
AE WUHAN AIAOLI CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2018861266
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing catalyst-based cyclic carbonate comprises taking epoxy compound and carbon dioxide as reactant, morpholine ionic liquid or ionic liquid crystal as main catalyst, and metal inorganic salt as cocatalyst, then reacting in the absence of organic solvent at 25-40 degrees C for 1-24 hours under pressure of 0.1 MPa, where molar ratio between reactant and catalyst is 100:1-10:1, then carrying out cycloaddition reaction of epoxy compound with carbon dioxide to obtain cyclic carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing catalyst-based cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The product: has simple preparation process, excellent stability and mild reaction condition; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing catalyst-based cyclic carbonate comprises taking epoxy compound and carbon dioxide as reactant, morpholine ionic liquid or ionic liquid crystal as main catalyst, and metal inorganic salt of MY as cocatalyst, then reacting in the absence of organic solvent at 25-40 degrees C for 1-24 hours under pressure of 0.1 MPa, where molar ratio between reactant and catalyst is 100:1-10:1, then carrying out cycloaddition reaction of epoxy compound with carbon dioxide to obtain cyclic carbonate.
   M = Li+, Na+, K+, Mg2+, Ca2+, Ba2+, Al3+, Sn2+, Pb2+, Cr3+, Mn2+, Fe3+, Co2+, Ni2+, Cu+, Cu2+, Zn2+, Ru3+, Pd2+, Ag+, Cd2+, Hg2+ or Hg+; and
   Y = F-, Cl-, Br-, I-, OAc-, NO3-, SO42-, BF4- or PO43-.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The morpholine ionic liquid or ionic liquid crystal is of formula (I). The molar ratio between main catalyst and cocatalyst is 10:1-1:1. The epoxy compound is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, styrene oxide, glycidol, allyl glycidyl ether, epoxypropyl phenyl ether or butyl oxirane. The molar ratio between epoxy compound and main catalyst is 100:1-10:1.R1 = H or methyl;R2 = 10-18C alkyl; andX- = Cl-, Br-, I-, BF4- or PF6-.

   CAO H
   LIU B
AE NINGXIA ZIGUANG TIANHUA METHIONINE CO (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2022421858
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of environmentally safe methionine, based on carbon dioxide acidification method, involves:
1) acidifying 5- (2-methylmercapto ethyl) -hydantoin alkaline potassium hydrolysate by carbon dioxide and separating into crude methionine and crystallization mother liquor; 2) concentrating the crystallization mother liquor, shunting partial concentrated solution when the dissociation degree of potassium bicarbonate in the system, is 0.3-0.6 and regenerating the residual concentrated solution for hydrolyzing 5- (2-methylthioethyl) -hydantoin; 3) cooling and crystallizing the split concentrated solution and separating to form filtrate and potassium bicarbonate; 4) acidifying the filtrate by carbon dioxide and separating the filtrate into a methionine crude product and an impurity enriched liquid.
   USE - The method is used for continuous production of methionine, industrially.
   ADVANTAGE - Methionine is prepared by an environment-friendly, safe and inexpensive process. An increased single yield is enabled by the process. The hydrolysis cycle is carried out more times by enriching formic acid and oil phase impurities and then respectively extracting, and effectively removing impurities in saponification liquid formed during the DL-methionine production process. The occurrence of large amount of accumulation in the continuous production process of methionine, is prevented and hence the quality of methionine is unaffected.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the process of a methionine preparation method.(Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step 1 during the methionine preparation method, the crude methionine is mixed with water and pulped, separated and dried to form methionine. The pulped water is combined with the crystallization mother liquor. In step 2, the concentration temperature of the crystallization mother liquor, is 100-180degrees Celsius.The concentration of the split concentrate is 10-30 wt.%. The remaining concentrated solution is subjected to thermal decomposition at 160-200degrees Celsius, followed by pyrolyzing potassium bicarbonate into potassium carbonate and potassium methionine for hydrolysis of 5- (2-methylthioethyl) -hydantoin.In step 3, the mass ratio of potassium methionine and water in the concentrated split solution, is a. a is such that 0.15 is less than or equal to a less than or equal to 1. In step 3, crystallization is performed by preserving heat for 30-360 minutes at 10-30degrees Celsius.Step 4 involves returning of methionine crude product to the crystallization mother liquor formed in step 1, to form secondary mother liquor which is partially extracted without methionine solid, followed by repeating steps 3to5 and returning the residual secondary mother liquor with methionine solid for acidification of hydrolysis liquid in step 1.The impurity-enriched solution is separated into methionine solution and potassium sulfate by using hydrogen type cation resin.After adjusting the pH of methionine solution to acidic, oil phase impurities are removed by using a layering mode or organic solvent extraction. The formic acid is removed by using reduced pressure distillation and finally, the methionine solution, potassium hydroxide solution and sulfuric acid solution are separated from the deacidified water phase.

PT P
PN WO2021090038-A1; HU201900375-A1
TI Converting carbon dioxide to short chain organic acids involves e.g. dissolution of silver containing fulvate chlatrate komplex catalyst in water to produce catalyst solution and charging catalyst solution into pressure reactor.
AU KOENTOES Z
   MEGDETOVICS RAHIMKULOV R
   KANTAS ZOLTAN
   RUSZLAN MEGDETOVICS RAHIMKULOV
AE IOI INVESTMENT ZRT (IOII-Non-standard)
GA 202152453K
AB    NOVELTY - Converting (p1) carbon dioxide to short chain organic acids comprise: (a) dissolution of silver containing fulvate chlatrate komplex catalyst in water to produce a catalyst solution; (b) charging the catalyst solution into a pressure reactor; (c) introducing a carbon dioxide source into the reactor; (d) exposing the reactor space with sunlight or artificial light in wavelength of 380-780 nm, while measuring the concentration of the produced organic acids; (e) repeating the steps of (c) and (d) until the concentration of the produced organic acids reaches 2-5 wt.% in the reaction mixture; (f) if the concentration of the produced organic acids reaches 2-5 wt.%, collecting the aqueous solution from the reactor, filtering and concentrating the same.
   USE - The process is useful for converting carbon dioxide to short chain organic acids.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is carried out in a purely aqueous medium is that they do not require expensive and in many cases harmful organic solvents for their industrial application.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing (p2) silver containing fulvate-clathrate complex catalyst comprising the mixing fulvic acid with a silver salt in an aqueous medium in a molar ratio of 1:1-10, preferably 1:1-5, followed by concentrating and crystallizing; and
   (2) use of silver containing fulvate-clathrate complex as photocatalytic catalyst in process for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide source comprises air, flue gas, dry ice or carbon dioxide bound in rocks and carbon dioxide bound in oceans. The reactor comprises a vertical tank or tubular reactor, a gas-liquid heterogeneous reactor, a liquid film reactor, a packed column reactor, a rotary disk reactor, plate and bubble cap columns and sprayed reactors. Silver-containing fulvate-clathrate complex catalyst is fulvic acid of formula (C24H38NkO22Sz) (I), where the silver is present in the form of a silver metal or silver iodide salt and the molar ratio of fulvic acid to silver metal and/or silver iodide is 1:1-10, preferably 1:1-5.z = less than 1.The silver is present in the complex as silver metal having the following physicochemical parameters: 7.8 wt.% silver; melting point: 226 degrees C; UV absorption (96 mu g/ml; 1 cm; water) 263, 430 nm, Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (potassium bromide pellet) (cm-1): 3384, 2927, 2359, 1642, 1383, 1152, 1077, 1023, 575. The silver is present in the complex as silver-iodide and having the following physicochemical parameters: 6.36 wt.% silver; melting point: 193 degrees C; Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy (potassium bromide pellet) (cm-1): 3306, 2920, 1714, 1415, 11147, 1009, 918, 765,575. Preferred Process: The process is carried out in the case of a tank reactor in batch, continuous, semi-continuous mode, ideally in cascade mode with isothermal, adiabatic or cooled operation. The process is carried out in the case of a tubular reactor in an isothermal, adiabatic or polytropic mode. The heating or cooling of the vertical tank or tubular reactor is carried out as required, i.e. by changing the temperature of the reaction medium using a heating mantle, and a coil or a heat exchanger. The silver content in the silver-containing fulvate-clathrate complex is present in the form of metallic silver or silver iodide. The depletion of the catalyst can be determined from the concentration gradient of the organic acids formed, where the catalyst is depleted if the concentration change of organic acids is less than 1% due to carbon dioxide introduced per unit hour compared to the previous measured value in relation to the same carbon dioxide introduced per unit hour.
   EXAMPLE - 450 ml Carbonated water (saturated aqueous solution of carbon dioxide) was charged to a 500 ml clear bottle with a screw cap and 5 mg silver-fulvate clathrate complex catalyst was added. After closing the glass bottle, it was exposed to sunlight and after 5 days, the reaction products and their amount were identified by 500 MHz 'H NMR and HPLC.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN WO2021045568-A1; KR2021028594-A; US2022193637-A1
TI Photocatalyst used for reducing carbon dioxide, comprises electron acceptor, metal catalyst, and polymer concentrator which absorbs light so that electrons are transferred from concentrator to catalyst through conduction band of acceptor.
AU SON H J
   KANG S O
   KANG S
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202124231N
AB    NOVELTY - A photocatalyst (200) comprises an electron acceptor (EA) comprising a metal oxide, a polymer concentrator (CL) deposited on the electron acceptor, and a metal catalyst (MC) deposited on the electron acceptor. When the polymer concentrator absorbs light, electrons are transferred from the polymer concentrator to the metal catalyst through a conduction band of the electron acceptor.
   USE - Photocatalyst is used for reducing carbon dioxide (claimed) from industrial machinery and transportation equipment to carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst has improved carbon dioxide reduction efficiency and stability and long lifetime.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) production of the photocatalyst, which involves preparing a source solution (S1) containing a polymer condenser, preparing a preliminary photocatalyst in which the polymer concentrator is deposited on the electron acceptor by providing the electron acceptor to solution (S1), and depositing the metal catalyst on the electron acceptor by providing the preliminary photocatalyst to a source solution (S2) containing the metal catalyst; and
   (2) reduction of carbon dioxide, which involves preparing the photocatalyst, mixing the photocatalyst with an electron donor and a photoreaction solvent, providing carbon dioxide to the photocatalyst, and irradiating light.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the photocatalyst and a preliminary photocatalyst formed during manufacture of the photocatalyst.
   Preliminary photocatalyst (100)
   Photocatalyst (200)
   Polymer concentrator (CL)
   Electron acceptor (EA)
   Metal catalyst (MC)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst further comprises an additive adsorbed on a surface of the electron acceptor to control energy level of the electron acceptor. The energy level of the electron acceptor to which the additive is adsorbed is a difference between a reduction potential (P1) of the metal catalyst and a reference range. The metal oxide is titanium dioxide, tungsten trioxide, tin oxide, or zinc oxide. The metal catalyst comprises rhenium. The photoreactive solvent comprises the additive. Preferred Property: The energy level of the electron acceptor to which the additive is adsorbed is -1.5 V to -1.8 V.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive comprises water, trifluoroethanol, or triethanolamine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polymer concentrator comprises poly-3-hexylthiophene.

The ratio (%) of number of secondary amines with respect to total of nitrogen atoms in polyethyleneimine is 25-55. The ratio (%) of number of tertiary amines with respect to total of nitrogen atoms in polyethyleneimine is 10-40. The polyethyleneimine is alkylated branched polyethyleneimine in which portion(s) of primary amine and secondary amine in branched polyethyleneimine is alkylated by 1-5C lower alkyl group. The ratio of number of lower alkyl groups bonded to nitrogen atoms by alkylation with respect to number of primary amines in branched polyethyleneimine is 0.2-1.5.
   USE - Material is used for adsorption/desorption of carbon dioxide in separation and recovery of carbon dioxide from high-temperature waste gas from power station, waste gas from factory, and waste gas from motor vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The material enables efficient adsorption/desorption of carbon dioxide at relatively high temperature of 60 degrees C and adsorption/desorption operation pressure of 1-10 kPa. The amount of polyamines supported on the carrier is appropriately maintained even after performing common pretreatments, such as vacuum-drying at 80 degrees C for 2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lower alkyl group is methyl group, ethyl group, n-propyl group, isopropyl group, n-butyl group, sec-butyl group, isobutyl group and/or tert-butyl group.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The branched polyethyleneimine has an average molecular weight of 200-10000.

PN KR2020105074-A; KR2199044-B1
TI System useful for producing calcium carbonate comprises cylinder, recovery chamber connected to discharge pipe of cylinder, and control unit connected to and controlling each of cylinder and recovery chamber.
AU JO H Y
   KIM J
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202088799S
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a cylinder (100), a recovery chamber connected to the discharge pipe of the cylinder, and a control unit connected to and controlling each of the cylinder and the recovery chamber.
   USE - The system and method are useful for producing calcium carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system and method improve reaction efficiency by utilizing supercritical carbon dioxide and high concentration calcium solution, produce precipitated calcium carbonate in short time through discharge, can easily control particle size through the control of the reaction time, and are economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing calcium carbonate, comprising (a) supplying calcium hydroxide slurry into inner space of the cylinder body (120) by a slurry supply pipe (125), heating the slurry to predetermined temperature and converting carbon dioxide into supercritical phase by pressurizing carbon dioxide to the slurry through an injection pipe; (b) reacting calcium hydroxide which is dissolved in carbon dioxide to change into supercritical phase for predetermined time while maintaining by the control unit, and (c) discharging the solution of dissolved supercritical carbon dioxide and the calcium hydroxide into a recovery chamber through a discharge pipe to recover a suspension containing carbonate crystals having rapid crystallization.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The block diagram shows a block diagram of the system in which method for producing calcium carbonate is performed.
   Cylinder (100)
   Upper opening and closing part (110)
   Cylinder body (120)
   Slurry supply pipe (125)
   Heating part (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The cylinder includes cylinder body portion having an inner space, slurry supply pipe for supplying calcium hydroxide slurry from an upper side of the cylinder body to the inner space, a heating unit provided on one side of the cylinder body, an observation unit provided on the other side of the cylinder body, discharge pipe provided under the cylinder body, an injection pipe for injecting carbon dioxide from a lower portion of the cylinder body, a temperature and pressure measuring tube provided under the cylinder body and a stirrer provided in the inner space of the cylinder body. The heating unit includes in a form of heating belt or heating jacket. The discharge pipe is in a form of cross-sectional area of end portion narrower in the inner direction of the recovery chamber than the cross-sectional area of the middle portion. The recovery chamber has a low pressure state lower than atmospheric pressure, and cooler provided at the bottom is a sealed chamber that maintains a low temperature condition lower than room temperature. Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises control unit heating the calcium hydroxide slurry at 31 degrees C through a heating unit provided on one side of the cylinder body; and pressurizing, by the control unit, the carbon dioxide at a pressure of greater than or equal to 74 bar through the injection pipe. The step (b) further comprises agitating the carbon dioxide and changing calcium hydroxide slurry into the supercritical phase using a stirrer provided in the lower center of the cylinder body. The step (c) comprises detecting by the controller, temperature and pressure of a solution in which supercritical carbon dioxide and calcium hydroxide slurry present in the inner space of the cylinder body mixed through a temperature pressure measuring tube; determining, by the control unit whether or not to discharge the mixed solution of the supercritical carbon dioxide and calcium hydroxide slurry to the recovery chamber; discharging the mixed solution through the discharge pipe to the recovery chamber at a discharge speed range of 200 to 340 ml/second; and collecting the carbon dioxide by control unit gas introduced into the recovery chamber through a collection tube.

GA 202018906K
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of gaseous constituents dissolved                in liquid reaction mixture in copolymerization of                alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide, involves (i)                expanding the reaction mixture having an absolute                pressure of 5.0-100.0 bar before the relaxation by                at least 50% of the applied pressure, (ii)                performing droplet separation with foam destruction                (d1), and (iii) performing bubble separation with                foam destruction (d2). The steps (i)-(iii) are                carried out one or more times until the obtained                reaction mixture has an absolute pressure of 0.01                to less than 5.00 bar.
   USE - Separation of gaseous constituents e.g. carbon                dioxide and residual alkylene oxide dissolved in                liquid reaction mixture during manufacture of                polyether carbonate polyol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient, energy-saving                and economical separation of gaseous constituents                dissolved in liquid reaction mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of polyether carbonate polyol, which                involves bonding alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide                to hydrogen-functional starter substance at 50-150                degrees C for 1.0-20.0 hours in a reactor in the                presence of a double metal cyanide catalyst or a                metal complex catalyst comprising zinc and/or                cobalt metals to obtain a reaction mixture                comprising the polyether carbonate polyol,                continuously transferring the obtained reaction                mixture to a post-reactor and reducing free                alkylene oxide in the reaction mixture, and                separating off carbon dioxide and any dissolved                residual alkylene oxide by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The reaction mixture                  has a temperature of 60-150 degrees C before the                  expansion in process step (i). Preferred Process:                  The droplet separation is carried out by                  centrifugal forces. The step (iii) is carried out                  using coalescer comprising at least one fabric                  element.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Composition: The fabric element is                  a metal knitted fabric. Preferred Properties: The                  fabric element has a density of 600-1200 kg/m3,                  preferably 800-1050 kg/m3.

PN US2019321773-A1; US10639584-B2
TI System for capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixture, comprises lean solvent, absorber containing portion of lean solvent, stripper configured to receive rich solvent stream, cross-exchanger fluidly coupled to rich solvent.
AU MATHIAS P M
   REDDY S
   YONKOSKI J
AE FLUOR TECHNOLOGIES CORP (FLUO-C)
GA 201988284Y
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises a lean solvent, an absorber containing portion of the lean solvent, a stripper configured to receive the rich solvent stream, a cross-exchanger fluidly coupled to a rich solvent outlet on the absorber and a rich solvent inlet on the stripper, a reboiler fluidly coupled to a lower portion of the stripper, and a condenser fluidly coupled to a vapor outlet of the stripper. The lean solvent comprises 3-amino-1-propanol, 2-dimethylamino-2-methyl-1-propanol and water. The absorber is configured to receive the lean solvent and a gaseous stream comprising carbon dioxide, contact the lean solvent with the gaseous stream, and produce a rich solvent stream and a gaseous stream depleted in carbon dioxide. The reboiler is configured to generate a vapor stream from the rich solvent and pass the vapor stream back to the stripper.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide from gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has enhanced efficiency by exchanging heat from the lean solvent into the rich solvent, and reduced total energy consumption for the absorption-stripping process and high reaction rate by utilizing the combination of 3-amino-1-propanol and 2-dimethylamino-2-methyl-1-propanol. The aqueous carbon dioxide absorbent comprising lean solvent shows better reaction kinetics, absorption capacity, and low energy requirement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The lean solvent comprises approximately 5-45 wt.%, preferably 10 wt.% 3-amino-1-propanol and 5-55 wt.%, preferably 40 wt.% 2-dimethylamino-2-methyl-1-propanol, where ratio of dimethylamino-2-methyl-1-propanol and 3-amino-1-propanol is 1:11-5:1.

The upper bubbling bed is connected to a transporting bed. The lower bubbling bed comprises an air distribution plate for placing carbon dioxide adsorbent. A central air duct is arranged in a middle of an air distribution plate, where the air distribution plate is commonly provided with a raising pipe. The raising pipe and the central air duct are coaxially arranged together to form a gap between an air outlet of the central air duct and an inlet of the raising pipe. A material returning valve is arranged on an overflow pipe.
   USE - Multi-stage serial bubbling bed-transporting bed overlaying carbon dioxide adsorption reactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor can control gas- solid contact time to adjust the circulating flow rate of the adsorbent, and effectively suppress gas-solid counter-current back mixing, reduces carbonation difference of the adsorbent and ensures efficient and stable operation of de-carbonization system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a multi-stage serial bubbling bed-transporting bed overlaying carbon dioxide adsorption reactor utilizing method.
