PT P
PN CN108423744-A
TI Reducing nitrate nitrogen in water by photoinduced formic acid and hydrogen peroxide to generate carbon dioxide radical used for nitratenitrogen removal of groundwater and municipal sewage, involves adding hydrogen peroxide and formic acid.
AU LIU J
   ZHOU J
   XU Y
   CHEN J
   ZHOU Q
AE UNIV SHANGHAI (USHN-C)
GA 201866739N
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing nitrate nitrogen in water by photoinduced formic acid and hydrogen peroxide to generate carbon dioxide radical involves adding hydrogen peroxide and formic acid to a molar ratio of (0.1 to 1.1):1 to the aqueous solution containing nitrate nitrogen. The concentration of nitrate in the aqueous solution containing nitrate nitrogen is 20-400 mg N/l. The aqueous solution is thoroughly mixed to form a reactant system solution in which the concentration of hydrogen peroxide in the reactant system solution is not less than 53.0 mM. The high-pressure mercury lamp with a power of 200-600 W as a light source of the light-inducing system is used, irradiating the reactant system solution with ultraviolet light emitted from a high-pressure mercury lamp under a non-oxidizing atmosphere to make the reactant. The system solution is subjected to a redox reaction for 60 to 180 minutes to generate carbon dioxide radicals to reduce nitrate nitrogen in the water.
   USE - Method for reducing nitrate nitrogen in water by photoinduced formic acid and hydrogen peroxide to generate carbon dioxide radical used for nitratenitrogen removal of groundwater and municipal sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce nitrate nitrogen in water by photoinduced formic acid and hydrogen peroxide to generate carbon dioxide radical in a simple and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: The initial pH of the reaction system solution is 2-11. The initial pH of the reaction system solution is adjusted to 2-3 or 5-11. The hydrogen chloride solution or sodium hydroxide solution are used to adjust the initial pH of the reaction system solution. The mol ratio of hydrogen peroxide and formic acid is 0.5-1.1:1. The concentration of nitrate in the aqueous solution containing nitrate is reduced to 20-100mgN/l.The redox reaction is carried out for 120-180 minutes. Preferred Components: The nitrate in the aqueous solution of nitrate nitrogen is any one of potassium nitrate, ammonium nitrate and sodium nitrate.

PT P
PN US2018228398-A1; US10383546-B2
TI Method for determining whether additional medical tests need to be conducted for diagnosis of pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) condition in patient, involves providing generated signal to indicator.
AU HEMNES A R
   NEWMAN A
   NEWMAN J
AE UNIV VANDERBILT (UVAN-C)
GA 201863452Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining that the patient is at rest. A partial pressure of carbon dioxide in a composite gas measured. The expiration from the mouth of patient is transferred to a carbon dioxide analyzer. The end tidal carbon dioxide (EtCO2) from each of oral expirations from the patient at rest is measured (2402). A composite EtCO2 value in response to the oral expirations from the patient at rest is calculated (2404). The composite EtCO2 value is compared (2408) to a range of stored carbon dioxide partial pressure values. A signal indicates whether additional medical tests need to be conducted in response to the comparing is generated (2410). The generated signal is provided (2412) to an indicator which is adapted to respond to the generated signal to provide an output in a human cognizable format.
   USE - Method for determining whether additional medical tests need to be conducted for diagnosis of pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) condition in patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The device includes superior algorithms that can reduce alarms, improves workflow and can provide clinical utility for improved patient safety.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the method for diagnosing pulmonary arterial hypertension.
   Step for measuring EtCo2 (2402)
   Step for calculating composite EtCO2 value (2404)
   Step for comparing composite EtCO2 value to range of stored carbon dioxide partial pressure values (2408)
   Step for generating signal (2410)
   Step for providing generated signal to indicator (2412)

AB    NOVELTY - The high space-time resolution CO2 flux                inversion method involves collecting carbon dioxide                observation data through a carbon dioxide (CO2)                observation station. A CO2 concentration set                pre-reporting field is obtained on an observation                station. The CO2 observation data collected is used                as an observation variable of a set square root                filter assimilation model. The CO2 observation data                is taken as the observation variable as a set of                the CO2 emission data collected as the set of CO2                emission data. A joint analysis vector is obtained.                The CO2 analysis field is represented as a CO2 flux                analysis field. A pre-reported CO2 flux set is                utilized as a discharge source in a next time set                forecast CO2 pollution concentration field.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a CO flux                inversion system with high space-time                resolution.

TI Low-cost W/C reverse micelle comprises lipophilic            particles, anionic surfactant, carbon dioxide,            deionized water, acrylic acid, sodium hydroxide,            ammonium persulfate and            N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide.
AU YAO Y
   FAN R
AE SUZHOU HUMBERT ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202210437W
AB    NOVELTY - Low-cost W/C reverse micelle comprises 2-20                wt.% lipophilic particles, 0.2-3 wt.% anionic                surfactant, 40-70 wt.% carbon dioxide, 5-15 wt.%                deionized water, 5-20 wt.% acrylic acid, 3-12 wt.%                sodium hydroxide, 0.05-0.5 wt.% ammonium persulfate                and 0.05-0.5 wt.%                N,N'-Methylenebisacrylamide.
   USE - Used as low-cost W/C reverse micelle.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-cost W/C reverse micelle: preparation                method avoids the use of fluorine-containing and                silicon-containing high-cost surfactants and the                complex and difficult surface modification process                of Pickering emulsifiers, use of a large amount of                water and organic solvents and is                environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                reversed-phase Pickering emulsion polymerization of                low-cost W/C reverse micelle, comprising (i) adding                the lipophilic particles and anionic surfactant                into 20 ml high pressure visible reaction kettle,                pumping carbon dioxide and starting the magnetic                stirring at 800-1500 revolutions/minute; (ii)                taking acrylic acid monomer, neutralizing it with                sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, adding initiator                ammonium persulfate and cross-linking agent                N,N'-methylenebisacrylamide into the aqueous                solution, where the mass ratio of carbon dioxide                and water is 8:3-14:1; (iii) injecting the aqueous                solution slowly into the high pressure visible                reaction kettle by a high pressure injection pump                and stirring magnetically for 800-1500                revolutions/minute to form the carbon dioxide                coating aqueous emulsion; (iv) heating the reaction                kettle to 30-60degrees Celsius, controlling the                pressure of the polymer kettle at 3-40 MPa,                magnetic stirring for 800- 1500 revolutions/minute                and polymerizing for 2-12 hours; and (v) cooling to                room temperature, releasing the pressure, opening                the reaction kettle to take out the gel particles,                washing with methanol for many times, and drying at                50-100degrees Celsius to constant weight to obtain                white granular resin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The low-cost W/C                  reverse micelle comprises 5 wt.% lipophilic                  particles, 0.7 wt.% anionic surfactant, 50 wt.%                  carbon dioxide, 12 wt.% deionized water, 20 wt.%                  acrylic acid, 12 wt.% sodium hydroxide, 0.1 wt.%                  ammonium persulfate and 0.2 wt.%                  N,N'-Methylenebisacrylamide.Preferred Method: The magnetic stirring in                  the step (i) and (iii) is 1300                  revolutions/minute, in the step (ii), the mass                  ratio of carbon dioxide and water is 4:1, the                  heating of the reaction kettle in the step (iv)                  is carried out at 50degrees Celsius, controlling                  the pressure of the polymer kettle to 21.2MPa,                  carrying out polymerization for 5 hours and the                  drying in the step (v) is carried out at                  80degrees Celsius to constant weight obtains a                  white granular resin with a yield of 90.2%.

PN WO2021162799-A1; AU2021221075-A1; CA3165520-A1; KR2022147094-A
TI System for storing intermittent, renewable            electrical energy has fossil-fueled power plant with            carbon dioxide capture subsystem that carbonizes liquid            electrolyte solution, decarbonizing subsystem, and            thermal management subsystem.
AU MACEDA J P
AE MACEDA J P (MACE-Individual)
   MACEDA J (MACE-Individual)
GA 202195736C
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises fossil-fueled power plant                with post-combustion, carbon dioxide capture                subsystem that carbonizes an acidic, basic, or                buffer, liquid electrolyte solution; decarbonizing                subsystem that regenerates carbonized electrolyte                solution; thermal management subsystem that                integrates power plant waste heat and/or external                heat sources with the thermal needs of the other                subsystems; power management subsystem that                integrates and optimizes all internal electrical                needs with external supplies and loads; and control                system to manage all of the above listed subsystems                and respond to varying internal and external                demands, interruptions, and events.
   USE - A system for storing intermittent, renewable                electrical energy for later use (claimed). Can be                used for improving the efficiencies of power and                other industrial process plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves the efficiency of a                process plant and power generation by                polygeneration of electricity, heat, hydrocarbons,                and oxygen. These products, derived from the same                fossil-fuel feedstock, create more high value                product to sell, increasing the gross income of the                power plant and to more efficiently use the                original fossil-fuel feedstock. Post-combustion                carbon dioxide emissions and atmospheric carbon                dioxide are captured and used as reactant                feedstock, reducing or eliminating the carbon                footprint of the emitting process plant and                reducing that of the final products. Pre-combustion                carbon content of the primary fossil fuel input, as                well as atmospheric carbon dioxide, are captured                and used as reactant feedstock, reducing or                eliminating the carbon footprint of the emitting                process plant and reducing that of the final                products. Generated plant waste heat, along with                available thermal energy from non-fossil-fuel heat                sources, are integrated internally to supplement                the thermal energy needed for driving the                hydrocarbon synthesis reaction. Generated plant                electrical energy, along with non fossil-fuel                electrical energy sources, are integrated                internally to supplement the electrical energy                needed for driving the hydrocarbon synthesis                reaction. The system enables cost-competitive,                long-distance transport of hydrogen, and storage of                surplus intermittent renewable electrical sources,                such as wind and solar.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: Carbon dioxide capture                  is performed in one stage of carbonate to                  bicarbonate. Carbon capture is performed in two                  stages, i.e. first stage captures 50% in                  hydroxide to carbonate and 2nd captures the                  remainder in the newly formed carbonate to                  bicarbonate. Thermal decarbonization using steam                  regenerates the bicarbonate to carbonate and                  carbon dioxide, which can be vented, collected,                  sold, and/or sequestered. Decarbonization is                  performed in an electrochemical cell using                  electrical and/or thermal input to strip                  bicarbonate of oxygen at one electrode and                  hydrocarbons and/or oxygenated hydrocarbons at                  the other, where one or both of these species are                  exported for off-site sale or use.                  Decarbonization is performed in an                  electrochemical cell using electrical and/or                  thermal input to strip the bicarbonate of oxygen                  at one electrode and hydrocarbons and/or                  oxygenated hydrocarbons at the other, where one                  or both of these species are recycled for                  internal plant use. Preferred Component:                  Fossil-fueled or supplied industrial process                  plant includes steel, aluminum, cement, paper,                  fertilizer, petrochemical, hydrogen, etc.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Processes: A portion of the                  hydrocarbons produced are used to make the                  electricity needed to drive the process, and the                  balance are exported for off-site sale or use.                  Hydrocarbons produced are used to make the                  electricity needed to drive the process, and the                  balance are recycled for internal plant use. A                  portion of the power plant output provides the                  electricity needed to drive the process, and the                  products are exported for off-site sale or use. A                  portion of the power plant output provides the                  electricity needed to drive the process and the                  products are recycled for internal plant use.                  Imported electricity provides the electricity                  needed to drive the process and the products are                  exported for off-site sale or use. Imported                  electricity provides the electricity needed to                  drive the process and the products are recycled                  for internal plant use.

PT P
PN CN112142804-A
TI Preparing high-purity decitabine involves dissolving decitabine Intermediate in a solvent, dissolving mixture of decitabine and alpha-isomer of decitabine in alcohol solvent, and reacting in supercritical fluid carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG G
   BAI W
   ZHU Y
AE LUNAN PHARM CO LTD (LNZY-C)
GA 202103118L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity decitabine involves dissolving decitabine Intermediate (I) in a solvent, removing the protective group in the presence of organic base. The solvent is evaporated under reduced pressure after the reaction, and the residue is re-dissolved and heated under reflux for extraction and filtered. The filtrate is evaporated under reduced pressure to remove the solvent, and a mixture of decitabine and alpha-isomer of decitabine is obtained. The mixture of decitabine and alpha-isomer of decitabine are dissolved in an alcohol solvent, in an autoclave, and liquid carbon dioxide is pumped into it. The temperature is gradually increased to make the closed system reach the supercritical state. After a period of time, the system temperature is reduced, and the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide is ejected from the nozzle after the system pressure drops to 5 megaPascal, and the solid decitabine is collected at the nozzle.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity decitabine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for industrial production of Decitabine with simple operation process, high separation efficiency, high product yield and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing high-purity decitabine involves dissolving decitabine Intermediate of formula (I) in a solvent, removing the protective group in the presence of organic base. The solvent is evaporated under reduced pressure after the reaction, and the residue is re-dissolved and heated under reflux for extraction and filtered. The filtrate is evaporated under reduced pressure to remove the solvent, and a mixture of decitabine and alpha-isomer of decitabine is obtained. The mixture of decitabine and alpha-isomer of decitabine are dissolved in an alcohol solvent, in an autoclave, and liquid carbon dioxide is pumped into it. The temperature is gradually increased to make the closed system reach the supercritical state. After a period of time, the system temperature is reduced, and the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide is ejected from the nozzle after the system pressure drops to 5 megaPascal, and the solid decitabine is collected at the nozzle..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent for dissolving Decitabine Intermediate of formula (I) is pyridine. The organic base mentioned is triethylamine. The solvent for reflux extraction is anhydrous methanol. The alcohol solvent is selected from methanol, ethanol or isopropanol, preferably anhydrous methanol. Preferred Conditions: The mass-volume ratio (g/mL) of decitabine Intermediate of formula (I) and pyridine is 1:2-3. The mass-volume ratio (g/mL) of Decitabine Intermediate of formula (I) and triethylamine is 1:2-3. The reaction temperature in step a is 20-30 degrees C. The feed mass volume ratio (g/mL) of the residue and the anhydrous methanol is 1:10-15, preferably 1:12. When performing reflux extraction, activated carbon is also added for decolorization, where the mass ratio (g/g) of the residue to activated carbon is 1:1 to 1.5. The reflux extraction time is 2-6 hours. The feed mass volume ratio of the decitabine and alpha-isomer mixture of decitabine and the alcohol solvent is 1:5-7 g/mL, preferably 1:6 g/mL. The temperature when reaching the supercritical state is 50-55 degrees C, and the system pressure is 50-70 megaPascal, and the supercritical reaction is performed for 0.5-2 hours. The cooling method of the system temperature involves reducing the temperature in the kettle to 30 degrees C within 10 minutes, and then gradually cooling to 10 degrees C, or natural cooling to 10 degrees C, or program cooling down slowly to 10 degrees C.

TI Reclaiming apparatus for carbon dioxide recovery            apparatus, has control device which determines ending            timing of absorption-agent recovery process that            recovers steam containing absorption agent by heating            liquid with heating device.
AU MIYAMOTO O
   KAMIJO T
   TSUJIUCHI T
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
GA 202029320X
AB    NOVELTY - The reclaiming apparatus (10) has an                absorption-liquid supply line (54) that is provided                for supplying the container with an absorption                liquid containing an absorption agent. A water                supply line (58) is provided for supplying the                container with water. A steam discharge line (62)                is provided for discharging steam generated from                the container. A heating device (52) is provided                for heating a liquid containing the water or the                absorption liquid. A control device (80) is                configured to determine an ending timing of an                absorption-agent recovery process which recovers                the steam containing the absorption agent through                the steam discharge line by heating the liquid with                the heating device based on the temperature of the                liquid stored in the container.
   USE - Reclaiming apparatus for reclaiming of                absorption liquid, for use in carbon dioxide                recovery apparatus (claimed) used for recovering                carbon dioxide contained in exhaust gas from power                generation facility.
   ADVANTAGE - The ending timing of the absorption agent                recovery process of recovering steam containing the                absorption agent is determined easily and quickly,                on the basis of the temperature of the liquid                heated by the heating device. Thus, reclaiming of                the absorption liquid is performed more                efficiently. The absorption agent recovery process                is ended after sufficiently decreasing the                concentration of the absorption agent in the                solvent inside the container by appropriately                setting the predetermined temperature. Thus, loss                of the absorption liquid is suppressed by                reclaiming effectively. The target concentration of                the absorption agent is achieved by changing the                first predetermined temperature, even if the                concentration of the absorption agent at the time                of operation start is different.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide recovery apparatus;                and
   (2) a reclaiming method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                reclaiming apparatus in carbon dioxide recovery                apparatus.

   Heating device (52)

PT P
PN CN109269727-A
TI Rubber glove leak detecting device has controller whose input ends are respectively associated with air pressure sensors and liquid level sensor that are electrically connected, and whose output end is connected to electromagnetic valve.
AU PENG Q
AE PENG Q (PENG-Individual)
GA 201911808Y
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a vacuum mechanism (3) which includes a vacuum pump and a first air pipe, and the vacuum pump is fixed to a back side of detection box. A control box (4) includes a controller inside and an alarm light outside. A gas supply mechanism (5) includes a carbon dioxide gas tank and a second gas pipe, and the carbon dioxide gas tank is fixed to other side of back side of detection box. One end of a second air pipe is in communication with the carbon dioxide gas tank, and the other end of second air pipe is connected to second connecting pipe. The input ends of controller are respectively associated with first air pressure sensor, second air pressure sensor and the liquid level sensor that are electrically connected, and the output end of controller is respectively connected to first electromagnetic valve. The solenoid valve, the vacuum pump, the screw motor and the warning lamp, the external power source provides a working voltage for the device case.
   USE - Rubber glove leak detecting device.
   ADVANTAGE - The rubber glove leak detecting device firstly draws the inside of the rubber glove into a vacuum, and then introduces compressed carbon dioxide into the inside, and the lifting mechanism dipped the rubber glove into the clarified lime water of the water tank. The carbon dioxide can enter the clarified lime water through the leak point and clarifies, when the rubber glove is damaged. The lime water becomes turbid, very intuitive, and can be observed by the naked eye. The detection process is simplified, and the detection efficiency is improved, while ensuring the detection accuracy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the external structure of rubber glove leak detecting device.

TI Container for decreasing pulsating of supercritical carbon dioxide flow at e.g. hydraulic pump, has container body provided with inlet port that is fixed with barrel, and induction pipe fixing inlet port, outlet port and chambers together.
AU LEE J H
   KIM S Y
   KIM S J
   SON S W
AE DAEJOO MACHINERY CO LTD (DAEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201878378K
AB    NOVELTY - The container (100) has a container body (110) provided with an inlet port (111) that is fixed with a barrel. Upper end portion and lower end portion of the barrel are shut tightly in an upper end. An outlet port (112) is prepared in a lower part of the container body. Baffle plates (120, 130) are installed in an inner side of the container body and mark off the inner side of the container body with multiple chambers (R1-R3). Induction pipes (140, 150) fix the inlet port, the outlet port and the chambers together. First baffle plate is installed at an upper part of a center of an inner side of the container body. Second baffle plate is installed at a lower part of the center of inner side of the container body. An internal space of the container body is segmented by first and second chambers and prepared in a lower part of the baffle plates of the container body. Third chamber is prepared in an upper portion of the first baffle plate.
   USE - Container for decreasing pulsating of supercritical carbon dioxide flow at a hydraulic pump and a reciprocating compressor utilized in an electron regulating vehicle braking system.
   ADVANTAGE - The container minimizes pressure loss in process for reducing pulsating of the supercritical carbon dioxide flow due to speed change, facilitates stable fluid flow and prevents a supercritical state from being destroyed. The container divides turbulent energy into small whirlpool with structural shape finally and converts the turbulent energy into the internal energy due to viscosity effect so as to decrease pressure ripples within the pressure container.

PN CN108613896-A; CN108613896-B
TI Method for detecting carbon content of fly ash in            coal-fired power generation boiler, involves sucking            gas in burning furnace into sealed container and            measuring carbon dioxide content in container by carbon            dioxide concentration detector.
AU ZHANG J
   DU L
   FENG X
   WU Y
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 2018803394
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding (A) solid particle                i.e. addition aluminum oxide solid particles in                solid particle container into inside of inner                cylinder of burning container. The burning                container is placed (B) on vibration platform and                shaken for 3-5 minutes. The burning container is                weighed (C). The fly ash is introduced (D) into                respective ash inlets from the sampling port. The                burning container is again placed (E) on vibration                platform and shaken for 3-5 minutes. The burning                container is transported (F) to the drying furnace                and nitrogen is continuously introduced into drying                furnace. The burning container is weighed (G). The                burning container is transported (H) to burning                furnace and oxygen is continuously introduced into                the burning furnace. The gas in burning furnace is                sucked into sealed container by vacuum pump. The                carbon dioxide content in the sealed container is                measured (I) by the carbon dioxide concentration                detector, and transmited to a central control unit                for calculation.
   USE - Method for detecting carbon content of fly ash                in coal-fired power generation boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The defects of low measurement accuracy and                reliability of the coal-fired power generation                boiler fly ash carbon content detection technology                is overcome. The accuracy and the reliability of                measurement in the carbon content of the fly ash                are improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                the method for detecting carbon content of fly ash                in a coal-fired power generation boiler. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Step for solid particle addition (A)
   Step for vibrating burning container                (B)
   Step for weighing burning container                (C)
   Step for adding fly ash (D)
   Step for secondary vibration of burning                container (E)
   Step for drying burning container (F)
   Step for secondary weighing of burning                container (G)
   Step for burning (H)
   Step for detection of carbon dioxide content                (I)

PT P
PN CN110465654-A; CN110465654-B
TI Preparation of additive involves melting electrolytic manganese metal flakes, refining, passing pure carbon dioxide gas and manganese liquid into atomizer, feeding manganese into venturi duster, spraying into chamber and packaging.
AU WANG Z
   WANG G
   YANG J
AE WANG Z (WANG-Individual)
   XIANGXI FENGDA ALLOY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201999067G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of additive involves melting electrolytic manganese metal flakes, refining to remove upper scum, passing pure carbon dioxide gas and manganese liquid into atomizer, feeding the ultra-fine manganese metal powder into the venturi duster, spraying into the spray chamber, coagulating into powder, recycling the manganese metal powder, accelerating, dispersing the aluminum powder in the hot manganese powder particles, melting, cooling, pumping into mixer from the separator, mixing aluminum with manganese to obtain manganese mixed powder, adding the binder and mold releasing agent, mixing evenly, pressing the powder by forging press or roller press to obtain 200-800 g product having density of 3.8-4.5 g/cm3, packing in kraft paper bags or cartons. The mass ratio of manganese metal and carbon dioxide gas in the atomization chamber is 8-20:1 and the mass ratio of manganese metal and aluminum powder in the atomization chamber is 10-12:1.
   USE - Preparation of additive.
   ADVANTAGE - The process economically produces high-performance manganese additive in high yield by short method. The ultra-fine manganese metal powder can be reused for the production of manganese powder.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of additive involves melting electrolytic manganese metal flakes by placing in an induction melting furnace, controlling the melting temperature to 1350-1500 degrees C, refining molten manganese liquid to remove upper scum, passing pure carbon dioxide gas into atomizer, sucking the molten manganese liquid into the atomizer, atomizing into small droplets in the atomization chamber, passing carbon dioxide gas into venturi duster, feeding the ultra-fine manganese metal powder into the venturi duster, spraying into the spray chamber, performing collision and adsorption with atomized manganese droplets, coagulating into powder, recycling the manganese metal powder, accelerating, adjusting and controlling the temperature of the atomizing chamber to 600-750 degrees C using cooling coil and water cooling jacket, controlling the carbon dioxide gas pressure to 0.8-4 MPa, placing the hot manganese metal powder at the bottom of the atomization chamber, passing the carbon dioxide gas into the venturi duster to suck the aluminum powder, passing into the manganese metal powder, blowing out the spray chamber together into the cooler, dispersing the aluminum powder in the hot manganese powder particles, melting, cooling to normal temperature, draining from the lower portion of the cooler to the mixer, feeding the powdery gas into primary separator from the upper outlet of the cooler, then into secondary separator, and feeding into dust collector, pumping into mixing mixer from the separator, feeding the ultra-fine powder from the discharge port of the secondary separator and discharge port of the dust collector into atomization chamber through venturi duster, cooling the exhaust gas at the outlet of the dust collector, pressurizing, returning to the atomization chamber with carbon dioxide gas, mixing aluminum with manganese to obtain manganese mixed powder, adding the binder and mold releasing agent, mixing evenly, pressing the powder by forging press or roller press to obtain 200-800 g product having density of 3.8-4.5 g/cm3, packing in kraft paper bags or cartons. The mass ratio of manganese metal and carbon dioxide gas in the atomization chamber is 8-20:1 and the mass ratio of manganese metal and aluminum powder in the atomization chamber is 10-12:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The manganese metal is prepared by using electrolytic manganese metal flakes and atomized with gas. The binder comprises one or mixture of ethylene glycol diacetate and propylene glycol stearate. The mold releasing agent is zinc stearate. Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide gas comprises a mixture of 99.9% or more carbon dioxide and less than 0.01% oxygen. The atomizing is carried out under an atmosphere comprising less than 0.05% oxygen. The additive comprises 90-95 %mass manganese metal, 4-10 %mass aluminum metal, 0.8 %mass binder, 0.2 %mass mold releasing agent. The manganese metal powder comprises 99.7 %mass or more manganese, 0.1 %mass or less oxygen, 0.01- %mass or less selenium and 0.1 %mass or less sum of iron and silicon. The aluminum metal comprises 99.8 %mass or more aluminum. Preferred Property: The aluminum metal has median diameter particle size (D50) of 140 mu m.

PN US11305230-B1
TI System that is used for capturing large scale carbon dioxide directly from atmospheric air, slab having flat top surface, either of rectangular or elliptical shape defined by length and width, or by radius, and perimeter wall bounding slab.
AU VAALER E G
   KESHNER M S
AE VAALER E G (VAAL-Individual)
   KESHNER M S (KESH-Individual)
GA 202251718V
AB    NOVELTY - System has slab having a flat top surface, either of rectangular or elliptical shape defined by a length and a width, or by a radius, and having an area greater than 400 square meter. The perimeter wall bounding the slab and projecting above the flat top surface of the slab, one or more rotary fans, having a combined area sufficient to provide air flow to the entire slab, each fan being configured to generate uniform, high volume, low velocity, turbulent air flow over the surface of the slab. The transport system operable to transfer powder comprising mixture of air and a particulate material to and from all locations on the slab or on mesh screen located above the slab, the transport system comprising either vacuum/deposition system comprising one or more vacuum/deposition heads, or conveyor system comprising one or more conveyors. The kiln is closed to outside air and configured to heat particulate material, after delivery to the kiln by the vacuum/deposition or conveyor system.
   USE - System that is used for capturing large scale carbon dioxide directly from atmospheric air (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system used for capturing carbon dioxide directly from the air on a huge scale at very cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for capturing large scale carbon dioxide directly from atmospheric air, which involves: (a)creating a receptacle of large area, where the receptacle comprises a slab having a flat top surface, and a perimeter wall bounding the slab and projecting above the flat top surface of the slab;(b)depositing by pumping or conveying, a powder comprising a mixture of air and a particulate material of a first chemical composition, capable of absorbing carbon dioxide, as a layer either on the flat top surface of the slab, or on a screen positioned above the slab;(c)providing a uniform, high volume, low velocity, turbulent air flow to expose the particulate material in the deposited layer to overlying air, such that the particulate material absorbs carbon dioxide from the air;(d)transferring by pumping or conveying, the powder from the slab to and from a kiln, closed to outside air;(e)heating the particulate material in the kiln (106) such that carbon dioxide previously absorbed by the particulate material in the powder is released;(f)compressing the released carbon dioxide for transport to a storage location;(g) pumping or moving by one or more conveyors, from the kiln to the slab, an output that comprises either the heated particulate material mixed with air or a processed version of the heated particulate material mixed with air, the processing being carried out within the kiln, and depositing the output either on the flat top surface of the slab or on a screen positioned above the slab, where the method is fully operable while the receptacle and any layer of powder present on or above the top surface of the slab of the receptacle are exposed to atmospheric air and weather in an outdoor environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a method for capturing large scale carbon dioxide directly from atmospheric air.
   Fan (102)
   Concrete slab (103)
   Pumped back (104)
   Kiln (106)
   Steam (107)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particulate material is of a first chemical composition, capable of absorbing carbon dioxide, it is an oxide or hydroxide of a metal, and after the particulate material has absorbed carbon dioxide, it is a carbonate of the metal, where the metal includes calcium, sodium, aluminum, and iron.

PN CN113731135-A; CN113731135-B
TI Amino acid waste liquid for preparing carbon dioxide scavenger for fertilizer, comprises obtaining inorganic strong alkali water solution, organic amine amino acid aqueous solution, metal salt solution, and water-soluble alcohol modified amino acid solution.
AU SU W
   SU B
AE BEIJING JINXIU NEW TECHNOLOGY DEV CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021E64846
AB    NOVELTY - Amino acid waste liquid for preparing carbon dioxide scavenger for fertilizer, involves (a) obtaining an amino acid salt aqueous solution, (b) reacting the organic amine aqueous solution with the second amino acid waste liquid to obtain an organic amine amino acid aqueous solution, (c) obtaining amino acid trace element chelate, (d) reacting the alcohol solution with the fourth amino acid waste liquid to prepare a water-soluble alcohol-modified amino acid solution, (e) sequentially mixing the solutions obtained from step (a) to step (d) into modified amino acid and amino acid salt mixture, adding inorganic salt modified activator solution, and homogenizing to obtain inorganic salt modified activated amino acid salt solution, and (e) adding sugar source and carbon source, mixing, adding the composite microbial inoculum and sealing to form the composite microbial inoculant compound amino acid salt solution, to obtain a carbon dioxide scavenger for fertilizers.
   USE - The amino acid waste liquid is useful for preparing carbon dioxide scavenger for fertilizer for preventing plant diseases and insect pests, sterilizing and killing virus, and promoting the function of plant growth. The scavenger is used as a fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The amino acid waste liquid has excellent biodegradability and low toxicity, and prevents plant diseases and insect pests, sterilizing and sterilizing, and promotes the function of plant growth. The photosynthetic efficiency of the plant leaves is improved. The carbon dioxide scavenger has the function of efficiently capturing carbon dioxide, and simultaneously can be used as a fertilizer by itself or after adsorbing saturated carbon dioxide, and can kill viruses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Amino acid waste liquid for preparing carbon dioxide scavenger for fertilizer, involves (a) mixing and reacting the inorganic strong alkali aqueous solution with the first amino acid waste liquid to obtain an amino acid salt aqueous solution, (b) reacting the organic amine aqueous solution with the second amino acid waste liquid to obtain an organic amine amino acid aqueous solution, (c) reacting the metal salt solution capable of dissociating trace element metal ions with the third amino acid waste liquid to obtain amino acid trace element chelate, (d) reacting the alcohol solution with the fourth amino acid waste liquid to prepare a water-soluble alcohol-modified amino acid solution, (e) sequentially mixing the solutions obtained from step (a) to step (d) 45-55 wt.%, 20-30 wt.%, 10-25 wt.% and 5-20 wt.% into modified amino acid and amino acid salt mixture, using amine substances to adjust pH, adding inorganic salt modified activator solution according to 0.1-1% of the modified amino acid and amino acid salt mixture ratio, and homogenizing to obtain inorganic salt modified activated amino acid salt solution, and (e) adding sugar source and carbon source to the inorganic salt modified activated amino acid salt solution in step (e), evenly stirring and mixing, adding the composite microbial inoculum and sealing at room temperature to form the composite microbial inoculant compound amino acid salt solution, and adjusting the pH value with amine substances, so as to obtain a carbon dioxide scavenger for fertilizers.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: From step (a) to step (f), the pH of the solution is adjusted to 6.5-8.5. From step (a) to step (f), the reaction temperature of the solution is 50-80degrees Celsius.Preferred Composition: The amino acid waste liquid includes glycine, alanine, arginine and proline.Preferred Components: The composite microbial inoculum is two or more of effective microorganisms (EM) bacteria or Bacillus subtilis, Bacillus cereus, Bacillus cereus var. mycoides variant, Bacillus licheniformis, Bacillus megaterium, Paenibacillus polymyxa, Bacillus velezensis, Bacillus pumilus, Bacillus stearothermophilus, and Bacillus thuringiensis.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (a), the molar ratio of the molar content of the inorganic strong base in the inorganic strong base aqueous solution to the molar content of the amino acid in the first portion of the amino acid waste liquid is (2:1)-(1:2). In step (b), the molar ratio of the molar amount of the organic amine in the organic amine aqueous solution to the molar amount of the amino acid in the second amino acid waste liquid is (1:1)-(1:3). In step (c), the molar ratio of the amino acid molar amount in the second amino acid waste liquid to the trace element metal ion is (2:1)-(1:3). In step (d), the molar ratio of the hydrogen bond donor in the alcohol substance to the amino acid in the fourth amino acid waste liquid is (4:1)-(8:1).Preferred Components: In step (b), the organic amines in the organic amine aqueous solution are one ore more of monoethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), triethanolamine (TEA), 3-methylaminopropylamine, N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA), piperazine (PZ), N,N-diethylethanolamine (DEEA), N-ethylethanolamine and 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanolamine (AMP). In step (d), the alcohol solution is one or more of methanol, 1-propanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol, glycerol, isopropanol, 2-methyl 1-propanol, 1-pentanol, 1-butanol or triacontanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (c), the metal salt in the metal salt solution that can dissociate trace element metal ions is two or more of magnesium sulfate heptahydrate, magnesium nitrate trihydrate, calcium hydroxide, copper sulfate pentahydrate, copper chloride, boric acid, sodium tetraborate, sodium octaborate tetrahydrate, zinc sulfate, zinc nitrate, zinc chloride, EDTA chelated zinc, manganese sulfate, manganese chloride, EDTA chelated manganese, ferrous sulfate, ferrous ammonium sulfate and EDTA chelated iron. In step (e), the inorganic salt modification activator is one or more of potassium carbonate, potassium nitrate, potassium sulfate, tripotassium phosphate, dipotassium phosphate and potassium dihydrogen phosphate or borate.
   ACTIVITY - Virucide; Plant Protectant; Insecticide; Pesticide.

Chemical aspects of compost production.); C03 (Other organic compounds, inorganic compounds and multicomponent mixtures. Polymers and proteins.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI System useful for removing hydrogen sulfide in carbonizing process of strontium sulfide solution, comprises e.g. carbonizing tower, bubble tower, strontium slurry storage tank, carbon dioxide storage tank and hydrogen sulfide gas cabinet.
AU QIU W
AE NANJING JINYAN STRONTIUM IND CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018902498
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a carbonizing tower, a bubble tower, a strontium slurry storage tank, a carbon dioxide storage tank and a hydrogen sulfide gas cabinet, where the discharging opening of the bottom of the carbonizing tower is connected with the feeding opening of the bubble tower. The discharging opening of the bottom of the bubble tower is connected with the feeding opening of the strontium slurry tank. The gas outlet of the tower top of the bubble tower is connected with the gas inlet of the hydrogen sulfide gas cabinet. The gas outlets of the carbon dioxide storage tank are connected with the gas inlets of the carbonizing tower and the bubble tower respectively through the pipeline and carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the carbonizing tower and the bubble tower.
   USE - The system is useful for removing hydrogen sulfide in carbonizing process of strontium sulfide solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can eliminate emissions of unorganized exhaust gases and achieves clean production of pulp.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing hydrogen sulfide in carbonizing process of strontium sulfide solution using the system, comprising (i) adding 50-60% strontium sulfide solution of carbonizing tower volume into the carbonizing tower, introducing carbon dioxide into the carbonizing tower, starting vulcanizing strontium with three towers connected in series; (ii) introducing carbon dioxide flow rate in step (i) is accumulated for 1-1.5 times of the theoretical amount under the control of the controller, automatically closing the serial valve on the serial pipeline between the carbonizing towers; (iii) automatically closing the first gas inlet valve of the carbonizing tower and automatically closing the first gas inlet valve of the carbonizing tower when the pressure in the carbonizing tower is greater than or equal to 0.2 Mpa, automatically opening the first discharging valve of the tower bottom, discharging the material in the carbonizing tower into the bubble tower, opening the gas outlet valve on the connection pipeline of the bubble tower and the hydrogen sulfide gas cabinet, discharging the mixed gas in the bubble tower into the hydrogen sulfide gas cabinet; (iv) automatically closing the first discharging opening at the tower bottom of the carbonizing tower when the pressure in the carbonizing tower is less than or equal to 0.07 MPa of the second gas inlet valve on the connection pipeline of the carbon dioxide storage tank and the bubble tower and introducing the carbon dioxide storage tank carbon dioxide gas into the bubble tower; (v) closing the gas outlet valve on the connection pipeline of the bubble tower and the hydrogen sulfide gas cabinet, after an interval of 30 seconds when the second gas inlet valve opening time reaches 6~10 minutes and closing the second gas inlet valve, simultaneously automatically opening the second discharging valve of the tower bottom of the bubble tower and discharging the processed material in the bubble tower into the strontium slurry storage tank; and (vi) automatically closing the discharging pump and the second discharging valve when the first content gauge in the bubble tower is 0 or the reading of the second content gauge in the strontium slurry storage tank is higher than the set value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbonization tower is provided with a plurality of connection with feeding port of the bubble tower bottom of discharge hole of each of the carbide. The air inlet of each carbide tower respectively through branch pipeline is connected with the main pipeline and the total pipeline and the outlet of the carbon tank is connected. The carbonizing tower is further provided with a serial pipeline, where the serial pipeline is connected to the inner cavities of each carbonizing tower. The branch pipeline of the carbonization tower is respectively provided with a first intake valve. The pipeline between each of carbide tower is respectively provided with valve in series. The connection pipeline between the feeding port of the discharging opening and the bubble of the carbonization tower is provided with a first valve. The connecting pipe feeding port of the strontium slurry tank of the bubble is provided with a second valve. The connecting pipeline outlet of the bubbling tower air inlet of the carbon dioxide storage tank is provided with a second inlet valve. The connecting pipeline of the air inlet of outlet and vulcanized hydrogen gas cabinet of the bubble is provided with an air outlet valve. The total pipeline carbide tower and the carbon dioxide storage tank is further provided with a flowmeter and the third air valve. The bubble tower is provided with a first liquid level meter. The strontium slurry storage tank is provided with a second liquid level meter. The connecting pipeline between the bubbling tower and strontium slurry tank is provided with a discharging pump. The flowmeter, the first liquid level and the second liquid level meter is connected with the controller communication connection and transmitting data to the controller. The first air valve, second air valve, third air valve, a series valve, air outlet valve, the first valve and the second valve are pneumatic ball valve connected with the controller. The controller controlling opening and closing of discharging pump is connected with the controller and controlled by controller to work.

PN WO2018129426-A1
TI Simultaneous consumption of carbon dioxide and generation of multiple-carbon reaction products e.g. hydrocarbons, involves reacting hydrogen and carbon monoxide in portion having temperature higher than that of cooling portion of reactor.
AU KING P E
   LIVNEH B Z
AE ECOKAP TECHNOLOGIES LLC (ECOK-Non-standard)
GA 201855525B
AB    NOVELTY - Method for simultaneously consuming carbon dioxide and generating multiple-carbon reaction products involves introducing a flow of a dehumidified gaseous source of carbon dioxide (130) and methane (140) into a higher-temperature portion (101) of a reaction vessel (100), irradiating a catalytic material (111) in the portion (101) with microwave energy (120) to produce hydrogen and carbon monoxide, cooling a lower-temperature portion (102) of the vessel, in which the irradiated the portion (101) exhibits a higher temperature than the cooled portion (102), allowing hydrogen and carbon monoxide to flow from the portion (101) into the portion (102) having a catalytic material (112) which catalyzes exothermic reactions involving carbon monoxide and hydrogen to produce multiple-carbon reaction product(s), allowing a mixture comprising reaction product(s) to flow out of vessel from the portion (102), and separating a portion(s) of the multiple-carbon reaction products (160) from the mixture.
   USE - Method for simultaneously consuming carbon dioxide and generating one or more multiple-carbon reaction products e.g. linear or branched-chain aliphatic hydrocarbons, linear or branched-chain aliphatic primary alcohols, linear or branched-chain aliphatic aldehydes or ketones, linear or branched-chain aliphatic carboxylic acids, linear or branched-chain aliphatic esters, and linear or branched-chain aliphatic acid anhydrides (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently prevents and suppresses electric arcing, electric discharge, and plasma generation within the reaction vessel by irradiating the catalytic material with microwave energy in the higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel, and improves carbon dioxide conversion rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The catalytic material (111) is irradiated in portion (101) of the reaction vessel with microwave energy so as to heat the catalytic material (111) and drive an endothermic reaction of carbon dioxide and methane, catalyzed by the catalytic material (111). The lower-temperature portion (102) of the vessel is cooled to establish a temperature gradient within the vessel. At least a portion of heat required to maintain the temperature gradient is supplied by the microwave energy irradiating the catalytic material (111) in the higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel. The higher-temperature portion of the reaction vessel, the catalytic material, or the irradiating of the catalytic material are arranged so as to suppress electric arcing, electric discharge, and plasma generation within the reaction vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining microwave-assisted catalyzed conversion of carbon dioxide and methane to carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the high-temperature portion of the single reaction vessel, and catalyzed conversion carbon monoxide and hydrogen into multiple-carbon reaction products in the lower-temperature portion of the same reaction vessel.
   Reaction vessel (100)
   High-temperature portion (101)
   Lower-temperature portion (102)
   Catalytic materials (111,112)
   Microwave energy (120)
   Carbon dioxide (130)
   Methane (140)
   Multiple-carbon reaction products (160)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gaseous source of carbon dioxide comprises combustion exhaust. The gaseous source of methane comprises natural gas. The higher-temperature portion comprises one or more of quartz, silica, zirconia, cordierite, boron nitride, and alumina. The catalytic materials comprise magnetite. Preferred Composition: The water content of the gaseous source of carbon dioxide and methane is less than 1 vol.%. The water content of all gases entering the reaction vessel is less than 3 vol.%. The gaseous source of carbon dioxide comprises 0-80 vol.% nitrogen.

TI Cyclone flash reduction direct steelmaking system comprises a carbon monoxide preparation device, a cyclone flash reduction furnace, an electric heating melting furnace, and an exhaust gas aftertreatment device.
AU LI L
   LI M
   YANG X
   ZOU Z
   SHAO L
   LI Q
AE UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
   UNIV NORTHEASTERN (UYDB-C)
GA 201996824J
AB    NOVELTY - Cyclone flash reduction direct steelmaking system comprises a carbon monoxide preparation device, a cyclone flash reduction furnace, an electric heating melting furnace, and an exhaust gas aftertreatment device. The exhaust gas after-treatment device comprises a preheating/pre-reduction device, an exhaust gas dust-removing and purifying device, a heat exchanger and an exhaust gas separating device. The carbon monoxide preparation device comprises a carbon dioxide reduction reactor (1), a carbon monoxide storage tank (2) and an oxygen storage tank (3), and the carbon dioxide reduction reactor is connected to the carbon monoxide storage tank and the oxygen storage tank through a pipeline. The cyclone flash reduction furnace is provided with a feed port, a carbon monoxide nozzle, and an exhaust gas outlet, and the exhaust gas outlet is disposed at the top of the furnace body.
   USE - Cyclone flash reduction direct steelmaking system.
   ADVANTAGE - The cyclone flash reduction direct steelmaking system does not rely on fossil fuels, recycles carbon monoxide, processes no pollutants and emits carbon dioxide, and achieves clean smelting.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of performing cyclone flash reduction direct steelmaking process based on the system, which comprises:
   (A) introducing carbon dioxide is into the carbon dioxide reduction reactor for electrochemical reduction to obtain pure carbon monoxide and oxygen, where the purity of carbon monoxide is greater-than 99%, the purity of oxygen is greater-than 99%, carbon monoxide is transported through a pipeline to a carbon monoxide storage tank, and oxygen is transported through a pipeline to an oxygen storage tank;
   (B) entering oxygen in storage tank to obtain preheated oxygen, where the iron ore powder and the flux powder enter the preheating/pre-reduction device to obtain the preheated/pre-reduced powder, and the powder is sprayed into the cyclone flash reduction furnace together with the preheated oxygen, and the carbon monoxide nozzle is sprayed with carbon monoxide to control the temperature in the furnace to be 900-1500 degrees C;
   (C) putting the pre-reduced iron powder/iron droplet formed in the cyclone flash reduction furnace directly into the electric heating melting furnace connected with the lower portion due to the gravity during the rotary motion of the furnace wall, and controlling the electric heating melting furnace by the electrode heating, where the temperature is 1600-1750 degrees C, and the pre-reduced iron powder / iron drop is electrothermally melted; and carring out the continuous steel making process out to obtain molten steel, slag and smelting exhaust, and discharging the molten slag and the molten steel continuously from the tapping port and the tapping port respectively or intermittently; and
   (D) sending the exhaust gas to the dust removal and purifying device, and after purifying and dedusting, obtaining the purified exhaust gas and the dust removing ash; returning the dust removal ash to the cyclone flash reduction furnace, and purifing the exhaust gas into the heat exchanger for waste heat utilization, preheating the oxygen, where the carbon monoxide is transported through the pipeline to the carbon monoxide storage tank, and the reducing gas is returned to the cyclone flash reduction furnace and the electric heating melting furnace for recycling, and the carbon dioxide is sent to the carbon dioxide reduction reactor through the pipeline for recycling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the cyclone flash reduction direct steelmaking system.
   Carbon dioxide reduction reactor (1)
   Carbon monoxide storage tank (2)
   Oxygen storage tank (3)
   Iron ore powder feed hopper (4)
   Flux powder feed hopper (5)

TI Preparation for production of alcoholic aqueous            beverages with carbon dioxide content, comprises            ethanol adsorbed in carbohydrate, and propellant which            produces carbon dioxide after addition of water and            flavoring.

AE VAN GASTEL A L (VGAS-Individual)
GA 2022B6710V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation comprises ethanol adsorbed in a                carbohydrate, and a propellant which produces                carbon dioxide after the addition of water and                flavoring. The propellant is present in an amount                such that per mole of ethanol in the end product                0.05-0.8 moles of carbon dioxide are generated. The                carbohydrate is dextrin having a dextrose                equivalent in the range 5-25 and a bulk density of                0.1-0.5 g/cm3.
   USE - The preparation is useful for producing                alcoholic aqueous beverages including carbonated                alcoholic beverage, carbonated beer, carbonated                soda and carbonated water.
   ADVANTAGE - The inventive preparation can be shipped and                transported without the risk of premature pressure                build-up. It can be opened again along one of the                edges, where both chambers (or all chambers) of the                packaging become accessible at the same time. The                preparation can then either be placed in a                container or drinking vessel which already contains                water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   multi-chamber packaging containing the                preparation; and
   producing a free-flowing preparation.

PN WO2022186647-A1; KR2022124998-A
TI Producing inorganic compound using desulfurized            gypsum by filtering mixture of desulfurized gypsum,            eluent and water, separating filtrate and anhydrite            cake, drying, supplying carbon dioxide-containing gas            to filtrate, and reusing eluent solution.
AU LEE Y H
   CHUNG Y K
   YUN B K
   SUNG D J
   KIM C H
   HO L
   CHUNG Y
   YOON B K
   SEONGDAE-JIN
AE HYUNDAI OILBANK CO LTD (HYUN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B5929A
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing an inorganic compound                using desulfurized gypsum involves filtering a                mixture of desulfurized gypsum, eluent and water,                separating the filtrate and anhydrite cake, and                drying the separated anhydrite cake, supplying a                carbon dioxide-containing gas to the filtrate                separated in the anhydrite powder manufacturing                step and filtering, and separating it into a                calcium carbonate cake and an eluent solution,                drying the separated calcium carbonate cake, and                reusing the eluent solution separated in the                calcium carbonate production step by supplying it                to the anhydrite production step.
   USE - The method is used for producing an inorganic                compound using desulfurized gypsum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly,                reduces the cost of a heat source and greenhouse                gases such as carbon dioxide generated in the                quicklime manufacturing process, prepares high                added value product using desulfurized gypsum, an                undervalued circulating resource, and greatly                contributes to reducing greenhouse gas emissions by                reacting with carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desulfurized                  gypsum is prepared in a CFBC boiler, and 40-60                  wt.% calcium sulfate, and 25-45 wt.% calcium                  oxide. The eluent is chosen from ammonium                  chloride, ammonium nitrate and/or their                  combinations. The weight ratio of desulfurized                  gypsum and water is 1:5-1:45. The weight ratio of                  the desulfurized gypsum and the eluent is                  1:0.7-1:2 in the anhydrous gypsum powder                  preparation step. The carbon dioxide-containing                  gas is an exhaust gas of a circulating fluidized                  bed combustion boiler. Preferred Conditions: The                  step of preparing of anhydrite powder is                  performed at 20-30&#176; C for 2-10 hours.                  The calcium carbonate powder preparation step is                  performed at 20-30&#176; C for 1-5                  hour.

TI Preparation of photocatalyst composite material            for preparing photocatalytic film for reducing carbon            dioxide, involves using cobalt compound, nickel            compound and three-dimensional porous palm-leaf-like            graphite carbon framework, as precursor, and calcining            obtained precursor in air atmosphere.
AU ZHAO Y
   HE L
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 2022A2343B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of photocatalyst composite                material involves using cobalt compound, nickel                compound and three-dimensional porous                palm-leaf-like graphite carbon framework, as                precursors, and calcining the precursor in an air                atmosphere and raising the temperature to                600-900&#8451; to obtain a composite material                of cobalt(II,III) oxide and nickel oxide and                three-dimensional porous palm leaf-shaped graphitic                carbon framework.
   USE - Preparation of photocatalyst composite                material used for preparing photocatalytic film                (claimed) for reducing carbon dioxide by palm leaf                structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is simple in operation,                low in cost and convenient for                popularization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for preparing photocatalytic carbon dioxide                reducing film by utilizing the photocatalyst                composite material, which involves: (a) adding the                photocatalyst composite material with the palm                leaf-like structure reducing carbon dioxide into                water, and ultrasonically dispersing; and (b)                drying to form a film and uniformly dispersing                deionized water on surface of the film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method preferably                  involves dissolving cobalt compound, nickel                  compound and three-dimensional porous palm-leaf                  graphitic carbon framework in a solvent,                  performing magnetic stirring at room temperature,                  and stirring and drying the mixture to form a                  pink powder, which is precursor. Preferred                  Components: The cobalt compound is selected from                  cobalt chloride or cobalt nitrate. The nickel                  compound is selected from nickel chloride or                  nickel nitrate. The three-dimensional porous                  palm-leaf graphite carbon framework is selected                  from at least one of melamine, graphene,                  graphitic carbon, urea and polyvinylpyrrolidone.                  The solvent is selected from deionized water, tap                  water or drinking water. Preferred Composition:                  The mass ratio of sum of cobalt compound and                  nickel compound to the three-dimensional porous                  palm-leaf graphite carbon framework is                  (1-4):(1-4). The mass ratio of cobalt compound                  and nickel compound is (1-3):(1-3).

TI Preparing low-odor polypropylene grafted maleic anhydride beads under assistance of supercritical carbon dioxide comprises mixing polypropylene, maleic anhydride, monomer, antioxidant and initiator, filling supercritical carbon dioxide, vacuumizing, extruding, cooling, dehydrating and granulating.
AU ZHAO Y
   WANG Y
   LUAN B
   LIU J
   SHI L
   LIU L
   ZHAO M
   WANG S
   YUAN W
   YANG X
AE SHANDONG CHAMBROAD PETROCHEMICALS CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022481692
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing low-odor polypropylene grafted maleic anhydride beads under assistance of supercritical carbon dioxide comprises taking 100 pts. wt. polypropylene, 0.5-3 pts. wt. maleic anhydride, 0.5-3 pts. wt. monomer, 0.1-1 pts. wt. antioxidant, 0.1-1 pts. wt. initiator, mixing uniformly, filling supercritical carbon dioxide, vacuumizing and extruding, orderly cooling, and removing water, granulating to obtain low-odor polypropylene grafted maleic anhydride beads, where the monomer includes styrene, cyclopentadiene and thiuram, the initiator includes benzoyl peroxide, di-tert-butyl peroxide isopropyl benzene, 2, 3-dimethyl-2, 3-diphenyl butane, and 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-di-tert-butylperoxyhexane.
   USE - The maleic anhydride bead is useful for automobile, household appliance, cable and packaging material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can ensure prepared polypropylene grafted maleic anhydride bead has low odor, narrow molecular weight distribution, high maleic anhydride grafting rate, excellent mechanical property, low processing temperature, low cost, and can be continuously produced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pressure of the filling supercritical carbon dioxide is 7.38-10MPa. The preparation process is carried out in a double-screw extruder, the heating area of the double-screw extruder from the feeding port to the machine head is 12 sections, orderly set as 160-170degrees Celsius, 170-190degrees Celsius, 160-195degrees Celsius, 160-210degrees Celsius, 160-220degrees Celsius, 160-220degrees Celsius, 160-220degrees Celsius, 160-220degrees Celsius, 160-220degrees Celsius, 160-220degrees Celsius, 160-220degrees Celsius, 160-210degrees Celsius, the rotating speed of the screw is 150-400 revolutions/minute, the feeding speed is 10-50kg/hour.Preferred Components: The antioxidant includes hindered phenol antioxidant, phosphite ester antioxidant and thioester antioxidant. The polypropylene includes isotactic polypropylene and the melt flow index of the polypropylene is 1-50 g/10minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide is filled with 1-5% of the feeding speed of the polypropylene. The length of the low-odor polypropylene grafted maleic anhydride bead is 1-1.5 mm, and the diameter is 0.5-1.5 mm. The raw material includes 100 pts. wt. polypropylene, 1.2 pts. wt. maleic anhydride, 1.2 pts. wt. styrene, 0.2 pts. wt. antioxidant and 0.1 pts. wt. di-tert-butyl peroxide isopropyl benzene, and pressure of filling supercritical carbon dioxide is 7.5 MPa.

PT P
PN EP3922659-A1; WO2021249814-A1; EP3922659-A4
TI Manufacture of polyether carbonate polyol used for            e.g. fuel additive, involves adding alkylene oxide and            carbon dioxide to H-functional starter substance under            atmosphere of gas mixture containing carbon dioxide and            at least one further gas, in presence of e.g. dimethyl            carbonate catalyst.
AU VOR D E L
   WOLF A
   MACHAT M
   MEURESCH M
   LANGANKE J
   GUERTLER C
AE COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
   COVESTRO DEUT AG (CVST-C)
GA 2021E3086Q
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polyether carbonate polyols                involves adding an alkylene oxide and carbon                dioxide to a H-functional starter substance in the                presence of a dimethyl carbonate catalyst or a                metal complex catalyst based on the metals zinc                and/or cobalt. The addition process is performed                under an atmosphere of a gas mixture containing                12.0-85.0 vol.% carbon dioxide and 15.0-88.0 vol.%                at least one further gas based on the volume of the                gas mixture.
   USE - Manufacture of polyether carbonate polyols.                Uses include but are not limited to polyurethane                foams, detergent formulations, drilling fluids,                fuel additives, ionic and non-ionic surfactants,                lubricants, process chemicals for paper or textile                production and cosmetic formulations.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of polyether                carbonate polyol with improved selectivity (i.e.                the lowest ratio of cyclic carbonate to linear                polyether carbonate polyol) and low content of                by-products and without problems.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas mixture                  contains preferably 20-40 vol.% carbon dioxide                  and 0-80 vol.% at least one further gas. The                  further gas contains 10 vol.% or less at least                  one compound chosen from carbon monoxide,                  nitrogen, noble gases and hydrogen and 1 vol.% or                  less one or more gases that differ from carbon                  monoxide, nitrogen, noble gases and                  hydrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves                  placing optionally a partial amount of the                  H-functional starter substance and/or suspending                  agent that does not contain H-functional groups                  in a reactor, optionally together with dimethyl                  carbonate catalyst and/or a metal complex                  catalyst based on the metals zinc and/or cobalt,                  adding portion of alkylene oxide to the obtained                  mixture at 0-150 degrees C, and interrupting                  addition of the alkylene oxide, attaching                  alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide to the                  H-functional starter substance in the reactor in                  the presence of a double metal cyanide catalyst                  or in the presence of the metal complex catalyst                  based on the metals zinc and/or cobalt, to obtain                  a reaction mixture containing the polyether                  carbonate polyol, remaining the reaction mixture                  in the reactor, continuously transferring to a                  post reactor, and reducing the content of free                  alkylene oxide in the reaction mixture by way of                  a post-reaction. The H-functional starter                  substance, alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide are                  continuously introducing into the reactor during                  the reaction. The additional dimethyl carbonate                  catalyst is continuously introduced into the                  reactor and the resulting reaction mixture is                  continuously removed from the reactor. Preferred                  Components: The H-functional starting substance                  is chosen from at least one of ethylene glycol,                  propylene glycol, 1,3-propanediol,                  1,3-butanediol, 1,4-butanediol, 1,5-pentanediol,                  2-methylpropane-1,3-diol, neopentyl glycol,                  1,6-hexanediol, 1,8-octanediol, diethylene                  glycol, dipropylene glycol, glycerol,                  trimethylolpropane, pentaerythritol, sorbitol,                  polyether carbonate polyols with molecular weight                  of 150-8000 g/mol with a functionality of 2-3 and                  polyether polyols with molecular weight of                  150-8000 g/mol with functionality of 2-3.
   EXAMPLE - 50 g Polyethylene terephthalate, and 0.058                dimethyl carbonate catalyst were charged in                reactor. The mixture was freed from volatile                components in vacuum under a gentle stream of                nitrogen at 130 degrees C over 20 minutes. The                reactor was then adjusted to 25 bar with carbon                dioxide. The reactor was pressurized with 25 bar                carbon monoxide to total pressure of 50 bar,                resulting in carbon dioxide partial pressure of 25                bar. 5.0 g Portion of propylene oxide was added at                10 mL/minute and waited until reaction starts. The                temperature was then reduced to 115 degrees C and                55.7 g propylene oxide was added continuously in                180 minutes. Carbon dioxide was added so that the                pressure remains constant. After the continuous                addition, the mixture was stirred for further 60                minutes at 115 degrees C and constant pressure.                After cooling, sample was taken for proton-NMR                analysis and gel permeation chromatography to                obtain polyether carbonate polyol with number                average molecular weight of 1.26 kg/mol and                polydispersity index of 1.04.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A87 (Textile auxiliaries.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); ; D21 (Preparations for dental or toilet purposes - including filling alloys, compositions for dentures or dental impressions, anti-caries chewing gum, plaque disclosing compositions, toothpastes, cosmetics, shampoos, topical anti-sunburn compositions and toilet soaps (A61K).)
