PN CN113617367-A
TI Ruthenium-based catalyst used for photothermal catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises cadmium sulfide, molybdenum sulfide and/or zinc sulfide as carrier and ruthenium dispersed on carrier surface in form of single atom.
AU CAI S
   JIA H
AE INST URBAN ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (CUBN-C)
GA 2021D0137E
AB    NOVELTY - A ruthenium-based catalyst comprises carrier and ruthenium dispersed on carrier surface in the form of a single atom. The carrier is at least one of cadmium sulfide, molybdenum sulfide and zinc sulfide.
   USE - Ruthenium-based catalyst used for photothermal catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ruthenium-based catalyst has excellent carbon dioxide adsorption capacity, photo-generated charge separation capacity, light absorption capacity, and photothermal conversion capacity. The preparation method is simple, fast and economical, and can realize the monoatomic dispersion of the ruthenium with high specific gravity on the surface of cadmium sulfide, while ensuring the photothermal catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide and high methane selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the ruthenium-based catalyst, which involves (s1) dispersing carrier and ammonium carbonate in deionized water to obtain suspension, and (s2) dripping ruthenium salt aqueous solution, stirring and reacting, washing reaction product, drying and performing hydrogen reduction treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carrier is preferably cadmium sulfide. The ruthenium-based catalyst is 0.1% ruthenium/cadmium sulfide (Ru/CdS), 0.5% Ru/CdS, 1% Ru/CdS or 3% Ru/CdS. The ruthenium salt is at least one of ruthenium chloride, ruthenium bromide, ruthenium nitrate, ruthenium nitrite, ruthenium sulfate, and ruthenium acetate.Preferred Condition: The reaction in step (s2) is carried out at room temperature for 10-36 hours. The washing in step (s2) is carried out by alternately washing the reaction product with absolute ethanol and deionized water. The hydrogen reduction treatment is carried out at 200-400degrees Celsius for 2-8 hours.Preferred Process: The cadmium sulfide carrier is prepared by adding cadmium acetate dihydrate and L-cysteine to deionized water, continuously stirring until a milky white solution is formed, adding ethanolamine to the solution, continuously stirring until a transparent solution is formed, hydrothermally reacting and post-treating.Preferred Property: The shape of carrier is nanorods. The ruthenium-based catalyst is in powder form, and has particle size of 20-250 nm, and specific surface area of 10-150 m2/g.Preferred Composition: The ruthenium-based catalyst comprises 0.01-10 %mass ruthenium. The ratio of carrier, ammonium carbonate and deionized water is 1 g:10-40 mmol:80-150 mL. The concentration of ammonium carbonate solution is 0.5-2 mol/L, preferably 10-50 mL and ruthenium salt aqueous solution is 1-5 mg/mL. The mass ratio of ruthenium salt to the carrier is 0.01-5 wt.%.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.)

PT P
PN CN113402308-A
TI Preparing slow-release fertilizer by adding            calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate powder formed            by reacting nanofiltration seawater with carbon dioxide            to liquid coating material, adding dispersant and            surfactant to form slow-release coating material and            coating coating material on fertilizer.
AU WANG R
   NIE X
   LIU Y
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0785L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing slow-release fertilizer                using nanofiltration seawater to capture carbon                dioxide products, comprises adding nanofiltration                seawater to capture carbon dioxide to obtain                calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate powder,                adding calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate                powder to liquid coating material, adding a                dispersant and a surfactant to prepare a                slow-release coating material with better                slow-release performance, and coating the                slow-release coating material on the surface of a                granular fertilizer, and drying to obtain the                slow-release fertilizer.
   USE - The method is used for preparing slow-release                fertilizers using nanofiltration seawater to                capture carbon dioxide products.
   ADVANTAGE - The slow-release fertilizer is                environment-friendly, reduces production cost,                increases the calcium, magnesium and other nutrient                elements, and has a particle strength of 40-60 N, a                nutrient release period of 60-120 days, and a                degradation period of the coating material of                90-150 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Property: The calcium carbonate                  and magnesium carbonate obtained by the                  nanofiltration seawater capturing carbon dioxide                  are powders with a diameter of 10-1000 nm.                  Preferred Composition: The purity of calcium                  carbonate and magnesium carbonate is 60-99%. The                  liquid coating material is a modified starch                  aqueous solution and an amide aqueous solution                  with a mass concentration of 1-20%, polyvinyl                  alcohol aqueous solution, polylactic acid aqueous                  solution, and/or sodium silicate aqueous                  solution. The added amount of calcium carbonate                  and magnesium carbonate is 1-30% of the mass of                  the slow-release coating material. The amount of                  dispersant and surfactant is 0.1-2% of the dosage                  of the sustained-release coating material. The                  amount of the slow-release coating material is                  1-10% of the total fertilizer. Preferred                  Components: The dispersant comprises sodium                  silicate, sodium tripolyphosphate, sodium                  hexametaphosphate, sodium pyrophosphate, glycerin                  fatty acid, polysorbate, stearic acid, and/or                  sodium dodecylbenzene sulfonate.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN JP2021115515-A
TI Manufacture of low chlorine waste material involves blowing ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas with respect to mixture obtained by adding water to waste material containing salt content, and obtaining mixture containing sodium hydrogen carbonate and ammonium chloride.
AU MARUYA E
AE UBE IND LTD (UBEI-C)
GA 2021918927
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of a low-chlorine waste material involves blowing an ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas into a mixture (M1) obtained by adding water to a salt-containing waste to perform reaction of sodium chloride, water, ammonia and carbon dioxide to obtain a mixture (M2) comprising sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride, and heating mixture (M2) to convert 2 equivalent of sodium bicarbonate to sodium carbonate, carbon dioxide and water and to convert ammonium chloride to ammonia and hydrochloric acid.
   USE - Manufacture of low-chlorine waste material. Uses include but are not limited to civil engineering material, cement raw material, heat-energy source in cement plant, solid-fuel (refuse derived fuel), and earthwork material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of low chlorine waste material in a short period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing equipment of the low-chlorine waste material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The salt-containing waste is a food-adhered waste or a seawater-adhered waste. The carbon dioxide gas is a gas discharged from a kiln of a cement plant.

AU ZHAO R

AE ZHAO R (ZHAO-Individual)
GA 202190110C
AB    NOVELTY - Degradable packaging material comprises not greater than 60 wt.% polylactic acid, 5-30 wt.% polytrimethylene terephthalate (PBAT), 1-20 wt.% polybutylene succinate (PBS), 0.1-5 wt.% carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane (PPCU), 0.01-5 wt.% ADR chain extender and 0.1-3 wt.% additive.
   USE - The degradable packaging material is useful in preparing packaging film bag (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The degradable packaging material: uses quaternary blend system with polytrimethylene terephthalate, polybutylene succinate, and carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane; and uses ADR as a chain extender through cold crystallization, and can prepares film with high transparency and excellent mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:

   (2) preparing the packaging film bag, comprising adding polylactic acid, poly-poly-terephthalic acid-polybutylene adipate, polybutylene succinate, carbon dioxide-based thermoplastic polyurethane, ADR chain extender and additive, adding double-screw extruder for melting, pelleting, after granulating, adding extruder by blowing film, and the thickness of the packaging film bag is 1-50 mu m.

The ethylene absorbent is potassium permanganate, potassium bromate, silver nitrate, sodium thiosulfate, calcite, inclined zeolite and mordenite.

TI Method for for controlling air-conditioner,            involves obtaining indoor environment parameter, and            obtaining primary infection risk value through            infection risk estimation unit according to indoor            environment parameter.
AU WU J
AE NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   NINGBO AUX INTELLIGENT COMML AIR CONDITI (AUXS-C)
   AUX AIR CONDITIONER CO LTD (AUXS-C)
   NINGBO AUX ELECTRIC CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 2021620142
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining indoor                environment parameter, where the indoor environment                parameter comprises indoor relative humidity value,                indoor temperature value and indoor carbon dioxide                concentration value. Primary infection risk value                is obtained through an infection risk estimation                unit according to the indoor environment parameter.                Secondary infection risk value is obtained                according to the indoor carbon dioxide                concentration value if the indoor carbon dioxide                concentration value is greater than preset carbon                dioxide concentration value. Relationship between                the primary infection risk value, the secondary                infection risk value and preset risk value is                judged.
   USE - Method for for controlling an air-conditioner                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining size of various                infection risk values through the indoor                environment parameter in an effective manner.

   (1) a device for controlling an                air-conditioner; and

TI Membrane used for separation of gas from feed gas            stream, comprises support layer, and selective polymer            layer comprising polymer matrix and guanidine-based            mobile carrier dispersed within polymer matrix,            arranged on support layer.
AU HO W S W
   HAN Y
AE OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
   OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
GA 2020C0671D
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane comprises a support layer, and a                selective polymer layer comprising a polymer matrix                and a guanidine-based mobile carrier dispersed                within the polymer matrix, arranged on the support                layer.
   USE - Guanidine-containing membrane is used for                separation of gas e.g. carbon dioxide, hydrogen                sulfide, and/or hydrogen chloride from feed gas                stream e.g. hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen                sulfide, hydrogen chloride, carbon monoxide,                nitrogen, methane, steam, sulfur oxides, and/or                nitrogen oxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The guanidine-containing membrane exhibits                selective permeability towards gases, such as                carbon dioxide, and has excellent carbon                dioxide/nitrogen separation performance with high                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) separation of a gas (G1) from a feed gas                stream, which involves contacting the membrane with                the feed gas stream comprising gas (G1) to carry                out transmembrane permeation of gas (G1); and
   (2) production of the membrane, which involves                depositing the selective polymer layer on support                layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The graphene oxide                  has a carbon/oxygen ratio of 3-20 or 1-3. The                  graphene oxide is nanoporous. The feed gas                  further comprises a gas (G1) such as nitrogen,                  hydrogen, and/or carbon monoxide. The membrane is                  selectively permeable to an acidic gas, and a                  fluid chosen from carbon dioxide, hydrogen                  sulfide, sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide,                  nitrogen oxide, hydrogen chloride, and water. The                  membrane exhibits a gas (G1)/gas (G2) selectivity                  of 20-500 at 77 degrees C and a feed pressure of                  4 bar.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The guanidine-based                  mobile carrier comprises a compound of formula                  (I), preferably tetramethylguanidine and/or                  piperazine-1-carboximidamide.            alkylcarbamyl, di(1-6C alkyl)carbamyl, carboxy,                  1-6C alkylcarbonyl, 1-6C alkoxycarbonyl, 1-6C                  alkylcarbonylamino, 1-6C alkylsulfonylamino,    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer matrix                  comprises a hydrophilic polymer and/or an                  amine-containing polymer. The amine-containing                  polymer is at least one chosen from                  polyvinylamine, polyallylamine,                  polyethyleneimine, poly-N-isopropylallylamine,                  poly-N-tert-butylallylamine,                  poly-N-1,2-dimethylpropylallylamine,                  poly-N-methylallylamine,                  poly-N,N-dimethylallylamine,                  poly-2-vinylpiperidine, poly-4-vinylpiperidine,                  polyaminostyrene, chitosan, and their copolymers,                  preferably polyvinylamine. The hydrophilic                  polymer is at least one chosen from polyvinyl                  alcohol, polyvinyl acetate, polyethylene oxide,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyacrylamine,                  polyallylamine, polyvinylamine,                  polyethyleneimine, polysiloxane, and/or their                  copolymers. The selective polymer layer further                  comprises an amine-containing mobile carrier, a                  carbon dioxide-philic ether, a crosslinking                  agent, 0.01-5 wt.% graphene oxide dispersed                  within the polymer matrix, and carbon nanotubes                  dispersed within the polymer matrix. The support                  layer comprises a gas-permeable polymer(s) chosen                  from polyamide, polyimide, polypyrrolone,                  polyester, sulfone-based polymer, nitrile-based                  polymer, polymeric organosilicone, fluorinated                  polymers, polyolefin, and their copolymers,                  preferably polyethersulfone or polysulfone,                  arranged on a base which is a nonwoven fabric                  comprising fibers made of a polyester. The                  membrane is in the form of hollow fibers, a flat                  sheet, a spiral-wound, or a plate-and-frame                  configuration. Preferred Properties: The                  amine-containing mobile carrier has a molecular                  weight of less than 1000 Da. The selective                  polymer layer has carbon dioxide/nitrogen                  selectivity of at least 50, preferably 150 and a                  carbon dioxide/hydrogen selectivity of 50-350, at                  77 degrees C and a feed pressure of 4 bar.

PN KR2020113890-A; KR2350573-B1
TI Flowable composition comprises carbon dioxide traps, boiler ash which is produced in circulating fluidized bed boiler that uses coal and limestone as combustion medium to continuously circulates heat, slag, activator and rapid payment agent.
AU JEONG D
   U BAK C
   JO A R
   U B C
AE SIN CHANG ENC (SINC-Non-standard)
GA 202099556A
AB    NOVELTY - Flowable composition comprises 10-15 wt.% carbon dioxide traps, 30-40 wt.% boiler ash, 40-50 wt.% slag, 4-7 wt.% activator, and 1-3 wt.% rapid payment agent. The boiler ash is produced in a circulating fluidized bed boiler that uses coal and limestone as a combustion medium, and continuously circulates heat to completely burn coal and limestone.
   USE - Used as flowable composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The flowable composition has excellent fluidity, and compaction performance even with little compaction, is environmentally friendly that does not use cement because slag and carbon dioxide traps are used as binders, and is easy to apply to sites where it is difficult to resolve by securing liquidity while maintaining initial strength and long-term strength.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a carbon dioxide trap production process.
   Exhaust gas (10)
   Liquid (20)
   Calcium carbonate (30)
   First air diffuser (100)
   Second air diffuser (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The boiler ash has specific gravity of 2.6-2.8 and powderiness of 5800-6800 cm2/g. The boiler ash comprises 0.08-0.12 pts. wt. silicon dioxide, 0.03-0.06 pts. wt. alumina, 0.4-0.5 pts. wt. calcium oxide, 0.14-0.18 pts. wt. iron oxide, 0.002-0.01 pts. wt. magnesium oxide, and 0.18-0.22 pts. wt. sulfur trioxide. The carbon dioxide trap comprises an exhaust gas (10) discharged from factory which is passed through a liquid (20) in which sodium hydroxide is dissolved, and carbon dioxide contained in exhaust gas is calcium carbonate (30) produced by reacting with the sodium hydroxide. Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide trap is produced by (i) collecting the exhaust gas, (ii) treating the exhaust gas in a first air diffuser (100) filled with the liquid, and (iii) treating the exhaust gas that is passed through step (ii) in a second air diffuser (200) filled with the liquid. In the step (ii) and (iii), the exhaust gas is supplied to the first and second air diffusers in fine bubbles.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: In the first air diffuser and the second air diffuser, calcium carbonate recovery device for recovering the precipitated calcium carbonate by reacting the carbon dioxide with the sodium hydroxide is installed. The calcium carbonate recovered in the recovery device is dehydrated in dehydration device, and is moved to a drying device to be dried.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).); L02 (Refractories, ceramics, cement - includes manufacturing methods, limes, soil preparation for (road) building, magnesias and slags, cements, mortars, concretes, abrasives, thermal or acoustic insulation (non)oxide ceramics and ceramic composites, but not brick making, concrete mixers or casting or pottersâ€™ wheels (C04).)

TI High ammonia nitrogen organic waste water resource            recycling, ammonia recovery and synchronous carbon            dioxide absorption system comprises three-chamber            microbial electrolytic cell system.
AU HE W
   DIAO X
   FENG Y
   LIU S
   WANG N
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2020154283
AB    NOVELTY - High ammonia nitrogen organic waste water                resource recycling, ammonia recovery and                synchronous carbon dioxide absorption system                comprises three-chamber microbial electrolytic cell                system. The three-chamber microbial electrolytic                cell system includes anode chamber provided with                anode and cation exchange membrane, cathode chamber                provided with a gas diffusion module, gas                absorption chamber and resource recovery                system.
   USE - As high ammonia nitrogen organic waste water                resource recycling, ammonia recovery and                synchronous carbon dioxide absorption system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for regulating the recycling of                high-ammonia-nitrogen organic waste water, the                recovery of ammonia, and the synchronous carbon                dioxide absorption system, which involves                controlling continuous flow by regulating applied                voltage (0.2-2 V) of microbial electrolytic cell or                sequential batch operation (operating cycle 4-7                days), catholyte type (high ammonia nitrogen                organic waste water, carbon ammonia removed high                ammonia nitrogen organic waste water, phosphate                buffer solution (PBS), sodium chloride solution and                deionized water), and regulating pH of the anolyte                inlet water and chemical oxygen demand (COD)                removal rate and hydrogen production rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: The ammonium ions                  migrate to cathode through cation exchange                  membrane and cathode electrochemical reaction                  generates hydroxide radical ions to obtain                  solution with high ammonia concentration and high                  pH to absorb carbon dioxide acid gas. The ammonia                  in catholyte is recovered in form of heat                  recovery or condensation crystallization with                  carbonate ion and bicarbonate. The carbonate gas                  in flue gas or biogas is absorbed, form of heat                  recovery is used for catholyte using flue gas                  waste heat or biogas combustion, and the                  catholyte is decomposed by heating to generate                  ammonia and carbonate gas. The mixed gas is                  passed into absorption liquid to obtain                  concentrated ammonium bicarbonate solution for                  further resource utilization. The method involves                  regulating usage period of the catholyte (1-50                  cycles) and current density (0.01-100 A/m2)                  adjust ammonia recovery rate and ammonia                  concentration of the absorption liquid by                  adjusting pressure of the absorption chamber (0-1                  MPa), the concentration of carbon dioxide of                  2-100%, and the diffusion rate of carbon dioxide                  to the cathode to regulate effectiveness of                  carbon dioxide absorption.

PN CN109680298-A
TI Bacterial cellulose film loaded nano-copper catalyst electrode useful in electrochemical reduction electrode of carbon dioxide, comprises copper sulfate, sodium borohydride, sodium hydroxide, ethanol, and a bacterial cellulose membrane.
AU QIAO J
   ZHOU Y
   LI X
   GUO X
   HONG F
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 201941090E
AB    NOVELTY - Bacterial cellulose (BC) film loaded nano-copper catalyst electrode comprises copper sulfate, sodium borohydride, sodium hydroxide, ethanol, and a bacterial cellulose membrane cultured from glucose, where the electrode obtained by an aqueous solution by a chemical reduction method.
   USE - The BC film loaded nano-copper catalyst electrode is useful in electrochemical reduction electrode of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode increases the electrochemical reduction catalytic activity for carbon dioxide reduction, improves the utilization of carbon dioxide, suppresses the hydrogen evolution reaction, and enhances the selectivity of the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the BC film loaded nano-copper catalyst electrode, comprising (a) dissolving copper sulfate in ethanol, adding a bacterial cellulose membrane, placing in a water bath thermostat shaker, stirring well, and mixing uniformly; (b) adding a mixed solution of sodium borohydride and sodium hydroxide to the mixture under ice bath conditions; and (c) shaking the mixture in a water bath thermostat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper sulfate is copper sulfate pentahydrate. The sodium borohydride is a sodium borohydride aqueous solution having a concentration of 0.2-0.4 M. The sodium hydroxide is a sodium hydroxide aqueous solution having a concentration of 1 M. The ethanol is a 70% by volume ethanol solution. In the step (c), the size of the BC film-supported nano-copper electrode is 1x (1-3)x 3 cm2 . Preferred Method: The preparation of bacterial cellulose membrane comprises (i) dissolving glucose, yeast powder, peptone in deionized water, adjusting the pH of the nutrient solution to 5.2 with citric acid monohydrate and transferring to the incision bottle with a pipette, (ii) placing the incision bottle in a sterilization pot and sterilizing at 115 degrees C for 30 minutes, (iii) pipetting the seed liquid into the incision bottle in the biological safety cabinet, (iv) placing incision bottle in an incubator at 30 degrees C for 7 days, taking out the BC membrane and placing in a 1% sodium hydroxide solution, and cooking at 80 degrees C for 2 hours, continuing for 3 times, cooking, boiling in deionized water for 2 hours until the pH of the solution by 6-7. The ratio of glucose, yeast powder, peptone, and deionized water is 50 g: 3 g: 5 g: 1l. In the step (iii), the volume ratio of the seed liquid to the nutrient solution in the incision bottle is 1:12.5. In the step (i), the mixed solution obtained by dissolving copper sulfate in ethanol has a copper sulfate concentration of 0.4 M. The soaking time of the bacterial cellulose film is 8-24 hours. In the step (i) and step (iii), the reaction temperature is 30 degrees C.

PN CN109608498-A
TI Preparing supercritical reaction fosaprepitant comprises e. g. turning on drive motor, and rotating inner tank rotates slowly to the outer can, adding a raw material and catalyst into the inner tank through a raw material and catalyst inlet.
AU ZHOU Y
   GAO T
   BAO Y
AE SICHUAN PHARM INC (SCPY-C)
GA 201936535B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing supercritical reaction fosaprepitant comprises (i) turning on the drive motor, and the inner tank rotates slowly relative to the outer can; (ii) adding a raw material and a catalyst into the inner tank through a raw material and a catalyst inlet; (iii) passing carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide inlet to displace the air in the tank; (iv) introducing hydrogen from the hydrogen inlet to form partial pressure of hydrogen of 0.5-0.8 MPa; (v) introducing carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide inlet to make the gas pressure in the tank 13-15 MPa; (vi) turning on the heating element so that the temperature inside the tank is 31-40 degrees C; and (vii) adjusting the drive motor, rotating at 800-1200 revolutions/minute, and stirring for 1-2 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing supercritical reaction fosaprepitant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high stirring and short preparation period.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (vii), an organic solvent is introduced from the solvent inlet, and the reaction product is dissolved under stirring. In the step (viii), opening the control switch, the dissolved filtrate is allowed to pass through the filter and flow from the pipe into the lower end space of the outer tank. The step of depressurizing the organic solvent to the tank is performed at pressure of 0.5-5 MPa. The method further comprises opening the discharge port two and releasing the filtrate and residual pressure. Preferred Components: The raw material is aprepitant dibenzyl phosphate, and the catalyst is 5-10% palladium carbon. The palladium carbon is added in an amount of 0.25-1% by weight based on the weight of fosprepitant dibenzyl phosphate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The side wall of the inner tank has an outward convex curved structure, and the stirring paddle horizontally faces the inner concave portion of the curved structure. The stirring blades are two and the bottom portions thereof are respectively provided with sub-blades facing the inner concave portion of the curved structure. A stirring shaft is provided inside the section of the inner tank to provide a heating element.

TI Composite photocatalyst useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises bismuth molybdate quantum dot and reduced graphene oxide.
AU DAI W
   YU J
   WANG T
   LUO S
   MA Z
   LUO X
   YANG L
   TU X
   ZOU J
AE UNIV NANCHANG HANGKONG (UYNH-C)
GA 201933617Y
AB    NOVELTY - Composite photocatalyst comprises bismuth molybdate quantum dot and reduced graphene oxide, where bismuth molybdate quantum dot are loaded on the surface of the reduced graphene oxide by intermolecular forces; and bismuth molybdate quantum dot having an average size of 4.5-5 nm.
   USE - The composite photocatalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalyst: improves the stability and life cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing composite photocatalyst comprising (i) adding an bismuth solution into an aqueous solution of sodium oleate to obtain a mixed liquid, (ii) adding molybdenum source-graphene oxide aqueous dispersion into the mixed liquid obtained in step (i) to obtain a reaction liquid, and (iii) performing hydrothermally reaction of reaction liquid obtained in step (ii) to obtain composite photocatalyst; and
   (2) use method of composite photocatalyst in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprising mixing composite photocatalyst with water to obtain a reaction liquid containing a composite photocatalyst under vacuum condition, mixing carbon dioxide with reaction liquor, performing dark reaction and photoreaction to obtain methanol and ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio between bismuth molybdate quantum dot and reduced graphene oxide in the composite photocatalyst is 0.3-0.5 mmol:10 mg. In step (i), concentration of the bismuth solution is 0.03-0.05 mol/l; concentration of aqueous sodium oleate solution is 0.20-0.24 mol/l; molar ratio between sodium oleate and bismuth source is 5-6:1; sodium oleate is based on oleate ions, where bismuth source is bismuth ion. In step (ii), molar ratio between molybdenum source and bismuth source is 0.8-1.2:1; molybdenum source is molybdate ion; and bismuth source is bismuth ion. The mass ratio between molybdenum source and graphene oxide is 0.6-0.8 mmol:4-25 mg. The mass ratio between composite photocatalyst and water is 15-25 mg:30 ml. Preferred Method: In step (iii), temperature of hydrothermal reaction is 140-180 degrees C, and time is 16-20 hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Preparing nickel oxide/ silica catalyst for            reforming methane, involves forming nickel-ammonia            complex solution, adding silica sol into solution,            forming suspension, subjecting it to ammonia            distillation and drying to form product containing oxy            hydroxide of nickel and silica, and calcining.
AU TAO W
   CHEN Z
   LIU C
   LI J
   ZHAO Y
   LV J
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2022A0243S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of nickel oxide/ silica (NiO/SiO2)                catalyst, involves: 1) adding aqueous ammonia into                nickel nitrate solution to form a nickel-ammonia                complex solution; 2) adding silica sol into                nickel-ammonia complex solution, followed by                stirring to form a suspension; 3) subjecting the                suspension to ammonia distillation and drying                treatment to form a reaction product containing oxy                hydroxide of nickel and silica (Ni 3 Si 2 O 5 (OH)                4 /SiO2) and calcining the product.
   USE - Method is used for preparing NiO/SiO2 catalyst                used for reforming methane and carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The NiO/SiO2 catalyst prepared, has high                catalytic activity. When used for catalyzing the                reforming reaction of methane and carbon dioxide,                the catalyst shows improved stability. The active                components of the catalyst are not easily sintered,                and also the stable high catalytic activity is                maintained throughout the reforming reaction, to                enhance the conversion rate of methane during the                reforming reaction. The preparation method is a                simple and easy to control process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. nickel oxide/ silica catalyst;
   2. use of NiO/SiO2 catalyst for reforming                methane and carbon dioxide, which involves                performing hydrogen reduction activation treatment                on the catalyst to form an activated catalyst which                is used to catalyze the reforming reaction of                methane and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of                  nickel nitrate and silica sol used in the                  preparation of the catalyst, is 1: (8.5-16.5).                  The amount of silica sol used in step 2 of the                  process, is 20-30 wt.%.Preferred Conditions: The pH of the                  nickel-ammonia complex solution used, is 10-12.                  In step 2 of the process, stirring is performed                  for 6-12 hours. In step 3 of the process, ammonia                  evaporation treatment is performed at                  80-95&#8451;. The pH of the suspension                  subjected to ammonia evaporation treatment, is                  6-7. The drying process is performed at                  100-160&#8451; for 8-16 hours.Preferred Process: In step 2, before                  performing drying process, the suspension is                  washed. The calcining process is carried out at                  700-900&#8451; for 2-8 hours.The use method of the catalyst in reforming                  reaction of methane and carbon dioxide, involves                  performing the reaction at                  700-850&#8451;.The mass space velocities of the methane and                  the carbon dioxide are 1.0-4.0 h -1,                  respectively. The molar ratio of methane to                  carbon dioxide, is 1: (1.0-1.2).Preferred Properties: The NiO/SiO2 catalyst                  obtained, has granularity 20-40 meshes.

   XIONG Y
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2021E99188
AB    NOVELTY - Ruthenium-based photocatalyst comprises titanium oxide support with oxygen vacancy, and ruthenium supported on surface of titanium oxide carrier in form of single atom and cluster.
   USE - Ruthenium-based photocatalyst used in light-driving carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared photocatalyst has good stability and can effectively couple the photothermal effect with the photocatalytic process and it has excellent activity and methane product selectivity in carbon dioxide photocatalytic hydrogenation under medium and low temperature condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for ruthenium-based photocatalyst for carbon dioxide hydromethanation reaction, which involves: (a)using hydrothermal or solvothermal method to obtain titanium oxide carrier with oxygen vacancy;(b)mixing the ruthenium source compound and the titanium oxide carrier with oxygen vacancy in an aqueous ammonia solution;(c)loading ruthenium on the surface of the titanium oxide carrier through strong electrostatic adsorption to obtain the titanium oxide with the ruthenium-ammonia complex adsorbed on the surface;(d) calcining the titanium oxide adsorbed with the ruthenium-ammonia complex in a hydrogen/argon atmosphere to reducing the ruthenium-ammonia complex adsorbed on the surface of the titanium oxide to obtain a ruthenium-based photocatalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a X-ray diffraction pattern chart of the oxygen vacancy type titanium dioxide and ruthenium-oxygen vacancy type titanium dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The crystal form of titanium oxide includes anatase and rutile. The titanium source compound is selected from tetraethyl titanate, tetrabutyl titanate and tetraisopropyl titanate. The reducing agent includes ascorbic acid and sodium ascorbate. The lithium salt is selected from lithium chloride, lithium sulfate and lithium acetate. The ruthenium source compound is selected from ruthenium trichloride, ruthenium nitrate and ruthenium acetylacetonate. The raw material for the reaction is carbon dioxide/hydrogen mixed gas. Preferred Process: The preparation of titanium oxide carrier with oxygen vacancy involves mixing N,N-dimethylformamide, acetic acid, lithium salt, reducing agent and titanium source compound, and heating to reacting to obtain a titanium oxide carrier with oxygen vacancy. The preparation of titanium oxide with the ruthenium ammonia complex adsorbed on the surface involves dispersing the titanium oxide carrier with oxygen vacancy in water, adding aqueous ammonia to adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution, adding the ruthenium source compound, forming the ruthenium ammonia complex by Liaoyuan compound is adsorbed on the surface of titanium oxide nanocrystal through electrostatic adsorption to obtain titanium oxide with the ruthenium ammonia complex adsorbed on the surface. Preferred Conditions: The mass fraction of ruthenium is 0.05-3 wt.%. The oxygen vacancy content in the titanium oxide carrier is 0.2-5 wt.%. The volume ratio of the N,N-dimethylformamide and acetic acid is 3:1-1:3. The concentration of the lithium salt is 0.05-1.0 molL-1, the reducing agent is 0.01-0.20 molL-1, and the titanium source compound is 0.2-1.0 molL-1, and the ruthenium source compound is 0.1-10.0 mmolL-1. The N,N-dimethylformamide, acetic acid, lithium salt, reducing agent and titanium source compound are mixing, and heating at temperature of 160-200degrees Celsius, and the reaction time is 12-36 hours. The mass-volume ratio of the titanium oxide, water and aqueous ammonia is 1 g: 100-300 mL: 50-250 mL; The titanium oxide carrier with oxygen vacancy is dispersing in water, adding aqueous ammonia to adjusting the pH value of the mixed solution to 10-12. The light source of the reaction of carbon dioxide hydromethanation reaction is a xenon lamp, a light emitting diode lamp or a solar concentrator; The light intensity of the light source is 0.4-20 watt.cm-2.

AU YANG Z
   LI C
   LEI P
   ZHANG L
   FENG L
AE GUANGDONG PHNIX ENERGY SAVING EQUIP CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021A4648T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves presetting a pressure threshold value for comparing with a pressure difference value to obtain different adjusting modes of an electronic expansion valve. A first-stage compression exhaust pressure value and a second-stage compression exhaust pressure value are obtained. A pressure difference value of the second stage compression exhaust pressure value and the first stage compression exhaust pressure value are calculated. The pressure difference value is compared with the pressure threshold value. A signal is sent for adjusting an opening degree of the electronic expansion valve.
   USE - Method for controlling a carbon dioxide (CO2) heat pump system.
   ADVANTAGE - Method enables flexibly adjusting the electronic expansion valve opening degree according to different comparison result so as to avoid the second-stage compression failure of the double-stage rotor compressor in advance. The method enables preventing the rotor compressor second stage compression failure caused by accident in the working process and ensuring the reliability of the working.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a control device for CO2 heat pump system;
   (2) a control terminal with first pressure sensor; and
   (3) a computer-readable storage medium having stored instructions for implementing the method for controlling a carbon dioxide (CO2) heat pump system.

PN CN112209647-A; CN112209647-B
TI Preparing green energy-saving artificial lightweight aggregate comprises grinding converter steel slag powder as raw material for preparing aggregate, controlling average grain diameter of steel slag powder particles.
AU LIN Z
   JIANG Y
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 202107820Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing green energy-saving artificial lightweight aggregate involves using the grinding converter steel slag powder as raw material for preparing aggregate, with an average grain diameter of 1-100 microns. The steel slag powder is placed into the granulating device and introduced carbon dioxide (CO2) gas until its concentration reaches 20-100 wt.% to form the steel slag powder-carbon dioxide-based gas-solid blending environment. The mass ratio of the steel slag powder is 0.2:1-0.5:1 of the standard preparation granulating water, and the speed of 50-100 milliliters/minute in the form of atomizing water ball to obtain steel slag powder-carbon dioxide-water mixing environment. The granulating device is operated to obtain the artificial aggregate blank. The artificial aggregate is placed at 20-40 degrees C, the relative humidity is 50-95 %, the CO2 concentration is 0-100 wt.% of the environment to perform subsequent curing for 1-4 days to obtain the artificial lightweight aggregate.
   USE - Method for preparing green energy-saving artificial lightweight aggregate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need a foaming agent and calcining at high temperature, it is simple and easy, cost-effective, at the same time, it can fix carbon dioxide, reduce carbon emission, green and environment-friendly, it provides a new preparation method for preparing light aggregate for artificial sandstone aggregate industry; it has an important meaning.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Parameters: The steel slag powder is used as the only solid material. The state of the water is atomized water ball. The grain diameter of the water ball is 1 mu m-1 mm. Preferred Conditions: The grain diameter of the aggregate is 1.18-20 millimeters (mm). The porosity of the aggregate is 20-50 %. The bulk density of the aggregate is 500-1000 kilograms/meter-cube (kg/m3). The grain diameter of the solid powder material is 1-50 micrometers ( mu m). The environment in the granulating device is a gas-solid blending environment and the concentration of carbon dioxide is 20-100 wt.%. The mass ratio of the dosage of the water and the steel slag powder is 0.2:1-0.5:1, the water supply speed is 40-100 millimeters/minute (mL/min). The subsequent maintenance condition is temperature 20-40 degrees C, relative humidity 50-95 %, carbon dioxide concentration 0-100 wt.%, and the time is 1-4 days.

AB    NOVELTY - Hydrothermally preparing ultrafine bismuth-rich bismuth bromide oxide nanotubes comprises (i) adding de-ionized water to polytetrafluoroethylene-lined reaction kettle, respectively adding polyol, bismuth source and surfactant, and mixing to obtain mixed solution; (ii) slowly dropping 0.2 mol/l sodium bromide solution into mixed solution, stirring at 1000 revolutions/minute, continuously dropping 2 mol/l sodium hydroxide solution, adjusting pH to 10-13 and continuously stirring for more than 10 minutes; and (iii) hydrothermally reacting at 140-180 degrees C for 2-12 hours under reaction kettle, cooling to room temperature, centrifuging, and sequentially washing with ethanol and de-ionized water. In the step (i), the volume ratio of polyol, bismuth source, surfactant and de-ionized water is 2-300:0.5-1.5:4-16:25-40 (mmol:mmol:mmol:ml). In the step (ii), the volume ratio of mixed solution, sodium bromide solution and sodium hydroxide solution is 25-40:1-5:0.8-1.3 (ml:ml:ml).
   USE - The nanotubes is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method simple to operate; and has mild and controllable reaction condition, and strong practicability. The nanotubes has high-efficiency of photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction; and provides the new way for designing and synthesizing functional photocatalytic materials, and efficient catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The polyol is mannitol, ethylene glycol or glycerin, preferably mannitol. The bismuth source is bismuth(III) nitrate, bismuth(III) acetate, bismuth chloride or bismuth sulfate, preferably bismuth(III) nitrate. The surfactant is polyvinylpyrrolidone, cetrimonium chloride or ionic liquid, preferably polyvinylpyrrolidone. The volume ratio of polyol, bismuth source, surfactant and de-ionized water is 2.5:1:4:25 (mmol:mmol:mmol:ml), where the polyol is mannitol, bismuth source is bismuth(III) nitrate and surfactant is polyvinylpyrrolidone. The volume ratio of mixed solution, sodium bromide solution and sodium hydroxide solution is 25:5:1 (ml:ml:ml). The diameter of nanotubes is 5-6 nm. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the pH is adjusted to 11.65. The hydrothermal reaction is carried out at 160 degrees C for 3 hours under reaction kettle.

AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of methane involves supplying the source gas (G0) containing carbon dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen into the reactor (1) of the reaction apparatus (10) comprising catalyst, heating the reactor to more than the predetermined temperature by supplying heat from an external heat source, performing methanation reaction for converting carbon dioxide to methane in the reactor, reducing the temperature of the heat source to a temperature less than the predetermined temperature, and continuing the methanation reaction. The carrier comprises a metal oxide comprising at least one metal element chosen from cerium, zirconium, yttrium, aluminum, silicon, and magnesium. The catalyst metal comprises at least one metal chosen from nickel, ruthenium, rhodium, potassium, calcium, sodium and iridium.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacturing system, which consists of a reactor apparatus comprising a reactor and a catalyst provided in the reactor, and an oxygen concentration adjustment component for adjusting the oxygen concentration of the source gas supplied to the reactor.

AB    NOVELTY - The preparation of nitrogen-phosphorus-doped porous carbon material involves (1) dissolving 4,4'-bipyridyl, hydroxyethylidene diphosphonic acid and soluble copper salt in water, adding alkali to adjust pH of solution to 3-5, stirring reaction at room temperature, completing the reaction, obtaining a turbid solution, (2) filtering the turbid solution obtained in step (1), washing repeatedly with deionized water until the filtrate is neutral, obtaining a solid to obtain an nitrogen-phosphorus-doped metal organic framework (NPMOF) precursor, (3) calcining nitrogen-phosphorus-doped metal organic framework precursor obtained in step (2) under a nitrogen atmosphere, heating obtained powder to reflux with nitric acid, repeatedly rinsing with deionized water and ethanol, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of nitrogen-phosphorus-doped porous carbon material for rapid adsorption of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The nitrogen-phosphorus-doped porous carbon material has high specific surface area and polar surface, and high carbon dioxide adsorption selectivity with respect to nitrogen and oxygen molecules under 273 K. The nitrogen-phosphorus-doped porous carbon 700 material exhibits very high carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of 99.4 mL/g at 273 K. The adsorption capacity of nitrogen-phosphorus-doped porous carbon 700 material for carbon dioxide remained unchanged with almost no attenuation. The continuous adsorption enthalpy desorption test under condition of 298 K shows excellent cycle regeneration ability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble copper salt is copper nitrate, copper chloride and/or copper sulfate. Preferred Conditions: The heating rate of step (3) is 10 degrees C/minute, and the temperature at which the precursor is calcined is 500-800 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 4,4'-bipyridyl, hydroxyethylidene diphosphonic acid and soluble copper salt in step (1) is (1-1.5):(1-1.5):(1-1.5).

PN JP2018126090-A
TI Reforming process gas for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into methane by microorganisms reaction by supplying mixed gas to reactor containing hydrogen-assimilating chemical autotrophic methane-producing at a controlled flow volume.
AU YAO Y
   MURAI R
   WASHIMI I
   MOGI Y
AE JFE STEEL CORP (JFES-C)
GA 201863532W
AB    NOVELTY - Process gas is reformed to convert carbon dioxide and hydrogen contained in process gas emitted in iron production process into methane by microorganisms reaction, by preparing mixed gas by which composition is controlled by adding 2nd process gas which has different composition from 1st process gas to 1st process gas as component adjustment gas, and supplying mixed gas to reactor containing hydrogen-assimilating chemical autotrophic methane-producing microorganisms at a controlled flow volume.
   USE - A process gas reforming method for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen contained in process gas emitted in iron production process into methane by microorganisms reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the process gas emitted in an iron production process are converted into methane at highly efficient, simple, low-energy, and low-cost manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for process gas reformer comprising gas mixing unit for mixing 1st process gas and 2nd process gas to prepare mixed gas, reactor containing the hydrogen-assimilating chemical autotrophic methane-producing microorganisms, flow-control unit which controls the flow volume of the mixed gas supplied to the reactor, analysis unit which analyzes greater than or equal to 1 composition of the 1st process gas and the 2nd process gas, and control unit which controls the gas mixing unit based on the analysis result by the analysis unit to prepare the composition of the mixed gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Conditions: Mixed gas is reformed to methane at less than or equal to 100 degrees C in the reactor. Preferred Compositions: The 1st process gas is selected from blast furnace gas, combustion exhaust gas, or blended blast furnace gas and combustion exhaust gas. Molar ratio (H2/CO2) of hydrogen (H2) with respect to carbon dioxide (CO2) in the mixed gas is greater than or equal to 5. Preferred Processes: The gas after reaction in the reactor is discharged from the reactor, and at least a portion of the discharged gas is returned to the reactor. The hydrogen separated or concentrated from the process gas and/or the carbon dioxide separated or concentrated from the process gas are used as a portion of the 2nd process gas. Impurity which inhibits the microorganisms reaction is removed from 1st process gas, 2nd process gas, and/or mixed gas. Preferred Devices: The reformer further comprises circulation unit which returns greater than or equal to 1 portion of the gas discharged from the reactor to the reactor and impurity removal unit which removes the impurity which inhibits microorganisms reaction from 1st process gas, 2nd process gas, and/or mixed gas.

PN CN107723330-A
TI Preparation of high-yield natural astaxanthin comprises preparing fermentation substrate from kelp waste, activating Phaffia rhodozyma, inoculating in expansion medium with fermentation substrate, extracting strain, and purifying extract.
AU LUO S
AE LUO S (LUOS-Individual)
GA 201817141M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-yield natural astaxanthin comprises preparing fermentation substrate comprising kelp waste enzymolyzed with bromelain and papain mixed with straw cellulose dry matter, ammonium sulfate and molasses, inoculating activated Phaffia rhodozyma in expansion medium, mixing with fermentation substrate, cultivating sequentially with sterile oxygen aeration, and sterile carbon dioxide, oxygen and ethylene mixture aeration, placing obtained strain in extraction kettle with methanol as entrainer, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide, and purifying extract.
   USE - Method for preparing high-yield natural astaxanthin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple cultivation and high astaxanthin extraction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of high-yield natural astaxanthin comprises crushing kelp waste, ultrasonicating, enzymolyzing with bromelain and papain, mixing hydrolysate with straw cellulose dry matter, ammonium sulfate and molasses, and sterilizing by high-pressure steam to obtain fermentation substrate; inoculating Phaffia rhodozyma in activation medium with shaking, inoculating activated strain in expansion medium, adding fermentation substrate, stirring for 45-50 hours, cultivating for 45-50 hours while passing sterile oxygen continuously, aerating with 0.2-0.4 V/V.minute mixture of sterile carbon dioxide, oxygen and ethylene having 2-3:1-2:0.03-0.05 volume ratio, and culturing further for 45-50 hours to obtain Phaffia rhodozyma with high astaxanthin content; and placing obtained strain in extraction kettle with methanol as entrainer, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide, and purifying extract.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The mass ratio of bromelain and papain is 3-4:1. The mass ratio of hydrolysate, straw cellulose dry matter, ammonium sulfate and molasses is 1-2:1-2:0.2-0.4:1-2. The activation culture medium comprises 8-10 g/L glucose, 2-3 g/L yeast extract, 4-5 g/L peptone, and 2-3 g/L wort. The pH of activated culture medium is 5.5-6.5. The expansion culture medium comprises 0.01-0.05 g cinnamaldehyde, 3-4 g molasses, 4-5 g glucose, 2-3 g yeast extract, 4-5 g peptone and 2-3 g wort in 1 L water. The pH of expansion culture medium is 4.5-5.5. Preferred Method: The enzymolysis is carried out at 45-50 degrees C for 60-80 minutes. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction is carried out at 41-43 degrees C with extracting pressure of 38-42 MPa for 30-50 minutes.

TI Gas response-type resonant optical molecular            imprinted sensor comprises carrier, and imprinted            polymer, which is connected to carrier, and is obtained            by polymerizing carbon dioxide response functional            monomer and comonomer, where target virus template is            connected to imprinted polymer.

   GONG X
AE UNIV FOSHAN XIANGTAN GREEN INTELLIGENT (UYFO-Non-standard)
   UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 202184970C
AB    NOVELTY - A gas response-type resonant optical molecular                imprinted sensor comprises a carrier, and an                imprinted polymer, which is connected to the                carrier, and is obtained by polymerizing a carbon                dioxide response functional monomer and a                comonomer, where a target virus template is                connected to the imprinted polymer.
   USE - Gas response-type resonant optical molecular                imprinted sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas response-type resonant optical                molecular imprinted sensor adopts carbon dioxide                response type imprinted polymer, which improves                specific recognition capability of the sensor to                the target virus template, and effectively avoids                the substance accumulation caused by the                stimulation, resulting in failure to respond                compared with imprinted polymer of other chemical                stimulation mode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the gas response-type resonant                optical molecular imprinted sensor, which involves                adding carrier, carbon dioxide responsive                functional monomer, comonomer, crosslinking agent,                initiator and target virus template, and subjecting                to precipitation polymerization reaction at a low                temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the ZIF-8 carrier involves separately dissolving                  zinc source compound and 2-methylimidazole in a                  mass ratio of 1:(1-4) in methanol, quickly                  introducing the latter solution into former                  solution, stirring at room temperature for 8-20                  hours, centrifuging the obtained product, washing                  with methanol, and drying. The preparation method                  of ZIF-8 carrier involves adding 13-68 ml                  methanol to per 1 mg zinc source compound, and                  adding 6-31 ml methanol to per 1 mg                  2-methylimidazole. The modification process of                  the ZIF-8 carrier involves coating silicon                  dioxide on the surface of the carrier, and                  modifying the carbon-carbon double bond on the                  silicon dioxide-coated carrier. The coating step                  involves: adding carrier, solvent and deionized                  water, dispersing, adding catalyst under stirring                  conditions, dripping silylation reagent, and                  reacting at room temperature for 8-15 hours; and                  collecting the product by centrifugation, washing                  several times with deionized water and ethanol,                  and drying. The modifying step involves:                  dissolving the silicon dioxide-coated carrier in                  solvent, dispersing, introducing nitrogen, slowly                  adding compound providing carbon-carbon double                  bonds, reacting at 80-100 degrees C for 20-30                  hours, washing several times with methanol, and                  vacuum drying. Preferred Components: The                  initiator is a combination of ammonium persulfate                  and sodium bisulfite, or a combination of                  potassium persulfate and sodium bisulfate. The                  carrier is ZIF-8, silicon dioxide, iron(II,III)                  oxide, MIL-101, MOF-5 or UiO-66. The surface of                  the carrier is covered with a silicon dioxide                  layer grafted with carbon-carbon double bonds,                  and the imprinted polymer is connected to the                  carrier through the carbon-carbon double bonds.                  The zinc source compound is zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate, zinc nitrate monohydrate, zinc                  chloride, zinc acetate dihydrate or zinc bromide,                  preferably zinc nitrate hexahydrate. The catalyst                  is aqueous ammonia. Preferred Composition: The                  mass ratio of N,N-methylenebisacrylamide to                  ammonium persulfate or potassium persulfate is                  (1-5):1. The mass ratio of ammonium persulfate or                  potassium persulfate to sodium bisulfite is                  (5-20):1. The mass ratio of zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate and 2-methylimidazole is 1:2.2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent in the                  coating step is absolute ethanol, methanol or                  isopropanol. The silylation reagent is tetraethyl                  orthosilicate. The compound providing                  carbon-carbon double bond is                  3-methacryloxypropyltrimethoxysilane. The solvent                  in modification step is toluene, anhydrous                  methanol or anhydrous ethanol. Preferred                  Composition: The volume ratio of deionized water                  to solvent is 1:(4-10). The volume ratio of                  silylation reagent to catalyst is 3:(5-10).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The imprinted polymer is                  prepared by taking dimethylaminoethyl                  methacrylate as carbon dioxide response                  functional monomer, acrylamide as comonomer, the                  target virus template as template molecule, and                  N,N-methylenebisacrylamide as crosslinking agent,                  and initiating polymerization reaction with an                  initiator to form the imprinted polymer connected                  with target virus template. The preparation                  method of the sensor involves: adding carrier,                  comonomer and crosslinking agent, and introducing                  argon to remove oxygen; adding a carbon                  dioxide-treated aqueous solution containing                  carbon dioxide response functional monomer and                  the target virus template, and dispersing; and                  adding initiator and polymerizing at 0-5 degrees                  C for 10-30 hours in a carbon dioxide atmosphere,                  where 10-70 mu l target virus template is added                  to per 100 mg carrier, and 7.86-31.42 mg carbon                  dioxide response functional monomer is added to                  every 50 mu l target virus template. The molar                  ratio of carbon dioxide response functional                  monomer to comonomer is 1:(3-9). The mass ratio                  of the sum of the carbon dioxide response                  functional monomer and the comonomer to the                  crosslinking agent is (1-3):1. The carbon dioxide                  treatment process is carbon dioxide bubbling                  treatment.

GA 202169891S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves oxidizing a sample (1) of the liquid hydrocarbons in a given measured atmosphere to carbon dioxide and water by combustion. The proportions of isotopes of an element contained in the oxidized sample are determined spectroscopically. The determined proportions are corrected by the known reactant proportions from the measured atmosphere to the isotope proportions in the sample. The measured atmosphere contains more than ninety nine percent oxygen. The sample is evaporated in a chemically inert environment before it is completely oxidized to carbon dioxide and water. The sample is atomized in the measuring atmosphere through an injector before the sample is completely oxidized to carbon dioxide and water.
   USE - Method for determining isotope ratios in liquid hydrocarbons.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the build-up required for the oxidation of the sample, and eliminates the need for sample heating.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of the method for determining isotope ratios in liquid hydrocarbons.

   Combustion chamber (3)

PN CN112357937-A
TI Preparation of battery grade lithium carbonate involves stirring industrial lithium iron phosphate and distilled water in hydrogenation kettle, reacting with carbon dioxide, adding precipitator, standing, filtering, standing, and drying.
AU MO M
AE PINGXIANG TUOYUAN IND CO LTD (PING-Non-standard)
GA 202118204N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of battery grade lithium carbonate comprises adding industrial lithium iron phosphate and distilled water into a hydrogenation reaction kettle to prepare slurry, closing cover, introducing proper carbon dioxide gas into valve, and closing the valve; continuously stirring the slurry and fully reacting with carbon dioxide; adding precipitator into the lithium bicarbonate solution, standing for 10-20 minutes, separating out impurity, and filtering again to obtain pure lithium bicarbonate solution; introducing into a heating kettle, heating at high temperature, taking out, and filtering the waste residue; adding into heating kettle, adding proper amount of calcium hydroxide, and heating; filtering, adding filtrate into a pressurizing kettle, continuously increasing pressure, and converting the pure filtrate into a concentrated solution; and heating, introducing carbon dioxide to react, standing for 20-30 minutes, separating out impurities, and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparation of battery grade lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared lithium carbonate has improved purity, is good for subsequent use, and meets multi-aspect requirement.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of the industrial lithium iron phosphate to the distilled water is 1:20. The precipitator is polyaluminum chloride, polyferric sulfate, and/or polymeric ferric aluminum sulfate.

PN CN111139051-A; CN111139051-B
TI Foam sealant useful for carbon dioxide flooding of heavy oil reservoirs, comprises alpha-alkenyl sulfonate, quaternary ammonium salt imidazoline, hydroxyethyl cellulose, sodium triphosphate and water.
AU JIANG L
   LI S
   QI S
   ZHU D
   QIN Y
   DING X
   WU Q
   LI J
   HU P
   YANG S
AE SHANDONG XINGANG CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202042450V
AB    NOVELTY - Foam sealant comprises 40-60 wt.% alpha -alkenyl sulfonate, 10-20 wt.% quaternary ammonium salt imidazoline, 3-5 wt.% hydroxyethyl cellulose, 10-15 wt.% sodium triphosphate and water (balance amount).
   USE - The foam sealant is useful for carbon dioxide flooding of heavy oil reservoirs (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The foam sealant: controls gas channeling and fluidity, thus increases sweep volume of carbon dioxide flooding to achieve the purpose of regulating flooding and equal fluidity control; and achieves the goal of improving recovery factor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for:
   (1) preparing foam sealant, comprising adding water into reaction kettle at 40-60 degrees C, then adding sodium triphosphate to the reactor with stirring until sodium triphosphate is dissolved, then adding slowly hydroxyethyl cellulose to the reactor and continuing to stir until dissolved, and adding alpha -alkenyl sulfonate and quaternary ammonium salt imidazoline to the reactor, then stirring and mixing to obtain final product; and
   (2) use of foam sealant for carbon dioxide flooding of heavy oil reservoirs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alpha -alkenyl sulfonate is of formula (R1-CH=CH-(CH2)nSO3M) (I). The quaternary ammonium salt imidazoline is of formula (II). The hydroxyethyl cellulose is of formula (III). The mass ratio between water, sodium triphosphate and (III) is 4-5.7:3-3.4:1. The mass ratio between (I), (II) and water is 2-6:1:1-1.7. Preferred Method: The foam sealer is suitable for reservoir temperature of less than or equal to 160 degrees C; salinity of less than or equal to 100x 103 mg/l; sum of calcium(II) ion and magnesium(II) ion concentration of less than 1200 mg/l, reservoir permeability of 0.05x 103-5000x 103 mu m2 in carbon dioxide flooding. The foam sealant is used in indoor experiments: when the mass concentration is 0.5%, the bubble volume is not less than 195 ml, and half-life is not less than 65 minutes; when the mass concentration is 1 %, the corrosion inhibition rate is not less than 73%.R1 = 9-13C alkyl;n = 1-3;M = Na+ or K+;R2 = (CH2)nCOO- (IV) or (CH2)nSO3- (V);m = 1-4;R3 = alkyl group having less than 4 carbon atoms;R = H or hydroxy moiety of formula (VI);x = 1 or 2; andp = 150-7500.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a Grey Wolf                optimizer (GWO)-least squares support vector                machine (LSSVM) prediction model. Minimum phase of                non-pure carbon dioxide and crude oil by using an                optimized prediction model. Error analysis is                performed on a prediction result. PN CN109676219-A
TI Welding method for single-side welding and double-face forming of carbon dioxide ceramic liner, involves adjusting welding pitch is according to actual groove size, and removing ceramic liner on back of weld.
AU XIA X
AE CHONGQING XINYU PRESSURE CONTAINER MFR (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201940772P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves assembling welding groove. The base metal (2) to be welded is assembled and fixed according to the required groove shape, and ceramic liner (3) is pasted on the back of the groove. The groove surface is cleaned. The first layer of bottom welding is performed on the weld at the groove by carbon dioxide gas shielded welding, and the welding pitch is adjusted according to the actual groove size. The ceramic liner on the back of the weld is removed, and weld cleaning is performed.
   USE - Welding method for single-side welding and double-face forming of carbon dioxide ceramic liner.
   ADVANTAGE - The welding speed and welding efficiency are improved. The high stability of base metal welding is achieved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram illustrating the welding method for single-side welding and double-face forming of carbon dioxide ceramic liner.
   Welding rod (1)
   Base metal (2)
   Ceramic liner (3)

PT P
PN CN109647497-A; CN109647497-B
TI Catalyst useful for preparing dimethyl carbonate from epoxide, methanol and carbon dioxide comprises 0.005-20 wt.% metal oxide and balance amount is HZSM-5 molecular sieve.
AU SONG Q
   HAN L
   LIU P
   ZHANG K
   JI K
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 2019394693
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises 0.005-20 wt.% metal oxide and the balance amount is HZSM-5 molecular sieve.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for preparing dimethyl carbonate from epoxide, methanol and carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: is economical and has simple reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS is also included for:
   (1) preparing the catalyst by an impregnation method; and
   (2) use of the catalyst for preparing dimethyl carbonate, epoxide, methanol and carbon dioxide comprising adding quaternary ammonium salt and catalyst to the reaction system, controlling the reaction time in the reaction bed for 1-8 hours at 60-180 degrees C and carbon dioxide is 0.1-6 MPa, where the ratio of epoxide and methanol to the reactor volume is 10-50%, the molar ratio of epoxide and methanol is 1:1 to 1:30, and the molar ratio of quaternary ammonium salt and epoxide is 0.05 to 0.2. The molar ratio of the epoxide to the catalyst is 0.05-0.2 mmol/g based on the metal content of the metal oxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is zinc, magnesium, cerium, calcium, cobalt, lanthanum, gallium ,and/or zirconium oxide. The metal nitrate is a nitrate of zinc, magnesium, cerium, calcium, cobalt, lanthanum, gallium , and zirconium. The epoxide is ethylene oxide or propylene oxide. The quaternary ammonium salt is tetrapropylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium chloride, tetrapropylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium chloride, and tetrabutylammonium bromide. Preferred Method: The HZSM-5 molecular sieve has a molar ratio of silicon/aluminum of 10-130, a specific surface area of 200-1000 m2.g1, a particle size of 20-10000 nm, and an average pore diameter is 0.5-20 nm. The preparing of metal nitrate solution having a metal cation concentration of 0.01-0.50 g.ml1, adding to HZSM-5 molecular sieve, immersing for 1-24 hours, and drying at 60-150 degrees C for 2-24 hours to obtain solid, grinding and calcinzing at 200-450 degrees C for 3-12 hours.
DC E13 (Heterocyclics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN109137576-A
TI Dyeing of textile fabric, involves soaking fabric base in hot water, drying, immersing in desizing solution, drying, dyeing, transferring to reaction kettle, and fixing color in presence of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
AU LIU G
AE FOSHAN YIXIANG DISI CLOTHING DESIGN CO (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201905427Y
AB    NOVELTY - Dyeing of textile fabric, involves soaking the textile fabric base in hot water at 70-80 degrees C for 1-2 hours, taking out, drying, immersing in a desizing solution for a preset time, drying to obtain a pretreated textile fabric, dyeing the pretreated textile fabric in a dyeing system, transferring to a reaction kettle, introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction kettle, controlling the pressure of the reaction kettle at 8-15 MPa and temperature at 35-40 degrees C such that the fluid in the reaction kettle is supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, fixing color for 2-3 hours in the reaction kettle, relieving the pressure, opening the reaction kettle, and taking out the textile fabric after color fixing.
   USE - Dyeing of textile fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses supercritical carbon dioxide for chromogenic color fixing, which reduces the complexity of the entire dyeing process. The method enables easy control of dyeing quality, without requiring cleaning of dyeing system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Composition: The desizing solution is a desizing enzyme solution comprising BH-7658 enzyme, a salt, penetrant JFC (alkylphenol ethoxylate), and water. The dyeing agent comprises 1-5 pts. wt. polypropylene glycol, 1-5 pts. wt. zinc stearate, 4-8 pts. wt. trimethylolpropane, 4-8 pts. wt. camphor leaf extract, 2-6 pts. wt. butyl methacrylate, 4-8 pts. wt. hydroxypropyl methyl cellulose, 3-7 pts. wt. dibutyltin dilaurate, 3-7 pts. wt. iron oxide red, 3-7 pts. wt. imidazoline, 5-9 pts. wt. blocked cyanate, 5-9 pts. wt. aluminum sulfate, and 2-6 pts. wt. polyvinylpyrrolidone. Preferred Material: The fabric base comprises natural fiber and/or synthetic fiber. Preferred Condition: When the fabric base in immersed in the slurry, it is turned once every half an hour.
DC A87 (Textile auxiliaries.); F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).); F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN CN108909114-A
TI Carbon dioxide-based composite material for hose, comprises outer layer, barrier layer comprising polypropylene carbonate-lactic acid and ethylene-vinyl alcohol copolymer, adhesive layer, and inner layer, from outside to inside.
AU JIANG D
   CHEN S
   PENG X
   MENG Y
   WANG S
AE SHENZHEN BEAUTY STAR CO LTD (SHEN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU LIYING PLASTICS CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201898484Q
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide-based composite material comprises an outer layer, a barrier layer, an adhesive layer, and an inner layer, arranged from outside to inside in order. The barrier layer comprises polypropylene carbonate-lactic acid, and ethylene-vinyl alcohol copolymer.
   USE - Carbon dioxide-based composite material for hose.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material has excellent transparency, interlayer adhesion, and water blocking performance, and is prepared using economical and environmentally-friendly raw materials. The composite material is prepared using barrier material having excellent barrier properties, mechanical properties and thermal stability, and adhesive layer having excellent adhesion force and bond with barrier material, and which can be prepared using simple method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the carbon dioxide-based composite material, which involves adding polyethylene, hot melt adhesive layer material and barrier layer material to a screw extruder (A), screw extruder (B), and screw extruder (C), respectively, melting and extruding, stretching, cooling, and slitting.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The barrier layer comprises 50-70 parts mass polypropylene carbonate-lactic acid, and 30-50 parts mass ethylene-vinyl alcohol copolymer. The adhesive layer comprises 70-90 parts mass ethylene-(meth)acrylic copolymer and 10-30 parts mass rosin. Preferred Property: The polypropylene carbonate-lactic acid has number average molecular weight of 30000-100000, and melt index of 2-5 g/10 minutes. The adhesive layer has melt index of 2-7 g/10 minutes. Preferred Components: The inner layer and outer layer comprise polyethylene material. An adhesive layer is further arranged between the outer layer and barrier layer. Preferred Conditions: The screw extruder (A) is operated at 160-190 degrees C, and 400-600 rad/second. The screw extruder (B) is operated at 100-190 degrees C, and 800-1100 rad/second. The screw extruder (C) is operated at 150-190 degrees C and 600-900 rad/second.
