PN CN108479691-A
TI Preparing lithium silicate-based carbon dioxide            adsorbent comprises e.g. dissolving lithium nitrate in            ethanol, heating, adding silicon dioxide waste residue            and halloysite nanotube, stirring, drying and heating            in muffle furnace.
AU DING T
   XIE H
   GAO N
   LI X
   TIAN Y
   WU M
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 201870755N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium silicate-based carbon                dioxide adsorbent comprises (i) dissolving lithium                nitrate in absolute ethanol, placing in water bath                and heating to 30-55 degrees C to obtain lithium                nitrate solution; (ii) adding silicon dioxide waste                residue produced during processing of coal-based                kaolin and halloysite nanotube into lithium nitrate                solution, and stirring, where the mass of                halloysite nanotube is 5-20% mass of silicon                dioxide waste residue; (iii) adding aqueous                ammonia, stirring, allowing to stand and completely                drying at 80 degrees C; and (iv) heating the                mixture obtained in step (iii) to 650-800 degrees C                in a muffle furnace for 4-6 hours and cooling to                room temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lithium                silicate-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent realizes low temperature                adsorption, is economical, and has carbon dioxide                adsorption capacity of 220, 300 and 350 mg at 550,                600 and 650 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  heating is carried out at 40-55 degrees C. In the                  step (iv) heating rate is 20-30 degrees C/minute.                  Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the mass                  of halloysite nanotube is 5-15% mass of silicon                  dioxide waste residue.

PN KR1853260-B1
TI Manufacture of amine polymer sevelamer carbonate salt used as e.g. antihyperphosphatemic agent, involves reacting poly(allylamine) aqueous solution and carbon dioxide, crosslinking, adding sodium bicarbonate and reacting with carbon dioxide.
AU JIN K D
   HUI K C
   HEUNG S T
   HEE C I
   BEA L S
AE FIRSON (FIRS-Non-standard)
GA 201847009Q
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of amine polymer sevelamer carbonate salt (I) involves reacting a poly(allylamine) aqueous solution with a carbon dioxide gas to produce carbonate salt of poly(allylamine) polymer including 25-45 wt.% poly(allylamine), adding a crosslinking agent e.g. epichlorohydrin, performing crosslinking reaction, obtaining reaction liquid including sevelamer carbonate and small amount of sevelamer carbonate hydrochloride, adding sodium bicarbonate, mixing, and reacting with carbon dioxide gas or sodium hydrogen carbonate aqueous solution. The amount of sodium bicarbonate is 1.2 equivalent or more based on amount of the reaction liquid.
   USE - Manufacture of amine polymer sevelamer carbonate salt used in pharmaceutical composition and used as antihyperphosphatemic agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, efficient, environmentally-friendly, economical and easy manufacture of amine polymer sevelamer carbonate salt having chloride content of less than 0.05%, without using excessive amount of organic solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The amine polymer sevelamer carbonate salt is of formula (I).
   a,b = number of primary amino;
   a+b = 90;
   c = 1;and
   n = positive integer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of epichlorohydrin and poly(allylamine) compound is 3-12/1. The concentration of poly(allylamine) aqueous solution is 7-17 weight/weight%.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN US2018127637-A1
TI Enhancing recovery from a subterranean formation, comprises injecting a treatment solution containing at least one water soluble carbon dioxide-generating compound into a subterranean formation containing oil and/or natural gas.
AU HARWELL J
   SHIAU B
   KADHUM M
   WANG S
AE UNIV OKLAHOMA (UOKH-C)
GA 201835944J
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing recovery from a subterranean formation, comprises injecting a treatment solution containing at least one water soluble carbon dioxide-generating compound into a subterranean formation containing oil and/or natural gas, where the water soluble carbon dioxide-generating compound dissociates to form carbon dioxide, thus enhancing recovery of at least one of oil and natural gas recovery from it.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing recovery from a subterranean formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the step of injection of the treatment solution into the subterranean formation that causes at least one of oil phase swelling, reduction of oil viscosity, and reduction of oil-water interfacial tension in the subterranean formation, and causes a reduction of the residual oil saturation of the subterranean formation of at least 1%, preferably at least 5-15% more than a reduction resulting from a conventional water flooding treatment; and enhances oil recovery by at least 5%, preferably 5-25% greater than oil recovery obtained by conventional water flooding (claimed).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the treatment solution is introduced into the subterranean formation in the absence of a surfactant or chelating agent. Preferred Components: The water soluble carbon dioxide-generating compound includes ammonium carbamate or urea. The concentration of the water soluble carbon dioxide-generating compound in the treatment solution is 1-15 (preferably 5-8)%. The treatment solution comprises a catalyst able to catalyze the dissociation of the water soluble carbon dioxide-generating compound, where the catalyst is at least one of vanadium pentoxide and a carbon nanotube-silica nanohybrid nanoparticle. The reservoir of the subterranean formation is an unconventional reservoir.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. PN CN109619338-A
TI Preparing fresh autumn pear beverage comprises            e.g. mixing juice, sodium fluoride, citric acid,            xylitol, and sodium bicarbonate, filtering, clarifying            juice, subjecting to high pressure carbon dioxide            sterilization, sealing and sterilizing.
AU TONG W
   WANG W
   WANG Y
   WANG Z
   JIA X
   DU Y
   JIANG Y
   SONG L
AE CAAS POMOLOGY INST (CAGS-C)
GA 201937737V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing fresh autumn pear beverage comprises                e.g. (i) pre-processing picked pear fruit, placing                outdoor, and selecting a pear fruit with a hardness                of 2.5-3.2 kg/cm2; (ii) washing off the soil and                leaves on the surface of the pear fruit, and                removing residual pesticide and bacteria on the                surface; (iii) directly squeezing without peeling                the core, and adopting screw extrusion; (iv) adding                the juice obtained in step (iii) into ascorbic acid                and stirring; (v) filtering and removing large                particles from the juice; (vi) mixing juice                obtained in step (iv), sodium fluoride, citric                acid, xylitol, and sodium bicarbonate; (vii)                filtering the juice through membrane filter for                clarifying the juice and removing bacteria in the                juice; (viii) subjecting to high pressure carbon                dioxide sterilization; (ix) adding saturated carbon                dioxide through a carbon dioxide machine; and (x)                sealing the prepared carbonate pear juice into a                package and sterilizing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fresh                autumn pear beverage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces labor intensity. The                beverage is nutritious; and has good taste.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing fresh autumn pear beverage comprises                (i) pre-processing picked pear fruit, placing                outdoor for 5-7 days, and selecting a pear fruit                with a hardness of 2.5-3.2 kg/cm2; (ii) washing off                the soil and leaves on the surface of the pear                fruit, and removing residual pesticide and bacteria                on the surface; (iii) directly squeezing without                peeling the core, and adopting screw extrusion;                (iv) adding the juice obtained in step (iii) into                0.2-0.5 wt.% ascorbic acid and stirring uniformly;                (v) filtering and removing large particles from the                juice; (vi) mixing uniformly 70-86 pts. wt. juice                obtained in step (iv), 1-5 pts. wt. sodium                fluoride, 1-5 pts. wt. citric acid, 1-5 pts. wt.                xylitol, and 1-5 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate; (vii)                filtering the juice through membrane filter for                clarifying the juice and removing bacteria in the                juice; (viii) subjecting to high pressure carbon                dioxide sterilization at 35-40 degrees C for 50-60                minutes under pressure of 25-30 MPa; (ix) adding                50-100 pts. wt. saturated carbon dioxide through a                carbon dioxide machine; and (x) sealing the                prepared carbonate pear juice into a package and                sterilizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The step (vi)                  comprises 74 pts. wt. juice, 5 pts. wt. sodium                  fluoride, 5 pts. wt. citric acid, 1 pts. wt.                  xylitol, and 1 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate.                  Preferred Method: The high pressure carbon                  dioxide sterilization is carried out at 38                  degrees C for 55 minutes under pressure of 38                  MPa. In the step (v), the filtering is carried                  out through 200 mesh filter cloth.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-A04; D03-H01A; D03-H01G1; D03-H02F; D03-K08

AU WU J
AE WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
GA 2018618924
AB    NOVELTY - Hypochlorous acid preparation device for producing chlorohydrin epoxide, comprises hypochlorous acid reactor (1), dehydrochlorination reactor (2), gas-liquid separator (3), injector (4) and carbon dioxide recovery reactor (5). The device is connected between pipeline and middle tank, material conveying device, control and display instrument and accessories, hypochlorous acid reactor is provided with water inlet with chlorine. The hypochlorous acid reactor outlet of the dehydrochlorination reactor inlet through pipeline, communication line or the dehydrochlorination reactor is provided with calcium carbonate, gas-liquid separator is provided with a material inlet, liquid phase outlet and a gas phase outlet. The material inlet of the dehydrochlorination reactor of the outlet of the gas-liquid separator through pipeline. The gas phase inlet of gas-liquid separator of the vapor outlet of injector through pipeline connected with other inlet of injector is calcium hydroxide (slurry) inlet.
   USE - The device is used for preparation of hypochlorous acid, which is used for producing chlorohydrin epoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The hypochlorous acid preparation device improves alcohol reactor outlet concentration, increases the production capacity of device, reduces the saponification waste water amount and has easy processing and resource utilization, economic benefit and environmental benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing epoxide hypochlorous acid producing device, which comprises:
   (A) adding chlorine water into hypochlorous acid reactor while flowing, mixing, and reacting to generate hydrogen chloride and hypochlorous acid;
   (B) mixing hydrogen chloride and hypochlorous acid mixed liquid and calcium carbonate into dehydrochlorination reactor;
   (C) reacting calcium carbonate and hydrogen chloride reacting to generate calcium chloride, carbon dioxide and water;
   (D) adding mixed material of the dehydrochlorination reactor into the gas-liquid separator for gas-liquid separation;
   (E) separating gas-liquid separator of carbon dioxide and calcium hydroxide in the ejector for mixing and initial reaction;
   (F) adding mixture from liquid outlet of injector into carbon dioxide recovery reactor; and
   (G) mixing, and reacting carbon dioxide with calcium hydroxide to generate calcium carbonate and water, adding into dehydrochlorination reactor to obtain hypochlorous acid aqueous solution chlorine-free from the liquid phase outlet of the gas-liquid separato.

PT P
PN IN201611037018-A
TI Metal salts of amino acids used as catalyst for copolymerizing epoxides with carbon dioxide, comprises metal salt precursors and amino acids in preset molar ratio, and exhibit vibrational frequencies in glycine at preset wavenumbers.
AU PATNAM P L
   KUMAR P
   JAIN S L
   RAY S S
AE COUNCIL SCI & IND RES INDIA (COUI-C)
GA 201836283C
AB    NOVELTY - Metal salts of amino acids comprises metal salt precursors and biocompatible amino acids in a molar ratio of 1:1-1:5, and exhibit vibrational frequencies related to carboxylate anion groups in glycine at wavenumbers of 1680 cm-1 and 1391 cm-1, when measured using Fourier-transform infrared spectroscopy.
   USE - Metal salts of amino acids used as catalyst for copolymerizing epoxides e.g. propylene oxide, styrene oxide, cyclohexene oxide, and glycidyl methacrylate, preferably propylene oxide with carbon dioxide for producing poly(alkylene carbonate) (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to structural components, impact-resistant glazing, streetlight globes, household appliance components, electrical/electronic device components, compact disks, automotive components, reusable bottles, and food and drink containers.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal salts of amino acids are efficiently derived from economical, biodegradable, easily accessible amino acids and metal salt precursors, and used for copolymerizing epoxides with carbon dioxide to selectively produce poly(alkylene carbonate) without any formation of side products and with high purity and high yield. The copolymerization of epoxides with carbon dioxide using the catalyst is simple, economical and environmentally-friendly, and can be carried out under solvent-less conditions. The catalyst can be recycled and reused for 5 runs without any decrease in its catalytic activity during copolymerization.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of metal salts of amino acids, which involves reacting amino acids with metal salt precursors in a polar protic medium at 50-70 degrees C and at atmospheric pressure for 2-5 hours to obtain a clear solution, adding dropwise hydroxide solution in the clear solution to adjust the pH to 6-10 to obtain precipitate, separating the metal salts of amino acids from precipitate by centrifugation, washing and drying; and
   (2) copolymerization of epoxide with carbon dioxide, which involves copolymerizing epoxide with carbon dioxide in an autoclave batch reactor comprising 0.02-0.5 wt.% metal salt of amino acids as catalyst, at 50-150 degrees C and carbon dioxide pressure of 10-60 bar for 5-30 hours with stirring under solvent-less conditions, cooling the reactor to 20-35 degrees C, separating poly(alkylene carbonate) from the mixture by extraction with polar aprotic solvent, and recovering the catalyst for reuse. The weight ratio of epoxides to catalyst in the reactor is 10:1-100:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal salt precursors are chosen from zinc chloride, zinc acetate, copper chloride, copper acetate, copper sulfate, nickel acetate, chromium chloride, cobalt acetate, and cobalt chloride. The hydroxide solution is sodium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amino acids are chosen from glycine, aspartic acid, glutamic acid, cysteine and lysine. The polar protein medium is hot distilled water, ethanol, methanol, butanol, and isopropanol, preferably water. Preferred Properties: The conversion of epoxide is 40-80% and selectivity of poly(alkylene carbonate) is 100%, in the copolymerization process.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); P41 (Crushing: centrifuging, separating solids (B02, B03, B04).)
MC A02-A; A05-E06A; A08-S02; A10-D03; A10-D05; J01-K; P41-E05; P41-U13

PN CN210520788-U; CN112237375-A
TI Bubble water preparation device for drinking water            machine, has mixing chamber provided with input port            and output port, where input port is utilized for            inputting carbon dioxide gas and purified water and            output port is utilized for outputting air bubble            water.
AU LUO Q
   GAO Y
   CHENG B
AE FOSHAN MIDEA CHONGHO WATER PURIFICATION (MEDE-C)
   MIDEA GROUP CO LTD (MEDE-C)
GA 202044875M
AB    (CN210520788-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims an air bubble water                producing device and drinking water machine, bubble                water preparation device comprising: a plurality of                mixing chamber are separated from each other, any                mixing chamber provided with an input port and an                output port, the input port for inputting carbon                dioxide gas and pure water, the output opening for                outputting air bubble water, gas tank, is connected                with the input port and used for containing the                carbon dioxide. The invention is provided with a                plurality of mixing cavity, a plurality of mixing                chamber can input the carbon dioxide gas and the                purified water, so it can be used for preparing                bubble water, and a plurality of mixing chamber are                separated from each other, so it can respectively                preparing bubble water with different                concentrations, so as to provide different                concentration of bubble water to the consumer so as                to meet several consumer demand of different                tastes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                bubble water preparation device. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Gas tank (10)
   Pressure reducing valve (20)
   Pressure relief valve (30)
   First gas path electromagnetic valve                (41)
   Second gas path electromagnetic valve                (42)
EA    NOVELTY - The device has multiple mixing cavities that                are separated from each other. Each mixing cavity                is provided with an enclosed space. An input port                and an output port are connected with the enclosed                space. The input port inputs carbon dioxide gas and                purified water. The output port outputs air bubble                water. A gas tank is connected with the input port                for containing carbon dioxide. Each mixing cavity                is provided with a first mixing chamber and a                second mixing chamber. The first mixing chamber and                the second mixing chamber are connected with each                other.
   USE - Bubble water preparation device for use in                water dispenser.
   ADVANTAGE - The device can respectively prepare bubble                water with different concentrations so as to                provide different concentration of bubble water to                the consumer, so that requirement of multiple                consumers for different taste can be                satisfied.

PT P
PN CN109703143-A; CN109703143-B
TI Preparing stretch resistant degradable barrier plastic packaging film comprises e.g. uniformly mixing carbon dioxide copolymer, maleic anhydride, melt-blending, crystalline polylactic acid, and anti-blocking agent, longitudinal stretching.
AU CHEN Q
   ZAN H
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
   SHANTOU SPECIAL ECONOMIC ZONE HETONG (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201942350K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stretch resistant degradable barrier plastic packaging film comprises e.g. (i) uniformly mixing carbon dioxide copolymer and maleic anhydride, then placing into extruder and melt-blending, (ii) mixing with hydroxy group-rich monodisperse silica microsphere, active monomer N-vinyl pyrrolidone, chain extender diisocyanate, radical initiator and extruding into an extruder as intermediate barrier, (iii) uniformly mixing crystalline polylactic acid, toughening agent, antistatic agent, antioxidant, slip agent and anti-blocking agent, and adding the ingredients into the corresponding extruder to melting and plastic, to obtain polylactic acid layer material, and (iv) using a three-layer co-extrusion extruder to combining in the T-die, using the intermediate barrier as intermediate layer, and using the polylactic acid layer as surface layer, covering both sides of carbon dioxide copolymer barrier layer, cooling, performing transverse stretching, longitudinal stretching, heat setting.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing stretch resistant degradable barrier plastic packaging film.
   ADVANTAGE - The plastic packaging film: has improved the tensile property and improved interlayer barrier property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing stretch resistant degradable barrier plastic packaging film comprises (i) uniformly mixing carbon dioxide copolymer and maleic anhydride as raw materials, then placing into extruder, melt-blending and modifying at 120-160 degrees C, and extruding and granulating to obtain anhydride-terminated carbon dioxide copolymer pellets, (ii) mixing the anhydride-terminated carbon dioxide copolymer pellets with hydroxy group-rich monodisperse silica microsphere, active monomer N-vinyl pyrrolidone, chain extender diisocyanate, radical initiator and extruding into an extruder as intermediate barrier, (iii) uniformly mixing crystalline polylactic acid, toughening agent, antistatic agent, antioxidant, slip agent and anti-blocking agent, and adding the ingredients into the corresponding extruder to melting and plastic at 150-180 degrees C to obtain polylactic acid layer material, and (iv) using a three-layer co-extrusion extruder to combining in the T-die, using the intermediate barrier obtained in step (ii) as intermediate layer, and using the polylactic acid layer obtained in step (iii) as surface layer, covering both sides of the carbon dioxide copolymer barrier layer, forming a melt film, cooling to obtain a film, performing transverse stretching, longitudinal stretching, heat setting to obtain final product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for stretch resistant degradable barrier plastic packaging film obtained above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), the mass ratio between carbon dioxide copolymer and maleic anhydride is 10-20:5-15. In step (ii), the radical initiator is cyclohexanone peroxide, dibenzoyl peroxide, or t-butyl hydroperoxide. In step (ii): the mass ratio between anhydride-terminated carbon dioxide copolymer pellets, hydroxy group-rich monodisperse silica microsphere, active monomer N-vinyl pyrrolidone, chain extender diisocyanate, radical initiator is 20-40:5-15:3-10:3-8:0.1. In step (iii), the toughening agent is maleic anhydride grafted ethylene-propylene copolymer, maleic anhydride grafted ethylene-propylene-diene terpolymer or copolymer of styrene and butadiene. The antistatic agent is carbon black, graphite, graphene, carbon nano-tubes or conductive metal powder the antioxidant is a phenolic antioxidant; the phenolic antioxidant is hydroquinone, thiobisphenol or trisphenol, the slip agent is erucamide or oleamide, the anti-blocking agent is silica, calcium carbonate, alumina, titanium dioxide, barium sulfate, or talc. The mass ratio between crystalline polylactic acid, toughening agent, antistatic agent, antioxidant, slip agent, and anti-blocking agent is 10-25:5-10:3-8:2-6:5-8:3-10. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the extrusion temperature of the extruder is 80-120 degrees C. In step (iv), the cooling temperature is 30-50 degrees C, the transverse stretching is that the film is preheated at 40-110 degrees C, the preheating time is 10-40 minutes, and the stretching ratio is 2-6 times, the longitudinal stretching is that the film is preheated at 40-85 degrees C, the preheating time is 10-40 minutes, the stretching ratio is 1-3 times, and the heat setting temperature is 50-160 degrees C. In step (iv), the heat setting temperature is 50-160 degrees C.

(including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A92 (Packaging and containers - including ropes and nets.); P73 (Layered products (B32).)

PT P
PN CN109322650-A; CN109322650-B
TI Method for cracking organic matter-containing formation to improve recovery rate of adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, involves injecting heat into shale layer containing organic matter, heating and mixing oil with carbon dioxide.
AU MO W
   LUO L
AE AMMAN SHALE TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (AMMA-Non-standard)
GA 201918563T
AB    NOVELTY - The method for cracking organic matter-containing formation to improve recovery rate of adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, involves injecting heat into shale layer containing organic matter, heating (I) shale, transferring carbon dioxide, 2-5C light hydrocarbon gas generated by initial cracking of organic matter to adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, mixing oil with carbon dioxide and light hydrocarbons to form gas flooding and carbon dioxide/light hydrocarbon miscible enhanced oil recovery, producing crude oil in reservoir from production well installed in reservoir, injecting heat into shale formation, heating (II), producing large amount of gas and light oil by production well installed in the shale, re-opening the oil production well of the oil-bearing reservoir in the oil recovery stage of terminating the shale formation, using natural gas generated by shale layer at high temperature to drive oil-bearing reservoir and maximizing oil recovery from oil-bearing reservoirs.
   USE - Method for cracking organic matter-containing formation to improve recovery rate of adjacent oil-bearing reservoir.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for enables cracking of organic matter-containing formation to improve recovery rate of adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, achieves high recovery rate, does not require fracturing of the oil-bearing reservoir and improves economic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for cracking organic matter-containing formationto improve recovery rate of adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, involves injecting heat into shale layer containing organic matter, heating shale at above 150 degrees C, transferring carbon dioxide, 2-5C light hydrocarbon gas generated by initial cracking of organic matter to an adjacent oil-bearing reservoir, completely mixing oil with carbon dioxide and light hydrocarbons by increasing pressure of oil-bearing reservoir to form gas flooding and carbon dioxide/light hydrocarbon miscible enhanced oil recovery, producing crude oil in oil-bearing reservoir from a production well installed in the reservoir, continuously injecting heat into the shale formation, heating at above 320 degrees C, producing a large amount of gas and light oil by production well installed in the shale, re-opening the oil production well of the oil-bearing reservoir in the oil recovery stage of terminating the shale formation, using natural gas generated by shale layer at high temperature to drive the oil-bearing reservoir and maximizing oil recovery from oil-bearing reservoirs.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The production well installed in the oil-bearing reservoir is closed at an appropriate stage. Preferred Conditions: The heating (I) temperature of shale is 200-320 degrees C. The heating (II) temperature of shale is 330-350 degrees C. The pressure in shale layer is maintained in the optimal oil generation range.
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

PN CN108084046-A; CN108084046-B
TI Preparing N,N'-dialkyloxyaldehyde amideby            artificial photosynthesis comprises reacting organic            amine and carbon dioxide in presence of a            catalyst.
AU SUN D
   LI C
   CHEN Q
   FANG Y
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 201843413S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing N,N'-dialkyloxyaldehyde amide by                artificial photosynthesis comprises reacting                organic amine and carbon dioxide in presence of a                catalyst to obtain N,N-dialkylformamide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                N,N'-dialkyloxyaldehyde amide by artificial                photosynthesis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic amine is                  alicyclic amine, aromatic amine and/or                  naphthylamine. The alicyclic amine is                  N-propylamine, butylamine, isobutylamine,                  N-amylamine, N-hexylamine, 1,6-hexanediamine,                  1,4-butanediamine, cyclobutylamine,                  cyclopentylamine, morphine or morpholine;                  aromatic amine is aniline, p-methylaniline,                  diphenylamine and/or trifluoromethylaniline; and                  naphthalene amine is 1-naphthylamine,                  2-naphthylamine and/or beta -naphthylamine. The                  catalyst is inorganic or organic semiconductor                  material having visible light response or                  modified semiconductor material. The inorganic                  semiconductor material is cadmium selenide,                  cadmium sulfide, cadmium oxide, iron(II) oxide,                  molybdenum trioxide, copper(I) oxide,                  tungsten(VI) oxide, titanium dioxide, vanadium                  pentoxide, zinc oxide, tantalum oxide, zirconium                  dioxide, Iridium(III) oxide, cobalt(II,III)                  oxide, stannic oxide, palladium(II) oxide,                  bismuth phosphate, molybdenum disulfide, iridium                  dioxide, platinum dioxide, titanium dioxide, p-n                  heterojunction type photo-catalyst, ohmic contact                  compound semiconductor, ABO3 structural composite                  of perovskite, perovskite-type structure,                  titanium dioxide or nano-scale p-n heterojunction                  novel titanium dioxide photocatalyst doped with                  at least one elements in the p region, the d                  region, and the f region of the periodic table or                  ABO4 structural composite, where A and B atoms                  include all transition metal elements in the                  periodic table that have different d orbital; or                  C2D2O7 structural composite, where C element                  includes elements of all rare earths or specific                  inert lone pairs in the periodic table; D-site                  element is transition metal element having a                  variable oxidation state in the periodic table;                  EF2O4 structural composite oxide, where E and F                  elements are +1 to +6 valence; organic                  semiconductor material is naphthalimide, perylene                  diimide, pentacene, triphenylamine, fullerenes,                  phthalocyanines, perylene derivatives or                  cyaninetype organic semiconductor material,                  polyacetylene, polyphenylene, polythiophene,                  polyaniline, polypyrrole, poly-copolymer type                  high-molecular type organic semiconductor                  material; and modified semiconductor material is                  phthalocyanine dye, rose bengal,                  8-hydroxyquinoline, violet, metal porphyrin                  compound semiconductor of                  photosensitizer-modified titanium dioxide e.g.                  2,2'-bipyridyl rhenium carbonyl chloride                  (bromide), cobalt-nitrogen-containing                  heterocyclic compound or nickel-carbene                  complexes. The solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide,                  sulfolane, propylene carbonate,                  dimethylformamide, hexamethylphosphoric triamide,                  N,N-dimethylformamide, N-methylpyrrolidone,                  diethylene glycol dimethyl ether,                  1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone, methyl tert-butyl                  ether, isopropyl ether, methyl tert-butyl ether,                  methyl ether, tetrahydrofuran,                  N-dimethylacetamide, diethylene glycol dimethyl                  ether, dichloromethane, dioxane, isopropyl ether,                  pyridine, acetonitrile,                  1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone, acetone,                  isopropyl ether, ethyl acetate, ethanol,                  methanol, N-butanol, N-pentanol with Lewis basic                  functional group or basic functional group or                  having various functional ionic liquid of L basic                  and B basic. The amount of catalyst is 0.01-4                  mol.% the organic amine. The amount of catalyst                  is 0.01-4 mol.% the molar amount of organic                  amine; and weight ratio between organic amine and                  solvent is 1-10:30. Preferred Method: The                  reaction temperature is room temperature to 120                  degrees C, reaction time is 4-72 hours, and                  catalytic light is first visible light. The                  reaction pressure is 0.1-8 Mpa, and carbon                  dioxide is derived from burning tail gas.

PN WO2022049966-A1; JP2022041378-A
TI Composition useful for improving cognitive functions e.g. understanding, judgment, memory, attention, calculation, thinking, learning and language, comprises carbon dioxide gas dissolved in water.
AU KYOSUKE W
   KEI M
   KYOKO E
   KANAKO T
   YASUYOSHI W
   WATARU K
   SEIICHI M
   WATANABE K
   MIZUNO K
   HIRUKO K
   TAJIMA K
   KOSUGI W
   MIZUNO S
AE ASAHI SOFT DRINKS CO LTD (ASAK-C)
   ASAHI GROUP HOLDINGS LTD (ASAK-C)
GA 202234599B
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises carbon dioxide gas dissolved in water as an active ingredient.
   USE - The composition in the form of beverage, preferably carbonated water is useful for improving cognitive function (all claimed). The cognitive function includes understanding, judgment, memory, attention, calculation, thinking, learning, information processing, work efficiency and language. The composition is also useful for promoting health.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is safe for long term ingestion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The dissolved amount of the carbon dioxide gas is 3.5 gas volume (GV) or more.
   ACTIVITY - Nootropic. In an in vivo test, the improvement in cognitive function attention-changing power was measured using advanced trial making test (ATMT) by administering 300 ml of carbonated water with 4.5 GV twice a day for 4 weeks to 44 subjects, having an average age of 38.6 plus minus 6.9 years. The results showed that the average value of the number of tasks was increased from 167.7 to 174.5 and the variation in reaction time was decreased from 1.53 seconds to 1.36 seconds.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The composition is administered orally at a dose of 100-500 ml, preferably 300-500 ml.
   EXAMPLE - The example provides details about the experiments on the change in cognitive function that do not support the claims.

The polymerization temperature is from about 35-65 degrees C, where number average molecular weight is from about 650-4000, and where polydispersity of poly(tetramethylene ether) diacetate is from about 1.8-5. The polymerization temperature is from about 40-60 degrees C, where number average molecular weight is from about 1000-3000, and where polydispersity is from about 2.0-4.0. The polymerization temperature is from about 35-65 degrees C, where polydispersity of polymerized polymer is from about 1.8-5, preferably 2.0-4, and where n is from 10-40, preferably 20-30. The polymerization temperature is from about 30-65 degrees C, and where number average molecular weight of poly(tetramethylene ether) diacetate is from about 500-5000, preferably 650-4000 and the polydispersity of poly(tetramethylene ether) diacetate is from about 1.5-6, preferably 1.8-5. The average pore diameter of halloysite catalysts is from about 125-250 angstroms, and polymerizing tetrahydrofuran or 3-methyl tetrahydrofuran with acetic anhydride in the presence of modified halloysite catalysts to produce poly(tetramethylene ether) diacetate. The polymerization temperature is from about 35-65 degrees C, preferably 40-60 degrees C, where number average molecular weight is from about 650-4000, preferably 1000-3000, and where polydispersity of poly(tetramethylene ether) diacetate is from about 2.0-4.0. The poly(tetramethylene ether) diacetate is produced by making a morphologically modified acid-based halloysite catalyst comprising, subjecting a halloysite catalyst to a supercritical carbon dioxide treatment, where supercritical treatment temperature is from about 200-290 degrees C, preferably 250-290 degrees C, where supercritical treatment pressure is from about 1200-3000 psi, where halloysite catalyst has the structural formula Al2Si2O5(OH)4.2H2O where ratio of SiO2 to Al2O3 is from about 1.7-3.0. The amount of any zeolite compound is about 10 wt.% or less based upon the total weight of halloysite catalyst, modified halloysite catalysts having an average pore diameter of from about 50-500 Angstrom , and polymerizing tetrahydrofuran or 3-methyl tetrahydrofuran with acetic anhydride in the presence of modified halloysite catalysts to produce poly(tetramethylene ether) diacetate..

PN WO2022123215-A2; WO2022123215-A3; GB2603743-A
TI Carbon dioxide recovering method for oxyfuel            combustion engine exhaust stream, involves condensing            portion of waste gas stream to provide partly condensed            stream, and separating condensed stream to form two            liquefied streams.
AU FELBAB N
AE LGE IP MANAGEMENT CO LTD (LGEI-Non-standard)
GA 202280359T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing and separating                an oxyfuel combustion engine exhaust stream to                provide a first liquefied CO2 stream and a first                waste gas stream. A portion of the first waste gas                stream is condensed to provide a partly condensed                waste gas stream. The condensed waste gas stream is                separated to provide a second waste gas stream and                a second liquefied CO2 stream. A portion of the                first waste gas stream is condensed using                combustion engine fuel and/or oxidant source                streams. The engine fuel or oxidant source stream                is provided as a cryogenic fuel or oxidant source                stream. The cryogenic oxidant source stream is                provided as liquefied oxygen.
   USE - Method of recovering carbon dioxide (CO2) from                oxyfuel combustion engine exhaust stream used for                heavy machinery and ship engine for industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the temperature of the cold                exchange working fluid (CEWF) in the carbon dioxide                condenser, thus preventing surface freezing of the                CO 2 in the CO2 Condenser, and hence improving the                performance of the gas engine. The method increases                the efficiency of the exhaust gas recirculation fan                (EGF), and reduces the pressure drop in the engine                exhaust stream, thus increasing the engine                efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                apparatus for recovering carbon dioxide from                oxyfuel combustion engine exhaust stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                method of recovering carbon dioxide from an oxyfuel                combustion engine exhaust stream.

   4First fuel heater
   6Oxygen
   8First oxygen heater

PT P
PN WO2018170263-A1
TI Producing ethylene involves combusting fuel gas stream and oxidant gas to form combustion product, pyrolysis of first reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons and portion of combustion product and cooling formed pyrolysis reaction product.
AU MAMEDOV A
   NAIR B
   GAUTAM P S
   PANNALA S
   SANKARANARAYANAN K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   NAIR B (NAIR-Individual)
   GAUTAM P S (GAUT-Individual)
   PANNALA S (PANN-Individual)
   SANKARANARAYANAN K (SANK-Individual)
GA 2018740277
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) ethylene comprises (a) introducing fuel gas stream and oxidant gas to combustion zone (110) to produce combustion product, (b) introducing first reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons and at least a portion of combustion product to first reaction zone (120) to produce pyrolysis reaction product via pyrolysis reaction, (c) cooling the portion of the pyrolysis reaction product in quench zone (130) to produce cooled pyrolysis reaction product, (d) introducing second reactant mixture comprising ethane and the portion of cooled pyrolysis reaction product to second reaction zone (140) to produce a second product mixture, (e) separating at least a portion of the second product mixture into an ethylene and acetylene stream and a carbon dioxide stream, and (f) recycling at least a portion of the carbon dioxide stream to the second reaction zone.
   USE - The process is useful for producing ethylene and methanol (claimed), where the ethylene is used in break-resistant containers and packaging materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes improve the overall efficiency for the conversion of natural gas to ethylene, while providing high yield of ethylene and methanol and reducing carbon dioxide emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing (P1) ethylene comprises (a) introducing fuel gas stream and oxidant gas to combustion zone (110) to produce combustion product, (b) introducing first reactant mixture comprising hydrocarbons and at least a portion of combustion product to first reaction zone (120) to produce pyrolysis reaction product via pyrolysis reaction, where the combustion product heats the hydrocarbons to first temperature effective for the pyrolysis, and the pyrolysis reaction product comprises unconverted hydrocarbons, acetylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and optionally ethylene, (c) cooling the portion of the pyrolysis reaction product in quench zone (130) to produce cooled pyrolysis reaction product, where the cooled pyrolysis reaction product has a second temperature that is lower than first temperature, (d) introducing second reactant mixture comprising ethane and the portion of cooled pyrolysis reaction product to second reaction zone (140) to produce a second product mixture, where the second reaction zone excludes a catalyst, and a portion of the second reactant mixture undergoes a dehydrogenation reaction to produce ethylene, where carbon dioxide of the second reactant mixture undergoes hydrogenation reaction to carbon monoxide, and the second product mixture comprises ethylene, carbon monoxide, acetylene, carbon dioxide and unconverted hydrocarbons, where amount of ethylene in the second product mixture is greater than amount of ethylene in the pyrolysis reaction product, and second product mixture has a third temperature that is lower than the second temperature, (e) separating at least a portion of the second product mixture into an ethylene and acetylene stream and a carbon dioxide stream, where the ethylene and acetylene stream comprises ethylene, acetylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and unconverted hydrocarbons, and (f) recycling at least a portion of the carbon dioxide stream to the second reaction zone. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing (P2) ethylene and methanol, comprising the step (a) as per se, (b1) separating a natural gas stream into methane and ethane, (c1) introducing a first reactant mixture comprising at least a portion of the methane and the combustion product to a first reaction zone to produce a pyrolysis reaction product via a pyrolysis reaction, where the combustion product heats the methane to a first temperature greater than or equal to 2500 degrees C, (d1) introducing at least a portion of the ethylene and acetylene stream and a polar aprotic solvent to a third reaction zone to produce a third reaction zone effluent via hydrogenation to ethylene of at least a portion of the acetylene in the ethylene and acetylene stream, where the third reaction zone comprises an acetylene hydrogenation catalyst, and the third reaction zone effluent comprises ethylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, unconverted methane, and unconverted ethane, and an amount of ethylene in the third reaction zone effluent is greater than an amount of ethylene in the ethylene and acetylene stream, (e1) separating at least a portion of the third reaction zone effluent into an ethylene stream, a syngas stream, an unconverted methane stream, and an unconverted ethane stream, where the syngas stream comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide, and (f1) introducing at least a portion of the syngas stream to a fourth reaction zone comprising a methanol production catalyst to produce a methanol stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic view of an ethylene production system.
   Combustion zone (110)
   First reaction zone (120)
   Quench zone (130)
   Second reaction zone (140)
   Ethylene production system (1000)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Process: The first reaction zone further excludes a catalyst. A common reactor comprises both the first and the second reaction zone and optionally the quench zone and the combustion zone. The first reactor comprises the first reaction zone and optionally the combustion zone, and where a second reactor comprises the second reaction zone and optionally the quench zone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The recycled carbon dioxide stream is further contacted with additional carbon dioxide during step (f). The recycled carbon dioxide stream and ethane are introduced to the second reaction zone via a common stream. The ethane dehydrogenation reaction further produces hydrogen, where at least a portion of the hydrogen from the ethane dehydrogenation reaction is used for the hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. A portion of ethane of the second reactant mixture and a portion of acetylene of the second reactant mixture undergo a comproportionation reaction to ethylene. The process (P1) further comprises steps (d1), (e1) and (f1) as above. The step (e) further comprises separating a recovered ethane stream from the unconverted hydrocarbons stream and at least a portion of the recovered ethane stream is recycled to the second reaction zone. At least a portion of the ethane of the second reaction mixture is separated from natural gas, shale gas, associated gas, well head gas and/or enriched gas. The first temperature is greater than or equal to 2000 degrees C, the second temperature is 700-1000 degrees C and the third temperature is 20-200 degrees C. The reduction in temperature from the second temperature to the third temperature is due to endothermic reactions occurring in the second reaction zone, where the endothermic reactions comprises ethane dehydrogenation reaction and the hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. The step (c) further comprises introducing a quenching fluid to the quench zone, where the quenching fluid comprises water, hydrocarbons and/or oil. The quenching fluid comprises water, where at least a portion of the quenching fluid is converted to steam during step (c). A carbon dioxide conversion in the second reaction zone is greater than or equal to 55%, where ethane conversion in the second reaction zone is greater than or equal to 65%. In the process (P2) at least a portion of the unconverted methane stream is recycled to the step (c1) as the methane and at least a portion of the unconverted ethane stream is recycled to the step (e1) as the ethane. Preferred Components: The second reactant mixture has a carbon dioxide to ethane molar ratio of 0.8:1-2:1.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises a top cycle combustion                engine generating system, a middle circulating                supercritical carbon dioxide generating system and                a bottom circulating absorption refrigeration                circulating pre- cooling system. The top                circulating combustion engine generation system                comprises a pre-heat exchanger, a combustion engine                compressor M, and a combustion chamber L, and the                combustion turbine J, a waste heat boiler heat                exchanger I, a intermediate heat exchangers D and a                generator Q sequentially. The middle circulation                super critical carbon dioxide power generation                system is provided with a main compressor A, a                low-temperature heat regenerator B, an intermediate                heat exchange D, a re-compression compressor C, a                high temperature heat regeneration E, the waste                heat boilers I, the high pressure turbine F, the                low-pressure turbine G, a coolant Y and a                pretension V sequentially connected with each                other.
   USE - Combined cycle system for pre-cooling                combustion engine waste heat based on supercritical                carbon dioxide cycle used in coal-fired power                station, nuclear power station and ship power                propulsion field.
   ADVANTAGE - The combined circulating system realizes the                low temperature cold source, the step utilization                of the high temperature heat source improves the                thermodynamic performance of the total circulating                system, at the same time, it avoids the performance                of system caused by the over-high environment                temperature, and widens the stable working                condition range of the system.

PN CN114925899-A
TI Prediction method of blast furnace gas component,            involves calculating carbon monoxide volume and carbon            dioxide volume based on first oxygen content, second            oxygen content and furnace gasification carbon            content.
AU LIU W
   HUO S
   WU J
   LI Y
   ZHAO H
   GE X
AE BEIJING ZHIYE INTERNET TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022B10806
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining (S101) a first                relevant parameter of the oxygen content of the air                blast in a preset time period. The second relevant                parameter of oxygen content is obtained (S102) in                the ore. The third relevant parameter is obtained                (S103) for calculating the gasified carbon quantity                of the blast furnace. The first oxygen content in                the blast air is calculated (S104) based on the                first relevant parameter. The second oxygen content                in the ore is calculated based on the second                relevant parameter. The volume of the carbon                monoxide and the volume of the carbon dioxide is                calculated (S105) based on the first oxygen                content. The second oxygen content and the amount                of the carbon monoxide are gasified by the blast                furnace.
   USE - Method for predicting blast furnace gas                component e.g. carbon monoxide gas and carbon                dioxide gas, in blast furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The blast furnace gas component prediction                method enables to know carbon monoxide volume and                carbon dioxide volume under the condition of                without carbon dioxide online monitoring device,                and enables to work staff the reaction condition in                the blast furnace.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a blast furnace gas component prediction                device;
   an electronic device;
   a computer readable storage medium storing                program for prediction of blast furnace gas                component.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                the blast furnace gas component prediction process.                (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   S101Step for obtaining first relevant                parameter of the oxygen content of the air blast in                a preset time period
   S102Step for obtaining second relevant                parameter of oxygen content in ore
   S103Step for obtaining third relevant                parameter for calculating gasified carbon quantity                of blast furnace.
   S104Step for calculating first oxygen content                in blast air based on first relevant                parameter
   S105Step for calculating volume of carbon                monoxide and volume of carbon dioxide based on                first oxygen content

AB    NOVELTY - Combination composition comprises 10-25 wt.% Pueraria lobata; 1-4 wt.% apricot leaf and sand ginseng; 2-5 wt.% Lycoris radiata; 8-16 wt.% Rehmannia glutinosa; 1-3 wt.% staghorn gum, 2-4 wt.% cinnamon, 0.5-1 wt.% Angelica, 0.2-0.8 wt.% Angelica dahurica, 0.4-0.6 wt.% white peony, 0.3-0.7 wt.% atractylodes rhizome, 2-3 wt.% roasted licorice, 4-9 wt.% sugar, 30-65 wt.% water, 2-6 wt.% rice wine and 3-6 wt.% additives.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the composition comprising adding fresh nostoc sphaeroides into water to soak for 2-3 hours, soaking and draining, putting dried nostoc sphaeroides into juice extractor, pulping and filtering to obtain nostoc sphaeroides liquid; weighing apricot, lycoris, prepared rehmannia root, cinnamon, Angelica, Angelica dahurica, radix paeoniae alba, large-headed atractylodes rhizome and licorice, cleaning and crushing, mixing, softening at 30-60 degrees C for 2-3 hours, spraying 30-50% Chinese herbal medicine warm water, processing mixture for supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction, and adding modifying agent in carbon dioxide fluid; adding 8-16 wt.% first carrying agent in carbon dioxide fluid, controlling extraction temperature to 80-100 degrees C and pressure of 12-18 MPa for 1-1.5 hours, and separating extract; introducing carbon dioxide fluid with modifier, adding second carrying agent in carbon dioxide fluid, extracting at 90-110 degrees C and 25-30 MPa for 1.5-2 hours, and separating extract; adding carbon dioxide fluid with modifier, extracting at 100-120 degrees C and 25-30 MPa for 1-2 hours, separating extract from Chinese herbal medicine, and purifying to obtain purified liquid; and melting deerhorn glue, yellow wine and ice sugar, mixing with extracting solution to obtain mixed solution, adding nostoc sphaeroides liquid into mixed solution, adding additive into mixed solution and injecting into water.

The first passage (11) is connected to the gas separation device so as to introduce an atmosphere into the gas separation device. The second passage (13) is connected between an exhaust passage (2) of the internal combustion engine (1) and the first passage so as to introduce exhaust gas of the internal combustion engine into the gas separation device. A gas separation membrane (121) is configured to separate a gas supply side (122) and a gas permeation side (123). The first passage is connected to the gas supply side.
   USE - Gas separation system such as carbon dioxide separation system mounted on vehicle such as hybrid vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The water vapor contained in the exhaust gas of the internal combustion engine is supplied to the gas separation device which improves the separation efficiency of the gas, by mounting the gas separation device on the vehicle provided with the internal combustion engine. The humidity of the gas to be introduced into the gas separation device is maintained more than the lower limit value of the predetermined range, while suppressing the consumption of water and energy. The energy consumed by the humidification device can be decreased. The amounts of HC and particulate matter (PM), flowing into the carbon dioxide separation device can be reduced. The HC and PM can be suppressed from adhering to the carbon dioxide separation membrane while decrease in the separation efficiency of carbon dioxide due to the HC and PM adhering to the carbon dioxide separation membrane can be suppressed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide separation system.
   Internal combustion engine (1)
   Exhaust passage (2)
   First passage (11)
   Gas separation device (12)
   Second passage (13)
   Gas separation membrane (121)
   Gas supply side (122)
   Gas permeation side (123)

TI Extracorporeal system for removing carbon dioxide            from e.g. patients blood, has first membrane component            fixed within cavity and for removing bicarbonate from            fluid passing between fluid inlet and fluid            outlet.
AU KELLER S P
   CHANG B Y
   WANG J
AE KELLER S P (KELL-Individual)
   CHANG B Y (CHAN-Individual)
   WANG J (WANG-Individual)
   KELLER S (KELL-Individual)
   CHANG B (CHAN-Individual)
   WANG J (WANG-Individual)
   CHANG B Y (CHAN-Individual)
   KELLER S P (KELL-Individual)
   CHANG B Y (CHAN-Individual)

   KELLER S P (KELL-Individual)
   CHANG B Y (CHAN-Individual)
   WANG J (WANG-Individual)
   KELLER S P (KELL-Individual)
GA 2019261405
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first membrane component                (112) fixed within a cavity (104) and for removing                bicarbonate from fluid passing between a fluid                inlet (108) and a fluid outlet (110), where the                first inlet in fluid communication with a second                membrane component (114) and delivers a sweep gas                to the first membrane component such that sweep gas                is passed through the first membrane component in                first direction and the first outlet in fluid                communication with the first membrane component and                receives sweep gas passed through the first                membrane component and the second inlet in fluid                communication with the second membrane component                and delivers a dialysate to the second membrane                component such that the dialysate is passed through                the second membrane component in a second direction                and the second outlet in fluid communication with                the second membrane component and receives the                dialysate passed through the second membrane                component.
   USE - Extracorporeal system for removing carbon                dioxide from a fluid i.e. patients blood to treat                HRF. Uses include but are not limited to chronic                obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), emphysema                and/or chronic bronchitis, asthma, cystic fibrosis,                obesity hypoventilation syndrome, pulmonary                fibrosis, chronic lung allograft dysfunction,                bronchiolitis obliterans syndrome, neuromuscular                disorders such as amyotrophic lateral sclerosis and                muscular dystrophy, myasthenia gravis.
   ADVANTAGE - The system maintains blood pH at desired level                and overall systemic homeostasis and generates                adequate blood flow. The system realizes removal of                carbon dioxide from patient's blood or another                fluid in safe and efficient manners and reduces                complexity, improves efficiency of removal of high                carbon dioxide load from the blood.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the                following:
   (1) a method for removing gaseous carbon                dioxide and bicarbonate from fluids
   (2) a method for treating a hypercarbic                respiratory failure (HRF).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a system for facilitating carbon dioxide removal                from patient's blood.
   Housing (102)
   Cavity (104)
   Fluid inlet (108)
   Fluid outlet (110)
   Membrane components (112,114)

PN US2018057780-A1; US10633620-B2
TI Accelerated aging spirit/beverage, comprises            generating wood fibers/chips, and contacting wood            fibers/chips with spirits/beverages in presence of            subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide, to            obtain an aged spirit/beverage.
AU DECARO M
   WEI G
AE DECARO M (DECA-Individual)
   WEI G (WEIG-Individual)
GA 201816617H
AB    NOVELTY - Accelerated aging (M1) spirit/beverage,                comprises generating wood fibers/chips, and                contacting wood fibers/chips with spirits/beverages                in presence of subcritical/critical/supercritical                carbon dioxide, to obtain an aged                spirit/beverage.
   USE - The method is useful for accelerated aging                spirit/beverage, where the spirit is a tequila, a                red wine, a whiskey, a scotch, a brandy, a vodka, a                rum, a bourbon, a beer, a rice wine or an eau de                vie, and the beverage is a tea, a sports drink, an                energy drink, a coffee, a juice or water (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides aged spirit/beverage that                approximates or exceeds the taste and quality of a                spirit/beverage aged 1 month to 30 years in a wood                barrel, and has at least one added characteristic                (i.e. aroma, appearance and/or taste) from the                spirit/beverage before the aging process; reduces                the aging time from more than 1 year to 6 months,                preferably to 5 minutes; and utilizes the wood                fibers/chips which are re-used or re-cycled with                fresh wood fibers/chips, and re-activated after                completing the aging process (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) accelerated aging (M2) spirits/beverages,                comprising generating wood fibers/chips, an                activating wood fibers/chips and soaking activated                wood fibers/chips with spirits/beverages to obtain                aged spirit/beverage; and
   (2) a system for accelerated aging of                spirits/beverages, comprising a carbon dioxide                supply device (152) with a pump (158) and                filtration filter (154), a                subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide                aging vessel with a temperature control element and                a pressure monitor, a permeable cage device for                holding wood fibers/chips and/or natural entities,                and a carbon dioxide separation and recycle                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of                system.
   Carbon dioxide supply device (152)
   Filtration filter (154)
   Chiller (156)
   Pump (158)
   Aging reactor (160)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The wood fibers/chips are                  generated from an oak wood, a chestnut wood, a                  lemon wood, a berry wood and/or a beech wood. The                  step of generating wood fibers/chips further                  comprises a heat treatment of wood before or post                  fiber/chip generation, where the heat treatment                  is toasting, charring or baking the wood at                  60-500 degrees C for 5 minutes to 96 hours. The                  step of contacting wood fibers/chips with                  spirits/beverages is repeated at least one time.                  Preferred Components: The                  subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon dioxide                  has a pressure of 50-600 bar and a temperature of                  20-150 degrees C, where the contacting time is 5                  minutes to 24 hours. In the method (M1), during                  contacting wood fibers/chips with                  spirits/beverages, the ratio of wood fiber/chip                  to spirit/beverage is 1:2-1:10000. The activated                  wood fibers/chips comprise an increased surface                  area of 1-200 times. The step of activating wood                  fibers/chips further comprises: impregnating                  flavors from natural entities, where the natural                  entity is a herb, a fruit, a vegetable, a flower                  and/or a therapeutic; and soaking wood                  fibers/chips in                  subcritical/critical/supercritical carbon                  dioxide, and the soaking time is 5 minutes to 96                  hours. The step of soaking activated wood                  fibers/chips is: under a vacuum environment of                  800 mTorr to 50 mTorr; and under a positive                  pressure environment of 2-500 bar, where the                  positive pressure is under subcritical, critical                  or supercritical carbon dioxide. The steps of                  activating wood fibers/chips and soaking                  activated wood fibers/chips are repeated for                  greater than or equal to 1 times.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The permeable cage                  device has multiple compartments for holding                  different wood fibers/chips and/or natural                  entities. The different wood fibers/chips are                  from raw wood, activated wood, impregnated wood                  or re-activated wood.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN113510250-A; CN215902726-U
TI Multi-material and integrated intelligent manufacturing device for material addition and reduction equipment, has powder feeding nozzle externally connected with cooling jacket that is filled with carbon dioxide aqueous solution.
AU LU H
   GAO X
   YUAN X
AE SUZHOU YANTUO AUTOMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2021C8689M
AB    NOVELTY - The device has an equipment frame (2) installed on the upper end of the equipment fixing base (1). A movable console is connected to the equipment frame telecommunication. An iron core frame is installed at the middle of the upper end of the movable operating table. A laser generator is telecommunicationly connected in the equipment frame. A refraction frame is installed at the lower end of the laser generator. The inner walls of the left and right ends of the refraction frame are fixedly connected with refraction lenses. The lower end of the refraction frame is fixedly connected with a powder feeding nozzle. The inside of the powder feeding nozzle is communicated with the inside of the refraction frame. A number of annular tubes are fixedly connected to the powder feeding nozzle. The powder feeding nozzle is externally connected with a cooling jacket. The cooling jacket is filled with carbon dioxide aqueous solution.
   USE - Multi-material and integrated intelligent manufacturing device for material addition and reduction equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The aspirator extracts the splashed metal powder and recovers it into the suction frame. The device can achieve laser emission through the laser generator, and the refractive lens concentrates the laser, avoids dispersion, and adds metal powder to the powder feeding nozzle to converge into the laser beam. The spring pulls the attracting block to reset, without causing the percussion hammer to strike the inner wall of the ring tube. The impact of the percussion hammer does not cause the carbon dioxide gas to overflow. The temperature of the carbon dioxide is cooled by the endothermic heat insulation of the carbon dioxide gas and the carbon dioxide aqueous solution to prevent the temperature of the metal powder from becoming too high. A circumferential induced current is generated in the manufactured workpiece, and the induced current is used to magnetize the workpiece to generate a closed magnetic field and adsorb metal powder through the change of the magnetic flux in the iron core in the additive manufacturing process. The utilization rate is improved, and the aspirating machine extracts the splashed metal powder and recovers into the suction frame for recycling, which effectively improves the utilization rate of the metal powder and avoids waste of resources.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a multi-material and integrated intelligent manufacturing device for material addition and reduction equipment.
   Equipment fixing base (1)
   Equipment frame (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: Two refractive lenses are made of zinc arsenic materials. Multiple annular tubes are made of high temperature resistant materials.Multiple suction blocks are all made of metal materials, and the surfaces of multiple suction blocks are all painted with a heat-insulating coating.Multiple springs are all made of Inconel 718 material.The inner walls of two suction frames are both coated with a magnetic layer made of magnetic powder material, and the surfaces of the two suction frames are both coated with a high temperature resistant coating.Two suction pipes and the exhaust pipe are made of high-temperature resistant materials, and the surfaces of the two suction machines are painted with high-temperature resistant coatings.

MC M22-H03G; P53-V06C; P53-C; X25-A08B
