TI Use of calcium carbonate-based composition            comprising first component being natural ground calcium            carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate, and second            component being surface-reacted calcium carbonate, as            anticaking agent for controlling, reducing or            preventing caking.
AU SHARMA L
   LEX M
   NEGRINI R
   BUDDE T
   LANDER S
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2021A37408
AB    NOVELTY - Use of calcium carbonate-based composition is                claimed, as an anticaking agent, where the calcium                carbonate-based composition comprises a first                component being a natural ground calcium carbonate                or a precipitated calcium carbonate, and a second                component being a surface-reacted calcium                carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground                calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate                with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium ions                donor, where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ                by at least one hydronium ions donor treatment                and/or is supplied from an external source.
   USE - The calcium carbonate-based composition is                used as an anticaking agent for controlling,                reducing or preventing caking.
   ADVANTAGE - The calcium carbonate-based composition has an                improved flow properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a particulate composition comprising a                calcium carbonate-based anticaking composition and                a base component, where the calcium carbonate-based                anticaking composition comprises a first component                being a natural ground calcium carbonate or a                precipitated calcium carbonate, and a second                component being a surface-reacted calcium                carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground                calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate                with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium ions                donor, where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ                by at least one hydronium ions donor treatment                and/or is supplied from an external source, where                the particulate composition comprises 0.1-50 wt.%,                preferably 0.1-20 wt.%, more preferably 0.1-10                wt.%, even more preferably 0.2-5 wt.%, and most                preferably 0.3-2.5 wt.% calcium carbonate-based                anticaking composition; and
   (2) a method for producing particulate                composition involves mixing a calcium                carbonate-based anticaking composition with a base                component, where the calcium carbonate-based                anticaking composition comprises a first component                being a natural ground calcium carbonate or a                precipitated calcium carbonate, and a second                component being a surface-reacted calcium                carbonate, where the surface-reacted calcium                carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground                calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate                with carbon dioxide and at least one hydronium ions                donor, where the carbon dioxide is formed in situ                by at least one hydronium ions donor treatment                and/or is supplied from an external source, where                the calcium carbonate-based anticaking composition                is admixed into the base component in an amount of                from 0.1-50 wt.%, preferably 0.3-2.5 wt.%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The calcium                  carbonate-based composition comprises first                  component and second component. The calcium                  carbonate-based composition is added to a base                  component chosen from a food composition, a feed                  composition, a nutraceutical composition, a                  pharmaceutical composition and a cosmetic                  composition, preferably the composition is chosen                  from a food composition or a feed composition.                  The food composition is food salt, curing salt,                  salt substitute, milk powder, skimmed milk                  powder, cream powder, egg powder, whey fat                  powder, protein powder, vending machine powder,                  grated cheese, sugar, powdered food flavor,                  spice, seasoning, packet soup mixture, baking                  mixture, pudding powder, mousse powder, or sauce                  powder, or the feed composition is pet food, milk                  replacer for animals, or mineral salt for                  animals. Preferred Component: The weight ratio of                  the first component to the second component                  99:1-1:99, preferably 95:5-10:90, even more                  preferably 90:10-20:80, even more preferably                  85:15-30:70, and most preferably 80:20-40:60. The                  first component is a natural ground calcium                  carbonate chosen from marble, chalk, limestone,                  and their mixtures, or the first component is a                  precipitated calcium carbonate chosen from                  precipitated calcium carbonates having an                  aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic crystal form,                  and their mixtures. The surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate is a reaction product of natural ground                  calcium carbonate chosen from marble, chalk,                  limestone, and their mixtures with carbon dioxide                  and at least one hydronium ions donor, where the                  carbon dioxide is formed in situ by at least one                  hydronium ions donor treatment and/or is supplied                  from an external source, or the surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate is a reaction product of                  precipitated calcium carbonate chosen from                  precipitated calcium carbonates having an                  aragonitic, vateritic or calcitic crystal form,                  and their mixtures, with carbon dioxide and at                  least one hydronium ions donor, where the carbon                  dioxide is formed in situ by at least one                  hydronium ions donor treatment and/or is supplied                  from an external source. The hydronium ions donor                  is chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulphuric acid,                  sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, citric acid,                  oxalic acid, an acidic salt, acetic acid, formic                  acid, and their mixtures, preferably at least one                  hydronium ions donor is chosen from hydrochloric                  acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric                  acid, oxalic acid, hydrogen peroxide, being at                  least partially neutralized by a cation chosen                  from lithium ions, sodium ions and/or potassium                  ions, hydrogenphosphate, being at least partially                  neutralized by a cation selected from lithium                  ions, sodium ions, potassium ions, magnesium                  ions, and/or calcium ions, and their mixtures,                  more preferably at least one hydronium ions donor                  is chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid, oxalic acid, or                  their mixtures, and most preferably at least one                  hydronium ion donor is phosphoric acid. The first                  component has a volume median particle size d50                  of 0.1-50 mu m, preferably 0.8-8 mu m, and/or the                  first component has a specific surface area of                  0.5-30 m2/g, preferably 1-10 m2/g, measured using                  nitrogen and the Brunauer Emmett Teller (BET)                  method. The second component has a volume median                  particle size d50 of 0.5-50 mu m, preferably 3-10                  mu m, and/or the second component has a specific                  surface area of 15-200 m2/g, preferably 70-120                  m2/g, measured using nitrogen and the BET method.                  The calcium carbonate-based composition has a                  residual moisture of less than 10 wt.%,                  preferably less than 5 wt.%.

TI Two-stage reactor system for producing syngas and            carbon, comprises compression unit for compressing gas            feed inputs, reactor for producing solid carbon and            unreacted gases, electrostatic precipitator, heat            exchanger, and other reactor.
AU ELBASHIR N O
   CHALLIWALA M A
   SENGUPTA D
   EL-HALWAGI M M
   MAHMOUD E M
AE QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
   QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
   QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
GA 201878130E
AB    NOVELTY - A two-stage reactor system comprises a                compression unit (5) for compressing gas feed                inputs, a reactor (1) configured for receiving the                compressed gas feed and producing a solid carbon                (7) and unreacted gases, an electrostatic                precipitator (12) configured for receiving the                unreacted gases and separating a recovered solid                carbon (15) and a reactor feed gas, a solid carbon                and catalyst recovery unit (8) configured for                receiving the solid carbon from the reactor (1) and                the recovered solid carbon from the precipitator, a                heat exchanger (16) configured for receiving the                reactor feed gas from the precipitator and                providing high temperature reactor feed gases, and                a reactor (20) configured for receiving the high                temperature reactor feed gases from the heat                exchanger and providing an output of high                temperature syngas (21) back to the heat                exchanger.
   USE - Two-stage reactor system used for producing                synthesis gas used as feedstock for production of                value-added chemicals e.g. hydrocarbon cuts such as                liquid transportation fuels, methanol and dimethyl                ether, and carbonaceous compound (all claimed) used                for production of black ink for printer and                production of graphite of different grades for                manufacturing cast iron/steel or batteries of                different grades.
   ADVANTAGE - The two-stage reactor system provides enhanced                carbon dioxide utilization for chemical and fuel                processes, while ensuring fixation of                carbon-dioxide. The production method is economical                and has high efficiency and capability to convert                more than 65% carbon dioxide per pass of reactor.                The inexpensive catalyst helps to improve carbon                formation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production of syngas and carbonaceous material,                which involves providing a compressed feed gas (19)                comprising carbon dioxide and at least one volatile                organic compound to the reactor (1) which is carbon                generating reactor and comprising a catalyst (A),                producing a gaseous product including at least one                of flue gases, carbon dioxide and unreacted                methane, and a solid carbon in the reactor (1),                removing the solid carbon from the reactor (1),                feeding the gaseous product produced in the reactor                (1) to a reactor (20) comprising a catalyst (B)                which is different from the catalyst (A).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                two-stage reactor system for enhanced carbon                dioxide fixation.
   Reactors (1,20)
   Steam (4,18)
   Compression unit (5)
   Solid carbon (7)
   Solid carbon and catalyst recovery unit                (8)
   Electrostatic precipitator (12)
   Recovered solid carbon (15)
   Heat exchanger unit (16)
   Feed gases (19)
   High temperature syngas (21)

TI Preparing coal-fired power plant flue gas carbon dioxide capture coupling biochar involves separating gas film, liquefying at low temperature, treating molten carbonate salt fuel cell technology by catalytic reforming molten carbonate.
AU YANG Z

TI System for producing power and capturing carbon            dioxide at offshore site, has air separation units for            separating air into nitrogen and oxygen, and gas            turbine generator unit for generating electricity with            oxygen and gas.
AU PARMAR C
   MICK M B
AE EXMAR OFFSHORE CO (EXMA-Non-standard)
GA 2022C7777V
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has an offshore vessel in                fluid communication with a reservoir. Separator                units (108) separate fluid produced from a                reservoir into water fraction, oil fraction and gas                fraction. An oxy-firing gas turbine generator unit                (101) is adapted to receive as fuel input gas                fraction separated by the separator units. Air                separation units (102) separate air into nitrogen                and oxygen, where the generator unit receives                oxygen from the air separation units and compress                exhaust gas from the oxy-firing gas turbine                generator units to produce compressed exhaust gas.                Dehydration units dehydrate the compressed exhaust                gas from gas compression units to produce                dehydrated compressed exhaust gas. Gas pumps pump a                remaining portion of the dehydrated compressed                exhaust gas, where the gas turbine generator unit                generates electricity with the oxygen, the gas and                a portion of the dehydrated compressed exhaust                gas.
   USE - System for producing power and capturing                carbon dioxide at offshore site i.e. offshore oil                and gas production.
   ADVANTAGE - The system allows the oxy-firing gas turbine                generator units to generate the electricity with                the oxygen, the gas and the portion of the                dehydrated compressed exhaust gas, so that carbon                dioxide is captured from offshore oil and gas                facilities, thus enhancing oil recovery using the                captured carbon in an efficient manner. The                oxy-firing gas turbine generator unit receives a                portion of the dehydrated compressed exhaust gas,                so that the carbon dioxide is mixed with the air                and fuel so as to enhance volume of gas heated                during combustion process, thus improving overall                efficiency of a turbine generator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for generating power and capturing carbon dioxide                CO at an offshore site.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of a system for producing oil, generating power and                capturing carbon.

   USE - The bimetallic solid acid catalyst is useful in reducing energy consumption of organic amine adsorption carbon dioxide regeneration (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bimetallic solid acid catalyst: can effectively improve the problems of low carbon dioxide desorption efficiency and high regeneration energy consumption; has peak rate of carbon dioxide desorption from amine solution increased by 127%; has high stability and energy efficiency of five cycles is higher than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing bimetallic solid acid catalyst comprising (a) dissolving the soluble salt of metal oxide and zirconium sulfate in deionized water to prepare metal salt solution, (b) adding molecular sieve aqueous solution in a drop wise manner to the metal salt solution in step (a) via separating funnel and evaporating magnetic stirring in the water bath to dryness to obtain a precursor and (c) heating precursor at 300-800degrees Celsius through a tube furnace at a speed of 5-10degrees Celsius/min and calcining for 5-8 hours to obtain bimetallic solid acid catalyst; and
   (2) use method of bimetallic solid acid catalyst comprises adding bimetallic solid acid catalyst to the organic amine aqueous solution containing carbon dioxide, reacting carbon dioxide and desorbing at 80-100 DEG C to realize the regeneration of the organic amine.

The molecular sieve in the molecular sieve aqueous solution in the step (b) and the total mass ratio of the metal oxide soluble salt and the zirconium sulfate in the step (a) is 0.5-1.0:1. In the step (b), the addition amount of aqueous molecular sieve solution is 3-10 ml/minute.Preferred Method: In the step (a), dissolution is performed at 50-80degrees Celsius. The temperature of the water bath is 50-80degrees Celsius and magnetic stirring is performed at a speed of 200-500 revolutions/minute.

PT P
PN CN111521577-A
TI Quantitative analysis method for infrared spectroscopy using peak area of carbon dioxide, involves establishing detection curve, and performing analysis processing, for certain substance of unknown concentration.
AU LIU W
   ZHAO F
   LIU S
   MI W
   YIN S
   LIU X
   LI C
   LIU C
AE UNIV TSINGHUA HEFEI INST PUBLIC SAFETY (UYQI-C)
GA 202078609V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves taking (S1) the purity of analytically pure chemical substance as the standard sample of infrared spectrum detection. A carbon dioxide peak as reference peak is selected (S2), in the infrared spectrogram of the standard sample. An analysis processing is carried out (S3) aiming at the standard sample with the concentration of 100 percent. The analysis processing is performed (S4), for the standard sample with the concentration of 10 percent. The analysis processing is perfumed (S5), aiming at the standard sample with different concentration. A detection curve is established (S6). The analysis processing is performed (S7), for a certain substance of unknown concentration. The infrared spectrum signal of the substance is collected and analyzed. The infrared spectrum of the substance is obtained. The peak area of the characteristic peak in the infrared spectrum of the substance and the peak area of the carbon dioxide peak is calculated.
   USE - Quantitative analysis method for infrared spectroscopy using peak area of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes accurate quantitative analysis of mixture, adulterant and non-pure substance, solves the substance detection problem with low purity and large interference, and realizes the quantitative detection function of infrared spectrum on the basis of qualitative analysis of infrared spectrum.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a quantitative analysis method for infrared spectroscopy using peak area of carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for taking the purity of analytically pure chemical substance as the standard sample of infrared spectrum detection (S1)
   Step for selecting carbon dioxide peak as reference peak (S2)
   Step for carrying out analysis processing (S3)
   Step for performing the analysis processing (S4)
   Step for performing the analysis processing, aiming at the standard sample with different concentration (S5)
   Step for establishing a detection curve (S6)
   Step for performing the analysis processing, for a certain substance of unknown concentration (S7)
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation); T01 (Digital Computers)
MC E11-Q03C; E31-N05C; J04-C01; J04-C02C; J04-C04A; S03-E04A5; T01-J13

PT P
PN US2020207684-A1; US11008265-B2
TI Production of hydrocarbon compounds, such as            alkanes, e.g. ethane, involves performing oxidative            coupling of methane, and separating into stream            comprising hydrocarbon compounds, and stream comprising            carbon dioxide, hydrogen and methane.
AU RAFIQUE H A
   VUDDAGIRI S
   RADAELLI G
   MCCORMICK J
   DUGGAL S
   CIZERON J
AE LUMMUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (CBRI-C)
   LUMMUS TECHNOLOGY LLC (CBRI-C)
GA 202153734P
AB    NOVELTY - Production of hydrocarbon compounds comprising                2C or more compounds involves performing (i)                oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) in an OCM                reactor (201) to produce an OCM effluent (204)                comprising carbon dioxide, hydrogen, 2C or more                compounds and methane, separating (ii) the OCM                effluent into a stream (s1) comprising 2C or more                compounds, and a stream (s2) comprising carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen and methane,                methanating (iii) the stream (s2) to produce an OCM                reactor feed (f1) comprising methane formed using                hydrogen and carbon monoxide and/or carbon dioxide                in the stream (s2), methanating (iv) a stream (s3)                comprising methane and hydrogen from an ethylene                cracker to produce an OCM reactor feed (f2)                comprising methane, and directing (v) the reactor                feeds (f1,f2) to the OCM reactor.
   USE - Production of hydrocarbon compounds comprising                2C or more compounds, such as alkanes e.g. ethane,                propane, butane, pentane, etc., or alkenes e.g.                ethylene, propylene, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical, efficient and                commercially-viable production of hydrocarbon                compounds comprising 2C or more compounds with high                yield, and reduced energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for OCM                system, which comprises an OCM subsystem which                inputs a feed stream comprising methane and an                oxidizing agent, and generates a product stream                comprising 2C or more compounds, a separation                subsystem which separates the product stream into                stream (s1) and stream (s2), fluidically coupled to                the OCM subsystem, a methanation subsystem which                converts hydrogen and carbon dioxide and/or carbon                monoxide into methane, fluidically coupled to the                stream and to the OCM subsystem, and ethylene                cracker subsystem which provides methane, hydrogen,                carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide to the                methanation subsystem, fluidically coupled to the                methanation subsystem.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                oxidative coupling of methane system for                small-scale olefin production.
   Oxidative coupling of methane reactor                (201)
   Oxygen stream (202)
   Methane stream (203)
   Oxidative coupling of methane effluent stream                (204)
   Methanation reactor (207)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The methanation                  subsystem provides methane to the OCM subsystem.                  The ethylene cracker subsystem comprises a                  demethanizer that provides methane, hydrogen,                  carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide to the                  methanation subsystem. The stream (s2) is passed                  to the ethylene cracker subsystem, and the                  ethylene cracker subsystem is configured to                  fractionate the stream (s1). The separation                  subsystem comprises PSA unit. The OCM subsystem                  comprises at least one OCM reactor, and at least                  one post-bed cracking unit within the at least                  one OCM reactor or downstream of the at least one                  OCM reactor. The post-bed cracking unit is                  configured to convert portion of alkanes in the                  product stream to alkenes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The stream (s2) and the                  stream (s3) are methanated in a single                  methanation reactor (207). The process further                  involves feeding oxygen which is generated from                  air as an oxidizing agent to the OCM reactor, and                  providing at least a portion of the stream (s1)                  to a gas compressor or a fractionation unit of                  the ethylene cracker. The separation in the                  process (ii) is performed in a pressure swing                  adsorption (PSA) unit, a carbon dioxide removal                  system or a process gas dryer. The OCM effluent                  is compressed prior to the process (ii).                  Preferred Composition: The stream (s3) is the                  overhead stream of a demethanizer unit of the                  ethylene cracker. The OCM effluent comprises                  carbon monoxide that is converted into methane in                  the process (iii). The OCM reaction involves                  reacting methane from natural gas with oxidizing                  agent in the OCM subsystem to achieve additional                  ethylene production.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)

PN US2019383751-A1
TI Apparatus for monitoring respiratory stream i.e. respiratory gas, in operating room setting, has breathing circuit adapter coupled to breathing circuit, and color change material comprising reactive portion and unreactive portion.
AU MORETTI E W
   WOOD R L
   SHANG A B
   YAUCH S S
AE RESPIRION LLC (RESP-Non-standard)
GA 2019A60178
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a breathing circuit adapter coupled to a breathing circuit to provide respiratory stream. A color change material (100) comprises a reactive portion and an unreactive portion. The color change material is positioned partially in an inner side of the breathing circuit adapter to contact with the respiratory stream. The reactive and unreactive portions are separated from each other, where the reactive portion provides first color responsive to carbon dioxide level in the respiratory stream and the unreactive portion provides second color different from the first color responsive to the carbon dioxide level in the respiratory stream.
   USE - Apparatus for monitoring a respiratory stream i.e. respiratory gas, in an operating room setting using a carbon dioxide indicator (claimed) to perform emergency laryngoscopy and intubation under a variety of conditions and duress by first responders, respiratory therapists and critical care personnel, and level of carbon dioxide in closed breathing systems at facilities. Uses include but are not limited to mass transit system such as train, airplane, bus, fire fighting breathing apparatus, mining environments, underwater breathing equipment i.e. scuba, space application, and military application and large crowds congregate e.g. stadium.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus utilizes control composition and/or control material to be responsive to the carbon dioxide level after particular time period is finished to indicate that shelf life of a device is expired. The apparatus comprises detectors to provide sufficient accuracy to guide clinical decisions regarding effectiveness of emergency procedures such as ventilation and/or CPR. The apparatus selects substrate material to provide mechanical support for a color change layer with desirable optical properties to facilitate photometric measurement of the color change layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an apparatus for monitoring a respiratory stream in an operating room setting using a carbon dioxide indicator.
   Color change material (100)
   Interior surface (1101)
   Electronic emitter (1105)
   Adapter (1107)
   Electronic sensor (1110)

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:

   (5) weighing the bag and getting the oil resin seasoning bag; and
   (6) use of oil resin seasoning bag is claimed for cooking brine goose.

Preferred Condition: The oil resin seasoning bag, salt, and white sugar are put into water, and the brine goose is cooked. PN BR102016026301-A2
TI System for production of carbonate water and removal of carbon dioxide from gasses produced with submarine separation by stationary hydrocarbon production unit installed near surface of sea, comprises gas-liquid separator.
AU RIBEIRO LIMA P C
AE RIBEIRO LIMA P C (LIMA-Individual)
GA 201848274U
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises gas-liquid separator which secretes the gasses produced from the liquid phase from the reservoir, compressor of the gasses produced by water pump, mixer of gasses produced with water, descending flow pipe, where water mixture flows with produced gases, gas-water separator (105) installed near the seabed, which segregates carbonated water from undissolved gases, carbonated water to be injected into an oil reservoir or underground formation, carbonate water injector wells, and undissolved gasses to be transported to natural gas treatment units.
   USE - System used for production of carbonate water and removal of carbon dioxide from gasses produced with submarine separation by stationary hydrocarbon production unit installed near surface of sea.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for producing carbonated water and removing carbon dioxide from produced gases, which involves:
   (A) separating the gasses produced from the liquid phase, from an oil reservoir, into a production unit;
   (B) treating gasses produced;
   (C) compressing produced gases;
   (D) capturing, treating and compressing water;
   (E) mixing the produced gasses with water;
   (F) transporting the mixture of gasses produced with water produced in the mixer installed in the production plant to the water-gas separator installed near the seabed by downward flow pipe;
   (G) producing carbonated water in the downward flow pipe;
   (H) segregating the carbonated water from the undissolved gasses in the water separator;
   (I) treatng carbonated water;
   (J) recovering oil better or sequestering carbon dioxide by injecting carbonated water into a reservoir or underground formation; and
   (K) treating and dehydrating undissolved gasses on the seabed or in production units near the surface of the sea to transport treated and dehydrated non-dissolved gasses for consumption, to pipelines, or to conventional units for the removal of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of producing of carbonate water and removal of carbon dioxide device.
   Production stationary unit (101)
   Gas-water separator (105)
   Liquid flow (110)
   Gaseous flow (115)
   Treatment gases produced (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The injection of the carbonated water into an oil reservoir or underground formation is carried out simultaneously with a fraction of the undissolved gases, preferably carbonyl water or not. The undissolved gasses is treated on the seabed or in production units. The removal of carbon dioxide from various remissions of the gasses is produced with water. The gasses produced is desulphurized. The method for producing carbonated water and removing carbon dioxide from produced gasses involves subjecting desulfation, desalination and reduction of the acidity of water and carbonated water.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Method for manufacturing panel for display device with liquid crystal display, involves providing substrate, where home is formed on substrate using a drill, and burr protruded to hole inside is removed, and dry ice is sprayed.
AU HONG J I
   KWAK J W
AE LG DISPLAY CO LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 201838152E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a substrate (110,120), where a home is formed on the substrate using a drill. The groove inside the substrate is removed, where a hole (111) is formed on the substrate. The burr (112) protruded to a hole inside is removed, where the burr for spraying the dry ice (611) is removed by using a carbon dioxide spraying equipment (600). The residue separated from the substrate is inhaled by the carbon dioxide spraying equipment, where the dry ice is sprayed on the burr. The dry ice is sprayed through a nozzle (620) included in the carbon dioxide spraying equipment.
   USE - Method for manufacturing a panel for a display device (Claimed) with a liquid crystal display, a plasma display device, an electric field emission display device, and electrophoresis image display, an electro phoretic display device, an electrowetting display device and an organic light emitting display device.
   ADVANTAGE - The burr protruded to a hole inside is removed, where the burr for spraying the dry ice is removed by using a carbon dioxide spraying equipment, and hence enables minimizing the interference between a burr and a camera module due to the generated process margin and minimizes the size of the burr, and also reduces the power consumption, thus reduces the weight of a display device and minimizes the size of the burr.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a display device with a board; and
   (2) a deburring equipment with a storage for storing the carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of a method for spraying the dry ice on a particle of a substrate left in a groove inside.
   Substrates (110,120)
   Hole (111)
   Burr (112)

   Sealant (130)
   Carbon dioxide spraying equipment (600)
   Dry ice (611)

   DONG Y
AE TAIHE KANGMEI BEIJING CHINESE MEDICINE (TAIH-Non-standard)
GA 202208658C
AB    NOVELTY - A Lycium ruthenicum extract is prepared by crushing Lycium ruthenicum, drying to obtain Lycium ruthenicum powder, adding into the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction apparatus for supercritical extraction under 15-40 MPa at 45-60degrees Celsius at carbon dioxide flow rate of 5-15 L/hour using ethyl acetate-ethanol mixed solution, entrainer for 1-2 hours, and separating the distilled water in the separating kettle.
   USE - Method for preparing Lycium ruthenicum extract used in skin care product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good anti-stimulation, water replenishing and moisturizing effect, and has wide application prospect in the cosmetic field.

Preferred Method: The preparation of Lycium ruthenicum extract further comprises taking 50-65% of ethanol-water solution as entrainer according to mass volume ratio of 1:1-1:10, preferably 1:1-1:5, continuously extracting under 15-40 MPa at 45-60degrees Celsius at carbon dioxide flow rate of 5-15 L/hour for 0.5-2 hours, separating entrainer containing Lycium ruthenicum active ingredient from the separating kettle, and taking 70-95% of ethanol-water solution as entrainer according to mass volume ratio of 1:1-1:10, preferably 1:1-1:5, continuously extracting under 15-40 MPa at 45-60degrees Celsius at carbon dioxide flow rate of 5-15 L/hour for 0.5-2 hours, separating entrainer containing Lycium ruthenicum active ingredient from the separating kettle, combining obtained entrainer, and removing entrainer to obtain Lycium ruthenicum extract.

TI Operating incineration devices e.g. ovens or boilers in integrated metallurgical plants, involves using gas mixture comprising hydrogen, methane and inert gas produced in climate-neutral manner as fuel gas under reduced carbon dioxide emissions.
AU SCHAPER R
   LAPP T
AE SALZGITTER FLACHSTAHL GMBH (SALZ-C)
GA 2021988548
AB    NOVELTY - Operating incineration devices involves using a gas mixture comprising at least of hydrogen, methane and inert gas as fuel gas under reduced carbon dioxide emissions. The fuel gas is produced synthetically and at least portion of the hydrogen and/or at least portion of the methane and/or at least portion of the inert gas are produced in a climate-neutral manner.
   USE - Operating incineration devices e.g. ovens or boilers in integrated metallurgical plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures less carbon dioxide emission.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The synthetically produced fuel gas comprises 55-75 vol.% hydrogen, 20-35 vol.% methane, and 4-15 vol.% nitrogen. The fuel gas comprises at least 5 vol.%, preferably at least 95 vol.% hydrogen, at least 5 vol.%, preferably at least 95 vol.% methane, at least 5 vol.%, preferably at least 95 vol.% nitrogen, and at least 5 vol.%, preferably at least 95 vol.% gas mixture. To achieve a saving of at least 40% carbon dioxide emissions compared to the use of natural gas when burning the gas mixture according to the combustion devices, at least the hydrogen and the nitrogen contained in the synthetically produced fuel gas are produced in a completely climate-neutral manner. Preferred Components: All of the hydrogen used and at least some of the inert gas and/or at least some of the methane are generated in a climate-neutral manner. All or portion of the hydrogen is obtained from the electrolysis of water, steam reforming and/or methane pyrolysis as a climate-neutral product. The hydrogen and/or the nitrogen used as inert gas obtained completely or partially from climate-neutral ammonia or other nitrogen-hydrogen compounds. The methane used is completely or partially climate-neutral from biomass and/or carbon dioxide obtained from the air or exhaust gases and green turquoise or blue hydrogen. The inert gas comprises nitrogen or argon. All or portion of the nitrogen or argon used is obtained from the decomposition of air as a climate-neutral product. Preferred Process: The method involves generating hydrogen by electrolysis of water using electricity from carbon dioxide-free generation, and/or by steam reforming and/or by methane pyrolysis, and mixing the hydrogen, methane, inert gas and optionally other components, generating methane from biomass and/or carbon dioxide extracted from air or exhaust gases and green turquoise or blue hydrogen, mixing the methane with hydrogen, inert gas and optionally other components. Preferred Property: The lower calorific value, the density, the minimum air requirement and the adiabatic combustion temperature of the fuel gas with respect to the corresponding values of the coke gas each or in total is less than 15%, preferably less than 5%.

   ZHANG T

The base                material is soaked in oxidizing of chemical                processing liquid surface is nanowire structure of                electrode precursor. The electrode precursor is                electrochemically reduced to obtain an electrode                with a nanowire structural layer attached to the                surface.
   USE - Method for preparing electrode for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare electrode which                provides more active sites for carbon dioxide                electrochemical reduction reaction and improves                yield of target product, has subsequent reaction                course of supercleaning modulation to change                selectivity of target product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The conductive material                  is carbon material, carbon paper, carbon cloth or                  carbon felt. The metal base material is copper,                  silver, gold, palladium, and lead. The base state                  is foil, sheet, block, or supported on conductive                  base material. The cation comprises alkali metal                  ion or ammonium ion. Preferred Condition: The                  anion is sulfate and hydroxide. The sulfate                  concentration is 0.05-0.5 molar. The best                  concentration range is 0.1-0.3 molar. The pH                  range is 9-14 in chemical processing solution of                  persulfate in oxidizing. The best pH range is                  12-13. Preferred Process: The treatment time of                  base material in the oxidizing chemical treatment                  liquid is 10-600 minutes, and optimal treatment                  time is 30-180 minutes. The temperature is from                  room temperature to 60 degrees C. The optimum                  treatment temperature is from 30-50 degrees C, it                  is washed to neutrality and dried with nitrogen                  or argon. The substrate material is                  decontaminated includes chemical treatment or                  electrochemical treatment, where chemical                  treatment is concentrated hydrochloric acid at                  mass concentration of 36-38 wt.%, soaking in acid                  solution, immersing time is 5-30 minutes, washing                  to neutrality and drying with nitrogen or argon.                  Preferred Process: The electrochemical treatment                  is based on mass concentration of 85-87 wt.%                  phosphoric acid as treatment liquid. The                  treatment potential is 1-4 volt. The optimum                  treatment potential is 2-3 volt. The treatment                  time is 5-30 minutes. The treatment temperature                  is room temperature. The weak basic salt solution                  is bicarbonate solution or a carbonate solution,                  where metal is alkali metal and pH of the                  solution is 8-9. The electrode is an electrode                  having nanowire structure layer attached to                  surface of base material, and density of nanowire                  is 100-1000 per centimeter square. The nanowires                  have length of 2-20 mu m and diameter of 30-500                  nanometer. The electrochemical reduction                  treatment process involves introducing nitrogen                  or argon gas is into weak alkaline salt solution                  to saturation. The platinum slice is used as                  counter electrode. The saturated mercury                  electrode is used as reference electrode,                  electrode precursor is used as working electrode,                  and potentiostat is used as a power source, after                  current density is (-)5-(-)50 milliampere per                  centimeter square. The precursor of electrode is                  reduced for 5-60 minutes. The working electrode                  is taken out. The treatment current density is                  preferably 20-40 milliampere per centimeter                  square. The treatment time is preferably 10-30                  minutes.

   X = Cl, Br, I, OTf, OTs or OMs; and
   R = not defined.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reaction solvent includes dimethyl sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide, tetrahydrofuran, acetonitrile, N-methylpyrrolidone, toluene, xylene, mesitylene or dioxane. The catalyst includes metal salt of iron, cobalt, nickel, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, iridium, copper, zinc, oxide, or complex with organic ligand. The product further comprises co-catalyst, where co-catalyst is inorganic salt. The reducing agent includes hydrogen, silane or borane. The molar ratio between total amount of carbon dioxide and ammonia and organic (pseudo)halide is 1:1-100:1. The molar ratio between carbon dioxide and ammonia is 0.1:1-100:1. The pressure of the reaction is 1-100 bar. The carbon dioxide and ammonia used in the reaction are isotopically labeled 13CO2, 14CO2, and 15NH3.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nanomaterials by                multi-physics field regulation and crystallization                of calcium-based solid waste dissolution and                crystallization involves dissolving calcium-based                solid waste under alkaline conditions, heating and                stirring at 60-100degrees Celsius for 1-8 hours,                filtering with 0.2-0.45 mum membrane, adding                crystallizer and seed crystal to the filtrate,                continuous introducing carbon dioxide gas under the                conditions of multi-physics field at 60-100degrees                Celsius for 30-360 minutes, solid-liquid                separating, and drying solid.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium-based                  solid waste comprises phosphogypsum, fluorine                  gypsum, desulfurized gypsum, waste slag from the                  desulfurization process of molten iron in the                  iron and steel industry, solid salt from the                  chlorinated wastewater treatment industry, and                  calcium carbonate solid waste from mining and                  metallurgical beneficiation. PT P
PN EP3878550-A1
TI Novel amine-functionalized cellulose polymer for removing carbon dioxide from a gas.
AU KEH E Y M
   YAO L
   CHAN A
   ZHANG H
   LAM U T
   CHEN X
AE HONG KONG RES INST TEXTILES & APPAREL LT (HKRE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0351F
AB    NOVELTY - An amine-functionalized cellulose polymer (I) is new.
   USE - An amine-functionalized cellulose polymer or its conjugate salt for removing carbon dioxide from a gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The amine-functionalized cellulose polymers are capable of adsorbing CO2 from a gas, such as ambient air, by chemical reaction between CO2 and amines allowing significant uptake of CO2, even at low CO2 partial pressures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An amine-functionalized cellulose polymer comprising a repeating unit of formula (I) or its conjugate salt is new.
   R1=-(C=O)NHR2 or -H(C=N-R2); and
   R2=polyalkylenimine or alkylsilane, each optionally crosslinks greater than or equal to 2 repeating units (I), and R2 in two instances of repeating unit (I) are optionally crosslinked with a moiety of formula (i);
   n=2-6; and
   Nasterisk=N in the polyalkylenimine.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) removing carbon dioxide (CO2) from a gas which involves contacting the amine-functionalized cellulose polymer with the gas to form amine-functionalized cellulose polymer-CO2 conjugate and removing CO2 from the gas; and
   (2) preparing the amine-functionalized polymer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Oxidant comprises laccase and (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidin-1-yl)oxyl (TEMPO) or sodium perchlorate (NaClO), (2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidin-1-yl)oxyl (TEMPO), and sodium bromide (NaBr). Cellulose polymer is present at a concentration of 0.5-2 wt.%; laccase is present at a concentration of 0.1-10 wt.%; and TEMPO is present at a concentration of 0.1-10 wt.%. Cellulose polymer is present at a concentration of 5-10 wt.%; NaBr is present at a concentration of 0.1-1 wt.%; TEMPO is present at a concentration of 0.01-0.1 wt.%; and NaClO is present at a concentration of 0.1-10 wt.%. Group R3 is CO2H, and coupling agent comprises N-(3-(dimethylamino)propyl)-N'-ethylcarbodiimide hydrochloride (EDC) and N-hydroxysuccinimide (NHS). EDC is present at a concentration of 2-8 wt.%; and NHS is present at a concentration of 2-8 wt.%. Preferred Processes: Group R3 is -CHO; the step of contacting the cellulose polymer with the oxidant is conducted at 35-45 degrees C, where the cellulose polymer is present at a concentration of 0.5-2 wt.%; the oxidant comprises 0.1-10 wt.% laccase and 0.1-10 wt.% TEMPO; and the step of contacting the oxidized cellulose polymer with the polyalkylenimine is conducted at 20-40 degrees C, where polyalkylenimine is 2-10 wt.% PEI. Group R3 is -CO2H; the step of contacting the cellulose polymer with the oxidant is conducted at 24-40 degrees C, where cellulose polymer is present at a concentration of 5-10 wt.%; the oxidant comprises 0.1-10 wt.% NaClO, 0.01-0.1 wt.% TEMPO, and 0.1-1 wt.% NaBr; and the step of contacting the oxidized cellulose polymer with the polyalkylenimine is conducted at 20-27 degrees C, where polyalkylenimine is 2-10 wt.% PEI, and the coupling agent comprises 2-8 wt.% EDC and 2-8 wt.% NHS.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (Claimed): Amine-functionalized polymer is prepared by contacting cellulose polymer comprising repeating unit of formula (2) with an oxidant to form an oxidized cellulose polymer comprising repeating moiety of formula (3) or its conjugate salt, contacting the oxidized cellulose polymer with a polyalkylenimine or an alkylsilane, provided that when R3 is -CO2H, a coupling agent is also present during the step of contacting the oxidized cellulose polymer with the polyalkylenimine or the alkylsilane, to form the amine-functionalized polymer, and optionally contacting the amine-functionalized polymer with CHO(CH2)nCH2CHO.R3=-CO2H or -CHO.Preferred Compositions: Polyalkylenimine is selected from polyethylenimine (PEI), tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), diethylenetriamine (DETA), and pentaethylenehexamine (PEHA). Cellulose polymer is present at a concentration of 0.5-10 wt.%. Polyalkylenimine is present at a concentration of 2-10 wt.%. Preferred Properties: Polyalkylenimine is PEI with an average molecular weight of 25000 amu, and amine-functionalized cellulose polymer has a CO2 adsorption capacity of 0.29-0.42 mmol CO2 per gram of cellulose powder. Preferred Processes: CO2 removal method further comprises heating the amine-functionalized cellulose polymer-CO2 conjugate at 50-120 degrees C to desorb CO2 from the cellulose polymer-CO2 conjugate and regenerating the amine-functionalized cellulose polymer. Preparation method further comprises the steps of combining the amine-functionalized polymer with a binder to form a surface finishing solution; contacting a textile with the surface finishing solution to form surface finishing solution treated textile; and optionally curing the surface finishing solution treated textile, where the cellulose polymer is in the form of a cellulose powder.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Component: Amine-functionalized cellulose polymer is in the form of a powder or a textile.
   Preferred Definitions:
   n=2.
   EXAMPLE - The examples do not provide details of the amine-functionalized polymer, but provide details of the production of amine-functionalized textiles and their carbon dioxide sorption and desorption tests.

PN CN113318744-A
TI Preparing iron-based catalyst with high carbon            hydrocarbon selectivity useful for carbon dioxide            hydrogenation reaction, comprises e.g. adding oxalic            acid and potassium sodium tartrate to ferrous sulfate            solution, and tableting brick powder.
AU NING W
   HU S
   GUAN F
   LI M
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2021A4168F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing iron-based catalyst with high carbon                hydrocarbon selectivity comprises (1) using                deionized water to dissolve ferrous sulfate to                obtain ferrous sulfate solution, and adding oxalic                acid and potassium sodium tartrate to the ferrous                sulfate solution at the same time to form yellow                turbid solution, and (2) performing multiple                centrifugation on the yellow turbid liquid to                obtain yellow precipitate, drying the yellow                precipitate and roasting to obtain brick red                powder, and tableting the brick red powder into                tablets to obtain iron-based catalyst.
   USE - The iron-based catalyst is useful for carbon                dioxide hydrogenation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and efficient. The                iron-based catalyst catalyzes the carbon dioxide                hydrogenation reaction at low temperature and low                pressure to generate high-carbon hydrocarbon                products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                iron-based catalyst prepared by the preparation                method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  mass ratio of ferrous sulfate to deionized water                  is 1:1-3. The mass ratio of the ferrous sulfate,                  oxalic acid, and potassium sodium tartrate is                  1:0.22-0.68:0.001-0.71. The specific surface of                  the iron-based catalyst is greater than or equal                  to 10 m2/g. The carbon dioxide conversion rate is                  more than 15%, the 5+c selectivity is more than                  30%, and the carbon monoxide selectivity is more                  than 30%. Preferred Method: In the step (2), the                  drying conditions are drying for 9-15 hours, and                  the temperature is lower than 150 degrees C. The                  calcination conditions are calcination 3-10                  hours, and temperature 450-700 degrees C.

PN CN112899428-A
TI Recycling and reusing kanbara reactor desulfurization slag comprises pouring KR desulfurization slag from a slag tank, blowing carbon dioxide gas, expanding and splitting into small particles, cooling, and performing slagging treatment.
AU XIA Y
   FAN D
   LI J
   SUN G
   TAO S
   CAO Y
AE UNIV ANHUI TECHNOLOGY (UYAT-C)
GA 2021680960
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling and reusing kanbara reactor (KR) desulfurization slag comprises (i) pouring the KR desulfurization slag from a slag tank, blowing carbon dioxide gas into the slag, quickly cooling the KR desulfurization slag, completely expanding and splitting into small particles after reacting with the carbon dioxide gas to achieve desulfurization, and recycling in a cold environment to obtain the circulating desulfurization slag, (ii) cooling and splitting the obtained circulating desulfurization slag after sieving, and adding a certain proportion of aluminum ash, (iii) removing the slag of the molten iron in the molten iron tank before processing, (iv) performing slagging treatment, stirring, continuously adding desulfurization agent and circulating desulfurization slag in the stirring process, and (v) removing the desulfurization slag.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling and reusing KR desulfurization slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the utilization efficiency of the desulfurization agent, and achieves recycling of the desulfurization slag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The desulfurization agent comprises not less than 86 wt.% calcium oxide, 3-5 wt.% calcium fluoride, 1-4 wt.% aluminum, 4-5 wt.% aluminum oxide and unavoidable impurities (remaining amount). The aluminum ash comprises 50-70 wt.% aluminum, 15-25 wt.% aluminum oxide, 2-5 wt.% magnesium oxide, 2-4 wt.% sodium oxide, not greater than 4 wt.% silicon dioxide, not greater than 1 wt.% iron oxide and magnesium oxide, and unavoidable impurities (remaining amount).

AB    NOVELTY - Synergistically converting and decomposing                calcium-arsenic precipitate by ultrasonic wave                comprises (i) mixing the calcium-arsenic                precipitate and water according to the                liquid-to-solid ratio of 2-10:1 for slurry                adjustment, introducing the carbon dioxide gas when                the leaching temperature is 25-90 degrees C,                introducing the ultrasonic cavitation generating                device, and reacting the calcium-arsenic                precipitate with the aqueous solution of carbon                dioxide to obtain mixed slurry; (ii) performing                liquid-solid separation of the mixed slurry to                obtain calcium carbonate and arsenic-containing                solution; and (iii) obtaining metal arsenic after                arsenic-containing solution is evaporated and                crystallized-carbothermal reduction.
   USE - The method is useful for synergistically                converting and decomposing calcium-arsenic                precipitate by ultrasonic wave.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the conversion                rate of calcium arsenic precipitates, reduces the                content of arsenic in calcium carbonate, and                ultimately realizes the goal of reducing the                enterprise's hazardous waste disposal fee and                harmless disposal of arsenic.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the                  ultrasonic frequency of the ultrasonic cavitation                  generator is F greater than or equal to 10 kHz,                  and the power density P greater than or equal to                  0.3 W/cm, the cavitation effect produced by the                  ultrasonic cavitation generator has an action                  time of 30-600 minutes, the introduction time of                  carbon dioxide is 30-600 minutes, the conversion                  and decomposition stages of carbon dioxide are                  1-10. In the step (iii), the carbothermal                  reduction temperature is 300-1200 degrees C, the                  time is 10-600 minutes, the reducing agent is                  graphite or coke or coal, and the amount of                  reducing agent is 0.1-5 times the molar ratio of                  C/As. Preferred Components: In the step (i), the                  molar ratio of carbon dioxide and calcium in the                  calcium-arsenic precipitate is 1-10:1.

TI Supercritical extraction of            bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide for lithium battery            electrolyte comprises e.g. heating            bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide salt and strong acid in            supercritical extraction device, and separating using            supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU SO C M
   ZHANG M
   SUN W Y R
   TSE M K
   CHAN A S
   SU Q
   XIN W
   XIE W
   CHEN X
AE LEE & MAN CHEM CO LTD (LEEM-Non-standard)
   ZHUHAI LEE & MAN MATERIALS SCI CO LTD (ZHUH-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU LIWEN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   ZHUHAI LIWEN NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2021482595
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical extraction method of                bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, comprises (a) introducing                bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide salt and strong acid into                extraction kettle of supercritical extraction                device; (b) heating the extraction kettle and                separator to 20-40 degrees C respectively,                introducing supercritical carbon dioxide, adjusting                the flow rate to 10-30 kg/hour, turning on the                high-pressure pump to increase pressure to 20-40                MPa, and circulating extraction for 1-4 hours; and                (c) flowing extracted bis(fluorosulfonyl)imine into                the separator with carbon dioxide, and separating                at 25-35 degrees C under pressure is 6-10 MPa to                obtain the finished product.
   USE - The method is useful in supercritical                extraction of bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide for lithium                battery electrolyte.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high-purity                bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide with high efficiency and                high quality, and is economical, simple, easy,                safe, environmentally friendly and suitable for                industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide salt includes lithium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, sodium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, potassium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, rubidium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, cesium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, calcium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, magnesium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, and/or ammonium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide. The molar ratio of                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide salt to strong acid is                  1:0.5-1:5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (b), the                  extraction temperature is 25-30 degrees C, the                  extraction pressure is 28-35 MPa, and extraction                  time is 1-2 hours. In the step (c), the                  separation pressure is 7-9 MPa, and the                  separation temperature is 28-32 degrees C.                  Preferred Components: The strong acid is                  concentrated sulfuric acid, concentrated                  phosphoric acid, and concentrated nitric                  acid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
